PN CN114566922-A
TI Intelligent building monitoring device for use in weak electricity engineering field, has distribution box connected with gas storage tank that is filled with liquid carbon dioxide, and air storage tank connected with square pipe through electromagnetic valve and guide pipe.
AU HOU X
   ZHOU J
   XU Y
   ZHU D
   XIA Q
AE SHANGHAI ZHENWAN NETWORK INFORMATION ENG (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022775648
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a distribution box (1) whose two sides are fixedly connected with a square tube (2). The square tube is installed in a building wall body. A wire (3) is mounted in the square tube. An end of the wire is extended to an interior of the distribution box that is provided with a fuse (4). The distribution box is fixedly connected with a gas storage tank (5) that is filled with liquid carbon dioxide. An air storage tank is connected with a square pipe (2) through an electromagnetic valve (6) and a guide pipe (7).
   USE - Intelligent building monitoring device for use in a weak electricity engineering field.
   ADVANTAGE - The device quickly generates high pressure gas by the liquid carbon dioxide in the air storage tank by opening the electromagnetic valve when the circuit is abnormal or fault to fill the high pressure gas in the square tube through the guide pipe, so that the square tube is isolated from the air, and the spark generated by the wire is extinguished, thus avoiding the occurrence of fire.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of an intelligent building monitoring device.
   Distribution box (1)
   Square tube (2)
   Wire (3)
   Fuse (4)
   Gas storage tank (5)
   Electromagnetic valve (6)
   Guide pipe (7)
DC P35 (Life-saving, fire-fighting (A62).); X12 (Power Distribution/Components/Converters); X13 (Switchgear, Protection, Electric Drives)
MC P35-C03; P35-C01; X12-G04A1; X13-C09; X13-C04X; X13-C01A
IP A62C-003/16; A62C-031/05; A62C-035/02; A62C-035/68; H02G-003/04; H02H-003/08; H02H-007/22

A last stage of the compressor is a high-speed radial flow rotor A low-speed axial-flow rotor and the high-speed radial flow rotor adopts a gear structure for increasing speed. A rotating speed ratio of the high-speed radial flow rotor and the low-speed axial-flow rotor. A side of the high-speed radial flow rotor is a compressor motor (11). The compressor motor is a high-speed permanent-magnet motor. The compressor motor is directly connected with the high-speed radial flow rotor through a coupler.

PN CN114002343-A
TI Method for measuring concentration of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) in a gas phase electrochemical reactor, involves measuring target conversion concentration of carbon dioxide, and obtaining carbon concentration of VOCs in sample gas to be detected.
AU YAN F
   LIU Y
   YAN Y
   LIU D
AE SHENZHEN PURUI MEITAI ENVIRONMENTAL (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202225918C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for measuring the concentration of volatile organic compounds (VOCs), involves (a) starting the sampling device, introducing VOCs in the to-be-tested sample gas into the gas-phase electrochemical reactor, performing gas phase electrochemical oxidation, converting the gas phase to carbon dioxide, (b) measuring the target conversion concentration of carbon dioxide to obtain the carbon concentration of the VOC in the sample gas to be detected.
   USE - Method for measuring concentration of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) in a gas phase electrochemical reactor. Uses include but are not limited to a gas chromatography-the ionization detector (G-FID), gas phase chromatography (GPC) detector, hydrogen flame ionization (HFID) detector and a hydrogen-phase chromatography detector.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas phase electrochemical reactor does not need to use high purity hydrogen when using, so it does not relate to the problem of inflammable gas safety so as to eliminate the risk of safety the concentration of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) in the process, and is simple and easy to operate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) concentration measuring device of volatile organic compounds (VOCs); and
   (2) computer-readable storage medium comprising a set of instructions for measuring the concentration of volatile organic compounds (VOCs).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for measuring concentration of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) in a gas phase electrochemical reactor (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN WO2021198782-A1; CN115210203-A
TI Producing ethylene glycol from carbon dioxide used            for producing polyethylene terephthalate by reducing            carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (CO), reacting CO            with amine and reducing oxamide, oxamate or oxalate to            form ethylene glycol.
AU ALLAH T N
AE FAIRBRICS SAS (FAIR-Non-standard)
GA 2021B5591C
AB    NOVELTY - An ethylene glycol is produced from carbon                dioxide (CO2) by (i) reducing CO2 to carbon                monoxide (CO); (ii) reacting the CO with an amine                to form an oxamide or oxamate or with alcohol to                form an oxalate; and (iii) reducing the oxamide,                oxamate or oxalate to form ethylene glycol.
   USE - Production of ethylene glycol used for                producing polyethylene terephthalate                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The reaction conditions of step (ii) may                influence the amount of solid formed during the                reaction. Under specific reactions conditions, the                amount of solid formed in step (ii) is greatly                reduced. Reducing solid formation in step (ii)                increases the yield of the reaction. Solid                deposition may reduce the heat transfer                coefficient, reducing the temperature of reaction                solution and conversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) production of oxamide, oxamate or oxalate                which involves reacting CO with an amine to form an                oxamide or an oxamate or with an alcohol to form an                oxalate underflow conditions;
   (2) production of ethylene glycol which                involves reacting CO with an amine to form an                oxamide or an oxamate or with an alcohol to form an                oxalate underflow conditions; and reducing the                oxamide, oxamate or oxalate to form ethylene                glycol; and
   (3) production of polyethylene terephthalate                which involves (a) producing ethylene glycol; and                (b) polymerizing the ethylene glycol produced in                step (a) with terephthalic acid or a terephthalate                di-ester to produce polyethylene                terephthalate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ligand which is                  bound to the support comprises a phosphorus atom                  which is bound to metal center and/or the metal                  center is palladium and/or the support comprises                  silica, alumina, transition metal oxide, a                  polymer, and/or carbon. The reaction of step (ii)                  is performed in the presence of a heterogeneous                  catalyst, preferably where the heterogeneous                  catalyst comprises a metal supported on a solid                  support. The metal comprises Group VIII to XI                  metal and/or the support comprises silica,                  alumina, a transition metal oxide, a polymer,                  and/or carbon. The molar ratio of CO in the                  atmosphere to amine or alcohol is 2:1-12:1,                  preferably 4:1-9:1. The promotor is present in an                  amount of 0.005-0.075 molar equivalents,                  preferably 0.02-0.03 molar equivalents of amine                  or alcohol. The catalyst used in the catalytic                  hydrogenation of step (iii) comprises greater                  than or equal to 1 metals from Groups VIII to XI,                  preferably a metal selected from silver, iron,                  ruthenium, rhodium, nickel, palladium, platinum                  and/or copper.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The CO produced is                  introduced directly into step (ii), and/or the                  oxamide, oxamate or oxalate produced in step (ii)                  is introduced directly into step (iii). Step (ii)                  is performed under flow conditions, preferably                  where steps (i) and (ii) or steps (ii) and (iii)                  are performed underflow conditions, more                  preferably steps (i), (ii) and (iii) are                  performed underflow conditions. Reduction in step                  (i) is electrochemical reduction, optionally                  where the electrochemical reduction in step (i)                  is carried out in the presence of a porphyrin                  and/or a phthalocyanine catalyst, preferably a                  CoPc2 catalyst of formula (I) or FeTPP catalyst                  of formula (II). Step (i) is carried out in the                  presence of water and water is reduced to produce                  hydrogen gas (H2), and where the H2 is recycled                  for use in step (iii). The CO produced in step                  (i) is purified prior to the reaction in step                  (ii). The reaction of step (ii) is performed in                  the presence of a supported homogeneous catalyst,                  preferably where the supported homogeneous                  catalyst comprises a support and a metal centre                  coordinated to greater than or equal to 1                  ligands, where the metal center is bound to the                  support via greater than or equal to 1 of the                  ligands. The reaction of step (ii) is performed                  in the presence of a homogeneous catalyst,                  preferably where the homogeneous catalyst                  comprises a metal center coordinated to greater                  than or equal to 1 ligands. In step (ii), the                  supported homogeneous or heterogeneous catalyst                  is separated from the reaction mixture by                  filtration when the reaction is complete. Step                  (ii) is performed under an atmosphere comprising                  CO and oxygen gas (O2) in a molar ratio of                  1:3-6:1, preferably 3:1-5:1. Step (ii) is                  performed at a pressure of 0.1-10 MPa, preferably                  2-5 MPa. Step (ii) is performed at 25-150 degrees                  C, preferably 40-100 degrees C. Step (ii) is                  carried out in the presence of a solvent system,                  preferably where the solvent system comprises                  tetrahydrofuran (THF), toluene, acetonitrile or                  dioxane. Step (ii) is carried out in the presence                  of a promotor, preferably the promotor comprises                  iodine, an iodide derivative, and/or ammonium                  salt. Step (ii) is performed under greater than                  or equal to 1 of conditions: in the presence of                  0.00001-0.05 molar equivalents of catalyst with                  respect to the amine or alcohol; under an                  atmosphere comprising CO and O2 in a molar ratio                  of 3.5:1-4.5:1; at a pressure of 2-6 MPa; at a                  temperature of 25-100 degrees C; in the presence                  of 100-900 mL solvent system per mole of amine or                  alcohol; in the presence of 0.02-0.03 molar                  equivalents promotor with respect to the amine or                  alcohol; and the reaction is performed for 6-25                  hours if the reaction is performed under batch                  conditions, or the residence time is from 15-35                  minutes if the reaction is performed underflow                  conditions, preferably where step (ii) is                  performed under all of these conditions.                  Reduction of step (iii) is catalytic                  hydrogenation. Preferred Components: The amount                  of solvent system is 50-6000 mL, preferably                  100-900 mL per mole amine or alcohol.

PN CN113209974-A
TI Copper-zinc oxide/aluminum oxide-zirconium dioxide            composite catalyst useful in reaction of preparing            methanol by carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation;            carrier of catalyst is aluminum oxide-zirconium dioxide            composite oxide.
AU LI S
   GUO Q
   HU Y
   SHEN L
AE HUAIYIN TECHNOLOGY INST (HUIA-C)
GA 202193929D
AB    NOVELTY - Copper-zinc oxide/aluminum oxide-zirconium                dioxide composite catalyst, is claimed. The carrier                of catalyst is aluminum oxide-zirconium dioxide                composite oxide with good mesoporous structure, the                active components are copper and zinc oxide, and                total loading amount is 10-20%, the molar ratio of                copper to zinc oxide is 1-3, and mass fraction of                zirconium dioxide in aluminum oxide-zirconium                dioxide composite oxide is 10-20 wt.%.
   USE - The composite catalyst is useful in reaction                of preparing methanol by carbon dioxide catalytic                hydrogenation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst: promotes the                dispersion of metallic copper and zinc oxide, and                improves activity and selectivity of carbon dioxide                catalytic hydrogenation to produce methanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the copper-zinc oxide/aluminum                oxide-zirconium dioxide composite catalyst                comprising (1) weighing aluminum isopropoxide and                zirconium nitrate, dissolving them in the meglumine                aqueous solution, using dilute nitric acid solution                to adjust the pH value of solution, drying to                remove water after self-assembling, calcining to                obtain white powdery mesoporous aluminum                oxide-zirconium dioxide composite oxide; and (2)                weighing copper nitrate and zinc nitrate dissolved                in absolute ethanol to prepare mixed solution,                immersing the mixed solution in the mesoporous                aluminum oxide-zirconium dioxide composite oxide                solid powder prepared in step (1), drying and                reducing to obtain copper-zinc oxide/aluminum                oxide-zirconium dioxide composite catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), mass                  of zirconium dioxide in the prepared aluminum                  oxide-zirconium dioxide composite oxide is 10-20                  wt.%. In the step (2), amount of copper nitrate                  and zinc nitrate is calculated based on the molar                  amount of copper and zinc oxide contained in it,                  molar ratio of copper to zinc oxide is 1-3. In                  the step (2), the total loading amount of copper                  and zinc oxide is 10-20%. Preferred Method: In                  the step (1), using meglumine as a pore-forming                  agent, the mass concentration of meglumine                  aqueous solution is 10-50 wt.%. In the step (1),                  using dilute nitric acid solution to adjust the                  pH of the solution to 5. In the step (1),                  calcination temperature is 600 degrees C, and                  time is 4-6 hours. In step (2), reduction                  temperature is 280-320 degrees C, the reducing                  gas is 10-20% H2/N2, and the time is 2                  hours.

TI Preparing magnetic zinc oxide-zinc ferrite            composite aerogel comprises e.g. mixing and stirring            iron source, zinc source, deionized water and absolute            ethanol, adding proton removing agent to clear mixed            solution, and pouring into mold.
AU CUI S
   HUANG S
   WU X
   TAI J
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
   JIANGSU RUIYING NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021565349
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing magnetic zinc oxide-zinc ferrite                composite aerogel comprises (1) mixing and stirring                iron source, zinc source, deionized water and                absolute ethanol at a molar ratio of                1:0.5-1:40-60:15-20 to obtain a clear mixed                solution, (2) adding proton removing agent to clear                mixed solution, mixing, stirring, and pouring into                a mold, and waiting for gel at 20-50 degrees C, (3)                allowing to stand wet gel at 20-25 degrees C for                8-12 hours, (4) aging wet gel in an oven for 3-5                days at 40-60 degrees C, (5) adding absolute                ethanol to aged gel for solvent replacement, (6)                subjecting composite wet gel after solvent                replacement to carbon dioxide supercritical drying                treatment, in which the carbon dioxide                supercritical drying method adopts carbon dioxide                gas protection, the drying temperature is 45-50                degrees C, controlling pressure of the autoclave at                10-12 MPa, and the reaction time is 12-24 hours,                and (7) subjecting aerogel to high temperature heat                treatment at 300-900 degrees C for 2-4 hours, and                cooling to obtain product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing magnetic                zinc oxide-zinc ferrite composite aerogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The aerogel: has high specific surface area,                catalytic activity site, and good application                prospect and infinite potential in photocatalytic                of degrading pollutant, and improves catalytic                efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  iron source is iron chloride, iron nitrate or                  iron acetate, and the zinc source is zinc                  chloride, zinc nitrate or zinc acetate. Preferred                  Method: In the step (1), the mixing and stirring                  time is 20-30 minutes. In the step (2), the                  proton removing agent is propylene oxide,                  ethylene oxide or epichlorohydrin, where the                  molar ratio of the iron source and the proton                  removing agent is 1:5-10. In the step (2), the                  mixing and stirring time is 2-5 minutes. In the                  step (5), the number of solvent replacement is                  6-8 times for each 6-9 hours. In the step (7),                  the high temperature heat treatment atmosphere is                  air or oxygen. In the step (7), the heating rate                  of the heat treatment is 2-4 degrees                  C/minutes.

   USE - The catalyst is useful producing low carbon alcohols by carbon dioxide conversion (claimed).

The method: can directly obtain high value-added alcohol fuels, e.g. ethanol and propanol; is economical, simple; and has very good industrial application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst comprising (a) mixing and dissolving soluble metal salt and organic ligand in water, where the organic ligand is nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compound, the molar ratio of total metal ion to organic ligand is 1:0.5-3, then adjusting the pH of mixed solution, performing hydrothermal treatment, and suction filtering the solution or evaporating the solvent to obtain solid powder, and (b) dispersing the solid powder in ethanol, oscillating ultrasonically, adding template agent while stirring, then adding ethyl orthosilicate in a drop wise manner, slowly adding alkali, heating for reacting to obtain a precipitate, then washing the precipitate, drying, and calcining under the protection of inert gas atmosphere.

The soluble metal salt comprises nitrate, sulfate, acetate, and/or chloride of copper or cobalt. The template agent comprises cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyvinyl alcohol, oleate and/or laurate. The solid content in the reaction solution is 5-50%. Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises adjusting the pH to 9-10, and performing hydrothermal reaction at 100-180 degrees C.

PN US2020224589-A1; EP3683423-A1; US11230948-B2
TI Gas turbine engine for use in commercial aircraft, has heat exchanger which comprises inlet which is connected to supercharged carbon dioxide bottoming cycle and outlet is connected to supercharged carbon dioxide bottoming cycle.
AU MCAULIFFE B T
   STAUBACH J B
   SOMANATH N
AE UNITED TECHNOLOGIES CORP (UNAC-C)
   UNITED TECHNOLOGIES CORP (UNAC-C)
   RAYTHEON TECHNOLOGIES CORP (RAYT-C)
GA 202067450M
AB    NOVELTY - The gas turbine engine has a primary flow path which fluidly connects a compressor section (110), a combustor section (120), and a turbine section (130). A heat exchanger (150) is mounted in the primary flow path downstream of the turbine section. The heat exchanger has a first inlet for receiving fluid from the primary flow path and a first outlet for expelling fluid received at the first inlet. The heat exchanger has a second inlet which is fluidly connected to a supercharged carbon dioxide (sCO2) bottoming cycle and a second outlet connected to the sCO2 bottoming cycle. The sCO2 bottoming cycle is an over expanded, recuperated Brayton cycle.
   USE - Gas turbine engine for use in commercial and military aircraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas turbine engines expel excess heat along with the turbine exhaust into the ambient atmosphere, without using the excess heat to generate additional shaft work. The multiple stages of compression with heat rejection in between creates an intercooled compression that increases the bottoming cycle efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for recovering work from waste heat in gas turbine engine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the gas turbine engine including over expanding, recuperated supercritical bottoming CO2 cycle.
   Compressor section (110)
   Combustor section (120)
   Turbine section (130)
   Exhaust casing (140)
   Heat exchanger (150)

GA 202036699P
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst is prepared by modifying zinc oxide                by doping indium(III) oxide by wet chemical                precipitation method, and forming bimetallic oxide                solid solution with rich oxygen vacancies with                indium(III) oxide as solute component and zinc                oxide as solvent component.
   USE - Catalyst for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to                methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high selectivity in the                process of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to                methanol, with high conversion rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A catalyst is prepared by modifying zinc oxide                by doping indium(III) oxide by wet chemical                precipitation method, and forming bimetallic oxide                solid solution with rich oxygen vacancies with                indium(III) oxide as solute component and zinc                oxide as solvent component. The catalyst is indium                zinc oxide of formula: InZnOx. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is included for preparation of catalyst, which                involves mixing indium nitrate monohydrate and zinc                nitrate hexahydrate to obtain 0.1-0.5 mol/L indium                zinc mixed solution, adding 0.1-0.5 mol/L ammonium                carbonate to indium zinc mixed solution, stirring                at 50-90 degrees C until mixture pH reaches 6-8,                aging for 2 hours, drying at 105 degrees C for 10                hours, and calcining at 400-600 degrees C for 6                hours, where mass ratio of amount of indium to                total amount of amount of indium and zinc is                5-20%.

PT P
PN CN109850922-A
TI Utilizing sodium soda desulfurization ash comprises e. g. dissolving and filtering baking soda ash to obtain solution, reacting solution with quicklime or lime milk and introducing gas containing carbon dioxide and oxygen to obtain slurry.
AU LI L
   LI Y
   YANG Z
   ZHAN X
   WU S
   LI M
AE DEC DONGFANG BOILER GROUP CO LTD (DFEC-C)
GA 201953354S
AB    NOVELTY - Utilizing sodium soda desulfurization ash comprises (i) dissolving and filtering the baking soda ash to obtain solution; (ii) reacting the solution with quicklime or lime milk and introducing gas containing carbon dioxide and oxygen to obtain slurry; (iii) performing solid-liquid separation of the slurry to obtain supernatant and by-product calcium sulfate or gypsum; and (iv) drying the baking soda crystal obtained by crystallizing the supernatant to obtain baking soda product, where the baking soda product has purity higher than 99%.
   USE - The method is useful for utilizing sodium soda desulfurization ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces bicarbonate desulfurization operation cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a sodium soda desulfurization ash utilizing device, comprising a dissolution unit, a filtration unit, an aeration reaction unit, a swirl unit, a crystallization unit, and a drying unit that are sequentially connected through a pipe, the solution obtained by dissolving and filtering the baking soda ash is introduced into the aeration reaction unit to react with the quicklime or lime milk and the introduced gas containing carbon dioxide and oxygen to obtain a slurry, the supernatant obtained by separating the slurry through a cyclone unit is crystallized by a crystallization unit to obtain baking soda crystals and the baking soda crystals are dried by a drying unit to obtain a baking soda product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the baking soda desulfurization ash is dissolved by using the process water or the crystal poor liquid separated by crystallization of the supernatant, 100-150 kg baking soda desulfurization ash is added per ton of process water, adding 40-50 kg baking soda desulfurization ash per ton of crystalline lean liquid and the dissolution temperature is 30-60 degrees C, preferably 35-40 degrees C. The quartz sand filter or anthracite filter used to filter, the filtration rate is 8-10 m/hour, preferably 9 m/hour, the filter layer has thickness of 0.8-1.5 meters, preferably 1-1.2 meters and D10 is 0.9-1.2. In the step (ii), controlling the mass ratio of quicklime or lime milk to baking soda desulfurization ash is 0.35:1-0.45:1, preferably 0.4:1-0.42:1, the gas containing carbon dioxide and oxygen is purified flue gas or hot air, the gas containing carbon dioxide and oxygen has temperature of 50-60 degrees C and reaction time of 0.5-1 hour, and the discharge temperature of the control slurry is not greater than 60 degrees C. In the step (iv), the baking soda added at the initial crystallization, and the crystallization temperature is 20-30 degrees C, the cooling crystallization medium is water or salt water, and the crystallization time is not less than 3 hours, the crystal growth time is not less than 6 hours, and the crystal size of the small soda crystal is greater than 0.5 mm. In the step (iv), the drying medium is purified flue gas or hot air, and the temperature of the drying medium is 70-80 degrees C, the drying time is not less than 20 minutes, and the discharge temperature of the control baking soda product does not exceed 60 degrees C, the drying medium after the heat exchange is cooled is introduced into the solution as gas containing carbon dioxide and oxygen to carry out reaction.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: A first stirring unit is disposed in the dissolving unit, and the dissolving unit is further connected to the crystallization unit through a crystallization lean liquid return pipe, the filter unit is provided with a filter layer of 0.8-1.5 m and the filter material of the filter layer is a quartz sand filter or anthracite filter. A second agitation unit and an aeration tube are disposed in the aeration reaction unit. The aeration tube is connected to the drying unit through a drying medium return pipe and a fan. The swirling unit is a suspension separator of 2-3 stages in series. The crystallization unit is an indirect heat exchange cooling crystallizer and is internally provided with a crystallizing zone, and the cooling medium is water or salt water and the drying unit is a dryer of normal pressure or micro-positive pressure, and the drying medium is for purifying flue gas or hot air.

AU ZHANG H
   ZHANG Z
   HU J
   XIN P
   ZHANG P
   BAI J
   LAI H
AE UNIV NORTHWEST A & F (UNAF-C)
   UNIV NORTHWEST A & F (UNAF-C)
GA 201880324Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using BP algorithm to establish the photosynthetic rate prediction model, and using improved fish swarm algorithm to optimize the photosynthetic rate model, according to the influence of changes of light, carbon dioxide and temperature on the photosynthetic rate of plants, the environmental factors of temperature, illumination and carbon dioxide concentration. The light saturation point is found under different temperature and carbon dioxide conditions by the optimization algorithm. The optical environment control model based on the random forest algorithm is established. The Raspberry Pi system framework and platform system is built to implement model migration. The basic parameters of control object are selected through the parameter selection interface, and the monitoring node real-time feedback environment condition is directly called and saved. The LED fill light node is controlled to perform dynamic and precise control according to actual light environment.

PN CN107794023-A
TI Thickener used for dry fracturing of supercritical            carbon dioxide, contains toluene diisocyanate,            polyethylene glycol, methyl acrylate, methacrylic acid,            styrene, vinyl benzoic acid, surfactant, chain extender            and catalyst.
AU ZHANG Q
AE PANJIN BAILI CHEM CO LTD (PANJ-Non-standard)
GA 201822161E
AB    NOVELTY - A thickener comprises 20-30 pts. wt. toluene                diisocyanate, 10-15 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol,                15-20 pts. wt. methyl acrylate, 15-20 pts. wt.                methacrylic acid, 5-10 pts. wt. styrene, 10-15 pts.                wt. vinyl benzoic acid, 3-5 pts. wt. surfactant,                5-8 pts. wt. chain extender, 1-2 pts. wt. catalyst,                1-2 pts. wt. initiator and 40-50 pts. wt. castor                oil.
   USE - Thickener used for dry fracturing of                supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The thickener with high efficiency is prepared                by simple, economical and environmentally-friendly                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation method of thickener used for dry                fracturing of supercritical carbon dioxide, which                involves adding toluene diisocyanate and                polyethylene glycol to reactor, heating with                stirring at 200-300 rpm at 80-90 degrees C under                inert gas atmosphere, adding half the amount of                catalyst, reacting for 1-2 hours, adding chain                extender, continuing reaction for 1-2 hours, adding                castor oil, cooling to 20-30 degrees C, adding                surfactant, stirring for 5-10 minutes, adding                styrene, vinyl benzoic acid, heating at 80-90                degrees C, adding initiator, distilling, refluxing                for 2-3 hours, adding methyl acrylate, mixing                methacrylic acid and remaining catalyst and vacuum                distilling, heating at 100-110 degrees C and                controlling vacuum degree at 0.05-0.08 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant is                  sorbitol polyoxyethylene ether. The catalyst is                  dibutyltin dilaurate. The chain extender is                  trimethylolpropane. The initiator is                  azobisisoheptanonitrile.

The seed medium includes 10 g peptone, 5 g beef cream, 5 g sodium chloride is added to 1000 ml distilled water, to obtain liquid medium or, adding 20 g agar, to obtain solid culture. In the step (i) culturing strains on seed medium at 30 degrees C for 15 hours. In step (ii) adjusting pH of filtered high ammonia wastewater is 6-8.

GA 2022B5045T
AB    NOVELTY - Improving recovery ratio by using drilling                waste liquid comprises (1) injecting the isolating                liquid, (2) injecting the waste liquid containing                well drilling back to the injection fluid, (3)                injecting the isolation liquid, and then injecting                carbon dioxide gas (CO2), and (4) injecting                water.
   USE - The method is useful for improving recovery                ratio by using drilling waste liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: injects the diluted drilling waste                fluid, silicate aqueous solution, suspending agent,                suspended fluid of dispersant and pre/post                injection of CO2; forms silicate gels and                solidifies the drilling waste fluid for treating                drilling waste fluid and improves oil                recovery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method includes (1)                  injecting isolation fluid, (2) injecting the                  re-injection fluid containing drilling waste                  fluid, and (3) injecting spacer fluid, and then                  injecting carbon dioxide gas when the injected                  oil reservoir is a carbon dioxide gas drive oil                  reservoir, and there is more than 0.00 1PV of                  carbon dioxide gas in the oil reservoir before                  injection. The method comprises (1) injecting the                  drilling waste liquid back-injecting fluid, and                  (2) injecting the isolation liquid, and then                  injecting carbon dioxide gas when the injected                  oil reservoir is CO2 gas-driven reservoir, and                  the CO2 gas does not exist in the reservoir                  before injection. The drilling                  waste liquid comprises 6-20 wt.% silicate aqueous                  solution, 0.01-1 wt.% suspending agent, 0.02-2                  wt.% dispersant, and remaining amount of diluted                  drilling waste fluid. The concentration of the                  silicate aqueous solution is 30-50 wt.%. The                  modulus of the silicate used to configure the                  silicate aqueous solution is &#8805; 1,                  preferably 3-3.5. The silicate used in preparing                  the silicate aqueous solution comprises sodium                  silicate, potassium silicate and/or lithium                  silicate. The suspending agent comprises                  partially hydrolyzed polyacrylamide, and modified                  cellulose, preferably xanthan gum. The dispersant                  is sodium humate and/or sodium lignin sulfonate.                  The diluted drilling waste liquid is the drilling                  waste fluid diluted with water, whose solid phase                  content of is 2-20%, preferably 8-12%.

AU TAN T
   YANG C
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2022186876
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing self-assembled core-shell structure nanoparticles modified perovskite oxide electrode material by in-situ precipitation process, comprises preparing perovskite oxide precursor, carrying in-situ reduction and precipitation process to obtain core-shell structure nanoparticles of iron(II) oxide-coated nickel iron alloy, and loading nanoparticles on the surface of the perovskite oxide.

   ADVANTAGE - The electrode material: can actively improves catalytic activity of cathode material for carbon dioxide; is simple to prepare; and has controllable conditions and obviously improved performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of the perovskite precursor comprises solid phase process, combustion process and sol-gel process; the in-situ reduction and precipitation process is to perform in-situ reduction and precipitation of the perovskite oxide precursor in a hydrogen (H2)-water atmosphere at 800-900degreesC for 2-5 hours; the volume content of water in the H2-water atmosphere is 1-5%. Preferred Components: The perovskite precursors include ABO3 single perovskite, An+1BnO3n+1, A2B2O6 double perovskite oxides; where, the A site is rare earth element and/or alkaline earth metal element, and the B site is a transition metal element and contains at least Ni and Fe. The perovskite precursors are (PrSr)2(NiFeMo)2O6-delta and (Pr0.5Sr0.5)0.95Ni0.2Fe0.5Ti0.3O3-delta. The preparation of perovskite precursor by the sol-gel process comprises adding the metal salt, EDTA and citric acid into the water, adding aqueous ammonia dropwise to adjust the pH value to 7-9, heating and stirring to a gel state, drying at 250-300degreesC to obtain black powder, and calcining the powder at 1000-1200degreesC for 5-10 hours in an air atmosphere to obtain the perovskite precursor; the metal salt includes ferric nitrate and nickel nitrate; the molar ratio of metal ion of metal salt, EDTA, and citric acid is 1:1-1.5:1-2; the preparation of perovskite precursor by the solid-phase process comprises the ball-milling the metal oxide or carbonate in anhydrous ethanol for 12-36 hours, and sintering at a high temperature of 1000-1200degrees C for 5-15 hours to obtain the perovskite precursor body powder.

TI Metal-anchored hollow covalent organic framework material useful for photocatalytic carbon dioxide comprises metal ions anchored inside the covalent organic framework by selective etching.
AU LI L
   DANG Q
   YU Y
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 202143460F
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-anchored hollow covalent organic framework material comprises metal ions anchored inside the covalent organic framework by selective etching.
   USE - The framework material is useful for photocatalytic carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The framework material: has imine bonds and unique hollow structure, mild preparation conditions, simple preparation and operation, microporous structure, good catalytic performance and application potential.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for synthesizing metal-anchored hollow covalent organic framework material comprising using ZIF-67, p-phenylenediamine and trialdehyde phloroglucinol in the o-dichlorobenzene/n-butanol/pyrrolidine solution system to catalyze the reaction to obtain egg yolk- MOF/COF material with eggshell structure, etching internal metal organic framework away by pickling and anchoring metal ions on covalent organic framework.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) obtaining ZIF-67@Am-TpPa by stirring and reacting ZIF-67, p-phenylenediamine and trialdehyde phloroglucinol, rotary evaporating, drying, adding to o-dichlorobenzene/n-butanol/pyrrole In the alkane solution system, allowing solvothermal reaction to produce egg yolk-egg shell ZIF-67@TpPa and (b) placing egg yolk-egg shell ZIF-67@TpPa prepared in step (a) into a 10 ml glass bottle, adding dioxane and acetic acid, etching for 1 h in acid, collecting product by centrifugation with acetone, vacuum drying powder to obtain the metal anchored hollow covalent organic framework material. The stirring reaction in step (a) is carried out at 50 degrees C for 12 hours. In the step (a), drying the solid by rotary evaporator at 50 degrees C and 70 revolutions/minute. The solvothermal reaction in step (a) is reacted at 120 degrees C for 3 days. The vacuum drying in step (b) is carried out at 60 degrees C for 24 hours. Preferred Components: In step (a), the mass ratio of ZIF-67, p-phenylenediamine and trialdehyde phloroglucinol is 6.67:3:4. In the step (a) the volume ratio of the o-dichlorobenzene/n-butanol/pyrrolidine solution is 9:1:1 and total volume is 1.65 ml.

PN US2021083062-A1; JP2021048198-A; US11329134-B2
TI Manufacture of semiconductor device e.g. metal oxide semiconductor field effect transistor, involves forming silicon oxide film on silicon carbide layer, and heating in atmosphere including nitrogen and carbon dioxide gases.
AU SHIMIZU T
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
GA 202127940P
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of semiconductor device (100) involves forming a silicon oxide film on the surface of a silicon carbide layer, and heating at 1200degrees Celsius or more in an atmosphere including nitrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Manufacture of semiconductor device e.g. metal oxide semiconductor field effect transistor and n-channel insulated gate bipolar transistor.
   ADVANTAGE - The semiconductor device having excellent reliability can be produced. Excessive oxidation of surface of silicon carbide layer can be suppressed. The leakage current of the gate insulating layer, and variation of threshold voltage can be suppressed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic cross-sectional view of semiconductor device.
   Substrate (12)
   Gate insulating layer (28)
   Gate electrode (30)
   Source electrode (34)
   Semiconductor device (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The initial heat treatment is performed at 1500degrees Celsius. The partial pressure of the carbon dioxide gas in the atmosphere is 10% or more. The partial pressure of the nitrogen gas in the atmosphere is 30% or more. The partial pressures of oxygen gas, nitrogen oxide gas, and water vapor in the atmosphere 0.01% or less. Secondary heat treatment is further is carried out in an atmosphere with an oxygen partial pressure of 0.1-2%.Preferred Property: The silicon oxide film has thickness of 30 nm or more and less than 100 nm.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); U12 (Discrete Devices); U21 (Logic Circuits, Electronic Switching and Coding); U11 (Semiconductor Materials and Processes)

PT P
PN CN112335649-A
TI Performing modified atmosphere preservation of            fresh cut flowers by placing fresh cut flowers in            modified atmosphere packing, reducing oxygen content,            raising carbon dioxide content, and storing modified            atmosphere package.
AU INVENTOR U
AE TIANJIN CNRO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202117487Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for modified atmosphere preservation of                fresh cut flowers involves (a) placing fresh cut                flowers in a modified atmosphere packing, (b)                reducing oxygen content in the modified atmosphere                packing to the first threshold range, (c) raising                carbon dioxide content in the modified atmosphere                packing to the second threshold range, and (d)                storing the modified atmosphere package at low                temperature, where the storage time is at least one                week.
   USE - The method is useful for performing modified                atmosphere preservation of fresh cut flowers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is convenient to operate, reduces                resource waste, reduces mildew, rot, water loss and                discoloration of the fresh cut flower, and prolongs                fresh-cut flower preservation cycle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The modified                  atmosphere packing comprises a modified                  atmosphere bag, a modified atmosphere box, a                  sealed bottle, or a modified atmosphere box. The                  air exchange rate of the modified atmosphere                  packing is &#8805; 0.02/day, preferably                  &#8805; 0.01/day. The modified atmosphere                  packing has a positioning shell inside which is                  configured to separately store the fresh cut                  flowers placed in the modified atmosphere                  packing. The modified atmosphere packing                  comprises an inner tray with vents and an outer                  modified atmosphere bag, or a modified atmosphere                  box, or a modified atmosphere box, or an open                  bottle on the inner layer and a modified                  atmosphere bag on the outer layer, or a modified                  atmosphere box, or a modified atmosphere box. The                  modified atmosphere packing comprises an inner                  positioning shell or a pallet with vents, a                  middle layer of a modified atmosphere bag, or a                  modified atmosphere box, or a modified atmosphere                  box, and the foam box or carton on the outer                  layer, or the bottle with the opening on the                  inner layer, the modified atmosphere bag in the                  middle layer, and the foam box or carton on the                  outer layer. The gas permeability of the modified                  atmosphere packaging is &#8804; 10 cm3/m2/24                  hours/0.1 MPa, &#8804; 3 cm3/m2/24 hours/0.1                  MPa. Preferred Method: The method further                  involves wrapping the buds of the fresh cut                  flowers, where the wrapping materials comprise                  paper and silk mesh. The first threshold range of                  oxygen content is 1-7%, preferably 2-5%. The                  second threshold range of carbon dioxide content                  is 2-10%, preferably 3-5%. The temperature range                  of low-temperature storage is 1-10&#176; C,                  preferably 2-5&#176; C. The method further                  involves inserting fresh cut flowers into clean                  water. The method further involves controlling                  the humidity in the modified atmosphere packing                  within a relative humidity of 50-80%.
DC D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN CN112190753-A; CN112190753-B
TI Preparation of antibacterial medical glue material involves mixing alkyl cyanoacrylate compound and polylactic acid, obtaining matrix, adding aerogel particles and propolis, carrying ionic liquid to process matrix, and sterilizing.
AU WANG W
   TIAN X
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG Y
   LI J
   LI X
   WANG Z
   LIANG Y
AE BEIJING FUALIE SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEV CO L (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202106876S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of antibacterial medical glue material involves mixing alpha -cyanoacrylate alkyl ester compound with polylactic acid, obtaining medical glue matrix, adding aerogel particles and propolis, mixing to gel state, placing resultant in airtight container, removing air, filling the container with carbon dioxide, controlling temperature and pressure in the airtight container to make carbon dioxide gas in a supercritical state, carrying ionic liquid to process the matrix, when the matrix is not in contact with ionic liquid, and sterilizing.
   USE - Preparation of antibacterial medical glue material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of antibacterial medical glue material having excellent adhesiveness for long period of time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alpha -alkyl cyanoacrylate alkyl ester compound includes n-butyl alpha -cyanoacrylate and n-octyl alpha -cyanoacrylate in a mass ratio of 50-60:40-50. The mass ratio of alkyl alpha -cyanoacrylate compound and polylactic acid is 60-80:20-40. The aerogel particles comprise gum arabic aerogel particles. The usage amount of the aerogel particles is 10-30 wt.% based on amount of the matrix. The usage amount of propolis is 20-30 wt.% based on amount of the matrix. The ionic liquid comprises choline chloride ionic liquid or choline bromide ionic liquid. The usage amount of ionic liquid is 10-20 wt.% based on amount of the matrix.

PN DE102019203065-A1; WO2020178330-A1
TI Production of methanol or methane, involves branching off exhaust gas stream comprising carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide occurring in plant, feeding branched off exhaust gas stream to another plant, and heating obtained exhaust gas stream.
AU SCHULZ A
AE THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
GA 202090087S
AB    NOVELTY - Production of methanol or methane involves branching off at least one exhaust gas stream comprising 20 wt.% or more carbon dioxide and less than 20 wt.% carbon monoxide occurring in a plant (p1) for producing methanol or methane, feeding the branched off exhaust gas stream to a plant (p2) which is connected to the plant (p1), and heating the exhaust gas stream in the plant (p2).
   USE - Production of methanol or methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of methanol or methane with improved combustibility of exhaust gas stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the plant for producing methanol or methane.
   Compressor (12)
   Electrolysis device (14)
   Methanol synthesis unit (22)
   High-pressure separator (24)
   Distillation device (30)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Arrangement: The plant (p1) is a plant having a capacity of less than 300 ton/day. The exhaust gas stream is obtained in a high-pressure separator (24) arranged downstream of a methanol synthesis unit (22), in which the gas stream is separated from a previously produced crude methanol. A gas stream separated in the high pressure separator is fed to a distillation device (30) in which water and/or gaseous or volatile substances are separated from the methanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The exhaust gas stream preferably comprises 40 wt.% or more carbon dioxide and less than 10 wt.% carbon monoxide. The weight ratio of carbon dioxide with respect to carbon monoxide in the exhaust gas stream is 2:1, preferably 4:1. The exhaust gas stream further comprises 20 wt.% or more, preferably 30 wt.% or more hydrogen.

PT P
PN CN111194825-A
TI Treating harmless sterol residue by subjecting fermentation residue obtained by fermenting steroid compounds to secondary fermentation culture with microorganism for completely degrading steroid compounds into carbon dioxide and water.
AU ZHANG R
   LI X
   FENG J
   LIU X
   CHEN X
   WU Q
   ZHU D
   MA Y
AE TIANJIN INST IND BIOTECHNOLOGY (TIIB-C)
GA 2020479527
AB    NOVELTY - Method for treating harmless sterol residue involves subjecting the fermentation residue obtained by fermenting steroid compounds to secondary fermentation culture with a single microorganism or multiple microorganisms for completely degrading the steroid compounds remaining in the sterilized residue after fermentation of the steroid compounds into carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - The method is useful for treating harmless sterol residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces steroid compound concentration to less than 0.0005 g/l after using microorganisms to degrade the steroid compound sludge, reduces energy consumption for wet bacteria incineration and is environmentally-friendly, and provides an effective new treatment method for the harmless and resource utilization of sterilization residual bacterial residue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The steroids produced comprises 4-androstenedione (4-AD) of formula (I), androstadienedione (ADD) of formula (II), 9 alpha -hydroxy-4-androstene-3,17-dione (9-OHAD) of formula (III), lactone of formula (IV), BA of formula (V), DBA of formula (VI) and dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) of formula (VII). Preferred Microorganisms: The microorganisms used are Mycobacterium smegmatis mc2155, Mycobacterium fortuitum 6841, Rhodococcus rhodochrous CGMCC4.1815, R.rhodochrous CGMCC4.1816, R.erythropolis 1-WY1406, Rhodococcus sp. 1-0130, Pseudomonas putida PP07 or their combinations. Preferred Method: The method involves adding M.fortuitum 6841 (ATCC 6841TM) and Rhodococcus sp. 1-0130 (CGMCC 1012281) as a single strain, which almost completely degraded steroid compounds (steroid metabolic intermediates).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The flows of air are                  directed into closed incubation chamber laterally                  of containers. The flows of air                  contain carbon dioxide content of 5-7                  vol.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109593608-A
TI Preparation of tieguanyin aroma components and            particles from gas-saturated solution microcapsule            involves crushing tieguanyin tea leaves, supercritical            carbon dioxide extracting, adding food grade paraffin,            mixing, expanding, and spraying.
AU CAI C
   WANG L
AE JIANGSU JINHUI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201934991F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of tieguanyin aroma components and                particles from gas-saturated solution microcapsule                comprises crushing tieguanyin tea leaves and                sieving; putting supercritical extraction device,                heating, introducing carbon dioxide, circulating                extracting for 2-3 hours, and extracting tieguanyin                aroma extract product; and adding food grade                paraffin as microcapsule wall material, mixing with                molten paraffin, pre-expanding, spraying through a                nozzle, and collecting microcapsules.
   USE - Method for preparig tieguanyin aroma                components and particles from gas-saturated                solution microcapsule.
   ADVANTAGE - The microcapsules meet the application                requirements of tobacco flavors and fragrances,                have high package rate of tieguanyin aroma                components and improved stability of aroma                components, controls release of aroma components,                and have no organic solvent and high safety.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The heating is done at                  40-50 degrees C and 50-60 degrees C. The                  pre-expanding is done at 60 degrees C and 10-15                  MPa.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); P15 (Tobacco (A24).)

PT P
PN PL422463-A1; PL235602-B1
TI System for feeding cultivations in warm air greenhouse from mine exhaust shaft, has switching solenoid connected with inlet pipe, and ventilation duct fitted with exhaust air intake temperature sensor and carbon dioxide level sensor.
AU KOZMIAN R
   KARLIKOWSKI S
   KUSZ A
   WAC-WLODARCZYK A
AE TOWARZYSTWO GOSPODARKI ENERGETYCZNEJ (TOWA-Non-standard)
GA 201924132U
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an exhaust shaft connected to a damper. A heater is connected to a ventilation duct between an air pre-filter and an exhaust air device. An end air filter is connected between the exhaust air device and the greenhouse object. The heater is connected to a control device through a switching solenoid. The switching solenoid is connected with an inlet pipe and an outlet pipe of the heater. The ventilation duct is fitted with an exhaust air intake temperature sensor and a carbon dioxide level sensor.
   USE - System for feeding cultivations in a warm air greenhouse from a mine exhaust shaft.
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PT P
PN CN108997474-A
TI Preparation of 28-homobrassinolide involves reacting 2,3,22,23-diepoxy-28-homobrassinolide in presence of e.g. carbon dioxide, obtaining 2,3,22,23-dicarbonyl-28-homobrassinolide, and hydrolyzing under action of alkaline or acidic reagent.
AU HUANG J
   CHENG L
   GUAN Y
   WU Y
   FANG W
   LAI L
   LI Q
AE UNIV JIANGXI AGRIC (UNJA-C)
   NANCHANG JIANGTENG PHARM CO LTD (NANC-Non-standard)
GA 2018A3765R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of 28-homobrassinolide involves reacting (2,22)-diene-24S -ethyl-5( alpha )-cholesteryl-6-one in presence of an oxidizing agent, a chiral catalyst (c1) and a solvent (a), obtaining (2( alpha ),3( alpha ),22R,23R)-2,3,22,23-diepoxy-28-homobrassinolide, reacting the obtained compound in presence of a catalyst (c2), carbon dioxide and a solvent (b), obtaining (2( alpha ),3( alpha ),22R,23R)-2,3,22,23-dicarbonyl-28-homobrassinolide, and hydrolyzing under the action of an alkaline reagent or an acidic reagent.
   USE - Preparation of 28-homobrassinolide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and environmentally-friendly industrial preparation of 28-homobrassinolide using easily available raw materials by a simple process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkaline reagent is sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, sodium phosphate, sodium hydrogen phosphate, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, potassium phosphate, potassium hydrogen phosphate, potassium dihydrogen phosphate, calcium phosphate, calcium hydrogen phosphate, calcium dihydrogen phosphate, barium phosphate, barium hydrogen phosphate, barium dihydrogen phosphate, potassium oxide, sodium oxide, calcium oxide, barium oxide, magnesium oxide, alumina, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide or barium hydroxide, preferably potassium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The solvent (a) is water, acetonitrile, formic acid, acetic acid, monochloroacetic acid, dichloroacetic acid, trichloroacetic acid, trifluoroacetic acid, propionic acid, methyl formate, ethyl formate, methyl acetate, ethyl acetate, methyl propionate, ethyl propionate, benzene, chlorobenzene, dichloromethane, chloroform, carbon tetrachloride, ether, n-propyl ether, diisopropyl ether, n-butyl ether, tert-butyl ether, neopentyl ether, anisole, diphenyl ether, alpha -naphthylmethyl ether, beta -naphthylmethyl ether, ethylene oxide, tetrahydrofuran or 1,4-dioxane, preferably water. The catalyst (c1) is chiral fructose. The acidic reagent is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid, formic acid, acetic acid, monochloroacetic acid, dichloroacetic acid, trichloroacetic acid, trifluoroacetic acid, propionic acid, butyric acid, valeric acid, caproic acid, heptanoic acid, octanoic acid, citric acid, citric acid, palmitic acid, stearic acid, oxalic acid, malonic acid, succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid, pimelic acid, suberic acid, azelaic acid, sebacic acid, maleic acid, fumaric acid, palmitic acid, acrylic acid, benzoic acid, alpha -naphthylacetic acid, beta -naphthylacetic acid, substituted benzoic acid on phenyl ring, phenylacetic acid optionally substituted on benzene ring, benzenesulfonic acid optionally substituted on benzene ring, phthalic acid, alpha -naphthoic acid, beta -naphthoic acid, alpha -naphthoic acid substituted on naphthalene ring, beta -naphthoic acid substituted on naphthalene ring, alpha -naphthylacetic acid substituted on naphthalene ring or beta -naphthylacetic acid substituted on naphthalene ring, preferably dilute sulfuric acid. The catalyst (c2) is tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium chloride, tetrabutylammonium hydrogen sulfate, benzyltrimethylammonium bromide, benzyltrimethylammonium chloride, benzyltriethylammonium chloride, benzyltriethylammonium bromide, phenyltrimethylammonium bromide or phenyltrimethylammonium chloride, or one of methyltrioctylammonium bromide, methyltrioctylammonium chloride, trimethyldodecylammonium bromide, trimethyldodecylammonium chloride, trimethyltetradecylammonium bromide and trimethyltetradecylammonium chloride, preferably tetrabutylammonium bromide. PT P
PN TW201838917-A; TW670229-B1
TI Method for separating calcium sulfate and calcium carbonate from desulfurized gypsum waste generated in flue gas desulfurization process.
AU HONG R
   LU Z
   LIU M
   CHEN D
AE HONG R (HONG-Individual)
GA 201911288N
AB    NOVELTY - The present invention relates to a method for separating calcium sulfate and calcium carbonate from desulfurized gypsum waste (also known as by-product lime) containing calcium oxide, calcium carbonate and calcium sulfate generated in a flue gas desulfurization process. The method of the present invention comprises the steps of pulverizing the waste and mixing it with water to form a suspension, enabling the suspension to react with carbon dioxide and then heating the same in stages, thereby separating the recyclable and reusable calcium sulfate and calcium carbonate from the waste. Moreover, the pH value of the suspension in the step of pulverizing and mixing is 8.0 to 12.0, and the suspension is reacted with carbon dioxide until the pH value of the suspension is 5.5 to 6.0.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

TI Oral composition used to e.g. treat human alcohol addiction or alcoholic impulse control disorder, comprises enteric resistant active agent for generating carbon dioxide gas expulsion from stomach through esophagus.
AU KRIWIN P
   POWIS DE TENBOSSCHE R
AE KRIWIN P (KRIW-Individual)
   POWIS DE TENBOSSCHE R (DTEN-Individual)
GA 201855476C
AB    NOVELTY - Oral composition (C1) comprises enteric resistant active agent for generating for at least 12 hours, preferably for at least 7 days, a carbon dioxide gas expulsion from the stomach through the esophagus.
   USE - The composition is useful for treating human alcohol addiction or an alcoholic impulse control disorder, or reducing the likelihood of relapse use of alcohol or practice of a behavior associated with an alcoholic impulse control disorder (claimed). No biological data given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for oral composition (C2) comprising a growth medium appropriate for aldehyde producing bacteria, preferably for aldehyde producing bacteria naturally present in the stomach, where growth media comprises a gelled phase.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition (C1) comprises 100 mg up to 5 g of enteric resistant active agent. The composition (C1) comprises: aldehyde producing bacteria and/or with a growth medium appropriate for aldehyde producing bacteria, preferably for aldehyde producing bacteria naturally present in the stomach, where the growth media comprises a gelled phase; and an enteric degradable dosage form comprising loose particles of enteric resistant active agent or bound particles suitable to be converted into loose particles in the stomach. The composition (C1) comprises or is associated to: a growth medium for aldehyde producing bacteria, preferably Acetobacter aceti naturally present in the stomach; or an effective dose of an acid working aldehyde producing bacteria, preferably Acetobacter aceti bacteria. The composition (C1) further comprises: an additional therapeutic agent, preferably an antidepressant; and glycine betaine. The composition (C2) comprises a carrier, preferably a gas retaining/releasing means associated to/with a coating comprising a gelling agent and ethanol, preferably comprising a sugar gelling agent and ethanol. Preferred Components: The enteric resistant active agent is suitable for: generating for at least 12 hours, preferably for at least 7 days, a carbon dioxide gas bubbling within the stomach; and lowering the carbon dioxide gas stomach content for at least 12 hours, preferably for at least 3 days. The enteric resistant active agent is formed by active flake particles having a density higher than 1, a particle surface of 1-50 mm2, preferably 1-20 mm2, and gas retaining/releasing means selected from pits, cavities and open pores. The flake particles have a thickness of less than 2 mm, preferably 10-500 mu m. The enteric resistant active agent is a gas retaining/releasing means adapted for collecting carbon dioxide gases dissolved within the enteric medium. The gas retaining/releasing means is adapted for: collecting carbon dioxide gases dissolved within the enteric medium, whereby lowering its apparent density below the enteric medium density; collecting carbon dioxide gases dissolved within the enteric medium, and for generating a carbon dioxide bubbling within the enteric medium. The gas retaining/releasing means is associated with a coating comprising a gelling agent and ethanol, preferably comprising a sugar gelling agent and ethanol. The growth medium comprises ethanol, and a gelification or gelling agent, preferably a sugar based gelling agent, preferably comprising galactose unit including agar-agar. The enteric resistant active agent is for accelerating the oxidation of alcohol in the stomach, and/or the formation of acetic acid in the stomach. The enteric resistant active agent is of natural origin.
   ACTIVITY - Antialcoholic; Antiaddictive.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN108157970-A
TI Preparation of silkworm pupa compound oil microcapsule comprises supercritical carbon dioxide extracting silkworm pupa using ethanol solution, collecting raffinate, gradient freezing, crystallizing, and microcapsule processing.
AU DONG S
AE HAIYAN LINGTE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HAIY-Non-standard)
GA 201848571V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of silkworm pupa compound oil microcapsule comprises supercritical carbon dioxide extracting silkworm pupa using entrainer of ethanol solution containing 5-10 wt.% ethyl acetate and 0.01-0.2 wt.% ofloxacin, collecting raffinate to obtain primary refined oil, gradient freezing, crystallizing to prepare high-quality silkworm pupa oil, and microcapsule processing.
   USE - Method for preparing silkworm pupa compound oil microcapsule.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple manufacturing technique and high automation and quickly extracts very high content of alpha -linolenic acid. The extracted composite oil has a natural fragrance and less residual organic solvent, is stable in nature, effectively prevents and treats hyperlipidemia, promotes liver cell regeneration, and prevents and treats non-alcoholic fatty liver.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical extraction is done at 35-40 degrees C and 30-40 MPa for 0.6-1 hour and separating at 10-12 MPa and 40-45 degrees C and 5-7 MPa and 15-20 degrees C. The gradient freezing and crystallizing are done by adding 20-30 wt.% carbamide in ethanol solution to refined oil at mass ratio of 1:7-12, mixing, gradient freezing, crystallizing, filtering, gradient freezing, filtering, acidifying with hydrochloric acid until pH value is 2-3, extracting with petroleum ether, separating petroleum ether phase, washing with distilled water to neutral, drying, vacuum distilling, and recycling petroleum ether. The gradient freeze-drying is done at 8-10 degrees C, for 2.5-3 hours, 4-5 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours, 1-1.5 degrees C for 0.8-1 hour, - 2 degrees C to - 1.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hour, - 4 degrees C to - 3.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hour, - 6 degrees C to - 5.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hour, - 8 degrees C to - 7.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hour and - 12 degrees C to - 11.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hour. The microcapsule processing is done by adding silk pupa oil with 0.5-0.8 times medium chain triglyceride to and 7-10 times Cremophor EL 35 (RTM: polyethylene glycol-35 castor oil), ultrasonically mixing at 15-30 kHz and 150-200 W for 5-10 minutes, adding 0.1-0.4 times absolute ethanol, and magnetically stirring. Preferred Components: The ofloxacin comprises 80-90 wt.% levofloxacin and 10-20 wt.% dexovafloxacin.
   ACTIVITY - Antilipemic; Antiinflammatory; Hepatotropic.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN108117074-A; CN108117074-B
TI Preparing super activated carbon involves contacting carbonaceous material with oxidizing agent and hydrated alkaline activator under vacuum to activate oxidizing agent and hydrated alkali, immersing agent in carbonaceous material.
AU PAN J
   CHENG J
   YANG Y
   SUN Y
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 201845506A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing super activated carbon involves contacting carbonaceous material with oxidizing agent and hydrated alkaline activator under vacuum to activate the oxidizing agent and hydrated alkali.The agent is immersed in the carbonaceous material, carbonaceous material immersed in the oxidizing agent and the hydrated alkaline activator which is subjected to a first heat treatment to activate the carbonaceous material and obtain carbon dioxide gas. The obtained activated carbonaceous material is cooled, washed and dried to obtain super activated carbon product and carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Method for preparing super activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare super activated carbon in environment-friendly manner which improves the yield of the product, recycles the waste byproduct, saves energy and has obvious social, environmental and economic benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbonaceous material is contacted with the oxidizing agent and the hydrated alkaline activator by subjecting the carbonaceous material to the oxidizing agent under vacuum conditions, contacting to immerse the oxidant in the carbonaceous material, and then subjecting the carbonaceous material immersed in the oxidant to heat treatment to perform first activation on the carbonaceous material and to obtain carbon dioxide gas. 0.01 to 4.5 pts. wt. oxidizing agent is added and treated at 300 to 990 degrees C for 5 to 60 minutes and at (-0.001) to (-0.099) megaPascal. The oxidizing agent is selected from ozone, oxygen, hydrogen peroxide, sodium peroxide, ammonium persulfate, potassium persulfate, potassium permanganate, potassium perchlorate, preferably sodium chlorate, nitric acid, sodium nitrate, sodium nitrite, potassium nitrate, lithium nitrate and sodium chromate. The first activated carbonaceous material is hydrated under vacuum, activated with alkaline activator into the first activated carbonaceous material, and then immersed the first hydrated alkaline activator in an inert gas atmosphere or a reducing gas atmosphere. The activated carbonaceous material is subjected to a first heat treatment, 40-650 pts. wt. hydrated alkaline activator is added and treated at 80-300 degrees C for 0.5-180 minutes. The hydrated alkaline activator comprises hydrated sodium hydroxide, the hydrated sodium hydroxide being selected from at least one of sodium hydroxide hydrate, sodium hydroxide dihydrate and sodium hydroxide tetrahydrate. The content of the hydrated sodium hydroxide is 50 to 99 wt.%. The hydrated alkaline activator further contains co-activator selected from the group consisting of potassium hydroxide, lithium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide. The amount of auxiliary activator is 0.1 to 35 wt.%. The carbonaceous material is contacted with the oxidant, and carbon dioxide gas. The carbonaceous material having a water content of 0.5 to 80 wt.%. The reaction is carried out at 300 to 900 degrees C for 5 to 100 minutes. The washing is performed by adding water to the activated carbonaceous material for the first Dissolving and leaching, and performing solid-liquid separation to obtain activated mother liquor and solid phase, where amount of water is such that the remaining hydrated alkaline activator is selectively dissolved and leached at 10-350 degrees C, replenished the activated mother liquor with alkali activator to obtain hydrated alkaline activator. The recycling is performed by adding water and carbon dioxide to the solid phase for second dissolution leaching, and solid-liquid separation is performed to obtain carbonate mother liquid and activated carbon slurry. The carbonate crystals are not precipitated in the system, and leached at 10-150 degrees C. The activated carbon slurry is added to a multistage water washing and acidification treatment. The acid used in the acidification treatment is selected from the group consisting of sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, formic acid, acetic acid, perchloric acid, fluorosilicic acid, phosphoric acid, and hydrofluoric acid. The carbonate mother liquor is passed through carbon dioxide and subjected to solid-liquid separation, and the first alkaline pH value enables the by-product hydrated silica and carbonate mother liquor. The salt crystals are precipitated, carbon dioxide is introduced into the carbonate mother liquid at second alkaline pH value and subjected to freeze treatment, and then solid-liquid separation is carried out to obtain by-product carbonate crystals and crystallization mother liquid. The second alkaline pH value does not cause precipitation of bicarbonate crystals in the system; preferably, temperature of the freezing treatment is (-15) degrees C-25 degrees C and time is 1-300 minutes. The carbon dioxide is introduced into the crystallization mother liquid, and solid-liquid separation is performed to obtain a by-product bicarbonate crystal and a crystallization mother liquid. The crystallization mother liquid is added, and water and carbon dioxide are added to the solid. The third pH value causes the water and carbon dioxide content in the obtained crystallization mother liquid to meet the demand. Preferred Condition: The drying is performed at 150-500 degrees C, preferably 400-1500 degrees C for 10-300 minutes, preferably 10-500 minutes. Preferred Composition: The carbonaceous material is selected from the group consisting of shell carbon, petroleum coke, and coke. The shell carbon is selected from coconut shell charcoal, apricot shell charcoal, gluten charcoal, chestnut charcoal, peach shell charcoal and rapeseed shell charcoal. The carbonaceous material has a particle size of 20-100 mesh, and amount is 3 wt.%, preferably less than 0.5 wt.%, more preferably 0.3 wt.%. The first alkaline pH is greater than a second alkaline pH, and the second alkaline pH is greater than third pH, preferably, first pH value is 12.2-13.9, second alkaline pH is 10.5-12.1, third pH is 3.9-10.4, more preferably, first alkaline pH is 12.5-13.3. The second alkaline pH is 10.6-11.3 and third pH is 4.5-8.2.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC E11-Q01C; E11-W; E31-N03C

The heating is done at 5-140 degrees C. The stripping operation is carried out at 20-100 degrees C. The hydrolysis is done for 0.5-3 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The reactor material is zirconium alloy, titanium alloy or nickel alloy. The material of reactor contains 16-22 wt.% chromium, 16-22 wt.% molybdenum, and 1-2 wt.% tantalum and zirconium.

TI Preparing chromium n-heptanoate by adding n-heptanoic acid, chromium oxide, initiator and water to reaction kettle, stirring, heating and reacting to make carbon dioxide overflow, and placing granular product in drying sieves.
AU ZAHNG F
AE TIANJIN CHEM REAGENT RES INST (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018238991
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of chromium n-heptanoate involves: (1) adding n-heptanoic acid, chromium oxide, an initiator and water to a reaction kettle, stirring, heating and reacting to make carbon dioxide overflow to generate the product; and (2) placing the granular product obtained by crystallization and separation process in an up-and-down air-permeable drying sieves, drying and sieving to obtain the finished product.
