   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for preparing ultra-thin Bi4O5Br2 photocatalyst,                which involves dissolving 5.1 mmol bismuth                containing compound in glycerol under stirring                condition, dissolving 2 mmol bromine containing                compound in the glycerol, dropwise adding glycerol                solution containing bromine element into glycerol                solution containing bismuth element in a ratio of                2.55:1, stirring and reacting solution at high                temperature and pressure in a reactor, drying                reactant, dissolving dried product in distilled                water, and hydrolyzing solution using water                bath.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bismuth containing                  compound is bismuth nitrate pentahydrate, and the                  bromine containing compound is hexadecyl ammonium                  bromide. Preferred Method: The method involves                  dissolving 5.1 mmol bismuth containing compound                  in 30-50 ml, preferably 40 ml glycerol, stirring                  solution for 20-100 minutes, preferably 60                  minutes, dissolving 2 mmol bromine containing                  compound in the glycerol, dropwise adding                  glycerol solution containing bromine element into                  glycerol solution containing bismuth element in a                  ratio of 2.55:1, stirring and reacting solution                  at 120-200 degrees c, preferably 160 degrees c                  for 14-18 hours, preferably 16 hours in an oven,                  drying reactant at 60-100 degrees C, preferably                  80 degrees C for 8-18 hours, preferably 12 hours                  in oven, dissolving 0.2-0.6 g, preferably 0.4 g                  dried product in 100-500 ml, preferably 300 ml                  distilled water, and hydrolyzing solution at                  40-70 degrees C, preferably 50 degrees C for                  21-26 hours, preferably 24 hours.

PN CN114343023-A
TI Pepper oil with cooked flavor useful in preparing food flavoring agent, prepared by e.g. mixing pepper oil resin and pepper extract oil, the pepper oil resin is extracted by carrying out supercritical treatment on pepper with carbon dioxide as fluid.
AU WU Y
   GAO W
   WEI Z
   QI L
   ZHANG J
AE CHENGUANG BIOTECH GROUP CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202259309P
AB    NOVELTY - Pepper oil with cooked flavor, prepared by mixing pepper oil resin and pepper extract oil in a mass ratio of 1:0.5-1.5. The pepper oil resin is extracted by carrying out supercritical treatment on pepper with carbon dioxide as fluid at 35-55degrees Celsius control temperature, 15-25 MPa extraction pressure, and extraction time of 4-6 hours. The pepper extract oil is obtained by extracting the pepper material by vegetable oil.
   USE - The pepper oil with cooked flavor is useful in preparing food flavoring agent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and convenient, and can be widely promoted in industrialization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The extraction of pepper extract oil comprises extracting the pepper material with vegetable oil as extracting solvent at 100-160degrees Celsius for 8-15 minutes, then reducing the temperature of extracting system to 70-80degrees Celsius in 5-40 minutes, and separating and removing the solid materials. In extraction of pepper extract oil, the mass volume ratio between pepper material and vegetable oil is 1:3-6; and the stirring is carried out at stirring speed of 20-60 revolutions/minute. In method, before preparing the pepper extraction oil, the pepper material is pretreated by heating to 80-100degrees Celsius for 5-10 minutes. The debittering treatment includes: performing supercritical treatment on pepper with carbon dioxide as fluid at 35-55degrees Celsius, and 15-25 MPa for 4-6 hours, and taking pepper residues. The method further comprises adding emulsifier to the pepper oil with cooked flavor, the emulsifier is mixture of mono-diglyceride fatty acid ester and propylene glycol in mass ratio of 9:0.8-1.2, and the addition amount of emulsifying agent is 5-15 % of the total mass of pepper oil resin and pepper extract oil. The method comprises (i) heating the pepper oil resin to 45-55degrees Celsius, adding emulsifier, and uniformly stirring, (ii) adding pepper extracting oil into the system, and uniformly stirring. In method before adding pepper, adding the positively charged diatomite to the pepper extract oil to absorb the rancidity product of the oil.Preferred Components: The pepper material is dry pepper or pepper residue after debitterizing treatment.

   BUIU O
   COBIANU C
   MARINESCU M R

GA 202247562R

   USE - Method for preparing aldehyde group-modified two-dimensional covalent organic framework material used for adsorption and removal of carbon dioxide (CO2) gas in gas (claimed).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for preparing COF of formula (I) by solvothermal method involves taking 16.2-48.6 mg 1,3,5-benzenetricarbaldehyde (0.1-0.3 millimolar, TFB) and 9-27 mg formyl hydrazide (0.15-0.45 millimolar, FH), and then heating and stirring in mixed solution of mesitylene and dioxane for reaction, where volume of mesitylene is 1-2 mL, volume of dioxane is 1-2 mL, and volume ratio of trimethylbenzene and dioxane is 0.5-2, temperature of amination reaction is 110-130 degrees C, and reaction time is 70-80 hours; required catalyst acetic acid concentration is 2-8 mol/L, and volume is 0.3 mL; after reaction is over, taking out COF material connected by imine bond and drying to obtain product. Preferred Conditions: The reaction vessel ampoules used are 5-10 mL. The molar ratio of functional groups participating in Schiff base reaction between formyl hydrazide and 1,3,5-benzenetricarbaldehyde is 0.5-1.5, and the preferred ratio of functional groups is 1:1. The COF material has higher specific surface area and better ordered structure, with specific surface area of 800-1000 m2/g, average pore diameter of 0.5-2 nanometer, good powder diffraction (PXRD) peak and highest peak intensity of PXRD is 15000-20000, and formed relatively uniform hollow tube structure with inner diameter and outer diameter of 30-60 nanometer and 80-100 nanometer, respectively.

PT P
PN CN112062783-A
TI New metalloporphyrin complex used for ultraviolet light-responsive copolymerization for forming polycarbonate.
AU ZHUO C
   WANG X
   WANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2020C6317B
AB    NOVELTY - An ultraviolet light-responsive metalloporphyrin complex (I) or (II), is new.
   USE - New metalloporphyrin complex used for ultraviolet light-responsive copolymerization.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal-porphyrin complex (III) or (IV) has excellent catalytic performance, high activity and high selectivity to prepare polycarbonate, which shows the polymerization state of carbon dioxide copolymerization reaction ON. The metalloporphyrin complex (I) or (II) has improved catalytic performance, especially when the catalytic activity is significantly reduced or even deactivated at low catalyst concentration, high activity and high selectivity to prepare polycarbonate, which shows the polymerization state of carbon dioxide copolymerization reaction OFF.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An ultraviolet light-responsive metalloporphyrin complex of formula (I) or (II), is new.
   Y' = axial substituent;
   M = metal element;
   n,m = 1-6;and
   R1-R10 = H, halo or 1-10C aliphatic group, or 6-30C aryl (both optionally substituted).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the complex (I) or (II).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The preparation of the complex (I) or (II) involves reacting a porphyrin derivative of formula (V) with an anthracene derivative of formula (VI) under the action of a catalyst to obtain porphyrin derivative of formula (VII), reacting the compound (VII) with the metal salt compound in a solvent to obtain compound (I), reacting a porphyrin derivative of formula (VIII) with (anthracen-1-yl)methanol of formula (IX) under the action of a catalyst to obtain a porphyrin derivative of formula of formula (X), reacting compound (X) with the metal salt compound in a solvent to obtain compound (I). Preferred Components: Under the action of long-wave ultraviolet light, the compound (I) is transformed into a metalloporphyrin complex with a two-center structure of formula (III), and compound (II) is transformed into a multi-center structure metalloporphyrin complex of formula (IV). Under the action of medium-wave ultraviolet light, the compound (III) is transformed into compound (I), and the compound (IV) is transformed into compound (II). In the ultraviolet light-controlled window type high-pressure polymerization reactor, the compound (III) and (IV) is used as the main catalyst. In the presence of the co-catalyst, it catalyzes carbon dioxide and epoxy compound to undergo copolymerization reaction. Under low catalyst concentration, the ON-OFF control of the polymerization reaction can be achieved by changing the wavelength of ultraviolet light, and the polycarbonate can be prepared selectively. The co-catalyst is chosen from tetraethylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium chloride, tetrabutylammonium hydrogen sulfate, bistriphenylphosphine ammonium chloride, bistriphenylphosphine ammonium bromide, bistriphenylphosphonium nitroamine, 4-dimethylaminopyridine and gamma -chloropropylmethyldimethoxysilane. The epoxide is chosen from ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, 1,2-butylene oxide, cyclohexane oxide, cyclopentene oxide, epichlorohydrin, glycidyl methacrylate, methyl glycidyl ether, phenyl glycidyl ether and styrene alkylene oxide. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the metalloporphyrin complex (I) or (II) to epoxide is 1:(2000-500000). Preferred Conditions: The pressure of the carbon dioxide is 0.1-8.0 MPa. The copolymerization reaction is carried out at 0-150 degrees C for 0.5-48 hours. Preferred Properties: The wavelength of the long-wave ultraviolet light is 300 nm or more. The wavelength of the medium-wave ultraviolet light is 300 nm or less.
   Preferred Definitions:
   Y' = halo, -NO3, CH3COO-, CCl3COO-, ClO4-, -BF4, -BPh4, -CN, -N3, p-toluic acid group, p-toluenesulfonic acid group, o-nitrophenoxide anion, p-nitrophenoxide anion, m-nitrophenoxide anion, 2,4-dinitrophenoxide anion, 3,5-dinitrophenoxide anion, 2,4,6-trinitrophenoxide anion, 3,5-dichlorophenoxide anion, 3,5-difluorophenoxide anion, 3,5-di-trifluoromethylphenoxide anion and pentafluorophenoxide anion;and
   M = Mg, Al, Zn, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ti, Y, Ni or Ru.

PN CN111072448-A; CN111072448-B
TI Synthesis of high-purity hexafluorobutadiene comprises heating hexachlorobenzene, chlorine gas and anhydrous hydrogen fluoride with catalyst, oxidizing product, reacting with sodium hydroxide, and treating mixed gas.
AU KE W
   ZHOU B
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 202038036M
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of high-purity hexafluoro-1,3-butadiene comprises heating mixture of hexachlorobenzene, chlorine gas and anhydrous hydrogen fluoride in the presence of catalyst to produce 1,2-dichloro-3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6-octafluorocyclohexene, heating mixture of product and solvent with strong oxidant to generate 1,6-octafluoroadipic acid, heating obtained product and solvent with sodium hydroxide to obtain sodium fluoride an mixed gas of water, carbon dioxide and hexafluoro-1,3-butadiene, and treating mixed gas to remove water and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for synthesizing high-purity hexafluoro-1,3-butadiene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses cheap and easily sourced raw materials, ensures good catalyst stability for long service, and has simple product separation and purification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst is magnesium, chromium, iron, or their respective complexes with bismuth, titanium, and other metals. The strong oxidant is potassium permanganate, manganese dioxide, or potassium dichromate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The contact time of the hexachlorobenzene, chlorine gas and anhydrous hydrogen fluoride with catalyst is 0.1-20 seconds at 300-600 degrees C. The reaction with strong oxidant is carried out at 30-150 degrees C. The reaction with sodium hydroxide is carried out at 30-150 degrees C. Preferred Component: The molar ratio of hexachlorobenzene, chlorine gas and anhydrous hydrogen fluoride is 1:3-5:10-28. The solvent is toluene, benzene, xylene, or ether. The molar ratio of 1,2-dichloro-3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6-octafluorocyclohexene, solvent, and strong oxidant is 1:0.1-0.5:1-5. The molar ratio of 1,6-octafluoroadipic acid, solvent and sodium hydroxide is 1:0.1-0.5:1-5.

PN US2020115301-A1; WO2020081517-A1; CN112969677-A; VN79494-A; IN202117017307-A; RU2769830-C1; IN409983-B
TI Removing light gases from hydrocarbon stream            including ethylene, ethane and heavier hydrocarbons            involves absorbing carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and            carbonyl sulfide from hydrocarbon stream by contact            with solvent.
AU ROMAN D A
   FRANKEN J
   EVANS D
   JORIS F
   DAVID E
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 2020299538
AB    NOVELTY - Removing light gasses from a hydrocarbon                stream including ethylene, ethane and heavier                hydrocarbons involves absorbing carbon dioxide,                hydrogen sulfide and carbonyl sulfide from the                hydrocarbon stream by contact with a solvent to                provide an absorbed hydrocarbon stream, selectively                hydrogenating acetylene in the absorbed hydrocarbon                stream to ethylene to provide a hydrogenated                hydrocarbon stream, and adsorbing one of water,                mercury, ammonia and mercaptan and one of methanol                and hydrogen cyanide and arsine from the                hydrogenated hydrocarbon stream to provide an                adsorbed hydrocarbon stream comprising                ethylene.
   USE - Method for removing light gases from                hydrocarbon stream including ethylene, ethane and                heavier hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to remove light gasses from                a hydrocarbon stream including ethylene, ethane and                heavier hydrocarbons, which is safe,                cost-effective, and removes additional                impurities.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Prferred Conditions: The absorbed                  hydrocarbon stream is washed with water to absorb                  amines from the absorbed hydrocarbon stream,                  nitrous oxides are converted to ammonia and                  oxygen to water while selectively hydrogenating                  acetylene in the absorbed hydrocarbon stream. The                  one of water, mercury, ammonia and mercaptan and                  one of methanol and hydrogen cyanide arsine is                  adsorbed in a first adsorption unit and adsorbing                  arsine in a second adsorption unit, regenerated                  adsorbent in first adsorption unit with a                  desorption gas. The hydrocarbon stream is                  compressed before absorption. The acid gasses are                  scrubbed from hydrocarbon stream before                  compressing hydrocarbon stream. The ethylene is                  recovered from adsorbed hydrocarbon stream. The                  acetylene is converted to ethylene, oxygen to                  water and nitrous oxides to ammonia with hydrogen                  previously resident in absorbed hydrocarbon                  stream. The one parameter of the process is                  sensed, generated signal or data from the sensing                  and transmitted signal or data.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.)

PN JP2020020016-A; JP7115123-B2
TI Refining lithium from wastewater by contacting            wastewater with ion exchange resin, contacting eluent            with ion exchange resin, adding carbonate source to            obtain lithium carbonate, introducing carbon dioxide            and heating resulted product.
AU TAKANO M
   KUDO Y
   IKEDA O
   MATSUMOTO S
   ASANO S
AE SUMITOMO METAL MINING CO (SUMM-C)
   SUMITOMO METAL MINING CO LTD (SUMM-C)
GA 2020102787
AB    NOVELTY - Lithium purification method for refining                lithium from a wastewater from a manufacturing                process containing lithium as a positive electrode                material for a lithium secondary battery involves                (a) contacting the wastewater with an ion exchange                resin, (b) contacting an eluent with the ion                exchange resin, (c) crystallizing by adding a                carbonate source to the post-elution solution to                obtain lithium carbonate, (d) introducing carbon                dioxide into lithium carbonate, and (e)                recrystallizing by heating the resulting product of                lithium hydrogen carbonate to obtain the                product.
   USE - The method is useful for refining lithium from                a wastewater from manufacturing process containing                lithium as a positive electrode material for a                lithium secondary battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high purity product, is                simple in operation and cost-effective.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing copper electrocatalyst, comprises (a) dissolving copper precursor in an organic solvent, and primarily heat treating copper precursor to prepare copper precursor solution, (b) dispersing carbon support in a mixed solution of fixing agent and organic solvent by ultrasonic waves to prepare carbon support solution, and (c) secondarily heat treating by mixing copper precursor solution and carbon support solution.
   USE - The electrocatalyst is useful for reducing carbon dioxide (claimed) and in copper/carbon-based electrode having excellent electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction performance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides copper and copper oxide nanoparticles of various sizes and shapes through wet synthesis of solution containing copper precursor hydrate.
TF EXAMPLE - 20 mg Copper(II) nitrate monohydrate (99.999%) was dissolved in 10 ml anhydrous ethylene glycol (99.8%), heated at 120 degrees C for 30 minutes to prepare copper nitrate solution, 20 mg Ketjenblack (RTM: Unique electro-conductive carbon black) was ultrasonicated for 30 minutes to prepare carbon black solution by dispersing 1 mg cysteamine (95%) in a solution mixed with 10 l ethylene glycol, then the carbon black solution was added into the copper nitrate solution at 120 degrees C for 20 minutes, and heated for 7 hours while increasing temperature at a rate of 3 degrees C/minute to 200 degrees C to obtain sphere type copper/carbon-based catalyst having average particle diameter of 5 nm.

PN WO2019053540-A1
TI Producing alpha, beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid salt, comprises reacting alkene and carbon dioxide with composition of carboxylation catalyst, organic base and inorganic base, under reaction conditions.
AU BEETSTRA D
   PASHA F A
   KOROBKOV I
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201926121L
AB    NOVELTY - Producing an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid salt, comprises reacting an alkene and carbon dioxide with a composition comprising a carboxylation catalyst, an organic base that is solubilized in the composition, and an inorganic base that is not solubilized in the composition, under reaction conditions to produce an inorganic base salt of alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid, where the organic base has a first acid dissociation constant (pKa) and the inorganic base has a second pKa that is greater than the first pKa.
   USE - The method is useful for producing an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the composition that does not include a Lewis acid (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic base is: an alkali metal and/or an alkaline earth metal containing base; or an alkali metal and/or alkaline earth metal carbonate, preferably cesium, rubidium, potassium, sodium or lithium carbonate. The inorganic base is an aqueous inorganic base that is immiscible in the composition. The reaction conditions include: maintaining the composition at a temperature of 30-180 degrees C; an alkene pressure of 0.1-5 MPa; and/or a carbon dioxide pressure of 0.1-5 MPa or 0.1-0.5 MPa. The carboxylation catalyst comprises e.g.: at least one transition metal of columns 4-10 of the Periodic Table; at least one transition metal, nickel or palladium; and at least one coordinating ligand, or a coordinating ligand comprising at least two coordinating atoms including phosphorus, nitrogen, oxygen, sulfur and carbene that coordinate with the transition metal. In the method, the alkene is ethylene and the produced inorganic base salt of alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid is an alkali metal or an alkaline earth metal acrylate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method: an organic base salt of alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid is formed between the organic base and produced alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid; and the organic base salt of alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid reacts with the inorganic base to produce the inorganic base salt of alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid. Preferred Components: The difference between the organic base pKa and the inorganic base pKa is 0.2-20. The organic base comprises nitrogen-based compound, an ether, an ester, a carbonyl, a carboxylate, an alkoxide, a phenoxide, a carboxylate, a sulfate, a sulfonate, a phosphate and/or a phosphonate. The nitrogen based organic compound is an amine, an imine, an amide or an aromatic amine. The organic base has e.g. substituted amine containing compounds of formula (N(R90)(R91)(R92)) (I) and 2,6-dimethyl pyridine.Either R90-R92 = aryl (optionally substituted), H, alkyl, branched alkyl, cycloalkyl, heterocyclyl, heteroaryl group, double bond or phosphorus group; orR90R91 = cycloalkyl, cycloalkenyl, aryl, aromatic heteroaryl or heterocyclyl.Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises an organic solvent, where the carboxylation catalyst and the organic base are each solubilized in the organic solvent, or the carboxylation catalyst is not solubilized in the solvent and the organic base is solubilized in the solvent.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2019030193-A1; TW201920067-A; CN111032615-A; EP3665151-A1; US2020361850-A1; EP3665151-B1; US11242310-B2; TW761572-B1
TI Converting cyclic alkyleneureas into their            corresponding alkyleneamines comprises reacting cyclic            alkyleneureas in liquid phase with water with removal            of carbon dioxide, and adding inorganic base and            reacting cyclic alkylene ureas.
AU KANTZER E N
   LAKE K F
   EHLERS I
   WERNERSSON S M
   EDVINSSON R K
   VAN DAM H
   TEN KATE A J B
   VENEMAN R
   RAAIJMAKERS M J T
   JOVIC S
AE AKZO NOBEL CHEM INT BV (ALKU-C)
   NOURYON CHEM INT BV (NOUR-Non-standard)
   NOURYON CHEM INT BV (ALKU-C)
GA 201915326F
AB    NOVELTY - Converting cyclic alkyleneureas into their                corresponding alkyleneamines, comprises (a)                converting cyclic alkyleneureas into their                corresponding alkyleneamines by reacting cyclic                alkyleneureas in the liquid phase with water with                removal of carbon dioxide, so as to convert 5-95                mole% of alkyleneurea moieties in the feedstock to                the corresponding amines, and (b) adding a                inorganic base and reacting cyclic alkylene ureas                remaining from the first step with the inorganic                base to convert them completely or partially into                their corresponding alkyleneamines.
   USE - The process is useful for converting cyclic                alkyleneureas into their corresponding                alkyleneamines (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides high conversion of                cyclic alkyleneureas, while using less strong                inorganic base; and shows a higher selectivity to                amines than the prior art process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic base is                  a material which does not contain carbon-carbon                  bonds and which has a pKb of less than 1. The                  feedstock comprises at least 10 (preferably at                  least 20) mole% of cyclic urea derivatives of                  alkyleneamine compounds comprising a                  (-NH-R3-NH-R3-NH-R3-NH-) moiety, calculated on                  the total of cyclic urea compounds present in the                  mixture.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process, the                  cyclic alkyleneurea compounds of formula (I)                  reacts to an alkyleneamine compounds of formula                  (R1-NH-R3-NH-R2) (II).R1, R2 = H, alkylene amine                  (X1-R3-(NH-R3-)p-, or alkoxy (X1-R3-(O-R3-))n-,                  or a group combining such alkylene amine and                  alkoxy units p and n, where optionally at least                  one unit -N-R3-N- may be present as either one of                  the rings piperazinyl-1,4-diyl and/or                  imidazolidin-2-one-1,3-diyl;R3 = optionally alkylene, preferably                  ethylene, propylene or isopropylene;X1 = OH, amine, linear or branched 1-20C                  hydroxyalkyl or 1-20C aminoalkyl; andn, p = at least 1, preferably 2-20,                  optionally containing at least one piperazine, or                  alkylene urea group, or when p or n is 0 may be a                  1-20C hydroxyalky or 1-20C aminoalkyl.The step (a) is carried out: at a                  temperature of at least 150 degrees C, preferably                  at most 320 degrees C; by reacting cyclic                  alkyleneureas in the liquid phase with water in                  an amount of 0.1-20 mole water per mole urea                  moiety, at a temperature of at least 230 degrees                  C; and in the presence of an amine compound                  comprising primary amines, cyclic secondary                  amines, and bicyclic tertiary amines. The                  inorganic base is added in the step (b) at a                  content of at least 0.2:1, preferably at least                  2:1 mole inorganic base per mole cyclic                  alkyleneurea moiety. In the step (b) the molar                  amount of cyclic ethylene urea moieties in the                  product provided to the treatment with the                  inorganic base is at most 20:1, preferably at                  most 3:1. The step (b) is carried out at a                  temperature of at least 100 degrees C, preferably                  at most 280 degrees C. In process, the salt                  formed in the step (b) is removed.
   EXAMPLE - Diurea of triethylenetetramine and water were                mixed in a 2 l pressure vessel under an atmosphere                of nitrogen. The reactor vessel was heated to 270                degrees C and when the temperature was around 235                degrees C a flow of nitrogen gas was applied. The                nitrogen gas was introduced at the bottom of the                reactor through a sparger (pore diameter 2 mu m).                The condenser was attached to the gas outlet of the                reactor. The flow of nitrogen was around 2                l/minutes and the pressure was 30 barg. The                agitation speed was 250 rpm. The reactor was                operated under these conditions for 5 hours before                it was cooled down to room temperature. The sample                was analyzed. Then the resultant was mixed in a                pressure autoclave under an atmosphere of nitrogen                with sodium hydroxide solution containing 13.2 mole                water/mole sodium hydroxide. The mixture was heated                for 2.5 hours and was then allowed to cool to give                linear triethylenetetramine.

PN CN109135860-A
TI Pre-processing biomass comprises crushing the biomass material, subjecting to cyclone separation and pulse dust removal treatment, washing, performing supercritical carbon dioxide blasting treatment, microwave drying treatment and crushing.
AU SHEN X
AE WUHU JUYAN BIOMASS ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (WUHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019054682
AB    NOVELTY - Pre-processing biomass comprises: (i) cutting or crushing the biomass material to obtain crumb having particle size of 10-60 mm; (ii) subjecting the crumb to cyclone separation and pulse dust removal treatment; (iii) washing the crumb with circulating water, and adjusting the moisture content to 10-30%; (iv) performing supercritical carbon dioxide blasting treatment of wet crumb; (v) subjecting the wet crumb to microwave drying treatment until the moisture content is less than or equal to 10%; and (vi) crushing the crumb to 100 meshes.
   USE - The method is useful for pre-processing biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes the impurities and reduces the alkali metal element content, and softens wood fiber to reduce slag.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The water temperature of the circulating water is 60-75 degrees C. In the step (iv), performing supercritical carbon dioxide blasting treatment of the crumb at 80-120 degrees C by passing carbon dioxide with a pressure of 10-15 MPa, then depressurizing immediately after 30-120 minutes. The microwave drying is carried out by: (a) adjusting the microwave radiation power to 200-600 w/kg at 70-80 degrees C; and (b) adjusting the microwave radiation power to 0.8-1.2 kw/kg at 100-120 degrees C until the moisture content is less than or equal to 10%. Preferred Components: The biomass material includes woody waste and/or herbaceous waste. The woody waste is wood chips, wood shavings, sawdust, bark, branches, leaves or bamboo. The herbaceous waste is crop straw, husk, corn cob, wheat bran, peanut hull or bagasse.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PN CN108623295-A
TI Processing heat insulating brick and producing acid involves milling phosphogypsum, low grade bauxite, additive and modifier, dissolving, solid-liquid separating, feeding carbon dioxide, filtering, cleaning, and drying.
AU CHEN X
   LI J
   WANG S
   LI M
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201880227N
AB    NOVELTY - Processing heat insulating brick and producing acid comprises milling phosphogypsum, low grade bauxite, additive and modifier, dissolving, solid-liquid separating, feeding carbon dioxide, filtering, cleaning, drying, burning, crushing, adding mullite powder, kaolin, polystyrene balls, silicon dioxide, zirconium dioxide, chromium sesquioxide, magnesium oxide and water, mixing, grinding, vacuum extruding, drying for 2-3 days, heating, drying, sintering, performing flotation, separating sulfide, roasting at 800-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours under oxygen-enriched environment, reacting with vanadium pentoxide and sulfuric acid, and absorbing.
   USE - Method for processing heat insulating brick and producing acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The brick has improved physical performance. The preparation method has improved resource utilization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of phosphogypsum and low-grade bauxite is 1:0.6-1.7. and                  deionized water, drying and then soaking in 100                  ml of 0.5 mol/l dilute hydrochloric acid for 30                  minutes, washing with deionized water and drying, C for 2-8 hours and cooling, during the                  entire heating-insulation-cooling process,                  maintaing the hydrogen gas/inert gas mixture,                  then cooling to 200 degrees C, then being                  switched to pure inert gas and not switching,                  (iv) constant temperature zone of tube furnace is                  used as the reaction zone, then placing selenium                  powder obtained in (i) on the inlet side, placing                  metal tungsten sheet obtained in (ii) on the                  outlet side or evenly spreading the selenium                  powder evenly on tungsten plate, then placing                  tungsten plate is placed in constant temperature                  zone and carrying out synthesis reaction of step                  (iii) to obtain in-situ growth of tungsten                  selenide nanowire film material.

PN BE1024373-B1
TI Preparing confectionery paste, comprises mixing cream sugar and at least one ingredient i.e. almond powder and/or almond paste, starch, glucose syrup, water, vegetable oil and glycerol and cooling by means of solid carbon dioxide.
AU RANSON L
   RANSON C
AE RANSON NV (RANS-Non-standard)
GA 2018218056
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing confectionery paste, comprises mixing cream sugar and at least one ingredient comprising almond powder and/or almond paste, starch, glucose syrup, water, vegetable oil and glycerol in mixing vessel of mixing arm, where the ingredients are mixed to form a confectionery paste at 70-85 degrees C, and cooling confectionery paste after mixing by means of solid carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing confectionery paste which is marzipan or fondant where the paste is useful for decorating patisserie.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the confectionery paste that has longer shelf life and is favorable for storage, transport and for the marketing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The confectionery paste is mixed in a cooling mixing vessel provided with kneading arm. The confectionery paste is cooled for 8-14 minutes. The solid carbon dioxide is formed from liquid carbon dioxide which is kept at a minimum of 30 bar in a pressure vessel, when it escapes from the pressure vessel through valve. The solid carbon dioxide originates near the valve, where the valve is provided with a nozzle which is directed into the cooling mixing vessel. The initial temperature of the solid carbon dioxide is -80 degrees C to -75 degrees C. The confectionery paste is cooled to 20-40 degrees C. The mixing arm is provided with at least one knife. The mixing arm speed during mixing is 2000-4000 revolutions per minute. The confectionery paste is divided into individual portions by means of extrusion. Preferred Components: The confectionery paste is marzipan where the ingredients comprise almond powder and/or almond paste, cream sugar, glucose syrup, glycerol and at least one stabilizer. The confectionery paste is fondant where the ingredients comprise flour sugar, starch, glucose syrup, water, vegetable oil, glycerol, a thickener, starch and at least one preservative, food acid, stabilizer and emulsifier. The ingredients further comprise at least one flavoring agent and at least one dye.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

GA 201833024L
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of marigold lutein comprises fermenting marigold petals, freeze-drying, crushing, adding to extraction kettle, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction with entrainer and carbon dioxide, ultrasonically distilling extract to obtain concentrated extract, and saponifying with sodium hydroxide ethanol solution.

   ADVANTAGE - The method uses entrainer so that the solute solubility in supercritical carbon dioxide increases, and has improved extraction rate. The product has pure color, high purity, and no peculiar smell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The potassium hydroxide ethanol solution has concentration of 45-48%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The fermentation is done by cold fermenting marigold petals at 25 degrees C for 5-7 days, steaming at 7-8 kg/cm2 for 90-100 seconds, piling, and fermenting for 16-24 hours. The crushing is done until particle size is 20-30 mesh. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is done at extraction kettle temperature of 40-50 degrees C and pressure of 23-25 MPa, first separating kettle temperature of 38-45 degrees C and pressure of 13-17 MPa, second separating kettle temperature of 35-48 degrees C and pressure of 9-11 MPa, and extraction time of 6-8 hours. The ultrasonic distilling is done at power of 100-200 W and frequency of 15-35 kHz for 15-20 minutes. Preferred Components: The entrainer is ethanol and acetone at ratio of 20-30:1, and has dosage of 3-7.5 wt.% of carbon dioxide.

   ZUBER M G

AE SYNHELION SA (SYNH-Non-standard)
   SYNHELION SA (SYNH-Non-standard)
   SYNHELION SA (SYNH-Non-standard)
GA 202163123M
AB    NOVELTY - Producing syngas involves providing a feed gas                consisting of a hydrocarbon, carbon dioxide and                optionally steam, and contacting a flow of the feed                gas with a metal oxide to form syngas, where the                feed gas comprises steam, the sum of the mole                fractions of carbon dioxide and steam, in the feed                gas, is 0.5-0.7, the feed gas comprises no steam,                the mole fraction of carbon dioxide is 0.5-0.7, and                the mole fraction of the hydrocarbon in the feed                gas is 0.3-0.5, preferably 0.45-0.5, and the feed                gas is contacted with the metal oxide at 1200-1600                K and the feed gas has a pressure of 2-20                bar.
   USE - Method for producing syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables producing syngas with                excellent hydrocarbon gas conversion and                hydrogen/carbon monoxide selectivity and providing                simplified process for the production of syngas to                the extent that the tendency for carbon depositions                during operation is diminished while allowing for                the production of syngas having a more favorable                ratio of hydrogen and carbon monoxide for the                subsequent liquid fuel synthesis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                device, which comprises a reaction chamber                comprising a metal oxide, a heat source capable of                heating and maintaining the reaction chamber at                1200-1600K, and controlled by a heat source control                device, a first mass flow controller capable of                controlling the mass inflow of feed gas into the                reaction chamber, and controlled by a first mass                flow controller control device, optionally an                analytic device capable of determining the mole                fraction of methane, carbon dioxide and steam in                the inflow of feed gas and capable of communicating                with, and controlling, a second mass flow                controller capable of controlling the addition of                either carbon dioxide, steam or both to the flow of                feed gas such as to adjust the mole fraction of                hydrocarbon, carbon dioxide and steam in the feed                gas towards a mole fraction of carbon dioxide or of                carbon dioxide and steam in the feed gas of 0.5-0.7                and/or a mole fraction of the hydrocarbon in the                feed gas of 0.3-0.5 when the mole fraction of                carbon dioxide or of carbon dioxide and steam in                the feed gas is not 0.5-0.7 and/or a mole fraction                of the hydrocarbon in the feed gas is not between                0.3-0.5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide is in                  the form of a packed bed of irregular particles                  of metal oxide. The metal oxide is chosen from                  ceria and solid solutions of ceria (Ce1-xMxO2),                  where M is zirconium, hafnium, samarium,                  lanthanum or scandium, from metal oxides having a                  perovskite structure ABO3, A is chosen from                  Strontium, calcium, barium or Lanthanum and B is                  chosen from manganese, iron, titanium, cobalt or                  aluminum such as calcium titanate, from iron                  oxides such as iron(II,III) oxide and mixed                  ferrites (MxFe3-xO4), M is zinc (Zn-ferrite),                  cobalt (Co-ferrite), nickel (Ni-ferrite),                  manganese (Mn-ferrite), from tungsten trioxide,                  stannic oxide, and ceria and solid solutions of                  ceria (Ce1-xMxO2), M is zirconium, hafnium,                  samarium, lanthanum or scandium such as                  Ce(0.85)Zr(0.15)O2 and Hf (Ce1-xHfxO2),                  preferably ceria.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon                  comprises methane, ethane, propane, butane,                  and/or pentane. The feed gas is a biogas. The                  mole fraction of carbon dioxide in the feed gas                  is 0.25-0.45 and the mole fraction of steam in                  the feed gas is 0.05-0.45. Preferred Method: The                  feed gas is obtained by the addition of carbon                  dioxide, steam, or steam and carbon dioxide to a                  biogas or natural gas.

PT P
PN CN113155583-A; CN113155583-B
TI Extracting high-efficiently carbon dioxide from calcite and dolomite in mixed carbonate rock based on different acidification temperature involves grinding mixed carbonate sample and placing prepared sample in oven.
AU LI S
   LIANG J
   ZHU H
   PENG K
AE QINGDAO INST MARINE GEOLOGY (CGSU-C)
   UNIV CHINA GEOSCIENCES WUHAN (UYCI-C)
   SHANDONG GEOLOGICAL ENVIRONMENT MONITORI (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202191471F
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting high-efficiently carbon dioxide from calcite and dolomite in mixed carbonate rock based on different acidification temperature involves grinding the mixed carbonate sample to a particle size range of 75-80 mu m, placing the prepared sample in an oven to remove the water content at the same time, preparing a phosphoric acid solution with a mass concentration of 100-104%, putting the prepared sample into the automatic sample feeding machine or Y-shaped pipe, accessing the purifying-gas collecting pipeline together with the phosphoric acid bath basin, vacuumizing the purifying-gas collecting pipeline, setting the temperature of the phosphoric acid bath basin to be 48.5-51.5 degrees C, putting the mixed sample carbonate rock the constant temperature phosphoric acid bath basin, setting the reaction time is 10 minutes, purifying the carbon dioxide generated after the reaction by purifying-gas collecting pipeline, and collecting as the calcite product in the mixed sample.
   USE - Method for extracting high-efficiently carbon dioxide from calcite and dolomite in mixed carbonate rock based on different acidification temperature.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has greatly improve the gas separation efficiency and test precision, and establishes the complete calcite and dolomite carbon dioxide separation extraction process, and the gas mixing ratio is controlled in a reasonable range.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting high-efficiently carbon dioxide from calcite and dolomite in mixed carbonate rock based on different acidification temperature involves grinding the mixed carbonate sample to a particle size range of 75-80 mu m, placing the prepared sample in an oven to remove the water content at the same time, preparing a phosphoric acid solution with a mass concentration of 100-104%, putting the prepared sample into the automatic sample feeding machine or Y-shaped pipe, accessing the purifying-gas collecting pipeline together with the phosphoric acid bath basin, vacuuming the purifying-gas collecting pipeline, setting the temperature of the phosphoric acid bath basin to be 48.5-51.5 degrees C, putting the mixed sample carbonate rock the constant temperature phosphoric acid bath basin, setting the reaction time is 10 minutes, purifying the carbon dioxide generated after the reaction by purifying-gas collecting pipeline, collecting as the calcite product in the mixed sample, heating the phosphoric acid bath tub in the reaction for 10 minutes at the same time, closing the inlet valve and the outlet valve in the constant temperature phosphoric acid bath tub, adjusting quickly the water bath temperature of the constant temperature phosphoric acid bath tub to 88.5-91.5 degrees C, purifying the carbon dioxide purifying collecting pipeline after collecting carbon dioxide, vacuuming, curing the purifying-gas collecting pipeline to remove the residual carbon dioxide and water in the purifying-gas collecting pipeline, extracting the dolomite carbon dioxide in the mixed carbonate rock when purifying the residual carbon dioxide and water in the gas collecting pipeline, opening the outlet valve in the constant temperature phosphoric acid bath basin, and purifying the carbon dioxide generated after the reaction and collecting as the dolomite product in the mixed sample.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the phosphoric acid bath is set to 50 degrees C. The temperature of the water bath is quickly raised to 90 degrees C through the temperature adjustment knob. The time for vacuuming and curing the purifying-gas is 30-50 minutes, the vacuum degree of the vacuum is less than 100 millitorr, and the curing temperature is 180-200 degrees C. The extraction process of calcite CO2 and dolomite CO2 in mixed carbonate rocks, the total reaction time is set to 80-100 minutes. The method can extract CO2 of calcite and dolomite in mixed carbonate samples with different components, and the gas pollution ratio between each other is less than 8%. The error ratio of carbon isotope values of calcite and dolomite in the extracted mixed carbonate rock samples with different components is less than or equal to 5%, the error ratio of oxygen isotope value is less than or equal to 8%, and the error ratio of cluster isotope value is less than or equal to 4%.

PN WO2019032609-A1; CA3072322-A1; US2020247773-A1; US11299470-B2
TI Purifying cannabis-derived compound from a mixture involves transporting mixture of first and second components, and collecting first and second fractions of mobile phase of first and second components, respectively over first time interval.
AU COLTON C K
   MOSES J M
   WILSON V M
   DALZELL W H
   LAYUROVA M
   ELIAN T J
   CAI T H
   MACKAY J
AE MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
   MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
   MASSACHUSETTS TECHNOLOGY INST (MASI-C)
GA 201915367T
AB    NOVELTY - Chromatography method for purifying a cannabis-derived compound from a mixture (140), comprises: transporting the mixture, which comprises a first component and a second component, through a chromatography column (110) containing a stationary phase (120) comprising a packing material, where the first component comprises a first cannabis-derived compound, and the mixture is transported within a mobile phase (130) that comprises carbon dioxide and is substantially free of a co-solvent that is in liquid phase at standard room temperature and pressure through the column and in contact with the stationary phase; collecting a first fraction of the mobile phase comprising the first component over a first time interval from the second end of the column; and collecting a second fraction of the mobile phase comprising the second component over a second time interval different from the first time interval from the second end of the column.
   USE - The chromatography method is useful for purifying a cannabis-derived compound from a mixture (claimed) which is used to treat conditions including glaucoma, epilepsy (e.g. child epilepsy and seizures, adult epilepsy and seizures), severe or chronic pain, migraines, cancer, HIV, cachexia (i.e. wasting syndrome), multiple sclerosis, post-traumatic stress disorder, hepatitis C, amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, Crohn's disease, Alzheimer's disease, arthritis, fibromyalgia, inflammation, and stress.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not use toxic solvents or denature naturally occurring components; is performed under conditions that result in the carbon dioxide of the mobile phase having a relatively high density that increase the solubility of polar compounds; requires fewer steps than conventional chromatography methods (e.g. methods using a mobile phase that comprises carbon dioxide and a liquid co-solvent) as the carbon dioxide can simply evaporate from any collected fractions (e.g. fractions comprising the cannabis-derived compounds) at standard room temperature and pressure; may not be required additional purification steps; utilizes liquid co-solvent that enhances the solubility of polar compounds in the mobile phase; and utilizes mobile phase comprising 100% liquid carbon dioxide that may be effective at separating at least one cannabis-derived compound from at least one other component of a mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chromatography method for purifying a cannabis-derived compound from a mixture (140), comprises: transporting the mixture, which comprises a first component and a second component, through a chromatography column (110) containing a stationary phase (120) comprising a packing material, where the first component comprises a first cannabis-derived compound, the mixture is transported within a mobile phase (130) that comprises carbon dioxide and is substantially free of a co-solvent that is in liquid phase at standard room temperature and pressure through the column and in contact with the stationary phase, and the first component and the second component of the mixture are each transported within the mobile phase substantially continuously from a first end of the column to a second end of the column; collecting a first fraction of the mobile phase comprising the first component over a first time interval from the second end of the column, where the first fraction is substantially free of the second component; and collecting a second fraction of the mobile phase comprising the second component over a second time interval different from the first time interval from the second end of the column, where the second fraction is substantially free of the first component. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a chromatography system (100) for purifying a first cannabis-derived compound from a mixture, comprising a chromatography column containing a stationary phase comprising a packing material, a mobile phase comprising carbon dioxide, where the mobile phase is substantially free of a co-solvent that is in liquid phase at standard room temperature and pressure and the mixture, the mixture comprises a first component and a second component, the first component comprises the first cannabis-derived compound.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic diagram of chromatography system comprising a chromatography column, a stationary phase, a mobile phase, and a mixture comprising a cannabis-derived compound.
   Chromatography system (100)
   Column (110)
   Stationary phase (120)
   Mobile phase (130)
   Mixture (140)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The transporting step is: performed at a temperature of at least 31 degrees C and a pressure of at least 1100 psi; performed at a temperature of 31-70 degrees C and a pressure of 1085.7-30000 psi; and performed at a temperature of 0-30 degrees C and/or a pressure of 500-10000 psi. The chromatography method further comprises collecting a third fraction of the mobile phase comprising the third component over a third time interval different from the first time interval and the second time interval, where the third fraction is substantially free of the first component and the second component. Preferred Components: The mobile phase is substantially free of any co-solvent. The carbon dioxide comprises supercritical carbon dioxide or liquid carbon dioxide. The concentration in the mobile phase of the co-solvent that is in liquid phase at standard room temperature and pressure is less than or equal to 0.5 (preferably 0) vol.%. The concentration of carbon dioxide in the mobile phase is at least 80 (preferably 100) vol.%. The mobile phase comprises a co-solvent or diluent that is in gaseous phase at standard room temperature and pressure. The concentration in the mobile phase of the co-solvent that is in gaseous phase at standard room temperature and pressure is less than or equal to 20 vol.%. The co-solvent that is in gaseous phase at standard room temperature and pressure is nitrous oxide, dimethyl ether, ethane, propane, butane, sulfur hexafluoride, and/or a halocarbon. The first, second and third cannabis-derived compounds comprise cannabidiol, cannabinol and/or tetrahydrocannabinol. The second component comprises a second cannabis-derived compound. The mixture further comprises a third component comprising a third cannabis-derived compound. The packing material comprises silica, ethylene-bridged hybrid 2-ethyl pyridine, and/or 18C. The mixture is extracted from a Cannabis species plant. The concentration of the first component in the first fraction is at least 50 wt.%. The concentration of the second component in the first fraction is less than or equal to 0.1 wt.%. The total concentration of all components of the mixture other than the first component in the first fraction is less than or equal to 5 wt.%. The concentration of the second component in the second fraction is at least 50 wt.%. The concentration of the first component in the second fraction is less than or equal to 0.1 wt.%. The total concentration of all components of the mixture other than the second component in the second fraction is less than or equal to 5 wt.%. The concentration of the third component in the third fraction is at least 50 wt.%. The third fraction of the mobile phase has a concentration of the first component of less than or equal to 0.1 wt.% and a concentration of the second component of less than or equal to 0.1 wt.%. The mixture comprises at least 5 components, or at least 20 components.
   ACTIVITY - Ophthalmological; Anticonvulsant; Analgesic; Antimigraine; Cytostatic; Anti-HIV; Anabolic; Endocrine-Gen; Neuroprotective; Tranquilizer; Antiinflammatory; Hepatotropic; Virucide; Gastrointestinal-Gen; Nootropic; Antiarthritic; Muscular-Gen.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of cannabis-derived compound is oral or topical. No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN MY175789-A
TI Enhancing recovery of hydrocarbon fluid from oil reservoir involves e.g. injecting carbon dioxide displacing fluid and/or carboxylic acid that reduces minimum miscibility pressure lower than formation fracture pressure of hydrocarbon fluid.
AU AWANG M
   LIN V C
AE UNIV PETRONAS TEKNOLOGI (UYPE-Non-standard)
GA 2022901443
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing recovery of at least one hydrocarbon fluid from an oil reservoir using at least one additive capable of modifying the miscibility pressure with the hydrocarbon fluid, comprises: injecting a multi-contact displacing fluid and the additive at a predetermined quantity to reduce a minimum miscibility pressure (MMP) with the hydrocarbon fluid from an oil reservoir to a value below a formation fracture pressure of the hydrocarbon fluid, where the multi-contact displacing fluid is carbon dioxide displacing fluid injected into reservoir for mixing with the hydrocarbon fluid to reduce the MMP to achieve a desired oil recovery rate; generating at least one fluid including oil from the oil reservoir; and continuing to inject the mixture of carbon dioxide displacing fluid and carboxylic acid middle agent into the oil reservoir, where the carboxylic acid reduces the MMP to a pressure lower than the formation fracture pressure of the hydrocarbon fluid for enhancing the oil recovery rate.
   USE - The method is useful for: enhancing recovery of at least one hydrocarbon fluid from an oil reservoir using at least one additive capable of modifying the miscibility pressure with the hydrocarbon fluid, which is useful to oil and gas industry and is applicable to onshore and/or offshore operation (claimed); and enhancing oil recovery from subterranean oil reservoirs using an additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes carbon dioxide displacing fluid that reduces the viscosity of the hydrocarbon fluid to enhance the oil recovery; enables addition of carboxylic acid to the carbon dioxide displacing fluid and the hydrocarbon fluid, which is semipolar and safe for human operation; utilizes oleophilic chemical that reduces the carbon dioxide-crude oil MMP by enhancing the solvating power and polarity of carbon dioxide caused by formation of special interaction between solute and co-solvent molecules; is economically feasible and also reduces the pressure for the carbon dioxide to achieve miscibility; increases the viscosity of the carbon dioxide and crude oil mixture, which results in faster and efficient recovery of oil from the subterranean oil reservoir; and reduces operation cost for carbon dioxide enhanced oil recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enhancing recovery of at least one hydrocarbon fluid from an oil reservoir using at least one additive capable of modifying the miscibility pressure with the hydrocarbon fluid, comprises: injecting a multi-contact displacing fluid and the additive at a predetermined quantity to reduce a minimum miscibility pressure (MMP) with the hydrocarbon fluid from an oil reservoir to a value below a formation fracture pressure of the hydrocarbon fluid, where the multi-contact displacing fluid is carbon dioxide displacing fluid injected into the oil reservoir for mixing with the hydrocarbon fluid to reduce the MMP to achieve a desired oil recovery rate, the carbon dioxide displacing fluid is injected at a desired pressure to the displacing fluid to reduce the MMP to a desired extent, and the additive is carboxylic acid capable of acting in form of a middle agent between the carbon dioxide displacing fluid and the hydrocarbon fluid to reduce the MMP below the formation fracture pressure; generating at least one fluid including oil from the oil reservoir; and continuing to inject the mixture of carbon dioxide displacing fluid and carboxylic acid middle agent into the oil reservoir, where the carboxylic acid reduces the MMP to a pressure lower than the formation fracture pressure of the hydrocarbon fluid for enhancing the oil recovery rate, the carboxylic acid is oil soluble, low water solubility, having 4-8C, and the carboxylic acid for oil recovery process is useful to oil and gas industry and is applicable to onshore and/or offshore operation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The existing MMP of the carbon dioxide displacing fluid and the hydrocarbon fluid mixture in the oil reservoir is determined prior to injecting the carboxylic acid middle agent into the oil reservoir. The carbon dioxide displacing fluid is injected into the oil reservoir at a desired pressure to reduce the MMP in presence of a desired amount of carboxylic acid middle agent to achieve the desired oil recovery rate. The enhanced recovery of the hydrocarbon fluid from the oil reservoir is performed in presence of carbon dioxide displacing fluid and the carboxylic acid middle agent. The oleophilic chemical added to the carbon dioxide displacing fluid for reducing viscosity of the mixture of carbon dioxide for displacing fluid and the hydrocarbon fluid is carboxylic acid. Preferred Components: The carboxylic acid middle agent is an oleophilic chemical added to the carbon dioxide displacing fluid for reducing the interfacial tension between the carbon dioxide displacing fluid and the hydrocarbon fluid. The carboxylic acid acts in form of a middle agent between the hydrocarbon fluid and the carbon dioxide displacing fluid to reduce the MMP below the formation fracture pressure of the hydrocarbon fluid.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

   WANG Y
AE SHANGHAI KUAO REFRIGERATION EQUIP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A8114J
AB    NOVELTY - Environmentally-friendly combustion gas comprises 25-75 wt.% propane and olefin (remaining amount).
   USE - The environmentally-friendly combustion gas is useful for welding gun gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas has high combustion temperature and high combustion rate, and is pollution-free.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The combustion gas comprises propane 60-65 wt.% and olefin (remaining amount). Preferred Components: The olefin is ethylene, propylene and/or butylene. The volume ratio of the propylene and butylene is 1:(0.2-0.3). The ratio of the number of carbon atoms to the number of hydrogen atoms in the combustion gas, i.e. the ratio of carbon to hydrogen is 1:(2.2-2.4). The combustion gas is further added with 1-2 wt.% combustion-supporting gas to the total volume of the combustion gas in the using process. The combustion-supporting gas comprises 5-8 wt.% carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, 42-45 wt.% hydrogen and/or methane (remaining amount).

   MATSUYAMA K
   MATSUNOBU G
AE PRIME PLANET ENERGY & SOLUTIONS INC (PRIM-Non-standard)
   FUKUOKA TECHNOLOGY INST (FUKU-Non-standard)
   FUKUOKA INST TECHNOLOGY (FUKU-Non-standard)
   PRIME PLANET ENERGY & SOLUTIONS INC (PRIM-Non-standard)
   FUKUOKA INST TECHNOLOGY (FUKU-Non-standard)
   SCHOOL JURIDICAL PERSON FUKUOKA KOGYO DA (SCHO-Non-standard)
GA 202296501L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing an olefinic resin porous                material, comprises preparing a single phase in                which an olefinic resin and a solvent are mixed                each other, in a pressure-resistant container,                introducing high pressure carbon dioxide into the                pressure-resistant container, and releasing a                pressure in the pressure-resistant container, where                the step of introducing the high pressure carbon                dioxide is carried out such that a pressure in the                pressure resistant container reaches 6 MPa or                higher.
   USE - The method is used for producing an olefinic                resin porous material, which is used in: a battery                separator such as a separator for lithium ion                secondary batteries and a separator for                nickel-hydrogen batteries; a membrane filter such                as a reverse osmosis membrane, an ultrafiltration                membrane, and a microfiltration membrane; a                breathable-waterproof garment; a packaging                material; a construction material; a                sound-absorbing material; a cleaning tool; and a                make-up tool.
   ADVANTAGE - The method expands the applications of                olefinic resin porous materials, or provides                high-performance olefinic resin porous material in                the existing applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                olefinic resin porous material, comprising: either                an olefinic resin skeleton and pores communicating                with one another in the skeleton, where the                skeleton and the pores have an intertwined                structure based on a modulated structure, the                olefinic resin skeleton has a three-dimensional                network structure, and the pores have a three                dimensional network structure; or an olefin resin                skeleton based on a sea phase in a sea-island                structure and bubble-shaped pores based on an                island phase, where adjacent pores in the pores may                be joined; or an olefinic resin skeleton and pores                communicating with one another in the skeleton,                where the olefinic resin skeleton is formed through                which the primary particles of an olefinic resin                join one another to form secondary particles, and                the secondary particles join one another; or a                skeleton in which spongy olefinic resin particulate                bodies join one another.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The high pressure carbon                  dioxide introduced in the pressure-resistant                  container is carbon dioxide in a supercritical                  state.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is                  n-pentane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The olefinic resin is                  polyethylene or polypropylene.

   NING Z
   CHEN B
AE GUANGDONG SANVO CHEM IND TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022903384
AB    NOVELTY - Single-component polyurethane foam joint                mixture comprises 15-22 pts. wt. mixed polyether,                1.4-2.8 pts. wt. silicone oil, 14-17 pts. wt.                environment-friendly paraffin, 0.6-1.2 pts. wt.                catalyst, 2-4 pts. wt. pesticide, 2-6 pts. wt.                degradation agent, 1.5-4.5 pts. wt. long-acting                fertilizer, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. aging color indicator,                38-40 pts. wt. polymeric methylene diphenyl                diisocyanate (MDI), 14-18 pts. wt. propellant, and                1-3 pts. wt. carbon dioxide.
   USE - Single-component polyurethane foam joint                mixture for preventing insects in agricultural crop                plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The mixture has compound fertilizer, so that                the compound fertilizer has the characteristics of                accurate and comprehensive fertilization, the                utilization rate of the fertilizer is improved, and                the crop yield is improved; and related degradation                agent so that the product is rapidly and naturally                degraded, and the cleaning cost after use is                reduced. The product is safe to store, and good                storage stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing one-component polyurethane foam joint                mixture, which involves: proportionally mixing                deltamethrin and permethrin according to the weight                part ratio, and microencapsulating to obtain                nanometer microcapsule mainly containing pyrethroid                compounds; mixing the ground camphor, marigold and                geranium with the prepared nano-grade microcapsules                according to the weight part ratio to prepare the                pesticide; mixing urea formaldehyde, ammonium                polyphosphate and potassium polyphosphate according                to the weight part ratio, grinding, and carrying                out polyethylene coating treatment to obtain a                long-acting fertilizer; sequentially adding                environment-friendly paraffin, mixed polyether,                silicone oil, a catalyst, a pesticide, a                degradation agent, a long-acting fertilizer and an                aging color indicator into a reaction kettle                according to the weight part ratio, and uniformly                stirring to obtain combined polyether;                proportionally mixing polyether, polymeric MDI,                propellant and carbon dioxide, sequentially adding                into a pressure-resistant tank, and uniformly                vibrating to obtain the final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mixed polyether                  is a mixture of Softcel U-1000 (RTM: Polyether                  polyol), Arcol1150 (RTM: Polyether polyol) and                  PolyTHF250 (RTM: Polytetrahydrofurandiol) at                  ratio of 3-5: 6-8: 6-9. The environmentally                  friendly paraffin is liquid paraffin and the                  major component is 14-17C alkane. The catalyst is                  dimorpholinodiethyl ether,                  1,4-dimethylpiperazine, and                  tetramethylhexamethylenediamine; the silicone oil                  is foam stabilizers DC5357, DC5598 and DC 5564.                  The pesticide is camphor: marigold: geranium:                  pyrethroid compound-based nano-scale microcapsule                  at ratio of 0.5-0.75: 0.5-1: 0.75-1: 1-2; the                  pyrethroid-based nano-scale microcapsules are                  deltamethrin: permethrin at ratio of 0.5-1:                  0.5-1. The degradation agent is plastics                  additives of dicumyl peroxide (DCP)596, DCP542                  and DCP 381. The aging color indicator is at                  least one of photochromic powders of No. MC12,                  MC19, and MC22.The polymeric MDI is an isocyanate                  containing a different functionality; and the                  propellant is at least one of                  1,1,1,3,3-pentafluoropropane, HFC-1234 and                  1,1,1,2,3,3,3-heptafluoropropane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The long-acting                  fertilizer is a mixture of urea formaldehyde,                  ammonium polyphosphate and potassium                  polyphosphate at ratio of (0.5-1.5): 0.5-1.5:                  0.5-1.5.

PN CN113996279-A
TI Composite catalyst used for carbon dioxide            thermochemical catalytic hydrogenation to prepare            methanol, comprises indium oxide and graphene oxide in            specific amount.
AU YU G
   LV P
   WANG J
   BAI Y
   SONG X
   SHI Y
   SU W
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 2022258441
AB    NOVELTY - Composite catalyst comprises 85-98 wt.% indium                oxide (In2O3), and 2-15 wt.% graphene oxide (GO)                based on 100 wt.% of total weight.
   USE - Composite catalyst used for carbon dioxide                thermochemical catalytic hydrogenation to prepare                methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst has a more significant                inhibitory effect on side reactions at low                temperature, the addition of GO reduces the size of                the catalyst, and also converts portion of the                cubic phase In2O3 into the hexagonal phase,                increases the relative concentration of surface                oxygen vacancies to obtain high CO2 conversion rate                of 29.4%, and a methanol selectivity of up to 98.3%                in the hydrogenation reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for                following:
   (1) a method for preparing the composite                catalyst, which involves preparing GO into a                solution, mixing the solution with deionized water,                and carrying out ultrasonic treatment, where                preferable treatment time is 0.5-3 hours. The                deionized water is added into metal indium salt for                dissolving to obtain a metal indium salt aqueous                solution. The metal indium salt aqueous solution is                mixed with the GO solution obtained after                ultrasonic treatment, and carried out ultrasonic                treatment for 5-60 minutes, then stirred and mixed                the obtained mixture and urea to form a                precipitate, where metallic indium salt is                preferably indium nitrate and/or indium chloride.                The obtained precipitate is filtered, washed with                deionized water for multiple times, preferably 3-5                times, preferably washed with deionized water,                ethanol, propanol or mixture for the last time                until pH of the system is 7, and filtered to obtain                solid. The vacuum drying is carried out on the                obtained solid to obtain a powder sample, and then                roasted the powder sample.
   (2) an application of composite catalyst for                carbon dioxide (CO2) thermochemical catalytic                hydrogenation to prepare methanol, which involves                loading In2O3-GO composite catalyst on the fixed                bed reactor, and pre-processing the composite                catalyst on the reactor in an inert gas atmosphere.                The system is cooled at 150-200degrees Celsius, and                then introduced reaction raw material gas and the                temperature is raised to carry out the catalytic                reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The composite                  catalyst preferably comprises 88-94 wt.% In2O3,                  and 6-12 wt.% GO. The mass ratio of GO and indium                  nitrate is 1:120-1:10. The molar ratio of urea                  and indium nitrate is 5:1-20:1. The mass                  concentration of the GO solution obtained after                  ultrasonic treatment is 0.02-2 wt.%. The mass                  concentration of the indium nitrate aqueous                  solution is 0.5-5 wt.%. Preferred Components: The                  metallic indium salt is indium nitrate. Preferred                  Conditions: The mixing temperature is                  60-120degrees Celsius, and the mixing time is                  8-24 hours. The vacuum drying temperature is                  40-100degrees Celsius, and the vacuum drying time                  is 6-15 hours. The process is carried out under                  the condition of inert atmosphere, preferably                  nitrogen (N2) or argon (Ar), at heating rate of                  3-10degrees Celsius/min to 300-400degrees Celsius                  for 2-5 hours. The catalytic reaction process                  conditions includes reaction feed gas is carbon                  dioxide (CO2) and hydrogen (H2) in molar ratio in                  the reaction raw material is 6/1, and its flow                  rate is 5-12 mL/min, and/or airspeed is 3000-7000                  cm3 h-1 gcat-1, the pressure in the reactor tube                  is increased to 2-6 MPa, the reaction temperature                  is 200-450degrees Celsius, gradually increased                  the temperature from 200degrees Celsius, and the                  heating rate is 3degrees Celsius/min. The                  composite catalyst is mixed with quartz sand and                  diluted, and the addition of quartz sand is                  0.1-0.3 g. The loading of composite catalyst on                  the reactor is 0.02-0.5 g. The fixed bed reactor                  is a quartz tube reactor with an inner diameter                  of 6 mm reaction tube. The pretreatment process                  conditions includes inert gas is selected from                  Ar, N2 or He, preferably N2, preferably N2 flow                  rate is 80-90 mL/min, the pretreatment                  temperature is 250-350degrees Celsius, and the                  pretreatment time is 1-2 hours.

   USE - The method is useful for promoting hair regeneration with adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell factor complex.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can mass-produce complex factor; and greatly improves the production speed of the complex factor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Promoting hair regeneration with adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell factor complex comprises (i) collecting fat samples, (ii) isolating and purifying stem cells, isolating and purifying adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells in the collected fat samples, (iii) planting and culturing stem cells, purifying the obtained adipose-derived mesenchymal cells, inoculating and culturing with medium, planting the adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells in the cell factory, subculturing for 3-4 times, obtaining sufficient adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells, inoculating adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells into the cell factory, where the number of cells is 60,000/ml, changing the growth environment of adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells to promote the secretion of cytokines, culturing adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells to about 50% under the growth environment of 37degrees Celsius and 5% carbon dioxide, culturing adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells to about 90% under the growth environment of 5% oxygen, 5% carbon dioxide, and 90% nitrogen at 37degrees Celsius, and then the culturing the supernatant, and (iv) collecting and extracting secretions, performing high-speed centrifugation through a centrifuge, and then filtering to finally obtain compound factors.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the process of collecting and extracting the secretion, the secretion obtained by culture at least comprises vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF), keratinocyte growth factor (KGF), hepatocyte growth factor (HGH), transforming growth factor beta (TGF-beta), insulin-like growth factor (IGF) and platelet-derived growth factor (PDGF). The process of separating and purifying stem cells comprises (a) cleaning fat sample by cleaning the blood in the fat sample, (b) centrifuging the fat sample, and removing the supernatant, (c) cutting the fat sample, (d) adding collagenase to digest the fat sample, (e) filtering the digested mixture into a centrifuge tube for centrifugation, (f) washing and centrifuging again, removing the supernatant, washing the cell precipitate, and performing centrifugation again, and (g) dispersing cell pellet in phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), and then filtering and centrifuging.
