AU GUO K
   LIU B
   LI Z
   TONG H
   GAO L
   QU Y
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202192651M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate comprises preparing squaraamide anion catalyst by reacting an organic strong alkali with a squaraamide i.e. 3-amino-4-(benzylamino)cyclobut-3-ene-1,2-dione derivative (I), then carrying out cycloaddition reaction of epoxide compound (II) and carbon dioxide under the action of squaraamide anion catalyst to obtain final product, where the strong organic alkali is 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene, 1,4-diazabicyclo(2.2. 2)octane, 7-methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene, triazabicyclodecene, 2-tert-butylimino-2-diethylamino-1,3-dimethylperhydro-1,3,2-diazaphosphorine, tert-Butylimino-tri(pyrrolidino)phosphorane (BTPP), tri-tert-butylphosphine (t-BuP2) and phosphazene base (t-BuP4).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes catalyst, which does not contain metals and halogens, is easily available, has excellent chemical stability and higher catalytic efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cyclic carbonate comprises preparing squaraamide anion catalyst by reacting an organic strong alkali with a squaraamide i.e. 3-amino-4-(benzylamino)cyclobut-3-ene-1,2-dione derivative of formula (I), then carrying out cycloaddition reaction of epoxide compound of formula (II) and carbon dioxide under the action of squaraamide anion catalyst to obtain final product, where the strong organic alkali is 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene, 1,4-diazabicyclo(2.2. 2)octane, 7-methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)dec-5-ene, triazabicyclodecene, 2-tert-butylimino-2-diethylamino-1,3-dimethylperhydro-1,3,2-diazaphosphorine, tert-Butylimino-tri(pyrrolidino)phosphorane (BTPP), tri-tert-butylphosphine (t-BuP2) and phosphazene base (t-BuP4).
   n = 0 or 1;
   R1 = phenyl group or benzyl group (both are mono- or poly-substituted with T) or 1-6C branched or non-branched alkyl group;
   T = fluoro, methoxy, or trifluoromethyl;
   R2 and R3 = H, 1-4C branched or straight chain alkyl, enbutyl, phenyl, halogen or alkyl substituted phenyl, chlorine or bromine substituted alkyl, R4-O-CH2-; and
   R4 = phenyl, phenyl substituted 1-3C alkyl, allyl or 1-4C branched or straight chain alkyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound (I) is 3-(benzylamino)-4-(2-hydroxybenzylamino)cyclobut-3-ene-1,2-dione, 3-(benzylamino)-4-(3-hydroxybenzylamino)cyclobut-3-ene-1,2-dione, 3-(benzylamino)-4-(4-hydroxybenzylamino)cyclobut-3-ene-1,2-dione, 3-(3-hydroxyphenylamino)-4-(phenylamino)cyclobut-3-ene-1,2-dione and 3-(3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)phenylamino)-4-(3-hydroxyphenylamino)cyclobut-3-ene-1,2-dione; the organic alkali is 2,3,4,6,7,8,9,10-octahydropyrimido(1,2-a)azepine, 1-methyl-2,3,4,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido(1,2-a)pyrimidine, 2,3,4,6,7,8-hexahydro-1H-pyrimido(1,2-a)pyrimidine, tert-butyl(tris(pyrrolidin-1-yl)-lambdal(5)-phosphanylidene)amine, 2,3-N',3-N',3-N,3-N,5-N',5-N',5-N,5-N,7,7-undecamethyl-2,4,6-triaza-3lambdal(5),5lambdal(5)-diphosphaocta-3,5-diene-3,3,5,5-tetramine, 5-(tert-butylimino)-2,3-N',3-N',3-N,3-N,7-N',7-N',7-N,7-N,8-decamethyl-5-N-(tris(dimethylamino)-lambdal(5)-phosphanylidene)-2,4,6,8-tetraaza-3lambdal(5)-phospha-5lambdal(5),7lambdal(5)-diphosphanona-3,6-diene-3,3,5,7,7-pentamine. The compound (II) is 3-chloro-1,2-propylene oxide, 3-bromo-1,2-propylene oxide, 1,2-epoxyhexane, 1,2-epoxy-5-hexene, 4-chlorostyrene epoxide, 2-phenyl-ethylene oxide, 2-(methoxymethyl) ethylene oxide, 2-(tert-butoxymethyl) ethylene oxide, 1-allyloxy-2,3-epoxypropane, 1,2-epoxy-3-phenoxypropane or 2-((2-methylphenoxy)methyl)oxirane. Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) introducing (I), strong organic alkali and (II) to the reaction vessel, stirring at room temperature for 5-10 minutes, then replacing the air in the reaction vessel with carbon dioxide, (b) filling the reaction vessel with carbon dioxide to an initial pressure of 0.1 MPa and heating to 100-120 degrees C (c) reacting for 1-12 hours, cooling, passing the reaction solution through column chromatography to obtain final product. The reaction temperature of the preparation method is 20-140 degrees C, the initial pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.1-2 MPa and the ratio of (I) and strong organic alkali is 1/1-5/1. The reaction temperature of the preparation method is 100-120 degrees C, the initial pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.1 MPa and the ratio of (I) and organic alkali is 1/1-3/1. The squaraamide anion catalyst is prepared by (i) introducing amine compound of formula (R1NH2) (III) into methanol solution of diethyl squarate and reacting at room temperature, (ii) adding hydroxyaniline, hydroxybenzylamine or methoxybenzylamine into the methanol solution of the product obtained in step (i), reacting at room temperature, processing the reaction to obtain squaraamide and (iii) taking the epoxide (III) as the solvent medium and reacting squaraamide obtained in step (ii) with the strong organic base at room temperature to obtain final product.
   Preferred Definitions:
   T = 4-methoxy, 4-trifluoromethyl or 3,5-bistrifluoromethyl;
   R1 = isopropyl, n-butyl, cyclohexyl, phenyl, benzyl, 3,5-bis(trifluoromethyl)phenyl or 4-trifluoromethylbenzyl;
   R1 = benzyl;
   n = 1;
   R2 and R3 = H, n-butyl, halomethyl, phenyl, halophenyl, R4-O-CH2-; and
   R4 = phenyl, tolyl, allyl, tert-butyl or methyl.

PN CN113185626-A
TI Preparing high-lubricity oxidized polyethylene wax involves taking waxy low molecular weight polyethylene low polymer as raw material, liquid carbon dioxide as solvent, and monohydric short-chain alcohol as precipitating agent.
AU HONG Y
   WU W
   HU P
   YAN M
AE JIANGXI HONGYUAN CHEM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202190177L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-lubricity oxidized polyethylene wax involves taking waxy low molecular weight polyethylene low polymer as a raw material, liquid carbon dioxide as a solvent, and monohydric short-chain alcohol as a precipitating agent to perform reaction under reaction temperature condition to get low molecular weight polyethylene wax products with different average relative molecular mass grades through dissolution, concentration, precipitation, separation and drying. The oxidized polyethylene wax with high lubricating property is prepared under condition of no catalyst and oxygen as oxidant, where the polyethylene wax is prepared by oxidizing hydrogen atom of ethylene containing adjacent double bonds and tertiary hydrogen atom containing branched chain in the polyethylene wax structure.
   USE - Method for preparing high-lubricity oxidized polyethylene wax used as a lubricant, coating agent and dispersing agent for preparing polyvinyl chloride products, color master batches, inorganic nano fillers and pigments (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the high-lubricity oxidized polyethylene wax in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with good polarity, good compatibility, ideal lubricating and dispersing effects, small influence on the environment and the like, and higher market economic value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-lubricity oxidized polyethylene wax involves taking waxy low molecular weight polyethylene low polymer as a raw material, liquid carbon dioxide as a solvent, and monohydric short-chain alcohol as a precipitating agent to perform reaction under reaction temperature condition to get low molecular weight polyethylene wax products with different average relative molecular mass grades through dissolution, concentration, precipitation, separation and drying. The oxidized polyethylene wax with high lubricating property is prepared under condition of no catalyst and oxygen as oxidant, where the polyethylene wax is prepared by oxidizing hydrogen atom of ethylene containing adjacent double bonds and tertiary hydrogen atom containing branched chain in the polyethylene wax structure. The low molecular weight polyethylene wax is uniformly mixed with a solvent, added the mixture into a high-pressure reaction kettle for reaction, and controlled at 170-185 degrees C for 10-36 hours to prepare the high-lubricity oxidized polyethylene wax.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent liquid carbon dioxide is supercritical carbon dioxide. The precipitator is 2-4C monobasic short-chain alcohol. The low polymer of the waxy low molecular polyethylene is a polyethylene wax having a relative molecular mass of 2000.

TI Synthesis of cyclosporin derivative involves            premixing precursor fluid containing amide-containing            compound and alkaline fluid, adding premixed liquid            into reaction chamber, reacting reaction liquid with            carbon dioxide fluid, and then reacting with            chloroethanecarboperoxoyl chloride solution.
AU YANG Z

   LI F
   JIANG C
   GONG J

AE UNIV PEKING SHENZHEN GRADUATE SCHOOL (UYPK-C)

GA 202188597R
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of a cyclosporin derivative (I)                involves premixing the precursor fluid containing                amide-containing compound (II) and the alkaline                fluid to obtain a premixed liquid, adding the                premixed liquid into the reaction chamber (C1) for                reaction to prepare a reaction liquid (L1), adding                the reaction liquid (L1) into the reaction chamber                (C2) and reacting with the carbon dioxide fluid to                prepare a reaction liquid (L2), collecting the                reaction liquid (L2), and reacting the reaction                liquid (L2) with 2-chloroethanecarboperoxoyl                chloride solution.
   USE - Synthesis of cyclosporin derivative (I).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, safe, and enables                synthesis of cyclosporin derivative with high                yield, and high reproducibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of a cyclosporin derivative of                formula (I) involves premixing the precursor fluid                containing amide-containing compound of formula                (II) and the alkaline fluid to obtain a premixed                liquid, adding the premixed liquid into the                reaction chamber (C1) for reaction to prepare a                reaction liquid (L1), adding the reaction liquid                (L1) into the reaction chamber (C2) and reacting                with the carbon dioxide fluid to prepare a reaction                liquid (L2), collecting the reaction liquid (L2),                and reacting the reaction liquid (L2) with                2-chloroethanecarboperoxoyl chloride                solution.
   A = -CH=CHR, -CH=CH-CH=CHR or -CH2CH2R;
   R = -CH3, -CH2SH, -CH2S-R1, -CH2-COOR1',                -COOR1', -R2-COOR1' or CH2-R2-COOR1';
   R1 = 1-6C alkyl;
   R2 = 1-6C alkoxy;
   R1' = H, ammonium salt or 1-6C alkyl;
   B' = -CH3, -CH2CH3, -CH(OH)CH3, -CH(CH3)2 or                -CH2CH2CH3;
   C' = -H, -CH3, -CH2CH3 or -CH2CH2CH3;
   D' = -CH2CH(CH3)2, -CH2C(OH)(CH3)2,                -CH(CH3)CH2CH3 or                -CH2CH2-(4-(2-CH3OCH2CH2)-1-R3);and
   R3 = piperazinyl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for synthesis                of 1,3-substituted cyclosporin derivatives of                formula (III), which involves synthesizing                cyclosporine-H derivative of formula (IV), reacting                compound (IV) with olefin compound of formula:                H2C=CH-R (a) under olefin metathesis reaction to                prepare                N,4-dimethyl-2-(methylamino)pentanamide-containing                intermediate of formula (V), hydrogenating compound                (V) to prepare hydrogenated                N,4-dimethyl-2-(methylamino)pentanamide-containing                intermediate of formula (VI), and hydrogenating                compound (VI).
   R4 = -CH2CH2CH2NH(C=O)CH3, -CH2CH2CH2COOH or                group chosen from groups of formulae (b)-(d).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali in the                  alkaline fluid is chosen from lithium                  diisopropylamide, lithium bistrimethylsilylamide,                  and sodium bis(trimethylsilyl)amide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves                  reacting reaction liquid (L1) with the                  2-chloroethanecarboperoxoyl chloride solution to                  collect the reaction liquid (L2) into the                  2-chloroethanecarboperoxoyl chloride solution,                  maintaining the temperature of the                  2-chloroethanecarboperoxoyl chloride solution at                  (-55 degrees C)-(-45 degrees C) during the                  collection process, warming the reaction solution                  naturally to room temperature and reacting for                  3-20 hours. The method further involves reacting                  reaction liquid (L1) with the                  2-chloroethanecarboperoxoyl chloride solution,                  and post-processing the resulting reactant by                  adding water to the reactant and adjusting the pH                  of the reactant to 7-8, concentrating under                  reduced pressure, extracting with ethyl acetate,                  collecting the organic phase, and purifying by                  column chromatography. Preferred Components: The                  solvent of the precursor fluid, the alkaline                  fluid and the 2-chloroethanecarboperoxoyl                  chloride solution is chosen from                  2-methyltetrahydrofuran, tetrahydrofuran,                  n-hexane, methyl tert-butyl ether and diethyl                  ether. The mobile phase used in the column                  chromatography is dichloromethane and methanol                  with a volume ratio of (15-25):1. The precursor                  is cyclosporin A. Preferred Composition: The                  molar concentration of                  2-chloroethanecarboperoxoyl chloride in the                  2-chloroethanecarboperoxoyl chloride solution is                  0.3-0.7 M. Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of                  the input precursor fluid is 0.03-0.06 mM/minute.                  The flow rate of inputting the alkaline fluid is                  0.1-0.5 mM/minute. The temperature at which the                  precursor fluid and the alkaline fluid are fed                  into the reaction chamber (C1) for reaction is                  (-5 degrees C)-30 degrees C for 50-90 seconds.                  The flow rate of the carbon dioxide fluid is 5-12                  mL/minute. The temperature at which the reaction                  liquid (L1) is input into the reaction chamber                  (C2) to react with the carbon dioxide fluid is                  (-20 degrees C)-30 degrees C for 3-20                  seconds.

PN KR2021058319-A
TI Catalyst-free thermal combustion oxidizer for improved water quality analysis equipment, has shaft rotation device that is provided with shaft rotation gear is connected to power transmission device by motor on lower outer surface of tube.
AU KIM Y R
AE KIM Y R (KIMY-Individual)
GA 202158958H
AB    NOVELTY - The oxidizer has a ceramic reactor (20), a protection tube (10) which is a cylindrical ceramic pipe having an outer wall. Multiple ceramic balls are inserted into inside of the protection tube. A sheave has multiple holes perforated so that gas vaporized by a heating coil is easily discharged to the outside of reactor. A furnace includes the heating coil for controlling the temperature in the protection tube 10-1,200 plus minus 200 degrees C, and a temperature control device for supplying current to the heating coil. A catalyst-free thermal combustion gas converter converts sample water input into protection tube into carbon dioxide or nitrogen oxide gas. A furnace inclination fixing device (70) installs the reactor and furnace. A shaft rotation device (60) is provided with a shaft rotation gear (61) is connected to a power transmission device (62) by a motor (63) on the lower outer surface of the protection tube to pivot the protection tube inside the ceramic reactor.
   USE - Catalyst-free thermal combustion oxidizer for improved water quality analysis equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxidizer rotates the rotationally fixed protection tube with an inclination of 25 to 350 degrees based on a vertical line and uses multiple ceramic balls of the same diameter, thus generating carbon dioxide from the protection tube. By solving the vortex phenomenon of gas, it is possible to obtain more accurate and consistent non-dispersive infrared sensor measurement values by making the carbon dioxide gas generated from the sample more uniform. As the diameter of the gas outlet is increased, the clogging caused by salt and impurities can be prevented.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a use state of a non-catalytic thermal combustion oxidation device for an improved water quality analysis device. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Protection tube (10)
   Ceramic reactor (20)
   Shaft rotation device (60)
   Shaft rotation gear (61)
   Power transmission device (62)
   Motor (63)
   Furnace inclination fixing device (70)

PN CN111170915-A
TI Extracting astaxanthin, by crushing meat and soft tissues, adding methylene chloride solution to raw powder, extracting, combining leaching solutions, subjecting crude astaxanthin extract to column chromatography purification and eluting.
AU YU H
   ZHANG X
   CHEN J
   TAO H
AE FOSHAN DINGKE TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 202045390A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting astaxanthin, involves (a) removing the meat and soft tissues from aquatic product, washing with clean water and distilled water, freeze-drying and crushing, and sieving to obtain raw powder, (b) adding 8% methylene chloride solution to the raw powder, extracting, filtering to obtain a leaching solution, repeatedly extracting for 2 times and combining the leaching solutions, (c) heating and evaporating to remove methylene chloride in vacuum and concentrating the extract to an oily form, (d) subjecting the oily form to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and extracting, and (e) subjecting the crude astaxanthin extract to column chromatography purification on a silica gel solid phase chromatography column, using petroleum ether and ethyl acetate as eluents, and eluting and recovering with dichloromethane to obtain astaxanthin fine-extract sample.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting astaxanthin.
   ADVANTAGE - The astaxanthin has high yield and high purity. The method enhances leaching effect and involves removing various impurities in a variety of marine products to improve purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting astaxanthin, involves (a) removing the meat and soft tissues from aquatic product, washing with clean water and distilled water for 5-10 minutes, freeze-drying and crushing, reverting to normal temperature and sieving by a 40 mesh sieve to obtain raw powder, (b) adding 8% methylene chloride solution to the raw powder in a solid-liquid volume ratio of 1:8, extracting for 30 minutes, filtering to obtain a leaching solution, repeatedly extracting for 2 times and combining the leaching solutions, (c) heating and evaporating to remove methylene chloride in vacuum and concentrating the extract to an oily form, (d) subjecting the oily form to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and extracting at 40 degrees C for 2 hours with a pressure of 28 MPa and a flow rate of carbon dioxide of 30 l/hour, and (e) subjecting the crude astaxanthin extract to column chromatography purification on a silica gel solid phase chromatography column, using petroleum ether and ethyl acetate as eluents, and eluting and recovering with dichloromethane to obtain astaxanthin fine-extract sample.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aquatic product comprises fresh shrimp, crab or oyster. Preferred Method: The step (b) involves extracting involving ultrasonically treating with a power of 300 W for 30 second/gap 1 minute and the total duration of 10 minutes. The step of heating and evaporation in step (c) is carried out by adopting rotary heating at 35 degrees C. The step (d) involves adding absolute ethanol to the oily form in a volume ratio of 1:1 to dissolve the sample and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction. The step (e) involves using rotary heating at 35 degrees C to evaporate methylene chloride.

PN FR3082002-A1
TI Determining model of composition of exhaust gases of diesel engine involves measuring composition of exhaust gases (carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrocarbons, nitrogen oxides and oxygen) on set of engine operating points.
AU CHARLETTE F
   MALET R
AE PSA AUTOMOBILES SA (CITR-C)
GA 2019A20887
AB    NOVELTY - Determining a model of a composition of exhaust gases of a diesel engine, comprises measuring the composition of the exhaust gases (carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrocarbons, nitrogen oxides and oxygen) on a set of engine operating points being defined by a set of parameters of operating input (Xi), determining, from the values of the chemical parameters obtained for the operating points, a quadratic model of these chemical parameters as a function of the operating parameters (Xi), as given in the specification, and determining the exhaust gas composition model by replacing the chemical parameters in the expression of the exhaust gas composition by their quadratic model.
   USE - The method is useful for determining a model of a composition of exhaust gases of a diesel engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the number of required tests.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determining a model of a composition of exhaust gases of a diesel engine, comprises measuring the composition of the exhaust gases (carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, hydrocarbons, nitrogen oxides and oxygen) on a set of engine operating points being defined by a set of parameters of operating input (Xi), determining, from the values of the chemical parameters obtained for the operating points, a quadratic model of these chemical parameters as a function of the operating parameters (Xi), as given in the specification, and determining the exhaust gas composition model by replacing the chemical parameters in the expression of the exhaust gas composition by their quadratic model. For each of the operating points, the equilibrium coefficients (n, a, b, d, e, f, g, h, i, t) of a combustion balance equation (1) predetermined are determined from exhaust gas composition measurements as given in the specification. Chemical parameters are determined for each operating point as a function of these equilibrium coefficients (n, a, b, d, e, f, g, h, i, t) and the balance equation (1) of predetermined combustion, in which the combustion reaction is seen as the sum of a rich zone and a lean combustion zone, and the equilibrium coefficients are expressed as a function of these chemical parameters as per se. The composition of the exhaust gases is expressed as a function of these chemical parameters as given in the specification.

PN FR3081047-A1; FR3081047-B1
TI Extracting nitrogen from natural gas feed stream comprises introducing stream feed gas in refrigeration unit, introducing gas stream in phase separator means, introducing gas stream into cryogenic separation and recovering liquid stream.
AU KHAN O
   TERRIEN P
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 201995414L
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting nitrogen from a natural gas feed stream comprising at least nitrogen, methane, carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide, comprises: (a) introducing the stream feed gas in a refrigeration unit comprising at least one main exchanger, where the gas stream is condensed at least partially; (b) introducing the gas stream from the step (a) in a phase separator means at a pressure P to produce a gas stream and a liquid stream; (c) introducing the gas stream from the step (b) at a pressure P1 into a cryogenic separation means comprising at least one distillation column for separating the nitrogen from the gas stream; and (d) recovering a liquid stream enriched with methane obtained from the cryogenic separation.
   USE - The process is useful for extracting nitrogen from a natural gas feed stream comprising at least nitrogen, methane, carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process minimizes the number of rotating machines (one pump instead of two).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting nitrogen from a natural gas feed stream comprising at least nitrogen, methane, carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide, comprises: (a) introducing the stream feed gas in a refrigeration unit comprising at least one main exchanger, where the gas stream is condensed at least partially; (b) introducing the gas stream from the step (a) in a phase separator means at a pressure P to produce a gas stream and a liquid stream; (c) introducing the gas stream from the step (b) at a pressure P1 into a cryogenic separation means comprising at least one distillation column for separating the nitrogen from the gas stream; and (d) recovering a liquid stream enriched with methane obtained from the cryogenic separation, where the P is greater than P1 which is greater than 20 bars absolute and in that in step d), recovering the liquid stream enriched in methane from the cryogenic separation by pumping the bottom product of the column and/or pumping the liquid stream from step (b) to a pressure P2 greater than 25 bar absolute and preferably greater than the critical pressure of the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process: the liquid stream from step (c) is pumped and then mixed with the liquid stream from step (b), the mixture thus obtained is then pumped at a pressure P2 greater than 25 absolute bars and preferably greater than the critical pressure of the mixture; the liquid stream resulting from step (b) and the liquid stream resulting from step (c) are pumped independently of each other at a pressure P2 greater than 25 bar absolute and preferably higher than the critical pressure of each liquid product, then these streams are introduced into the main exchanger and mixed downstream of the main heat exchanger; the operating temperature of step (b) is greater than the gel temperature of the carbon dioxide and/or the hydrogen sulfide of the composition of the gas stream, at the pressure P; the feed stream is not subjected to a pre-treatment step for reducing the molar concentration of carbon dioxide and/or the hydrogen sulfide below 0.1 mol.%; the refrigeration unit is fed by an external refrigeration cycle in which circulates a refrigerant fluid in a closed loop; and the refrigeration cycle comprises compression of the feed gas, then cooling the compressed gas in the main heat exchanger, relaxing at least a portion of the cooled gas, and vaporization of the previously relaxed product. Preferred Components: The feed stream comprises at least 0.1 mol.% of carbon dioxide and/or at least 0.1 mol.% of hydrogen sulfide. The pressure P2 is greater than 40 bar absolute. The coolant is methane, nitrogen, a mixture of the two or hydrocarbons having more than 2C atoms.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110438068-A; CN110438068-B
TI Follicular maturation culturing solution useful for improving in vitro fertilized embryo development rate in river buffalo, comprises follicular fluid.
AU PANG C
   LIANG X
   CHEN M
   LIANG S
   MA X
   TAN Z
   LU X
   DUAN A
   DENG T
AE GUANGXI ZHUANG AUTONOMOUS REGION INST (GUAG-C)
   GUANGXI ZHUANG AUTONOMOUS REGION INST (GUAG-C)
GA 201996829D
AB    NOVELTY - Follicular maturation culturing solution comprises 1-4 %mass follicular fluid.
   USE - The follicular maturation culturing solution is useful for improving in vitro fertilized embryo development rate in river buffalo.
   ADVANTAGE - The culturing solution improves buffalo oocyte maturation, improves division rate of blastula in buffalo fertilized eggs, improves in vitro embryo development rate and production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of preparing the above-mentioned solution, which involves (i) performing in vitro culture of egg collection in slaughterhouse by collect buffalo ovaries, removing excess ovarian tissue, washing with 2-3 times normal saline containing double antibodies, sucking the follicles using syringe, selecting uniform cytoplasm and extracellular cells under stereo microscope and oocytes with 3 layers or more granular cells, washing 2 or 3 times, placing in mature solution A, culturing at 39 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide saturated air humidity incubator, culturing for 22-24 hours, removing cumulus cells surrounding the oocytes, washing twice with mature solution A, suspending to make microdrops, covering with paraffin and culturing at 39 degrees C in 5% carbon dioxide saturated air humidity culture in a box, fermenting buffalo sperm and oocytes for fertilization and in vitro embryo culture, recording the development of each embryo, collecting the blastocysts developed to 7th day and recording the total number of blastocyst cells using microscope, and (ii) performing in vitro culture of live egg collection by collecting live buffalo eggs in vitro, collecting oocytes, and calcifying odontogenic cyst (COC) with homogeneous cytoplasm and wrapping in one layer or more cumulus cells selected under the stereo microscope, washing 2 times with 20 mmol/l 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazineethanesulfonic acid (Hepes) in TCMl99 solution, washing once with mature solution B, transfering to 50 mu l/drop mature solution B, aging in an incubator at 39 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide having maximum relative humidity for 22-24 hours, fertilizing the buffalo sperm after hatching using in vitro embryo culture, recording the development of each embryo, collecting the blastocysts developed to 7th day and recording the total number of blastocyst cells using microscope.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The mature culture solution comprises a mature solution A used for in vitro culture of egg collection in a slaughterhouse and/or a mature solution B used for in vitro culture of live egg collection. Preferred Component: The follicular fluid is subjected to amino acid chelation treatment. The mature solution A is prepared by taking 50 ml basal solution, adding 200 mu l follicle stimulating hormone (FSH)-luteinizing hormone (LH) storage solution, 45 mu L estradiol (E2) storage solution, 100 mu L epidermal growth factor (EGF) solution, 50 mu l cysteamine storage solution to 1-4 %mass CM base solution. The CM base solution is prepared by (i) preparing Cm-stock solution by weighing 2.45-3.65 g TCM199, 0.18-0.30g sodium bicarbonate, 1-2 mg sodium pyruvate, 35-45 mu l 70% sodium lactate and 18-22 mg penicillin, 17-27 mg streptomycin, 9-13 mg phenol red, and dissolving components in 200 ml saline and mixing to obtain Cm-stock solution, and (ii) preparing CM base liquid by dissolving 15-25 ml fetal bovine serum in 50 ml Cm-stock solution. The FSH-LH storage solution is prepared by dissolving 120-150 ug FSH in 10 ml saline to obtain a follicular estrogen solution, dissolving 2.11-3.04 mg LH in FSH solution, mixing and dividing into 10 mu l/tube, storing at 20 degrees C. The E2 storage solution is prepared by dissolving 2.01-2.43 mg estradiol in 1 ml ethanol, mixing and dividing into 10 mu l/tube at -20 degrees C. The EGF solution is prepared by dissolving EGF into solution with 10-18 ng/ml solute content using distilled water. The cysteamine storage solution is prepared by weighing 7-7.5 mg cysteamine and dissolving in 10 mL CM base solution, dividing into 100 mu l/tube, and storing at -20 degrees C. The mature solution B comprises 8% fetal bovine serum, 10 mu l/ml FSH, 15 mu g/ml LH, 5 mu g/ml estradiol, 0.9 mmol/l sodium pyruvate, 100 mu mol/l cysteine, 35 ng/ml EGF, 1-4 %mass follicular fluid, and remainder of TCM199 solution. Preferred Method: The amino acid chelation treatment involves dissolving cysteine, methionine, alanine, lysine, tryptophan, and glycine in TCM199 solution so that the final concentration of cysteine is 500-700 mu mol/l, methionine is 300-500 mu mol/l, alanine is 100-300 mu mol/l, lysine is 100-300 mu mol/l, tryptophan is 50-100 mu mol/l and glycine is 50-150 mu mol/l, mixing to obtain follicular fluid in a mass ratio of 1:1 and culturing with shaking at 39 degrees C.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

AU BU W
   WANG L
AE NANTONG TENAIBO HIGH-TECH MATERIALS CO (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 201940848Q
AB    NOVELTY - Highly active aluminum oxide powder comprises 5-10 pts. wt. aluminum, 10-15 pts. wt. pure water, 2-5 pts. wt. impurity remover, 4-7 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide solution, 15-20 pts. wt. absolute ethanol, 5-10 pts. wt. hydrochloric acid solution, 2-5 pts. wt. diisopropanolamine, 5-10 pts. wt. titanium dioxide, 10-15 pts. wt. aqueous ammonia, 12-15 pts. wt. distilled water, 2-5 pts. wt. hydroxypropyl cellulose, 2-3 pts. wt. hydrofluoric acid and 2-3 pts. wt. carbon dioxide.
   USE - As highly active aluminum oxide powder.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the aluminum oxide powder, which involves melting aluminum to enable molten aluminum to form an aluminum melt, spraying the molten aluminum to pure water through an atomizer to obtain aluminum hydroxide, precipitating the aluminum hydroxide melt, taking out the impurities floating on the surface of the water, filtering the impurities under reduced pressure, washing the filtered material with 3-5 times absolute ethanol, drying the filtered aluminum oxide in an oven, introducing the dried aluminum oxide into a container, adding hydrochloric acid solution, reacting, stirring for 2-5 minutes, adding impurity remover while stirring, so that the stirred solution's solid is not dissolved, filtering, collecting the filtered solution, adding diisopropanolamine, and heating the mixed solution, adding sodium hydroxide solution to the heated solution, adjusting the pH of the solution to 12-13, introducing carbon dioxide, adding hydrochloric acid solution for filtration, cooling the container loaded with the solution, and maintaining the temperature of the adjusted solution at 5-7 degrees C, stirring the cooled solution for 5-10 minutes to make fully contact with air, adding titanium dioxide during the stirring process, so that the titanium dioxide and the solution are fully mixed, and adding hydroxypropyl cellulose, heating, stirring, adding aqueous ammonia, and stirring to enable the solution to be free of colloidal substance, washing the produced colloidal substance with distilled water, drying the washed substance in the oven, subjecting the substance to agglutination, calcining the agglutinated substance for 2-3 hours, and crushing the substance in a ball mill and sieving the crushed powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The hydrochloric acid solution is 37% hydrochloric acid. Preferred Component: The impurity remover is micro-aluminum hydroxide powder. Preferred Property: The purity of the aluminum is at least 99.99%.

TI Kit useful for evaluating immune function of healthy person, infected patient and cancer patient contains toll-like receptor agonist, anti-human cluster of differentiation (CD) 3 antibody, CD28 antibody, and CD40 ligand and interleukin-21.
AU XU J

GA 201939897W
AB    NOVELTY - Kit comprises toll-like receptor agonist, anti-human cluster of differentiation (CD) 3 antibody, and CD28 antibody, and CD40 ligand, a B cell activating factor, interleukin (IL)-21, and IL-4.
   USE - The kit is useful for evaluating immune function of healthy person, infected patient and cancer patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The kit is easy to operate and ensures simultaneously evaluvation of several sample and the immune cell function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for evaluating an immune function using the kit, which involves (a) evaluating innate immunity by (i) isolating peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) from the body to be evaluated is cultured in a cell culture plate, (ii) adding a TLR1/2 agonist, a TLR4 agonist, a TLR9 agonist to the cells, and culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator, and (iii) collecting the culture supernatant, detecting the expression of IL-6, IL-10 and interferon (IFN)- alpha in the culture supernatant, and detecting the concentration of the cytokine by flow cytometry, where evaluation of the body's immune function by detecting the amount of production of these cytokine, (b) evaluating T cell immunity by (i) separating PBMC to be evaluated and culturing in a cell culture plate, (ii) adding anti-human CD3 antibody and anti-human CD28 antibody to the cells, and placing in a carbon dioxide incubator for cultivation and (iii) collecting the culture supernatant, detecting the expression of IL-2, IL-4, IL-17 and IFN- gamma in the culture supernatant, and detecting concentration of cytokines by flow cytometry, evaluating immune function by detecting the amount of production of these cytokines, (C) evaluating ability of B cells to secrete antibody by (i) separating PBMC of the body to be evaluated and culturing in a cell culture plate, (ii) adding CD40L, BAFF, IL-21 and IL-4 to the cells, and placing them in a carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, (iii) collecting the culture supernatant, measuring secretion amount of immunoglobin G (IgG)1 and IgG2 in the culture supernatant and measuring concentration of IgG by flow cytometry, and evaluating immune function by detecting the amount of IgG production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Biomolecule: The toll-like receptor agonist in the kit includes Toll-like receptor (TLR)1/2 agonist, a TLR4 agonist, and a TLR9 agonist, where the above agonists is used singly or in combination. The anti-human CD3 antibody and CD28 antibody in the kit are coated on a magnetic bead or a petri dish for use. The TLR4 agonists includes LPS. The TLR9 agonists includes ODN 2216, ODN 2206, ODN 2243, or ODN 2395. The TLR1/2 agonist in the kit includes LPS-PG, LTA-SA standard, PGN-SA or Pam3CK4. Preferred Kit: The kit evaluates the immune function of the human body, the evaluation indexes are classified into three categories, where a Toll-like receptor agonist is used to evaluate innate immunity, its evaluation indicators areIL-6, IL-10, IFN- alpha , anti-human CD3 and CD28 antibody were used to evaluate T lymphocyte function, and the evaluation indexes were IL-2, IL-4, IL-17a, IFN- gamma , CD40 ligand, B cell activating factor, IL-21 and IL-4 were used to evaluate B lymphocyte function, and the evaluation indexes were IgG1 and IgG2. Preferred Process: The CD40 ligand, B cell activating factor, IL-21 and IL-4 in the kit is secreted by K562 cells transfected with CD40L, BAFF, IL-21 and IL-4 gene.

TI Extracting longicorn larval fat by supercritical method comprises e.g. selecting longicorn larvae, freeze-drying, pulverizing lyophilized larvae, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of larvae powder and centrifugating.
AU PANG J
AE TIANJIN TUOHUA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201928631B
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting longicorn larval fat by supercritical method comprises e.g. (i) selecting longicorn larvae and washing, (ii) adding larvae into vacuum drying oven and freeze-drying under vacuum pressure, (iii) pulverizing lyophilized larvae, and smashing into mesh size of 300-400 mesh, (iv) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of larvae powder, then performing two-stage separation at pressure of 8.5-9.5 MPa, controlling pressure of secondary separation tank at 6.5-8 MPa, temperature of primary separation tank at 40-50 degrees C, temperature of secondary separation tank at 45-55 degrees C and extraction time for 4 h-6 hours, and (v) collecting separated mixture from primary separation tank and secondary separation tank, directly taking isolated primary separation tank mixture as larvae oil, centrifugating secondary separation tank mixture, taking out the upper oil layer, collecting and incorporating into larvae oil primary separation tank and mixing to obtain final product.
   USE - The longicorn larval fat is useful as beauty and health care raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The product: expands the function of larvae in addition to food.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting longicorn larval fat by supercritical method comprises (i) selecting longicorn larvae and washing, (ii) adding larvae into vacuum drying oven and freeze-drying under vacuum pressure of 10-40 Pa at 25 degrees C for 4-6 hours, (iii) pulverizing lyophilized larvae, and smashing into mesh size of 300-400 mesh, (iv) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of larvae powder, then performing two-stage separation at pressure of 8.5-9.5 MPa, controlling pressure of secondary separation tank at 6.5-8 MPa, temperature of primary separation tank at 40-50 degrees C, temperature of secondary separation tank at 45-55 degrees C and extraction time for 4 h-6 hours, and (v) collecting separated mixture from primary separation tank and secondary separation tank, directly taking isolated primary separation tank mixture as larvae oil, centrifugating secondary separation tank mixture at a centrifugal speed of 4000-6000 revolutions/minute for 15 minutes, taking out the upper oil layer, collecting and incorporating into larvae oil primary separation tank and mixing to obtain final product.

PN CN109504478-A
TI Purification of liquefied natural gas involves buffering pressure swing adsorption process hydrogen extraction gas, pressurizing and purifying, converting organic sulfur into inorganic sulfur, introducing analytical gas and dehydrating.
AU WANG C
   ZHU L
   YANG X
   MENG X
AE HEILONGJIANG JIANLONG CHEM IND CO LTD (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 2019286327
AB    NOVELTY - The purification process of liquefied natural gas involves (1) buffering pressure swing adsorption process hydrogen extraction gas, pressurizing hydrogen extraction gas compressor, carrying out hydrodesulfurization reaction, converting organic sulfur into inorganic sulfur using iron-molybdenum catalyst, removing inorganic sulfur using medium-temperature zinc oxide desulfurizing agent, removing total sulfur in the analytical gas to below 1 mg/Nm3, (2) carrying out debenzene purification process using analytical gas, removing benzene and aromatic hydrocarbon in the analytical gas at 10 ppm, introducing analytical gas into temperature swing adsorption tower, adsorbing benzene and aromatic hydrocarbon impurity components in the raw material gas using adsorbent, removing benzene and trace aromatic hydrocarbon in the analytical gas, (3) decarburizing using analytical gas, removing carbon dioxide in the analytical gas and (4) dehydrating analytical gas and then drying and processing.
   USE - The process is useful for purification of liquefied natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enables purification of liquefied natural gas in environmentally-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The purification process of liquefied natural gas involves (1) buffering pressure swing adsorption process hydrogen extraction gas using 10000 m3 wet gas cabinet, pressurizing hydrogen extraction gas compressor under pressure of 1.5 MPa, carrying out hydrodesulfurization reaction, placing in hydrogenation reactor, converting organic sulfur into inorganic sulfur using iron-molybdenum catalyst, removing inorganic sulfur using medium-temperature zinc oxide desulfurizing agent and fine desulfurization tower, removing total sulfur in the analytical gas to below 1 mg/Nm3, (2) carrying out debenzene purification process using analytical gas, removing benzene and aromatic hydrocarbon in the analytical gas using temperature swing adsorption tower and debenzene adsorption adsorber at 10 ppm, introducing analytical gas into temperature swing adsorption tower, adsorbing benzene and aromatic hydrocarbon impurity components in the raw material gas at room temperature using adsorbent, removing benzene and trace aromatic hydrocarbon in the analytical gas using debenzene adsorption adsorber, (3) decarburizing using analytical gas, removing carbon dioxide in the analytical gas under carbon dioxide content of less than 50 PPM and (4) dehydrating analytical gas and then drying using molecular sieve dryer, removing water in the analytical gas using molecular sieve adsorption sieve and then filtering, placing in cryogenic system and processing.

AU RAJENDIRAN V R
   SAKTHIVAL S V
   MUKHERJEE A
   SAKTHIVEL S V
AE HEATCRAFT REFRIGERATION PROD LLC (HEAT-Non-standard)
   HEATCRAFT REFRIGERATION PROD LLC (HEAT-Non-standard)
   HEATCRAFT REFRIGERATION PROD LLC (HEAT-Non-standard)
GA 2018767993
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a gas cooler (130) operable to                cool refrigerant to a first temperature. A heat                exchanger comprises a first refrigerant inlet                coupled to the gas cooler, a second refrigerant                inlet coupled to the gas cooler, a first                refrigerant outlet coupled to a high pressure                expansion valve (135), and a second refrigerant                outlet coupled to the gas cooler. The high pressure                expansion valve is coupled to a flash tank (105)                and operable to reduce a pressure of the                refrigerant flowing from the heat exchanger to the                flash tank. The flash tank is coupled to multiple                refrigeration cases that are coupled to compressors                (120). The compressors are coupled to the gas                cooler. The refrigerant is a carbon dioxide                refrigerant.
   USE - Transcritical refrigeration system for use as                a sub-cooler for circulating a refrigerant i.e.                carbon dioxide refrigerant (all claimed) for                regulating a high ambient temperature environment                in residential and commercial spaces for                maintaining cold temperature for frozen food and                fresh food. Uses include but are not limited to a                fruit, vegetable, egg, milk, and beverages.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures subcooling the high                pressure refrigerant before the high pressure                expansion valve reduces the amount of flash gas and                increases the amount of liquid refrigerant produced                by the high pressure expansion valve. The system                can be efficiently operated in order to avoid power                wastage rate, and utilizes the expanded gas for                subcooling to be compressed so as to reduce the                pressure ratio of the compressor and power                consumption rate. The high pressure expansion valve                is coupled to the flash tank so as to reduce a                pressure of the refrigerant flowing from the heat                exchanger to the flash tank.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                sub-cooler.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a hydraulic circuit diagram                of a transcritical refrigeration system without a                subcooler at different temperatures and pressures                at different locations.

   Flash tank (105)
   Compressors (120)
   Gas cooler (130)
   High pressure expansion valve (135)

PT P
PN US2018243762-A1; US10518286-B2
TI Nozzle assembly for dispersing coolant into vessel through aperture in wall of vessel, has nozzle whose outer surface is extended along length of nozzle spaced from inner surface of housing to create gap when nozzle is coupled to housing.
AU ADAMS W
   BURROWS J
   CORY C
AE AIRGAS USA LLC (AIRG-Non-standard)
GA 201867510D
AB    NOVELTY - The nozzle assembly has housing that is attached to a wall of a vessel. The housing is provided to define a passageway extending along the length of the housing from a proximal end portion to a distal end portion. The passageway is delineated by an inner surface of the housing. A nozzle (150) is provided with a base portion (160) adapted for coupling to the distal end portion of the housing. A shaft portion (170) is set to extend from the base portion. A tip portion (180) is coupled to the shaft portion of the nozzle. The tip portion of the nozzle is set to define several orifices (185). The nozzle is provided with an outer surface extending along a length of the nozzle that is spaced from the inner surface of the housing to create a gap when the nozzle is coupled to the housing.
   USE - Nozzle assembly for dispersing coolant such as liquid carbon dioxide and liquid nitrogen (all claimed) to cool food composition such as poultry, meat, fish into vessel through aperture in wall of vessel.
   ADVANTAGE - The end plate of the housing extends to fit around nozzle while leaving a space suitable to prevent rapid cooling of the end plate, thus preventing undesired freezing of the food substance to the end plate. The effective and efficient dispersion of both the liquid carbon dioxide and the liquid nitrogen is enabled. The tip portion of the nozzle is prevented from contacting the end plate of the housing, thus preventing direct thermal conduction between the tip portion of the nozzle and the end plate of the housing and buildup of food composition on the end plate of the housing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for switching from delivery of liquid carbon dioxide to delivery of liquid nitrogen for vessel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the nozzle of the nozzle assembly.
   Nozzle (150)
   Base portion (160)
   Shaft portion (170)
   Tip portion (180)

PN CN107771965-A
TI Preparing hotpot oil, by heating salad oil, adding e.g. chili pepper, and watercress, stir-frying, adding e.g. pickled pepper, frying, adding e.g. crystal sugar, filtering, adding base material, and frying and extracting product.
AU QU M
   XIE Z
   DONG Z
   YANG G
AE CHONGQING MEIXIANGYUAN IND GROUP CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 2018205750
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing hotpot oil using supercritical extraction, involves (a) adding salad oil into pot, heating to 130-140 degrees C, adding chili pepper, and watercress, and stir-frying for 5-6 minutes, adding e.g. pickled pepper, and frying for 3-4 minutes, adding e.g. crystal sugar, and continuously frying for 5-6 minutes, adding red pepper, frying at 100-110 degrees C, filtering, collecting filtrate and filtrate residues, (b) adding base material without salad oil, and performing second frying, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and mixing product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hotpot oil using supercritical extraction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares product which has improved utilization rate, contains high content of active components, and avoids slow taste deterioration of flavoring substance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing hotpot oil using supercritical extraction, involves (a) adding 70-85 pts. wt. salad oil into pot, heating to 130-140 degrees C, adding 15-18 pts. wt. chili pepper, and 8-10 pts. wt. watercress, and stir-frying for 5-6 minutes, adding 10-12 pts. wt. pickled pepper, 6-8 pts. wt. ginger, and 6-8 pts. wt. garlic, and frying for 3-4 minutes, adding 4-6 pts. wt. crystal sugar, 4-6 pts. wt. fermented glutinous rice, 5-7 pts. wt. white wine, 18-20 pts. wt. complex spice, and continuously frying for 5-6 minutes, adding 10-14 pts. wt. red pepper, frying at 100-110 degrees C, until the oil becomes bright and the fragrance overflows, adding 4-6 pts. wt. monosodium glutamate, 2-4 pts. wt. salt and 2-4 pts. wt. chicken essence, stirring, filtering, collecting the filtrate and filtrate residues, (b) adding base material without salad oil and other raw materials used for first frying process to filtrate for second frying process, and filtering, collecting the filtrate and filtrate residues, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction by adding first and second fried products, and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 20-28 MPa for 6-12 hours, reducing pressure to 12 MPa and 6 MPa for two stage separation, collecting residues and mixing product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: The method involves using 77 pts. wt. salad oil, 16 pts. wt. chili pepper, 8 pts. wt. watercress, 12 pts. wt. pickled pepper, 6 pts. wt. ginger, 8 pts. wt. garlic, 5 pts. wt. crystal sugar, 5 pts. wt. fermented glutinous rice, 6 pts. wt. white wine, 18 pts. wt. complex spice, 12 pts. wt. red pepper, 2 pts. wt. salt, 4 pts. wt. monosodium glutamate and 2 pts. wt. chicken essence. The complex spice comprises 8-10 pts. wt. cassia bark, 4-5 pts. wt. dried orange peel, 10-14 pts. wt. aniseed, 14-18 pts. wt. Amomum tsaoko fruit and 20-25 pts. wt. thyme.

TI Guide cooling cylinder for rapid cooling of coal burning boiler, has liquid carbon dioxide storage device provided with temperature monitoring system, pressure measuring device and liquid high monitor.
AU WANG X
   GU S
   XUE D
   SUN Z
AE SINO-INDO ALLWIN BEIJING TRADING CO LTD (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 202226775M
AB    NOVELTY - The cylinder has a guide cooling cylinder (2) located at an upper end of a combustion area in a combustion chamber (16) of a coal burning boiler. An outer portion of the guide cooling cylinder is connected with a liquid carbon dioxide storage device (7) through a connecting pipeline. The liquid carbon dioxide storage device comprises a liquid inlet, a storage tank, a liquid outlet and an exhaust port. The liquid carbon dioxide storage device is provided with a temperature monitoring system, a pressure measuring device and a liquid high monitor.
   USE - Guide cooling cylinder for rapid cooling of a coal burning boiler used in a refrigeration system using liquid carbon dioxide as working substance. Can also be used for preserving food, artificial rainfall and industrial raw material used for preparing soda, urea and soda.
   ADVANTAGE - The cold coil pipe and surface of the cold guide cylinder are closely contact, which improves cooling efficiency. The outer wall of the cold guide cylinder absorbs high temperature heat generated by combustion of inner hearth, and is transmitted to an inner side of a cold guide cylinder through the cold guide cylinder after liquid carbon dioxide enters the cold guide cylinder, and rapidly vaporizes at instant of contact generating heat absorption refrigeration effect, thus rapidly cooling the cold guide plate and the outer wall. The boiler realizes rapid cooling, and reduces problem of high steam temperature caused by safety of hidden trouble and damage.

PT P
PN CN111632644-A; CN111632644-B
TI Rice processing method involves extracting and drying carbon dioxide in storage chamber, and putting rice in storage state into color sorter, and subjecting rice to color selection to obtain high-quality rice.
AU QIN J
   YANG J
AE GUIZHOU MEITAN MAOBA YUSHAN RICE IND CO (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2020896253
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves drying the harvested rice. The drying process is carried out in a storage chamber (10). The dry ice is added to the storage chamber and the storage chamber is closed after the harvested rice is laid in the storage chamber. The gas in the storage chamber is continuously pumped to perform a vacuumizing process on the interior of the storage chamber. The compressed stored carbon dioxide is dried into a storage compartment, the temperature in the storage chamber is controlled at 30 degrees C to 35 degrees C, and the storage compartment is maintained in a normal pressure state. The carbon dioxide in the storage chamber is extracted and dried, and then injected into the storage chamber to circulate the carbon dioxide in the storage chamber. The rice in the storage state is put into the color sorter, and the rice is subjected to color selection to obtain high-quality rice. The high-quality rice is milled and polished to obtain rice.
   USE - Rice processing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide formed by sublimation of dry ice will build up in the bottom of the storage chamber, while the gas in the extraction chamber is withdrawn from the top, which can be withdrawn from the top and carbon dioxide can be extracted, and thus facilitating separation of carbon dioxide and oxygen. The quality and taste of the rice can be guaranteed by drying the rice at low temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating of the rice processing method.
   Storage chamber (10)
   Placing plate (11)
   Discharging port (12)
   Cylinder housing (20)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (40)

PT P
PN CN109201007-A; CN109201007-B
TI Carbon dioxide adsorbent used for capturing carbon dioxide in air and flue gas is prepared by freeze-drying method, and comprises biomass carbon, organic amines, and ionic liquids.
AU ZHAO P
   ZHANG G
   XU Y
   SUN Y
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2019076126
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide adsorbent is prepared by a freeze-drying method. The carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises 40-60 wt.% biomass carbon, 30-50 wt.% organic amines, and 5-10 wt.% ionic liquids.
   USE - Carbon dioxide adsorbent used for capturing carbon dioxide in air and flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The excellent dispersibility and stability of the organic amine and the ionic liquid in the adsorbent has excellent adsorption capacity, high cycle stability and low cost, and efficiently captures carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of carbon dioxide adsorbent whish involves washing, drying, carbonizing and pulverizing the walnut shell to 200-300 mesh, obtaining the walnut shell carbonized material (A), the carbonization condition is vacuum, and the temperature is 600-800 degrees C, washing, drying, carbonizing and pulverizing the pine wood dried to 200-300 mesh, obtaining pine wood carbonized material (B), the carbonization condition is vacuum, and the temperature is 500-600 degrees C, mixing walnut shell carbonized material (A) and pine wood carbonized material (B) in proportion, ultrasonically irradiating in an ultrasonic oscillator equipped with deionized water for 10-20 minutes, filtering and drying to obtain material (C), putting the material (C) in the modifier solution for heating and reflux modification for 8-10 hours, suction filtering, drying at 100 degrees C for 10-12 hours to obtain biomass carbon (D), stirring and immersing aqueous solution of biomass carbon (D) and organic amine and ionic liquid at room temperature for 5-8 hours, and ultrasonically treating for 2-3 hours in an ultrasonic oscillator, freezing resulting mixture in liquid nitrogen at -196 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, freeze-drying mixture in a freeze dryer at -65 degrees C for 24-48 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The biomass carbon is prepared by mixing a 30-70 wt.% walnut shell carbonized material with a 30-70 wt.% pine wood carbonized material. Preferred Process: The application process is carried out by having an adsorption temperature of 50-80 degrees C, and an adsorption pressure of 0.01-0.1 MPa, packaging 1 g of adsorbent in a 10 mm tubular fixed bed, and stoppering quartz wool at both ends, raising temperature to 50-80 degrees C, and passing nitrogen gas during the heating process, stabilizing the temperature, switching the nitrogen gas to a carbon dioxide gas mixture having a volumetric solubility of 15%, and the gas mixture flow rate is 60 ml/minute, and reaching the adsorption amount of the adsorbent at 4.12-4.75 mmol/g. Preferred Properties: The concentration of the modifier solution is 1-2.5 mol/L. The solid-liquid ratio of biomass carbon to deionized water is 1g/5-10 ml. Preferred Conditions: The ultrasonic irradiation conditions of the biomass carbon in preparation of carbon dioxide adsorbent has a temperature of 30-50 degrees C, and an ultrasonic frequency of 20-30 KHz. The solid-liquid ratio of the biomass carbon and the modifier solution is 1g/50-100 ml, and the heating reflux temperature is 100-120 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic amine comprises tetraethylenepentamine, polyethyleneimine, and diethylenetriamine. The ionic liquid comprises 1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolium salt, 1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate, 1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, and 1-aminoethyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide. The modifier is 1-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)-3-ethylcarbodiimide hydrochloride, and 1-hydroxybenzotriazole.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Apparatus for preparing synthetic fuel, comprises liquid component separator for separating condensate and oil from natural gas fed from limiting gas field, and desulfurizer for removing sulfur components from condensate and gas.
AU MOON D J
   SHIN S A
   YANG E H
   NOH Y S
   HONG G H
   PARK J I
   JUNG J S
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 201878429X
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises a liquid component separator (20) for separating C5+ condensate and oil from the natural gas fed from the limiting gas field, and a desulfurizer (30) for removing sulfur components from C5+ condensate and 1-4C gas from which oil has been separated in the liquid component separator. A pre-reformer (40) is provided for converting two or multiple carbon-rich hydrocarbons to methane in the desulfurized gas in the desulfurizer. A reforming reactor (50) is provided for generating a synthesis gas containing hydrogen and carbon monoxide through a steam carbon dioxide reforming (SCR) process for reforming methane and carbon dioxide into steam after a pretreatment process of the pretreater.
   USE - Apparatus for preparing synthetic fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus produces synthetic fuel in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with optimum carbon dioxide concentration adjusted with the molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide, improved energy utilization efficiency, and suppressed steam carbon dioxide reforming (SCR) loss prevention and greenhouse gas emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A Fischer-Tropsch reactor is provided for producing a synthetic fuel from a synthesis gas produced in the reforming reactor through a Fischer-Tropsch reaction. A carbon dioxide separation device (62) is provided for separating and removing carbon dioxide from the syngas to control the molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon monoxide contained in the syngas. A bypass device is provided for bypassing syngas to bypass the carbon dioxide separation device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of the apparatus for producing a synthetic fuel. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Liquid component separator (20)
   Desulfurizer (30)
   Pre-reformer (40)
   Reforming reactor (50)
   Carbon dioxide separation device (62)

TI System for performing gasification of coal in            supercritical carbon dioxide power cycle system, has            supercritical carbon dioxide power cycle system for            performing supercritical carbon dioxide power cycle for            generating power.
AU WORMSER A
   PARKES J
   TODD D M
   PARKS J
AE WORMSER ENERGY SOLUTIONS INC (WORM-Non-standard)
   WORMSER ENERGY SOLUTIONS INC (WORM-Non-standard)
   WORMSER ENERGY SOLUTIONS INC (WORM-Non-standard)
   WORMSER ENERGY SOLUTIONS INC (WORM-Non-standard)
   WORMSER ENERGY SOLUTIONS INC (WORM-Non-standard)
GA 201855936K
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an indirect gasifier (106)                including a combustion chamber i.e. combustor                (112), to receive syngas and oxidant e.g. oxygen                and air, and to burn mixture of the syngas with the                oxidant to provide heat for gasification and for                heating incoming flows for generating steam and                carbon dioxide. The combustion chamber transfers                the heat for gasification from the combustion                chamber to a gasification chamber (110) using                circulating refractory sand. A syngas cooler (114)                includes an inlet coupled to one of outlets of the                gasification chamber of the indirect gasifier. The                syngas cooler cools the syngas and generates steam                at an output. A supercritical carbon dioxide power                cycle system includes an input that is coupled to                an output of the syngas cooler and performs                supercritical carbon dioxide power cycle for                generating power.
   USE - System for performing gasification of                carbonaceous fuel i.e. coal (claimed), in a                supercritical carbon dioxide power cycle system to                produce chemicals e.g. fertilizers, methanol and                diesel fuel or electricity at an integrated                gasification combined cycle (IGCC) power plant, a                demonstration pilot plant and a large commercial                power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The system allows demonstration pilot plants                and large commercial power plants that can greatly                benefit from an all-steam gasification system using                an indirect gasifier to increase power generation                efficiency, reduce size and cost and generally                result in a lower environmental footprint, while                using coal or other carbonaceous fuels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                carbonaceous fuel gasification method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of an                all-steam gasification system with air-based                combustion for a generic supercritical carbon                dioxide power cycle system with a carbon capture                system.

PT P
PN CN107973697-A
TI Preparing biomass based methanol comprises e.g. pyrolysis of biomass to obtain biomass carbon powder, reacting biomass carbon powder with water vapor and passing the resultant synthesis gas into fixed-bed reactor.
AU MO Z
AE MO Z (MOZZ-Individual)
GA 201835145E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biomass based methanol comprises e.g. pyrolysis of biomass to obtain biomass carbon powder, reacting biomass carbon powder with water vapor to obtain hydrogen and carbon dioxide gas, passing the resultant synthesis gas into fixed-bed reactor containing water-gas shift catalyst and reacting some carbon dioxide with water vapor to produce hydrogen and carbon dioxide gas to obtain crude raw gas, passing crude raw gas into calcium hydroxide solution to remove excess carbon dioxide to obtain raw gas, and passing raw gas into methanol synthesis catalyst layer to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing biomass based methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly and renewable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing biomass based methanol comprises (i) pyrolysis of biomass to obtain biomass carbon powder, (ii) reacting biomass carbon powder with water vapor at a temperature of 700-850 degrees C to obtain hydrogen and carbon dioxide gas, (iii) passing synthesis gas prepared in step (ii) into fixed-bed reactor containing a water-gas shift catalyst and reacting some carbon dioxide with water vapor at 260-300 degrees C to produce hydrogen and carbon dioxide gas to obtain crude raw gas, (iv) passing crude raw gas in step (iii) into calcium hydroxide solution to remove excess carbon dioxide to obtain raw gas, and (v) passing raw gas in step (iv) into methanol synthesis catalyst layer to obtain final product, where the catalyst layer is composed of 20-23 wt.% nickel(II) oxide, 26-36 wt.% zinc oxide and 41-54 wt.% aluminum oxide; the reaction temperature is 210-270oC, the reaction pressure is 4.5-5.5 MPa, and a space velocity of biomass based methanol is 9000-11000 h(-1).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (iii), the water gas shift catalyst contains 18-22 wt.% nickel(II) oxide, 20-36 wt.% zinc oxide and 42- 62 wt.% aluminum oxide. In step (i), the biomass is straw, wooden meal and/or rice hull. Preferred Method: The step (iv) comprises passing raw gas into calcium hydroxide solution to remove excess carbon dioxide and adjusting total equivalent ratio M of hydrogen, carbon oxide and carbon dioxide to 2.0-2.05. The step (i) comprises subjecting biomass to high temperature baking and then baking at 300-450 degrees C under anaerobic or anoxic conditions to obtain biomass carbon powder.

TI Method for desorption of carbon dioxide from            sorbent bed, involves exposing steam stream having            steam pressure of specific range, and passing portion            of exhaust stream through liquid ring pump having            associated ring cooler.
AU YEGANEH M S
   FULTON J W
   NARAYANAN S
   BARCKHOLTZ T A
   WESTON S C
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202295669R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves exposing steam stream                comprises steam pressure of 4-50 kPa to the sorbent                to form an exhaust stream comprising steam and at                least a portion of the sorbed carbon dioxide. The                steam stream comprises a temperature of 30-8oC                prior to entering the bed. The exhaust stream is                passed through a liquid ring pump comprising an                associated ring cooler to form carbon chromium                (CCh)-containing stream. The bed comprises sorbed                CO2 and a first average sorbent temperature.
   USE - Method for desorption of carbon dioxide from                sorbent bed,.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively desorbs carbon dioxide                from the sorbent bed using low temperature steam at                pressures below 50 kPa-a, thus reducing the cost of                desorption and regeneration of the bed of absorbent                or adsorbent, and reducing the amount of power                required to operate the bed, and hence reducing the                power consumption of the system. The method reduces                the production of additional carbon dioxide, and                increases the efficiency of the adsorption of the                carbon dioxide in the bed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an system                for sorption and desorption of CO2 using sorbent                bed.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for manufacturing cement involves producing methane by adding hydrogen to carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from cement production facility or carbon dioxide separated and recovered from exhaust gas, supplying methane as portion or all of fossil fuel to calcination furnace of cement production facility as alternative fuel, calcining cement raw material by calcination furnace, and calcining the calcined cement raw material by cement firing kiln of cement production facility to obtain cement clinker.
   USE - Method for manufacturing cement using methane as alternative fuel for fossil fuels in calcination furnace in cement manufacturing equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes methane, reduces carbon dioxide originating from energy, reduces greenhouse gases, produces cement while reducing fuel nitrogen oxide and sulfur oxide originating from fuel, and suppresses the release of carbon dioxide from combustion exhaust gas to atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a cement production system comprising methanation device for producing methane by adding hydrogen to carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from cement production facility or to carbon dioxide separated and recovered from exhaust gas, and a methane supply device for supplying methane to cement production facility as alternative fuel for some or all of fossil fuels, where the cement production system has a calcination furnace for calcining cement raw material and cement firing kiln for calcining calcined cement raw material to produce cement clinker.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide is obtained by separating and recovering exhaust gas from cement production facility by contacting exhaust gas with carbon dioxide absorbent. The method involves directly adding hydrogen to exhaust gas to produce methane. Preferred Device: The device for separating and recovering carbon dioxide is provided for separating and recovering carbon dioxide by contacting exhaust gas from cement production facility with carbon dioxide absorbent. The methanation device is provided for directly adding hydrogen to exhaust gas to produce methane.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

AU WEI Y
   YU B
   MI Z
   LIAO H
   LIANG Q
   WANG Z
   WANG J
AE BEIJING INST TECHNOLOGY (BEIT-C)
GA 202142015L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting global atmospheric carbon dioxide concentration data using carbon dioxide data collector of the carbon satellite. The real-time carbon dioxide concentration data at different heights in sample areas is detected using carbon dioxide content detector of ground fixed observation station. The carbon dioxide emission data generated by various mobile sources is detected using mobile source carbon dioxide emission detector. The changes of climatic factors including temperature, precipitation, sea level and illumination caused by the changes of social-economic factors are calculated. The bidirectional dynamic coupling of the global-regional-national socio-economic model and the earth system model is realized, and the construction of the integrated assessment method of climate change is finished based on multi-source carbon data.
   USE - Integrated assessment method of climate change based on multi-source carbon data.
   ADVANTAGE - The problem of incompleteness and discreteness of carbon information is solved, and the uncertainty of assessment results is effectively reduced. The two-way dynamic correlation of the socio-economic system and the earth system is constructed, which improves the accuracy of climate change socio-economic loss assessment and the precision of carbon dioxide emission accounting. The practical guiding significance of the climate change integrated assessment models is enhanced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a workflow diagram of the coupling module of socio-economic model and earth system model.

PN CN107858207-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method of            Camellia nitidissima essential oil, involves cleaning            Camellia nitidissima leaves to-be-extracted, draining            water, microwave drying, crushing, sieving, swelling,            and extracting.
AU WEI N
AE GUANGXI & HONG KONG NAT TREASURE HI-TECH (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018264097
AB    NOVELTY - The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                method of Camellia nitidissima essential oil                involves cleaning C.nitidissima leaves                to-be-extracted, draining water, and microwave                drying, crushing the C.nitidissima leaves, sieving,                swelling, transferring the C.nitidissima leaves to                a supercritical carbon dioxide device, extracting                two times, and collecting the extraction liquid to                obtain C.nitidissima crude oil, freezing,                centrifuging, removing impurities, vacuum                distilling, purifying, and condensing.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method                of C.nitidissima essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction of C.nitidissima essential oil                with high yield, rapid and high efficiency and                without solvent residue, and does not contaminate                the extracted material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                method of Camellia nitidissima essential oil                involves (1) cleaning C.nitidissima leaves                to-be-extracted, draining water, and microwave                drying to reduce the moisture content below 10%,                (2) crushing the C.nitidissima leaves, sieving by                40-60 mesh sieve, and swelling, (3) transferring                the C.nitidissima leaves to a supercritical carbon                dioxide device, extracting two times, and                collecting the extraction liquid to obtain                C.nitidissima crude oil, (4) placing the                C.nitidissima crude oil in an environment of 8-11                degrees C, freezing for 30-35 minutes, placing the                frozen oil in a high-speed refrigerated centrifuge,                centrifuging at speed of 8000-10000 rpm for 20-30                minutes, removing impurities, placing in 45-50                degrees C, vacuum distilling, purifying, and                condensing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In the step (1), the                  frequency of the microwave drying is 800-1000                  MHz, and the drying time is 2-4 minutes. In step                  (2), the parameters of the screw extrusion                  process are 180-220 rpm screw speed, 115-145                  degrees C extrusion temperature and 15-25 rpm                  feeding speed, the air blown technology                  processing parameters are 60-80 degrees C                  operating temperature, 200-400 kPa expansion                  pressure difference, and 50-75 minutes puffing                  time, and the microwave expansion technology                  parameters are 1000-2000 W microwave power and                  2-4 minutes processing time. In step (3), the                  supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is                  performed at 30-40 MPa and 35-40 degrees C for                  30-60 minutes and the carbon dioxide fluid flow                  rate is 700-800 L/hour, and 45-55 MPa and 45-50                  degrees C for 60-120 minutes and the carbon                  dioxide fluid flow rate is 800-900 L/hour. In the                  step (4), the vacuum distillation is performed                  using a distillation tank at 0.06 MPa pressure                  and vacuum degree of 60-70 MPa.
