TI Viscose-based nitrogen-containing activated carbon fiber material useful for carbon dioxide adsorption and carbon dioxide/methane separation.
AU LI Y
   GAO Y
   WANG B
   WANG Y
   ZHANG T
AE UNIV HENAN POLYTECHNIC (UYHP-C)
GA 202180333P
AB    NOVELTY - Viscose-based nitrogen-containing activated carbon fiber material, is claimed.
   USE - The activated carbon fiber material is useful for carbon dioxide adsorption and carbon dioxide/methane separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The activated carbon fiber material: has selectivity coefficient of 3.29-5.48 in carbon dioxide/methane separation; has specific surface area of 609-1159 cm2/g, total pore volume of 0.31-0.54 cm3/g, and nitrogen content of 4.27-7.43 wt.%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing viscose-based nitrogen-containing activated carbon fiber material comprising (1) soaking and drying the clean and dry viscose fiber fabric in chloride salt solution, (2) heating the viscose fiber fabric in a tube furnace, and performing the temperature rising process in a nitrogen atmosphere, raising the temperature to the target temperature, converting the nitrogen into carbon dioxide gas, performing a constant temperature carbonization-activation treatment to obtain a semi-finished product of nitrogen-containing activated carbon fiber material, and the target temperature is 700-900 degrees C, and (3) soaking semi-finished nitrogen-containing activated carbon fiber material, washing with an acid solution until the washing liquid becomes neutral, drying to obtain the viscose-based nitrogen-containing activated carbon fiber material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the viscose-based nitrogen-containing activated carbon fiber material, the pore volume of ultra-micropores with a pore diameter less than 1 nm is 50-65 wt.% of the total pore volume. The pore volume of micropores with a pore diameter of less than 2 nm is 67-80 wt.% of the total pore volume. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the chlorine salt solution is the mixed salt solution composed of ammonium chloride and zinc chloride, where the soaking time is 60 minutes, drying for 240 minutes at 60 degrees C. In the step (1), the clean and dry viscose fiber fabric is obtained by ultrasonically cleaning the waste viscose fiber fabric in ethanol and de-ionized water and hen drying. In the step (2), the heating speed of the heating treatment is 3 degrees C/minute, in the heating process, the flow rate of nitrogen in the tubular furnace is 40 ml/minute. In the step (2), the flow speed of the carbon dioxide in the tubular furnace is 40 ml/minute, preferably, the carbonization-activation treatment time is for 60 minutes. In the step (3), the acid solution is hydrochloric acid solution with concentration of 0.1 mol/l, preferably, the acid solution soaking time is for 30 minutes. In the step (3), drying for 240 minutes at 60 degrees C.

PN CN109847719-A
TI Ionic copolymer composite adsorbent used for carbon dioxide adsorption separation, comprises ionic copolymer and metal organic framework where ionic copolymer is obtained by copolymerizing ionic liquid monomer and divinylbenzene.
AU GUAN G
   WAN H
   GUO Q
   CHEN C
   FENG N
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2019534347
AB    NOVELTY - Ionic copolymer composite adsorbent comprises ionic copolymer and metal organic framework where content of metal organicframework is 9-19 wt.%. The ionic copolymer is obtained by copolymerizing ionic liquid monomer and divinylbenzene. The micropores and mesoporous structures are present in composite material.
   USE - The ionic copolymer composite adsorbent is used for carbon dioxide adsorption separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has both microporous and mesoporous structures, high porosity and abundant basic functional groups which are favorable for carbon dioxide chemisorption; exhibits high carbon dioxide adsorption amount and faster carbon dioxide adsorption diffusion rate; and improves amount of carbon dioxide adsorbed under low pressure without decreasing after repeated use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of ionic copolymer composite adsorbent comprising adding ionic liquid monomer, divinylbenzene and initiator to solvent, reacting under nitrogen atmosphere, centrifuging the resulting precipitate, washing, vacuum-drying to obtain porous ionic copolymer, dissolving precursor metal salt in ethanol to obtain metal salt solution, adding porous ionic copolymer, ultrasonically dispersing, filtering, washing, dispersing in solvent, reacting by adding organic ligand, centrifuging mixture, washing and vacuum-drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal salt is zinc nitrate hexahydrate, cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, copper nitrate trihydrate or ferric chloride hexahydrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal organic framework material is zeolitic imidazolate framework-8, zeolitic imidazolate framework-67, metal organic framework-100 (Fe) or Hongkong University of Science and Technology-1. The initiator is azobisisobutyronitrile or azobisisoheptanenitrile. The solvent is ethanol, deionized water or acetonitrile. The organic ligand is 2-methylimidazole or 1,3,5-benzenetricarboxylic acid. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of ionic liquid monomer, divinylbenzene, initiator and solvent is 1:0.2-0.7:0.03-0.1:22-42. The mass ratio of porous ionic copolymer, metal salt and ethanol is 1:5-15:31-48. The mass ratio of metal salt, organic ligand and solvent is 1:0.1-0.5:4-20. Preferred Method: The reaction step is done at 70-90 degrees C for 16 to 24 hours. The reaction after adding organic ligand is carried out at 25-120 degrees C for 12-24 hours. The ultrasonic treatment is done for 1-3 hours with ultrasonic power of 50-200 W.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid monomer is 1-ethyl-3-vinylimidazolium bromide, 1-aminoethyl-3-vinylimidazolium bromide or 1-ethyl-4-vinylpyridinium bromide.

PT P
PN KR2019043343-A; KR2046794-B1
TI Adsorbent useful for capturing carbon dioxide, comprises promoter including eutectic mixture, and magnesium oxide/titanium dioxide complex.
AU SEO J G
   CHUNG W J
   HWANG S H
   VISHWANATH H
   LOUISE T M
   HIREMATH V
   TRIVINOMONICALOUISE
AE UNIV MYONGJI IND & ACAD COOP FOUND (UYMY-C)
   UNIV MYONGJI IND & ACADEMIA COOP FOUND (UYMY-C)
GA 201937842F
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorbent comprises promoter including eutectic mixture, and magnesium oxide/titanium dioxide complex.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful for capturing carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has excellent carbon dioxide adsorption performance at high temperature, and excellent cycle characteristics because structural change of magnesium oxide is suppressed during cycling of adsorption and regeneration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing adsorbent comprising (a) mixing more than two alkali nitrate salts and pulverizing to prepare eutectic mixture, (b) heating the eutectic mixture to temperature above the eutectic temperature to homogenize the eutectic mixture, (c) cooling the homogenized eutectic mixture and re-pulverizing to obtain a eutectic mixture powder, (d) mixing the eutectic mixture powder with magnesium oxide/titanium oxide composite, and (e) calcining the mixture prepared in the step (d);
   (2) capturing carbon dioxide, comprising (A) heating the adsorbent to temperature above the eutectic temperature of eutectic mixture to induce melting of eutectic mixture, and (B) introducing carbon dioxide gas into the adsorbent in which eutectic mixture is melted; and
   (3) regenerating adsorbent, comprising heating the adsorbent which is completed adsorption of carbon dioxide at 400-550 degrees C for 5-20 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The magnesium oxide/titanium oxide composite is prepared by (i) adding metal precursor including magnesium precursor and titanium precursor and acid to solvent and stirring the solvent, (ii) evaporating the solvent to obtain a solid, and (iii) calcining the solid at 500-900 degrees C for 4-10 hours. The step (b) is carried out at 80-150 degrees C for 8-16 hours. The calcination is carried out at 300-500 degrees C under nitrogen gas for 4-8 hours. In the step (B), 100% carbon dioxide is introduced when the adsorbent is heated to 250-325 degrees C, and less than or equal to 30% carbon dioxide is introduced when the adsorbent is heated to 200-300 degrees C. The regeneration of adsorbent is carried out in nitrogen atmosphere. Preferred Components: The eutectic mixture is mixture of more than two alkali nitrates, preferably mixture containing potassium nitrate and lithium nitrate in weigh ratio of 1:1-1:4. The adsorbent comprises 20-40 wt.% eutectic mixture. The magnesium oxide/titanium oxide composite comprises 2-10 wt.% titanium dioxide. The magnesium precursor includes magnesium hydroxide, magnesium nitrate and/or magnesium acetate. The titanium precursor includes titanium tetrabutoxide, titanium propoxide, titanium isopropoxide, titanium methoxide and/or titanium ethoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid includes nitric acid, acetic acid, hydrochloric acid and/or sulfuric acid. The solvent includes ethanol, methanol and/or isopropyl alcohol.
   EXAMPLE - 1 g Pluronic P-123 (RTM: Poly(ethylene glycol)-block-poly(propylene glycol)-block-poly(ethylene glycol)) was added to 50 ml anhydrous ethanol (94.5%) and dissolved for 4 hours. Magnesium nitrate hexahydrate (99%) and titanium isopropoxide ( less than or equal to 97%) was added to dissolve the magnesium oxide in total complex to ratio of 0.90, 0.95, 0.97 and 0.98, 5 ml nitric acid (70%) was added for complete hydrolysis, stirred for 5 hours, solution was allowed to evaporate at 60 degrees C for 48 hours to induce self-assembly of metal precursor. The solvent has evaporated, and resulting solid was calcined at 550 degrees C for 5 hours at 5 degrees C ramping to obtain magnesium oxide-titanium dioxide composite aligned mesoporous adsorbent.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN114433158-A
TI Nitrogen-doped graded porous carbon-based catalyst            useful in carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction            catalyst.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHENG Q
   QIU Y
   YAO P
   LI X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2022662967
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrogen-doped graded porous carbon-based                catalyst, is claimed. The catalyst has microporous                and mesoporous structures, where content of                graphitic nitrogen in the catalyst is higher than                that of zinc-nitrogen.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in carbon dioxide                electrochemical reduction catalyst (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: is suitable for catalyzing the                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide; and                has the graded porous structure, high electrical                conductivity and adjustable components, reasonable                the content of various types of nitrogen elements,                and excellent Faradaic efficiency for carbon                dioxide electrochemical reduction to carbon                monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   preparing the nitrogen-doped graded porous                carbon-based catalyst, comprising impregnating                Zeolitic imidazolate framework-8 (ZIF-8) in a                bismuth salt solution to obtain solution I, and                separating to obtain product I, and calcining the                product I at 900-1050&#176;C; and
   a carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction                catalyst, comprising the nitrogen-doped graded                porous carbon-based catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The content of                  graphitic nitrogen in the nitrogen-doped graded                  porous carbon-based catalyst is 1.40-2% higher                  than that of zinc-nitrogen. The total content of                  nitrogen in the nitrogen-doped graded porous                  carbon-based catalyst is 4-7%. The content of                  graphitic nitrogen in the nitrogen-doped graded                  porous carbon-based catalyst is 0.2-1% higher                  than that of pyridine nitrogen. The content of                  graphitic nitrogen in the nitrogen-doped graded                  porous carbon-based catalyst is 0.3-0.9% higher                  than that of pyrrole nitrogen. The proportion of                  the micropores is 30-50%, and the proportion of                  the mesopores is 50-70%. The micropore diameter                  is 0.7-1.1 nm, and the mesopore diameter is 3-10                  nm. The catalyst has a total volume of 0.6-1.2                  cm3/g, a micropore volume of 0.18-0.45 cm3/g, and                  a mesopore volume of 0.5-0.8 cm3/g, has a total                  surface area of 700-1000 m2/g, and a micropore                  surface area of 400-800 m2/g, has long-range                  ordered graphitization stripes. The bismuth salt                  comprises bismuth sulfate, bismuth chloride,                  and/or bismuth nitrate. The solvent of the                  solution I comprises ethanol solution, propanol                  solution and/or methanol solution. The                  concentration of ZIF8 in described solution I is                  1-10 g/l. The concentration of bismuth salt in                  the solution I is 2-4 g/l. The carbon dioxide                  electrochemical reduction catalyst is a carbon                  dioxide electrochemical reduction cathode                  catalyst. Preferred Method: The calcination is                  carried out at heating rate of                  2-5&#176;C/minute under the condition of                  inert gas for 1-3 hours. The impregnation is                  specifically carried out by stirring the mixture                  of ZIF8 and bismuth salt solution for 12-24                  hours. The separation is specifically carried out                  by centrifuging and freeze-drying or drying at                  60-90&#176;C for 12-24 hours.

TI Method of preparing alkylene carbonate from alkylene oxides and carbon dioxide by reacting alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide, and contacting reaction materials with catalyst.
AU GUO J
   LI X
   ZHANG X
AE SHANDONG SHIDA SHENGHUA CHEM IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG SHIDA SHENGHUA CHEM IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG SHIDA SHENGHUA CHEM IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019488082
AB    NOVELTY - Method of preparing alkylene carbonate from alkylene oxides and carbon dioxide comprises taking alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide as raw materials; reacting at 60-200 degrees C and 0.1-10.0 MPa; and contacting reaction materials with catalyst based on catalyst to alkylene oxide mass ratio of (0.001-1):1 to form alkylene carbonate. The catalyst is multi-based complex compound catalytic system containing imidazole-containing group, salicylic acid group, and zinc, in which molar ratio of zinc, imidazole-containing group and salicylic acid group is 5:(1-50):(1-50).
   USE - The method is form preparing alkylene carbonate from alkylene oxides and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high conversion rate of ethylene oxide and high selectivity of ethylene carbonate. It adopts catalyst system with simple preparation process, low cost and good activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The zinc precursor is oxide or halide of zinc.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction step is performed at 80-160 degrees C and 0.5-8.0 MPa. The catalyst is prepared by mixing zinc precursor and salicylic acid precursor at room temperature; adding solvent; stirring; heating to obtain intermediate product; mixing intermediate product with imidazole-based precursor; adding solvent; stirring; heating; and removing solvent to obtain catalyst product. Preferred Component: The catalytic system contains zinc atom, imidazole group, and salicylaldehyde group. The alkylene oxide is ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, and/or butylene oxide. The mass ratio of catalyst to alkylene oxide is preferably (0.005-0.5):1. The salicylic acid precursor is salicylic acid or side chain salicylic acid. The imidazole-based precursor is imidazole or alkylimidazole. The solvent used is methanol, ethanol or diethyl ether.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing synthesis gas product involves                contacting gaseous mixture includes hydrocarbon and                carbon dioxide with catalyst includes noble metal                on solid support which includes cerium oxide.
   USE - Method for producing synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce synthesis gas                that improves overall thermodynamics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for reforming carbon dioxide-steam, which                involves:
   (A) contacting gaseous mixture includes one or                more hydrocarbons, carbon dioxide, and water with                catalyst to produce stream of synthesis gas product                has molar hydrogen and carbon monoxide ratio from                1.5:1-2.3:1;
   (B) processing rate of carbon formation that                is less than baseline rate of carbon formation of                baseline process; and
   (C) replacing water of gaseous mixture with                equimolar amount of oxygen as carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The noble metal is                  selected from platinum, rhodium, ruthenium,                  palladium, silver, osmium, iridium, and gold. The                  catalyst comprises two noble metals selected from                  platinum, rhodium, ruthenium, palladium, silver,                  osmium, iridium, and gold. The gaseous mixture                  further comprises water. The gaseous mixture is                  obtained by combining hydrocarbon-containing                  feedstock and carbon dioxide-containing oxidant.                  The hydrocarbon-containing feedstock is                  methane-containing feedstock comprises natural                  gas or methane from renewable methane source. The                  one or more hydrocarbons include methane. The                  gaseous mixture is obtained by further combining                  water-containing oxidant with                  hydrocarbon-containing feedstock and carbon                  dioxide-containing oxidant. The synthesis gas                  product comprises hydrogen in amount representing                  about 70% of hydrogen in hydrogen-containing                  composite in gaseous mixture. Preferred                  Conditions: The reforming conditions includes                  temperature from about 649-816 degrees C.                  Preferred Compositions: The cerium oxide is                  present in amount of about 95 wt.% of solid                  support. The platinum is present in amount from                  about 0.05-5 wt.% of catalyst. The Rh is present                  in amount from about 0.05-5 wt.% of catalyst. The                  gaseous mixture comprises 100 mol-parts per                  million of total sulfur. The gaseous mixture                  comprises aromatic and olefinic hydrocarbons in                  combined amount from 1-25 mol-%. The reforming                  conditions further includes gage pressure from                  about 0-517 kilopascal. The hydrocarbon is                  methane and conversion of methane 85% at                  temperature of at most about 704 degrees C. The                  catalyst comprises solid support comprises cerium                  oxide and has platinum and rhodium deposited,                  each in amount from about 0.05-5 wt.% of                  catalyst. Preferred Process: The method involves                  contacting is performed by flowing gaseous                  mixture through reactor vessel containing                  catalyst, at weight hourly space velocity from                  about 0.1-2.5 hr 1, to provide synthesis gas                  product as effluent from reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Producing bread with addition of carbon dioxide-tomato waste meal involves e.g. mixing high-quality wheat flour with carbon dioxide-tomato waste meal, adding yeast suspension, aqueous salt solution and water and kneading and leavening dough.
AU MIGALATIEV O
   CARELINA M
   CARAGIA V
   DRAGANOVA E
   GORDEEVA V
AE GARDENING & FOOD TECHNOLOGIES INST (GARG-Non-standard)
   GARDENING & FOOD TECHNOLOGIES SCI & PRAC (GARD-Non-standard)
GA 201941395J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing bread with addition of carbon dioxide-tomato waste meal, comprises: mixing high-quality wheat flour with 0.5% of carbon dioxide-tomato waste meal of the total meal weight, dry or humidified; adding the yeast suspension, the aqueous salt solution and water; kneading the dough for 12-14 minutes; leavening the dough for 20 minutes; dividing the dough into pieces with the shaping of semi-finished products; additional leavening for 20 minutes; pre-leavening and scoring the semi-finished products; and final leavening at 35-40 degrees C for 20 minutes and baking at 240 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for producing bread with addition of carbon dioxide-tomato waste meal.

TI Adsorption agent used in electrode for carbon dioxide reducing device, comprises metal, ligand which can be coordinated to metal, and carbon nanotubes containing functional group combinable with metal, and is porous.
AU MANABE T
   IMANAKA Y
AE FUJITSU LTD (FUIT-C)
GA 201848684B
AB    NOVELTY - An adsorption agent (3) comprises a metal, a ligand which can be coordinated to the metal, and carbon nanotubes containing a functional group combinable with the metal, and is porous.
   USE - Adsorption agent is used in electrode for carbon dioxide reducing device (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorption agent has excellent electroconductivity and retainability of carbon dioxide. The device having electrode containing the adsorption agent can reduce carbon dioxide efficiently.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of the adsorption agent, which involves heating a liquid mixture containing a metal salt, ligand, water and carbon nanotubes;
   (2) electrode (1), which comprises a metal-containing component (2) and the adsorption agent; and
   (3) carbon dioxide reducing device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional schematic view of electrode.
   Electrode (1)
   Metal-containing component (2)
   Adsorption agent (3)

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The functional group is carboxyl group, hydroxyl group and/or amino group. The ligand includes aromatic hydrocarbon ring(s), two pairs of carboxyl groups and hydroxyl group which are bonded with the aromatic hydrocarbon ring(s), in which each of the carboxyl group and hydroxyl group is coupled with adjacent two carbon atoms in aromatic hydrocarbon ring group(s).

AB    NOVELTY - The carbon-dioxide supply apparatus has a                adsorption unit (43) adsorbs carbon dioxide                included in an exhaust gas produced by heating in a                greenhouse for cultivating plants. The control unit                causes the exhaust gas produced by heating to pass                through the adsorption unit. The adsorption unit                has an accumulation surface (43a) and a supply                surface on the outer surfaces. Air passing through                the adsorption unit flows into the adsorption unit                through the supply surface and flows out from the                adsorption unit through the accumulation                surface.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide supply apparatus for green                house.
   ADVANTAGE - The heating is performed by the heating                machine. The storage unit can perform accumulation                and supply of a carbon dioxide by the method                accompanied by mechanical pressurization.

PN CN109370671-A; CN109370671-B
TI Separation of petroleum associated gas comprises pretreating petroleum, gas-liquid separating, carbon dioxide separating using composite membrane, and gas-recovering.
AU LI K
   LU J
   LI S
AE HANGZHOU BOYANG ENERGY EQUIP CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 201920930A
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of petroleum associated gas comprises pretreating petroleum, gas-liquid separating, carbon dioxide separating, and gas-recovering. The carbon dioxide separation is done by using composite membrane for separating carbon dioxide, where composite film is polysulfone film layer on surface layer with intermediate layer of three-layer structure in which gel filtration layer and bottom layer are nonwoven fabric layer.
   USE - Method for separating petroleum associated gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite membrane has high porosity, selectivity, and permeation flux, is not easy to be deformed, has good separation effect, and is compact and environment-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The separation of petroleum is done by separating oil in associated gas by gravity sedimentation to remove free water and impurities and pressurizing; adding to gas-liquid separator to remove water; heating and separating carbon dioxide through a composite membrane to obtain organic steam; passing organic steam into liquefied gas tower for fractionation and transferring fractionated organic vapor to liquefaction tank for storage. The heating is done at 50-70 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The composite membrane is obtained by heating gel filtration layer at 200 degrees C, melting non-woven fabric and polysulfone membrane, binding on both sides of composite film, cooling, solidifying, preparing calcium alginate gel and imidazole salt crystals to form filter skeleton, and forming polyethersulfone film on the filter skeleton.

PT P
PN CN107602419-A; CN107602419-B
TI Preparing 1,5-pentamethylene diisocyanate, comprises e.g. whole cell catalytic decarboxylation of lysine to obtain pentamethylenediamine and carbon dioxide, reacting, and with phosgene, desolventizing, and carrying out vacuum distillation.
AU CHEN K
   XU S
   TIAN W
   HE Y
   WANG J
   WEI G
   CHENG Q
   OUYANG P
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
   UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2018086114
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 1,5-pentamethylene diisocyanate, comprises (i) whole cell catalytic decarboxylation of lysine to obtain pentamethylenediamine and carbon dioxide, and collecting carbon dioxide, (ii) reacting carbon dioxide obtained in step (i) with pentamethylenediamine obtained in step (i) to obtain pentamethylenediamine carbonate, and (iii) reacting pentamethylenediamine carbonate with phosgene, desolventizing under reduced pressure, and carrying out vacuum distillation to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 1,5-pentamethylene diisocyanate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low cost; and is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The method utilizes a conversion tank, which is used for decarboxylation of lysine to pentamethylenediamine and carbon dioxide; a carbon dioxide storage tank, which is used to collect carbon dioxide generated during decarboxylation; pentamethylenediamine conversion liquid storage tank, which is used to collect decarboxylated pentamethylenediamine; salt forming tank, which is used to react the carbon dioxide with pentamethylenediamine to obtain pentamethylene diamine carbonate; high-temperature photochemical tank, which is used to react the pentamethylenediamine carbonate with phosgene to obtain final product; desolvation rectifying tank, which is used for vacuum desolventization, vacuum distillation; pure 1, 5-pentamethylene diisocyanate collecting tank, which is used for collecting purified 1, 5-pentamethylene diisocyanate, where one outlet of the conversion liquid storage tank and carbon dioxide storage tank, salt forming tank, high-temperature photochemical tank, desolvation rectification tank, pure 1,5-pentamethylene diisocyanate collection tank are connected in sequence. The outlet of the conversion tank and pentamethylenediamine conversion liquid storage tank and salt forming tank are connected in sequence. The production system comprises carbon dioxide dehydration tank, which is used to dehydrate the carbon dioxide before it reacts with pentamethylene diamine. The inlet of the carbon dioxide dehydration tank is connected to the carbon dioxide storage tank. The outlet of the carbon dioxide dehydration tank communicates with the salt tank. The production system comprises separation refining tank, which is used for the purification of decarboxylated pentamethylene diamine; pure pentamethylene diamine storage tank for the collection of purified pentamethylene diamine; pentylenediamine and inert gas premix tank for mixing purified pentamethylene diamine with inert gas; where the separation refining tanks and pure pentamethylene diamine tanks and pentylenediamine and inert gas premix tanks are connected in sequence. The separating the inlet and the inlet of the refining tank, the discharge port of the diamine conversion liquid storage tank is communicated with pentamethylene diamine and inert gas, the outlet of the mixing tank communicates with the inlet of the salting tank.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (ii), the pentamethylene diamine is mixed with an organic solvent, the dehydrated carbon dioxide is introduced at room temperature, stir until the pH reaches to 6.8-7.2, where the organic solvent is chlorobenzene and/or o-dichlorobenzene, and the molar ratio between pentamethylene diamine and organic solvent is 1:10-20. In step (iii), the pentamethylenediamine carbonate and phosgene is reacted at 120-135 degrees C. In step (iii), after phosgenation reaction, incubating system temperature, filling nitrogen until the system pH reaches to neutral, and detecting chlorine ion content in the system. Preferred Components: In step (ii), the carbon dioxide is dehydrated before being reacted with pentamethylenediamine.

TI Liquid carbon dioxide production device comprises            gas separating device having compression mechanism,            dealkylation mechanism, purifying mechanism and oxygen            supply mechanism, and liquefying and purifying            device.
AU LI D
AE HUIZHOU KAIMEITE GASES CO LTD (HUIZ-Non-standard)
   HUIZHOU KAIMEITE GASES CO LTD (HUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2021E9489J
AB    (CN215113521-U)   NOVELTY - The invention claims a liquid carbon dioxide                production device and system. the liquid carbon                dioxide production device comprises a gas                separating device and a liquefying and purifying                device; the gas separating device comprises a                compression mechanism, a dealkylation mechanism, a                purifying mechanism and an oxygen supply mechanism;                the liquefying and purifying device comprises a                liquefying and purifying mechanism, a liquid                ammonia refrigerating mechanism, a storage tank, a                first switch valves and a circulating switch valves                the output end of the purifying mechanism is                connected with the input end of the liquefying and                purifying mechanism through the first switch                valves, the liquefying and purifying mechanism is                connected with the liquid ammonia refrigerating                mechanism through the circulating switch valves,                the first switch valves and the circulating switch                valves are used for closing after the central                control device sends parking signal. after the                central control device sends parking signal, the                first switch valves the purifying mechanism is                isolated from the liquefying and purifying                mechanism, reduces the leakage rate of the liquid                carbon dioxide, switch valves the refrigeration                cycle path blocking, reduces the leakage rate of                the liquid ammonia, so that the emergency treatment                in emergency and safety in the emergency                condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                production system of liquid carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                device for producing liquid carbon dioxide.

EA    NOVELTY - The device (10) has a gas separation device                (100) including a compression mechanism (110), a                de-hydrocarbyl mechanism (120), a purification                mechanism (130) and an oxygen supply mechanism                (140). An output end of the oxygen supply mechanism                is connected with an input end of the compression                mechanism, and the input end of the compression                mechanism is also used for connecting with a raw                material gas supply device. An output end of the                compression mechanism is connected with an input                end of the de-hydrocarbyl mechanism, and an output                end of the de-hydrocarbyl mechanism is connected                with an input end of the purification mechanism. A                circulation switch valve (250) is used to circulate                the liquid ammonia refrigerant between a                liquefaction purification mechanism (210) and a                liquid ammonia refrigeration mechanism (220). A                first switch valve (240) and the circulation switch                valve are used for closing after a central control                device sends a stop signal.
   USE - Device for producing liquid carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid carbon dioxide production device                has high timeliness and high safety of emergency                treatment under emergency abnormal condition.

PT P
PN WO2022130380-A1
TI Making alkali carbonate/bicarbonate salts by            feeding aqueous alkali hydroxide solution into            gas-liquid contactor, forcing incoming carbon            dioxide-containing gas stream, adding hydrogen peroxide            to orifices, and discharging effluent.
AU MAAYAN M
   STOIN U
AE AIROVATION TECHNOLOGIES LTD (AIRO-Non-standard)
GA 202281654U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alkali carbonate/bicarbonate salts,                comprises: continuously feeding aqueous alkali                hydroxide solution into a gas-liquid contactor                (10); forcing incoming carbon dioxide-containing                gas stream through a sparging device submerged in                the gas-liquid contactor below the surface level of                the aqueous alkali hydroxide solution, to generate                bubbles and/or microbubbles; adding hydrogen                peroxide in proximity to orifices of the sparging                device, from which the bubbles and/or microbubbles                evolve, where the supply of hydrogen peroxide is                adjusted to decrease alkali carbonate formation and                increase alkali bicarbonate formation; and                continuously discharging an effluent from the                gas-liquid contactor and recovering carbonate and                bicarbonate alkali salts from it predominated by                the bicarbonate component.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing alkali                carbonate/bicarbonate salts.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   a gas-liquid contactor comprising a                longitudinal horizontal housing bounded by a bottom                surface, a top section and lateral faces, an array                of tunnel-shaped sparging units placed horizontally                and parallel to each other in the interior of the                housing, where a sparging unit (28) is bounded by                an upward facing curved surface with orifices                distributed on the curved surface, an array of                tubes placed horizontally and parallel to each                other in the interior of the housing, each tube is                provided with nozzle tips arranged along its                length, with at least one tube being disposed in a                space between a pair of adjacent tunnel-shaped                sparging units, a gas inlet manifold (14) coupled                to the array of tunnel-shaped sparging units for                introducing individual gas streams into the                tunnel-shaped sparging units, a gas outlet opening                located in the top section connected to a gas                discharge line, a first liquid feed line configured                to provide a liquid flow of an aqueous alkali                hydroxide into the housing via one or more liquid                inlet openings, a second liquid feed line                configured to provide liquid flow of aqueous                hydrogen peroxide across the array of tubes, and a                discharge opening, to which an effluent discharge                line is connected, to remove reaction product from                the gas-liquid contactor; and
   an apparatus for producing alkali                bicarbonate/carbonate, to be placed at the vicinity                of a carbon dioxide-emitting plant e.g. a power                plant, incineration plant and steam methane                reforming plant, comprising (a) the gas-liquid                contactor, (b) a first blower forcing air/carbon                dioxide stream through the gas-liquid contactor,                (c) a set of pumps to deliver liquids and slurries,                (d) upstream processing units including (i) at                least one tank accommodating an aqueous alkali                hydroxide solution, connected to the first liquid                feed line of the gas/liquid contactor, (ii) at                least one tank accommodating hydrogen peroxide,                connected to the second liquid feed line of the                gas/liquid contactor, and (iii) a first heat                exchanger using ambient air for heat transfer, the                heat exchanger is provided with a feed line to                receive air/carbon dioxide stream from a chimney of                the plant, where the outlet of the heat exchanger                is connected to a first gas liquid separator,                equipped with a gas discharge line and a liquid                discharge line, to withdraw air/carbon dioxide and                water streams, respectively, and the gas discharge                line is connected to the gas inlet manifold of the                gas-liquid contactor, and (a) downstream processing                units including a gas-liquid separator fed by an                effluent discharge line of the gas-liquid                contactor, where a gas discharge line of the                gas-liquid separator optionally joins the gas                discharge line of the gas-liquid contactor, and a                liquid discharge line of the gas-liquid separator                splits into two lines, a first line is a recycle                line connected to the gas-liquid contactor, the                recycle line being equipped with a second heat                exchanger using chilled water for heat transfer,                and a second line which is a feed line of an                evaporation unit , the gas discharge line of the                evaporation unit is provided with a condenser, and                the liquid discharge line of the evaporation unit                is the feed line of a liquid-solid separation unit,                equipped with a conveyer supplying separated solids                to a dryer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of                perspective view of the interior of the gas-liquid                contactor.
   10Gas-liquid contactor
   14Gas inlet manifold
   24Pipe
   28Sparging units
   32Dashed line
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the contactor: the                  gas inlet manifold is installed externally to a                  first lateral face of the gas-liquid contactor;                  the aqueous alkali hydroxide inlet openings are                  located in the first lateral side of the housing;                  and the second liquid feed line is installed in a                  second lateral side, opposite to the first                  lateral side. The second feed line comprises a                  manifold splitting the main hydrogen peroxide                  feed solution into many individual stream which                  enter the gas-liquid contactor and flow across                  the array of tubes disposed in the gas/ liquid                  contactor. The orifices are distributed densely                  along the length of the curved surface of the                  tunnel-shaped sparging unit, creating a pattern                  consisting of orifices arranged in transverse                  arcs, where adjacent arcs are spaced 0.5-10 mm                  apart, each arc consisting of a many orifices,                  with the center-to-center distance between                  adjacent orifices being 0.5-5 mm. The diameter of                  the orifice is 50-700 &#956; m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step of incoming                  carbon dioxide-containing gas stream flows                  through an array of tunnel-shaped sparging units                  submerged in the gas-liquid contactor below the                  surface level of the aqueous alkali hydroxide                  solution, where a sparging unit is bounded, at                  least in part by a curved surface, the orifices                  of the sparging unit are distributed on its                  curved surface and the diameter of the orifices                  is 50-700 &#956; m. The addition of hydrogen                  peroxide to the gas-liquid contactor takes place                  by injecting many individual hydrogen peroxide                  streams in proximity to the orifices. The flow                  rate of aqueous hydrogen peroxide is increased or                  decreased in response to analysis of the                  distribution of bicarbonate/carbonate salts in                  the product mixture. Preferred Components: The                  concentration of the alkali hydroxide solution is                  10 wt.% and up to saturation, the flow rate of                  the alkali hydroxide solution is 10-120 m3/hour,                  the concentration of the hydrogen peroxide                  solution is 4-50 wt.% and the flow rate of the                  hydrogen peroxide solution is adjusted of 1-20                  m3/hour. The molar concentration of the alkali                  bicarbonate in the product recovered is                  &#8814; 90%. The carbon dioxide-containing                  gas stream is from a chimney of a power plant,                  incineration unit or steam methane reforming                  plant.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of a set of                experiments was conducted to study the effect of                addition of hydrogen peroxide on the chemical                absorption of carbon dioxide from carbon                dioxide/air stream bubbled through sodium hydroxide                solution that do not support the claims.

PT P
PN US2021354987-A1
TI Helium purification process, comprises e.g.            separating feed stream containing helium, nitrogen,            hydrogen and methane, and which contains no carbon            dioxide or only contains carbon dioxide, partly            removing water generated by catalysis of            helium-enriched, purifying, and separating.
AU HERNANDEZ A
   LE GULUDEC E
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2021D0285U
AB    NOVELTY - Helium purification process, comprises:                separating feed stream containing at least 10%                helium, at least 10% nitrogen, hydrogen and                methane, and which contains no carbon dioxide or                only contains carbon dioxide as minor component by                partial condensation and/or distillation at partial                condensation process and/or distillation process,                respectively to form helium-enriched stream                containing hydrogen, first stream (9) enriched in                nitrogen and in methane, and second stream (11)                enriched in nitrogen and in methane; sending the                helium-enriched stream to a catalytic unit in                presence of oxygen to reduce its hydrogen content;                treating the helium-enriched to at least partly                remove water generated by catalysis in the                catalytic unit; purifying the helium-enriched                stream from which at least part of the water has                been removed by adsorption in first adsorption unit                to obtain helium-rich gas comprising at least 95                mol.% helium and helium-depleted residual gas                containing water, and separating.
   USE - The method is useful for purification of                helium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids discharging of methane into                the atmosphere, which is gas with strong greenhouse                effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Helium purification process, comprises:                separating feed stream containing at least 10%                helium, at least 10% nitrogen in addition to                hydrogen and methane, and which contains no carbon                dioxide or only contains carbon dioxide as minor                component by partial condensation and/or                distillation at a partial condensation process                and/or distillation process, respectively to form                helium-enriched stream containing hydrogen, first                stream (9) enriched in nitrogen and in methane, and                a second stream (11) enriched in nitrogen and in                methane; sending the helium-enriched stream to a                catalytic unit in presence of oxygen to reduce its                hydrogen content; treating the helium-enriched to                at least partly remove water generated by catalysis                in the catalytic unit; purifying the                helium-enriched stream from which at least part of                the water has been removed by adsorption in a first                adsorption unit to produce helium-rich gas                comprising at least 95 mol.% helium and                helium-depleted residual gas containing water;                separating the helium-depleted residual gas                containing water by adsorption in a second                adsorption unit to remove water and producing                residual gas from which water is removed; sending                the first stream enriched in nitrogen and in                methane to a combustion unit to participate in                combustion to produce combustion gases that are                discharged to the atmosphere or recovered; sending                the second stream enriched in nitrogen and in                methane, as regeneration gas, to the second                adsorption unit to produce spent regeneration gas;                and sending the spent regeneration gas to the                combustion unit to participate in combustion.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                helium purification unit, comprising: a separation                unit for separation by partial condensation and/or                by distillation; a catalytic unit adapted and                configured to receive helium-enriched stream from                the separation unit and reduce hydrogen content in                presence of oxygen; a cooling unit adapted and                configured to receive, from the catalytic unit, the                hydrogen-reduced helium-enriched stream, the                cooling unit further being adapted and configured                to at least partly remove water from the                hydrogen-reduced helium-enriched stream, by                condensation, that is generated by catalysis in the                catalytic unit; a first adsorption unit adapted and                configured to receive at least partially                water-removed and hydrogen-reduced helium-enriched                stream from the cooling unit, the first adsorption                unit is a pressure swing adsorption unit, the first                adsorption unit is further adapted and configured                to produce, from the at least partially                water-removed and hydrogen-reduced helium-enriched                stream, helium-rich gas comprising at least 95                mol.% helium and helium-depleted residual gas                containing water; a second adsorption unit adapted                and configured to a) receive helium-depleted                residual gas from the first adsorption unit, (b)                separate the helium-depleted residual gas                containing water by adsorption remove water to                produce residual gas from which water is removed,                and (c) receive a second stream enriched in                nitrogen and in methane from the separation unit,                as regeneration gas, and produce a spent                regeneration gas, the second adsorption unit is a                temperature swing adsorption unit; and a combustion                unit adapted and configured to (a1) receive a first                stream enriched in nitrogen and in methane, from                the separation unit, (b1) receive the spent                regeneration gas, (c1) producing combustion gases                from combustion of the first stream and the spent                regeneration gas, and (d1) discharge the combustion                gases to the atmosphere or allow the combustion                gases to be recovered.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  comprises sending the residual gas to the partial                  condensation process and/or distillation process.                  In the process: the first adsorption unit is a                  pressure swing adsorption unit; and the second                  adsorption unit is a temperature swing adsorption                  unit.Preferred Components: In the process: the                  residual gas and the feed stream are compressed                  upstream of the partial condensation process                  and/or distillation process in a same compressor;                  the first and second streams have a same                  composition; the feed stream contains at least 30                  mol.%, preferably at least 40 mol.% helium; and                  the second stream contains at least 85 mol.%                  nitrogen and at least 1 mol.% methane.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J07 (Refrigeration; ice; gas liquefaction/ solidification - including machines, freezing of (semi)liquids, gas separation/ liquefaction by cooling or pressure, fractionation of air (F25).); Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))

PT P
PN CN107898877-A
TI Manufacturing anti-aging plant extract comprises e.g. taking garlic, encapsulating allicin, taking grape seeds, subjecting for solid-liquid separation, taking green tea leaves, performing ultrasonic extraction, mixing and storing.
AU DING W
AE DING W (DING-Individual)
GA 201830182L
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing anti-aging plant extract comprises e.g. (i) taking garlic, peeling, chopping cleaned garlic and encapsulating allicin; (ii) taking Polygonum cuspidatumroot, chopping, heating and lowering the pressure; (iii) taking grape seeds, soaking in ethanol aqueous solution and subjecting soaked grape seed for solid-liquid separation; (iv) taking green tea leaves and performing ultrasonic extraction; and (v) mixing allicin, Polygonum cuspidatum root extract, grape seed extract, and green tea extract, sealing mixture under vacuum and storing in a freezer.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing anti-aging plant extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high extraction effect and produces product with high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing anti-aging plant extract comprises (i) taking garlic, peeling garlic, removing head , molding, removing rotten garlic, washing, chopping cleaned garlic to a particle size of not greater than 2 mm, soaking in absolute ethanol of 5-8 times the mass of the garlic for 5-10 minutes, subjecting soaked garlic crumbs to solid-liquid separation to obtain a liquid ethanol-garlic liquid, adding ethanol and garlic liquid into the extraction kettle setting in the supercritical extraction equipment, continuously introducing carbon dioxide with a purity of not less than 99.95% in the extraction kettle, heating to 32-35 degrees C, by increasing the pressure to 7.3-7.5 Mpa and passing carbon dioxide is in a critical state of solid and liquid through the extraction kettle, passing the critical state fluid through the extraction kettle into the flash tank, lowering the pressure to 6-6.5 MPa, remaining in the flash tank containing allicin, encapsulating allicin under vacuum and storing in a freezer at 0-5 degrees C to obtain the desired allicin; (ii) taking Polygonum cuspidatumroot, removing rotten roots, washing, chopping roots of Polygonum cuspidatum to a particle size of not greater than 0.5 mm, soaking in an aqueous 85% ethanol solution for 15-20, allowing to stand for 5-10 minutes, soaked roots subjecting to solid-liquid separation to obtain a liquid ready solution, adding the solution into the extraction kettle setting in the supercritical extraction equipment, continuously introducing carbon dioxide with a purity of not less than 99.95% in the extraction kettle, heating to 32-35 degrees C, increasing the pressure to 7.3-7.5 Mpa, passing carbon dioxide is in a critical state of solid and liquid through the extraction kettle, passing the critical state fluid through the extraction kettle into the flash tank, lowering the pressure to 6-6.5 MPa, remaining in the flash tank containing extract of root of Polygonum cuspidatum; (iii) taking grape seeds, washing the grape seeds, chopping grape seeds into a particle size of not greater than 0.1 mm, soaking in a 50% ethanol aqueous solution having a mass of 15-20 times that of the grape seed grains, and maintaining for 5-10 minutes, subjecting soaked grape seed for solid-liquid separation to obtain a liquid ready solution, adding solution into the extraction kettle setting in the supercritical extraction equipment, continuously introducing carbon dioxide with a purity of not less than 99.95% in the extraction kettle, heating to 32-35 degrees C, increasing the pressure to 7.3-7.5 Mpa, passing carbon dioxide is in a critical state of solid and liquid through the extraction kettle, passing the critical state fluid through the extraction kettle into the flash tank, lowering the pressure to 6-6.5 MPa, remaining in the flash tank containing grape seed extract; (iv) taking green tea leaves, removing mildew green tea leaves, washing, chopping cleaned green tea leaves mechanically into a particle size of 1-2 mm, adding ethyl acetate at weight ratio of 1:8.5-10, stirring, placing mixture in a 50-70 degrees C and using a 100-200W ultrasonic generator for ultrasonic extraction of the mixture for 5-8 hours to obtain a solid content of 20-40% concentrated extract and useless leaf residue to obtain green tea extract; and (v) mixing 20-30 pts. wt. allicin, 15-20 pts. wt. Polygonum cuspidatum root extract, 35-45 pts. wt. grape seed extract, and 12-15 pts. wt. green tea extract, sealing mixture under vacuum and storing in a freezer at 0-5 degrees C. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for anti-aging plant extract, manufactured as mentioned above.
TF ACTIVITY - Nootropic; Dermatological.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)
MC B04-A08; B04-A10; B04-A98; B10-A04; B14-J01A4; B14-N17; B14-S18

PN US2022145337-A1
TI Performing biological and chemical technique for capture and conversion of carbon dioxide into organic compounds including biofuels or other useful industrial products, involves introducing carbon dioxide gas, either alone and/or dissolved in mixture or solution into environment.
AU REED J S
AE KIVERDI INC (KIVE-Non-standard)
GA 202267172D
AB    NOVELTY - Performing biological and chemical technique for the capture and conversion of carbon dioxide into organic compounds involves introducing carbon dioxide gas, either alone and/or dissolved in a mixture or solution further comprising carbonate ion and/or bicarbonate ion into an environment suitable for maintaining chemoautotrophic organisms and/or chemoautotroph cell extracts and fixing the carbon dioxide and/or inorganic carbon into organic compounds within the environment via at least one chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction utilizing chemoautotrophic microorganisms comprising a Ralstonia sp., an Alcaligenes sp., or a Hydrogenomoas sp., where the chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is driven by chemical and/or electrochemical energy provided by electron donors and electron acceptors that have been generated chemically and/or electrochemically and/or are introduced into the environment from at least one source external to the environment.
   USE - Method for performing biological and chemical technique for the capture and conversion of carbon dioxide into organic compounds including biofuels or other useful industrial, chemical, pharmaceutical, or biomass products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform biological and chemical technique for the capture and conversion of carbon dioxide into organic compounds, which enables the effective capture of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere or from a point source of carbon dioxide emissions for the production of liquid transportation fuel and/or other organic chemical products, which can help address greenhouse gas induced climate change and contribute to the domestic production of renewable liquid transportation fuels without any dependence upon agriculture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The electron donor comprises hydrogen and electron acceptor comprises carbon dioxide and/or oxygen, where the environment suitable for maintaining chemoautotrophic organisms and/or chemoautotroph cell extracts is maintained using continuous influx and removal of nutrient medium and/or biomass, in substantially steady state where the cell population and environmental parameters are targeted at a substantially constant suitable or optimal level over time, and where biomass is produced by the at least one chemosynthetic reaction, and where the biomass is separated from the environment and is processed into a product comprising an animal feed, a fertilizer, a soil additive, a soil stabilizer, a carbon source for large scale fermentations, a nutrient source for the growth of other microbes or organisms, and/or as a source of pharmaceutical, medicinal or nutritional substances.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The fixing step is followed by one or more process steps in which organic and/or inorganic chemical products of chemosynthesis are separated from a process stream produced during the fixing step and processed to form products in a form suitable for storage, shipping, and sale. The electron donors and/or electron acceptors are generated or recycled using renewable, alternative, or conventional sources of power that are low in greenhouse gas emissions, and where sources of power are selected from at least one of photovoltaics, solar thermal, wind power, hydroelectric, nuclear, geothermal, enhanced geothermal, ocean thermal, ocean wave power, tidal power, and carbon capture and sequestration enabled methane reforming or carbon capture and sequestration enabled gasification or pyrolysis of coal or biomass. The method involves reacting carbon dioxide with minerals to form a carbonate or bicarbonate product. The carbon dioxide is introduced in the introducing step, and where the carbon dioxide is dissolved in aqueous solution. The molecular hydrogen acts as an electron donor and where hydrogen concentrations between 4 to 74.5% are avoided. The culture broth is continuously removed from the environment suitable for maintaining chemoautotrophic organisms and flowed through membrane filters to separate the cell mass from the broth. The biomass produced by at least one chemosynthetic reaction is centrifuged and then dried with evaporation, and where the biomass product is collected from the dryers. The remaining water and nutrients separated from the biomass are pumped back into the environment suitable for maintaining chemoautotrophic organisms. The hydrogen is generated through an electrolysis method selected from the group comprising proton exchange membrane electrolysis, liquid electrolyte electrolysis, high pressure electrolysis, and high temperature electrolysis of steam.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)
MC D05-A04A; E11-K03; E10-J02D; E11-F03; E11-G05; E11-D; H09-F03; H06-A04; H06-P

PN CN114456852-A
TI Method for processing waste liquid for blast            furnace coal gas carbon capture, involves conveying            carbon dioxide absorbing liquid in liquid box and            cooling raw liquid in raw liquid box, and controlling            temperature reducing time of heat exchanging sheet set            on inner wall of carbon capturing tower.
AU XU T
   LIU C
   LIU Q
   LIU S
AE CHINA COAL WEIKUN BEIJING ENERGY CONSERV (CHCO-Non-standard)
   CCGC SECOND EXPLORATION BUREAU GROUP CO (CCGC-Non-standard)
GA 202268745E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves controlling an air inlet                adjusting valve fixed on an air inlet pipeline to                input a blast furnace gas with maximum air inlet                amount by using a central control unit (S1). Carbon                dioxide spraying absorption liquid in multiple                layers of medicine spraying device spraying agent                in a carbon capturing tower is controlled (S2) by                the central control unit to spray vortex blast                furnace gas. A circulating water pump fixed on the                carbon dioxide enriched solution discharge pipe to                work is controlled (S3) by the central control                unit. Carbon dioxide absorbing liquid in a liquid                box and cooling raw liquid in a raw liquid box are                conveyed (S4). Temperature reducing time of a heat                exchanging sheet fixed on an inner wall of a carbon                capturing tower is controlled (S5).
   USE - Method for processing waste liquid for blast                furnace coal gas carbon capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving carbon dioxide                capture efficiency and carbon dioxide and capture                amine absorption liquid regeneration utilization                efficiency. The method allows the carbon capturing                tower to be provided with a gas temperature probe                for detecting temperature in the carbon capture                tower, thus preventing temperature from being too                high and preventing decomposition and release of                carbon dioxide when actual temperature of the tower                exceeds the tower standard temperature, and hence                cooling the tower temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                the method for processing waste liquid for blast                furnace coal gas carbon capture. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
   S1Step for controlling air inlet adjusting                valve fixed on air inlet pipeline to input blast                furnace gas with maximum air inlet amount by using                central control unit
   S2Step for controlling carbon dioxide                spraying absorption liquid in multiple layers of                medicine spraying device spraying agent in carbon                capturing tower by central control unit to spray                vortex blast furnace gas
   S3Step for controlling circulating water pump                fixed on carbon dioxide enriched solution discharge                pipe to work by central control unit
   S4Step for conveying carbon dioxide absorbing                liquid in liquid box and cooling raw liquid in raw                liquid box
   S5Step for controlling temperature reducing                time of heat exchanging sheet fixed on inner wall                of carbon capturing tower

PN CN110847988-A; CN110847988-B
TI Peak shaving energy storage device using carbon            dioxide as working medium, has energy release subsystem            that includes energy release heat exchanger, external            heat and temperature storage tank that are provided for            multi-stage energy release.
AU XIE Y
   SUN L
   ZHANG D
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 202020091V
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an energy storage system and an                energy release system. The energy storage system                includes a carbon dioxide energy storage                circulation subsystem and first and second energy                storage subsystems. The first energy storage                subsystem includes a first energy storage heat                exchanger (3), a third energy storage heat                exchanger (7), a second low temperature storage                tank (11), a second high temperature storage tank                (12), and a first ice water mixed storage tank (15)                and second ice-water mixed storage tank (16). The                energy release system includes a carbon dioxide                energy release cycle subsystem, and first and a                second energy release subsystems. The second energy                release subsystem includes a fourth energy release                heat exchanger, external heat, a first low                temperature storage tank (9), a first high                temperature storage tank (10), a high pressure                carbon dioxide storage tank (13), and low pressure                carbon dioxide storage tank (14) which are provided                for multi-stage release of energy.
   USE - Peak shaving energy storage device using                carbon dioxide as working medium.
   ADVANTAGE - The peak shifting and valley filling of                electric power are effectively realized, and the                flexibility and efficiency of user energy                utilization is improved. The device is simple and                flexible in application, realizes multiple levels                of energy storage and release functions, and meets                a wide range of energy storage applications. The                device has large adjustment range, stable                operation, and is easy to popularize and use. The                motor is connected to balance the axial thrust and                improves the compactness of the device. The                multi-level energy storage and release are                realized, and flexibly performs peak shift and                valley filling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for controlling a peak-shaving energy storage                device using carbon dioxide as a working                medium.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the                peak shaving energy storage device using carbon                dioxide as working medium.
   First energy storage heat exchanger                (3)
   Third energy storage heat exchanger                (7)
   First low temperature storage tank (9)
   First high temperature storage tank                (10)
   Second low temperature storage tank                (11)
   Second high temperature storage tank                (12)
   High pressure carbon dioxide storage tank                (13)
   Low pressure carbon dioxide storage tank                (14)

PN CN114015480-A
TI High-purity carbon dioxide process system used for deep purification of blast furnace gas by-product, comprises water spray cooler, dechlorination and dust removal tower, absorption tower, regeneration tower, drying tower, and hydrogen sulfide removal tower are connected through pipelines.
AU WU X
   CHEN M
AE SPIC YUANDA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION EQU (CPIC-C)
GA 202228715J
AB    NOVELTY - High-purity carbon dioxide process system comprises a water spray cooler (1), a dechlorination and dust removal tower (2), an absorption tower, a regeneration tower (3), a drying tower (11), and a hydrogen sulfide removal tower (12) are connected through pipelines. The blast furnace gas is cooled to 40-60degrees Celsius through the spray cooler, after entering the dust removal tower, the chlorine ions and dust in the gas are adsorbed and removed by the dechlorination agent, and enter the absorption tower, after the carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, carbonyl sulfide, and carbon disulfide in the gas are absorbed by a lean liquid solvent, and are sent to an existing gas pipeline network. The rich liquid that has absorbed carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, carbonyl sulfide, and carbon disulfide is sent to the regeneration tower by the rich liquid pump through the lean and rich liquid heat exchanger, after being treated by the regeneration tower, lean liquid and regeneration gas are formed.
   USE - The high-purity carbon dioxide process system is useful for deep purification of blast furnace gas by-product.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is efficient and safe, and quickly separates the carbon dioxide in the blast furnace gas to obtain high-purity carbon dioxide gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the overall structure of a high-purity carbon dioxide process system.
   water spray cooler (1)
   Dechlorination and dust removal tower (2)
   Regeneration tower (3)
   Lean liquid pump (5)
   Regeneration tower bottom reboiler (10)
   Drying tower (11)
   Hydrogen sulfide removal tower (12)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dechlorination agent is a solid mixture composed of oxides and carbonates of alkali metals and alkaline earth metals, and the main components comprise sodium carbonate, calcium carbonate, calcium oxide, magnesium oxide, and magnesium carbonate. The lean liquid solvent is a solution prepared of monoethanolamine, methyldiethanolamine, diisopropanolamine and demineralized water. The hydrogen sulfide removal tower has a two-layer structure, where the lower layer adopts solid iron oxide, and the upper layer adopts zinc oxide as the absorbent.

PN CN113145123-A
TI Low-temperature high-activity nickel-based catalyst used for carbon dioxide methanation process comprises active metal nickel and oxide carrier.
AU YAN B
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 202191533A
AB    NOVELTY - A low-temperature high-activity nickel-based catalyst comprises 10-90 wt.% active metal nickel and oxide carrier. The active metal nickel and oxide carrier are uniformly dispersed and have strong interaction.
   USE - The low-temperature high-activity nickel-based catalyst is used for carbon dioxide methanation process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can achieve 100% carbon dioxide conversion and 100% methane selectivity within 200-300 degrees C, and has good thermal stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst comprising adding 0.002-0.060 mol soluble salt of active metal nickel and 0.002-0.060 mol soluble salt of oxide carrier metal M to 50-250 mL solvent respectively, and stirring at 20-40 degrees C for 10-30 minutes to obtain mixed metal solution; adding 0.05-0.40 mol precipitant solid to 100-250 mL solvent, and stirring at 20-40 degrees C for 10-30 minutes to obtain precipitant solution; continuously co-precipitating the above two solutions at flow rate of 5-50 mL/minute respectively to obtain precipitation slurry, stirring for 1-5 minutes, centrifuging at speed of 3000-6000 revolutions/minute for 2-5 minutes, discarding supernatant, washing precipitate in centrifuge tube, adding solvent as detergent, mixing precipitate and solvent, repeating above centrifugal operation, and washing for 2-5 times to obtain precipitate; and drying to 80-150 degrees C for 10-15 hours, grinding for 5-20 minutes, calcining to 300-600 degrees C for 1-4 hours; and
   (2) a carbon dioxide methanation process comprising adding low temperature and high activity nickel-based carbon dioxide methanation catalyst into methane reformer, and performing carbon dioxide methanation reaction at carbon dioxide volumetric space velocity of 10000-120000/hour, hydrogen and carbon dioxide molar ratio of 20-2000, reaction temperature of 200-400 degrees C and at pressure of 1-5 bar.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxide carrier is cerium dioxide, lanthanum oxide, europium oxide, zirconium dioxide and/or samarium oxide. The concentration of active metal nickel in the mixed metal solution is 0.001-1 mol/L. The soluble salt of active metal nickel is nickel acetate, nickel chloride and/or nickel nitrate. The carrier metal M is cerium, lanthanum, europium, zirconium and/or samarium. The soluble salt of carrier metal M is chloride, acetate and/or nitrate. The concentration of precipitation agent is 0.1-5 mol/L. The solvent used is absolute ethanol and/or deionized water. The precipitant is oxalic acid, ammonium carbonate, ammoniaand/or sodium hydroxide.

TI Method for the introduction of carbon dioxide into            thermal water in an underground reservoir designated            and suitable for the geothermal generation of energy by            an apparatus, involves discharging carbon dioxide from            a nozzle.
AU BUTZ U
   BORCHERT F
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE DEUT GMBH (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE DEUT GMBH (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202122083D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves discharging the carbon                dioxide from a nozzle (4) and introducing into the                thermal water (2) in the gaseous state at a                discharge flow velocity. The discharge flow                velocity is above a lower limit value which is                selected in such a way that backflow of the thermal                water into the nozzle is prevented and is below an                upper limit value which is selected in such a way                that stripping of carbon dioxide dissolved in the                thermal water is prevented. The lower limit value                is in the range from 1.5 to 2.5 m/s. The upper                limit value is in the range from 9 to 14 m/s. The                discharge flow velocity is controlled while taking                one of the following properties of the thermal                water into account a temperature, a pH, a                composition, a flow velocity and a pressure.
   USE - Method for the introduction of carbon dioxide                into thermal water in an underground reservoir                designated and suitable for the geothermal                generation of energy by an apparatus                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide introduction makes it                possible to reduce the carbon dioxide loss due to                mechanical stresses, so that the formation of the                corresponding deposits is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for the geothermal generation of                energy; and
   (2) an apparatus for the introduction of                carbon dioxide into thermal water in an underground                reservoir designated and suitable for the                geothermal generation of energy.
