PN CN111473540-A; CN212157716-U
TI Carbon dioxide supercritical power generation            coupled transcritical refrigeration cycle system            comprises heat energy recovery device, expander,            generator and power cycle regenerator, where outlet of            expander is connected to generator.
AU LI M
   ZHANG Y
   WU Y
   CHEN R
   LING C
   ZHANG Q
   YE B
AE UNIV GUANGDONG OCEAN (UYOG-C)
GA 2020744701
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide supercritical power generation                coupled transcritical refrigeration cycle system                comprises a heat energy recovery device (1), an                expander (2), a generator (4), a power cycle                regenerator (5), a mixer (27), a seawater                desalination heat exchanger (28), a gas cooler (6),                a diversion valve (26) and a high pressure                compressor (3), where the circulating working fluid                in the supercritical carbon dioxide power                generation system is carbon dioxide. The working                fluid outlet of the heat energy recovery device is                connected to the inlet of the expander. The outlet                of the expander is connected to the generator. The                outlet of the expander is connected to the inlet of                the power cycle regenerator. The outlet of the                power cycle regenerator is connected to the inlet                of the mixer. The inlet of the mixer is connected                to the outlet of a medium pressure compressor (15).                The outlet of the mixer is connected to the inlet                of the seawater desalination heat exchanger.
   USE - Carbon dioxide supercritical power generation                coupled transcritical refrigeration cycle                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide supercritical power                generation coupled transcritical refrigeration                cycle system effectively utilizes the thermal                performance of carbon dioxide, ensures the coupling                effect on waste heat recovery working conditions                and refrigeration working conditions, and realizes                the combined design of the power generation                function and the refrigeration function.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of the carbon dioxide supercritical power                generation coupled transcritical refrigeration                cycle system.
   Heat energy recovery device (1)
   Expander (2)
   High pressure compressor (3)
   Generator (4)
   Power cycle regenerator (5)
   Gas cooler (6)
   Medium pressure compressor (15)
   Diversion valve (26)
   Mixer (27)
   Seawater desalination heat exchanger                (28)

PT P
PN WO2019028519-A1
TI Forming carbon dioxide adsorbent of rebreather apparatus for removing carbon dioxide from individual user's breathing gas, comprises forming paste comprising mixture material and solvent into shaped body and heat treating the shaped body.
AU LYONS T P
   HILL M R
   MULET X
   LAU C H
   BATTEN M
AE COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)
   ONE ATMOSPHERE PTY LTD (ONEA-Non-standard)
GA 2019154083
AB    NOVELTY - Forming carbon dioxide adsorbent of a rebreather apparatus comprises: preparing a SIFSIX-3-Ni (sic) paste comprising a mixture of SIFSIX-3-Ni material and a solvent, where the SIFSIX-3-Ni material is substantially comprising 2-dimensional SIFSIX-3-Ni structure; forming the SIFSIX-3-Ni paste into a shaped body having at least one mean dimension of greater than 0.5 mm; and heat treating the shaped body in a reduced pressure environment comprising a pressure of less than 500 mbar at a temperature of at most 160 degrees C to substantially remove the solvent from the shaped body and form 3-dimensional SIFSIX-3-Ni crystal structure in the shaped body, thus producing shaped adsorbent body for use in a rebreather apparatus.
   USE - The method is useful for forming carbon dioxide adsorbent of rebreather apparatus which is used for removing carbon dioxide from individual user's breathing gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method forms the apparatus that is replacement of soda lime and performance at the gram-scale has been shown to be 37% that of soda lime..
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) forming carbon dioxide filter of a rebreather apparatus, comprising producing shaped adsorbent bodies and sealing the shaped adsorbent bodies into a housing under an inert gas atmosphere; and
   (2) rebreathing apparatus for removing carbon dioxide from an individual user's breathing gas, including a carbon dioxide filter comprising housing containing in it, a packed bed of shaped adsorbent bodies, where the shaped adsorbent bodies comprise SIFSIX-3-Ni having at least 60% 3-dimensional SIFSIX-3-Ni crystal structure, and the balance being 2- dimensional SIFSIX-3-Ni structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: At least 60%, preferably at least 99% of the 2-dimensional SIFSIX-3-Ni structure of the shaped body is transformed into 3-dimensional SIFSIX-3-Ni crystal structure in the heat treatment step. The heat treatment step is conducted at a pressure of less than 100 mbar, preferably less than 35 mbar. The heat treatment step is conducted at 110-160 degrees C. The heat treatment step is conducted for at least 5 hours, preferably at least 10 hours. The heat treatment step comprises an initial heating step of heat treating the shaped body at least 80 degrees C, preferably 80 degrees C at a pressure of less than 500 mbar, preferably less than 100 mbar for at least 12 hours, preferably at least 24 hours. The heat treatment step comprises a temperature regime of a first heating step in which the temperature is kept at 140-160 degrees C, preferably 150 degrees C for less than 5 hours, preferably 2-5 hours and second heating step in which the temperature is lowered to at most 130 degrees C for at least 5 hours, preferably 8-12 hours. The inactivated SIFSIX-3-Ni material is synthesized from a mixture of silicon hexafluoride precursor compound, a nickel precursor compound and pyrazine, in which the precursor compound is salt of silicon hexafluoride (SiF6) and the nickel precursor compound is at least one of a salt comprising nickel2and nickel nitrate. The step of forming the paste into a shaped body comprises at least one of extruding, palletizing or molding the paste into a desired 3-dimensional configuration. The forming step, the paste is extruded into an elongate body which is subsequently longitudinally divided. The method further comprises drying the shaped body at a temperature of at least 20 degrees C for at least 1 hour prior to the heat treatment step. The heat treatment step is conducted in a fluid tight housing, and the method further comprises flushing an inert gas through the fluid tight housing. After the heat treatment step further comprises cooling the shaped body to at most 80 degrees C, preferably at most 60 degrees C under reduced pressure of at most 500 mbar, preferably at most 100 mbar. The method further comprises: adsorbing carbon dioxide onto the surfaces of the shaped body; and heating at a temperature of at least 80 degrees C, preferably 120 degrees C for at least 1 hour, preferably at least 2 hours at less than 100 mbar, preferably less than 50 mbar, thereby desorbing the carbon dioxide from the surfaces of the SIFSIX-3- Ni shaped body to produce a regenerated shaped body. The heating step is conducted under inert gas flushing. The housing includes an outlet that, in use, has a gas flow having 0% carbon dioxide gas stream for at least 1 hour. At least 70%, preferably at least 99%, the shaped adsorbent bodies comprise the 3-dimensional crystal structure. The adsorbent bodies are sealed within the housing in an inert gas atmosphere prior to use. Preferred Components: The shaped body has at least one mean dimension of greater than 0.8 mm, preferably at least 1.5 mm. Each of the mean width, mean depth and mean height of the shaped body are greater than 0.5 mm, preferably greater than 1 mm. The shaped body comprises an elongate body having a circular, or regular polygonal cross-sectional shape. The solvent comprises a non-basic polar solvent or a non-self ionizing polar solvent, preferably an alcohol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The housing includes two spaced apart support membranes configured to allow gas flow through each membrane, where the bodies forms a packed bed between and being compressed. The housing includes an inner container housing the support membranes and packed bed, the inner container being seated within the housing using a fitted insert which at least friction fits the inner container within the inner walls of the housing. The inner container is removably fitted within the housing. The apparatus further includes: first liquid free gas flow space that is in fluid communication with an inlet for the user's exhaled breathing gas, the exhaled breathing gas including carbon dioxide; second liquid free gas flow space that is in fluid communication with an outlet for fluid flow of gas that is substantially free of carbon dioxide where the first liquid free gas flow space is in fluid communication with the second liquid free gas flow space through the packed bed such that, in use, an exhaled gas stream can flow through the packed bed to facilitate adsorption of carbon dioxide from the user's exhaled breathing gas onto the adsorbent bodies. The shaped adsorbent bodies comprise elongate bodies having a circular, or regular polygonal cross-sectional shape. The adsorbent bodies are configured to adsorb carbon dioxide from an individual user's exhaled gas, and be regenerated to a substantially carbon dioxide free state in situ, within the filter, by heat treatment. The apparatus further includes at least one breathing apparatus, a breathing mask, a breathing suit or other life support system in fluid connection to the carbon dioxide filter.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inert gas atmosphere comprises at least one of nitrogen, or noble gas, preferably helium or argon.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).)

PN US2018179125-A1; US10793490-B2
TI Producing methanol and hydrocarbon compounds,            comprises directing methane and oxygen into oxidative            coupling of methane reactor, generating effluent stream            of carbon dioxide from formed stream, and directing            effluent to methanol reactor.
AU RADAELLI G
   BRIDGES R
   RAFIQUE H A
   DUGGAL S
   VUDDAGIRI S
   CIZERON J
   MCCORMICK J
   PATEL B
   LAKHAPATRI S
AE SILURIA TECHNOLOGIES INC (SILU-Non-standard)
   LUMMUS TECHNOLOGY LLC (CBRI-C)
GA 201850623V
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methanol and hydrocarbon compounds                containing at least two carbon atoms (more than 2C                compounds-A1), comprises (a) directing methane and                oxygen (130) into an oxidative coupling of methane                (OCM) reactor (128) to produce a product stream                comprising compounds-A1, carbon monoxide and/or                carbon dioxide and unreacted methane, (b)                generating an effluent stream comprising the carbon                monoxide and/or carbon dioxide from the product                stream, where a concentration of the carbon                monoxide and/or carbon dioxide in the effluent                stream is greater than a concentration of carbon                monoxide and/or carbon dioxide in the product                stream, and (c) directing the effluent stream to a                methanol reactor to produce methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for producing methanol                and hydrocarbon compounds containing at least two                carbon atoms (more than 2C compounds-A1).
   ADVANTAGE - The method consumes less than 150, preferably                less than 50 MMBtu of energy per ton of the                compounds-A1 isolated; provides the compounds-A1                selectivity of at most 70%, preferably at most 35%;                and is economical and/or exhibits improved                operational flexibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system for producing methanol and hydrocarbon                compounds containing at least two carbon atoms                (more than 2C compounds-A1), comprising the OCM                reactor that receives methane and oxygen and reacts                the methane and oxygen to yield a product stream                comprising the compounds-A1, carbon monoxide and/or                carbon dioxide, and unreacted methane, and a                methanol reactor that receives the carbon monoxide                and/or carbon dioxide from the product stream and                reacts the carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide to                produce methanol.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the                oxidative coupling of methane process.
   Natural gas (100)
   Ethane (102)
   Methanation module (128) Oxidative coupling                of methane reactor (110)
   Oxygen (130)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further                  comprises: the SMR that receives the unreacted                  methane from the product stream and provides                  hydrogen and at least one of carbon monoxide and                  carbon dioxide to the methanol reactor to produce                  methanol; a separation unit downstream of the OCM                  reactor and upstream of the methanol reactor,                  where the separation unit produces an effluent                  stream comprising the carbon monoxide and/or                  carbon dioxide from the product stream, and a                  concentration of the carbon monoxide and/or                  carbon dioxide in the effluent stream is greater                  than a concentration of carbon monoxide and/or                  carbon dioxide in the product stream; a                  separation unit downstream of the OCM reactor and                  upstream of the SMR, where the separation unit                  produces a methane stream comprising the                  unreacted methane from the product stream, and a                  concentration of the unreacted methane in the                  methane stream is greater than a concentration of                  the unreacted methane in the product stream; a                  cracker that receives the compounds-A1 and cracks                  or refines the compounds-A1; a cracker that                  receives at least a portion of the unreacted                  methane; and a methane-consuming module that                  receives the unreacted methane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: generating a methane stream comprising                  the unreacted methane from the product stream,                  where a concentration of the unreacted methane in                  the methane stream is greater than a                  concentration of the unreacted methane in the                  product stream; directing at least a portion of                  the methane stream to a steam methane reformer                  (SMR) that produces hydrogen and carbon monoxide                  and/or carbon dioxide, where at least a portion                  of the carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide is                  used to produce methanol; directing the carbon                  monoxide and/or carbon dioxide produced in the                  SMR to the methanol reactor, where all of the                  carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide from the                  product stream and all of the carbon monoxide                  and/or carbon dioxide from the SMR is converted                  to methanol in the methanol reactor; providing                  the compounds-A1 to a cracker that cracks or                  refines the compounds-A1, where at least 80% of                  the methane consumed by the SMR is from the                  methane stream; directing a portion of the                  methane stream to a cracker, where at least 80%                  of the methane consumed by the SMR and the                  cracker is from the methane stream; directing at                  least a portion of the methane stream to a                  methane-consuming process, where at least 80% of                  the methane consumed by the SMR, the cracker and                  the methane-consuming process is from the methane                  stream. The unreacted methane is provided as:                  fuel to the SMR; and feedstock to the SMR, where                  the SMR converts the unreacted methane into the                  hydrogen and at least one of the carbon monoxide                  and carbon dioxide for conversion to methanol in                  the methanol reactor, where at least 95% of the                  methane is converted into methanol or more than                  2C products. The product stream comprises carbon                  monoxide and/or carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Mechanism sand pumping concrete for sealing carbon            dioxide, comprises cement, fly ash, slag, wet            machine-made sand, dry machine sand, large stone, small            stone, water, water reducing agent, and carbon            dioxide.
AU ZHANG Y
   TIAN Y
   LUO S
   LIN Z
AE FANGCHENGGANG ZHIHUI DOUBLE CARBON IND (FANG-Non-standard)
GA 2022B7813R
AB    NOVELTY - Mechanism sand pumping concrete for sealing                carbon dioxide comprises cement, fly ash, slag, wet                sand, dry sand, large sand, small sand, water,                water-reducing agent and carbon dioxide. The                cement, fly ash, slag, wet machine-made sand, dry                machine sand, large stone, small stone according to                proportion into the stirring pot stirring.
   USE - Machine-made sand pumping concrete for sealing                carbon dioxide used in civil engineering material.                Uses include but are not limited to cement, fly                ash, slag, wet machine made sand, dry machine sand,                large stone, small stone, water, and water reducing                agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The mechanical sand pumping concrete                effectively seals the carbon dioxide permanent in                the concrete, reduces the emission of greenhouse                gas when the pre-mixed concrete, it has a certain                social benefit, at the same time, and improves the                concrete strength of 8-15 %, under the condition of                keeping the gel material dosage is not changed,                improving the strength grade of the concrete. It                can reduce the dosage of about 5-8 % of gel                material, so as to make each square concrete                generate additional value, it can greatly reduce                the cost, has large economic value, and the                preparation time of the invention is short, and it                greatly improves the industrial production                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the                mechanism sand pumping concrete for sealing carbon                dioxide and preparation method there.

PT P
PN CN114059088-A
TI Reducing and co-electrolyzing chloride salt and            carbon dioxide used in e.g. pharmaceutical industry,            involves taking transition metal-nitrogen supported            ordered mesoporous carbon to assemble electrolytic            cell, adding electrolyte, introducing carbon dioxide            into cathode side, and electrolyzing.
AU HU X
   DONG L
   LI W
   LU A
   HE L
   GE R
   HAO G
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202232078G
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing and co-electrolyzing chloride salt                and carbon dioxide involves: (S1) taking transition                metal-nitrogen supported ordered mesoporous carbon                as a cathode catalyst and ordered mesoporous                transition metal oxide as an anode catalyst to                assemble an electrolytic cell; (S2) adding an                electrolyte, and continuously introducing carbon                dioxide into the cathode side to make it saturated;                and (S3) continuously electrolyzing each                current/potential by constant current/potential                mode.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing and                co-electrolyzing chloride salt and carbon dioxide                used in fine chemical industry, pharmaceutical,                paper making and sewage treatment industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses ordered mesoporous transition                metal oxide as anode catalyst compared with the                commonly used noble metal ruthenium iridium                electrode, reduces the material cost, and can reach                the Faraday efficiency of 92%. The ordered                mesoporous structure of the cathode and anode can                fully diffuse carbon dioxide/chloride ion, which                increases the Faraday efficiency, improves the                energy efficiency of the system by 40% compared                with the traditional carbon dioxide reduction. The                single-chamber electrolytic cell is used for                co-electrolysis. When the cell voltage is 3.5 V,                the current density reaches 250 mA/cm2, the current                density is increased by 6 times than the                hydrogen-type electrolytic cell under the same cell                voltage to realize industrial-grade current                density.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier of the                  transition metal-nitrogen supported ordered                  mesoporous carbon catalyst is hexagonal ordered                  mesoporous carbon (CMK3) or noodle-like                  mesoporous carbon nanomaterial (CMK5). The                  transition metal includes iron, cobalt, and/or                  nickel. The ordered mesoporous transition metal                  oxide is an oxide of iron, cobalt or nickel. The                  cathode electrolyte is potassium bicarbonate                  solution, potassium chloride solution or sodium                  chloride solution. The anode electrolyte is                  potassium chloride solution or sodium chloride                  solution.Preferred Composition: The loading amount of                  the transition metal is 1-6%. The concentration                  of the electrolyte solution of the cathode or the                  anode is 0.5-3 M.Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the transition metal-nitrogen supported ordered                  mesoporous catalyst involves: (A) dissolving                  transition metal salt and ligand in a molar ratio                  of 1:1-1:4 in distilled water to form a complex                  solution; (B) testing the pore volume of the                  ordered mesoporous carbon by nitrogen adsorption,                  and soaking the ordered mesoporous carbon                  according to the pore volume in the complex                  solution; and (C) heat treating in an inert                  atmosphere, and cooling to room temperature. The                  preparation method of the ordered mesoporous                  transition metal oxide involves: (i) dissolving                  the transition metal salt in a solvent to form an                  immersion solution; (ii) testing a pore volume of                  mesoporous silica hard template (SBA-15) by                  nitrogen adsorption, and soaking the SBA-15                  according to the pore volume in half of the                  immersion solution; (iii) cooling to room                  temperature, calcining in air, and repeating step                  (ii) with the remaining immersion solution; (iv)                  calcining in air; and (v) leaching the SBA15,                  washing with water until neutral, and drying. The                  pore volume of the SBA-15 soaked with the                  transition metal salt solution is 7.5-30%, and                  the solvent is distilled water or ethanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ligand is                  o-phenanthroline or ethylenediamine.

PN US2021293165-A1
TI Process for removing carbon dioxide from exhaust            gas from gas turbines, boilers, and fired heaters in            coal-fired power plants, involves introducing gas            stream comprising carbon dioxide to non-scraped heat            exchanger, introducing antifreeze agent to gas stream,            and introducing mixed stream.
AU KAMINSKY R D
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2021B00978
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide removing process involves                introducing a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide                to a non-scraped heat exchanger to cool the gas                stream to a first temperature greater than about 0                degree Celsius, and less than about 10 degree                Celsius. An antifreeze agent is introduced to the                gas stream downstream from the non-scraped heat                exchanger to form a mixed stream, and introduced                the mixed stream to other non-scraped heat                exchanger to cool the mixed stream to a second                temperature greater than a freezing temperature of                the mixed stream. A cooled mixed stream is formed,                and introduced a portion of the cooled mixed stream                to a scraped heat exchanger to cool the cooled                mixed stream to a third temperature below which                solid carbon dioxide forms.
   USE - Process for removing carbon dioxide from                exhaust gas from gas turbines, boilers, and fired                heaters in coal-fired power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively removes carbon dioxide                from exhaust gas without being concerned that the                process will become less effective over time. The                method does not become less cost effective as the                scale of the process decreases.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a gas                turbine.
   Exhaust gas stream (12)
   Coolers (14,28)
   Exits separators (18,70)
   Compressor (24)
   Cooled gas streams (30,34,46)

PN WO2019060992-A1; AU2018340887-A1; CA3077118-A1; EP3687960-A1; US2020255349-A1; CN111527056-A; BR112020006048-A2; EP3687960-A4
TI Use of carbon dioxide gas to cure concrete media by applying a pressure differential created through a partial replacement of the original ambient volume of air present in a curing enclosure with pure carbon dioxide gas.
AU AL-GHOULEH Z
   HARGEST P W
AE CARBOCLAVE CORP (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBOCLAVE CORP (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBOCLAVE CORP (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBOCLAVE CORP (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 201930581R
AB    NOVELTY - Use of carbon dioxide gas to cure concrete media by applying a pressure differential created through a partial replacement of the original ambient volume of air present in a curing enclosure with pure carbon dioxide gas is claimed.
   USE - The carbon dioxide gas is useful to cure concrete media and for the preparation of precast or dry-cast concrete products, reinforced and non-reinforced, where the precast or dry-cast concrete products are masonry units, pavers, synthetic stone, bricks, hollow-core slabs, pipe, wall panels, fiber-cement boards, or retaining walls (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easily adaptable industrial process that practically exploits carbonation as a means to actively engage Portland cement within the timeframe of a conventional production cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) displaced air carbonation system using carbon dioxide gas to cure concrete media comprising airtight and pressurizable chamber having a composite polyurethane/polyurea sprayed inner lining with a flexible or rigid door assembly, where a pressure differential is applied and created through the partial replacement of original ambient volume of air present in a curing enclosure with pure carbon dioxide gas; and
   (2) kit to transform a chamber system into an airtight and pressurizable enclosure comprising composite polyurethane/polyurea sprayed inner lining, and polymer sheet door assembly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises air displacement step preceding carbonation intended for creating suction and achieving sub-atmospheric pressures within the curing enclosure of 0 to -5 psig, purging step preceding carbonation where carbon dioxide gas is continuously flowed into the curing enclosure to displace partial volumes of ambient air through an open outlet, where the outlet is closed when the exhausted air reaches a concentration of carbon dioxide gas that exceeds 10000 ppm, after the purging step, the curing enclosure reaches an internal carbon dioxide gas concentration of 20-50%, further step of regimented injection of carbon dioxide gas until an internal pressure of 0 to +2 psig is reached and kept constant for the duration of carbonation processing where the pressure differential is to create pressure-head for better gas diffusion within concrete material and to improve carbonation reaction where a need for pressure during carbonation is marginalized. The concrete curing enclosure is kiln, chamber, autoclave, vessel, tent, where the enclosure is designed or modified to be air-tight and capable of withstanding marginal deviations in pressure both below and above ambient conditions.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The door assembly is constructed from materials that include polymer, plastic, aluminum, and/or steel. The composite inner lining consists of a primary layer of spray polyurethane foam of 25-50 mm, and a secondary layer of polyurea of 2-10 mm thickness, where the composite lining is intended to alleviate internal stresses arising from the system's applied pressure differential.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU SHAO H
   LI D
   LIU Z
   HU Z
   ZHANG J
   ZOU Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
GA 201836631Q
AB    NOVELTY - The device has absorption towers (3,5) which are sequentially connected together. The first absorption tower absorbs the carbon components in the catalytic dry gas. The second absorption tower absorbs the carbon dioxide in the unabsorbed carbon components from the first absorption tower. A desorption column (6) desorbs the carbonaceous components from the second absorption tower. A bottom portion of desorption tower is connected to the absorption towers and is set as absorber back into the absorption towers.
   USE - Device for recycling carbon dioxide from catalytic dry gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The device structure is simple easy to operate. The carbon dioxide can be recovered from the catalytic dry gas with high recovery efficiency. The carbon content of the carbon product is relatively low, and can be used as the raw material for the ethylbenzene device. The propylene refrigeration systems and drying systems are not required, thus reducing plant investment and energy consumption. The carbon are timely separated and sent out of the boundary area. The serious damage to the performance of absorbents and devices, after continuous accumulation, are avoided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for recycling carbon dioxide from catalytic dry gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device for recycling carbon dioxide from catalytic dry gas.
   Absorption towers (3,5)
   Compressor (4)
   Desorption column (6)
   Air cooler (7)
   Methane discharging port (10)

PT P
PN CN113750961-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprises mixing and stirring waste incineration bottom ash and acid solution, drying and calcining, mixing colloidal carbon balls, third solid powder and water, heating and stirring.
AU LIU L
   SHEN B
   ZHANG S
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 202204566N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprises (i) mixing and stirring the waste incineration bottom ash and acid solution to separate solid and liquid to obtain liquid and solid residue, (ii) drying and calcining the liquid obtained in step (i) to obtain the first solid powder, (iii) drying and calcining the solid residue obtained in step (i) to obtain the second solid powder, (iv) introducing first solid powder and the second solid powder to water, heating and stirring to obtain a mixed solution, (v) drying and calcining the mixed solution obtained in step (iv) to obtain the third solid powder; (iii) mixing the colloidal carbon balls, third solid powder and water, heating and stirring after mixing, drying and calcining to obtain the carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method of carbon dioxide adsorbent effectively improve the adsorption performance of the adsorbent, and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide adsorbent prepared by the preparation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid solution described in step (i) is an acetic acid solution, and the concentration of the acetic acid solution is 25-35 wt.%, and the mass ratio of the waste incineration bottom ash and the acid solution is 1:8-12, the stirring temperature is 55- 65 degrees Celsius, and stirring time is 1-3 hours.Preferred Method: The drying temperature in step (ii) is 101-110degreesC, and the drying time is 15-20 hours; the calcination temperature is 850-950degreesC, and the calcination time is 3-5 hours; the drying temperature in step (iii) is 101- 110degreesC, the drying time is 6-8 hours, the calcination temperature is 1400-1600degreesC, and the calcination time is 1-3hours; the drying temperature described in step v) is 101-110degreesC, and the drying time is 18-22 hours; calcination temperature is 550-650degrees Celsius, and the calcination time is 1-3hours. In the step (iv), the mass ratio of the first solid powder and the second solid powder is 1-3: 7-9, the mass ratio of the total weight of the first solid powder and the second solid powder to the water is 1: 4-6; the heating and stirring temperature is 60-70 ; the heating and stirring time is 8-12 hours. In the step 2) After the calcination is completed, it also includes the step of grinding the calcined solid. In the step (iii) After the calcination is completed comprises grinding the calcined solid. In the step (v) After the calcination is completed comprises grinding the calcined solid, grinding steps. The addition amount of the colloidal carbon balls in step (vi) is 10-20 wt.% of the weight of the third solid powder, and the addition amount of the water is 4.5-5.5 times the total weight of the colloidal carbon balls and the third solid powder; heating and stirring temperature 60-70degrees Celsius, heating and stirring time is 8-12 hour, drying temperature is 101-110degrees Celsius, drying time is 10-20 hours, calcinating at 650-750degrees Celsius, and calcining time is 2-4 hours. The preparation method of the colloidal carbon spheres in step (vi) comprises performing a hydrothermal reaction on an aqueous glucose solution, and filtering, washing and drying after the reaction to obtain the colloidal carbon spheres. In the preparation method of the colloidal carbon ball, the hydrothermal reaction temperature is 170-190degrees Celsius, the hydrothermal reaction time is 8-12 hours, the drying temperature is 101-110degrees Celsius; the drying time is 10-20 hours, the used washing solvent is absolute ethyl alcohol and deionized water; the concentration of the glucose water solution is 0.1-1 mol/l. The waste incineration bottom ash comprises 35-50 wt.% calcium oxide, 5-15 wt.% silicon dioxide, 3-10 wt.% aluminum dioxide.

AU CHEN D
   GAN L
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST (UYSW-C)
GA 2021C25625
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing synthesis gas by reforming natural                gas non-catalytic partial oxidation combined with                carbon dioxide involves: (1) preheating natural                gas, oxygen, and carbon dioxide, and mixing; and                (2) feeding the mixed gas and pure oxygen into a                reactor, igniting at the inlet, flowing out the                product from the outlet of the reactor, cooling the                product by water quenching, and separating the                synthesis gas.    USE - The method is useful for preparing synthesis                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reach the annual production                capacity of synthesis gas of 3.6 tons per reactor,                creates a new process for the production of                synthesis gas and realizes the conversion and                utilization of carbon dioxide by limiting the                composition ratio of reaction raw materials and                combining computational fluid dynamics and detailed                chemical reaction mechanism.

PT P
PN CN110681857-A; CN110681857-B
TI Purifying silver nanowires involves putting silver nanowire mixture into pressure-resistant separation kettle, and controlling temperature of pressure-resistant separation kettle in certain range.
AU ZHAO Y
   XIAO D
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 202007465L
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying silver nanowires involves putting a silver nanowire mixture into a pressure-resistant separation kettle, controlling the temperature of the pressure-resistant separation kettle in a certain range, and pumping carbon dioxide into a pressure-resistant separation kettle through a carbon dioxide high-pressure pump to a predetermined pressure. The temperature and pressure of the pressure-resistant separation kettle is set at a preset value, the valve of the separator is opened, the carbon dioxide is released from the pressure-resistant separation kettle, and the pumped carbon dioxide flow is maintained to a certain value. The carbon dioxide is vented or recycled from the pressure-resistant separation tank after passing through the impurity collector. The silver nanowires are blocked by a separation membrane with a certain pore size and remain in the separator.
   USE - Method for purifying silver nanowires.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to purify silver nanowires in a simple and eco-friendly manner on industrial scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The pumping carbon dioxide is stopped to reduce the pressure of the pressure-resistant separation kettle to normal pressure after the separation time reaches the predetermined value. The kettle lid is opened to obtain purified silver nanowires.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersant is ethanol, deionized water, or methanol.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).)
MC L03-E01A; M22-H02; P53-C; P53-V04E
IP B22F-001/00; B82Y-040/00

PT P
PN US2019381445-A1; US10596513-B2
TI Capturing and storing carbon dioxide involves contacting carbon dioxide-containing stream with an adsorbent comprising zeolite and metal organic framework to adsorb carbon dioxide from stream and form carbon dioxide loaded adsorbent.
AU MANSOUR R B
   QASEM N
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 2019A5877W
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing and storing carbon dioxide (CO2) involves contacting CO2-containing stream with CO2 adsorbent to adsorb at least a portion of CO2 from the CO2-containing stream and form a CO2 loaded adsorbent, where the CO2 adsorbent comprises an adsorbent matrix comprising a zeolite and/or a metal organic framework, and carbon nanotubes that are dispersed within the adsorbent matrix, where the zeolite is zeolite 13X, the metal organic framework is selected from the group consisting of Mg-MOF-74 and MIL-100(Fe), and where a weight percent of the carbon nanotubes in the CO2 adsorbent is in a range of 0.01-5.0 wt.%, relative to a total weight of the CO2 adsorbent, then heating the CO2 loaded adsorbent to release the CO2 and form a gaseous stream that is substantially CO2 and injecting the gaseous stream into a geologic formation.
   USE - Method for capturing and storing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to minimize CO2 emission into the atmosphere and capture and store it to be used as pollution-free source of energy, e.g. renewable energy sources.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The CO2 adsorbent comprises 0.05-1.5 wt.% carbon nanotubes. Preferred Components: The adsorbent matrix comprises the zeolite and the metal organic framework. The carbon nanotubes are single-walled/multi-walled carbon nanotubes. The CO2-containing stream contains CO2 and at least one gaseous substance selected from the group consisting of nitrogen, oxygen, argon, helium, water vapor, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane, and ethane. Preferred Conditions: The volumetric ratio of CO2 to gaseous substance is in a range of 1:50 to 5:1. The carbon nanotubes are dispersed in the adsorbent matrix without forming chemical bonds with the adsorbent matrix.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN DE102018003332-A1; WO2019206450-A1
TI Preparation of synthesis product e.g. ethene, involves performing co-electrolysis of electrolysis insert comprising carbon dioxide and water, and feeding e.g. carbon monoxide contained in formed crude gas to hydroformylation reactor.
AU PESCHEL A
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 201988163H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of synthesis product involves performing co-electrolysis of an electrolysis insert comprising carbon dioxide and water to obtain a crude gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, feeding portion(s) of carbon monoxide contained in the crude gas and portion(s) of carbon dioxide contained in the crude gas to hydroformylation reactor(s) (30), and performing hydroformylation to obtain hydroformylation products.
   USE - Preparation of synthesis product. Uses include but are not limited to ethene, propene, butene, pentene, hexene, heptene, dodecane, other mono- and poly-unsaturated olefins, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical preparation of synthesis product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for plant (100,200), which comprises the hydroformylation reactor(s) adapted to supply portion(s) of carbon monoxide contained in the crude gas and carbon dioxide contained in the crude gas, to perform hydroformylation, and to obtain hydroformylation products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show a schematic view of the plant.
   Co-electrolysis unit (10)
   Carbon dioxide separation unit (20)
   Hydroformylation reactor (30)
   Separator (40)
   Plant (100,200)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The co-electrolysis is water electrolysis, and the crude gas is formed as hydrogen-containing crude gas. The co-electrolysis is pure carbon dioxide electrolysis, and the crude gas is formed as largely hydrogen-free raw gas. The portion(s) of the crude gas without carbon dioxide separation is feed to hydroformylation reactor to obtain hydroformylation products and unreacted components. In the hydroformylation process, hydrogen is added to the crude gas or its portion to obtain the hydroformylation products. The preparation of synthesis product involves reacting the unreacted components and the hydroformylation products in the hydroformylation reactor(s) in a common component mixture of the hydroformylation reactor(s), or in separate component mixtures from the hydroformylation reactor(s), and the unreacted components in gas-liquid separation are partially removed and separated from the hydroformylation products. The hydroformylation reactors components are partially recycled to the hydroformylation. The unreacted components are obtained in the form of a fraction which is subjected to product separation before the unreacted components in the hydroformylation reactor(s) are partially recycled. The co-electrolysis is performed at a pressure level (p1), and the hydroformylation is performed at a pressure level (p2), where the pressure level (p1) and the pressure level (p2) are the same or different. The hydroformylation is performed, such that the hydroformylation product has a molecular weight of 58 g/mol or more, preferably 86 g/mol. Preferred Composition: The ratio of hydrogen with respect to carbon dioxide in the crude gas is 0.8-1.2. The insert comprises condensed water.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

TI Carbon dioxide expansion loosening device, has cone head distributed with multiple groups injection hole, where each group injection hole is formed with injection passage, and gas passage distributed with periphery of injection hole.
AU LU Y
   NIE J
   LIAO Y
   ZHOU X

   YUAN H
   NIE X
   CHENG H
   ZHU Q
   TU H
   WANG Y
AE HUNAN SOIL & FERTILIZER INST (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 201864390N
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a pipe body whose end is formed with a filling liquid carbon dioxide cavity. Another end of the pipe body is provided with an air outlet. An outlet end of the pipe body is sealed with a cone head that is formed with a gas passage. The gas passage is connected with the air outlet of the pipe body through a pressure valve on an air channel. The cone head is axially distributed with multiple groups injection hole, where each group injection hole is formed with an injection passage. The gas passage is uniformly distributed with a conical periphery of the injection hole. The pipe body is provided with a charging opening filling liquid carbon dioxide cavity.
   USE - Carbon dioxide expansion loosening device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has light weight, small volume and operation convenience, and avoids large mechanical compacted soil for selecting depth of a loose soil layer and realizes uniformly with the desired soil.

PN CN108118361-A; CN108118361-B
TI Improving carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction reaction electrode catalytic activity involves using surface modifier to carry out surface modification to electrode material, and adding ester compound containing carbonyl group.
AU QIU Y
   ZHANG H
   ZHONG H
   LI X
   ZHANG T
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201845480B
AB    NOVELTY - Improving carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction reaction electrode catalytic activity involves using surface modifier to carry out surface modification to electrode material, adding ester compound containing carbonyl group as surface modifying agent, dissolving it in solvent preparation concentration of 0.5 wt.% of surface modifying agent solution of 50 millimole, where electrode material per square centimeter of surface area using surface modifying agent solution 0.1-1 milliliter corresponding to relationship between surface modifying agent solution into electrode material surface, standing for 5-60 second at room temperature under inert atmosphere drying or drying.
   USE - Method for improving carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction reaction electrode catalytic activity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction reaction electrode catalytic activity which has adsorption and ability to capture carbon dioxide and changes electrode surface for absorbsintensity of reaction intermediate product of electrode, improves generation rate of earnings response coefficient reaction target product, releases restriction of carbon dioxide mass transfer polarization, reduces generating rate of competitive evolution side reaction so as to improve selectivity of earnings response coefficient reaction target product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: carbonyl group is polycarbonate, dimethyl carbonate or diethyl carbonate in the one kind of or more than two. Preferred Condition: The concentration of the solution is 5-20 millimole. The electrode surface is drop coating, spray coating or immersion. The time of standing at room temperature is 20-40 second. The electrode material is non-porous metal of copper, silver, gold, or pallidium. The gas flow rate is more than 80 standard cubic centimeter per minute. Preferred Process: The pretreatment includes chemical treatment or electrochemical treatment and chemical treatment involves taking concentration of 36 wt.% and Soaking in hydrochloric acidsolution, immersing time is 5-30 minutes, washing to neutral and drying with inert gas in electrochemical treatment by using phosphoric acid with 85-87 wt.% of mass concentration as treatment liquid, where treatment potential is 1-4 volt. The optimal treatment potential is 2-3 volt. The treatment time is 5-30 minutes. The solution is treated at room temperature, after treatment, it is washed to neutrality and dried with inert gas.

Injectors are pointed with inclination of 60 degree. A Dewar bottle is filled with liquid nitrogen.

PT P
PN CN114190230-A
TI Indoor controllable cultivation of ornamental Ganoderma lucidum by inoculating mushroom in mushroom spawn, cultivating until full of mycelia, stacking on shelf, maintaining temperature, relative air humidity and carbon dioxide concentration, pruning growth point, drying and painting mushroom.
AU CAI J
   WANG W
   LAN Q
AE SHIYAN SHIITAKE MUSHROOM TECHNOLOGY CO (SHIY-Non-standard)
GA 202245033F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for indoor controllable cultivation of ornamental Ganoderma lucidum involves selecting G.lucidum variety, packing mushroom spawn in full sawdust bags or small log bags, sterilizing, inoculating the G.lucidum, transporting the inoculated mushroom spawn to the culture room for cultivation until full of mycelia, transporting the mushroom spawn of mycelium to the outhouse, stacking the mushroom spawn on a shelf, maintaining temperature, relative air humidity and carbon dioxide concentration, dividing the G.lucidum of different shapes, placing on each shelf, controlling the temperature for terminating the growth of G.lucidum, pruning each growth point to intervene in the shape of G.lucidum, then maintaining temperature, relative air humidity and carbon dioxide concentration, if modeling is not continued, adjusting the temperature to terminate the growth of the mushroom, boiling and drying the G.lucidum and then painting the G.lucidum.
   USE - The method is useful for indoor controllable cultivation of ornamental G.lucidum
   ADVANTAGE - The cultured lucid ganoderma product is with spirit and spirit, posture is thousand, and has ornamental value and collection value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for indoor controllable cultivation of ornamental Ganoderma lucidum involves (a) selecting G.lucidum variety, packing mushroom spawn in full sawdust bags or small log bags, sterilizing and inoculating the G.lucidum, (b) transporting the inoculated mushroom spawn to the culture room for cultivation until full of mycelia, (c) transporting the mushroom spawn of mycelium to the outhouse, and stacking the mushroom spawn on a shelf, maintaining the temperature at 25-30degrees Celsius, relative air humidity at 80-87% and carbon dioxide concentration at 950-1100 mg/kg using fan, using a fluorescent tube as the light source to make the illuminance reach 450-510 LX and continuing cultivating for 7-15 days after the young mushroom grows on the mushroom spawn, (d) dividing the G.lucidum of different shapes, placing on each shelf, controlling the temperature at 18plus minus1degrees Celsius, terminating the growth of G.lucidum for 3-9 days until the growth point shrinks, pruning each growth point to intervene in the shape of G.lucidum and then maintaining the temperature at 28-32degrees Celsius, relative air humidity at 85-92%, and carbon dioxide concentration at 550-620 mg/kg, (e) when 70% of the caps grow to a diameter of 5 cm, again pruning, maintaining the temperature at 24-28degrees Celsius for 18-24 days, then maintaining the temperature at 18-20degrees Celsius, relative air humidity at 70-80% and light intensity at 1200-1700 LX, where the growth rate of G.lucidum slows down until it matures and optionally harvesting, (f) if modeling is not continued, adjusting the temperature to 10-15degrees Celsius to terminate the growth of the mushroom and (g) boiling and drying the G.lucidum and then painting the G.lucidum.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The step (d) involves pruning using a strong acid pen to point, draw, and trace the young mushroom. The step (e) involves pruning using scissors, strong acid solution or mold. The step (e) involves when not harvesting, continuing to cultivating after cutting and engraving on the edge of G.lucidum by maintaining the temperature at 26-30degrees Celsius, the relative air humidity at 86-92%, carbon dioxide concentration at 600-800 mg/kg, and light intensity at 1000-1500 Lx to differentiate new G.lucidum and repeating the above-mentioned process to set the expected shape. The step (e) involves if you need to increase the color of the G.lucidum surface, maintaining the light intensity to 2000-2500 LX for 6-8 hours/day and then maintaining the light intensity at 500-800 LX for 6-8 hours/day.

PN PL426469-A1; PL237180-B1
TI Capturing carbon dioxide from flue gas and post-process gas on solid sorbent by directing flue gas to carbon dioxide capture system and discharging adsorption gas, a portion of which is used to fill adsorbers after pressure equalization.
AU WAWRZYNCZAK D
   MAJCHRZAK-KUCEBA I
   NOWAK W
   BUDNER Z
   ZDEB J
   SMOLKA W
   ZAJCHOWSKI A
AE TAURON WYTWARZANIE SA (TAUR-Non-standard)
GA 202031554W
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide (CO2) from flue gas and post-process gas is captured on a solid sorbent by directing flue gas to CO2 capture system consisting of greater than or equal to 4 adsorbers, divided into upper and lower sections, operating according to a repeatable schedule, according to which individual technological operations are carried out in each section of the adsorber, with a time offset relative to remaining adsorbers, ensuring continuity of feed gas and recirculation gas flow, discharging resulting adsorption gas, free of CO2, from the installation, using a portion of resulting adsorption gas to fill the adsorbers after pressure equalization, and using gas from co-current expansion process in pressure equalization operations between adsorbers or flushing bed to produce recirculation gas, directed to the bottom section of the adsorber, to further enrich it in CO2 or to produce a low-pressure product (CO2), high purity gas of greater than or equal to 90%.
   USE - A two-stage method for effective capture of carbon dioxide from flue gas, as well as from post-process gas, on a solid sorbent by vacuum pressure adsorption with re-enrichment of low-pressure product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide (CO2) from flue gas and post-process gas is captured on a solid sorbent by directing flue gas to CO2 capture system consisting of greater than or equal to 4 adsorbers, divided into upper and lower sections, operating according to a repeatable schedule, according to which individual technological operations are carried out in each section of the adsorber, with a time offset relative to remaining adsorbers, ensuring continuity of feed gas and recirculation gas flow, discharging resulting adsorption gas, free of CO2, from the installation, using a portion of resulting adsorption gas to fill the adsorbers after pressure equalization, and using gas from co-current expansion process in pressure equalization operations between adsorbers or flushing bed to produce recirculation gas, directed to the bottom section of the adsorber, to further enrich it in CO2 or to produce a low-pressure product (CO2), high purity gas of greater than or equal to 90%. Gas flow is possible due to the appropriate opening of individual valves in the installation, two blowers, one of which increases the pressure of the feed gas, and the other pressure of the recirculation gas, to the set value in the adsorption stage, as well as two vacuum pumps removing the recirculation gas and low-pressure product (CO2) from the appropriate sections of the adsorber at a given vacuum. In appropriate technological operations, from the lower section of the lower adsorber, during vacuum desorption, in addition to recirculation gas, a low-pressure product (CO2) is also obtained. The resulting gaseous products are periodically stored in post-adsorption gas and recirculation gas buffer tanks, while the low-pressure product (CO2) can be stored or sent for further use.

PN CN109486556-A
TI Processing supercritical extraction method for high bioactive wheat germ oil comprises e.g. placing wheat germ in oven, inactivating lipase activity, and reducing water content, storing in a refrigerator, pulverizing, and extracting.
AU LIU R
   SHEN Y
   MI T
   ZHAO Q
   DING J
   XIE R
AE YUANMI AGRIC TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201927139C
AB    NOVELTY - Processing supercritical extraction method for high bioactive wheat germ oil comprises (i) placing wheat germ in an oven at 100-110 degrees C for 40-80 minutes, inactivating the lipase activity, and reducing water content to 4-6%, and storing in a refrigerator at 4 degrees C, and (ii) pulverizing the obtained wheat germ to desired particle size, (iii) adding obtained wheat germ into an extraction device for extraction.
   USE - The method is useful for processing supercritical extraction method for high bioactive wheat germ oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high extraction rate, no solvent residue in oil, and better protection of functional components in special oils.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for:
   (1) using the wheat germ oil supercritical extraction device, comprising loading the wheat germ into the extraction kettle, adding charged germ into a extraction kettle, setting the extraction and separation temperature required for the experiment, when the temperature of the extraction kettle and the separation kettle reaches the set temperature, opening the cylinder and the related valve, so that the carbon dioxide is pressed out of the cylinder and passed through the refrigeration coil and enters the high pressure pump, purifying the gas passes through the heat exchanger, dehydrating, and removing into extraction vessel, adjusting the pressure of the extraction kettle, when the extraction pressure and temperature reach a predetermined value, closing the high pressure pump and maintaining the pressure of the extraction kettle, maintaining the pressure to maintain a stable static extraction for a period of time, opening the separation kettle valve, adjusting the extraction, separating pressure and carbon dioxide flow, when the extraction pressure, temperature and carbon dioxide flow rate reach a predetermined value, starting timing, when the dynamic extraction time reaches a predetermined time, opening the separation kettle I to collect the wheat germ oil, and collecting part of the remaining wheat germ oil from the separation kettle II and Mixed impurities where the device comprises extraction device used in the supercritical extraction method of the high bioactive wheat germ oil further comprises: a carbon dioxide storage tank, a refrigeration coil, a plunger pump, a preheater, a purifier, an extraction kettle, a separation kettle I, and a separation Kettle II, and aflow meter; and
   (2) optimized method for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of wheat germ oil comprising according to the results of single factor experiment, three factors that have great influence on the extraction rate of wheat germ oil were selected, extraction pressure, extraction temperature and extraction time, according to the Box-Behnken Center combination test design principle, the wheat germ oil extraction rate and the vitamin E content in the extract oil were used as the response values, and designing the three-factor and three-level response surface analysis experiments, relationship between factors and response values, and process optimization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) further comprises heating the wheat germ in an oven at 102-108 degrees C for 50-70 minutes, inactivating the lipase activity, and reducing the water content to 4.5-5.5%, and placing it at 4 degrees C refrigerator. In the step (b), the wheat germ obtained in the step A is pulverized and sieved to a desired particle size, and the required particle size is 60-80 meshes. The predetermined value of the extraction pressure is 25-35 MPa. The extraction temperature is predetermined to be 40-60 degrees C. The predetermined value of the separation pressure is 4-6 MPa.

AU WU F
   LI Z
   QU D
   DING D
   JIAO A
   SHENG K
   YUE D
   XU R
   NIU Y
AE UNIV LIAONING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (ULST-C)
   UNIV LIAONING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (ULST-C)
GA 201905519T
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a conveying unit, a feeding system, a discharging system, right above carbon dioxide recycling system and automatic control system. The conveying unit by the conveyor with stirring function and a tubular furnace on the conveyor outer wall with stirring function. The feeding system is set on the conveyor with stirring function. The discharging system is set at the bottom of the left side of the conveyor with stirring function. The discharging system comprises a refractory fiber insulation heat decomposition chamber, with the refractory fiber insulation of heat decomposition chamber connecting the cooling system and an automatic discharging machine. A carbon dioxide recovery system is set at the left side above the conveyor with stirring function.
   USE - The device is useful for producing light burned magnesia by using electric energy continuous stepping decomposition (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing light burned magnesia by continuously stepping decomposition of electric energy using the device, comprising (a) uniformly feeding powdered magnesite or concentrate powder from the feeding port on the conveyor cylinder into the interior of the conveyor barrel with agitating function through the automatic feeding machine installed under the feed bin; (b) starting automatic control system of tubular electric furnace, heating the conveyor barrel with a stirring function, heating the conveyor barrel and conveying and stirring blades to 400-800 degrees C, simultaneously starting automatic control system of a spindle drive system, aligning the conveying and stirring blades in a defined direction and rotational speed, pushing the powdered magnesite or concentrate powder to the end of the cylinder, and conveying and agitating blades for heat exchange and decomposition while conveying magnesite or concentrate powder into the conveyor barrel; (c) decomposing more than 90% of the magnesite powder or concentrate powder at the discharge end of the conveyor cylinder at the third conveyor unit group, forming light burned magnesium powder at 700-750 degrees C,Lightly burning magnesia powder into refractory fiber insulation the waste heat decomposition chamber is slowly cooling, continuing to complete the unfinished decomposition; (d) when the temperature of the lightly burned magnesium powder is lower than 400 degrees C, it conveying to the cooling chamber, and the cold air blown by the blower of the heat exchanger absorbs the heat of the powder, and rapidly cools the lightly burned magnesia powder to below 50 degrees C and packing by the automatic discharge machine installed under the cooling bin; and (e)generating pure carbon dioxide gas by the decomposition of magnesite powder or concentrate powder passes through the exhaust port provided at the upper part of the conveyor, through the exhaust pipe, entering after the bag filter is dedusted, recycling it by a carbon dioxide collector to produce high pressure gas or dry ice.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device.

PN WO2018136767-A1
TI Producing unsaturated 2C hydrocarbons and oxidation compounds, comprises e.g. subjecting hydrocarbon feedstock to oxidative conversion process, separating carbon dioxide from resultant, and isolating e.g. carbon monoxide from resultant.
AU MAMEDOV A K
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   MAMEDOV A K (MAME-Individual)
GA 2018582345
AB    NOVELTY - Producing unsaturated 2C hydrocarbons and oxidation compounds, comprises: subjecting a hydrocarbon feedstock to an oxidative conversion process to form a product mixture comprising at least ethylene, ethane, a carbon dioxide product, a carbon monoxide product, and an unconverted hydrocarbon product; separating at least a portion of the carbon dioxide product from the product mixture; isolating at least a portion of the carbon monoxide product and a portion of the unconverted hydrocarbon from the product mixture by separating at least a portion of the ethylene and the ethane from the product mixture; and combining the isolated portion of the carbon monoxide product and isolated portion of the unconverted hydrocarbon product with a hydrocarbon feedstock to undergo the oxidative conversion, where an oxidative conversion of the isolated portion of carbon monoxide generates heat to facilitate the oxidative conversion of the hydrocarbon feedstock.
   USE - The methods are useful for producing unsaturated 2C hydrocarbons and oxidation compounds (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methods: allow a generated carbon monoxide and unreacted or unconverted methane from the oxidative coupling of methane (OCM) process to be redirected to the OCM reactant mixture, thus eliminating additional process schemes for carbon monoxide conversion; and improve the recycling of the carbon monoxide and unconverted methane product throughout the reaction processes back to the oxidative coupling of methane process step.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing unsaturated 2C hydrocarbons and oxidation compounds, comprising reacting a hydrocarbon feedstock with molecular oxygen in the presence of a catalyst to form a product mixture comprising at least ethylene, ethane, a carbon dioxide product, a carbon monoxide product, and an unconverted hydrocarbon product, isolating at least a portion of the ethylene and the ethane by separating at least a portion of the carbon monoxide product and a portion of the unconverted hydrocarbon from the product mixture, and recycling the isolated portion of the carbon monoxide product and the isolated portion of the unconverted hydrocarbon product to the hydrocarbon feedstock to react with molecular oxygen in the presence of the catalyst, where the reaction of the isolated portion of the carbon monoxide generates heat energy to facilitate the reacting of the hydrocarbon feedstock with the molecular oxygen; and
   (2) a system comprising a reactor configured to effect an oxidative conversion of a methane feed stream to form a product mixture comprising at least ethylene, ethane, a carbon dioxide product, a carbon monoxide product, and an unconverted methane product, and a separator unit configured to effect an isolation of at least a portion of the carbon monoxide product and at least a portion of the unconverted methane product from the product mixture, where the isolated portion of the carbon monoxide product and the isolated portion of the unconverted methane product are directed to the reactor and subjected to oxidative conversion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises any one of oxides, peroxides, carbonates, phosphates and silicates of elements comprising lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, cesium or beryllium, peroxides, hydroxides, carbonates, phosphates and silicates of elements of strontium or barium, or carbonates, phosphates and silicates of elements comprising lead, tin, germanium, magnesium or calcium. The catalyst comprises a chlorine compound. The catalyst comprises an oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reactor comprises a catalyst or a catalyst system configured to facilitate the reacting of the methane feedstream with molecular oxygen to produce unsaturated 2C hydrocarbons and oxidation compounds. The catalyst is disposed in a catalyst bed. The directing the isolated portion of the carbon monoxide product and the isolated portion of the unconverted methane product to the reactor facilitates the oxidative conversion of the methane feedstream.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The separating the carbon dioxide product from the product mixture comprises an amine adsorption process. At least a portion of ethane of the product mixture undergoes thermal dehydrogenation to form at least a portion of ethylene of the product mixture. The separating the portion of the ethylene and the ethane from the product mixture comprises a cryogenic separation process. The oxidative conversion process comprises combustion of the hydrocarbon feedstock in the presence of molecular oxygen. The oxidative conversion process occurs in the presence of a catalyst. The oxidative conversion proceeds at a temperature of 500-900 degrees C. The oxidative conversion comprises an oxidative coupling process. The carbon monoxide product is formed in a quantity of less than 10%, preferably less than 5%, based on the quantity of the product mixture. A weight ratio carbon monoxide product to a combined ethane and ethylene in the product mixture is 0.1-0.4. Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon feedstock comprises methane.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE UNIV GUANGDONG OCEAN (UYOG-C)
GA 2022B9574L

   USE - The method is useful for reducing tropomyosin                sensitization.

PT P
PN IN202221019763-A
TI Synthesizing mesoporous silica supported ionic liquid based polymeric membrane for carbon dioxide/methane gas separation, by e.g. synthesizing mesoporous MCM-48, MCM-41, SBA-15, and synthesizing mesoporous silica supported with ionic liquid.
AU BANDYOPADHYAY M
   DHARASKAR S
   SINGH D K
   PANDEY D K
   PATIL T V
   JADAV D T
AE BANDYOPADHYAY M (BAND-Individual)
GA 2022576446
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing of mesoporous silica supported ionic liquid based polymeric membrane for carbon dioxide/methane gas separation which is a highly efficient membrane for the separation of carbon dioxide/methane gas from a mixture of gases, comprises: (i) synthesizing mesoporous MCM-48; (ii) synthesizing mesoporous MCM-41; (iii) synthesizing mesoporous SBA-15; (iv) synthesizing mesoporous silica supported with ionic liquids; and (v) synthesizing mesoporous ionic liquid based polymeric membrane.
   USE - The process is useful for synthesizing of mesoporous silica supported ionic liquid based polymeric membrane for carbon dioxide/methane gas separation.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is simple, feasible and efficient; provides membrane having improved permeability and selectivity for carbon dioxide/methane gas separation; and is energy-efficient and cost-effective as it operates at room temperature with much lower operating pressure and application range.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The synthesis of mesoporous MCM-48 comprises: adding cetyltrimethylammonium chloride to 1M sodium hydroxide in a Teflon (RTM: Polytetrafluoroethylene) bottle; adding water and stirring the mixture in a water bath at 50 degrees C until it becomes homogenous; adding tetraethoxysilane in the mixture whose final molar gel composition is 1 tetraethoxysilane:0.7 cetyltrimethylammonium chloride:0.5 sodium hydroxide:64 water; pouring the gel in a Teflon (RTM: Polytetrafluoroethylene)-lined autoclave and keeping at 90 degrees C for four days; filtering off the product, washing thoroughly with water and drying at room temperature; and removing the surfactant in the synthesized material by calcination at 540 degrees C for 5 hours. The synthesis of mesoporous MCM-41 comprises: dissolving cetyltrimethylammonium bromide in a solution of sodium hydroxide (0.1 M) at 40 degrees C; adding tetraethoxysilane to the mixture and stirring the mixture for 2 hours and pouring in a Teflon (RTM: Polytetrafluoroethylene) bottle; keeping the gel at 100 degrees C for 24 hours whose final molar gel composition is 1 tetraethoxysilane:0.12 cetyltrimethylammonium bromide:0.23 sodium hydroxide:130 water; filtering off the product, washing thoroughly with water and drying at room temperature; and removing the surfactant template by calcination at 540 degrees C for 5 hours. The synthesis of mesoporous SBA-15 comprises: dissolving 4 g Pluronic 123 (RTM: Symmetric triblock copolymer comprising poly(ethylene oxide) and poly(propylene oxide)) in 125 g 2 M hydrochloric acid solution with stirring at room temperature; stirring the solution at 40 degrees C for 0-3 hours followed by slowly adding tetraethoxysilane into the solution where the molar composition of the mixture is 1 tetraethoxysilane:6.1 hydrochloric acid:0.017 pluronic 123:165 water; stirring the resultant mixture at 40 degrees C for 20 hours and transferring into a polypropylene bottle and aging at 90 degrees C under static condition for 24 hours; recovering the solid product by filtration and drying at room temperature overnight; and performing template removal by calcination at 540 degrees C for 5 hours. In the stage 4, imidazolium based ionic liquid is supported on the three different calcined mesoporous silicas to synthesize the mesoporous silica supported with ionic liquid where the process comprises: mixing 50% ionic liquid (0.025 g imidazolium based ionic liquid per 0.05 mg mesoporous silica support) with 25 ml ethanol in a closed round bottom flask and stirring at room temperature for 2 hours; activating specific quantity of mesoporous silica at 90 degrees C for 2 hours; mixing the activated mesoporous silica with the ionic liquid mixture and allowing to stir under vacuum at 80 degrees C for 2 hours; and vacuum drying ethanol from the solution and drying at 120 degrees C for 24 hours. In stage 5, solution casting technique is used to synthesize the composite membrane containing all the prepared ionic liquid impregnated mesoporous materials where the process for the synthesis involves: preparing 15 wt.% Pebax-1657 (RTM: Poly ether-block-amide) polymeric solution using a mixed solvent of ethanol/water (70/30); stirring for 30 minutes at 70 degrees C until a homogenous solution is prepared; adding 10 wt.% ionic liquid impregnated mesoporous materials to the homogenous solution and keeping this mixture for stirring at 70 degrees C for 4 hours under reflux condition; utilizing the homogenous solution to prepare the membrane by uniformly casting over a glass plate using a digital casting knife; keeping the glass plates in ambient conditions for 30 minutes for normal cooling; and placing the glass plates in a vacuum oven and keeping at 75 degrees C overnight for obtaining the polymeric membrane.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

GA 202168872R
AB    NOVELTY - Performing non-invasive breathing                end-breathing carbon dioxide detection method                involves turning on the non-invasive ventilator and                starting oxygen delivery, collecting the exhaled                gas, and obtaining the temperature and carbon                dioxide (CO2) concentration data of the exhaled                gas. The detected CO2 concentration is corrected                according to the temperature of the exhaled air to                obtain the corrected CO2 concentration of the                end-tidal gas. The gas source composition spectrum                analysis method is used to obtain the CO2 partial                pressure parameter. The CO2 partial pressure                periodic change function model is established, and                solved the model to obtain the analysis waveform of                the CO2 partial pressure change with time.
   USE - Non-invasive breath carbon dioxide detection                method.
