PN CN112943185-A
TI Processing composite fracturing based on            supercritical carbon dioxide pre-fracturing comprises            e.g. injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into            formation for pre-fracturing to form fracture network            and carrying out hydraulic sand-carrying.
AU LIU W
   XU X
   ZHANG J
   LIU C
   BAI J
   CHEN S
   LI Y
   XU Y
   TONG L
AE CHINA GEOLOGICAL SURVEY OIL GAS RESOURCE (CGSU-C)
GA 202173342H
AB    NOVELTY - Processing composite fracturing based on                supercritical carbon dioxide pre-fracturing                comprises (i) injecting supercritical carbon                dioxide into the formation for pre-fracturing to                form a fracture network; (ii) injecting fracture                network with different types of fracturing fluids                carrying proppants to carry out hydraulic                sand-carrying fracturing to expand, support, and                polish the fracture network, during the hydraulic                sand-carrying fracturing process, injecting liquid                nitrogen throughout the process; (iii) injecting                slippery water for displacement operation to push                the fracturing fluid into the formation.
   USE - The method is useful for processing composite                fracturing based on supercritical carbon dioxide                pre-fracturing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can realizes the effects of                complex fracture network volume modification of                formations, preferably shale reservoirs, support of                fracture networks, and formation energy                enhancement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The high viscosity gel                  fracturing fluid is: 0.-0.8 wt.% carboxymethyl                  hydroxypropyl guar gum, 1-2 wt.% potassium                  chloride anti-swelling agent, 0.3-0.5 wt.%                  fluorine carbon auxiliary discharging agent,                  0.5-0.7 wt.% crosslinking agent, 0.1-0.2 wt.%                  resistance reducing agent and 95-98% clean water,                  the anti-swelling rate of the high viscosity gel                  fracturing fluid is more than 85%, the gel                  breaking time is less than 1 hour at 90 degrees                  C, the resistance reducing rate is more than 70%,                  the low-viscosity sliding water fracturing fluid                  is: 0.1-0.2 wt.% carboxymethylhydroxypropyl guar                  gum, 1-2 wt.% potassium chloride anti-swelling                  agent, 0.3-0.5 wt.% fluorocarbon auxiliary agent,                  0.1-0.2 wt.% resistance reducing agent and 96-98                  wt.% clean water, the anti-swelling rate of the                  low-viscosity sliding water fracturing fluid is                  more than 85%, the resistance reducing rate is                  more than 70%, the middle viscosity linear rubber                  fracturing fluid is: 0.2-0.4 wt.% carboxymethyl                  hydroxypropyl guar gum, 1-2 wt.% potassium                  chloride anti-swelling agent, 0.3-0.5 wt.%                  fluorocarbon auxiliary agent, 0.3-0.5 wt.%                  cross-linking agent, 0.1-0.2 wt.% resistance                  reducing agent and 95-98 wt.% clean water, the                  anti-swelling rate of the middle viscosity linear                  rubber fracturing fluid is more than 85%, the gel                  breaking time is less than 1 hour at 90 degrees                  C, the resistance reducing rate is more than 70%,                  and/or the compressive strength of the ceramsite                  is more than 70 Mpa, the density of the ceramsite                  is less than 1.6 g/cm3. Preferred Method: In the                  step (ii), the delivery capacity of the whole                  liquid nitrogen is 150-200 l/minute. The                  supercritical carbon dioxide is obtained by                  increasing the temperature of the liquid carbon                  dioxide to the supercritical state, the critical                  temperature is 31.1 degrees C and 7.38 MPa                  pressure and/or, the injection displacement of                  the supercritical carbon dioxide is 3-4                  m3/minute. The hydraulic sand carrying fracturing                  comprises orderly injecting high-viscosity gel                  fracturing fluid carrying coarse grain diameter                  supporting agent to these wing net according to                  14-18 m3/min displacement for expanding the                  sewing net, injecting low-viscosity sliding water                  fracturing fluid carrying small grain diameter                  propping agent according to 12-14 m3/min under                  displacement for supporting all sewing nets,                  injecting the medium-viscosity linear rubber                  fracturing fluid with medium-diameter propping                  agent according to the delivery capacity of 14-16                  m3/min for grinding the sewing net, where the                  viscosity of the high viscosity gel fracturing                  fluid is more than 50 mPa mPa.s, the coarse grain                  diameter supporting agent is 30 to 50meshes of                  ceramsite, the sand adding proportion is 4-6                  wt.%, the viscosity of the low viscosity slip                  water fracturing fluid is 3-5 mPa.s, the small                  particle diameter supporting agent is 70-140                  meshes of ceramsite, the sand adding content is                  10-15 wt.%, the viscosity of the medium viscosity                  linear rubber fracturing fluid is 10-30 mPa.s,                  the middle particle size supporting agent is                  40-70 meshes of ceramsite and the sand adding                  content is 8-10 wt.%. The method comprises                  choosing cluster, determining the seat sealing                  position of the bridged plug and the position of                  each section of perforation, and then carrying                  out composite fracturing for each single section                  according to the steps (i) to (iii) respectively.                  The section length of each single section is                  controlled within 20-40 mu m and/or, the                  selection principle of the perforation position                  includes: choosing the position with brittle                  mineral content to less than 50%; the rupture                  pressure of the perforation point in the same                  section is lower than 50% of the average value,                  and the fracture pressure difference range of                  each perforation point is less than 20%, the                  total porosity of the reservoir is more than 4%,                  and the permeability is more than 0.01 md, the                  total hydrocarbon in gas measurement is more than                  0.8%, the TOC is more than 1%, and in the step                  (i) more than 0.3 mg/g, the location where the                  logging sound amplitude is less than 20% and/or,                  the number of clusters in a single segment is 3-5                  clusters, the cluster length is 1-2 m, and the                  cluster spacing is 10-20 clusters/m. Before the                  step (i), the method further comprises a step of                  performing perforation operation according to the                  position of the bridged plug seat and the                  perforation position, where the material is                  injected into the well cylinder formed by the                  perforation in step (i-iii). The bridged plug is                  soluble bridged plug ,the temperature resistance                  of the bridged plug and the soluble ball is more                  than 130 degrees C, the compressive strength is                  more than 90 MPa, the dissolving time is less                  than 20d in the electrolyte solution with the                  mass concentration of 1%, and/or the penetration                  depth of the perforating bullet is more than 1 m,                  the arrangement position of the perforating                  bullet is arranged by 360 degrees spiral, the                  arrangement distance of the perforating bullet is                  controlled at 8-12 holes/m, the total perforation                  number of the single section is not more than 40                  holes. The total amount of the single fracturing                  fluid is controlled at 1000-1800 m3 per section.                  and/or the single-stage supercritical carbon                  dioxide injection amount is controlled at 200-400                  m3.

PN CN112023870-A
TI Preparing high temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises e.g. dissolving zirconium salt and lithium salt in glycol solution of citric acid, stirring, adding potassium salt and rubidium salt, heating, reacting and adjusting pH value.
AU CHEN H
   TIAN X
   DING R
   ZHANG Q
   WANG X
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2020C42960
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises (i) dissolving zirconium salt and lithium salt in glycol solution of citric acid and stirring to obtain clear transparent solution I, (ii) adding potassium salt and rubidium salt into clear transparent solution I, then heating, reacting and adjusting pH value, (iii) allowing to stand reacted solution and then carrying out aging, distilling and drying to obtain lithium zirconate xerogel, and (iv) calcining xerogel to obtain rubidium(1+)/potassium ion doped lithium zirconate material.
   USE - The high temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent is useful for strengthening methane reforming reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The high temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent has uniform and small particle size, fast carbon dioxide adsorption rate and high adsorption capacity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i): the molar ratio between zirconium salt and lithium salt is 1:1-1:4, the molar ratio between zirconium salt and citric acid is 1:3-1:8, the ratio between zirconium salt (unit mol), the volume of ethylene glycol (unit l) and the volume of aqueous ammonia (unit l) is 1:0.1-0.5:0-0.2. The zirconium salt is zirconyl nitrate, zirconium nitrate or zirconium oxychloride octahydrate. The lithium salt is lithium nitrate, lithium carbonate or lithium acetate, the potassium salt is potassium carbonate, and the rubidium salt is rubidium carbonate. Preferred Method: In step (ii): the molar ratio between rubidium(1+) and potassium ion is 1:1-4, adjusts the pH value to 8-10 using aqueous ammonia and urea solution, the amount of the zirconium salt in step (i) is A, and the sum of the amounts of rubidium(1+) and potassium ion in step (ii) is B, and the ratio between A:B is 1:0.1-1:0.5. In step (ii), the heating and reaction is carried out at 50-90 degrees C for 2-6 hours. In step (iii), the distillation temperature for the aged solution is 100-150 degrees C, and the distillation time is 4-12 hours. The step (iv) comprises carrying out primary calcination at 200-400 degrees C for 2-6 hours and carrying out secondary calcination at 600-950 degrees C for 2-6 hours.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN111622722-A; CN111622722-B
TI Improving recovery rate of coalbed methane involves using supercritical carbon dioxide and nano-silica, mixing coal sample with nano-silica thoroughly, and combining supercritical carbon dioxide and nano-silica.
AU LI Z
   WU Y
   LIANG W
   DENG C
   WANG Q
   GUO H
   CHEN X
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2020874908
AB    NOVELTY - Improving recovery rate of coalbed methane involves using supercritical carbon dioxide and nano-silica. The coal sample is mixed with nano-silica thoroughly. The uniformly mixed coal sample is placed into the reactor. The supercritical carbon dioxide and nano-silica are combined organically. The coal sample is leached to analyze the changes in the pores of the coal sample and changes in the molecular structure of organic matter in the coal. The pressure and temperature are set in the reactor to reach the experimental conditions. The coal sample functions in the supercritical carbon dioxide environment.
   USE - Method for improving recovery rate of coalbed methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to improve recovery rate of coalbed methane that improves development of pores and cracks in the coal body, increases coal permeability, and CBM production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The method involves collecting geological data of the target coalbed methane field, selecting the coal samples of the target coal seams for collection, and drying and crushing the coal samples for experimental needs. The silicon dioxide is mixed with particle size of 15 plus minus 5 nanometer with experimental coal sample uniformly, and then coal sample is placed into reactor to process the coal sample. The gas environment is controlled in the reactor and supercritical carbon dioxide treatment is conducted. The supercritical carbon dioxide isothermal adsorption device is used to build supercritical carbon dioxide experimental environment in the reactor. The flow of carbon dioxide is adjusted to control the pressure in the reactor, and temperature of the isothermal adsorption device thermostat is set to control the temperature in the reactor. The coal sample is leached to study the effect of supercritical carbon dioxide and nano-silica on the pore and fissure structure of the coal sample and the dissolution and dissolution of the coal sample. The gas is connected in the reaction vessel to the separation vessel through pipeline. The temperature of the separation vessel is controlled through water bath heating, and pressure is controlled in the separation vessel through valve and barometer at inlet of the separation vessel. The organic matter dissolved in the experiment is separated. The organic matter in the separation kettle is extracted with CH2Cl2, and molecular structure of these organic matter is analyzed. The pore structure of the reacted coal sample is analyzed. The method involves collecting geological data of the target coalbed methane field, selecting coal sample of the target coal seam for collection, and drying, crushing the coal sample into different coal samples required for numbering, performing elemental and industrial analysis, mixing the coal sample with Nano-Silica, and placing into the reactor of the supercritical carbon dioxide isotherm adsorption device to conduct leaching experiments on the coal sample in supercritical carbon dioxide environment. The pressure and temperature is set in the reactor through the supercritical carbon dioxide isothermal adsorption device and air pumped to achieve supercritical experimental conditions, and soaked the coal sample under this condition to dissolve portion of the organic matter in the coal and improve the coal seam pore and fissure structure. The carbon dioxide gas stream carrying organic matter after soaking enters the separation kettle to extract the supercritical carbon dioxide and nano-silica treated coal of the organic matter dissolved in the sample is analyzed, and molecular structure of the organic matter is analyzed, and pore structure of the reacted coal sample is analyzed. The molecular structure analysis of the organic substance adopts a gas chromatography-mass spectrometer. The described pore structure analysis is low-pressure liquid nitrogen adsorption test and mercury intrusion test on coal samples. The low-pressure liquid nitrogen adsorption is mainly used to analyze the pores of 1.7-50nm in the coal sample to obtain pore volume and pore size distribution.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); Q49 (Mining (E21))

TI Laser welding a coated metal workpiece using carbon dioxide laser, involves welding at least two metal workpieces, assembling the metal workpieces and laser welding the assembled metal workpieces using carbon dioxide laser welding device.
AU YANG S
   WANG Y
   YAO B
AE SHANGHAI CAS SHENGUANG OPTOELECTRONIC (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020414828
AB    NOVELTY - Laser welding a coated metal workpiece using carbon dioxide laser, comprises (a) providing at least two metal workpieces, where at least one of which has low melting point coating on the surface, (b) welding and assembling the metal workpieces, and (c) performing laser welding on assembled metal workpieces using carbon dioxide laser welding device under protective gas including mixed gas of oxygen and inert gas, where the volume of oxygen in the mixed gas 2-15%.
   USE - The method is useful for laser welding coated metal workpiece using carbon dioxide laser.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes oxygen, which reacts with low melting point coating to form oxides to improve laser absorption of metal workpieces and penetration of welded joint, and thus improves welding quality; and reduces usage amount of inert gas, and thus reduces welding cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The metal workpiece is a metal workpiece, whose base material is steel. The low melting point coating layer is a galvanized layer. The assembly form is lap or butt. The thickness of the metal work piece is 0.3-3.5 mm. The inert gas is helium, argon, neon, krypton and/or xenon. The outlet diameter of the protective gas pipeline is 1-5 mm. Preferred Method: The power of the laser welding is 500-10000 W and the welding speed is 0.2-10 m/minute. The flow rate of the protective gas during the laser welding is 10-25 l/minute. The protective gas is discharged from the protective gas pipeline.

PN CN110607531-A
TI Electrochemically conversing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide gas cycle comprises reducing carbon monoxide-containing gas at electrochemical cathode, contacting with gas containing hydrogen sulfide, and returning.
AU LI C
   ZONG X
   MA W
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202000914E
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemically conversing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide gas cycle comprises reducing carbon monoxide-containing gas at electrochemical cathode to obtain carbon monoxide, and obtaining anode liquid containing oxidation state mediator at an electrochemical anode, contacting anode liquid containing oxidation state mediator with gas containing hydrogen sulfide to obtain sulfur simple substance comprising mixture of hydrogen ions and reduced state mediator, and product gas, and returning the mixture containing hydrogen ions and reduced state mediator to at the electrochemical anode for recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for electrochemically conversing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide gas cycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: converts carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide into high value-added products; ensures recycling between products; increases high processing efficiency; and reduces waste generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for electrochemically conversing hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide gas cycle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises returning carbon dioxide contained in hydrogen sulfide-containing gas and product gas to the electrochemical cathode for carbon dioxide reduction. The method comprises pumping the oxidized medium obtained in the electrocatalytic device to an absorption device, contacting and reacting with hydrogen sulfide to obtain sulfur, hydrogen ions and reduced medium, where the sulfur is separated and recovered, the hydrogen ions and the reduced medium are pumped to an anode chamber to complete the cycle. Preferred Components: The mediator comprises hydroquinone/quinone, Potassium ferricyanide/ potassium hexacyanoferrate(III) and/or iron(II)-EDTA/iron(III)-EDTA, where the molar concentration of the medium is 1x 10-5-10 mol/l. The electrochemical reaction catalyst in the electrochemical cathode includes gold, silver, palladium, gold-silver alloy, palladium-silver alloy, zinc, graphene-coated zinc oxide, copper, copper-zinc alloy, copper-tin alloy, cobalt phthalocyanine, iron phthalocyanine, magnesium porphyrin, zinc porphyrin and/or iron porphyrins. The electrochemical reaction catalyst of in electrochemical anode includes titanium, carbon paper, graphite, graphene and/or carbon nanotubes. The electrolyte of the electrochemical anode includes hydroquinone, potassium ferricyanideand and/or iron(II)-EDTA. The electrolyte of the electrochemical cathode comprises alkali metal carbonate, alkali metal bicarbonate, alkali metal sulfate, and/or imidazole-based ionic liquid. The molar concentration of electrolyte of the electrochemical anode and the molar concentration of the electrolyte of the electrochemical cathode, respectively is 0.01-5 mol/l, preferably 0.25-1 mol/l. The electrochemical anode includes an anode, and the electrochemical cathode include a cathode, where the voltage applied between the anode and the cathode is 0-10 V. The absorption device is provided with multiple sieve plates along the longitudinal interval of the absorption device, where the sieve plate is quartz sand sieve plate with a pores diameter of 10-500 mu m.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PT P
PN CN109867654-A; WO2020169032-A1; EP3805208-A1; CN109867654-B; EP3805208-A4
TI Preparing alkylene carbonate using epoxy alkane and carbon dioxide, involves taking epoxy alkane and carbon dioxide as raw materials, and contacting reaction raw material with catalyst to generate alkylene carbonate.
AU GUO J
   LI X
   MA G
   LI G
   XUE M
AE SHANDONG SHIDA SHENGHUA CHEM IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG SHIDA SHENGHUA CHEM IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG SHIDA SHENGHUA CHEM IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHENGHUA NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY DONGYING CO (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG SHIDA SHENGHUA CHEM IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201954520N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing alkylene carbonate using epoxy alkane and carbon dioxide, involves (i) taking epoxy alkane and carbon dioxide as raw materials, where the reaction temperature is 60-200 degrees C, and the reaction pressure is 0.1-10.0 MPa, and (ii) contacting a reaction raw material with a catalyst to generate alkylene carbonate, where the mass ratio of catalyst to epoxy alkane is (0.001-1):1. The catalyst is a multi-group complexed compound catalytic system comprising triphenylphosphine group, salicylaldehyde group and zinc, where the molar ratio of the catalyst is 5:(1-50):(1-50).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing alkylene carbonate using epoxy alkane and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides alkylene carbonate with ethylene oxide conversion rate of 99.8%, and ethylene carbonate selectivity of 99.5%, and obtains excellent technical effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalyst is prepared by (a) mixing zinc precursor with salicylic acid precursor at room temperature, adding a solvent, stirring and heating to obtain an intermediate product, and (b) mixing an intermediate product with a triphenylphosphine-based precursor, adding solvent, stirring, heating, and removing the solvent to obtain a catalyst product. Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature is 80-160 degrees C, and the reaction pressure is 0.5-8 MPa. The mass ratio of the catalyst to epoxy alkane is (0.005-0.5):1. Preferred Components: The catalytic system comprises at least one zinc atom, at least one triphenylphosphine group and at least one salicylaldehyde group. The epoxy alkane is one or more of ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epoxy chloropropane and epoxybutane. In the preparation of the catalyst, the zinc precursor used in step (a) is an oxide or halide of zinc, the salicylic acid precursor used in step (a) is salicylic acid or salicylic acid with a side chain, the triphenylphosphine precursor used in step (b) is triphenylphosphine, alkyltriphenylphosphine or halogen triphenylphosphine, and the solvent used in steps (a) and (b) is methanol, ethanol or diethyl ether.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN109306363-A
TI Pretreating corn cob waste by supercritical carbon dioxide assisted peroxide involves using corncob waste residue as raw material, and placing corncob waste residue in supercritical extraction kettle, heating extraction kettle.
AU LI M
   ZHANG J
   CHEN D
   JIANG X
   ZHENG X
   ZENG F
   YANG S
AE ZHEJIANG HUAKANG PHARM CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   JIAOZUO HUAKANG SUGAR ALCOHOL TECHNOLOGY (JIAO-Non-standard)
GA 2019160690
AB    NOVELTY - Pretreating corn cob waste by supercritical carbon dioxide assisted peroxide involves using corncob waste residue as a raw material, and placing corncob waste residue in a supercritical extraction kettle. The extraction kettle is heated, and the carbon dioxide is pressurized and pressurized, and a certain amount of the peroxide solution is introduced after stabilization. The material is taken out for drying, after a certain period of extraction, and then the dried material is formulated into a suspension solution. Glucose preparation is added to the suspension solution for saccharification to prepare glucose.
   USE - Method for pretreating corn cob waste by supercritical carbon dioxide assisted peroxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to pretreat corn cob waste by supercritical carbon dioxide assisted peroxide, where the process is simple, and conditions are easy to control.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves screening the corncob waste with a 40-60 mesh sieve, drying the corncob waste residue at 60-70 degrees C for 20-30 hours, weighing 50g of dried corncob waste residue into the extraction kettle. The extraction vessel is heated to 60-90 degrees C, supercritical carbon dioxide is introduced into the extraction kettle, and the pressure is raised to 10-25 megapascal, the peroxide solution is pumped with a flow rate of 1-3ml/minute, time 30-60 minutes, static extraction 1-1.5 hours. The outlet valve is open to remove lignin. The previous both steps are repeated to 4 times. The corncob waste residue material is removed, rinsed with five times of water, and then dried. The deionized water is used to prepare the suspended corncob waste residue material to have a substrate concentration of 10-25% suspension. The pH of the suspension is adjusted to 5.0-6.0, and then add 0.5-1% cellulase to saccharify the corncob waste residue to prepare glucose, and the saccharification time is 40-60 hours. The peroxide solution delivered by the pump is a hydrogen peroxide solvent.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

TI Production of ammonia synthesis gas using shift gas, involves desulfurizing shifted gas comprising hydrogen, nitrogen, oxygen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, performing carbon monoxide conversion, establishing catalytic conversion.
AU WANG W
   YANG H
   ZHONG J
AE YANG H (YANG-Individual)
GA 201875869R
AB    NOVELTY - The method for producing ammonia synthesis gas using shift gas, involves desulfurizing shifted gas comprising greater than or equal to 52 wt.% hydrogen, less than 7 wt.% nitrogen, 0.1 wt.% oxygen, less than or equal to 0.2 wt.% carbon monoxide and more than 40 wt.% carbon dioxide, adding 0.1 wt.% pure oxygen or same amount of air to the oxygen required for carbon monoxide conversion, establishing a catalytic conversion of carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide, converting less than or equal to 0.2 wt.% carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide, controlling residual concentration of carbon monoxide at less than 1 ppm, subjecting the conversion gas to fine deoxidation process, performing pressure swing adsorption to remove carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide to below 10 ppm, and obtaining ammonia synthesis gas.
   USE - Method for producing ammonia synthesis gas using shift gas.

PN CN108191631-A
TI Reducing carbon dioxide based on cadmium sulfide involves adding inorganic cadmium salt, diethylenetriamine, sulfur powder and de-ionized water, mixing and stirring to obtain uniform solution, and transferring.
AU CHEN D
AE DONGGUAN LIANZHOU INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY (DOGN-C)
GA 201851444C
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing carbon dioxide based on cadmium sulfide involves adding inorganic cadmium salt, diethylenetriamine, sulfur powder and de-ionized water, mixing and stirring to obtain uniform solution, and transferring to hydrothermal reaction kettle, centrifuging, precipitating and washing, and drying, to obtain nano cadmium sulfide. The prepared cadmium sulfide is added to sodium carbonate aqueous solution. The sodium dodecyl sulfate is added and ultrasonic dispersed for 1-2 hours at 500 Watts power to prepare reaction liquid. The prepared reaction solution is added into internal illumination type photocatalysis reactor with carbon dioxide, irradiated reaction solution with 500 Watts xenon lamp, and reacted.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide based on cadmium sulfide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce carbon dioxide based on cadmium sulfide that has simple operation and high catalytic efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic cadmium is cadmium nitrate, cadmium chloride, cadmium acetate, cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate, cadmium chloride pentahydrate, and cadmium acetate dihydrate. Preferred Condition: The ratio of inorganic cadmium salt, sulfur powder, deionized water, and diethylenetriamine is 1:(4-8):(0.3-0.7):(200-500). The reaction temperature is 60-100 degrees C. The reaction time is 24-72 hours. The concentration of the sodium carbonate aqueous solution is 0.1 mol/L. The ratio of nano cadmium sulfide and sodium carbonate aqueous solution is (0.01-0.03) g:200 ml. The flow rate of the carbon dioxide is 40-360 mL/minute. The ratio of nano cadmium sulfide and sodium dodecyl sulfate is 1:(0.005-0.015).

PN WO2018049938-A1; CN107837818-A; CA3007570-A1; VN59975-A; US2019016964-A1; ID201809704-A; US10472573-B2; CN107837818-B; CA3007570-C; IN201827031652-A; IN357429-B; VN10031794-B
TI Preparation of gasoline fraction hydrocarbon e.g.            isoparaffin, involves converting carbon dioxide and            hydrogen in presence of iron-based carbon dioxide            hydrogenation catalyst and/or metal-modified or            non-modified molecular sieves.
AU GE Q
   WEI J
   XU H
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
   CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
   CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
   CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201822519U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of gasoline fraction hydrocarbon                involves directly converting a mixed gas comprising                carbon dioxide and hydrogen as raw material under                the catalytic action of multifunctional composite                catalyst. The composite catalyst is a mixture of                iron-based carbon dioxide hydrogenation catalyst as                component (c1) and one or two or more of                metal-modified or non-modified molecular sieves as                component (c2). The mass ratio of component (c1)                and component (c2) is 1:10-10:1, preferably                1:3-3:1.
   USE - Preparation of gasoline fraction hydrocarbon                e.g. isoparaffin and aromatic hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides gasoline fraction                hydrocarbon with high selectivity of 70%,                conversion of carbon dioxide of 33% or more,                methane selectivity of less than 8%, and high                octane value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of                  iron-based hydrogenation catalyst involves mixing                  a soluble iron (II) salt and soluble iron (III)                  salt, or soluble iron (III) salts and adjuvant                  salts to form a salt solution (s1), adding an                  alkali solution to solution (s1), gradually                  adjusting the pH of the solution of 0-3 to the                  basic pH of 9-12, aging the solution for 1-5                  hours, separating the deposited product by                  magnetic adsorption, centrifugation or suction                  filtration, and washing the deposited product                  thoroughly with deionized water, drying and                  baking at 200-600 degrees C for 2-10 hours to                  obtain iron-based hydrogenation catalyst. The                  preparation of metal-modified molecular sieve                  involves immersing the molecular sieve into                  aqueous solution of a metal salt chosen from                  nitrate, chloride salt, bromide salt, acetate                  salt, acetylacetonate salt, citrate, oxalate, and                  benzoate, and stirring or ion exchanged for 2-24                  hours, washing, drying, baking, and calcining the                  mixture at 300-700 degrees C for 2-10 hours. The                  multifunctional composite catalyst is prepared by                  (1) powder mixing mode which involves weighing                  the iron-based catalyst and the molecular sieve                  catalyst powder, grinding the mixture in a mortar                  and mixing uniformly and pressing to form the                  composite catalyst, or (2) the particle mixing                  method which involves weighing the iron-based                  catalyst and molecular sieve catalyst powder and                  mixing particles uniformly to form a composite                  catalyst, or (3) the layered filling method which                  involves packing the catalyst bed in a reactor                  from top to bottom with or without inert material                  isolating layer. Preferred Condition: The                  preparation of gasoline fraction hydrocarbon is                  carried out at temperature of 250-450 degrees C,                  a pressure of 0.01-10.0 MPa, and raw material gas                  space velocity of 500-50000 ml/(hour.g).                  Preferred Components: The iron-based carbon                  dioxide hydrogenation catalyst is ferric oxide.                  Optionally, an auxiliary agent is added in the                  gasoline fraction hydrocarbon preparation method.                  The auxiliary agent is oxide, preferably sodium                  oxide, potassium oxide, manganese oxide, copper                  oxide, zirconium oxide, vanadium oxide, zinc                  oxide, and/or cerium oxide. The molecular sieve                  is one or more chosen from ZSM-5, ZSM-22, ZSM-23,                  Y, beta , MOR, and MCM-22, preferably ZSM-5                  molecular sieve having silica to alumina ratio of                  20-350 and MCM-22 molecular sieve having silica                  to alumina ratio of 20-200. The metal present in                  the modified molecular sieve is one or more                  chosen from molybdenum, zinc, rhodium, ruthenium,                  gallium, chromium, cobalt, nickel, sodium,                  copper, palladium, platinum, and lanthanum. The                  soluble iron (II) salt and the soluble iron (III)                  salt are water-soluble salt compounds, preferably                  chloride salt, a nitrate salt, and/or an acetate                  salt. The carbon dioxide is a gas-containing                  carbon dioxide, preferably carbon                  dioxide-containing industrial exhaust gas,                  automobile exhaust gas, coal gas and/or carbon                  dioxide absorbed in the atmosphere and seawater.                  Preferred Composition: The concentration of iron                  (III) in the salt solution is 0.05-1 mol/l. The                  concentration of hydrochloric acid is 5-12.1                  mol/l which adjusts the pH to 0-3. The molar                  ratio of iron (III) to iron (II) in the salt                  solution is 2:1-3. The concentration of the                  alkali solution is 0.1-10 mol/l. The mass ratio                  of molecular sieve and aqueous solution is                  1:10-200. The mass ratio of inert material                  isolating layer and the composite catalyst is                  0.01-10. The mass ratio of hydrogen and carbon                  dioxide is of 0.5-8.0. The content of the                  auxiliary agent is 0-30 %mass, preferably 0.5-10                  %mass based on total mass iron-based catalyst.                  The metal content in the modified molecular sieve                  is 0.1-20 %mass, preferably 0.5-10 %mass.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The adjuvant salt is                  water-soluble salt compounds. The alkali solution                  is one or more chosen from sodium hydroxide,                  potassium hydroxide, sodium bicarbonate, disodium                  carbonate, potassium carbonate, potassium                  bicarbonate, sodium oxalate, potassium oxalate,                  sodium salt of carboxylic acid of formula: RCOONa                  (a), potassium salt of carboxylic acid of                  formula: RCOOK (b), and ammonia.R=organic functional group comprising 1-20C                  alkyl, 1-20C alkenyl, or 6-20C aryl, preferably                  methyl, ethyl, or phenyl.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Preparing methanol from biomass, involves            hydrothermally treating biomass raw material, forming            hydrothermal biomass slurry, subjecting slurry to            solid-liquid separation, forming biomass and wood            vinegar, forming mixed gas and gasified slag from            biomass, subjecting mixed gas to methanol            synthesis.
AU CAO D
   JIANG F
AE ZHONGHE SMART ENERGY TECHNOLOGY SHENZHEN CO              LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2022C9182X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of methanol from biomass,                involves: (1) performing hydrothermal treatment on                biomass raw material to form hydrothermal biomass                slurry, followed by performing solid-liquid                separation to form hydrothermal biomass and wood                vinegar (pyroligneous acid); (2) performing biomass                gasification reaction on hydrothermal biomass, to                form a mixed gas containing carbon monoxide and                hydrogen, and a gasified slag; (3) with carbon                monoxide and hydrogen of the mixed gas, as raw                materials, performing methanol synthesis reaction                to form methanol. The treatment atmosphere used in                hydrothermal treatment process, is carbon dioxide.                The gasifying agent prepared in step (2), comprises                carbon dioxide and water vapor.
   USE - Method is used for preparing methanol from                biomass.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrothermal treatment involved in the                preparation process, enables to reduce the tar                content of product. The addition of carbon dioxide                in the process, further reduce the tar content                produced in the gasification reaction. The ratio of                hydrogen and carbon dioxide in syngas formed during                the process, is regulated to achieve efficient use                of biomass to obtain environmentally safe                methanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                used for preparing methanol from biomass, which                comprises a hydrothermal unit, gasification unit                and a methanol synthesis unit. The hydrothermal                unit comprises a hydrothermal device (1) provided                with a carbon dioxide input port (11), a first                steam input port (12), a biomass raw material feed                port (13), a hydrothermal biomass slurry discharge                port (14), a hydrothermal mixed gas outlet (15), a                first steam outlet (16) and a second steam outlet                (17). The gasification unit comprises a                gasification furnace (2) provided with a                hydrothermal biomass feeding hole (21), a                hydrothermal mixed gas inlet (22), steam reheating                outlet gas (25), a biomass gasification reaction                mixed gas outlet (23), a gasification slag                discharging hole (24), a steam reheating inlet                (26), a steam reheating device (27) and an exhaust                gas inlet (28). The hydrothermal mixed gas inlet                (22) is connected to the hydrothermal mixed gas                outlet (15). The methanol synthesis unit comprises                a methanol synthesizer (3) and a gas-liquid                separator (7) positioned downstream of methanol                synthesizer (3). The methanol synthesizer (3) is                provided with a synthesis gas raw material inlet                (31), a gas-liquid mixed material outlet (32), a                third steam outlet (33) and a cooling water inlet                (34). The gas-liquid mixed material outlet (32) is                connected to gas-liquid separator (7) which is                provided with a methanol discharge hole (71) and a                methanol synthesis reaction discharge gas outlet                (72).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic diagram of system                used for producing methanol from biomass. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).
   1Hydrothermal device
   11Carbon dioxide input port
   12First steam input port
   13Biomass raw material feed port
   14Hydrothermal biomass slurry discharge                port
   15Hydrothermal mixed gas outlet
   16First steam outlet
   17Second steam outlet
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: 1) The biomass                  gasification reaction mixed gas generated in step                  (2) of the methanol preparation process, is                  subjected to a carbon dioxide removal step (4)                  which involves removing carbon dioxide from the                  mixed gas, followed by passing removed carbon                  dioxide to hydrothermal treatment process, to                  provide carbon dioxide atmosphere.2) The process further involves a heat                  exchange step (5) and combustion step (6).3) Step 5 involves passing water produced in                  hydrothermal treatment step (1), biomass                  gasification reaction mixed gas produced in step                  (2), and methanol synthesis reaction exhaust gas                  produced in the step (3), to a heat exchanger,                  followed by transferring heat from the biomass                  gasification reaction mixed gas and the methanol                  synthesis reaction exhaust gas, to convert water                  into steam, then passing the produced steam to                  the hydrothermal treatment process back to step                  (1), as a steam heat transfer medium and then                  passing to the biomass gasification reaction in                  step (2) as a steam gasifying agent.4) The combustion step (6) involves                  combusting the biomass formed in step (1), to                  form a fifth product biomass ash, followed by                  passing combustion flue gas generated by                  combustion, to the biomass gasification reaction                  in step (2).Preferred Components: The biomass comprises                  biomass feedstock and/or the hydrothermal                  biomass.Preferred Method: The biomass raw material                  used in step (1), is obtained by crushing, adding                  water, stirring and removing impurities from the                  initial biomass, and is subjected to                  pretreatment, before using in step 1.

TI Co-culturing breast cancer cells and T cells to            improve ability of immune cells to kill tumors,            involves utilizing medical carbon dioxide incubator            which comprises box body is internal hollow            structure.

GA 202274852A
AB    NOVELTY - Co-culturing breast cancer cells and T cells                involves utilizing a medical carbon dioxide                incubator. The medical carbon dioxide incubator                comprises a box body. The main body of the box body                is an internal hollow structure. The front end of                the box is connected with the box door through the                pin. The four corners of the left end face of the                box door are fixedly connected with the pin, the                box door and post together form a support                structure. Two passages are opened in the interior                of the box door in a linear array. A cover plate is                hinged inside the through grooves opened in the box                door. A guide rod is fixedly connected on the top                surface of the box door. The two guide rods are                fixedly connected to the top surface of the box                door in a linear array. The side of the two guide                rods away from the box door is fixedly connected                with a contact block. The contact block is made of                metal material. A contact groove is formed on the                bottom end surface of the contact block.
   USE - Co-culturing method of breast cancer cells and                T cells to improve the ability of immune cells to                kill tumors.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the existing problem that                when taking out or putting in the work, realizes                the automatic opening and closing of the culture                plate under the premise of non-electric control                driving, and achieves the purpose of protecting the                culture inside the box.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-culturing breast cancer cells and T cells                involves utilizing a medical carbon dioxide                incubator. The medical carbon dioxide incubator                comprises a box body. The main body of the box body                is an internal hollow structure. The front end of                the box is connected with the box door through the                pin. The four corners of the left end face of the                box door are fixedly connected with the pin, the                box door and post together form a support                structure. A guide rod is fixedly connected on the top                surface of the box door. The guide rods are                provided with two places. The two guide rods are                fixedly connected to the top surface of the box                door in a linear array. The side of the two guide                rods away from the box door is fixedly connected                with a contact block. The contact block is made of                metal material. A contact groove is formed on the                bottom end surface of the contact block. The                contact groove opened on the bottom end surface of                the contact block matches the proximity                switch.

PN CN111023588-A; CN111023588-B
TI Solar energy-utilizing heat-collecting chemical storage and hydrocarbon fuel preparing system contains light collecting and tracking system, carbon material storage tank, metal carbonate composite material storage tank, and calciner.
AU XUAN Y
   DA Y
   TENG L
AE UNIV NANJING AERONAUTICS & ASTRONAUTICS (UNUA-C)
   UNIV NANJING AERONAUTICS & ASTRONAUTICS (UNUA-C)
GA 2020338375
AB    NOVELTY - A solar energy-utilizing heat-collecting chemical storage and hydrocarbon fuel preparing system (S) comprises a light collecting and tracking system, a carbon material sealed storage tank, a metal carbonate composite material sealed storage tank, a calciner, a product synthesis gas collection device, a metal oxide composite material sealed storage tank, a carbon dioxide sealed storage tank and an acidification furnace. The light collecting and tracking system collects solar energy and directly irradiates the metal carbonate composite particles in the calciner. The metal carbonate composite material sealed storage tank is respectively connected with the calciner and the acidification furnace, and is used to provide metal carbonate composite particles into the calcination furnace. The carbon material sealed storage tank is connected to the calciner, and is used to provide carbon material (I), to the calciner.
   USE - Solar energy-utilizing heat-collecting chemical storage and hydrocarbon fuel preparing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The solar energy-utilizing heat-collecting chemical storage and hydrocarbon fuel preparing system has excellent cycle stability and enables preparation of hydrocarbon fuel having high quality. The composite particles has improved anti-sintering property due to direct absorption of solar energy, and excellent mechanical property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A solar energy-utilizing heat-collecting chemical storage and hydrocarbon fuel preparing system (S) comprises a light collecting and tracking system, a carbon material sealed storage tank, a metal carbonate composite material sealed storage tank, a calciner, a product synthesis gas collection device, a metal oxide composite material sealed storage tank, a carbon dioxide sealed storage tank and an acidification furnace. The light collecting and tracking system collects solar energy and directly irradiates the metal carbonate composite particles in the calciner, and the metal carbonate composite particles directly absorbs solar radiation energy. The metal carbonate composite material sealed storage tank is respectively connected with the calciner and the acidification furnace, and is used to provide metal carbonate composite particles into the calcination furnace. The carbon material sealed storage tank is connected to the calciner, and is used to provide carbon material of formula: CxHyOz (I), where x-z are 1 or more, to the calciner. The calciner is used to provide a place and reaction conditions for the chemical reaction of metal carbonate composite particles and the carbon material. The product synthesis gas collection device is connected to the calciner, and is used to collect the product gas generated by the chemical reaction in the calciner. The metal oxide composite material sealed storage tank is connected to the calciner and the acidification furnace, such that solid products after chemical reaction in the calciner is collected and transferred to the acidification furnace. The carbon dioxide sealed storage tank is connected to the acidification furnace, to supply carbon dioxide gas to the acidification furnace. The acidification furnace is used to provide a place and reaction conditions for the contact reaction between the metal oxide composite particles and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The light collecting and tracking system is one or more chosen from tower system, trough system, butterfly system and Fresnel system. The calciner comprises a thermochemical reactor with a transparent window. System (S) further comprises an energy utilization system connected to the acidification furnace. The energy utilization system is a hot water energy utilization system or a turbine power generation system.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal carbonate composite particles include metal carbonate and catalyst. The metal carbonate undergoes an endothermic decomposition reaction in the calciner to produce metal oxides and carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide and Component (I) undergo oxidation-reduction reaction to generate hydrocarbon fuel comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The metal carbonate is calcium carbonate or magnesium carbonate. The catalyst is one or more chosen from copper, iron, ruthenium, rhenium, nickel, cobalt, platinum, iron(II, III) oxide, and cobalt(II, III) oxide. Preferred Process: Component (I) reacts with iron(II, III) oxide in the calciner to generate iron, carbon monoxide and hydrogen. Component (I) reacts with cobalt(II, III) oxide in the calciner to generate cobalt oxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The regeneration of iron(II, III) oxide in the acidification furnace involves reacting 3 mol of iron and 4 mol of carbon dioxide to generate 1 mol iron(II, III) oxide and 4 mol carbon monoxide. The regeneration of cobalt(II, III) oxide in the acidification furnace involves reacting 3 mol of cobalt oxide and 1 mol of carbon dioxide to generate 1 mol cobalt (II, III) oxide and 1 mol carbon monoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Component (I) is one or more chosen from ethanol, glycerin, acetic acid, and methane.

PT P
PN CN110436409-A
TI Preparing fruit tree fertilizer involves using nitrogen fertilizer production process and raw materials containing hydrogen, and nitrogen gas, and refining-removing impurities other than hydrogen and nitrogen in raw material gas.
AU ZHUANG F
AE XUZHOU JIANONG AGRIC DEV CO LTD (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201996867A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fruit tree fertilizer involves using nitrogen fertilizer production process and raw materials containing hydrogen, and nitrogen gas, then refining-removing impurities other than hydrogen and nitrogen in the raw material gas, so that the raw material gas is purified. The pure nitrogen and hydrogen are mixed in the volume ratio of 1:3 at high pressure, and synthesizing it into ammonia under the action of a catalyst and high temperature and further subjecting to ammonia processing processing ammonia to obtain nitrogen fertilizer. The raw materials of nitrogen fertilizer production mostly includes natural gas, refinery gas, coke oven gas, heavy oil, coal and coke and other gas, liquid and solid raw materials.
   USE - Method for preparing fruit tree fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare fruit tree fertilizer, which effectively avoids the occurrence of spontaneous combustion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The gas storage is used to process carbon-containing fuels such as coal, coke, heavy oil, and natural gas in a gas generator, and perform gasification reaction with air and water vapor at a high temperature to produce half of the water gas used as a raw material for ammonia synthesis. The main components of semi-water gas are carbon monoxide, hydrogen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide and methane, because this gas contains more nitrogen for ammonia synthesis, although the content of sulfide in the desulfurized gas is not high, it is deactivate to various catalyst poisoning, where hydrogen sulfide is very corrosive to equipment and pipelines, which is an important factor causing leakage, hence the sulfide is removed and adsorbed with activated carbon. The desulfurized gas contains a large amount of carbon monoxide, and a large amount of water vapor is needed to treat the carbon monoxide to convert it into carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The different production process of nitrogen fertilizer are compressed according to the process requirements using compressor. The compressor is divided into multiple sections, and the raw material gas is pressurized in sections, and most of them are compressed and synthesized into ammonia. The carbon dioxide is detached and using activated carbon adsorption method to desorb the carbon dioxide inside, so that the raw material gas is refined. The liquid gas is refined using the nitrogen gas washing method and using liquid nitrogen to wash the raw material gas to absorb carbon monoxide at a higher pressure. The copper washing method also uses copper ammonia salt solution to wash the raw material gas to absorb carbon monoxide at a higher pressure and carbon dioxide and other gasses to achieve the purpose of refining. The pure nitrogen and hydrogen mixed gas of ammonia are mixed in the weight ratio of 1:3 in a synthesis tower at a high temperature above 400 degrees C and a pressure of 2000 MPa, and in the presence of a synthesis catalyst, and performing gas-solid contact exotherm to generate ammonia.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of ammonia cannot exceed 40 degrees C, ammonia liquid tank cannot be exposed to the sun for a long time, and the spraying device is used in an open-air environment and is cooled down. The absorbent having corrosive effect on the equipment is washed with water under the presence of carbon dioxide at 60 degrees C and has a corrosive effect on carbon steel equipment. The equipment is selected and anticorrosive measures are taken. The fire resistance rating of structures such as desulfurization tank farms, conversion tank farms, and decarbonization tower farms using reinforced concrete frame structures and frameworks are of class two, to meet the requirements of fire protection. The steel frame of the device is coated with fireproof coating to avoid the damage to the frame and equipment caused by the fire. The sulfur generated in the production process needs to be cleaned once a day, and the amount of sulfur stored in the factory building cannot exceed the daily storage amount. The ferrous sulfide which is liable to spontaneous combustion is often generated in the carbon steel equipment and pipes of the desulfurization process, when it is dried, it encounters air at room temperature, then it ignite spontaneously and generate irritating sulfur dioxide gas and during maintenance, the equipment and pipelines is wet with water to avoid spontaneous combustion.

PN CN109233249-A
TI Improving mechanical properties of aqueous polyurethane rubber comprises adding the modified nano silica aerogel into the aqueous polyurethane emulsion, heating and stirring.
AU SHU L
AE ANHUI YUTANG RAIN GEAR CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019129182
AB    NOVELTY - Improving mechanical properties of aqueous polyurethane rubber comprises adding the modified nano silica aerogel into the aqueous polyurethane emulsion, heating to 70-80 degrees C and stirring for 2-3 hours at 600-700 rotations/minute. The method for preparing modified nano silica aerogel comprises (i) taking 25-28 g tetraethyl orthosilicate and 2-3 ml methyl triethoxysilane, placing in 250 ml beaker, adding 30-35 ml ethanol, heating water bath at a constant temperature, stirring, preparing 40-50 ml hydrochloric acid solution; (ii) placing the premix in a four-necked flask, heating to 60-65 degrees C under agitation, adding drop-wisely 3-3.5 ml isophorone diisocyanate-acetone solution, adding 1-1.5 g maleic anhydride; and (iii) placing the aged gel solution in a supercritical drying oven, reducing the temperature in the dryer to 2-3 degrees C, passing liquid carbon dioxide, and slowly releasing carbon dioxide gas when the temperature returns to indoor conditions.
   USE - The method is useful for improving mechanical properties of aqueous polyurethane rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The aqueous polyurethane rubber: is cold resistance, heat resistance, corrosion resistance; has good adhesive property. The method improves the cohesion, mechanical properties and bonding strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving mechanical properties of aqueous polyurethane rubber comprises adding the modified nano silica aerogel into the aqueous polyurethane emulsion, heating to 70-80 degrees C and stirring for 2-3 hours at 600-700 rotations/minute, where the amount of modified nano-silica aerogel is 0.8-0.9 wt.% aqueous polyurethane emulsion. The method for preparing modified nano silica aerogel comprises (i) taking 25-28 g tetraethyl orthosilicate and 2-3 ml methyl triethoxysilane, placing in 250 ml beaker, adding 30-35 ml ethanol, heating water bath at a constant temperature, stirring for 20-30 minutes, preparing 40-50 ml hydrochloric acid solution with a concentration of 0.25-0.28 mol/l, adding into beakers 3 times, each time for 15-20 minutes, simultaneously adding drop-wisely 4.2-4.5 ml dimethylformamide with stirring; (ii) placing the premix in a four-necked flask, heating to 60-65 degrees C under agitation, adding drop-wisely 3-3.5 ml isophorone diisocyanate-acetone solution, reacting until the isocyanate is not detected by infrared spectroscopy, cooling the reaction solution to 48-50 degrees C, adding 1-1.5 g maleic anhydride, stirring and dissolving, passing to a constant temperature and humidity chamber of 68-70 degrees C, incubating for 24-27 hours and taking out and placing at 30-35 degrees C for 2-3 days; and (iii) placing the aged gel solution in a supercritical drying oven, reducing the temperature in the dryer to 2-3 degrees C, passing liquid carbon dioxide, performing solvent replacement, removing water and ethanol from the gel, changing the setting conditions in the supercritical drying oven when all are replaced by carbon dioxide liquid and slowly releasing carbon dioxide gas when the temperature returns to indoor conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aqueous polyurethane emulsion has a solid content of 53-55%. The isophorone diisocyanate in the isophorone diisocyanate-acetone solution is for 5.5-6 wt.%. Preferred Method: in the step (i), the water bath is heated at a constant temperature of 36-40 degrees C. In the step (iii), the setting conditions in the change supercritical drying oven comprises the temperature of 30-32 degrees C and the pressure of 7-7.2 MPa.

PN CN109089772-A
TI Planting and improving quality of early-maturing watermelon by cultivating early-maturing watermelon varieties seedlings, providing carbon dioxide gas fertilizing effect, applying fertilizer and spraying regulator on young watermelon.
AU HU B
AE BENGBU YUHUI MACHENG HUBENHAO FAMILY FAR (BENG-Non-standard)
GA 201902608K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for planting and improving quality of early-maturing watermelon involves (a) cultivating early-maturing watermelon varieties seedlings, mulching the early-maturing watermelon seedlings, transplanting the seedlings to greenhouse, growing for 1-2 days, applying base fertilizer to the planting site 2 months before transplanting, and covering with straw, (b) providing carbon dioxide gas fertilizing effect to the greenhouse after 8-10 days of transplanting the seedlings using 1.04-1.05 g/kg carbon dioxide, closing the greenhouse, maintaining the temperature of greenhouse at 28-30 degrees C, turning on the ventilation after 2-3 hours, inserting drawbar vertically into the planting soil to support climbing vine and fix the growth direction of the plant, and removing weak branches and 2-3 old leaves 7-8 days before plants enters bud stage, and (c) applying fertilizer containing nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium, and spraying 0.8-1 ml regulator for 4-5 times in 3-5 days on young watermelon.
   USE - The method is useful for planting and improving quality of early-maturing watermelon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases yield and quality of watermelon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for planting and improving quality of early-maturing watermelon involves (a) cultivating early-maturing watermelon varieties seedlings in early January and mid-February, mulching the early-maturing watermelon seedlings, transplanting the seedlings to greenhouse, where the transplanting planting density is 260-280 plants/acre, which was protected from light after transplanting, growing for 1-2 days, applying base fertilizer to the planting site 2 months before transplanting, and covering with straw, where the base fertilizer comprises 550-560 kg/acre chicken manure, 25-30 kg/acre compound fertilizer, 22-24 kg/acre superphosphate, and 17-18 kg/acre potassium sulfate, (b) providing carbon dioxide gas fertilizing effect to the greenhouse after 8-10 days of transplanting the seedlings using 1.04-1.05 g/kg carbon dioxide, closing the greenhouse, maintaining the temperature of greenhouse at 28-30 degrees C, turning on the ventilation after 2-3 hours, inserting drawbar vertically into the planting soil to support climbing vine and fix the growth direction of the plant, and removing weak branches and 2-3 old leaves 7-8 days before plants enters bud stage, and (c) applying fertilizer containing nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium in a ratio of 13.8-14.0:9.5-9.7:4.6-4.9 to the seedlings after entering the fruit setting period, and spraying 0.8-1 ml regulator for 4-5 times in 3-5 days on young watermelon, where the regulator comprises 1.2-1.4 pts. wt. acetic acid, 2-2.5 pts. wt. acetone, 3-3.5 pts. wt. glycerol, 0.7-0.8 pt. wt. propylene carbonate, 0.4-0.6 pt. wt. ethyl 2-methylbutyrate, 2.3-2.5 pts. wt. potassium iodide solution, and 8-10 pts. wt. water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The watermelon seedlings are germinated at 32-33 degrees C. The step (b) involves providing carbon dioxide gas fertilizing effect for 2-3 times in morning using carbon dioxide gas generator. The step (c) involves applying urea and compound fertilizer comprising nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium in a ratio of 4.4-4.6:2.3-2.4:1.7-1.9 after the first watermelon is harvested. The concentration of potassium iodide aqueous solution is 8-10 %mass.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PN WO2018213724-A1; AU2018270106-A1; CA3064080-A1; KR2020011454-A; IN201917047162-A; EP3625327-A1; SG11201910665-A1; CN110997896-A; US2020181659-A1; BR112019024244-A2; JP2020523033-W; US10934566-B2; EP3625327-A4; ID202001710-A1; HK40025963-A0
TI Process for bioconverting methane to product            containing polyhydroxyalkanoate, by passing gas            comprising methane-containing gas and oxygen-containing            gas to reaction zone and separating            polyhydroxyalkanoate-containing methanotrophs.
AU HICKEY R F
   MORSE M C
   PIEJA A J
AE HICKEY R F (HICK-Individual)
   MORSE M C (MORS-Individual)
   PIEJA A J (PIEJ-Individual)
   MANGO MATERIALS INC (MANG-Non-standard)
   MANGO MATERIALS INC (MANG-Non-standard)
   MANGO MATERIALS INC (MANG-Non-standard)
   MANGO MATERIALS INC (MANG-Non-standard)
   HICKEY R F (HICK-Individual)
   MORSE M C (MORS-Individual)
   PIEJA A J (PIEJ-Individual)
GA 2018930029
AB    NOVELTY - High productivity process for bioconverting                methane to product containing polyhydroxyalkanoate,                involves (a) passing substrate gas comprising a                methane-containing gas and an oxygen-containing gas                to a reaction zone for contact under fermentation                conditions with an aqueous medium having a                population of methanotrophs, (b) passing a                methane-containing gas and an oxygen-containing gas                to a reaction zone for contact under fermentation                conditions with at least a portion of the                methanotroph-rich aqueous medium, where the medium                has a limitation or a substantial absence of at                least one nutrient required for the growth of the                population of the methanotrophs to create nutrient                limited conditions that inhibit the growth of the                population of methanotrophs, and withdrawing                unreacted gas from the reaction zone, and (c)                separating the polyhydroxyalkanoate-containing                methanotrophs from the aqueous medium of step                (b).
   USE - The process is useful for bioconverting                methane to product containing polyhydroxyalkanoate                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process bioconverts methane to product                containing polyhydroxyalkanoate with high                productivity in an economically-attractive                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High productivity process for bioconverting                methane to product containing polyhydroxyalkanoate,                involves (a) passing substrate gas comprising a                methane-containing gas and an oxygen-containing gas                to a reaction zone for contact under fermentation                conditions with an aqueous medium having a                population of methanotrophs, where the medium                contain nutrients for the growth of the population                of the methanotroph to provide a methanotroph-rich                aqueous medium, where the growth of the population                of methanotrophs further results in the                co-production of carbon dioxide, water and heat,                and withdrawing unreacted gas from the reaction                zone, (b) passing a methane-containing gas and an                oxygen-containing gas to a reaction zone for                contact under fermentation conditions with at least                a portion of the methanotroph-rich aqueous medium,                where the medium has a limitation or a substantial                absence of at least one nutrient required for the                growth of the population of the methanotrophs to                create nutrient limited conditions that inhibit the                growth of the population of methanotrophs and to                cause production of polyhydroxyalkanoate by the                methanotrophs and the co-production of carbon                dioxide, water and heat, and withdrawing unreacted                gas from the reaction zone, and (c) separating the                polyhydroxyalkanoate-containing methanotrophs from                the aqueous medium of step (b), where (i) the rate                of passing at least one substrate gas-containing                gas to the reaction zone in each of steps (a) and                (b) is at substrate-diffusion conditions, (ii) a                portion of the aqueous medium is continuously                withdrawn from the reaction zone and contacted with                a stripping gas to remove carbon dioxide and                provide a carbon dioxide-lean aqueous medium, and                (iii) passing at least a portion of the carbon                dioxide-lean aqueous medium to the reaction zone of                at least one of steps (a) and (b), where an                oxygenated CI compound of at least one of methanol                and formic acid or water soluble salt is supplied                to the aqueous medium in at least one of steps (a)                and (b).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The substrate-limiting                  gas comprises methane-containing gas. The                  methane-containing feed contains hydrogen sulfide                  and the aqueous medium absorb absorbs at least a                  portion of the hydrogen sulfide to provide an                  unreacted gas stream containing a reduced                  concentration of hydrogen sulfide. The                  methane-containing gas comprises biogas and                  anaerobic digester gas. The methane-containing                  gas comprises at least one of a landfill gas and                  a tail gas derived directly or indirectly from                  another fermentation process. Preferred Process:                  The rate of passing methane-containing gas to the                  reaction zone in at least one of steps (a) and                  (b) is modulated to provide a substantially                  stable molar concentration of methane in the                  unreacted gases. The reaction zone of at least                  one of steps (a) and (b) is a deep tank, bubble                  column reaction zone characterized in                  substantially uniform liquid composition and                  substantially non-uniform gas composition over                  the height of the reaction zone, and at least a                  portion of the substrate-containing gas is                  introduced in a lower portion of the reaction                  zone. The rate of withdrawal of the broth in step                  (ii) is sufficient to remove an amount of carbon                  dioxide equivalent to at least 40% carbon dioxide                  produced by the metabolic activity in the                  reaction zone. The rate of withdrawal of the                  broth in step (ii) is sufficient to remove 50-75%                  carbon dioxide produced by the metabolic activity                  in the reaction zone. The oxygenated CI compound                  is added to the reaction zone of step (a) when                  the rate of passing methane-containing gas to the                  reaction zone is no longer under                  methane-diffusion conditions. The portion of the                  carbon dioxide-lean aqueous medium passed to the                  reaction zone of at least one of steps (a) and                  (b) is cooled. The steps (a) and (b) are                  performed sequentially in a reactor vessel, where                  each steps are performed in separate reactor                  vessels. The portion of the aqueous medium in the                  reaction zone of step (a) is passed to the                  reaction zone of step (b). The reaction zones of                  step (b) are provided for each reaction zone of                  step (a) and a portion of the aqueous medium of                  step (a) is passed to at least one of the step                  (b) reaction zones at a given time to provide a                  semi-batch process. The carbon dioxide-lean                  aqueous medium is subjected to indirect heat                  exchange with a cooling fluid for further cooling                  of the carbon dioxide-lean aqueous medium and                  heating of the cooling fluid, where the heated                  cooling fluid from the indirect heat exchange is                  passed to a heat pump to provide a superheated                  fluid. The superheated fluid is used to heat the                  stripping gas. The superheated fluid is used in                  the recovery of polyhydroxyalkanoate from                  methanotrophs. The aqueous medium is withdrawn                  from a lower portion of the reaction zone for at                  least one of step (a) and (b), subjected to                  flashing conditions to remove a portion of the                  carbon dioxide, and then subjected to contact                  with a stripping gas.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); ; A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

AB    (CN207085148-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a low pressure carbon                dioxide extinguishing system. it comprises a                fire-extinguishing device, a heating device and a                control cabinet, fire extinguishing device                comprises tank, starting the pipeline and CO2                conveying pipeline, the pipeline and the CO2                conveying pipeline are connected with the storage                tank; CO2 conveying pipeline is provided with a                conveying main pipeline and a conveying branch and                at least two conveying branch, conveying main                pipeline, the branch is provided with a selector                valve and a nozzle, a conveying main pipeline is                provided with a valve, the main valve and pressure                switch, starting the pipeline comprises a main                pipeline and at least two starting branch starting,                starting the main pipeline connected with the                starting branch, the main pipeline is provided with                a starting pipe service valve, a pressure reducing                valve and a first electromagnetic valve, starting                the main pipeline connected with the main valve,                the branch is provided with a second                electromagnetic valve, starting the branch                connected with the selection valve, the tank is                provided with a refrigerating and heating device on                the tank. The utility model satisfies the                requirement of low temperature and bad environment                area fire, convenient maintenance, adopting heating                and refrigeration mode, expands the application                environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                low pressure carbon dioxide fire extinguishing                system.
