EA    NOVELTY - The system has a starting main line (110)                provided with a starting line maintenance valve                (112), a pressure reducing valve (113) and a first                electromagnetic valve (114). The starting main line                is connected with a main valve (126). A second                electromagnetic valve (115) is placed on a starting                branch (111). The starting branch is connected to a                selector valve (122). A refrigeration unit (13) is                placed on a storage tank (10). A heating device (2)                is placed on the storage tank. A control cabinet                (3) is provided with an audible and visual alarm                (30), a deflation indicator (31), a manual                release/stop button (32) and an electrical                isolation maintenance switch (33).
   USE - Low pressure carbon dioxide fire extinguishing                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system satisfies the requirements of fire                protection in a low temperature and harsh                environment area, and is convenient to maintain,                and adopts a dual mode of heating and cooling to                expand the application environment.

TI Carbon dioxide adsorbent used in fluidized bed            process, comprises amine-grounded MOF/alumina composite            comprising amine-introduced porous metal-organic            skeleton and aluminum oxide bonded to metal ions of            porous metal-organic skeleton.
AU HONG C S
   CHOE J H
   JONGHYEOK C
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 2020C3001F
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-dioxide adsorbent comprises an                amine-grounded MOF/alumina composite comprising an                amine-introduced porous metal-organic skeleton and                aluminum oxide bonded to metal ions of the                amine-introduced porous metal-organic skeleton,                where the surface of the composite is coated with a                hydrophobic silane.
   USE - Carbon dioxide adsorbent used in fluidized bed                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide adsorbent has excellent                structural stability from moisture generated from                exhaust gas, and reduced generation of renewable                energy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous                  metal-organic framework is chosen from                  metal-4,4'-dioxido-3,3'-biphenyldicarboxylate of                  formula: M2(dobpdc) (I),                  metal-2,5-dioxido-1,4-benzenedicarboxylate of                  formula: M2(dobdc) (II),                  metal-4,6-dioxido-1,3-benzenedicarboxylate of                  formula: M2(m-dobdc) (III),                  metal-1,5-dioxide-2,6-naphthalenedicarboxylate of                  formula: M2(dondc) (IV) and                  metal-4,4'-dioxido-3,3'-triphenyldicarboxylate of                  formula: M2(dotpdc) (V). The mine is of formula                  (VI) or (VII), preferably amine is                  ethylenediamine, 1-methylethylenediamine,                  1,1-dimethylethylenediamine or                  N-ethylethylenediamine. The hydrophobic silane is                  a compound of formula (VIII), preferably chosen                  from compounds of formulae (IX)-(XIV).M = Mg, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu or                  Zn;R1-R10,R1'-R3' = (CH2)m-CH3;n = 1-20;andm,l = 0-20.Preferred Composition: The hydrophobic                  silane is coated at a weight ratio of 3-10 times                  that of the composite.

PT P
PN CN111675925-A; CN111675925-B
TI Mixed dye used for supercritical carbon dioxide anhydrous color matching, comprises Disperse Red 54, Disperse Blue 79, and/or Disperse Red 167.
AU ZHENG H
   ZHENG L
   JING X
   YU Z
   LI S
AE UNIV DALIAN POLYTECHNIC (UYDP-C)
GA 2020936570
AB    NOVELTY - Mixed dye comprises at least two of Disperse Red 54, Disperse Blue 79 and Disperse Red 167.
   USE - The mixed dye is used for supercritical carbon dioxide anhydrous color matching (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed dye has a simple preparation process and rich colors, and is clean and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a supercritical carbon dioxide anhydrous color matching method using the mixed dye, comprising using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid as a dyeing medium, and using mixed dye to dye the textile to be dyed in one step.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed dye is a mixture of Disperse Red 54 and Disperse Blue 79, a mixture of Disperse Red 167 and Disperse Blue 79, a mixture of Disperse Red 54 and Disperse Red 167, or a mixture of Disperse Red 54, Disperse Blue 79 and Disperse Red 167. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of Disperse Red 54 to Disperse Blue 79 is 4:1, 3:1, 1:1, 1:2, or 1:3. The weight ratio of Disperse Red 167 to Disperse Blue 79 is 3:1, 1:1, or 1:3. The weight ratio of Disperse Red 54 to Disperse Red 167 is 3:1 or 1:1. The weight ratio of Disperse Red 54, Disperse Blue 79 and Disperse Red 167 is 1:1.25:0.15 or 1: 2:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Method: The method involves placing 1-6% on weight of fabric (owf) mixed dye in a dye kettle, placing the textiles to be dyed in the dyeing kettle, and dyeing the textiles to be dyed at 80-140 degrees C and 16-28 MPa for 20-90 minutes. Preferred Components: The material of the textile to be dyed is polyester, super-imitation cotton, acrylic, wool or aramid. The type of the textile to be dyed is fiber, cheese, hank, fabric, webbing or shoe material.

PT P
PN US2020054962-A1; US10688410-B2
TI Extracting Cannabis oil, comprises e.g. performing            grinding assisted extraction comprising grinding plant            material in presence of solvent.
AU VANAMAN B
   DIMITRELOS G
AE EVELLO INT LLC (EVEL-Non-standard)
GA 202014641F
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting Cannabis oil, comprises: performing                grinding assisted extraction comprising grinding                plant material in the presence of solvent;                performing co-solvent extraction using subcritical                carbon dioxide comprising adding liquid carbon                dioxide to the mixture, where the mixture is                maintained at a temperature and pressure                combination sufficient to maintain the liquid                carbon dioxide in a subcritical state; degassing                the carbon dioxide; and separating the mixture by                centrifugation.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting Cannabis                oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not require that plants be                dried before being fed into the system; and avoids                time and costs associated with drying                processes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: agitating the mixture during                  co-solvent extraction with the liquid carbon                  dioxide; heating the mixture after the co-solvent                  extraction to drive degassing of the liquid                  carbon dioxide co-solvent, where heating the                  mixture after the co-solvent extraction comprises                  heating the mixture to 26-32 degrees C. The plant                  material ground in the presence of the solvent is                  wet plant material. The step of grinding the                  plant material in the presence of solvent                  comprises grinding the plant material to micron                  scale, where the plant material is ground to less                  than 20 microns during grinding assisted                  extraction. The mixture is at a temperature of 30                  degrees C and -18 degrees C and a pressure of                  5.2-72 bar when the liquid carbon dioxide is                  added to the mixture. The step of centrifugation                  separates the mixture into a solid phase, oil                  phase, and water phase, where the oil phase                  comprises extract extracted from the plant                  material, where the method further comprises                  winterizing the extract in an inline                  winterization unit, filtering the winterized                  extract with a micron filter, preheating the                  filtered extract with a preheater, evaporating a                  first portion of the extract in an evaporation                  chamber of a short-path distillation unit, and a                  second portion of the extract passes through the                  evaporation chamber without evaporating,                  condensing the first portion of the extract in a                  vertical condenser, and evaporating at least one                  additional portions of the second portion of the                  extract in the same or at least one additional                  evaporation chambers at higher temperatures and                  condensing these portions separately in the same                  or at least one additional vertical condenser to                  obtain refined cannabis oil pay product                  comprising cannabinoids isolated by weight. The                  step of degassing the carbon dioxide comprises                  heating the mixture before or during                  centrifugation, where at least a portion of the                  carbon dioxide is degassed during centrifugation,                  and degassing the carbon dioxide comprises allows                  a supernatant component of the separated mixture                  generated by the centrifugation to expand.                  Preferred Components: The ratio of solvent to                  plant material present during the grinding                  assisted extraction is greater than 1:1 on a                  weight basis, or greater than 2:1 on a weight                  basis. The solvent is a food grade solvent, or a                  non-polar solvent. The non-polar solvent is                  glycerol monooleate (preferred), pentane, hexane,                  benzene, toluene, carbon tetrachloride, benzene,                  diethyl ether, hexane, methylene chloride, carbon                  dioxide, methane, ethylene, D-limonene, olive                  oil, soybean oil, coconut oil, medium chain                  triglycerides, methanol, ethanol, propylene                  glycol, polysorbates 20 and 80, poloxamer 188,                  chloroform, diethyl ether, and/or deuterated                  chloroform. The liquid carbon dioxide is added to                  the plant material and solvent mixture at ratio                  of greater than 1:1 co-solvent to mixture on a                  weight basis.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC D10-A01; J01-A; A12-V01; A12-W11

PT P
PN CN110563592-A; CN110563592-B
TI Preparation of dimethylamine involves reacting            hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and ammonia in presence of            core-shell-type catalyst including catalyst for            catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol,            and methanol amination catalyst.
AU GAO X
   CHEN G
   ZHAO T
   ZHANG J
   MA Q
   FAN S
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
   UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 2019A6664U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of dimethylamine involves reacting                hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and ammonia in a molar                ratio of 1-3:1:1-3, in presence of a                core-shell-type catalyst in a fixed bed reactor at                200-400 degrees C and 0.1-5 MPa. The reaction                catalyst comprises a catalyst (c1) for catalytic                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol, and                methanol amination catalyst (c2) for forming                methylamine.
   USE - Preparation of dimethylamine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of                dimethylamine with reduced energy                consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Catalyst (c1) is                  copper-based catalyst. Catalyst (c2) is a                  molecular sieve catalyst. The core-shell-type                  catalyst comprises the copper-based catalyst as a                  core, and the molecular sieve catalyst as a                  shell. The mass ratio of copper-based catalyst                  and molecular sieve catalyst is 0.25-4:1. The                  copper-based catalyst is of formula: Cu/MxOy. The                  molecular sieve catalyst is ZSM-5-type molecular                  sieve catalyst, SAPO-34-type molecular sieve                  catalyst, beta -type molecular sieve catalyst,                  MOR-type molecular sieve catalyst, and/or Y-type                  molecular sieve catalyst.MxOy = ZnO, Al2O3, Cr2O3, ZrO2, SiO2, or                  TiO2.Preferred Process: The copper-based catalyst                  is prepared by co-precipitation method involving                  preparing a salt solution (A) comprising copper                  and element (M) in molar ratio of 1:1-10, mixing,                  stirring for 10-60 minutes, preparing an aqueous                  solution (B) chosen from aqueous ammonia, sodium                  hydroxide aqueous solution and sodium bicarbonate                  aqueous solution, uniformly mixing solution (A)                  and solution (B) at 100-500 rpm, controlling pH                  to 8-14, stirring, precipitating, aging for 2-48                  hours, washing with deionized water, drying at                  60-120 degrees C for 5-24 hours, calcining at                  350-500 degrees C for 2-24 hours, milling, and                  sieving using 20-40 mesh sieve. The                  core-shell-type catalyst is obtained by mixing a                  silicon source, an aluminum source, a phosphorus                  source, and a template, stirring, forming a gel,                  obtaining molecular sieve, mixing 20-40                  mesh-sized copper-based catalyst and molecular                  sieve in molar ratio of 0.25-4:1, stirring at                  room temperature for 2-24 hours, transferring                  resultant to polytetrafluoroethylene-lined                  stainless steel reactor, crystallizing at 160-250                  degrees C and 1-5 rpm for 24-75 hours, performing                  centrifugal washing, drying, and calcining at                  500-600 degrees C for 4-6 hours.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E10-B04C2; E11-D02; E11-F07A; J04-E04B; A04-E08; A11-B04A; A12-W11; A12-W11K; N02-D01; N06-A; N06-B03; N06-E01; N06-F; N07-B02; N07-D08A

TI Hydrometallurgical multi-stage reaction and            separation system comprises raw material pre-washing            device, multi-stage solid-liquid separation device,            by-product preparation device and recovery water            circulation device.
AU ZHAO H
   ZHOU X
   HUANGJING Z
   XIAOZHOU Z
   ZHU Z X
   ZHAO H J
   ZHOU X Z
AE GREENORE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY (GREE-Non-standard)
   GREENORE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNO (GREE-Non-standard)
   GREENORE CLEANTECH SHANGHAI CO LTD (GREE-Non-standard)
   GREENORE CLEANTECH SHANGHAI CO LTD (GREE-Non-standard)
   GREENORE CLEANTECH SHANGHAI CO LTD (GREE-Non-standard)
   RON KUANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHN (RONK-Non-standard)
   RON KUANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY              SHANGHAI CO LT (RONK-Non-standard)
GA 2020837158
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrometallurgical multi-stage reaction and                separation system comprises raw material                pre-washing device which continuously inputs                industrial wastes or ore raw materials or tailings,                reagents and water into raw material pre-washing                device to fully mix into slurry, reaction device,                multi-stage solid-liquid separation device,                by-product preparation device and recovery water                circulation device where pre-washed raw material is                transferred to reaction device through power device                and continuously pump carbon dioxide into reaction                device under certain pressure. The multi-stage                solid-liquid separation device performs multi-stage                solid-liquid separation of the reacted slurry                continuously discharged through the reaction                device, where unreacted solid particles obtained                from the solid-liquid separation will be recycled                as raw material for next stage of reaction and                separation. The recovery water circulation device                is to recycles recovered water after formation of                by-product and passed to system.
   USE - Hydrometallurgical multi-stage reaction and                separation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can effectively realize continuous                reaction and processing capacity and provide the                possibility for multi-stage cyclic reaction, and                reduce the loss of carbon dioxide in the                discharging process under intermittent                reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrometallurgical multi-stage reaction and                separation system comprises raw material                pre-washing device which continuously inputs                industrial wastes or ore raw materials or tailings,                reagents and water into the raw material                pre-washing device to fully mix into slurry,                reaction device, multi-stage solid-liquid                separation device, by-product preparation device                and recovery water circulation device where                pre-washed raw material is transferred to reaction                device through power device and continuously pump                carbon dioxide into the reaction device under a                certain pressure. The multi-stage solid-liquid                separation device performs multi-stage solid-liquid                separation of the reacted slurry continuously                discharged through the reaction device, where                unreacted solid particles obtained from the                solid-liquid separation will be recycled as a raw                material for next stage of reaction and separation.                The by-product preparation device to prepare                calcium and magnesium products by continuously                input to the target calcium ion and magnesium ion                contained in clear separated liquid in by-product                preparation device. The recovery water circulation                device is to recycles recovered water after                formation of calcium and magnesium products and                passed to system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of multistage                reaction and separation system. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Raw material pre-washing device (10)
   Reaction apparatus (20)
   Multistage solid-liquid separation apparatus                (60)
   Product preparation device (70)
   Solid particles (M)
   Iron hydroxide (N)
EA    (JP2021191890-A)   NOVELTY - A multistage reaction and separation system                comprises a raw material pre-washing device (10), a                reaction apparatus (20), a multistage solid-liquid                separation apparatus (60), a product preparation                device (70), and a reclaimed water circulation                apparatus. The product preparation device is                provided for preparing a calcium-magnesium product                from target calcium ions and magnesium ions present                in the supernatant liquid continuously fed into the                product preparation device. The reclaimed water                circulation apparatus circulates the collected                water after producing a product from the                supernatant liquid to the system. The pre-washing                device is continuously charged with industrial                waste, ore raw material or slag, reagents, and                water, and mixed at a constant mixing ratio. The                slurry mixed by the pre-washing device is                continuously sent to the reaction apparatus by a                power device. In the reaction apparatus, carbon                dioxide is continuously bubbled at constant                pressure, mixed with the slurry, and reacted.
   USE - Multistage reaction and separation system for                hydrometallurgy based on carbon dioxide generated                from combustion exhaust gas of power plant, steel                mill blast furnace, converter, refining furnace,                lime kiln, coal chemical plant and petrochemical                plant (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient recovery of                product from steel slag, and recovery of carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount of                  industrial waste or ore raw materials or tailings                  used is not more than 10 %mass with respect to                  water and the amount of reagent used is not more                  than 5 %mass with respect to water. The usage                  amount of reagents used to remove impurities in                  separated liquid is not more than 1 %mass                  coagulants and/or flocculant. The amount of                  carbon dioxide used is 15-98%. Preferred                  Components: The solution used to adjust pH of                  separated liquid is alkaline or acidic reagents.                  The clear liquid comprises high iron element e.g.                  iron hydroxide. The calcium and magnesium                  products are calcium magnesium carbonate, calcium                  carbonate, magnesium carbonate, calcium                  hydroxide, magnesium hydroxide, calcium magnesium                  sulfate or calcium magnesium nitrate. The                  industrial waste comprises steel slag, iron slag,                  fly ash, bottom ash, red mud, construction                  waste/waste cement and tailings. The ore raw                  material or tailings comprise calcium magnesium                  ore. The reagent is at least one of acid, salt                  and composition of organic acid radical. The                  carbon dioxide comes from power plant flue gas,                  steel blast furnace, converter, refining furnace,                  lime kiln flue gas, coal chemical tail gas or                  petrochemical tail gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The acids of organic                  acid radical composition is one of oxalic acid,                  citric acid, picolinic acid, gluconic acid,                  glutamic acid, formic acid, acetic acid,                  propionic acid, butyric acid, valeric acid,                  lactic acid, succinic acid, phosphoric acid,                  pyrophosphate, ascorbic acid or phthalic                  acid.
DC P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

PT P
PN JP2022127000-A
TI Carbon dioxide separation apparatus has separation            gas discharge control valve that is alternately carried            out, and second separation gas is provided as carbon            dioxide-concentrated gas that is discharged from            discharge flow passage.
AU KUSUYAMA T
   TAGUCHI K
   SUZUKI
AE PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
GA 2022B5906W
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has a desorption device                which desorbs the carbon dioxide adsorbed to the                adsorbent, and an exhaust gas flow passage (103)                connected to the adsorber to exhaust the gas not                adsorbed to the adsorber. A first separation gas                discharge control valve is switched to discharge                the first separation gas to the first separation                gas discharge flow passage alternately. An exhaust                gas control valve (132) is provided with an exhaust                gas control valve provided in the exhaust gas                passage. A second separation gas discharge control                valve is alternately carried out. The second                separation gas is used as the carbon                dioxide-concentrated gas that is discharged from                the carbon dioxide-enriched gas discharge flow                passage.
   USE - Carbon dioxide separation apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus can reconcile a high                concentration carbon dioxide concentration gas with                low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The control device includes a desorption gas                control valve that is provided in the desorption                gas reflux flow passage and adjusting the supply                amount of the desorption gas to the raw material                gas supply passage. An exhaust gas control valve is                provided in the exhaust gas passage and adjusts the                discharge amount of the exhaust gas from adsorbers.                The confluence point of the desorbed gas reflux                flow path and the source gas supply channel is                located downstream of the second separator located                at the most upstream of the at least one second                separator, and upstream of the separator. The                adsorbent adsorbs the supplied carbon dioxide in                any form of an amine compound, a carbonate, and a                bicarbonate. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                operation method of the carbon dioxide separation                apparatus, involves circulating the gas which is                not adsorbed by the adsorber to the exhaust gas                flow path is performed as the exhaust gas. The                first separation gas discharge control valve is                changed over to discharge the first separation gas                to the first separation gas discharge passage, and                the adsorption process and the desorption process                are repeated, and the second separation gas is                generated during the desorption process. The                control valve is provided in the raw material gas                supply flow path and the exhaust gas flow path                corresponding to the other adsorber is closed. The                control valve provided in the desorption gas reflux                passage is opened, and the detachment device is                operated to execute a desorption process for                desorbing the carbon dioxide adsorbed in the                adsorbent of the separate adsorber to flow into the                desorption gas reflux passage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                configuration of a carbon dioxide separator.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   100Carbon dioxide separation apparatus
   101Raw material gas supply channel
   102Separator
   103Exhaust gas flow passage
   132Exhaust gas control valve

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN EP4063320-A1; WO2022200127-A1
TI Thermoprocessing non-gaseous charge in furnace            involves e.g. introducing non-gaseous charge to            thermoprocessed into the furnace, heating by fuel with            oxidant combustion, generating atmosphere, and            subjecting to rWGS reaction with hydrogen.
AU JARRY L
   GRAEBNER M
   CAUDAL J
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2022C4490E
AB    NOVELTY - Thermoprocessing a non-gaseous charge in a                furnace comprises introducing a non-gaseous charge                to be thermoprocessed into the furnace, heating by                means of combustion of fuel with oxidant, supplying                the fuel and oxidant to the furnace at regulated                supply rates, generating atmosphere in the furnace,                then supplying hydrogen to the process and a                non-zero fraction of the flue gas containing at                least 50 vol.% and at most 100 vol.% carbon dioxide                combined with at least part of the supplied                hydrogen and subjecting to a rWGS reaction with the                hydrogen in a rWGS reactor, where at least part of                the carbon dioxide in the fraction is converted                into carbon monoxide, and the reaction product of                the rWGS reaction is supplied to the furnace as                part or all of the fuel to be combusted.
   USE - The method is useful for thermoprocessing a                non-gaseous charge in a furnace heated by                combustion of fuel with oxidant.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of electric heating to complement the                heat generated by combustion increases the                flexibility of the glass-melting furnace and makes                it easier to adjust the heating of the furnace to                changes in the composition of the glass-forming                charge and/or to changes of the glass-production                rate (pull rate), while maintaining the desired                quality of the molten glass. By combining the                heating of the furnace by the combustion of the                rWGS reaction product with other sources of thermal                energy, such as electric heating or the combustion                of an additional fuel, possibly both, the                flexibility of the process is increased in that it                is thereby made possible to meet the (varying) heat                requirement of the furnace independently of the                level of CO 2 release by the charge in the furnace.                A similar advantageous effect is obtained when, due                to the preheating of oxidant and/or fuel and/or the                drying and/or preheating of the glass-forming                material, the need to supply heat to the furnace by                means of combustion of fuel with oxidant is                reduced. According to an advantageous embodiment,                the process comprises the step of automatically                regulating the amount of additional fuel which is                injected into the furnace in addition to the                reaction product of the rWGS reaction so that the                instantaneous heat requirement of the furnace is                met.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Thermoprocessing a non-gaseous charge in a                furnace comprises introducing a non-gaseous charge                to be thermoprocessed into the furnace, heating the                furnace by means of combustion of fuel with                oxidant, supplying the fuel and oxidant to the                furnace at regulated supply rates, generating                atmosphere in the furnace, where the atmosphere                comprising gaseous mixture comprising (a)                combustion gas generated by the combustion of the                fuel with the oxidant and (b) carbon dioxide                released by the charge during thermoprocessing, the                thermoprocessed charge is withdrawn from the                furnace and the gaseous mixture is evacuated as                flue gas from the furnace, then supplying hydrogen                to the process and a non-zero fraction of the flue                gas containing at least 50 vol.% and at most 100                vol.% carbon dioxide combined with at least part of                the supplied hydrogen and subjecting to a rWGS                reaction with the hydrogen in a rWGS reactor, where                at least part of the carbon dioxide in the fraction                is converted into carbon monoxide, and the reaction                product of the rWGS reaction is supplied to the                furnace as part or all of the fuel to be                combusted.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fraction of the                  flue gas comprises at least 80 vol.%, preferably                  at least 98 vol.% carbon monoxide and/or 0-20                  vol.% water, preferably at most 10 vol.% water.                  The oxidant has an oxygen content of 70-100                  vol.%, preferably at least 95 vol.%. The fuel is                  combusted with the oxidant in multiple flames                  and/or by staged combustion. Preferred Process:                  The fraction of the flue gas is a dehumidified                  flue-gas fraction, preferably obtained by                  removing water therefrom by condensation. The                  heat is recovered from the evacuated flue gas and                  supplied to the rWGS reaction. In the process:                  heat recovered from the evacuated flue gas is                  supplied to the rWGS reaction by using the                  recovered heat to heat the fraction of the flue                  gas upstream of the rWGS reactor and/or to heat                  the at least part of the supplied hydrogen                  upstream of the rWGS reactor and/or to heat the                  combined fraction of the flue gas and the at                  least part of the supplied hydrogen upstream of                  the rWGS reactor and/or to heat the rWGS reactor,                  preferably to heat the fraction of the flue gas                  upstream of the rWGS reactor and/or to heat the                  combined fraction of the flue gas and the at                  least part of the supplied hydrogen upstream of                  the rWGS reactor and/or to heat the rWGS reactor,                  preferably to heat the fraction of the flue gas                  upstream of the rWGS reactor and/or to heat the                  rWGS reactor; heat is recovered from the                  evacuated flue gas and used for oxidant                  preheating and/or, fuel preheating and/or drying                  and/or preheating of the charge to be                  thermoprocessed; a further part of the hydrogen                  supplied to the process is injected into the                  furnace as additional fuel admixed with and/or                  separately from the reaction product of the rWGS                  reaction; a gaseous hydrocarbon-containing fuel                  is supplied to the process and injected into the                  furnace as additional fuel admixed with and/or                  separately from the reaction product of the rWGS                  reaction, the gaseous hydrocarbon-containing fuel                  being preferably sourced from a renewable source;                  the amount of additional fuel injected into the                  furnace is automatically regulated so that an                  instantaneous combustion heat requirement of the                  furnace is met by the combustion of the reaction                  product of the rWGS reaction together with the                  combustion of the injected additional fuel; the                  process is a glass-melting process and solid                  glass-forming material to be melted is introduced                  into the furnace as the non-gaseous charge to be                  thermoprocessed, carbon dioxide is released into                  the furnace atmosphere by the glass-forming                  material during thermoprocessing, and the molten                  glass is withdrawn from the furnace as the                  thermoprocessed charge; the furnace is heated                  simultaneously by means of (a) the combustion of                  fuel with oxidant and (b) electrodes immersed in                  the glass-forming material; 5-50 wt.%, preferably                  20-50 wt.% of the energy supplied to the furnace                  is supplied to the furnace by means of the                  electrodes; and 30-95, preferably 50-95 wt.% of                  the energy supplied to the furnace is supplied to                  the furnace by means of the electrodes.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); L01 (Glass - includes chemical compositions, batch treatment, furnaces, flat glass forming, hollow-ware forming, postforming and glass/ceramics, but not lens designs, bottling, bottle-washing, closures for containers, glazing designs, glass cutting, chamfering edges, printing on glass, disposing of used glass or the production of pure sodium silicate. Chemical aspects of optical fibres (C03).); Q77 (Furnaces, kilns, ovens, retorts (F27))
MC E31-N05B1; L01-C02; Q77-A99

TI Solar thermal power co-supply system based on energy storage across time scales has Kano battery power generation circuit with Kano battery boiler, heat recovery device, Kano battery condensate pipe, Kano battery circulating water pump, boiler inlet pipe, Kano battery boiler connected in sequence.
AU XI Y
   DUAN X
   WANG Y
   QU D
AE UNIV LANZHOU TECHNOLOGY (UYLZ-C)
GA 2022113782
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide circulation loop, a large temperature difference cogeneration Rankine cycle loop, a buried pipe cross-season heat storage loop, a thermal power station heating loop, a Kano battery molten salt circulation loop, a low temperature molten salt heat recovery loop and a Kano battery power generation loop. The Kano battery power generation circuit consists of a Kano battery boiler (31), a turbine air intake pipe (W9), a Kano battery turbine (35), a coupling (36), a generator (37), an output circuit (E3), a turbine exhaust pipe (W10), a heat recovery device (38), a Kano battery condensate pipe (W11), a Kano battery circulating water pump (39), a boiler inlet pipe (W12) and a Kano battery boiler (31) connected in sequence.
   USE - Solar thermal power co-supply system based on energy storage across time scales.
   ADVANTAGE - The solar thermal power generation efficiency is improved. The efficiency of heating system, and individuation adjusting freedom degree are improved. The flexibility and reliability of the system, are increased. The solar thermoelectric cooperative supply system is provided based on cross-time scale energy storage.

PN CN112978796-A; CN112978796-B
TI Preparing vanadium pentoxide from sodium vanadate            solution by performing vanadium precipitation and            calcination using sodium vanadate solution,            electrocausticizing process, carbon alkalization,            ammonization-carbonization and calcination.
AU ZHANG T
   LV G
   DOU Z
   LIU Y
   ZHANG W
   ZHAO Q
   ZHANG Z
   FU D
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 202171889Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cleanly preparing vanadium                pentoxide from a sodium vanadate solution involves                (1) performing vanadium precipitation and                calcination process by adjusting acid of the sodium                vanadate solution according to the molar ratio of                ammonium cation and tetrametavanadate of (1-2.5):1,                adding 50-300 g/l ammonium sulfate solution for                mixing, heating at 70-95 degrees C, maintaining                stirring speed of 200-500 rpm at 70-95 degrees C,                precipitating vanadium for 1-2 hours, precipitating                and filtering to obtain ammonium polyvanadate,                where the precipitated solution is sodium sulfate                solution and the obtained ammonium polyvanadate                undergoes a calcination process to prepare vanadium                pentoxide with a content of not less than 98.7% and                generating ammonia gas, where the calcination                temperature is 400-800 degrees C and the                calcination time is 2-4 hours, (2) performing                electrocausticizing process, (3) performing carbon                alkalization, (4) performing                ammonization-carbonization and (5) performing                calcium carbonate calcination.
   USE - The method is useful for cleanly preparing                vanadium pentoxide from a sodium vanadate                solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the recycling and zero                discharge of sodium, ammonium, carbon, calcium and                water in the process of preparing vanadium                pentoxide using sodium vanadate and is a clean                production method for vanadium pentoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for cleanly preparing vanadium                pentoxide from a sodium vanadate solution involves                (1) performing vanadium precipitation and                calcination process by adjusting acid of the sodium                vanadate solution according to the molar ratio of                ammonium cation and tetrametavanadate of (1-2.5):1,                adding 50-300 g/l ammonium sulfate solution for                mixing, heating at 70-95 degrees C, maintaining                stirring speed of 200-500 rpm at 70-95 degrees C,                precipitating vanadium for 1-2 hours, precipitating                and filtering to obtain ammonium polyvanadate,                where the precipitated solution is sodium sulfate                solution and the obtained ammonium polyvanadate                undergoes a calcination process to prepare vanadium                pentoxide with a content of not not less than 98.7%                and generating ammonia gas, where the calcination                temperature is 400-800 degrees C and the                calcination time is 2-4 hours, (2) performing                electrocausticizing process involves adding calcium                oxide to the sodium sulfate solution and obtaining                sodium hydroxide solution and gypsum calcium                sulfate through the electrocausticizing process,                where the molar ratio of calcium and sulfate is                (1-1.5):1, the reaction temperature is 10-90                degrees C and the stirring speed is 100-500 rpm,                (3) performing carbon alkalization process by                introducing carbon dioxide into the obtained sodium                hydroxide solution and preparing a sodium carbonate                solution through the carbon alkalization process,                (4) performing ammonization-carbonization process                by using ammonia gas generated in the calcination                process, calcium sulfate and carbon dioxide                generated in the electrocausticizing process and                performing ammonium-carbonization treatment to                obtain ammonium sulfate solution and calcium                carbonate and returning the ammonium sulfate                solution to step (1) for ammonium salt                precipitation, where the molar ratio of carbon                dioxide/ammonia/calcium sulfate is (1-2):2:1, the                reaction temperature is 20-90 degrees C, the                reaction time is 0.5-3 hours and the stirring speed                is 200-500 rpm and (5) performing calcium carbonate                calcination process by calcining the obtained                calcium carbonate to obtain calcium oxide and                carbon dioxide, where the calcium oxide returns to                the step (2) for causticization reaction; a part of                the carbon dioxide returns to the step (3) for a                carbon alkalization reaction and the other part                returns to the step 4 for ammonium-carbonization                treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (1) involves                  adjusting the pH value of the sodium vanadate                  solution to 1.8-2.5 by sulfuric acid. The step                  (3) involves introducing carbon dioxide generated                  by the calcination of calcium carbonate into the                  sodium hydroxide solution obtained by                  causticization, obtaining the sodium carbonate                  solution by reaction at 20-80 degrees C, and                  obtaining sodium carbonate solid after                  crystallization, which is used for the sodium                  calcination of vanadium slag.

PN CN112903395-A
TI In situ slide glass culturing of villi tissue cells comprises taking sample of villous tissue, cutting villi tissue sample into small pieces, centrifuging digested sample, adding fetal bovine serum medium to in-situ culture box, culturing.
AU SHEN X
   YANG X
   TANG K
AE HUZHOU MATERNITY & CHILD CARE HOSPITAL (HUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202166996J
AB    NOVELTY - In situ slide glass culturing of villi tissue cells comprises (a) pre-treating by taking sample of villous tissue under aseptic conditions, washing, adding double antibody solution after removing other tissues, incubating in carbon dioxide incubator, (b) digesting by cutting villi tissue sample into small pieces, centrifuging and discarding supernatant, adding fetal bovine serum medium to wash, centrifuging and discarding supernatant and adding tissue digestion solution, (c) inoculating by centrifuging digested sample and discarding supernatant, adding fetal bovine serum medium to wash, centrifuging and discarding supernatant, adding fetal bovine serum medium, uniformly mixing to obtain suspension, absorbing the suspension and adding to in-situ culture box, placing in-situ culture box flat on carbon dioxide incubator for pre-culturing and (d) culturing by adding fetal bovine serum medium to in-situ culture box after pre-culture, changing medium, changing medium again and culturing.
   USE - The method is useful for in situ slide glass culturing of villi tissue cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: harvests cells without trypsin digestion or scraper transfer, which simplifies the operation and avoids some chromosome loss and cell loss caused by experimental operations; and provides high-quality raw materials for subsequent chromosome preparation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - In situ slide glass culturing of villi tissue cells comprises (a) pre-treating by taking sample of villous tissue under aseptic conditions, washing until there is no visible blood red, adding double antibody solution after removing other tissues, incubating in carbon dioxide incubator at 36-38 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours, (b) digesting by cutting villi tissue sample into small pieces, centrifuging and discarding supernatant, adding fetal bovine serum medium to wash, centrifuging and discarding supernatant, adding tissue digestion solution, incubating in carbon dioxide incubator at 36-38 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours, (c) inoculating by centrifuging digested sample and discarding supernatant, adding fetal bovine serum medium to wash, centrifuging and discarding supernatant, adding fetal bovine serum medium, uniformly mixing to obtain suspension, absorbing the suspension and adding to in-situ culture box, placing in-situ culture box flat on the carbon dioxide incubator at 36-38 degrees C for pre-culturing and (d) culturing by adding fetal bovine serum medium to the in-situ culture box after pre-culture for 20-25 hours, changing medium after culturing for 20-25 hours, changing medium again after culturing for 20-25 hours and culturing for 20-25 hours to obtain villous cells slides. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing chromosomes of villi tissue cells comprising (a) preparing slide glass with villi cells based on in situ slide culture method of villi tissue cells, (b) adding colchicine solution to the in-situ culture box equipped with the glass slide of step (a), mixing with medium in the box, allowing to stand at 36-38 degrees C for 25-35 minutes, (c) removing liquid in in-situ culture box, adding hypotonic liquid, allowing to stand at room temperature for 25-30 minutes, (d) removing liquid in the in-situ culture box, adding pre-fixed solution, allowing to stand for 5-10 minutes, (e) removing liquid in the in-situ culture box, adding fixing solution to fix, removing liquid in the in-situ culture box and repeating fixation three times, (f) taking out slides and placing in the chromosome disperser for dispersion, (g) baking slides at 85-95 degrees C for 2.5-3.5 hours, allowing to stand at 36-38 degrees C for overnight and (h) using G banding staining method to stain the chromosomes of the cells on the slide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In step (a), the villi tissue sample is a villi branch sample of at least 10 mg. In step (b), the tissue digestion solution is trypsin solution or collagenase II solution. The glass slide includes a gripping area and a culture area, where the surface of the culture area is subjected to siliconization treatment, polylysine treatment or collagen treatment. The surface of the culture area is smooth. The hypotonic solution is a trisodium citrate solution. The pre-fixing solution is glacial acetic acid, and the fixing solution is a methanol-glacial acetic acid solution. Preferred Method: In the step (a), villi tissue sample is washed with sterile saline. In the step (e), repeating fixation for three times, performing first fixation, where static time after adding the fixative should not exceed 1 minute, performing second and third fixation, adding fixation and allowing to stand for 10-20 minutes. In the step (f), dispersing conditions set by the chromosome disperser is temperature of 23-26 degrees C and humidity of 48-53%.

PT P
PN CN112798589-A
TI Estimating the rate of carbon dioxide assimilation in soybean canopy involves using the field canopy slice method to measure the photosynthetically active radiation intensity in the canopy.
AU MA C
   GONG Z
   YAN C
   DONG S
   LV X
   LI J
   WANG C
AE UNIV NORTHEAST AGRIC (UNEA-C)
GA 202156581M
AB    NOVELTY - Estimating the rate of carbon dioxide (CO2) assimilation in soybean canopy involves using the field canopy slice method to measure the photosynthetically active radiation intensity in the canopy based on a continuous automatic counting photosynthetically active radiation recorder. The placement positions of the sensor probes includes 0.15 m above the canopy, the growth point, the middle of the stem and the cotyledon mark. The predicted soybean potted plant is put in the base, and then set up a gas collection box above the base, and connected the air outlet, air pump, dryer, and air outlet of the gas collection box through a hose. The CO2 gas analyzer is connected with the air inlet of the gas collection box to form a closed gas circulation system, in which the lower end of the gas collection box is inserted into the water tank of the base to realize the sealing of the base. The natural light used as a control.
   USE - The method is useful for estimating assimilation carbon dioxide speed of soybean crown layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables estimating the soybean crown layer assimilation carbon dioxide speed in a simple and accurate manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Estimating the rate of carbon dioxide (CO2) assimilation in soybean canopy involves using the field canopy slice method to measure the photosynthetically active radiation intensity in the canopy based on a continuous automatic counting photosynthetically active radiation recorder. The placement positions of the sensor probes includes 0.15 m above the canopy, the growth point, the middle of the stem and the cotyledon mark. The predicted soybean potted plant is put in the base, and then set up a gas collection box above the base, and connected the air outlet, air pump, dryer, and air outlet of the gas collection box through a hose. The CO2 gas analyzer is connected with the air inlet of the gas collection box to form a closed gas circulation system, in which the lower end of the gas collection box is inserted into the water tank of the base to realize the sealing of the base. The natural light used as a control, used a CO2 analyzer to measure the rate of absorption of CO2 by soybean plants, and after 30 seconds, read the CO2 concentration in the gas collection box as the initial value of the measurement. The CO2 concentration is read in the box every 3 minutes, measured for 12 minutes, measured the leaf area at the same time, and calculated the soybean photosynthetic rate. The field canopy sectioning method, the biomass of the upper, middle and lower stems, leaves, and stalks are used to classified and collected together, and selected representative fresh leaves to punch holes with a puncher, used the punch method to measure the leaf area of each layer, and calculated the leaf area index. A fitting equation of soybean net photosynthetic rate (Pn) and photosynthetically active radiation (PAR) are constructed, and use the average photosynthetically active radiation above the canopy and the growth point as the average photosynthetic active radiation in the upper canopy. The middle layer is the average photosynthetically active radiation at the growth point and the middle of the stalk, and the lower layer is the average photosynthetically active radiation at the middle of the stalk and the cotyledon mark. The average photosynthetic active radiation of the upper, middle, and lower layers are substituted into the fitting equation relationship, the average net photosynthetic rate of soybean canopy is obtained. The average net photosynthetic rate in the soybean canopy multiplied with the leaf area index to obtain the CO2 assimilation rate per unit ground area of the canopy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The base is made of stainless steel and has a closed bottom. The gas collection box is made of transparent organic glass and has a rectangular parallelepiped structure.

PN CN109111935-A; CN109111935-B
TI Oil shale circulating fluidized bed dry            distillation comprises activating a fluidized gas            containing a methane-rich gas mixture and carbon            dioxide, and performing dry distillation.
AU SONG L
   GE X
   WANG S
   WANG M
AE UNIV SHANGHAI ENG & TECHNOLOGY (USES-C)
GA 201903943D
AB    NOVELTY - Oil shale circulating fluidized bed dry                distillation comprises activating a fluidized gas                containing a methane-rich gas mixture and carbon                dioxide by the catalyst layer, entering into a                retorting chamber of the circulating fluidized bed                reactor, the oil shale particles are in the                atmosphere, the fluidized dry distillation is                carried out in the retorting chamber under an                activated mixed gas atmosphere, discharging a                generated semi-coke portion from the bottom of the                retorting chamber, the gas stream carries the                remaining semi-coke, other dry distillation                products other than semi-coke, discharging the                effluent shale and catalyst from the upper portion                of the dilute dense phase region of the retorting                chamber, separating the gas-solid, semi-coke,                un-dry distillate shale and catalyst are returning                to lower dense phase region of the dry distillation                chamber, the other dry condensing distillation                products other than the semi-coke, separating by                oil and water to obtain shale oil and water.
   USE - The method is useful for oil shale circulating                fluidized bed dry distillation.
   ADVANTAGE - The activating gas mixture of methane-rich gas                mixture and carbon dioxide can be fully connected                with oil shale, which is beneficial to the                combination of activated molecules and oil shale                retorting radicals, improves oil yield, reduces                hydrogenation pyrolysis cost and greenhouse gas                emissions, and improves the environment. The                catalyst is easy to recycle and reuse, easy to                operate and easy to produce.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Oil shale circulating fluidized bed dry                distillation comprises (i) activating a fluidized                gas containing a methane-rich gas mixture and                carbon dioxide by the catalyst layer and then                entering into a retorting chamber of the                circulating fluidized bed reactor, the oil shale                particles are in the atmosphere of an activated                mixture of methane-rich gas mixture and carbon                dioxide, the fluidized dry distillation is carried                out in the retorting chamber of the circulating                fluidized bed reactor under an activated mixed gas                atmosphere, (ii) discharging the generated                semi-coke portion from the bottom of the retorting                chamber and collecting, the gas stream carries the                remaining semi-coke, other dry distillation                products other than semi-coke, discharging the                effluent shale and catalyst from the upper portion                of the dilute dense phase region of the retorting                chamber, and (iii) after separating the gas-solid,                semi-coke, un-dry distillate shale and catalyst are                returning to the lower dense phase region of the                dry distillation chamber, and the other dry                distillation products other than the semi-coke are                condensed and separating by oil and water to obtain                shale oil and water, where the structure of                circulating fluidized bed reactor includes a dry                distillation chamber and a heat exchange gas                chamber arranged from top to bottom, a catalyst                layer is set on a junction of the bottom of the dry                distillation chamber and the heat exchange gas                chamber, and dry distillation chamber includes an                upper dilute phase region and a lower dense phase                region. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system for oil shale circulating fluidized bed dry                distillation, comprising a circulating fluidized                bed reactor (3), a gas-solid separator (8), a                condenser (10) and an oil-water separator (11). The                circulating fluidized bed reactor is provided with                a dry distillation chamber (32).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the oil shale circulating fluidized bed dry                distillation system. (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
   Circulating fluidized bed reactor (3)
   Gas-solid separator (8)
   Condenser (10)
   Oil-water separator (11)
   Dry distillation chamber (32)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i) the                  methane-rich gas mixture comprises natural gas,                  oil shale pyrolysis gas or shale gas. The                  oxidizing gas comprises a mixture of carbon                  dioxide and oxygen, water vapor and air. The                  catalyst in the catalyst layer comprises a load                  type catalyst for activating the methane,                  comprising a load transition metal, an alkaline                  earth metal or alkali-metal load type catalyst,                  where the carrier comprises aluminum oxide,                  titanium dioxide, silicon dioxide, magnesium                  oxide and calcium oxide. The system further                  comprises a silo and a spiral feeder. The silo                  passing through the spiral feeder is connected to                  the dense phase zone of the retorting chamber.                  Preferred method: In step (i) continuously                  passing the oil shale particles into the dense                  phase region of the retorting chamber in the                  circulating fluidized bed reactor under the                  action of screw feeder. The fluidized dry                  distillation is carried out at 400-600 degrees C.                  The methane-rich gas mixture and carbon dioxide                  having the volume ratio of 1:0.5-1.5, adding                  fluidizing gas to a oxidizing gas having a volume                  ratio of 1:0.3-1. In step (ii) passing the                  generated semi-coke portion through a semi-coke                  discharge pipe, set on the bottom of the                  retorting chamber, after heat exchange with the                  catalyst layer and the gas entering the heat                  exchange gas chamber from top to bottom,                  discharging and collecting.

PN CN112827501-A
TI Supported catalyst used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol comprises alpha-molybdenum carbide as carrier and indium as active component.
AU SHI C
   GUO X
   LIU Y
   WANG J
   ZHANG G
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202159754D
AB    NOVELTY - Supported catalyst comprises alpha -molybdenum carbide as carrier and indium as active component.
   USE - The supported catalyst is used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent catalytic performance in the reaction of pressurized carbon dioxide hydrogenation to methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass fraction of indium is 3-15%. The catalyst further comprises 1-10 wt.% oxide promoter and/or 0.1-5 wt.% metal promoter. The oxide promoter is zirconium dioxide. The metal assistant is platinum and/or cobalt. Preferred Method: The preparation of catalyst calcining metal precursor of molybdenum at 400-700 degrees C for 2-6 hours to obtain molybdenum trioxide particles, nitriding molybdenum trioxide at 590-800 degrees C in pure ammonia atmosphere, cooling to room temperature, carbonizing at 590-800 degrees C in mixed gas of methane and hydrogen to obtain catalyst support and impregnating indium nitrate solution on catalyst support.

AB    NOVELTY - Recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide has carbonic anhydrase activity, and comprises an amino acid sequence having at least 60% identity with amino acid (SEQ ID NO: 5), not given in the specification, and one or more amino acid differences as compared to amino acid (SEQ ID NO: 1), not given in the specification at residue positions selected from 3, 6, 11, 15, 17, 20, 24, 25, 38, 39, 48, 64, 79, 88, 119, 128, 130, 137, 145, 148, 149, 154, 160, 166, 168, 195, 199, 203, 210, and 223. The recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide has increased solubility and/or increased thermostability as compared to a corresponding carbonic anhydrase polypeptide lacking one or more amino acid differences.
   USE - Recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide used in an industrial process for capturing carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide-containing effluent or gas, and used in stock or feed solution at a concentration of 5-12g/L (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide has improved solubility and/or thermostability for enzyme-enhanced CO2, exhibits greater stability at gas-liquid interphase by reducing affinity for interface which is hydrophobic, facilitates solubilization of dried or lyophilized enzyme, minimizes enzyme loss due to aggregation, prepares highly concentrated feed solution, enables a more concentrated stock solution to be shipped from enzyme supplier, and reduces shipping cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an isolated polynucleotide, which encodes the recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide;
   (2) an expression or cloning vector, which comprises the isolated polynucleotide;
   (3) a host cell, which comprises isolated polynucleotide or expression vector;
   (4) a method for producing a recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide, which involves culturing host cell under conditions enabling expression of recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide, and recovering recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide; and
   (5) a method for absorbing carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide-containing effluent or gas, which involves contacting carbon dioxide-containing effluent or gas with an aqueous absorption solution to dissolve carbon dioxide into aqueous absorption solution; and providing recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide to catalyze hydration reaction of dissolved carbon dioxide into bicarbonate and hydrogen ions or the reverse reaction.

The recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide has increased solubility and/or increased thermostability as compared to corresponding carbonic anhydrase polypeptide in an alkaline carbonate solution such as a solution ranging from 1.38-1.85M potassium carbonate with alpha varying from 0.60-0.89. The recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide has increased solubility as compared to corresponding carbonic anhydrase polypeptide after a 24-hour exposure at 22 degrees C or 70 degrees C in an alkaline carbonate solution, such as a solution ranging from 1.38-1.85M potassium carbonate with alpha varying from 0.60-0.89. The recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide has increased solubility as compared to corresponding carbonic anhydrase polypeptide after a 24-hour exposure at 80 degrees C in an alkaline carbonate solution, such as a solution ranging from 1.38-1.85M potassium carbonate with alpha varying from 0.60-0.89. The polypeptide has increased solubility as compared to corresponding carbonic anhydrase polypeptide as determined by titration solubility testing comprising measuring turbidity of 2g/L of recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide in solutions ranging from 1.38-1.85M potassium carbonate with alpha varying from 0.60-0.89. The polypeptide has a solubility of greater than 2.5-12g/L after 24 hours at 80 degrees C in an alkaline carbonate solution, such as a solution ranging from 1.38-1.85M potassium carbonate with alpha varying from 0.60-0.89. The polypeptide has increased thermostability as compared to corresponding carbonic anhydrase polypeptide after a 72-hour exposure at 85 degrees C in an alkaline carbonate solution, such as a solution ranging from 1.38-1.85M potassium carbonate with alpha varying from 0.60-0.89, or after a 16-hour exposure at 95 degrees C in an alkaline carbonate solution, such as a solution ranging from 1.38-1.85M potassium carbonate with alpha varying from 0.60-0.89. The polypeptide comprises an amino acid sequence having at least 61-98%, or 99% identity with amino acid (SEQ ID NOS: 1-5). The polypeptide having carbonic anhydrase activity comprises an amino acid sequence having at least 60% identity with amino acid (SEQ ID NO: 5). The isolated polynucleotide is operably linked to a heterologous promoter. The host cell is a bacterial cell, yeast cell, or fungal cell. The method involves exposing recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide to an aqueous absorption solution, temperature, and/or pH conditions that leverage their improved solubility and/or thermostability that results in a decrease in rate of recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide consumption or depletion as compared to a corresponding process performed with carbonic anhydrase poly-peptide having amino acid (SEQ ID NOS: 1 or 2). The decrease in rate of recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide consumption results from a decrease in effective concentration of recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide required to achieve a target level of carbon dioxide capture as compared to a corresponding process performed with carbonic anhydrase polypeptide (SEQ ID NO: 1 or 2), a decrease in the rate of active recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide lost due to aggregation and/or thermal instability as compared to a corresponding process performed with carbonic anhydrase polypeptide (SEQ ID NOS: 1 or 2), or both. The absorption solution comprises an absorption compound which is a carbonate compound at concentration from 0.1-3M, or 1.25-1.75M. The method involves exposing recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide to a temperature of 10-98 degrees C or 99 degrees C at point during process. The carbon dioxide-containing effluent or gas comprises between 0.04-80 vol.% of carbon dioxide, comprises N2, O2, noble gases, VOCs, H2O, CO, SOx, NOx compounds, NH3, mercaptans, H2S, H2, heavy metals, dusts, ashes, or its combination, and is derived from natural gas combustion, coal combustion, biogas combustion, biogas upgrading, or natural gas sweetening, or its combination. The recombinant carbonic anhydrase polypeptide is exposed to a pH from 8-11, 8.5-11, 9-10.5, or 9-10 at point during process. The concentration of carbonic anhydrase polypeptide in absorption solution is 0.01-50g/L. PT P
PN CN111472056-A; CN111472056-B
TI Preparing adsorption membrane for purifying carbon dioxide in exhaust gas, involves adding polyethersulfone to dimethylformamide, stirring until completely dissolved, adding 2,3-epoxypropyl trimethylammonium chloride.
AU JIANG T
AE CHENGDU QIQIXIAOSHU TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI KENER NEW MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202074503W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing adsorption membrane involves adding polyethersulfone to dimethylformamide, stirring until completely dissolved, and then adding 2,3-epoxypropyl trimethylammonium chloride, reacting at 60-70 degrees C for 12-15 hours, cooling to room temperature, precipitating precipitate with acetone, washing with ethanol, drying, vacuuming to obtain quaternary aminated polyethersulfone. The quaternized polyethersulfone is dissolved in dimethylformamide to obtain solution A. The polyamic acid is dissolved in dimethylformamide to obtain solution B. The solution A, solution B, light absorber, and absorption enhancer are mixed to obtain spinning solution, then electrospinned to obtain quaternized polyethersulfone/polyamic acid composite nanofiber membrane, then heated, cured to 280-300 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain quaternized polyethersulfone/polyimide composite nanofiber membrane.
   USE - Method for preparing adsorption membrane for purifying carbon dioxide in exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare adsorption membrane that has low regeneration cost, good carbon dioxide adsorption capacity, and promotes the adsorption of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing adsorption membrane for purifying carbon dioxide in exhaust gas, involves adding polyethersulfone to dimethylformamide, stirring until completely dissolved, and then adding 2,3-epoxypropyl trimethylammonium chloride, reacting at 60-70 degrees C for 12-15 hours, cooling to room temperature, precipitating precipitate with acetone, washing with ethanol, drying, vacuuming to obtain quaternary aminated polyethersulfone. The quaternized polyethersulfone is dissolved in dimethylformamide to obtain solution A. The polyamic acid is dissolved in dimethylformamide to obtain solution B. The solution A, solution B, light absorber, and absorption enhancer are mixed to obtain spinning solution, then electrospinned to obtain quaternized polyethersulfone/polyamic acid composite nanofiber membrane, then heated, cured to 280-300 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain quaternized polyethersulfone/polyimide composite nanofiber membrane. The composite nanofiber membrane is alkali washed with sodium acetate solution to replace chloride ions with acetate ions to obtain adsorption membrane for purifying carbon dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for regenerating adsorption membrane for purifying carbon dioxide in exhaust gas, which involves evacuating closed transparent container, placing fiber membrane after adsorbing carbon dioxide in the container, placing under sunlight until detecting that carbon dioxide concentration in container no longer rises.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of polyethersulfone, 2,3-epoxypropyltrimethylammonium chloride, and dimethylformamide is 25:8-12:100. The mass ratio of quaternized polyethersulfone and dimethylformamide is 14-18:100. The mass ratio of polyamic acid and dimethylformamide is 12-16:100. The light absorber comprises cobalt sulfide, lead sulfide, and ferroferric oxide in mass ratio of 2:2:1. The absorption enhancer comprises 1-(4-chlorophenyl)-2-cyclopropyl ethyl ketone and sodium vinyl sulfonate in mass ratio of 2:1. The mass ratio of solution A, solution B, light absorber, and absorption enhancer is 30-35:60:5-10:0.5-1. The concentration of the sodium acetate solution is 1 mol/L.
