TI Adsorption material for e.g., abating carbon            dioxide from flue gas comprises metal-organic framework            containing metal ions and polytopic organic linkers,            and ligands which are amine appended to metal ion of            metal-organic framework.
AU LONG J R
   WESTON S C
   MILNER P J
   MARTELL J D
   SIEGELMAN R L
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 201913899P
AB    NOVELTY - An adsorption material comprises metal-organic                framework containing metal ions and polytopic                organic linkers where each polytopic organic linker                is connected to greater than or equal to 2 metal                ions; and ligands which are amine appended to metal                ion of metal-organic framework.
   USE - An adsorption material for abating carbon                dioxide from flue gas, removing carbon dioxide from                biogas and sequestering carbon dioxide produced by                a source (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material enables effective capture of                carbon dioxide at low partial pressured while being                regenerable at moderate temperatures with low                regeneration energy for carbon capture                applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An adsorption material comprises metal-organic                framework containing metal ions and polytopic                organic linkers where each polytopic organic linker                is connected to greater than or equal to 2 metal                ions; and ligands which are amine appended to metal                ion of metal-organic framework where each                respective ligand includes compound of formulae                X-N(R1)-Z-(R2)(R3)-Q-(R4)(R5)-NH-R6 or                X-N(R1)-Z(R2)(R3)-Q-(R4)(R5)-X(R7)(R8)-NH-R6.
   X=metal ion of metal-organic framework;
   Z,Q=C, Si, Ge, S or Se;
   R1-R5,R7,R8=H, halo, optionally substituted                alkyl, heteroalkyl, aryl, heteroaryl, or                heterocycloalkyl; and
   R6=optionally substituted alkyl, heteroalkyl,                aryl, heteroaryl, or heterocycloalkyl comprising                greater than or equal to 3 non-hydrogen                atoms.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) abating carbon dioxide (CO2) from a flue                gas which involves (a) contacting the flue gas with                adsorption material to reversibly adsorb CO2 from                the flue gas, generating an adsorption material                enriched for CO2; and (b) thermally stripping a                major portion of CO2 from the adsorption material                enriched for CO2 using a temperature swing                adsorption method or vacuum swing adsorption                method;
   (2) removing CO2 from biogas which involves                contacting the biogas with the adsorption material                to reversibly adsorb CO2 from the biogas to                generate adsorption material enriched for CO2 and a                residual gas that is greater than or equal to 98%                pure methane; and
   (3) sequestering carbon dioxide produced by a                source which involves exposing the carbon dioxide                to adsorption material where carbon dioxide is                reversibly sequestered into the adsorption                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Each metal ion (X) in                  the metal ions is magnesium (Mg), calcium (Ca),                  manganese (Mn), chromium (Cr), iron (Fe), cobalt                  (Co), nickel (Ni), copper (Cu) or zinc (Zn),                  preferably Mg.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Each ligand contains                  compound of formula                  X-N(R1)-Z-(R2)(R3)-Q-(R4)(R5)-NH-R6, where R6 has                  molecular weight of greater than or equal to 44                  g/mol, preferably 44-300 g/mol, preferably                  N-(n-heptyl)ethylenediamine. Polytopic organic                  linker is 4,4'-dioxidobiphenyl-3,3'-dicarboxylate                  (dobpdc4-),                  4,4''-dioxido-(l,l':4',l''-terphenyl)-3,3''-dicarboxylate                  (dotpdc4-), or dioxidobiphenyl-4,4'-dicarboxylate                  (para-carboxylate-dobpdc4-). Polytopic organic                  linker has structure of formulae (1), (2), (3),                  (4), (5), (6) or (7).R9-R18=H, halo, OH, methyl and halogen                  substituted methyl in structures (1) and (2);                  andR13=optionally substituted aryl, vinyl,                  alkynyl and heteroaryl in structures (3) and                  (4).Preferred Properties: Adsorption material                  exhibits a single carbon dioxide (CO2) adsorption                  step upon CO2 adsorption or single CO2 desorption                  step upon CO2 desorption. Adsorption material has                  77 K N2 Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area                  of greater than or equal to 3050 m2/g. Adsorption                  material has a Langmuir surface area of greater                  than or equal to 5800 m2/g. Crystallographic                  density of the adsorption material is 0.4-1.2                  g/cm3.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R6=optionally substituted 3-10C alkyl, 3-10C                n-alkane, propyl, butyl, hexyl or heptyl,                branched-chain alkane, optionally substituted                heterocycloalkyl, such as cyclopropane,                cyclobutane, cyclopentane or cyclohexane; and
   R1=H.

PN CN108409821-A
TI Producing megestrol acetate nanocrystals, by entering supercritical carbon dioxide into crystallizer, feeding megestrol acetate solution, precipitating, injecting protective gas, pulverizing, and crushing coarse particles.
AU LI S
   WANG J
AE QINGDAO GUOHAI BIOLOGICAL PHARM CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201865914G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing megestrol acetate nanocrystals, involves using a pump to input carbon dioxide into heater through pipeline, after heating to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide, entering into crystallizer, using a pump to feed megestrol acetate solution into crystallizer, mixing supercritical carbon dioxide and megestrol acetate solution in nozzle, precipitating crystal of megestrol acetate, continuously washing and drying the crystals using supercritical carbon dioxide for 30-45 minutes, transferring to pulverization chamber from feed port of jet mill, filtering protective gas, injecting the protective gas into pulverization chamber, pulverizing, transferring to classification zone with ascending airflow under action of fan suction force, separating coarse and fine particles under strong centrifugal force generated by high-speed rotating class wheel, collecting fine particles by classifier wheel into cyclone and dust collector, and crushing the coarse particles.
   USE - The method is useful for producing megestrol acetate nanocrystals.
   ADVANTAGE - The produced megestrol acetate nanocrystals have uniform crystal size and circularity, and increased surface area, dissolution rate and bioavailability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing megestrol acetate nanocrystals, involves using a pump to input carbon dioxide into heater through pipeline, after heating to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide, entering into crystallizer, using a pump to feed megestrol acetate solution into crystallizer, mixing supercritical carbon dioxide and megestrol acetate solution in nozzle, ejecting from nozzle, precipitating crystal of megestrol acetate, stopping input of megestrol acetate solution, continuously washing and drying the crystals using supercritical carbon dioxide for 30-45 minutes, transferring to pulverization chamber from feed port of jet mill, filtering protective gas, injecting the protective gas into pulverization chamber through the nozzle at high speed, at intersection of multiple high-pressure air streams, pulverizing, transferring to classification zone with ascending airflow under action of fan suction force, separating coarse and fine particles under strong centrifugal force generated by high-speed rotating class wheel, collecting fine particles by classifier wheel into cyclone and dust collector, and crushing the coarse particles, the megestrol acetate solution solvent is acetone or ethyl acetate. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for megestrol acetate, which has an average particle diameter of 0-800 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred : The crystallizer temperature and pressure are 30-60 degrees C and 100-200 bar respectively. The carbon dioxide is provided at a flow rate of 15-30 g/minute. The megestrol acetate solution is provided at a flow rate of 0.5-3.5 ml/minute. The protective gas is one or more of inert gases. The protective gas has gas pressure of 6-12 kgf/cm2. The classifying wheel speed is 2000-6000 revolutions per minute (rpm).

PT P
PN JP2022127390-A
TI Purified gas production system for obtaining            purified gas by concentrating methane in biogas, has            expansion valve that expands compressed removal gas,            and utilizes latent heat associated with the expansion            of the removed gas is utilized for cooling the biogas            in the cooler.
AU KAWAGUCHI N
   FUJINUMA M
   YAMAZAKI W
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 2022B5885A
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a separator (40) that is                set for separating the biogas to obtain a purified                gas with increased methane concentration and a                stripped gas with increased carbon dioxide                concentration. A cooler is set for cooling the                biogas before cooler is fed to the separator. A                stripped gas compressor (62) is provided for                compressing stripped gas discharged from the                separator. An expansion valve (65) is provided for                expanding the compressed removal gas. The latent                heat associated with the expansion of the removed                gas is utilized for cooling the biogas in the                cooler.
   USE - Purified gas production system for obtaining                purified gas by concentrating methane in                biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The explosion-proof property in the housing is                improved. The energy consumption for cooling with a                compact facility, is reduced. The removal gas is                continuously discharged into the housing, so that                the inside of the housing is prevented from                becoming a flammable atmosphere. The reduction of                the effective area of the separation membrane due                to dew condensation is also easily prevented.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The latent heat associated with expansion of                the removed gas is utilized to cool the biogas in                the cooler by blowing the removed gas ejected from                the expansion valve to the cooler. The cooler is                set as a dehumidification cooler (12) for cooling                biogas for dehumidification. The separator is set                as a membrane separation device having a separation                membrane. A housing (70) contains an outlet through                which the removed gas is expanded by the expansion                valve, and ejected. A densitometer (71) is set for                measuring the methane concentration in the housing.                The expansion valve discharges the removed gas out                of the housing, when the methane concentration                measured by the densitometer, exceeds a                predetermined value. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                included for a method for producing a purified gas                by concentrating methane in biogas to obtain a                purified gas, involves separating pre-cooled biogas                to obtain a purified gas having an increased                methane concentration and a removed gas having an                increased carbon dioxide concentration. The removed                gas is expanded after compression, and the latent                heat accompanying the expansion of the removed gas                is utilized to cool the biogas after compression.                The biogas is dehumidified by cooling, and the                dehumidified biogas is separated to obtain a                purified gas with an increased methane                concentration. A removed gas with an increased                carbon dioxide concentration is obtained, the                removed gas is expanded after being compressed, and                the latent heat associated with the expansion of                the removed gas is utilized to dehumidify the                biogas. The biogas is compressed and cooled, and                the cooled biogas is separated to obtain a purified                gas with an increased methane concentration and                carbon dioxide. The stripped gas is expanded after                compression, and the latent heat associated with                the expansion of the stripped gas for cooling the                biogas is utilized after compression. The biogas is                separated by a membrane separation process using a                separation membrane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                purified gas production system.
   1Purified gas production system
   12Dehumidification cooler
   40Separator
   62Stripped gas compressor
   65Expansion valve
   70Housing
   71Densitometer

Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PT P
PN CN114400685-A
TI Power supply multi-energy complementation and            source charge storage interaction in electric power and            energy storage fields, involves using solar energy            or/and wind energy and other unstable electric energy            to electrolyze water to prepare hydrogen, hydrogen and            carbon dioxide to prepare methanol.
AU LUO G
   LUO C
AE LUO G (LUOG-Individual)
GA 202263366H
AB    NOVELTY - Power supply multi-energy complementation and                source charge storage interaction involves using                solar energy or/and wind energy and other unstable                electric energy to electrolyze water to prepare                hydrogen, hydrogen and carbon dioxide to prepare                methanol, methanol and various biomass energy and a                little oil to prepare liquid fuel, where the liquid                fuel comprises 45-55 wt.% methanol, 5-10 wt.%                ethanol, 25-35 wt.% non-edible oil and waste                modified oil, 10-15 wt.% oil, 0.1-0.2 wt.% acetone,                the heat value is 8000-10000, the old waste, fusion                energy storage and biomass energy and oil, the                components except oil can be regenerated. The power                plant taking the liquid fuel as main heat source,                where the power generation load is flexible and                adjustable, stable and reliable, the machine                capacity can be adjusted according to need, can be                used as peak adjusting power station.
   USE - Method for facilitating power supply                multi-energy complementation and source charge                storage interaction in electric power and energy                storage fields and to wind power, photoelectric,                biomass energy and other renewable energy such as                large scale development.
   ADVANTAGE - The power supply multi-energy complementation                and source charge storage interactio realizes wind                energy, solar energy, biomass energy power supply                is complementary, source charge storage                interaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Power supply multi-energy complementation and                source charge storage interaction involves using                solar energy or/and wind energy and other unstable                electric energy to electrolyze water to prepare                hydrogen, hydrogen and carbon dioxide to prepare                methanol, methanol and various biomass energy and a                little oil to prepare liquid fuel, where the liquid                fuel comprises 45-55 wt.% methanol, 5-10 wt.%                ethanol, 25-35 wt.% non-edible oil and waste                modified oil, 10-15 wt.% oil, 0.1-0.2 wt.% acetone,                the heat value is 8000-10000, the old waste, fusion                energy storage and biomass energy and oil, the                components except oil can be regenerated. The power                plant taking the liquid fuel as main heat source,                where the power generation load is flexible and                adjustable, stable and reliable, the machine                capacity can be adjusted according to need, can be                used as peak adjusting power station, also can be                used as distributed or large centralized power                station, when the methanol dosage is not enough,                using methanol prepared by coal. The non-edible oil                and the waste deteriorated grease are mixed in any                proportion, the oil material comprises waste                mineral oil and fuel oil in any proportion, the                generating device is a combustion engine combined                cycle machine set or a boiler generating set. The                stable and complementary power system can be                randomly matched according to the wind and light                resource; the unstable, uncontrollable wind power                and photoelectric conversion is the power supply                stable and reliable like traditional fire, the                light generating ratio is 70%, fuel generating                ratio is 30% to match, such as wind, light resource                is less and electric load is large area wind, light                generating ratio is 30%, the fuel generating ratio                is 70%, the waste electrolytic water generated by                large wind and light electric field to produce                hydrogen. The methanol is prepared by hydrogen and                carbon dioxide, to generate the fuel for generating                electricity, the process reaches the purpose of                energy storage and carbon circulation, carbon                neutralization.

PT P
PN GB2599918-A; WO2022079599-A1
TI Method for determining atmospheric radiative forcing difference by optimizing contrail formation caused by aircraft, involves determining atmospheric radiative forcing difference based on tentative atmospheric radiative forcing quantity and optimum atmospheric radiative forcing quantity.
AU DURANT A
AE SATAVIA LTD (SATA-Non-standard)
GA 202252452W
AB    NOVELTY - The method (100) involves receiving weather parameters to determine contrail forecast data (102). Flying parameters associated with an aircraft are received (104) to determine flying data. A tentative atmospheric radiative forcing quantity is determined (106) along a tentative flying trajectory based on the contrail forecast data and the flying data. The flying parameters are altered (108) to determine an optimized flying trajectory with an optimum atmospheric radiative forcing quantity, where the optimum atmospheric radiative forcing quantity is less than the tentative atmospheric radiative forcing quantity, and the optimized flight trajectory is validated using an imagery data. An atmospheric radiative forcing difference is determined (110) based on the tentative radiative forcing quantity and the optimum atmospheric radiative forcing quantity to evaluate an offset value for a forcing parameter associated with the atmospheric radiative forcing difference.
   USE - Method for determining atmospheric radiative forcing difference by optimizing contrail formation caused by an aircraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing an efficient and robust solution for reducing global warming potential and net radiative forcing of contrail formation by the aircraft and generating an equivalent offset value to compensate for the emission of carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide equivalents by the aircraft. The method enables determining maintenance activity for an engine of the aircraft to enable an improved efficiency of combustion of the fuel in the engine and reduced emission of carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide equivalents from the exhaust of the aircraft. The method enables determining atmospheric radiative forcing difference by optimizing contrail formation caused by the aircraft in efficient, effective, and robust manner to mitigate atmospheric radiative forcing due to increasing air travel volume by optimizing flying operations and maintenance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The flying parameters are selected from a date and time of a flight, a destination, a trajectory, altitude, an expected arrival at the destination, a speed, a latitude for flying the aircraft, a longitude for flying the aircraft, a heading, a payload, an operating characteristic of a particular aircraft type, and a fuel data. The contrail forecast data includes a probability of contrail formation and persistency and a radiation budget of an atmospheric column.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (a) System for determining atmospheric radiative forcing difference by optimizing contrail formation caused by an aircraft;
   (b) Computer program product comprising a set of instructions for determining atmospheric radiative forcing difference by optimizing contrail formation caused by an aircraft.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for determining atmospheric radiative forcing difference by optimizing contrail formation caused by an aircraft.
   Method for determining atmospheric radiative forcing difference by optimizing contrail formation caused by aircraft (100)
   Receiving weather parameters to determine contrail forecast data (102)
   Receiving flying parameters associated with aircraft to determine flying data (104)
   Determining tentative atmospheric radiative forcing quantity along tentative flying trajectory based on contrail forecast data and flying data (106)
   Altering flying parameters to determine optimized flying trajectory with optimum atmospheric radiative forcing quantity (108)
   Determining atmospheric radiative forcing difference based on tentative radiative forcing quantity and optimum atmospheric radiative forcing quantity (110)
DC T01 (Digital Computers); T06 (Process and Machine Control); W06 (Aviation, Marine and Radar Systems)
MC T01-S03; T01-J07D3A; T06-B01X; W06-B01B1; W06-B01A5

PT P
PN CN114084897-A
TI New process for co-production of various molten salts with barium hydroxide octahydrate, involves adding sodium hydroxide solution to barium nitrate solution and heating to synthesize barium hydroxide, cooling and crystallization centrifuging to obtain crude barium hydroxide octahydrate.
AU WANG C
   LI G
AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 202236285H
AB    NOVELTY - New process for co-production of various molten salts with barium hydroxide octahydrate, which involves: i. centrifuging part of clear barium nitrate solution crystallization to obtain barium nitrate crystallization and mother liquor 1; ii. generated tail gas 1 is purified and enriched; iii. in the barium hydroxide section, adding sodium hydroxide solution to the remaining clear barium nitrate solution in step (1) and heating to synthesize barium hydroxide; iv. cooling the reaction solution of this step and crystallization centrifuging to obtain crude barium hydroxide octahydrate crystals and mother liquor 2 after the precipitation is removed; v. washing crude barium hydroxide octahydrate and centrifuging with low temperature water to obtain pure barium hydroxide octahydrate and mother liquor 3, vacuum-drying; vi. in the sodium nitrate section, the mother liquor 2 is fed into the carbon dioxide gas enriched in step (1).
   USE - New process for co-production of various molten salts with barium hydroxide octahydrate.
   ADVANTAGE - Co-production of various molten salts with barium hydroxide octahydrate has high purity, good quality and high economic value, meanwhile, each step pays attention to the treatment of barium-containing impurities and mother liquor so as to meet the requirements of environmental protection, low carbon and almost no pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - New process for co-production of various molten salts with barium hydroxide octahydrate, which involves: i. barium nitrate section, barium carbonate is put into the reaction kettle and adding water to make a slurry, and adding concentrated nitric acid for heating to synthesize barium nitrate; ii. reaction solution of this step obtains clear barium nitrate solution after deprecipitating; iii. centrifuging part of clear barium nitrate solution crystallization to obtain barium nitrate crystallization and mother liquor 1; iv. barium nitrate finished product is obtained after the barium nitrate crystal is dried, and the remaining part is used to clarify the barium nitrate solution for subsequent use; v. generated tail gas 1 (the main component is carbon dioxide) is purified and enriched; vi. in the barium hydroxide section, adding sodium hydroxide solution to the remaining clear barium nitrate solution in step (1) and heating to synthesize barium hydroxide; vii. cooling the reaction solution of this step and crystallization centrifuging to obtain crude barium hydroxide octahydrate crystals and mother liquor 2 after the precipitation is removed; ix. washing crude barium hydroxide octahydrate and centrifuging with low temperature water to obtain pure barium hydroxide octahydrate and mother liquor 3; x. Vacuum-drying pure barium hydroxide octahydrate crystal, the finished barium hydroxide octahydrate is obtained; xi. in the sodium nitrate section, the mother liquor 2 is fed into the carbon dioxide gas enriched in step (1), after the barium ions in the solution are precipitated, the sodium nitrate solution is obtained by filtering; xii. adjusting the pH of the sodium nitrate solution to neutrality using nitric acid, after evaporation and crystallization, the finished product of sodium nitrate is obtained by drying.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the carbon dioxide collection device of the co-production of various molten salts with barium hydroxide octahydrate.
   Exhaust gas scrubber (1)
   Heat exchanger (2)
   Desorption tower (4)
   Condenser (5)
   Reboiler (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: In the step (1), the stirring speed is 100-300 revolutions per minute, continue to stir and heat to 80-95 degreesC, pH value of the solution after the reaction is completed is about 5-7, further adjust the pH value to between 6.5-7, keep warming for 30-50 minutes. In the step (1), resulting clear barium nitrate solution, a part of it is cooled to below 20 degreesC to crystallize, after centrifugation, product is obtained by drying at 100-120 degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours, other part of the barium nitrate solution is kept at 80-95 degrees Celsius for standby use. As a raw material for the production of barium hydroxide octahydrate. Stirring speed is 100-300 revolutions per minute, continue to stir and heat to 80-95 degreesC, keep warming for 30-50 minutes. In the step (2), the reaction solution is first cooled to below 45 degreesC using normal temperature water, use -15--10 degreesC frozen brine to cool the reaction solution to 0-5 degreesC for crystallization, crystallization time should be 2-3 hours, low temperature water temperature is about 5 degreesC. In the described step (2), the obtained pure barium hydroxide octahydrate crystal, under the condition of vacuum degree of 0.05-0.07 megapascal, barium hydroxide octahydrate crystals is obtained by drying at 50-65 degrees Celsius for 120-300 minutes. Carbon dioxide enriched in the step (1) is introduced into the mother liquor 2 of the step (3), and the barium ions are precipitated in the form of barium carbonate until no more precipitation occurs, after filtration, a clear sodium nitrate solution is obtained. The frozen brine (the frozen brine is below 5 degreesC at this time) is used for secondary utilization, cooling the saturated sodium nitrate solution to below 20 degreesC to crystallize, after centrifugation, the finished product is obtained by drying at 100-120 degreesC, filtrate obtained by centrifugation still contains sodium nitrate of about 88 gram/100 gram water, collecting the filtrate and repeating the production for recycling, long-term evaluation of sodium nitrate yield is greater than 90%. Preferred Components: In the step (1), fully beating and mixing barium carbonate and water, the mass ratio of barium carbonate solid and water is: 1:3-4, ensure adequate hydration make the stirring resistance lower, it is beneficial to prevent premature precipitation of crystals during the reaction. In the step (1), nitric acid used is industrial concentrated nitric acid with a mass fraction of 50-70 mass%, barium carbonate is barium carbonate with a mass fraction of about 97 mass%, molar ratio of the two is 2-2.2:1, use room temperature water to dilute the reaction solution to 20-23%. In the step (2), mass fraction of clarified barium nitrate solution is 20-23 mass%, mass fraction of sodium hydroxide is 30 mass%, molar ratio of barium nitrate and sodium hydroxide is 1:2.0-2.4. Mass ratio of low temperature water and crude barium hydroxide octahydrate theoretical crystallization is 0.2-0.25. Adjust the pH value of the sodium nitrate solution to neutrality with 50-70% nitric acid and evaporate to a mass fraction of 64-68 mass%. Preferred Components: In the described step (1), the main components of the mother liquor 1 are barium nitrate and excess barium nitrate, it is reused as the beating water in step (1). Mother liquor 2 main components are sodium hydroxide and sodium nitrate as the raw material of the sodium nitrate section. Mother liquor 3 contains sodium hydroxide and a very small amount of barium hydroxide, which is used for pH adjustment in step (1).

PT P
PN US2020248086-A1; US11370983-B2
TI Production of syngas in syngas stream composition,            involves charging oxidant and feedstock composition            containing solid fossil fuel and plastics, and            gasifying feedstock composition together with            oxidant.
AU TRAPP W L
   MURPHY J W
   WEST N M
AE EASTMAN CHEM CO (EACH-C)
GA 202074978Y
AB    NOVELTY - Production of syngas involves (a) charging an                oxidant and a feedstock composition to gasification                zone within gasifier, (b) gasifying the feedstock                composition together with the oxidant in                gasification zone to produce syngas composition,                and (c) discharging at least portion of the syngas                composition from the gasifier. The gasifier is an                entrained flow gasifier. The feedstock composition                comprises solid fossil fuel and 5 wt.% or less                plastics with respect to the solid, and 90 wt.%                recycle plastics having particle size in the                largest dimension of 2 mm or less.
   USE - Production of syngas in syngas stream                composition (claimed). Uses include but are not                limited to beverage, food, detergent, cosmetic,                personal care, bottle cap, cigarette filter, rod,                eyeglass frame, bedding, seat cushion, seat cover,                beverage machine front, fuel tank, acrylic                sheeting, bucket, audio tape, plumbing pipe, septic                tank, toy, cling film, agricultural film, milk                carton coating, electrical cable coating, heavy                duty industrial bag, sound insulation, helmet, surf                boards, stretch film, industrial packaging film,                and thin-walled container.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of syngas with                high quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) feedstock slurry composition, which                comprises 0.1-25 wt.% recycle plastics, the solid                fossil fuel and at least 20 wt.% water, in which                the recycle plastics having particle size of 2 mm                or less, and solid fuel having particle size of                less than 2 mm, the slurry is stable as determined                by having an initial viscosity of 100000 cP or less                at 5 minutes using a Brookfield R/S Rheometer                equipped with V80-40 vane operating at a shear rate                of 1.83/second or a Brookfield viscometer with an                LV-2 spindle rotating at a rate of 0.5 rpm,                measured at ambient conditions, or the slurry is                pumpable as determined by having a viscosity of                less than 30000 cP after mixing to obtain a                homogeneous distribution of solids throughout the                slurry and using a Brookfield R/S Rheometer                equipped with V80-40 vane operating at a shear rate                of 1.83/s or a Brookfield viscometer with an LV-2                spindle rotating at a rate of 0.5 measured at                ambient conditions, or both; and
   (2) syngas composition stream, which is                produced by gasifying in a gasifier a feedstock                comprising the solid fossil fuel and the recycle                plastics (mixed feed) having a compositional                variability of 5% or less measured over a time                period that is the lesser of 12 days or feeding the                mixed feed to the gasifier, in which syngas                compositional variability is measured and satisfied                against at least one of the gaseous compounds (in                moles) of carbon monoxide amount, or hydrogen                amount, or carbon dioxide amount, or methane                amount, or hydrogen sulfide amount, or carbonyl                sulfide amount, or total content of hydrogen and                carbon monoxide amount, total content of hydrogen,                carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide amount, total                content of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, methane                amount, total content of hydrogen, carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide, and methane amount, total content                of hydrogen sulfide, and carbonyl sulfide amount,                or total content of hydrogen, carbon monoxide,                carbon dioxide, methane, hydrogen sulfide, and                carbonyl sulfide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of plant                design of plastics and solid fossil fuel as a                feedstock to a gasification process to produce                syngas.
   Coal grinding zone (2)
   Stream (3)
   Gasifier (9)
   Gasification reaction zone (12)
   Gas scrubber (17)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The method is                  performed by using syngas stream does not                  containing tar and containing less than 4 wt.%                  tar, and a feedstock stream comprising 4 wt.% or                  less any one of sewage sludge, waste paper,                  and/or biomass. The syngas composition                  variability is preferably 4% or less, and                  switching variability of negative, 0, or 15% or                  less, preferably 10% or less, the switching                  frequency is at least 1x /2 years and the                  switching variability is determined by using                  relation %SV = Vm-Vff/Vffx 100 (I), where Vm is                  the syngas compositional variability of a gaseous                  compound(s) using a mixed stream containing                  recycle plastics and the fossil fuel, and Vff is                  the syngas compositional variability of the same                  gaseous compound(s) using a fossil fuel only                  stream.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The recycle plastics                  comprise acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene (ABS)                  copolymer, cellulose acetate, cellulose                  diacetate, cellulose triacetate, cellulose                  acetate propionate, cellulose acetate butyrate,                  and regenerated cellulose, epoxy, polyamides,                  phenolic resins, polyacetal, polycarbonates,                  polyethylene terephthalate, copolyesters                  containing residues of                  2,2,4,4-tetramethyl-1,3-cyclobutanediol (TMCD) or                  cyclohexanedimethanol (CHDM) or propylene glycol                  or neopentylglycol (NPG) monomer, high density                  polyethylene, low density polyethylene,                  crosslinked polyethylene, polyphenylene-based                  alloys, polypropylene and/or copolymers, other                  polyolefins, polystyrene, poly(methyl                  methacrylate), polytetrafluoroethylene, styrenic                  containing polymers, polyurethane, vinyl-based                  polymers, styrene-acrylonitrile copolymer,                  thermoplastic elastomers other than tires, epoxy,                  urea containing polymers, and/or melamines. The                  recycle plastic further comprises cellulosics                  obtained from tool handle, ophthalmic product,                  optical film, photographic film, coating, button,                  and/or toys, copolyesters comprising multiple                  dicyclohexane dimethanol moeities, and/or                  2,2,4,4-tetramethyl-1,3-cyclobutanediol moieties,                  and polyolefins comprising low-density                  polyethylene, high density polyethylene, linear                  low-density polyethylene, polypropylene,                  polymethylpentene, polybutene-1, and/or                  copolymers, preferably plastics obtained from                  eyeglass frame and/or crosslinked polyethylene                  pipe.

PN CN111411002-A
TI Producing natural gas by using vinasse involves performing biogas buffer treatment, preparing biogas source, and performing purification treatment, wastewater treatment and purification treatment.
AU SHI X
AE SHANXI CHUANGXINCHENGDA TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202068938X
AB    NOVELTY - Producing natural gas by using vinasse involves performing biogas buffer treatment. The wine tank is connected with a biogas buffer tank, and the biogas generated in the wine tank is transported to the biogas buffer tank through a pipeline for buffer treatment. The buffered wine tank biogas is transported to the biogas compression device through the pipeline for biogas compression treatment. The compressed biogas is transported to the biogas collection tank through a pipeline to form a biogas source. The biogas source is washed with water through the water washing absorption tower to make the gas composition meet methane greater than or equal to 97%, hydrogen sulfide less than or equal to 15 mg/L. The carbon dioxide is less than or equal to 3%, and then the biogas source after the water has been eluted with carbon is connected in series with desulfurization equipment, dehydration equipment and filtration equipment through the pipeline.
   USE - Method for producing natural gas by using vinasse.
   ADVANTAGE - The whole process flow and device structure of the method are simple. The treatment efficiency is high, the operation is convenient, and the absorption carrier is water without adding any absorbent. The obtained product is cheap and easy to obtain, non-toxic, easy to regenerate, and has wastewater treatment effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing natural gas by using vinasse involves performing biogas buffer treatment. The wine tank is connected with a biogas buffer tank, and the biogas generated in the wine tank is transported to the biogas buffer tank through a pipeline for buffer treatment. The buffered wine tank biogas is transported to the biogas compression device through the pipeline for biogas compression treatment. The compressed biogas is transported to the biogas collection tank through a pipeline to form a biogas source. The biogas source is washed with water through the water washing absorption tower to make the gas composition meet methane greater than or equal to 97%, hydrogen sulfide less than or equal to 15 mg/L. The carbon dioxide is less than or equal to 3%, and then the biogas source after the water has been eluted with carbon is connected in series with desulfurization equipment, dehydration equipment and filtration equipment through the pipeline. Hydrogen sulfide gas, water and entrained solid impurities in the biogas are removed, and purified gas whose main components are methane and carbon dioxide is obtained. The waste water after the biogas source is washed by the washing absorption tower first enters the contact oxidation tank. The contact oxidation pool awards the biodegradable portion of the organic matter in the water for microbial decomposition, which greatly reduces the concentration of wastewater. The wastewater enters the hydrolysis and acidification tank, which can convert some large molecules and difficult-to-biodegradable substances into small molecules and easily biodegradable substances. After the water is discharged from the hydrolysis acidification tank, it enters the anoxic tank for further biodegradation. After the anoxic tank, the liquid is fed into the water collection tank. The purified gas after purification is sent to the biogas purification device for separation, the purified methane is transported to the natural gas pipeline through the pipeline, and the carbon dioxide is discharged into the air.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: There is a desiccant on the adsorption bed. When the biogas passes through the adsorption bed, the desiccant absorbs the moisture in the biogas, and the filter device is a filter screen. After dehydration, the entrained impurities are filtered through the filter screen. The contact oxidation tank and the hydrolysis acidification tank adopts combined biological fillers to immobilize microorganisms.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. TI Integrated gasification of syngas power generation of e.g. coal, involves producing gas by gasifying feedstocks, gas purifying for forming syngas by removing dust and pollutant, and discharging air-side exhaust gas and fuel-side exhaust gas.
AU JEONG D K
AE JEONG D K (JEON-Individual)
GA 202031821C
AB    NOVELTY - Integrated gasification of syngas power generation involves (a) producing gas by gasifying feedstocks, recalculating exhaust gas comprising carbon dioxide and water whose main component are discharged through superheated steam, (b) a gas purifying for obtaining syngas by removing dust, pollutants and tar from the generated gas, (c) generating combustion heat obtained by mixing and combustion of syngas and external air purified in process step (b) to evaporate and overheat water in the water pipe to maintain at 800-1000 degrees C, (d) supplying in steps (b) and (c) to the inside of the honeycomb porous component of a ceramic-based coating with a metal oxide, and (e) discharging air-side exhaust gas and the fuel-side exhaust gas in step (d) are introduced into the air-side gas turbine and the fuel-side gas turbine, respectively, and performing gas turbine driving and power generation process that generates power through a generator connected to the drive shaft of the turbine.
   USE - Integrated gasification of syngas power generation of coal, biomass, and combustible waste (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and enables integrated gasification of syngas power generation which is capable of generates electricity by driving fuel-side gas turbine and oxygen-side gas turbine, and has recycled and reacted in the gasification reactor to reduce the ultimate source of contamination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated gasification of syngas power generation involves (a) producing gas by gasifying feedstocks, recalculating exhaust gas comprising carbon dioxide and water whose main component are discharged through superheated steam at 800-1000 degrees C by using gasification reducing agent, (b) a gas purifying for obtaining syngas by removing dust, pollutants and tar from the generated gas generated in process step (a) and containing cyclone, a filter dust collector or an electric dust collector, an absorption tower or a venturi scrubber and a tar condenser, (c) generating combustion heat obtained by mixing and combustion of syngas and external air purified in process step (b) to evaporate and overheat water in the water pipe to maintain at 800-1000 degrees C and pressure of 5-10 bar, (d) supplying in steps (b) and (c) to the inside of the honeycomb porous component of a ceramic-based coating with a metal oxide, so that the synthesis gas and the metal oxide dioxide catalytic combustion reaction of carbon dioxide and water are discharged as main components and supplying air to regenerate the oxygen function of the metal oxide, so that the metal is reoxidized and discharged from the oxygen side, and (e) discharging air-side exhaust gas and the fuel-side exhaust gas in step (d) are introduced into the air-side gas turbine and the fuel-side gas turbine, respectively, and performing gas turbine driving and power generation process that generates power through a generator connected to the drive shaft of the turbine. The chemical circulating combustion reaction process is obtained by injecting the superheated steam to prevent direct contact of the synthesis gas with air and to remove carbon dioxide remaining in the oxidation catalyst combustion reaction.

TI Method for shortening morel species culture period, involves performing standardization of raw materials, determining culture medium followed by preparing culture material, and adjusting ventilation volume of culture room.
AU LUO X
   LI R
   ZHAO Q
   ZHANG J
   LU Q
   ZI L
   CHEN M
   LUO P
AE YUNNAN MUSHROOM WORLD BIO-TECHNOLOGY CO (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2019534862
AB    NOVELTY - A morel species culture period shortening method involves performing standardization of raw materials including standardization of main materials, standardization of auxiliary materials, and standardization of production water. Culture medium is determined including 40-50% wheat, 13-20% wooden meal, 15-20% rice hull, 18-20% soil, 1% light calcium carbonate, 1% plaster, 0.5% glucose and 0.5% monopotassium phosphate. Solid strains are prepared by preparing a culture material, making bacteria package using high temperature resistant polypropylene plastic bag for strain packaging, heating bacteria package, sterilizing and cooling followed by inoculating a species of Morchella species, placing inoculated bacteria in the culture room, culture temperature controlled at 17-19 degrees C, adjusting the ventilation volume of the culture room to control the carbon dioxide concentration.
   USE - Method for shortening morel species culture period.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables shortening the culture period of the morel species from 25 days to 15 days, improving the production efficiency, controlling the high consistency of the cultured mycelium, ensuring the mycelial vigor and quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A morel species culture period shortening method involves performing standardization of raw materials including standardization of main materials, standardization of auxiliary materials, and standardization of production water, where main materials include wheat grain, wood chips from broad-leaved tree shavings without harmful material species or coniferous wood chips naturally piled for more than six months, wood chips size of 8 mm, water content of less than 50%, rice husk, requiring complete grain rice husk cracking 2-4 petals, soil comply with the GB15618 soil environmental quality standard, using natural, uncontaminated peat soil or peat soil or soil below the farmland tillage layer, and auxiliary material including wheat bran, maize flour, soybean powder, and all raw materials require fresh, clean, dry, insect-free, mildew-free, and odor-free. Culture medium is determined including 40-50% wheat, 13- 20% wooden meal, 15-20% rice hull, 18- 20% soil, 1% light calcium carbonate, 1% plaster, 0.5% glucose and 0.5% monopotassium phosphate. Solid strains are prepared by preparing a culture material, making bacteria package using high temperature resistant polypropylene plastic bag for strain packaging, where package size is 180mmx 340 mm, the wet material weighs 1000 g per bag, the high temperature resistant plastic collar and cover are used, the specification is 40 mm in diameter, heating bacteria package, sterilizing and cooling followed by inoculating a species of Morchella species with a diameter of 20 mm and a length of 160 mm inserted in the middle of the bacteria bag to increase the gas permeability, increase the growth rate of the mycelium, and shorten the culture period of the strain, placing inoculated bacteria in the culture room, culture temperature controlled at 17-19 degrees C, the humidity of 50-60%, adjusting the ventilation volume of the culture room to control the carbon dioxide concentration of 1000 ppm, when the mycelial culture is 7 days under the same ventilation, the carbon dioxide concentration in the culture room reach 4000 ppm, increase the fresh air time of the culture house and control the carbon dioxide concentration in the culture room to 1500-2500 ppm, after 15 days of cultivation, the product of the morel species is obtained.

PN CN109762611-A
TI Preparing methanol by using low-order coal multi-path comprises e.g. processing the low-rank coal, drying by a gasification reduction, heating, purifying the rich gas, processing the purified rich gas and reacting.
AU LV B
   LI Z
   QIU Y
   JIANG X
   FENG Z
AE ZHEJIANG TIANLU ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201946421P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methanol by using low-order coal multi-path comprises e.g. processing the low-rank coal by a drying process to obtain the dried low-rank coal and the exhaust gas, and the exhaust gas obtains the coal powder through the first dust removal process, treating the low-rank coal after drying by a gasification reduction process to obtain a rich gas and a upgraded coal, and heating the gasification reduction process is drying under an oxygen-free or micro-aerobic condition a chemical reaction process in which the lower rank coal, purifying the rich gas by the first purification process to obtain the purified gas and waste water, sequentially processing the purified rich gas through the desulfurization process and the first compression process to obtain a reforming conversion feed gas, processing the reforming conversion feed gas by a reforming conversion process to obtain a first reformed gas comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and mixing the waste water.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methanol by using low-order coal multi-path.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has less impurity, is environmentally friendly and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing methanol by using low-order coal multi-path comprises (i) processing the low-rank coal by a drying process to obtain the dried low-rank coal and the exhaust gas, and the exhaust gas obtains the coal powder through the first dust removal process, (ii) treating the low-rank coal after drying by a gasification reduction process to obtain a rich gas and a upgraded coal, and heating the gasification reduction process is drying under an oxygen-free or micro-aerobic condition a chemical reaction process in which the lower rank coal, (iii) purifying the rich gas by the first purification process to obtain the purified gas and waste water, (iv) sequentially processing the purified rich gas through the desulfurization process and the first compression process to obtain a reforming conversion feed gas, processing the reforming conversion feed gas by a reforming conversion process to obtain a first reformed gas comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, (v) mixing the waste water, the coal powder and processing the coal water slurry additive to obtain a coal water slurry, and the coal water slurry and oxygen by a coal water slurry gasification process, a second water gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, where the second water gas is treated by a second purification process to obtain a second reformed gas, (vi) reacting the upgraded coal by with water and oxygen to prepare a third water gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and the third water gas is treated by a third purification process to obtain a third reformed gas, (vi) converting at least one of the first reformed gas, the second reformed gas, and the third reformed gas by a process decarburization and carbon supplementation process and the hydrogen supplementation process are combined to obtain a methanol synthesis gas having a hydrogen to carbon ratio R of 2.05-2.1, and (viii) sequentially subjecting the methanol synthesis gas to a second compression process and a methanol synthesis process to obtain crude methanol and methanol purge gas, and the crude methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the desulfurization process comprises a fine desulfurization process and a crude desulfurization process, and the purified rich gas is sequentially processed by a crude desulfurization process, a first compression process and a fine desulfurization process to obtain a reforming conversion raw material gas. In the step (iii), the first purification process comprises a dust removal process and a de-tarring process. The conversion conversion process converts carbon monoxide into hydrogen by reaction with water from the process of reforming and converting the raw material gas into the methanol synthesis gas. The method comprises first converting at least one of the first reformed gas, the second reformed gas, and the third reformed gas by a conversion conversion process to obtain a first transformation gas, the second shift gas, and the third shift gas, at least one of the first shift gas, the second shift gas, and the third shift gas is mixed and obtained by a decarburization carbon supplementation process to obtain a methanol synthesis gas. In the step (iv), the second reformed gas obtains a second shift gas by a conversion conversion process, the second shift gas and the second reformed gas, and least one of the three reformed gases is passed through a decarburization carbon supplementation process and mixed to obtain a methanol synthesis gas. In the step (vii), in the decarburization and carbon supplementation process, when the hydrogen-to-carbon ratio R value of the first conversion gas, the second conversion gas, and the third conversion gas is greater than 2.1, passing carbon dioxide to adjust the R value to 2.05-2.1, when the first reformed gas, the second reformed gas, and the third reformed gasification have a hydrogen to carbon ratio R value of less than 2.05, the carbon dioxide is removed so that the R value is adjusted to 2.05-2.1. In the step (iv), the process for producing hydrogen is from reforming conversion gas to the methanol synthesis gas process is added in the hydrogen process. In the step (i), the particle size of the coal powder is less than 1 mm. In the step (ii), the reaction temperature of the gasification reduction process is 350-800 degrees C. In the step (iv), the reforming conversion feedstock gas is treated by the reforming conversion process to reform the reforming feedstock gas, and the gas components are directly separated, and the reforming is directly converted into a partial hydrocarbon in the raw material gas. The reforming process is a reforming conversion process of carbon monoxide and hydrogen. In the step (iv), the reforming conversion process is steam reforming conversion, steam pure oxygen reforming conversion and pure oxygen reforming conversion. In the step (viii), the pressure of the second compression process is 40-50 kg, and the temperature is 200-350 degrees C.

TI Producing synthesis gas or products derived from synthesis gas comprises e.g. exposing feedstock to fluidized bed having solid particles in reactor under thermal cracking conditions and introducing oxygen stream and steam into gasifier.
AU HARANDI M N
   RAJAGOPALAN S
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 201942210N
AB    NOVELTY - Producing synthesis gas or products derived from synthesis gas comprises: exposing a feedstock comprising a T10 distillation point of greater than or equal to 343 degrees C to a fluidized bed comprising solid particles in a reactor under thermal cracking conditions to form a 343 degrees C-liquid product; introducing an oxygen stream comprising oxygen, a diluent stream comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and/or other inorganic gases, and steam into a gasifier; passing at least a portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke from the reactor to the gasifier; exposing at least a portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke to gasification conditions in the gasifier to form a gas phase product comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide and partially gasified coke particles; removing at least a first portion of the partially gasified coke particles from the gasifier; and passing at least a second portion of the partially gasified coke particles from the gasifier to the reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for producing synthesis gas or products derived from synthesis gas (claimed), preferably the synthesis gas is methanol, ammonia and urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides high quality synthesis gas from a fluidized coking system that includes an integrated gasifier, and allows for reduced or minimized production of inorganic nitrogen compounds by using oxygen from an air separation unit as the oxygen source for gasification; reduces, minimizes or eliminates nitrogen introduced as a diluent into the gasification; and can also allow for gasification of coke while reducing, minimizing or eliminating production of slag or other glass-like substances in the gasifier.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing synthesis gas or products derived from synthesis gas comprises: exposing a feedstock comprising a T10 distillation point of greater than or equal to 343 degrees C to a fluidized bed comprising solid particles in a reactor under thermal cracking conditions to form a 343 degrees C-liquid product, where the thermal cracking conditions comprise greater than or equal to 10 wt.% conversion of the feedstock relative to 343 degrees C, and the thermal cracking conditions being effective for depositing coke on the solid particles; introducing an oxygen stream comprising oxygen, a diluent stream comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and/or other inorganic gases, and steam into a gasifier, where the oxygen stream comprises greater than or equal to 55 vol.% of oxygen prior to combining the oxygen stream with at least one of the diluent stream and the steam; passing at least a portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke from the reactor to the gasifier; exposing at least a portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke to gasification conditions in the gasifier to form a gas phase product comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide and partially gasified coke particles, where the gas phase product comprises a combined volume of hydrogen and carbon monoxide that is greater than a volume of nitrogen in the gas phase product; removing at least a first portion of the partially gasified coke particles from the gasifier; and passing at least a second portion of the partially gasified coke particles from the gasifier to the reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: separating carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide from the gas phase product to form at least a synthesis gas stream; exposing at least a portion of the gas phase product to a methanol synthesis catalyst under methanol synthesis conditions to form methanol, and separating the oxygen stream and a nitrogen stream from air, where the nitrogen stream comprises greater than or equal to 95 vol.% of nitrogen, and exposing at least a portion of the nitrogen stream to a catalyst in the presence of hydrogen under ammonia synthesis conditions to form ammonia; and exposing at least a portion of the ammonia to a urea synthesis catalyst in the presence of carbon dioxide under urea synthesis conditions to form urea; and separating carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide from the gas phase product to form a carbon dioxide product stream, where exposing at least a portion of the ammonia to a urea synthesis catalyst in the presence of carbon dioxide under urea synthesis conditions comprises exposing at least a portion of the ammonia and at least a portion of the carbon dioxide product stream to the urea synthesis catalyst under urea synthesis conditions. The step of passing at least a portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke from the reactor to the gasifier comprises passing at least a portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke to a heater, and passing the portion of the solid particles comprising deposited coke from the heater to the gasifier. The step of passing at least a second portion of the partially gasified coke particles from the gasifier to the reactor comprises passing at least a second portion of partially gasified coke particles to a heater, and passing the second portion of the partially gasified coke particles from the heater to the reactor. The step of exposing the portion of the solid particles comprises coke to gasification conditions results in deposition of less than 0.1 wt.% of metal oxides on a wall of the gasifier, relative to a metals content of the feedstock. The step of exposing at least a portion of the nitrogen stream to an ammonia synthesis catalyst in the presence of hydrogen under ammonia synthesis conditions comprises exposing at least a portion of the nitrogen stream and at least a portion of the synthesis gas stream to the ammonia synthesis catalyst under ammonia synthesis conditions. Preferred Components: The diluent stream comprises a recycled portion of the carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide separated from the gas phase product, or the synthesis gas stream comprises greater than or equal to 80 vol.% of hydrogen and/or carbon monoxide. The oxygen stream is combined with at least a portion of the diluent stream prior to introducing the oxygen stream into the gasifier, or the oxygen stream is combined with at least a portion of the diluent stream upstream from the gasifier. The first portion of partially gasified coke particles comprises a first weight percentage of metals, relative to a weight of the first portion of partially gasified coke particles, that is greater than a weight percentage of metals in the feedstock, relative to a weight of the feedstock, and/or the first portion of partially gasified coke particles comprises a first combined weight percentage of nickel, vanadium, and iron, relative to a weight of the first portion of partially gasified coke particles, that is greater than a combined weight percentage of nickel, vanadium, and iron in the feedstock, relative to a weight of the feedstock. The solid particles comprise coke.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109701574-A; CN109701574-B
TI Preparation of nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst for catalyzing hydrogenation of pyridine ring compound to piperidine compound, by preparing nitrogen-modified activated carbon and supporting noble metal.
AU LU C
   ZHANG X
   ZHOU Y
   LIU Q
   NIE J
   FENG F
   MA L
   ZHANG Q
   ZHAO J
   XU X
   GUO L
   LV J
   CEN J
   LI X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2019423892
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst involves: (1) carrying out normalized selective treatment on carbon surface groups by taking activated carbon, oxidizing the activated carbon with a treatment agent, then heating the treated activated carbon in a flowing argon atmosphere for heat treatment, cooling to room temperature, taking out the sample under an argon atmosphere, sealing and storing to obtain a pretreated activated carbon; (2) preparing a nitrogen-modified activated carbon by condensation reaction by dissolving primary amines in water, adding the pretreated activated carbon and uniformly mixing, then placing in a hydrothermal kettle for hydrothermal reaction in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, separating the sample, washing and drying; and (3) supporting noble metals by loading noble metal onto the nitrogen-modified activated carbon obtained in the step (2) by UV light reduction method.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst, which is useful for catalyzing hydrogenation of a pyridine ring compound to synthesize a piperidine compound, preferably N-benzyl-2,3-pyridinyl phthalimide to 2,3-piperidinyl phthalimide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst with high conversion rate, good selectivity and stability and fast hydrogenation rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst involves: (1) carrying out normalized selective treatment on carbon surface groups by taking activated carbon, where the surface of activated carbon only contains carbon dioxide leaving group and the total content of the carbon dioxide leaving group is not higher than 0.8 mu mol/g, oxidizing the activated carbon with a treatment agent, which comprises perchloric acid, sodium chlorate or nitric acid, then heating the treated activated carbon in a flowing argon atmosphere at 500-700 degrees C for heat treatment, after the heat treatment is completed, cooling to room temperature, taking out the sample under an argon atmosphere, sealing and storing to obtain a pretreated activated carbon; (2) preparing a nitrogen-modified activated carbon by condensation reaction by dissolving primary amines in water, adding the pretreated activated carbon and uniformly mixing, then placing in a hydrothermal kettle for hydrothermal reaction in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, after the reaction is complete, separating the sample, washing the sample with water until neutral and drying; and (3) supporting noble metals by loading noble metal onto the nitrogen-modified activated carbon obtained in the step (2) by UV light reduction method, where the noble metal is palladium and/or platinum. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst prepared by the above preparation method for catalyzing hydrogenation of a pyridine ring compound to synthesize a piperidine compound, which involves putting pyridine ring compound and nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst into a high-pressure hydrogenation reactor, closing the reactor, replacing the air inside the reactor, then filling with hydrogen to start stirring, carrying out catalytic hydrogenation reaction at 20-60 degrees C and a hydrogen pressure of 0.1-1 MPa to obtain a piperidine compound, where the catalytic hydrogenation reaction is carried out in a solvent or in the absence of a solvent, and the solvent is one or a mixture of two or more of methanol, ethanol and dimethylformamide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), the step of treating the activated carbon with the treatment agent involves uniformly dispersing the activated carbon into 15-40 wt.% treatment agent solution, gradually heating the obtained suspension at 50-95 degrees C under stirring, continuously treating for 5-15 hours, circularly condensing during the period, and absorbing tail gas by alkali liquor, then cooling the suspension to room temperature, carrying out suction filtration, washing with water until the pH value is neutral, then drying. The UV light reduction method involves dispersing the nitrogen-modified activated carbon in water, dropwisely adding the solution containing precious metal ions to the nitrogen-modified activated carbon slurry under stirring, after the addition is completed, irradiating by UV light for a certain time at room temperature, controlling the wavelength of UV light to 280-200 nm, where the illumination time is 5 seconds to 5 minutes, then continuously stirring for 0.5-2 hours, filtering, washing the mixture with water to neutral pH and drying to obtain the nitrogen-modified carbon-supported noble metal hydrogenation catalyst. Preferred Conditions: The drying step is carried out at 80-120 degrees C for 3-5 hours under vacuum. The step of heating the treated activated carbon is carried out at a heating rate of 1-10 degrees C/minute to 500-700 degrees C, and the heat treatment time is 1-5 hours. The hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 150-250 degrees C for 10-20 hours under carbon dioxide atmosphere having a gas pressure of 1-5 MPa. Preferred Components: The size of the activated carbon particles meets the following conditions that the particle mass content of 200-300 mesh is not less than 80%. In the step (1), the volume of the treatment agent solution is 2-30 ml/g based on the mass of the activated carbon. In the step (3), the loading amount of the noble metal palladium and/or platinum is 0.1-10 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The primary amine organic substance contains two amine groups, preferably 1,2-propylenediamine, ethylenediamine or hexamethylenediamine. The mass ratio of the primary amine organic substance to the pretreated activated carbon is (1-10):1. The mass ratio of amine organic matter to water is 1:(1-10). The pyridine ring compound is a compound containing both a pyridine ring and a benzyl group. The coupled hydrogenation reaction of the hydrogenation of the pyridine ring and the hydrodebenzylation occurs during the hydrogenation reaction.

TI Cultivating seafood mushroom by inoculating            seafood mushroom into strain bottle, arranging culture            rack, installing spray pipe, placing bottle on rack,            controlling temperature and humidity, spraying water            and picking ripe mushrooms.
AU NI Z
AE CHENGDU NINGSHENG LVKANG FOOD CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A0855K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cultivating seafood mushroom                involves sterilizing the inoculation room using                ozone generator, inoculating the seafood mushroom                into strain bottle, controlling temperature and                relative humidity, opening the air inlet system,                controlling the concentration of carbon dioxide,                culturing, arranging culture rack in the                cultivation room, installing a spray pipe on the                top of the cultivation room, arranging nozzles,                placing the culture bottle on the culture rack,                controlling the indoor temperature and relative                humidity, spraying water, picking ripe mushrooms                having sturdy stipe.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating seafood                mushroom.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a suitable aseptic                environment for the growth of the seafood mushroom                at each stage to ensure its smooth growth.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for cultivating seafood mushroom                involves (a) wiping the culture bottle with 80-85%                ethanol and digging the old strain having diameter                of 0.3-0.5 mm using disinfected knife, (b)                sterilizing the inoculation room using ozone                generator and inoculating the seafood mushroom into                strain bottle, where the indoor and outdoor air                pressure difference is 15-17 Pa during the                inoculation process, (c) controlling the                temperature at 20-25 degrees C and relative                humidity at 68-70%, opening the air inlet system                once in every 2-4 hours and controlling the                concentration of carbon dioxide at 2700-3000 mg/l,                (d) continuing culturing for 10-15 days,                controlling the relative humidity at 70-75% and the                high center temperature of the culture bottle is                higher than the ambient temperature of 5-6 degrees                C during the culture process and opening the fresh                air system once in every 1-2 hours to supplement                the wind for 5-7 minutes, (e) continuing culturing                the seafood mushroom until bottle is covered with                hyphae, controlling the temperature at 25-30                degrees C and relative humidity at 75-80% and                monitoring the carbon dioxide concentration in real                time during the culture process to maintain the                carbon dioxide concentration at 2100-2300 mg/l, (f)                arranging culture rack having length of 2 m, width                of 0.5 m and height of 2 m at intervals of 1.5 m in                the cultivation room, installing a spray pipe on                the top of the cultivation room, and arranging                nozzles at interval of 7-10 cm, (g) placing the                culture bottle on the culture rack after 3-5 days                of mushrooming, controlling the indoor temperature                at 22-25 degrees C and relative humidity at 100%                and spraying water once in every 1-2 hours for 3-5                minutes, (h) placing the culture bottle on the                culture rack after 5-20 days of mushrooming,                controlling the indoor temperature at 25-27 degrees                C and relative humidity at 90% and spraying water                once in every 1-2 hours for 3-5 minutes, (i)                placing the culture bottle on the culture rack                after 20-40 days of mushrooming, controlling the                indoor temperature at 27-30 degrees C and relative                humidity at 80% and spraying water once in every                3-4 hours for 6-10 minutes, (j) placing the culture                bottle on the culture rack after 40-70 days of                mushrooming, controlling the indoor temperature at                30-35 degrees C and relative humidity at 70% and                spraying water once in every 3-4 hours for 6-10                minutes and (k) picking ripe mushrooms having                sturdy stipe after 70 days of mushrooming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  wiping the culture bottle with 82% ethanol and                  digging the old strain having diameter of 0.4 mm.                  The step (b) involves maintaining the indoor and                  outdoor air pressure difference at 16 Pa. The                  step (c) involves controlling the room                  temperature at 22 degrees C and the relative                  humidity at 69%, and controlling the carbon                  dioxide at 2800 mg/l once in every 3 hours. The                  step (d) involves continuing culturing for 12                  days, controlling the relative humidity at 72%                  and the high center temperature of the culture                  bottle is higher than the ambient temperature of                  5.5 degrees C during the culture process and                  opening the fresh air system once in every 1.5                  hours to supplement the wind for 1.5 minutes. The                  step (e) involves controlling the temperature at                  27 degrees C and relative humidity at 78% and                  maintaining the carbon dioxide concentration at                  2200 mg / liter. The step (g) involves                  controlling the indoor temperature at 23 degrees                  C and spraying water once in every 1.5 hours for                  4 minutes. The step (h) involves controlling the                  indoor temperature at 26 degrees C and relative                  humidity at 90% and spraying water once in every                  1.5 hours for 4 minutes. The step (i) involves                  controlling the indoor temperature at 28 degrees                  C and relative humidity at 90% and spraying water                  once in every 3.5 hours for 8 minutes. The step                  (j) involves controlling the indoor temperature                  at 32 degrees C and spraying water once in every                  3.5 hours for 8 minutes.
