TI Direct carboxylation of hydrocarbon compounds to produce carboxylic acids comprises contacting hydrocarbon compounds with carbon dioxide in presence of e.g. polar aprotic solvent having specific dielectric constant and/or dipole moment.
AU CORNELLA J
   JULIA HERNANDEZ F
   MARTIN ROMO R
AE FUNDACIO INST CATALA DINVESTIGACIO QUIMI (CATA-Non-standard)
   INST CATALANA RECERCA I ESTUDIS AVANCATS (CATA-Non-standard)
GA 201806356M
AB    NOVELTY - Direct carboxylation of hydrocarbon compounds (I) to produce carboxylic acids (IIa)-(IIc) comprises contacting (I) with carbon dioxide in presence of polar aprotic solvent having dielectric constant above 25 and/or dipole moment above 2.5 D and which does not release any protons under the conditions, and (a) nickel(ll) salt, (b) reducing agent comprising manganese or zinc or an electrochemical or photochemical reducing means, (d) ligand (Ib), and (d) hydrogen source comprising water or substituted alkylbromide compounds (Ic) and (Id).
   USE - The method is useful for direct carboxylation of (I) to produce (IIa)-(IIc).
   ADVANTAGE - The carboxylation: predominantly takes place at a preferred position of the molecule irrespective of the position of the activating group, and yields the desired regioisomer i.e. octanoic acid with the carboxy group in position 1 as the major product, irrespective whether the starting material is 2-bromoheptane, 3-bromoheptane or 4-bromoheptane; is regioconvergent so that a variety of different types of compounds are suitable as starting materials, thus allowing to use a single compound as a starting material or a mixture of at least two compounds suitable as the starting material, while the same product is obtained.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Direct carboxylation of a hydrocarbon compounds of formula (I) to produce carboxylic acids of formulae (IIa)-(IIc) comprises contacting (I) with carbon dioxide in the presence of polar aprotic solvent which has a dielectric constant above 25 and/or a dipole moment above 2.5 D and which does not release any protons under the conditions, and (a) a nickel(ll) salt comprising nickel(II) halide, nickel(II) acetylacetonate, nickel(II) sulfate, nickel(II) perchlorate, nickel(II) trifluoromethanesulfonate, nickel(II) hexafluoroacetylacetonate, nickel(II) sulfamate, nickel(II) carbonate, nickel(II) oxalate, substituted bis(formyloxy)nickel compound of formula (Ia) or their solvates, (b) a reducing agent comprising manganese or zinc or an electrochemical or photochemical reducing means, (d) a ligand of formula (Ib), and (d) a hydrogen source comprising water or substituted alkylbromide compounds of formula (Ic) and (Id).
   R4 = e.g. H;
   X1 = e.g. Br; either
   R3, R3 = e.g. H; or
   one of CR3R3, CR3R4 or CR3R4 = cyclic group e.g. 2-5 condensed rings;
   p = 0-15;
   u = 0-1;
   R = e.g. optionally branched 1-20C alkyl;
   R2, R2a = e.g. H;
   R1, R1a = e.g. H; either
   R10, R7, R8, R9 = e.g. H; or
   at least one of CR8R7, CR10R9, CR7R9 or CR8R10 = ring comprising 1-3 ring;
   R4a, R4b, R4c = R4;
   q = 0-7;
   r = 0-14; and
   q+r+1 = 1-15.
   With Provisos Full definitions are given in the DEFINITIONS (Full Definitions) Field. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of a reaction system for binding a carboxyl group to a starting material comprising an alkene or a leaving group-containing alkane, preferably (Ill), to generate a compound derived from the starting material that no longer contains the carbon-carbon double bond or leaving group but carries the carboxy group in a terminal position, comprising carbon dioxide, a polar aprotic solvent the components (a)-(d) as per se.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: : The reducing agent comprises manganese, zinc or an electrochemical reducing means.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The direct carboxylation yields a reaction product comprising saturated carboxylic compounds (IIa), (IIb), (IIc), and saturated carboxylic compounds of formula ((Z1)nY1(COOH)(CH2)u(R3)u(CH2)u(R4)u(CHR4)p-H) (IId) or (R3-(CH2)u(R3)u(CH2)u(R4)u(CHR4a)q-CH(R4d(COOH))(CHR4c)rH) (IIe). Either the (I) does not contain any electron withdrawing groups that are connected with the activating group only via secondary or tertiary aliphatic carbon atoms and the reaction is carried out under kinetic or thermodynamic control conditions, or the (I) does contain at least one electron withdrawing groups that are connected with the activating group only via secondary or tertiary aliphatic carbon atoms and the reaction is carried out under kinetic control reaction conditions. The reaction conditions are selected so that: the catalyst is formed using nickel(II) iodide; a photochemical reducing means is used; the ligand (Ib) is used; the catalyst is prepared in situ by mixing the components of the catalyst as specified with the starting material and reaction solvent where the addition of the individual catalyst components may be performed in any relative order; and the starting material is an alkene and the hydrogen source is used in at 0.9-11 molar equivalents relative to the starting material being 1 molar equivalent. The nickel(ll) salt is used at 0.9-11 mol.% with respect to the starting material being 100 mol.% and/or (I) is used at 0.9-55 mol.% with respect to the starting material being 100 mol.% and/or the reducing agent is used at 100-550 mol.% with respect to the starting material being 100 mol.%. The photochemical reducing means is used and the photochemical reducing means consists of a combination of a photosensitizer with an electron donor compound. The reaction is carried out at 0-90 degrees C, preferably 5-85 degrees C. Preferred Components: (I) is halo substituted hydrocarbon compound of formula (R3b-(CH2)l-CH(X1)-CH2-(CH2)p-H) (IIId). The solvent comprises (N,N-dimethylformamide), N,N-diethylformamide, dimethylsulfoxide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylpyrrolidine, N,N-dibutylformamide or 1-formylpyrrolidine. The photochemical comprises complexes are tris(bipyridine)ruthenium(II) chloride, bis(2-benzo(b)thiophen-2-yl-pyridine)(acetylacetonate)iridium(III), bis(2-phenylpyridinato-)(2,2 -bipyridine)iridium(III).

Preferred Definitions:
   u = 0;
   R3b = optionally branched (1-20-l)C alkyl (optionally substituted by 6-20C aryl (optionally substituted by 1-6C alkylcarbonyloxy, 1-6C alkoxycarbonyl or optionally condensed with a 5-6-membered heterocyclyl comprising C, N, O, S or P)), 3-6C cycloalkyl, -OC(O)R5, -C(O)OR5, -OR5, -C(O)R5, -C(O)N(R5)2, -CHO, mono- or bicyclic optionally saturated or aromatic heterocyclyl by 3-8 ring comprising C, N, O, S and P, optionally substituted by 1-6C alkylcarbonyloxy or 1-6C alkoxycarbonyl;
   R5 = phenyl (optionally substituted by 1-6C alkyloxy, 1-6C alkyloxycarbonyl, 1-6C alkylcarbonyloxy, F, Cl, phenylcarbonyl, phenoxycarbonyl, phenylcarbonyloxy or phenoxy, methyl, ethyl, propyl or butyl;
   R1, R1a = 1-naphthyl, 2-naphthyl or H;
   R2, R2a = CH3, C2H5, n-C4H9, n-C6H13;
   p = 0-3;
   l = 0-3; and
   p+l = 3.
   EXAMPLE - Oven-dried schlenk tube containing a stirring bar was charged with nickel(II) salt (in an amount of one tenth the amount of 2-bromoheptane), 2,9-dimethyl-4,7-diphenyl-(1,10)phenanthroline (in an amount of one fourth the amount of 2-bromoheptane) and manganese (in an amount of 3.6 times the amount of 2-bromoheptane). The schlenk tube was then evacuated and back-filled under a carbon dioxide flow (this sequence was repeated three times) and finally an atmospheric pressure of carbon dioxide was established. 1 M Solution of 2-bromoheptane in the solvent was subsequently added by syringe and the solution was stirred for 17 hours at 50 degrees C. The mixture was then carefully quenched with 2 M aqueous hydrochloric solution to hydrolyze the resulting carboxylate and extracted with ethyl acetate. The resulted carboxylic acid was further purified by silica gel column chromatography using hexanes/ethyl acetate (3/1) as elution system to obtain carboxylation of 2-bromoheptane.

TI Cultivating Pleurotus eryngii mushrooms in bottle            without sparse buds by ensuring that mushroom            cultivation cycle, harvesting, releasing from warehouse            and performing summer mode or winter mode for mushroom            cultivation.
AU JIANG L
   ZHANG L
   TANG W
   YI H
   YANG G
   SHEN F
   JI J
AE HUNAN CHIC FOODS CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202292533J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cultivating Pleurotus                eryngiimushrooms in a bottle without sparse buds                involves ensuring that the mushroom cultivation                cycle is 19 days from the time when the culture                bottle enters the mushroom cultivation room,                harvesting on the day 16, releasing from the                warehouse on the day 19, when the daily minimum                temperature is more than 10&#8451;, performing                the summer mode for mushroom cultivation, when the                daily maximum temperature is lower than                10&#8451;, performing the winter mode to                cultivate mushrooms and when the daily minimum                temperature is lower than 10&#8451; and the                maximum temperature is higher than 10&#8451;,                performing summer mode or winter mode to cultivate                mushrooms.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating P.eryngii                in a bottle without sparse buds.
   ADVANTAGE - The method not only obviously increases the                proportion of small mushroom, but also increases                the yield of each bottle of mushroom. It saves the                cost of artificial thinning and reduces the working                amount.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for cultivating Pleurotus eryngii                mushrooms in a bottle without sparse buds involves                ensuring that the mushroom cultivation cycle is 19                days from the time when the culture bottle enters                the mushroom cultivation room, harvesting on the                day 16, releasing from the warehouse on the day 19,                when the daily minimum temperature is more than                10&#8451;, performing the summer mode for                mushroom cultivation, when the daily maximum                temperature is lower than 10&#8451;, performing                the winter mode to cultivate mushrooms and when the                daily minimum temperature is lower than                10&#8451; and the maximum temperature is higher                than 10&#8451;, performing summer mode or                winter mode to cultivate mushrooms. The summer mode                involves maintaining the temperature at                12-14&#8451;, humidity at 90-100% and carbon                dioxide concentration at 500-2000 ppm on day 1-2,                maintaining the temperature at 15-17&#8451;,                humidity at 92-100% and carbon dioxide                concentration at 800-3200 ppm on day 3-5,                maintaining the temperature at 15-18&#8451;,                humidity at 72-90% and carbon dioxide concentration                at 1400-2800 ppm on day 6-8, maintaining the                temperature at 16-18&#8451;, humidity at 70-86%                and carbon dioxide concentration at 1600-6000 ppm                on day 9-11, maintaining the temperature at                16-18&#8451;, humidity at 80-98% and carbon                dioxide concentration at 5000-11000 ppm on day                12-13, maintaining the temperature at                15-18&#8451;, humidity at 80-95% and carbon                dioxide concentration at 4000-9000 ppm on day                14-15, maintaining the temperature at                11-15&#8451;, humidity at 80-95% and carbon                dioxide concentration at 4400-7900 ppm on day 16-17                and maintaining the temperature at                10-12&#8451;, humidity at 80-95% and carbon                dioxide concentration at 2500-5000 ppm on day 18.                The winter mode involves maintaining the                temperature at 16-18&#8451;, humidity at                90-100% and carbon dioxide concentration at                600-1100 ppm on day 1-2, maintaining the                temperature at 16-18&#8451;, humidity at 88-98%                and carbon dioxide concentration at 900-2300 ppm on                day 3-5, maintaining the temperature at                15.5-18&#8451;, humidity at 75-90% and carbon                dioxide concentration at 1000-2200 ppm on day 6-8,                maintaining the temperature at 16-18&#8451;,                humidity at 66-85% and carbon dioxide concentration                at 1900-6000 ppm on day 9-11, maintaining the                temperature at 16-18&#8451;, humidity at 82-95%                and carbon dioxide concentration at 4200-7000 ppm                on day 12-13, maintaining the temperature at                12.5-16.5&#8451;, humidity at 88-98% and carbon                dioxide concentration at 4400-8000 ppm on day                14-15, maintaining the temperature at                9.5-12.5&#8451;, humidity at 85-98% and carbon                dioxide concentration at 4200-8300 ppm on day 16-17                and maintaining the temperature at                9-11.5&#8451;, humidity at 85-95% and carbon                dioxide concentration at 3600-6000 ppm on day 18.                The summer mode involves performing illumination on                days 3-8 and days 14-17 and performing no                illumination on days 1-2, days 9-13 and day 18. The                winter mode involves performing illumination on                days 3-8 and days 14-16 and performing no                illumination on days 1-2, days 9-13 and days                17-18.

AE HUAIYIN TECHNOLOGY INST (HUIA-C)

GA 202191296W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing carbon nanotubes/porous                carbon material and hydrogen-rich synthesis gas in                combination with biomass pyrolysis, involves (a)                washing and drying 50 mesh corn straw powder for                removing excess impurities, (b) dissolving nickel                chloride hexahydrate with calcium chloride                dihydrate in water to obtain mixed aqueous solution                of nickel chloride and calcium chloride, soaking                obtained corn straw powder in mixed aqueous                solution, uniformly stirring, contacting corn straw                powder with mixed solution of nickel chloride and                calcium chloride, and drying to obtain dried                biomass material loaded with nickel-calcium, (c)                heating dried biomass material loaded with                nickel-calcium under inert atmosphere, heating and                pyrolyzing dried biomass material, where the cooled                solid product is carbon nanotubes/porous carbon                material, and (d) performing purification on gas                product obtained by pyrolysis, and performing                carbon dioxide adsorption by calcium hydroxide                solution.

   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the comprehensive                utilization efficiency of biomass pyrolysis                products, and provides way for high-value                utilization of biomass resources.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The step (a) and (b)                  involves drying at 105 degrees C for 24-48 hours.                  The step (c) involves heating pyrolysis at                  800-1000 degrees C for 45-90 minutes. Preferred                  Composition: The step (b) involves soaking every                  1 g corn stalk powder in 150-2000 ml mixed                  aqueous solution of nickel chloride and calcium                  chloride, where the concentration of nickel                  chloride and calcium chloride is 0.001-0.005 mol.                  The mass ratio of calcium hydroxide in the                  calcium hydroxide solution in step (d) and dry                  biomass material loaded with nickel-calcium in                  step (c) is (2-3):1, and the concentration of                  calcium hydroxide solution is 0.002-0.005                  g/ml.

PN BE1027865-B1
TI Method for packaging sliced fresh raw vegetables, involves providing sealable plastic packaging and the sliced fresh raw vegetables are cooled and the sliced fresh raw vegetables are placed in sealable plastic packaging.
AU VERKINDEREN D
AE DV FRESH BVBA (DVFR-Non-standard)
GA 202194601S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing sealable plastic packaging. The sliced fresh raw vegetables are cooled. The sliced fresh raw vegetables are placed in sealable plastic packaging. The sealed sealable plastic packaging contains 25 percent carbon dioxide and 70 percent nitrogen. The sliced fresh raw vegetables are cooled for a minimum of 5 minutes with the cold air of a maximum of 7 degrees celsius. The degree of respiration of sliced fresh raw vegetables is measured at about 5 degrees celsius. The transparent bag has a bi-axially oriented polypropylene film (BOPP) with a thickness of up to 35 micrometers. The tray is closed with a foil. The foil has a layer of polyethylene terephthalate (PET) with a thickness of 12 micrometers and a layer of molded polypropylene (CPP) with a thickness of 30 micrometers.
   USE - Method for packaging sliced fresh raw vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - The sealed sealable plastic packaging is sufficient to maintain minimal respiration for multiple days and allows a sufficiently long shelf life. The amount of CO2 formed is limited, so that no additional measures are required for the sealable plastic packaging to deal with a pressure increase due to the increase in carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN CN112844494-A
TI Cycling regeneration of magnesium-based alkylation reaction catalyst by drying waste magnesium oxide catalyst, grinding, calcining, obtaining slurry, introducing carbon dioxide, obtaining powdery solid, adding additive agent and forming.
AU YUE Y
   WANG X
AE UNIV HEBEI ENG (UYEG-C)
GA 202161962P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cycling regeneration of a magnesium-based alkylation reaction catalyst involves (1) drying a waste magnesium oxide catalyst and pulverizing and grinding the waste magnesium oxide catalyst, (2) calcining the crushed waste magnesium oxide catalyst to remove carbon deposit, (3) adding the calcined catalyst to deionized water at a certain temperature and stirring at a constant temperature to obtain a slurry, (4) stirring the slurry at a high speed under the condition of constant temperature and continuously introducing carbon dioxide, (5) filtering the slurry obtained in the step (4) to obtain a filter cake and drying the filter cake to obtain a powdery solid and (6) taking the powdery solid as a precursor, adding an additive agent such as a copper component and graphite, granulating, tabletting and forming to obtain a regenerated catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for cycling regeneration of a magnesium-based alkylation reaction catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively recycle the waste magnesium oxide catalyst and turns waste into wealth, eliminates consumption of acid and alkali, avoids the treatment of salt-containing wastewater and adopts simple process, effective control of harmful substances in the whole process, high environmental and economic value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (2) involves calcining the waste magnesia catalyst powder at 300-700 degrees C for 2-6 hours. The method involves adding the waste magnesium oxide catalyst powder after calcining in step (3) to deionized water at 60-100 degrees C, where the constant-temperature stirring time is 1-5 hours and the mass of the waste magnesium oxide catalyst powder and deionized water is (1:5)-(1:500). The step (4) involves feeding the slurry with carbon dioxide gas, where the flow rate of carbon dioxide gas is controlled at 200-1200 milliliters per minute per 100 g waste magnesium oxide catalyst, the stirring speed is controlled at more than 300 rpm and the constant temperature is controlled at 60-100 degrees C. The step (5) involves subjecting the slurry to vacuum filtration, performing centrifugal separation or plate frame pressure filtration to obtain the filter cake and adopting the drying temperature of the filter cake of 60-150 degrees C and the drying time of 6-48 hours. The step (6) involves adding the copper component in the form of a salt solution containing copper ions, preferably a copper nitrate solution. Preferred Property: The crushed particles of the waste magnesium oxide catalyst in step (1) has a specification of 80-180 mesh sieve.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC D04-A01; J04-E04; J04-E05

PN CN112595023-A
TI Concentrating helium from natural gas involves filling the compressor with liquid hydrogen and output the natural gas to the booster equipment, adjusting the pressure of the booster equipment at the same time adjust compressor temperature.
AU ZHANG Z
AE SHANGHAI LIANFENG ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202136089H
AB    NOVELTY - Concentrating helium from natural gas involves filling the compressor with liquid hydrogen and output the natural gas to the booster equipment, adjusting the pressure of the booster equipment to 527 kiloPascal, and at the same time adjust the compressor temperature to -80 degrees C, at this time the carbon dioxide is in a liquid state, collecting liquid carbon dioxide, the remainder is natural gas, sulfide and helium. The pressure of the booster equipment is adjusted to 20 megaPascal, and at the same time adjust the compressor temperature to -165 degrees C, at this time the natural gas is liquid, collected the liquefied natural gas, the remainder is sulfide and helium, The sulfide and helium are purified and separated by activated carbon to obtain high-purity helium, injected high-purity helium into the cryogenic system, and adjusted the temperature to -270 degrees C in a high-pressure environment to obtain product.
   USE - Method for concentrating helium from natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the separation of different gas melting point, so as to realize the purpose of separating helium gas; the other gas generated according to different melting point is separated; and the purity of the helium gas is improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The compressor adjustment temperature is determined by the melting point of carbon dioxide. The method of collecting carbon dioxide is the drainage method, which is specifically to guide the liquid carbon dioxide from the bottom of the equipment, and adjust the pressure at the connecting end of the guide pipe and the equipment to be the same as the pressure in the equipment, and close the bottom valve after the guidance is completed. The compressor adjustment temperature is determined by the melting point of natural gas. The method for collecting liquid natural gas is the same as that for collecting carbon dioxide. The pressure of the guide pipe is adjusted to 20 mega Pascal, and a filter plate is installed between the guide pipe and the equipment to separate sulfide. The separation method of sulfide and helium is high-pressure purification and separation.

AE XU Q (XUQQ-Individual)
GA 202131557P

PN CN112246111-A; CN112246111-B
TI Preparing metal-organic framework film involves adding polydimethylsiloxane/polysulfone film with polyvinyl alcohol layer to metal salt aqueous solution, soaking, drying, adding to ligand aqueous solution, soaking, and drying.
AU QIAO Z
   YU C
   LIANG Y
   ZHONG C
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 202111352V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal-organic framework film involves: (1) adding polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS)/polysulfone (PS) film as a substrate to a polyvinyl alcohol (PVA) aqueous solution, soaking (A), and drying to form PVA layer on the PDMS/PS film to obtain a PDMS/PS film with PVA layer; (2) adding the PDMS/PS film with PVA layer to a metal salt aqueous solution, soaking (B), drying, covering the film with a layer of metal to obtain PDMS/PS film with metal ion modified PVA layer, where the metal salt aqueous solution is 3 %mass aqueous solution of metal nitrate, preferably cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, zinc nitrate hexahydrate or copper nitrate hexahydrate; and (3) adding the PDMS/PS film with metal ion modified PVA layer to 0.3-3.20 %mass ligand aqueous solution, soaking (C), drying, then cleaning the excess ligand, and drying. The ligand is 2-methylimidazole or 1,3,5-terephthalic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing metal-organic framework film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables environmentally-friendly preparation of the metal-organic framework film with dense surface, film thickness of 80-350 nm, and excellent carbon dioxide permeability and carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity. The whole preparation process is carried out in an aqueous solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ligand aqueous solution has a concentration of 1.06 %mass.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The cleaning step is performed by using anhydrous methanol or deionized water. Preferred Properties: The PVA aqueous solution has a concentration of 0.02-0.08 vol.%, a polymerization degree of 1700 and an alcoholysis degree of 87-89%.

PN WO2020152647-A1
TI Producing methanol by feeding syngas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide to methanol reactor to produce methanol reactor effluent stream and performing a series of separation processes to recover methanol.
AU KOLAH A
   ALAMRO M A
   ALGHAMDI M M
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 202071257D
AB    NOVELTY - Methanol is produced by (a) feeding syngas to methanol reactor to produce methanol reactor effluent stream, where syngas comprises hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), and carbon dioxide (CO2), and methanol reactor effluent stream comprises methanol, water, H2, CO, CO2, and wax compounds; (b) separating at least a portion of methanol reactor effluent stream in 1st separating unit into (i) liquid stream comprising methanol, water, and wax compounds and (ii) 1st vapor stream comprising H2, CO, CO2, methanol, and water; (c) separating at least a portion of liquid stream in 2nd separating unit into (i) wax stream comprising at least a portion of wax compounds of liquid stream and (ii) 2nd vapor stream comprising methanol and water; (d) separating at least a portion of 1st vapor stream in 3rd separating unit into (i) crude methanol stream comprising methanol and water and (ii) 3rd vapor stream comprising H2, CO, and CO2; and (e) recovering at least a portion of methanol.
   USE - Production of methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes the wax compounds, avoiding fouling of downstream process equipment upon cooling and solidification of the wax compounds. Accordingly, as the need to clean waxy solids from process equipment is greatly reduced, likewise, costly process downtime is greatly reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: The 1st portion of the 3rd vapor stream is recycled to the methanol reactor; and the 2nd portion of the 3rd vapor stream is purged and/or used as fuel. The method further comprises (i) cooling at least a portion of methanol reactor effluent stream in 1st heat exchanger to yield cooled methanol reactor effluent stream, where at least a portion of the cooled methanol reactor effluent stream is introduced to the 1st separating unit in step (b); and/or (ii) cooling at least a portion of 1st vapor stream in a 2nd heat exchanger to yield a cooled 1st vapor stream, where at least a portion of the cooled 1st vapor stream is introduced to the 3rd separating unit in step (d). The method further comprises heating at least a portion of the syngas in the 1st heat exchanger and/or the 2nd heat exchanger to yield heated syngas, where at least a portion of the heated syngas is fed to the methanol reactor in step (a). The method further comprises (1) contacting at least a portion of crude methanol stream and at least a portion of 2nd vapor stream to form combined crude methanol stream; and (2) separating at least a portion of combined crude methanol stream in a distillation unit into a methanol stream and a water stream, where the distillation unit comprises greater than or equal to 1 distillation columns. The method further includes recycling greater than or equal to 80 wt.% of the 3rd vapor stream, based on the total weight of the 3rd vapor stream, to the methanol reactor; and optionally heating at least a portion of the syngas in the 1st heat exchanger and/or the 2nd heat exchanger to yield heated syngas, where at least a portion of the heated syngas is fed to the methanol reactor in step (a). Preferred Compositions: Amount of 1st portion of the 3rd vapor stream is 50-100 wt.%; and amount of 2nd portion of the 3rd vapor stream is 0-50 wt.%. At least a portion of the wax stream is in liquid phase at the 2nd separating unit temperature and at the 2nd separating unit pressure. Preferred Conditions: The 1st separating unit and/or the 3rd separating unit operate at a pressure that is about the same as the methanol reactor pressure. Methanol reactor pressure is 40-100 barg, preferably 80-100 barg. The 2nd separating unit operates at a 2nd separating unit pressure, which is lower than the methanol reactor pressure. The 2nd separating unit pressure is less than or equal to 30 barg, preferably less than or equal to 20 barg. Methanol reactor temperature is greater than 1st separating unit temperature, where 1st separating unit temperature is equal to or greater than 2nd separating unit temperature; and 3rd separating unit temperature is less than 1st separating unit temperature and/or 2nd separating unit temperature. Methanol reactor temperature is 170-250 degrees C, preferably 190-230 degrees C. The 1st separating unit temperature is 100-180 degrees C, preferably 120-160 degrees C. The 2nd separating unit temperature is 80-160 degrees C, preferably 100-140 degrees C. The 3rd separating unit temperature is 20-75 degrees C, preferably 25-65 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Wax compounds comprise C20+ compounds, C20+ hydrocarbons, C20+ alcohols, and/or C20+ ethers.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves heating the activated carbon raw material such as wood or fruit at 600-700 degrees for 5-30 min to obtain carbonized material. The obtained carbonized material is activated through an activation reaction to prepare activated carbon. The activation method of the activation reaction is microwave irradiation heating activation. The activated gas is carbon dioxide or a mixture of carbon dioxide and water vapor. The pressure is 0.4-1.0MPa, the activation temperature is 850-900 degrees , and the activation time is 30-60min. An activation reactor main portion is provided with a circulating gas inlet. The activation reactor main portion is provided with a discharging port.

PT P
PN WO2020058252-A1; WO2020058252-A9; EP3682901-A1; AU2019341523-A1; CN112770777-A; AR116461-A1; BR112021002719-A2; KR2021061379-A; EP3852807-A1; JP2022500450-W; US2022047511-A1; IN202117017127-A
TI Particulate excipient used in pharmaceutical and nutraceutical composition comprises microcrystalline cellulose, and surface-reacted calcium carbonate.
AU DE MIGUEL L
   LANDER S
   RANDALL S
   MIGUEL L D
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 2020237304
AB    NOVELTY - Particulate excipient comprises (a) microcrystalline cellulose, and (b) surface-reacted calcium carbonate, where the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and at least one H3O+ ion donors and the carbon dioxide is formed in situ by the H3O+ ion donors treatment and/or is supplied from an external source, where the wt. ratio of the microcrystalline cellulose to the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is 99.9:0.1-50:50.
   USE - The excipient is useful in pharmaceutical and nutraceutical composition (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The excipient has a loose bulk density of 0.25-0.9 (preferably 0.25-0.65g/ml) (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing (M1) the particulate excipient according comprising (a1) mixing microcrystalline cellulose and the surface-reacted calcium carbonate, (b1) co-processing the mixture obtained in step (a1), thus obtaining the particulate excipient;
   (2) composition comprising the particulate excipient and optionally at least one active ingredient, inactive pharmaceutical or nutraceutical precursors, biologically active ingredients and/or inactive biological precursors; and
   (3) manufacturing (M2) a pharmaceutical or nutraceutical composition comprising (a2) providing the particulate excipient and (b2) subjecting the particulate excipient to dry granulation, wet granulation, melt granulation or direct compression (preferred), thus obtaining the composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The wt. ratio of the microcrystalline cellulose to the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is 99.9:0.1-75:25, preferably 98:2-90:10. The excipient comprises co-processed microcrystalline cellulose and surface-reacted calcium carbonate in that the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is in intimate association with the microcrystalline cellulose. The composition further comprises at least one adjuvant comprising natural or synthetic scenting agent, natural or synthetic flavoring agent, natural or synthetic coloring agent, natural or synthetic sweeteners, lubricants, disintegrant and/or glidant. Preferred Process: The co-processing step (b1) is performed by dry- or wet-processing, preferably high-shear mixing, spray drying and/or milling. The mixing step (a1) is performed in an aqueous medium so as to form aqueous slurry comprising the microcrystalline cellulose and the surface-reacted calcium carbonate. The process (M2) further comprises a step (c) contacting the particulate excipient before step (b2) or the composition obtained in step (b2) with at least one active ingredient. Preferred Components: The surface-reacted calcium carbonate has (i) a volume median particle size d50 of 0.5-50 mu m, preferably 1.5-15 mu m, (ii) a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) specific surface area of 5-200 m2/g, preferably 40-100 m2/g, measured using nitrogen and the BET method according to ISO 9277:2010, and/or (iii) an intra-particle intruded specific pore volume 0.1-2.3 (preferably 0.6-1.6) cm3/g, calculated from mercury porosimetry measurement. The (i) the natural ground calcium carbonate comprises marble, chalk, and/or limestone, or (ii) the precipitated calcium carbonate comprises precipitated calcium carbonates having an aragonitic, vateritic and/or calcitic crystal form. The H3O+ ion donor comprises hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid (preferred), citric acid, oxalic acid, an acidic salt acetic acid, and/or formic acid, dihydrogen phosphate, being at least partially neutralized by a cation comprising lithium+, sodium+ and/or potassium+, magnesium 2+, and/or calcium 2+. The microcrystalline cellulose has a loose bulk density 0.2-0.52 (preferably 0.26-0.36 g/ml), and/or a weight median particle size d50 10-1000 mu m, preferably 20-200 mu m.
   EXAMPLE - Typical excipient comprised of: 75% microcrystalline cellulose; and 25% Surface-reacted calcium carbonate.

TI Manufacture of lithium carbonate for lithium-ion            battery, involves using stirrer with stirring blade            whose diameter is set to satisfy specific relation with            internal diameter of reaction tank, and wet-treating            lithium-ion battery waste.
AU ARIYOSHI H
   TOMITA I
   ABE H
AE JX NIPPON MINING & METALS CORP (NIHA-C)
   NIPPON MINING & METALS CO LTD (NIHA-C)
   NIPPON MINING & METALS CO LTD (NIHA-C)
   NIPPON MINING & METALS CO LTD (NIHA-C)
GA 202019254M
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of lithium carbonate involves                performing wet treatment on a lithium-ion battery                waste comprising a positive electrode material                component comprising at least one metal chosen from                cobalt, nickel, manganese, and lithium, separating                at least one metal from the battery waste to obtain                a crude lithium carbonate, dissolving the crude                lithium carbonate in a liquid (2) by stirring the                liquid with a stirrer (3) within a reaction tank                (1) under the supply of carbon dioxide gas, and                decarboxylating by heating the lithium solution and                desorbing carbonic acid. The ratio of diameter (d)                of a stirring blade (4) of the stirrer with respect                to an internal diameter (D) of the reaction tank is                0.2-0.5.
   USE - Manufacture of lithium carbonate used for                lithium-ion battery for mobile telephone and motor                vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces lithium carbonate by                effectively improving gas reaction efficiency when                dissolving the crude lithium carbonate in liquid                under supply of carbon dioxide gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                reaction tank used in dissolution process.
   Reaction tank (1)
   Liquid (2)
   Stirrer (3)
   Stirring blade (4)
   Internal diameter of reaction tank (D)
   Diameter of stirring blade (d)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The ratio of stirring                  power of the stirrer with respect to volume of                  the liquid charged with the crude lithium                  carbonate is 0.3-1 kW/m3. The circumferential                  speed of the stirrer is 1.3-1.9 m/second. The                  supply rate of carbon dioxide gas to the liquid                  is 0.6 L/minute/L or less. Preferred Process:                  Manufacture of lithium carbonate further involves                  washing, before dissolving, the crude lithium                  carbonate by re-pulping the crude lithium                  carbonate with pure water which is 0.5-2 times                  the wet weight of the crude lithium carbonate for                  several times, supplying carbonate ions to pure                  water to dissolve the crude lithium carbonate,                  neutralizing, after separating at least one                  metal, an acidic solution comprising nickel ions                  and magnesium ions by increasing pH of the acidic                  solution to 12-13, or comprising nickel ions and                  no magnesium ions by increasing pH of the acidic                  solution to 10-10.5, and carbonizing lithium in                  the neutralized solution. During dissolving of                  crude lithium carbonate, assuming that the entire                  amount of crude lithium carbonate is dissolved in                  pure water at 25 degrees C, pure water is used in                  such an amount that the lithium concentration                  becomes 7-9 g/L.

PN CN110403109-A
TI Preparing bubble grape juice comprises soaking            grape fruit, freezing, crushing, adding dry ice,            gravity pressing, filtering, adding e.g. fructose syrup            and honey, sterilizing, filtering, adding steam-water            mix and carbon dioxide and filling.
AU LEI B
AE NINGXIA SHENGYUANCHUN FOOD CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201994846Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bubble grape juice comprises (a)                soaking freshly picked grape fruit in sodium                chloride solution at 0-4 degrees C for 60-90                minutes, (b) placing at -25 to -30 degrees C for                quick freezing of ice, quickly crushing the grapes,                passing the grape juice into a dipping tank for                freezing and soaking, adding dry ice into the grape                juice for oxygen and temperature controlling and                controlling the whole process at 1-3 degrees C, (c)                gravity pressing the grape juice dipping tank to                obtain grape juice, filtering, adding fructose                syrup, citric acid, honey, malic acid, spirulina,                stabilizing agent and water to obtain a mixed                solution, (d) homogenizing the mixed solution to                obtain a homogeneous solution and sterilizing and                (e) cooling the homogeneous liquid to 0-2 degrees                C, filtering, adding steam-water mix, fully mixing                with carbon dioxide until saturated and                filling.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing bubble                grape juice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enhances the nutritional value of                grape juice by adding spirulina; reduces the damage                of aromatic substances; and enhances the aroma of                grape juice.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The concentration of                  sodium chloride in the sodium chloride solution                  in the step (a) is 3-8%. The step (c) comprises                  adding 45-55 wt.% grape juice, 3-8 wt.% fructose                  syrup, 0.1-0.3 wt.% citric acid, 5-8 wt.% honey,                  0.2-0.3 wt.% malic acid, 8-12 wt.% spirulina,                  0.1-0.2 wt.% stabilizing agent and the remaining                  amount of water. Preferred Method: The                  sterilization in the step (d) is ultraviolet                  sterilization at 20-25 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN110026660-A
TI Welding arc stud by balanced magnetic field control comprises e.g. cleaning the surface of the galvanized pipe, vertically contacting the stud to the welded surface of the galvanized pipe, (iii) opening the welding shielding gas.
AU LI J
AE FUNISI INTELLIGENT EQUIP ZHUHAI CO LTD (FUNI-Non-standard)
GA 201965342F
AB    NOVELTY - Welding arc stud by balanced magnetic field control comprises (i) cleaning the surface of the galvanized pipe, (ii) vertically contacting the stud to the welded surface of the galvanized pipe, (iii) opening the welding shielding gas, where the welding shielding gas is a mixed gas of argon gas and carbon dioxide, and the volume ratio of argon gas to carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 41/9, (iv) lifting the stud to generate pre-welding arc between the stud and the galvanized pipe welding surface, and (v) after stabilizing the arc ignition, and performing high current welding, in the steps (iv) and (v), the coil of the welding torch passes a current of 225 mA, and the direction of the current is disposed along the axial direction of the stud to form a magnetic field that rotates about the axis of the stud.
   USE - The method is useful for welding arc stud by balanced magnetic field control.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids problems e.g. large splash, forming arc, and transitional burning of zinc layer around the arc when welding the stud on the galvanized pipe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), wiping the surface of the galvanized tube with industrial alcohol or acetone to remove oil stains. In the step (iv), the pre-arcing current is 160 amps, and the pre-arcing time is 100 milliseconds. In the step (v), the welding current is 1200 amps, the welding time is 63 milliseconds, and the lifting height between the stud and the galvanized pipe welding surface is 1 mm. Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the gas flow rate of the welding shielding gas is 10 l/minute. The stud extension is 1.1 mm. In the step (iii), the duration of the welding shielding gas is 1000 milliseconds.

PT P
PN CN109939705-A; CN109939705-B
TI Preparing multi-biomass composite aerogel material for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves dispersing reduced graphene oxide/lanthanum(III) phosphate complex, adding soluble alginate, defoaming, drying, and solidifying.
AU TIAN Q
   ZHUANG J
   CAO Z
   LIU M
   XIE W
   DAI J
AE UNIV FUJIAN AGRIC & FORESTRY (UYFN-C)
GA 2019584999
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of multi-biomass composite aerogel material involves (a) dissolving graphene oxide, lanthanum(III) nitrate hexahydrate and diammonium phosphate in absolute ethanol, stirring for 30 minutes, transferring the solution to a reactor, reacting for 24 hours at 180 degrees C constant temperature, naturally cooling to room temperature, repeatedly washing the precipitate with deionized water, and drying at 60 degrees C to obtain reduced graphene oxide/lanthanum(III) phosphate complex, (b) ultrasonically dispersing the compound obtained in step (a) to uniform in water to obtain suspension, (c) adding soluble alginate to suspension, vigorously stirring to dissolve completely, and continuously stirring for 1-4 hours to obtain mixed solution, (d) defoaming the mixed solution, pouring into a mold, freezing and drying to obtain block material, and (e) putting the block material into curing agent solution to solidify, freezing and drying.
   USE - Preparation method of multi-biomass composite aerogel material for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (all claimed) and water to produce carbon monoxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of multi-biomass composite aerogel material with controllable shape, excellent strength, low density, large specific surface area, and easy recycling, by simple process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of graphene oxide, lanthanum(III) nitrate hexahydrate and diammonium phosphate is (0.010-0.1):1:(0.95-1). The concentration of the reduced graphene oxide/lanthanum(III) phosphate complex in the suspension obtained in the step (b) is 0.001-3 g/L. The concentration of soluble alginate in the mixed solution in step (c) is 1-10 g/L. The concentration of the curing agent solution is 10-30 g/L. Preferred Component: The curing agent is alkaline earth metal salt or trivalent metal salt. Preferred Conditions: In the step (d), the ultrasonication is used for defoaming, the ultrasonic time is 0-2 hours, and after ultrasound, the vacuum pumping is performed for 0-1 hour. The solidifying time in the step (e) is 5 minutes to 1 hour. The freeze-drying in step (e) is carried out at a rate of 0.1-10 degrees C/minute to -50 to -10 degrees C, freezing for 0.5-6 hours, and at -15 to 25 degrees C, and the degree of vacuum is 1-2000 Pa, and drying for 12-48 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The soluble alginate includes sodium alginate, potassium alginate, and/or propylene glycol alginate.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

AE DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
   DAIKIN KOGYO KK (DAIK-C)

   DAIKIN IND LTD (DAIK-C)
GA 201951811F
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises a refrigerant including carbon dioxide, difluoromethane (R32), pentafluoroethane (R125) and 1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane (R134a) in an amount (in %mass) of x, a, b, and c, respectively, where x is 2.7-6, and coordinates (a, b, c) on a ternary composition diagram wherein the total mass% of R32, R125, and R134a is (100- x) %mass are situated within a triangular range bounded by line segments connecting point E ((-0.0781x2+1.8309x+41.926, 1.1194x+19.094, 100-a-b-x)), point F ((-3x+54.4, -0.3945x+23.17, 100-a-b-x)), and point G ((-2.0597x+51.853, -2.1514x+27.908, 100-a-b-x)) or uponline segments, or x is 6 or more and less than 9, and coordinates (a, b, c) are situated within triangular range bounded by line segments connecting point E ((x+44.1, 1.0357x+19.593, 100-a-b-x)), point F ((-3x+54.4, -0.4x+23.2, 100-a-b-x)), and point G ((-2.1643x+52.493, -2.1x+27.6, 100-a-b-x)) or upon line segments.

PN CN109627281-A
TI Preparing Camellia tea saponin, comprises e.g. crushing-sieving husked Camellia tea seed, extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide and extracting Camellia tea saponin from deoiled Camellia tea seed powder by aqueous solution of ethanol.
AU ZENG J
   LING L
   LI Y
   DONG Y
AE HUNAN HEALTH-GUARD BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 201938598P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Camellia tea saponin, comprises (a) pulverizing and sieving the husked Camellia tea seed, and extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain oil Camellia tea seed oil and deoiled Camellia tea seed powder; (b) extracting Camellia tea saponin from deoiled Camellia tea seed powder at 20-40 degrees C with an aqueous solution of ethanol as a solvent to obtain an extract; (c) filtering the extract to obtain a filtrate and a filter residue; and (d) concentrating the filtrate, adding a flocculating agent, flocculating, filtering and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Camellia tea saponin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes rapid and complete dissolution in water at room temperature, does not cause precipitation and turbidity, produces product which is convenient to use when used as a detergent component, has good washing performance, and is suitable for wide washing range, thus it prevents saponin prepared by other methods dyes the clothes or turns the skin yellow when used.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for Camellia tea saponin comprising Camellia tea seed oil, deoiled oil Camellia seed powder, Camellia tea saponin, crude protein and crude polysaccharide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The amount of aqueous ethanol solution in the step (b) is 50-90 wt.%, and the extraction temperature is 30-40 degrees C. The concentration of aqueous ethanol solution is 75-90 wt.%. The extraction is performed twice, each time for 30-50 minutes, and/or the mass ratio of aqueous ethanol solution and deoiled Camellia seed powder is (4-8):1. The concentration according to the step (d) comprises: concentrating under reduced pressure at 50-65 degrees C until the mass of the concentrate is 1/5-1/7 of the total mass of the extract, and the solid content in the concentrate is 35-55%, and/or the drying by vacuum drying, spray drying or freeze drying. The flocculating agent in the step (d) is 1-5% chitosan solution, and the flocculation condition is adding flocculating agent is added in an amount of 10-30 wt.% of the concentrating, the flocculation temperature is 20-60 degrees C and flocculation time is 1-3 hours. The extraction temperature is 35-45 MPa, and the extraction temperature is 40-50 degrees C and extraction time is 2-3 hours; the pressure of the first stage separation tank is 14-16 MPa, the temperature is 45-55 degrees C and the pressure of the second stage separation tank is 11-13 MPa, temperature is 40-50 degrees C and the third stage separation kettle pressure is 4-6 MPa and the temperature is 25-35 degrees C, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 13-15 kg/hour/kg of raw material. The method further comprises drying the filtrate obtained in the filtration step in the step (c) to obtain a mixture of crude oil of the Camellia seed and the crude polysaccharide.

PT P
PN CN108622965-A
TI Plasma evaporation process using a hypochlorite solution as a target for excitation comprises ejecting plasma from bottom of evaporation chamber and directly combining with liquid for heat and mass transfer, discharging and condensing.
AU YIN H
AE JIANGSU WOQING ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201880652E
AB    NOVELTY - Plasma evaporation process using a hypochlorite solution as a target for excitation comprises ejecting plasma from the bottom of the evaporation chamber and directly combining with the liquid for heat and mass transfer, where the hypochlorite solution is used as an excitation target and a temperature of 3700-3900 degrees C by a 230-240 A DC current discharge method, the liquid absorbs heat and evaporates, discharging generated steam together with the plasma to the evaporation chamber, where the large amount of oxychloride free radicals, chlorine radicals, hydroxy radicals and high-energy electrons carried in the plasma have strong oxidizing ability, which can decompose volatile organic pollutants in steam into carbon dioxide and water and improves the evaporation process and the removal rate of organic pollutants in wastewater and condensing steam to give the evaporator water.
   USE - The method is useful for plasma evaporation process using a hypochlorite solution as a target for excitation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high-energy electron, strong oxidation resistance, steam can be in volatile organic contaminant decomposed into carbon dioxide and water, improves the evaporation process to the removal rate of the organic pollutant in the waste water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hypochlorite solution is the excitation target of the plasma. The hypochlorite is specifically sodium hypochlorite and calcium hypochlorite. Due to the difference in wastewater quality, the amount of hypochlorite used is determined by a small test. The plasma is generated by a current discharge of 230-240 A. The generated plasma temperature is 3,700-3,900 degrees C. The large amount of oxychloride radicals, chlorine radicals, hydroxy radicals and high-energy electrons carried in the plasma have strong oxidizing power and are capable of degrading volatile organic pollutants in steam.

AU WANG J
   LONG Y
   SU Z
   YE Y
   LIANG H
   JIANG Z
   ZHU D
   LIU L
   WEI J
   XU C
   JIANG J
AE GUANGXI MEDICAL UNI PHARM FACTORY (UGXM-C)
   UNIV GUANGXI MEDICAL (UGXM-C)
GA 201855768P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Phyllanthus emblica extract, comprises (i) (i) crushingPhyllanthus emblicaleaves, adding soaking liquid and soaking for 5-10 hours; (ii) adding water to the soakedPhyllanthus emblicaleaves for crushing and pulping, adding cellulase to the obtained slurry for enzymolysis treatment to obtain enzymolysis solution; (iii) performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction by placing the enzymolysis liquid into an extractor, where the extraction temperature is 35-45 degrees C, pressure is 20-30 MPa, the extracting flow speed is 50-65 l/hour and the time is 1-2 hours; (iv) placing the extracted mixture into a separation tank for separating at pressure of 5-8 MPa and temperature of 25-30 degrees C to obtain a crude extract; and (v) fractionating the crude extract, collecting the fraction at 150-660 degrees C, freezing and drying.
   USE - The composition and extract are useful in the treatment or auxiliary treatment of AIDS (all claimed)..
   ADVANTAGE - The method easily retains the active components in the leaves of Phyllanthus emblica extract, thus improving the quality, and also shortens the extraction time; and produces product having high purity and providing strong physique and improve the quality of life of the patients.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a composition comprising Phyllanthus emblica extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the soaking liquid is distilled water or an ethanol solution having a volume concentration of 50-80%. In the step (ii), the solid-liquid ratio of the slurry is 1:1-3, the amount of the cellulase is the total mass of the slurry of 0.1-0.3%, the temperature of the enzymatic treatment is 32-35 degrees C, and the enzymatic hydrolysis time is 1.5-2.5 hours. In the step (iii), the extraction temperature is 40 degrees C, the pressure is 25 MPa, the extraction flow rate is 55 l/hour, and the time is 1.5 hours. In the step (v), the pressure of the freeze-drying is 15-25 Pa, the temperature is -20 degrees C to -25 degrees C, and the time is 1-2 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The composition is prepared in the form of granules, tablets, oral liquids, capsules, liniment, pastes or injection by adding medicinal adjuvants to the extract.
   ACTIVITY - Anti-HIV.

PT P
PN US2020271310-A1
TI Generating electricity by converting carbon to hydrogen and carbon dioxide in gas generating unit, removing carbon dioxide, compressing hydrogen, combusting in combustor, and passing to steam turbine connected to electrical generator.
AU BILLINGS C
AE BILLINGS C (BILL-Individual)
GA 202082685N
AB    NOVELTY - Process for generating electricity, involves converting carbon to hydrogen and carbon dioxide in a gas generating unit and removing the carbon dioxide, compressing the resulting hydrogen, combusting it in a high-pressure combustor, and passing the resulting gas product to a steam turbine connected to an electrical generator.
   USE - The process is used for generating electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The process generates electricity in a cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a combination process for producing hydrogen; and
   (2) a process for producing hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Process: The capacity of the gas generating unit is sized to produce 60-80% hydrogen needed to operate the steam turbine at full rated capacity, and excess hydrogen from the gas generation unit is stored when the electrical generating capacity of the generator exceeds demand and utilizing the stored hydrogen to add additional feed to the burner when electrical generating demand exceeds the capacity of hydrogen afforded by the gas generating unit. The step of converting carbon to hydrogen and carbon dioxide is accomplished in a process comprising performing gasification of coal and coke to produce a gas containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide in a molten chloride salt mix of potassium chloride and calcium chloride at 1700 degrees F, and removing the carbon dioxide from the gas so produced by contacting with a molten salt mixture comprising lithium orthosilicate maintained at 1075 degrees F. In the method, finely ground limestone is added to the salt as sulfur absorbent. The step of converting carbon to hydrogen and carbon dioxide is accomplished in a process comprising performing gasification of natural gas to produce a gas containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide in tubular reactor having tubes containing a suitable catalyst, where the tubes are submerged in a molten salt bath maintained at 1300 degrees F to provide 1200 degrees F inside the tubes of the tubular reactor.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M)); Q72 (Steam generation (F22))

PN CN111303380-A; CN111303380-B
TI Preparing carbon dioxide-releasing polyurea polyol comprises mixing polyether polyol with hydrazine to obtain mixture, adding diisocyanate into mixture and removing residual diisocyanate monomer in product.
AU LI F
   LIU Y
   QIN C
   JU C
   LIU B
   LV S
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 2020593019
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide-releasing polyurea polyol comprises (i) mixing the polyether polyol with hydrazine to obtain a mixture; (ii) adding diisocyanate into the mixture in step (i); and (iii) removing the residual diisocyanate monomer in the product of step (ii).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon dioxide-releasing polyurea polyol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can reduce the amount of using foaming agent and produces foam with high hardness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) carbon dioxide-releasing polyurea polyol, prepared as mentioned above; and
   (2) a polyurethane foam prepared by using the polyurea polyol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the hydrazine is anhydrous hydrazine and/or hydrazine hydrate, preferably hydrazine hydrate. In the step (ii), the diisocyanate is diisocyanate A containing an oxadiazinone structure or a mixture of diisocyanate A and diisocyanate B, preferably a mixture of diisocyanate A and diisocyanate B. In the step (ii), the diisocyanate B is aliphatic isocyanate and/or aromatic polyisocyanate, preferably isophthalic diisocyanate, terephthalic diisocyanate (PPDI), polymethyl polyphenyl polyisocyanate, 2,4-toluene diisocyanate (TDI), 2,6-toluene diisocyanate, naphthalene diisocyanate, diphenylmethane 4,4'-diisocyanate (MDI), diphenylmethane 2,4-diisocyanate, diphenylmethane 2,2'-diisocyanate and hexamethylene diisocyanate (HDI), preferably mixture of 2,4-toluene diisocyanate and 2,6-toluene diisocyanate. In the step (ii), the weight ratio of the diisocyanate to the polyether polyol is (0.05-0.45):1 and/or, the ratio of the number of moles of cyanate ion in the diisocyanate to the number of moles of amino groups of hydrazine is (0.8-1.2):1. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the preparation of diisocyanate A comprises reacting an isocyanate containing a C6-C12 alkyl substituent group with carbon dioxide, preferably the diisocyanate A is obtained by reacting at least one of hexamethylene diisocyanate, cyclohexane methyl diisocyanate or tetramethyl butane diisocyanate with carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the preparation of polyether polyol comprises carrying out ring-opening polymerization of propylene oxide and ethylene oxide using a small molecule polyol containing 2-6 hydroxy groups as an initiator, preferably the hydroxy value of the polyether polyol is 10-80 mg potassium hydroxide/g, preferably 20-40 mg potassium hydroxide/g.

PT P
PN WO2018178831-A1
TI Producing phenol involves contacting zeolite supported catalyst with gaseous reaction mixture comprising benzene, oxygen and carbon dioxide to produce product stream comprising phenol.
AU ALMUSAITEER K
   PARK S E
   KULKARNI R
   MO Y H
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 2018776493
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing phenol involves contacting a zeolite supported catalyst with a gaseous reaction mixture comprises benzene, oxygen and carbon dioxide under conditions sufficient to produce a product stream comprising phenol. The molar ratio of oxygen to carbon dioxide is 0.1:99.9-99.9:0.1.
   USE - The process is useful for producing phenol using zeolite supported catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The process increases catalytic conversion and phenol selectivity, is economical and saves energy, maximizes the overall conversion of benzene to phenol and increases efficiency. The use of oxygen/carbon dioxide mixture promotes oxygen activity, while limiting coke formation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zeolite supported catalyst comprises a transition metal or its oxo species. The transition metal comprises copper, cobalt, iron, vanadium, molybdenum, tungsten or their combination, preferably copper. The zeolite supported catalyst further comprises a modifier. The modifier comprises an alkali metal, an alkaline earth metal, a column 4 metal, a transition metal, a noble metal or their combination. The alkali metal cation comprises sodium, potassium, rubidium or their combination. The alkaline earth metal cation comprises barium. The transition metal comprises chromium, copper, cobalt, iron, titanium or their mixtures. The noble metal comprises palladium, silver, gold, platinum or their combination. The zeolite support comprises a MFI zeolite (preferred), BEA, MOR or FAU framework structure. The zeolite support is a hydrogen-zeolite socony mobil-5 (H-ZSM-5) support and the zeolite catalyst is copper/H-ZSM-5.Preferred Composition: The total metal content in the zeolite supported catalyst is 10-300 mol%, preferably 30-200 mol% based on the degree of ion exchange capacity of the zeolite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of oxygen to carbon dioxide is 0.1:20. Preferred Process: The process is carried out at a temperature of 200-650 degrees C, preferably 300-500 degrees C, a pressure of 1-40 and a weight hourly space velocity of 0.01-10 per hour, preferably 0.2-0.8 per hour. The phenol selectivity is at least 2 times, preferably 4 times the selectivity of the process in the absence of carbon dioxide. The reaction temperature is 500 degrees C, the benzene conversion is at least 80% and the phenol selectivity is at least 40%. The reaction mixture is obtained by combining a gaseous benzene stream and a gaseous oxidizing stream. The benzene conversion is at least 10%, preferably at least 80%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN108607512-A; CN108607512-B
TI Preparation of molybdenum-based sulfide-modified metal organic framework (MOF) material for e.g. catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide, involves immobilizing molybdenum-based sulfide on MOF to obtain material with sulfide active center.
AU LIU Y
   PENG J
   WANG A
   SUN Z
   LI X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201880474N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of molybdenum-based sulfide-modified MOF material involves immobilizing molybdenum-based sulfide on the MOF material to obtain modified MOF material containing monodisperse molybdenum-based sulfide active center.
   USE - Preparation of molybdenum-based sulfide-modified MOF material (claimed) for carbon dioxide related gas adsorption separation and catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces modified MOF material which has effectively improved chemical adsorption capacity and selectivity to carbon dioxide, and high chemisorption energy in low pressure range, and the modification method has versatility for porous material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves (1) adding molybdenum-based sulfide precursor to raw material liquid, in a molar ratio of 0.05-1, or (2) contacting raw material liquid with the molybdenum-based sulfide precursor. The step (1) involves ultrasonically dispersing molybdenum-based sulfide precursor, and raw material liquid comprising organic ligand and metal salt, in organic solvent, reacting at 110-160 degrees C for 8-120 hours, cooling the reaction system to room temperature, suction filtering, washing to obtain powder sample, dispersing in organic solvent selected from N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-diethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, dioxane, and dimethyl sulfoxide, stirring at 40-80 degrees C for 1-3 hours, extracting by Soxhlet extractor using methanol or ethanol as solvent, for 12-24 hours, drying the product, and activating. The step (2) involves ultrasonically dispersing organic ligand and metal salt in organic solvent, reacting at 110-160 degrees C for 8-120 hours, cooling the reaction system to room temperature, suction filtering, washing to obtain powder sample, dispersing the powder sample in molybdenum-based sulfide precursor solution, heating and stirring at 40-80 degrees C for 1-3 hours, suction filtering, washing, drying, and activating. Preferred Components: The molybdenum-based sulfide precursor is ammonium tetrathiomolybdate. The metal salt is selected from salts of transition metals, boron group metals, and lanthanide metals. In step (2), the solvent of the molybdenum-based sulfide precursor solution is selected from water, dichloromethane, and chloroform. The raw material liquid is used for preparing MOF material. Preferred Composition: In the step (2), the molar ratio of the molybdenum-based sulfide precursor to organic ligand is 0.1-1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic ligand comprises 2,2'-bipyridine chelate structure of formula (I).R1,R2 = -COOH, -CN, -OH, -B(OH)2, -CHO, -PO(OH)2, or -NH2.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Pipe cleaning device for water and sewage, has            pressurizing unit that supplies pressure to mixture            supply unit to supply pressurized carbon dioxide gas            and ice slush mixture through pipe, and calcium            chloride supply unit supplies calcium chloride to            storage tank.
AU KIM C
AE KIM C (KIMC-Individual)
GA 2022C7305T
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an ice slush storage tank (10)                configured for supplying ice sludge for cleaning                pipes (1) by contacting with deposits attached to                an inner wall of water and sewage pipes. A carbon                dioxide storage tank (20) supplies carbon dioxide                gas to generate carbonated water to assist in                removal of attachments attached to the inner wall                of the pipe. A mixture supply unit (50) temporarily                stores a mixture of the gas and the sludge supplied                from a mixture supply pipe (40). A calcium chloride                supply unit (12) supplies calcium chloride to the                storage tank to prevent the ice slush stored in the                ice slush storage tank from melting. A carbon                dioxide recovery unit is provided at the other end                of the pipe to be cleaned and supplied from the                carbon dioxide storage tank to recover the carbon                dioxide gas used for cleaning the pipe from the                mixture of carbon dioxide gas and ice slush mixed                in a mixing tank (30).
   USE - Pipe cleaning device for water and                sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus cleans the pipe by injecting ice                slush into all sections of the pipe regardless of                pipe size and shape of a valve, maintains dense                density, and enables perfect pipe cleaning.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                pipe cleaning device. (Drawing includes non-English                language text)

   10Ice slush storage tank
   12Calcium chloride supply unit
   20Carbon dioxide storage tank

   50Mixture supply unit

The outer side of the plunger                clapboard is provided with a piston sheet (301)                jointed with the inner wall of the box body. The                box body and the plunger clapboard are                intermediately provided with a breathing                ventilating mechanism for air circulation flow in                the upper and lower half cavity of the box                body.
   USE - Used as a carbon dioxide culture box.
   ADVANTAGE - The culture box achieves circulating                ventilation between the upper and lower cavities of                the box body during the daily working process,                maintains balance state of carbon dioxide                concentration, reduces carbon dioxide concentration                deviation problem of the box body and hence improve                the culture effect of the carbon dioxide culture                box.

AB    NOVELTY - The capturing device has an absorbing                structure whose top portion is provided with a                first liquid inlet. A bottom portion of the                absorbing structure is formed with a second air                inlet and a first air outlet. A middle portion of a                lower portion of an upper end of the absorption                structure is connected with the second liquid                inlets. A first exhaust port is provided on the top                of the first top portion structure. A third liquid                outlet is provided at the bottom of the middle                portion. A second exhaust port and a fourth liquid                outlet are provided in the middle of the second top                portion. The second air outlet communicates with                the first liquid outlet. The third air outlet and                the fourth air outlet communicate with each                other.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capturing device for collecting                and separating carbon dioxide from a discharge                source, conveying to ocean, oil-gas field and other                places for long-term sealing.
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes the hierarchical                desorption and semi-barren liquid circulation, so                that the carbon dioxide removing rate reaches more                than 99 percent, at the same time, the heat                absorption single consumption is reduced by more                than 5 percent compared with the traditional                single-tower desorption process, to realize the                high carbon dioxide removing rate under the                condition of low energy consumption. The device                reduces the energy consumption and saves the                production cost.

PT P
PN WO2022148856-A1
TI Reducing carbon dioxide in electrolytical system            and/or self-cleaning gas diffusion electrode in system            operating carbon dioxide reduction, involves applying            regeneration voltage to system to force            electromigration of formed carbonate ions.
AU SARGENT E
   SINTON D
   XU Y
   EDWARDS J P
AE TOTALENERGIES ONETECH (PETF-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
GA 2022906851
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves applying an operational                voltage to an electrolytical system to operate                carbon dioxide reduction for a period of time                defining an operation cycle. Carbonate ions are                formed at a cathode side of the system and having a                local carbonate ion concentration. A regeneration                voltage is applied to the system for another period                of the time defining a regeneration cycle to force                electromigration of the formed carbonate ions to an                anode side, where the voltage is lower than the                operational voltage. The duration of the operation                cycle is chosen to maintain the concentration at                the cathode. The electrolytical system is: a                membrane electrode assembly comprising a gas                diffusion electrode serving as a cathode; or a flow                cell system comprising a liquid catholyte and a gas                diffusion electrode serving as a cathode. The                cathode comprises: a metal layer deposited on                substrate; a silver layer deposited on a carbon                paper substrate; or a copper layer deposited on a                PTFE substrate.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide in an                electrolytical system and/or for self-cleaning a                gas diffusion electrode in the system operating                carbon dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents salt formation by                alternating an applied cell voltage between an                operational voltage and a lower regeneration                voltage. The duration of the operation cycle is                chosen to maintain the local carbonate ion                concentration at the cathode side below a carbonate                salt solubility limit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                self-cleaning electrolytical system for carbon                dioxide reduction into C2 products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates schematic view of the                membrane electrode assembly carbon dioxide                electrolyzer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrolytical                  system comprises an anolyte which is an aqueous                  solution of at least one alkaline compound                  comprising one alkali metal cations comprising                  lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, and/or                  caesium.
