PN US2022135495-A1
TI Making light olefin e.g. ethylene by mixing molecular sieve with supported catalyst in reactor, introducing hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas into reactor, and heating reactor, in which hydrogenation reaction occurs between hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas.
AU KIM S
   SASMAZ E
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 202259863Q
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing (M1) a light olefin, comprises: precipitating a catalyst onto zirconia to produce a supported catalyst; adding the supported catalyst into a reactor; mixing a molecular sieve with the supported catalyst in the reactor; introducing hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas into the reactor; and heating the reactor, where a hydrogenation reaction occurs between the hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas to synthesize the light olefin.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing a light olefin including ethylene, propylene or butylene (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes cubic phase yttria-stabilized zirconia that prevents deactivation of the indium-based catalyst during hydrogenation, and exhibits excellent support for indium oxide particles; and is direct synthesis route that promotes a stable and efficient method for producing light olefins.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) synthesizing (M2) light olefin, comprising precipitating an indium-based catalyst onto cubic phase yttria-stabilized zirconia to produce a supported indium-based catalyst, adding the supported indium-based catalyst into a reactor, mixing a molecular sieve with the supported indium-based catalyst in the reactor, introducing a stream of hydrogen gas and a stream of carbon dioxide gas into the reactor, and heating the reactor, where a hydrogenation reaction occurs between the hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas to synthesize the light olefin; and
   (2) a catalyst composition for producing light olefins from carbon dioxide, comprising the indium-based catalyst supported on cubic phase yttria-stabilized zirconia, and molecular sieves.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the methods (M1) and (M2): the reactor is heated to 250-550degrees Celsius; and the reactor is maintained at a pressure of 10-100 bar.Preferred Components: The catalyst or indium-based catalyst is indium oxide, metallic indium, an indium alloy, an indium single atom catalyst, or an indium single atom alloy. The supported catalyst comprises at least 10 wt.% indium. The zirconia is cubic phase yttria-stabilized zirconia. The molecular sieve is SAPO-34 zeolite, SAPO-5 zeolite, ZSM-5, zeolite beta, or zeolite Y. The ratio of hydrogen gas to carbon dioxide gas is at least 3.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of synthesized supported indium oxide catalysts that do not support the claims.

PN CN113666869-A
TI Synthesis of amino-containing naphthalimide dye for dyeing fabrics e.g. natural fiber fabrics, involves using supercritical carbon dioxide, nitro-substituted naphthalimide compound as precursor, gold/titania catalyst, and hydrogen.
AU FU D
   ZHENG H
   ZHAO H
   YAN J
   LIN C
   LV L
   ZHENG L
   HOU J
   XIONG X
AE UNIV DALIAN POLYTECHNIC (UYDP-C)
GA 2021D85248
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing amino-containing naphthalimide dye in supercritical carbon dioxide, involves using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, a nitro-substituted naphthalimide compound (I) as a dye precursor, gold/titania as a catalyst, and hydrogen as a reducing agent.
   USE - Method for synthesizing amino-containing naphthalimide dye used in composition with supercritical carbon dioxide for dyeing fabrics e.g. natural fiber fabrics or chemical fiber fabrics (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of realizing the innovation of the clean production and dyeing method of fluorescent dyes, and providing innovative ideas for rational resource utilization of supercritical carbon dioxide and reduction of environmental pollution problems caused by dye synthesis and dyeing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for synthesizing amino-containing naphthalimide dye in supercritical carbon dioxide, involves using supercritical carbon dioxide as a solvent, a nitro-substituted naphthalimide compound of formula (I) as a dye precursor, gold/titania as a catalyst, and hydrogen as a reducing agent. R1=H or R4; R4=group chosen from groups of formulae (II)-(XVI); n=1-6; X=C or N; Y'=NH, S or O; R5=H, CH3, OCH3, F, Cl, Br or I; R2,R3=H, NO2, NH2 or NHCOCH3, and at least one of R2 and R3 is NO2.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for dyeing the amino-containing naphthalimide dye, which involves: (a) dyeing fabric to be dyed while synthesizing the amino-containing naphthalimide fluorescent dye, and obtaining dyed fabric.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The dyeing method preferably involves placing the dye precursor, the catalyst and the fabric to be dyed in supercritical carbon dioxide reaction kettle, introducing hydrogen as reducing agent, introducing carbon dioxide as reaction and dyeing medium, while adding or not adding organic solvent, turning on heating system to make the system temperature rise, turning on pressurization system to make the system pressure rise, when the temperature rises to the reaction temperature and the pressure rises to the reaction pressure, carrying out synthesis and dyeing of dyes simultaneously by the reaction, releasing the pressure and lowering the temperature to normal state to obtain dyed fabrics, and recovering remaining dyes. Preferred Components: The organic solvent is N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylformamide or ethylene glycol monomethyl ether. Preferred Composition: The loading amount of gold in the catalyst is 0.5-2.5 wt.%. The ratio of the dye precursor to the organic solvent is 0.05-5 g:0-10 mL. The mass ratio of the dye precursor to the gold/titania catalyst is 1:0.5, and the mass ratio of the dye precursor to the fabric to be dyed is 1:50-1:100.

TI Capture system for offshore carbon dioxide            capture, has two-phase atomizing nozzle that is            configured for two- phase flow of mixture of            pressurised flue gas and liquid solvent in order to            generate atomized solvent spray of liquid solvent.

AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) comprises a two-phase                atomizing nozzle is in fluid communication with a                pressurized flue gas source and a solvent source.                The atomizing nozzles are configured for two- phase                flow of a mixture of pressurized gas and liquid                solvent in order to generate an atomized solvent                spray (104) of the liquid solvent. The solvent                source is configured to provide a liquid solvent,                and the pressurized gaseous source is a gas turbine                engine (200). The pressurized air is taken from an                exhaust gas (201) of the engine. A solvent                regeneration unit (140) separates solvent from any                solute present in the solvent, preferably carbon                dioxide.
   USE - Offshore carbon dioxide capture system for use                in an offshore vessel i.e. ship, for capturing                and/or absorbing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides an improved, lower weight,                compact and more energy efficient carbon dioxide                capture system. By providing the pressurized flue                gas source in flow communication with the two-phase                atomizing nozzle, a separate gas source is not                needed to drive the solvent provided by the solvent                source. The weight and footprint of the capture                system is reduced.

   (1) an offshore vessel; and
   (2) a method for offshore carbon dioxide                capture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of an                offshore carbon dioxide capture system.

PN CN112377166-A; CN112377166-B; US2022186595-A1; US11371328-B1
TI Integrated nitrogen-assisted carbon dioxide            fracturing and development method for shale oil            reservoirs, involves opening well to implement            depletion production after simmering of well completed            and second cycle of production is terminated.
AU ZHOU X
   ZHAO Y
   ZHANG B
   SUN X
   ZHENG A
   SHI L
   ZHANG L
   ZENG B
   PAN J
   TIAN G
   WU F

   HE J
   WANG Y
   JIANG Q
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2021202110
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using nitrogen to assist                carbon dioxide to perform fracturing operations on                target shale reservoirs. The carbon dioxide gas is                injected into the target shale reservoir, and then                nitrogen gas is introduced into the target shale                reservoir to push the carbon dioxide gas into the                deep part of the reservoir after the fracturing                operation is completed. The simmering operation is                performed on the target shale oil reservoir again,                and the simmering time is 1.5 times. The well is                opened to implement depletion production after the                simmering of the well is completed. The second                cycle of production is terminated when the                reservoir pressure is depleted to 1/2 of the                original reservoir pressure. The injected gas slug                volume and the simmering time are 1.5 times of the                previous cycle in the subsequent production                process.
   USE - Integrated nitrogen-assisted carbon dioxide                fracturing and development method for shale oil                reservoirs.
   ADVANTAGE - The most efficient use of carbon dioxide gas                makes the development of shale oil reservoirs more                economical and efficient. The integrated design of                fracturing and development makes on-site operation                procedures and standardization, and different                departments can establish closer cooperation. The                nitrogen injected into the reservoir pushes the                carbon dioxide gas to the deep portion of the                reservoir to fully contact with the crude oil and                improve the oil recovery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical view of a                comparison diagram of the recovery rate of the                fracturing shale long core with different gas media                huff and puff experiments. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to carbon dioxide filling field, claims a liquid carbon dioxide tank car filling processing system, comprising a product filling component and a buffer recycling component; the product filling component comprises a liquid storage tank, a charging pipe, a tank car and a product filling pump; two ends of the charging pipe are respectively connected with the liquid storage tank and tank car; the buffer recycling component comprises an air discharging pipe, a buffer storage tank, a pressure sensor, a boosting circulating pipe, a gasifier, a pressure regulating filling pump, an air supply pipe and an adjusting valve; two ends of the air escape pipe are respectively connected with the tank car and the buffer storage tank; the first end of the boosting circulation pipe is connected with the first end of the buffer storage tank; the second end of the boosting circulation pipe is connected with the second end of the buffer storage tank through the gasifier; the first end of the air supply pipe is connected with the buffer storage tank; the second end of the air supply pipe is used for connecting with the carbon dioxide production device. capable of stably recycling carbon dioxide, reducing the loss of tank truck filling carbon dioxide, and preventing the problem of working noise caused by the outer row of the high pressure air flow.

PT P
PN WO2020110151-A1; IN201841045187-A; EP3887038-A1; US2022111354-A1; IN393404-B
TI Catalyst used in thermochemical carbon dioxide reduction, comprises group 10 metal impregnated in mixed metal oxide which is combination of indium oxide, copper(II) oxide, zinc oxide, zirconium dioxide and aluminum oxide.
AU PETER S C
   ROY S
   CHEREVOTAN A
   SAI M K
AE JAWAHARLAL NEHRU CENT ADVANCED SCI RES (JAWA-Non-standard)
   JAWAHARLAL NEHRU CENT ADVANCED SCI RES (JAWA-Non-standard)
   JAWAHARLAL NEHRU CENT ADVANCED SCI RES (JAWA-Non-standard)
GA 202049209A
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises at least one group 10 metal impregnated in a mixed metal oxide, where the mixed metal oxide is a combination of indium oxide, copper(II) oxide, zinc oxide, zirconium dioxide and aluminum oxide.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for thermochemical carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: provides a conversion efficiency of carbon dioxide of 25-38%; and is effective in conversion of carbon dioxide to value added products like methanol and carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing catalyst, comprising (a) contacting at least one metallic nitrate comprising indium nitrate, copper nitrate, zinc nitrate, zirconium nitrate and/or aluminum nitrate, and deionized water to obtain first solution, (b) contacting at least one precipitating agent, and deionized water to obtain a second solution, (c) contacting the first solution, and the second solution to obtain a first reaction mixture, (d) processing the first reaction mixture to obtain a mixed metal oxide, (e) contacting the mixed metal oxide and an aqueous solution of palladium nitrate to obtain a third solution, and (f) processing the third solution to obtain the catalyst; and
   (2) thermochemical carbon dioxide reduction, comprising (a1) activating the catalyst in a hydrogen stream to obtain an activated catalyst and (b1) contacting hydrogen gas, carbon dioxide and the activated catalyst at a reaction temperature of 200-300 degrees C under a reaction pressure of 30-60 bar with a space velocity of 1000-7200 hour-1 to thermochemically reduce carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The indium oxide has weight percentage of 1-5% with respect to the total catalyst, the copper(II) oxide has a weight percentage of 50-59% with respect to the total catalyst, the zinc oxide has a weight percentage of 10-20% with respect to the total catalyst, the zinc oxide has a weight percentage of 5-10% with respect to the total catalyst and the aluminum oxide has a weight percentage of 5-10% with respect to the total catalyst. The group 10 metal is palladium having a weight percentage of 0.1-2% with respect to the catalyst. The catalyst has a specific area of 25-50 m2/g. Preferred Process: The precipitating comprises sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, ammonium carbonate and/or sodium hydrogen carbonate. The contacting the first solution, and the second solution is carried out at a temperature of 50-90 degrees C for a period of 40-50 minutes to obtain a first reaction mixture. The processing the first reaction mixture comprises processes comprising ageing, filtering, washing, drying and/or calcining to obtain the mixed metal oxide. The processing the third solution comprises processes comprising stirring, sonication, drying, calcinations and/or extrusion. The calcination is carried out at a temperature of 300-450 degrees C at heating rates of 2-5 degrees C/minute.
   EXAMPLE - 167 g Copper(II) nitrate salt was dissolved in 37.5 ml deionized water, 109 g zinc nitrate salt was dissolved in 37.5 ml deionized water, 5.4 g indium nitrate dissolved in 37.5 ml deionized water, 9.4g zinc nitrate dissolved in 37.5 ml deionized water and 18.4 g aluminum nitrate was dissolved in 37.5 ml deionized water were all mixed to obtain a first solution. Then at least one precipitating agent, 7.9 of 1 M sodium carbonate was dissolved in 75 ml deionized water to obtain a second solution. Then the first solution and the second solution were mixed dropwise in a three necked-flasks with 150 ml deionized water, at a temperature of 70 degrees C in an oil bath, through constant stirring, for a period of 40-50 minutes to obtain a first reaction mixture. The pH of the first reaction mixture was maintained of 7-8 to smoothen the precipitation process. The first reaction mixture was further allowed to be heated to a temperature of 70 degrees C for 40-50 minutes, without stirring. The reaction flask containing the first reaction mixture (precipitate) was then aged for 60-90 minutes above the oil bath. The first reaction mixture (precipitate) was further filtered, washed with distilled water to remove the unwanted metal ions. The precipitate was dried in a vacuum oven at 90 degrees C temperature for overnight. The sample was further calcined at 350 degrees C temperature for 3 hours to obtain a mixed metal oxide, i.e. indium oxide, copper(II) oxide, zinc oxide, zirconium dioxide and aluminum oxide. The mixed metal oxide was suddenly quenched by bringing the mixed metal oxide to room temperature to provide thermal shocks to create defects and nano-structuring. This nano- structuring reduced the cluster size of the catalytically active copper centers and increases its effective surface area. The mixed metal oxide was further impregnated with an aqueous solution palladium nitrate salt to impregnate palladium particles onto the mixed metal oxide to obtain a third solution. The aqueous solution palladium nitrate salt was prepared by dissolving 2.2 g palladium nitrate salt in 95 ml of deionized water (0.1 M). 100 g Catalyst was prepared by alternate cycles of vigorous stirring and sonication of the mixed metal oxide for 2 hours followed by drying under continuous stirring. The impregnated mixture was vacuum dried for 12 hours and calcined at temperatures of 300-450 degrees C, at heating rates of 2-5 degrees C/minutes, under a hydrogen flow of 10-50% hydrogen content, to obtain 100 g of the catalyst.
DC E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E10-E04E1; E11-D02; E31-N05B1; E35-A; E35-C04; E35-F; E35-L; J04-E04A; J04-E11; N01-C03; N02-D; N03-B02; N03-F01; N03-G01; N06-E01; N07-B02; N07-J
IP B01J-023/44; B01J-035/02; B01J-035/10; B01J-037/02; B01J-037/03; B01J-037/18; C07C-029/153; C08F-279/00; B01J-023/00; B01J-023/825; B01J-037/00; B01J-037/04; B01J-037/08; B01J-037/34; C01B-032/40; C07C-029/154

GA 202044284Q

A release structure is provided at the right end of the output mechanism. A spray gun mechanism is provided on the right end of the body. The lower end of the machine body is provided with a walking mechanism. The left end of the absorption cavity is provided with a first filter mesh fixed to the upper and lower end walls. A second filter screen fixedly connected to the upper and lower end walls of the absorption cavity is provided at the right end of the first filter screen. A power shaft extends rightward into the rotating cavity and is fixedly installed with a first bevel gear. A compression cavity is provided between the absorption cavity and the rotating cavity. A compression device fixedly connected to the front and back walls of the compression cavity is connected to the lower end of the collection device.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide cleaning device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: collects carbon dioxide during work and releases purified air; has excellent carbon dioxide cleaning effect on the dirt and oil on the electrical equipment when the equipment needs to-be cleaned; simultaneously utilizes carbon dioxide to reduce the burden of vegetation; and reduces the greenhouse effect.

The nozzle comprises a blade (230).

The system allows fluid stream to flow straight through the channel without flowing interior channel walls to remove particulates from the fluid stream. The system has wide application range and high accuracy.

PN WO2018159563-A1; JP2019502991-X; US2020023320-A1
TI Composite membrane for gas separation, comprises support layer and separation layer comprising polymers differing in solubility parameter, transmission rate of carbon dioxide and ratio of transmission rate of carbon dioxide to methane.
AU IIZUKA Y
AE FUJI FILM CORP (FUJF-C)
   FUJI FILM CORP (FUJF-C)
GA 2018704382
AB    NOVELTY - A composite separation membrane (10) comprises a porous support layer (3) and a separation layer (2) provided on the support layer and containing polymers (a1) and (b1). The polymer (a1) is a polymer, in which the ratio of the transmission rate of carbon dioxide with respect to methane is 15 or more, transmission rate of carbon dioxide is less than that of the polymer (b1), and the solubility parameter is 21 or more. The polymer (b1) is a polymer in which the transmission rate of carbon dioxide is 200 GPU or more, ratio of the transmission rate of carbon dioxide to that of methane is less than that of polymer (a1), and the solubility parameter is 16.5 or less.
   USE - Composite membrane used in separation membrane module and separation apparatus for separation of mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite membrane has excellent permeability and separation selectivity at high level even under high pressure conditions. The separation of specific components in the fluid can be performed at high speed and high selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) composition for forming separation membrane, which comprises the polymers (a1) and (b1) and a solvent; and
   (2) manufacture of composite film, which involves coating the composition on porous support layer, forming a coating film, and drying.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the composite membrane.
   Separation layer (2)
   Porous support layer (3)
   Composite film (10)
   Layers (a2,b2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composite film comprises a layer (a2) containing the polymer (a1), and a layer (b2) containing the polymer (b1), sequentially provided on the support layer. The separation layer is formed using a coating solution obtained by dissolving the polymers (a1) and (b1) in a solvent. The content of polymer (a1) in the separation layer is less than that of polymer (b1). The separation layer contains 40 %mass or less, preferably 20 %mass or less polymer (a1) with respect to content of polymers (a1) and (b1). The polymer (a1) is a cellulose compound. Preferred Properties: The polymer (a1) has solubility parameter of 23.5-30. The polymer (b1) has solubility parameter of 15.5 or less, preferably 14-15. The ratio of the transmission rate of the carbon dioxide with respect to the transmission rate of methane of the polymer (a1) is 20 or more. The transmission rate of carbon dioxide of the polymer (b1) is 350 GPU or more.

PN IN201821006612-A
TI System for simultaneously reducing nitrogen oxide and carbon dioxide discharged from e.g. automobile, has pump for pumping absorbent, and gas sensor installed after carbon capture unit senses amount of nitrogen oxide passing to atmosphere.
AU KUMAR P
   MAKADIA U
   PARWANI A K
   DEB D
AE PULKIT K (PULK-Individual)
   UMANG M (UMAN-Individual)
   AJIT K P (AJIT-Individual)
   DIPANKAR D (DIPA-Individual)
GA 201823411S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a variable frequency drive (VFD) pump (107) for pumping absorbent from a carbon capture unit to a heat exchanger and installed in parallel to an exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) valve. A pneumatic pump (110) is fixed above the exchanger to pump carbon dioxide emitting from the exchanger. A pressure sensor (114) senses pressure inside a dead weight type accumulator (111). The accumulator replaces a fuel tank and stores fuel and captured carbon dioxide. An exhaust gas sensor (116) is installed after the carbon capture unit senses amount of nitrogen oxide passing to an atmosphere.
   USE - System for simultaneously reducing nitrogen oxide and carbon dioxide discharged from a diesel engine of a vehicle i.e. automobile, and stationary sources using exhaust gas recirculation and carbon capture. Can also be used for lorries, buses and trains.
   ADVANTAGE - The system decreases emission of carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide discharged from a vehicle by capturing and recirculating the carbon dioxide and the nitrogen oxide to the engine. The system locates two different units at an inner side of the engine to recirculate the exhaust gas and capture the carbon dioxide before passing to the atmosphere. The system stores the captured carbon inside a vehicle considering space and weight limitations in the vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of an engine.
   VFD pump (107)
   Pneumatic pump (110)
   Dead weight type accumulator (111)
   Pressure sensor (114)

AB    NOVELTY - Modular microorganism group system comprises a                core shell structure. The core part is composed of                Chlorellaand a hydrophilic carrier for immobilizing                Chlorella. The shell part is composed of edible                alkane bacteria and a lipophilic carrier for                immobilizing edible alkane bacteria and the                diameter of the core part is 100&#177;10                &#956;m and the thickness of the shell part is                20&#177;5 &#956;m.
   USE - The system is useful for processing petroleum                pollutant (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: can quickly absorb tiny oil                droplets in water; can degrade into carbon dioxide                and minerals by the alkane bacteria in its shell                part; can use carbon dioxide to convert into its                own substances and releases oxygen and enzymes to                the alkane bacteria for its own life activities and                oil degradation; has a degradation rate of 95.8%                for 7 days of waste water with 10 g/l of crude oil                pollution concentration through the above                synergistic effect and substance exchange effect                and reaches the unprecedented degradation                efficiency.

   a microfluidic generator for preparing the                modular microorganism group system comprising a                radial center provided with an inlet A for passing                in the hydrosol containing . Each micro-flow control unit sequentially                to the main pipeline flow direction comprises two                inlet parts B for introducing the hydrosol                containing edible alkane bacteria and lignin, which                are converged to the main pipeline at the same                time. PN CN114573515-A
TI Preparation of trimethoprim comprises adding            acetic acid, 3-hydroxy propionitrile, piperidine and            3,4,5-trimethoxybenzaldehyde, stirring overnight to            finish Knoevenagel condensation reaction, adding            guanidine carbonate and heating to finish cyclization            reaction to obtain trimethoprim.
AU WU G
   JI Y
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202278861D
AB    NOVELTY - A preparation method of trimethoprim                comprises: (i) stirring glacial acetic acid,                3-hydroxypropionitrile and catalyst piperidine,                adding 3,4,5-trimethoxybenzaldehyde and dissolving,                stirring overnight at room temperature to complete                the Knoevenagel condensation reaction; (ii) slowly                adding guanidine carbonate at room temperature,                stirring and dissolving, slowly releasing carbon                dioxide gas, after adding guanidine carbonate,                continuing to stir at room temperature for 0.1-1                hour, heating at 40-100&#8451; for 1-10 hours                to complete the cyclization reaction; and (iii)                after finishing the reaction, evaporating the                acetic acid in the reaction system under reduced                pressure, adding deionized water, increasing the                temperature to 50-100&#8451;, stirring and                dissolving, slowly adding sodium hydroxide solution                dropwise to adjust pH to 8.5-9.5, and cooling and                freezing to 5-0&#8451;, separating white                precipitate and washing with deionized water, and                drying to obtain trimethoprim.
   USE - The method is used for preparation of                trimethoprim.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method of trimethoprim                involves simple operation, provides high yield and                good atom economy, reduces environmental pollution,                avoids the use of auxiliary raw materials,                generates few waste, reduces the production cost,                and directly prepares trimethoprim in one-pot                cooking method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A preparation method of trimethoprim                comprises: (i) stirring glacial acetic acid,                3-hydroxypropionitrile and catalyst piperidine,                adding 3,4,5-trimethoxybenzaldehyde and dissolving,                stirring overnight at room temperature to complete                the Knoevenagel condensation reaction, where the                amount of glacial acetic acid is 1-10 times the                mass of 3,4,5-trimethoxybenzaldehyde, the mass                ratio of 3,4,5-trimethoxybenzaldehyde and                3-hydroxypropionitrile is 1:(1-1.5), the molar                ratio of 3,4,5-trimethoxybenzaldehyde to catalyst                piperidine is 1:(0.05-0.5); (ii) slowly adding                guanidine carbonate at room temperature, stirring                and dissolving, slowly releasing carbon dioxide                gas, where the molar ratio of                3,4,5-trimethoxybenzaldehyde to guanidine carbonate                is 1: (0.5-1), after adding guanidine carbonate,                continuing to stir at room temperature for 0.1-1                hour, heating at 40-100&#8451; for 1-10 hours                to complete the cyclization reaction; and (iii)                after finishing the reaction, evaporating the                acetic acid in the reaction system under reduced                pressure, adding deionized water, increasing the                temperature to 50-100&#8451;, stirring and                dissolving, slowly adding sodium hydroxide solution                dropwise to adjust pH to 8.5-9.5, and cooling and                freezing to 5-0&#8451;, separating white                precipitate and washing with deionized water, and                drying to obtain trimethoprim.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  the sodium hydroxide solution is 30-50%. The                  yield of the trimethoprim to 3, 4,                  5-trimethoxybenzaldehyde can reach more than 92%.                  The glacial acetic acid is 3-6 times of the mass                  of the 3, 4, 5-trimethoxylbenzaldehyde.Preferred Method: The Knoevenagel                  condensation reaction is carried out at                  0-60&#8451;, preferably, 20-30&#8451; for                  4-24 hours, preferably 6-12 hours. The                  cyclization reaction is carried out at                  50-70&#8451; for 2-6 hours.

TI Preparation of Lithocarpus litseifolius fruit gas            bubble Kangpu tea with high oxidation resistance            activity by boiling dried Lithocarpus litseifolius tea            and sucrose, adding pulp and stabilizing agent in crude            tea, canning and pasteurizing.
AU MAI Y
   XIA Y
   LI H
   GUO H
   LIU Y
   LIU H
   HE X
   GAN R
AE CHENGDU AGRIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY CENT (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2022288306
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of Lithocarpus litseifolius fruit                gas bubble Kangpu tea with high oxidation                resistance activity comprises adding dried                Lithocarpus litseifolius tea and sucrose into                boiling water and stirring, and cooling to obtain                Lithocarpus litseifolius sweet tea; cleaning and                sterilizing fresh high vitamin C fruit, peeling and                removing seed, juicing and filtering to obtain                fruit juice and pulp; mixing fruit juice and sugar                tea; inoculating 5-25 vol.% multiple probiotics                into composite fermentation culture medium, lightly                stirring uniformly by glass rod, sealing by 4                layers of sterile gauze, standing at constant                temperature of 25-30degrees Celsius and keeping                light for 3-7days, crushing upper concha tea fungus                film for 1-3 minutes at 1000 revolutions/minute to                obtain the concha crude product; and adding pulp                and 0.02-0.15 wt.% stabilizing agent in crude tea,                injecting food-grade carbon dioxide, canning and                pasteurizing.
   USE - The method is used for preparation of                Lithocarpus litseifolius fruit gas bubble Kangpu                tea with high oxidation resistance activity for                preparing health products (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of Lithocarpus litseifolius fruit                gas bubble Kangpu tea with high oxidation                resistance activity comprises adding 5-25 g dried                Lithocarpus litseifolius tea and 30-80 g of sucrose                into 1000mL of boiling water and stirring until the                sugar is melted, keeping the solution boiling for                5-15 minutes, filtering with sterile gauze when it                is hot, and naturally cooling to room temperature                to obtain Lithocarpus litseifolius sweet tea;                cleaning and sterilizing fresh high vitamin C fruit                without plant diseases and insects and mechanical                injury, peeling and removing seed, juicing and                filtering to obtain fruit juice and pulp; mixing                fruit juice and sugar tea at volume ratio of                10-50%, adding sterilized wide-mouth glass                container, and obtaining composite fermentation                culture medium; inoculating 5-25 vol.% multiple                probiotics into composite fermentation culture                medium, lightly stirring uniformly by glass rod,                sealing by 4 layers of sterile gauze, standing at                constant temperature of 25-30degrees Celsius and                keeping light for 3-7days, crushing upper concha                tea fungus film for 1-3 minutes at 1000                revolutions/minute to obtain the concha crude                product; and adding pulp and 0.02-0.15 wt.%                stabilizing agent in crude tea, injecting                food-grade carbon dioxide, canning and                pasteurizing.

PT P
PN CN113999741-A
TI Brewing couscous sour beer involves pulverizing malt, adding amylase and glucanase for saccharification, filtering after saccharification, adding hops in boiling process after acidification, performing fermentation using yeast.
AU ZHANG Y
AE JIUSHI YOULI JINAN BRAND MANAGEMENT CO (JIUS-Non-standard)
GA 202226063F
AB    NOVELTY - Brewing couscous sour beer involves pulverizing malt, the malt is composed of 300kg of pearson malt, 25kg of vienna malt, 125kg of wheat malt, 12.5kg of dextrin malt, and 25kg of oatmeal. The malt is prepared for saccharification after adding 1700L of water. 80ml of alpha-amylase and 80ml of beta-glucanase is added for saccharification. After saccharification, filter, 1500L of wort in the process is added, 2500L of wort is collected in the boiling pot. The wort is cooled to 45 degrees Celsius through a plate heat exchanger, 200g of lactic acid bacteria (acid-producing yeast) are added, and 4L/min of carbon dioxide is introduced for five minutes to start the acidification of the wort. After acidification, hops are added during the boiling process. In the last 5 minutes of boiling, 4kg of sea salt is added. After boiling and sterilization (60 minutes), it is connected to yeast for fermentation for about 20 days. After fermentation, filtered and canned to brew couscous sour beer.
   USE - Method for brewing couscous sour beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables brewing couscous sour beer, which can well control the quality of couscous sour beer without producing a heavy yeast taste, and at the same time has the slightly sour taste and freshness imparted by lactic acid bacteria.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Brewing couscous sour beer involves pulverizing malt, the malt is composed of 300kg of pearson malt, 25kg of vienna malt, 125kg of wheat malt, 12.5kg of dextrin malt, and 25kg of oatmeal. The malt is prepared for saccharification after adding 1700L of water. 80ml of alpha-amylase and 80ml of beta-glucanase is added for saccharification. After saccharification, filter, 1500L of wort in the process is added, 2500L of wort is collected in the boiling pot. The wort is cooled to 45 degrees Celsius through a plate heat exchanger, 200g of lactic acid bacteria (acid-producing yeast) are added, and 4L/min of carbon dioxide is introduced for five minutes to start the acidification of the wort, pH5.4 before acidification, acidification for 24 hours, stop acidification after pH reaches 3.5. After acidification, hops are added during the boiling process. In the last 5 minutes of boiling, 4kg of sea salt is added. After boiling and sterilization (60 minutes), it is connected to yeast for fermentation for about 20 days. After fermentation, it is filtered and canned to brew couscous sour beer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The lactic acid bacteria are Lactobacillus plantarum, Lactobacillus delbrueckii or Lactobacillus brevis.
DC C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN113666771-A; CN113666771-B
TI Adjusting alkalinity of solid waste-based            artificial planting soil by layering solid waste-based            artificial planting soil particles on multi-layer            platform, spraying water and industrial flue gas,            curing, drying and adding into roller crusher for            granulation.
AU CHENG F
   LINGHU C
   ZHOU K
   SU D
   GAO H
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 2021D7756H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for adjusting alkalinity of solid                waste-based artificial planting soil involves                layering the solid waste-based artificial planting                soil particles on a multi-layer platform, closing                the curing room, spraying water to adjust humidity,                spraying industrial flue gas from bottom pipes                around curing room, curing, turning off flue gas,                opening the air valve, pouring out the solid                waste-based artificial planting soil particles from                the multi-layer platform, drying in an oven or                naturally drying in a ventilated place and adding                the dried and formed solid waste-based artificial                planting soil into a roller crusher for granulation                to obtain solid waste-based artificial planting                soil.
   USE - The method is useful for adjusting alkalinity                of solid waste-based artificial planting soil
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the pH of the artificial                soil, improves adaptability of the plant growth,                improves the whole strength of the soil,                mineralizes and fixes carbon dioxide greenhouse                gas, and has positive contribution to carbon                dioxide emission reduction. The method uses                industrial flue gas as main raw material, adjusting                the soil alkalinity of solid waste based artificial                planting soil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for adjusting alkalinity of solid                waste-based artificial planting soil by utilizing                industrial flue gas involves (a) layering the solid                waste-based artificial planting soil particles with                a particle size of less than 5 mm on a multi-layer                platform by a distribution machine in a curing room                into a thickness of less than 60 mm, (b) closing                the curing room, spraying water in the curing room                with an atomizer to adjust the humidity greater                than or equal to 90%, spraying industrial flue gas                from the bottom pipes around the curing room and                curing at 20-200degrees Celsius and not less than 1                MPa for 5-30 minutes, (c) turning off the flue gas                and opening the air valve, where the multi-layer                platform carrying the solid waste-based artificial                planting soil particles exits the curing room along                the track, (d) pouring out the solid waste-based                artificial planting soil particles obtained in step                (c) from the multi-layer platform and drying at                50-120degrees Celsius in an oven or naturally                drying in a ventilated place and (e) adding the                dried and formed solid waste-based artificial                planting soil obtained in step (d) into a roller                crusher for granulation to obtain solid waste-based                artificial planting soil with a particle size of                less than 5 mm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In step (a), the solid                  waste-based artificial planting soil comprises                  solid waste, ordinary silicon cement, fly ash                  cement or self-gelling powder, foaming agent and                  water in a ratio of 90-98:2-10:0.01-0.09:0.1-0.5,                  where the solid waste comprises washing                  tailings/tailings or hydrometallurgical tailings,                  the solid waste comprises fly ash, coal gangue,                  iron tailings, copper tailings, red mud, kaolin                  mud or gypsum, the self-gelling powder comprises                  fly ash, steel slag, carbide slag, phosphogypsum                  and desulfurized gypsum in a mass ratio of                  60-70:15-25:5-15:5-10 and the foaming agent                  comprises aluminum powder, hydrogen peroxide or                  baking soda. In step (b), the industrial flue gas                  has carbon dioxide concentration of 5-25 wt.% and                  sulfur dioxide concentration of less than or                  equal to 100 mg/m3.

PN CN113621599-A
TI Preparing ethyl carbamate degrading enzyme comprises e.g. preparing culture solution and strains, filling culture solution, culturing, introducing strains, separating and purifying degrading enzyme, and extracting and storing ethyl carbamate degrading enzyme.
AU WANG B
   WANG X
   JI Y
   SHI X
AE UNIV SHIHEZI (USHZ-C)
GA 2021D0070Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ethyl carbamate degrading enzyme comprises (i) preparing the culture solution and the strains that need to be cultured, filling culture solution into culture solution feeding bin, and adding strains; (ii) detecting pH inside feeding bin of culture solution and the temperature inside the feeding bin of the strain; (iii) introducing strains inside strain loading bin into the strain feeding bin for culturing; (iv) injecting culture solution and strains into culture chamber, and filling culture chamber; (v) performing real-time detection of temperature, oxygen content, carbon dioxide content and pH in culture chamber, and culturing strains for fermentation efficiency; (vi) introducing degrading enzyme generated in the culture chamber to separation chamber to separate and purify degrading enzyme, extracting and storing ethyl carbamate degrading enzyme; and (vii) circulating (iii)-(vi) steps to ensure sufficient materials in the culture fluid feeding bin and strain feeding bin.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ethyl carbamate degrading enzyme.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively monitors the process of fermentation, ensures the efficiency of degrading enzyme preparation, and reduces the preparation time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ethyl carbamate degrading enzyme comprises (i) preparing the culture solution and the strains that need to be cultured, filling the culture solution into the culture solution feeding bin, and adding the strains that need to be cultured to the strain feeding bin; (ii) detecting the pH inside the upper silo of the culture solution and the temperature inside the feeding bin of the strain, and adjusting the pH inside the feeding bin of the culture solution and the temperature inside the feeding bin of the strain so that the culture solution and strains are in a suitable environment; (iii) introducing the strains inside the strain loading bin into the strain feeding bin so that the strain can be activated for culturing; (iv) injecting the culture solution and strains into the culture chamber according to the proportion to culture the strains, and filling the culture chamber with two-thirds and stopping injecting the culture solution and strains into the culture chamber; (v) performing real-time detection of temperature, oxygen content, carbon dioxide content and pH in the culture chamber, and culturing the strains for fermentation efficiency; (vi) introducing the degrading enzyme generated in the culture chamber to the separation chamber to separate and purify the degrading enzyme, extracting and storing the ethyl carbamate degrading enzyme, and conducting centralized treatment of production waste; and (vii) circulating (iii)-(vi) steps to ensure sufficient materials in the culture fluid feeding bin and strain feeding bin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The culture solution comprises 15 g/l maltose, 15 g/l glucose, 0.5 g/l sodium chloride, 7 g/l Knudson C, 40 g/l yeast extract powder and water. The strain is yeast, the inoculum of the strain is 7.5%, the ratio of the strain to the culture solution is 7.5:100, and the activation temperature of the strain is 25degrees Celsius.Preferred Method: The culturing is carried out in culture chamber at 38-42degrees Celsius with pH of 5.9-6.3 for 150 hours, and a low-speed runner is arranged inside the culture chamber to perform low-speed stirring of the culture solution and the strain. Control valves are provided between the culture fluid feeding bin and the culture bin, the strain feeding bin and the strain activation bin, the strain feeding bin and the culture bin, and the separation bin and the culture bin and a temperature sensor, a pH detector, an oxygen content tester and a carbon dioxide content tester are arranged inside the incubation chamber.

TI Detecting microelement morphology in metal concentrates by in vitro method involves using X-ray fluorescence spectrometer to measure content of micro element in concentrate, and preparing micro element liquid to be tested.
AU DING Y
   HOU J
   FENG Y
   JIANG C
   CHEN C
   ZHAO W
AE ANHUI YOUJINGUANHUA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOL (ANHU-Non-standard)
   NANJING CUSTOMS IND PROD TESTING CENT (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021D1148Q
AB    NOVELTY - Detecting microelement morphology in metal concentrates by in vitro method involves using X-ray fluorescence spectrometer to measure content of micro elements in concentrate, adding microelement liquid to be tested to lung cell line and stomach cell line, and dividing whole container into 37-5% carbon dioxide, 90-90% relative humidity of culture box for 24 hours, collecting sample of lung cell lines and stomach cell lines, digesting sample in volume ratio of 4:1 nitric acid-hydrogen peroxide solution, obtaining lung cell system digestion solution and gastric cell system fermentation solution, and using inductance coupling to measure content of micro element in lung-cell system digestion liquid and gastric-cell line digestion liquid.
   USE - Method for detecting microelement morphology in metal concentrates by in vitro method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to analyze different shapes of microelement, evaluates damage to human body, combines high performance liquid chromatography and inductance coupling spectrometry and has very low detection limit, wide dynamic linear range, less interference, high analysis precision, fast speed and can measure multi-element.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting microelement morphology in metal concentrates by in vitro method involves using X-ray fluorescence spectrometer to measure content of micro elements in concentrate, adding microelement liquid to be tested to lung cell line and stomach cell line, and dividing whole container into 37-5% carbon dioxide, 90-90% relative humidity of culture box for 24 hours, collecting sample of lung cell lines and stomach cell lines, digesting sample in volume ratio of 4:1 nitric acid-hydrogen peroxide solution, obtaining lung cell system digestion solution and gastric cell system fermentation solution, and using inductance coupling to measure content of micro element in lung-cell system digestion liquid and gastric-cell line digestion liquid. The test ore is taken, crushed, weighed ore into pickled centrifuge tube, performed shaking and centrifugation, and collected supernatant is filtered through sterile filter membrane to obtain liquid to be tested for microelements. The lung cell line and gastric cell line are cultured in container separated by porous membrane into top side chamber and basal side chamber. The lung cell line is cultured in top side chamber, and gastric cell line is cultured in basal side chamber. The high performance liquid chromatography and inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry are used to determine lung cell line digestion fluid and gastric cell line respectively content of different forms of microelements in digestion solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The ammonium dihydrogen phosphate solution is used as mobile phase A and water as mobile phase B for gradient elution. The helium or hydrogen are used as reaction gas.Preferred Components: The different forms of microelements comprises trivalent arsenic, pentavalent arsenic, antimony and cadmium. The lung cell line and gastric cell line are derived from humans.

TI Use of Cordyceps sinensis in ex-vivo erythropoiesis and platelet formation, by expansion of cells after culturing in presence of hydrocortisone, stem cell factor, interleukin-3, and erythropoietin, washing, and centrifuging cells.
AU SINGH V K
AE UNIV AMITY (UYAM-Non-standard)
GA 2021A36374
AB    NOVELTY - Use of Cordyceps sinensis in ex-vivo erythropoiesis and platelet formation, involves culturing the cells in Iscove's modified Dulbecco's Medium (IMDM) comprising Roswell Park Memorial Institute medium (RPMI 1640) supplemented with insulin, transferrin and human serum from male AB plasma; expansion of the cells after culturing in the presence of hydrocortisone, stem cell factor (SCF), interleukin (IL)-3, and erythropoietin (Epo); providing a predetermined diluted volume of the cell culture on a predetermined period in a predetermined volume of fresh medium; providing parallel to the above defined steps in another culture well, the same number of the cells, seeding without any growth factors; maintaining the culture at a predetermined temperature in carbon dioxide (CO2) in the air; washing the cells in phosphate buffered saline (PBS); and centrifuging the cells for the predetermined time at a predetermined RPM and a pellet is examined under a microscope.
   USE - The method is useful for using Cordyceps sinensis in ex-vivo erythropoiesis and for platelet formation. The umbilical cord blood cells are used for isolating CD34+ stem cells by density gradient methods using Ficol histopaque, centrifugation and subsequently by using magnetic bead based affinity column positive selection method and their standardization by flowcytometer based predetermined methods (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can provide mass scale production for regular supply of blood cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of Cordyceps sinensis in ex-vivo erythropoiesis and platelet formation, involves culturing the cells in Iscove's modified Dulbecco's Medium (IMDM) comprising Roswell Park Memorial Institute medium (RPMI 1640) supplemented with insulin, transferrin and human serum from male AB plasma; expansion of the cells after culturing in the presence of hydrocortisone, stem cell factor (SCF), interleukin (IL)-3, and erythropoietin (Epo); providing a predetermined diluted volume of the cell culture on a predetermined period in a predetermined volume of fresh medium containing SCF, IL-3, Epo, and hydrocortisone and renewing the the culture medium containing growth factors for a predetermined period; providing parallel to the above defined steps in another culture well, the same number of the cells, and seeding without any growth factors; maintaining the culture at a predetermined temperature in carbon dioxide (CO2) in the air and further, staining the cells with Giemsa stain, modified solution for morphologic analysis; washing the cells in phosphate buffered saline (PBS) and incubating with Giemsa in an Eppendorf tube for the predetermined period; and centrifuging the cells for the predetermined time at a predetermined RPM and a pellet is examined under a microscope.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further involves extraction, purification of Cordyceps sinensis and evaluation of the various chemical component identification strategies including UPLC and standardization and screening of their biological effects on various types of cells including CD34+ cells, KG-1a, JurkaT E6.1 cells to study possible cytotoxicity and apoptotic effects. The feasibility of different stem cells types to functionally mature RBCs progenitors up to a level of 109 from initial 103 cells involves using static culture conditions. The cells are isolated by using a Spectra optia aphaeresis system after a predetermined time of granulocyte-colony stimulating factor (G-CSF) administration. The cells are mobilized into peripheral blood after the infusion of GMCSF and retrieved through aphaeresis. Cells are further purified by density gradient cell separation to remove the traces of Red blood cells, platelets, and plasma. The morphologic analysis is done by using a graphical user interface on a digital device and antibody mediated flowcytometer based methods. The stem cells are cultured in a two dimensional (2D) in vitro culture system using IMDM and a cocktail of growth factors as define here for initial stem cell culture in phase 1. The cells are cultured in a different set of culturing conditions and growth factors including Cordyceps sinensis ultra purified extract and the method involves inducing their differentiation into phase 2-3 of their final erythropoietic differentiation.

PN CN112892603-A; CN112892603-B
TI Solid heterogeneous catalyst useful for nitroformylation reaction, comprises ruthenium, palladium, iridium, rhodium, nickel, molybdenum, copper and/or iron as metal component, and vinyl-functionalized phosphine based organic ligand polymer.
AU DING Y
   WANG G
   YAN L
   JIANG M
   CHENG X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202168173S
AB    NOVELTY - Solid heterogeneous catalyst comprises ruthenium, palladium, iridium, rhodium, nickel, molybdenum, copper and/or iron as metal component, and vinyl-functionalized phosphine based organic ligand polymer. The vinyl-functionalized phosphine based organic ligand polymer is polymer formed by solvothermal copolymerization of vinyl-functionalized phosphine based ligand monomer. The metal component forms coordinate bond with phosphorus atom in the polymer backbone of the organic ligand, and is present on the organic ligand polymer carrier.
   USE - The solid heterogeneous catalyst is useful for nitroformylation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The solid heterogeneous catalyst: utilizes polymer support has large specific surface area and hierarchical pore structure, and metal components can be highly dispersed on the support; has excellent catalytic reaction performance and stability; and is easily separated from the reactants and products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the solid heterogeneous catalyst, comprising (i) adding free radical initiator into solvent containing vinyl functionalized phosphine ligands under 273-473 K, preferably 293 K and inert gas atmosphere, and stirring for 0.5-100 hours, preferably 3 hours, (ii) allowing the solution in step (i) to stand in a hydrothermal autoclave under 333-473 K, preferably 373K and inert gas atmosphere for 0.5-100 hours, preferably 24 hours to carry out solvothermal polymerization reaction, (iii) removing the solvent under vacuum at 273-473 K, preferably 333 K to obtain vinyl-functionalized phosphine based organic ligand polymer, and (iv) placing the organic ligand polymer in a solvent containing active metal components, stirring for 0.5-100 hours, preferably 24 hours under an atmosphere of 273-473 K, preferably 293 K and an inert gas atmosphere, removing the solvent under vacuum at 273-473 K, preferably 333 K to obtain solid heterogeneous catalyst with active metal component supported by the organic ligand polymer; and
   (2) nitroformylation reaction, comprising subjecting organic amine raw material and carbon dioxide/hydrogen mixed gas to nitroformylation reaction in the presence of the solid heterogeneous catalyst in a fixed bed, trickle bed, slurry bed or tank reactor, where the nitroformylation reaction temperature is 293-573 K, preferably 373 K, the reaction pressure is 0.05-20 MPa, preferably 6 MPa, the liquid hourly space velocity is 0.01-20/hour, preferably 5/hour), and the gas space velocity is 100-20000/hours, preferably 1000/hour, and the molar ratio between carbon dioxide raw material and hydrogen raw material is 1:1-1:100, preferably 1:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solid heterogeneous catalyst comprises 0.01-40 wt.%, preferably 0.5 wt.% metal component. The vinyl-functionalized phosphine ligand is tris(4-vinylphenyl)phosphine, phenylbis(4-vinylphenyl)phosphine, tris(3-vinylphenyl)phosphine, tris(4-vinylcyclohexyl)phosphine, cyclohexylbis(4-vinylcyclohexyl)phosphine, 2-(bis(4-vinylphenyl)phosphino)-5-vinylpyridine, (9,9-dimethyl-9H-xanthene-4,5-diyl)bis(bis(4-vinylphenyl)phosphine) or 6,6'-(3,3'-di-tert-butyl-5,5'-divinylbiphenyl-2,2'-diyl)bis(oxy)didibenzo(d,f)(1,3,2)dioxaphosphepine. The organic ligand polymer has specific surface area of 100-3000 m2/g, pore volume of 0.1-5 cm3/g, and pore size distribution of 0.1-100 nm. The solvent used in steps (i) and (iv) is benzene, toluene, tetrahydrofuran, methanol, ethanol, dichloromethane, dichloroethane and/or deionized water. The free radical initiator used in step (i) is cyclohexanone peroxide, dibenzoyl peroxide, tert-butyl hydroperoxide, azobisisobutyronitrile and/or azobisisoheptonitrile. The weight ratio between free radical initiator and organic monomer is 1:500-1:5, preferably 1:25. In the use method, the organic amine is substituted amine compound of formula (R1R2-NH) (I), alkyl amine compound of formula (NH2(CH2)n-NH2) (III), substituted piperidine compound of formula (II), piperidine compound of formula (IV), 2-(piperidin-4-yl)ethanol, piperidin-4-ylmethanol, 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine, 2,6-dimethylpiperidine or morpholine.R1, R2 = 1-18C alkyl;m = 1-6; andn = 1-12.

PT P
PN CN112870983-A; CN112870983-B
TI Preparing a polyimide film for desorbing carbon dioxide from an organic solvent, involves dissolving polyimide raw material powder of the structural unit in organic solvent, while adding inorganic nanomaterials, stirring at specified range.
AU TANG Z
   BEN G
   GUO D
   ZHANG Z
   LIU Y
   ZHOU M
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 202164383R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a polyimide film involves dissolving polyimide raw material powder of the 2-methyl-5-(2-(o-tolyl)-1,3-dioxo-isoindoline-5-carbonyl)isoindoline-1,3-dione derivative (I) in an organic solvent, while adding inorganic nanomaterials, stirring for 1-12 hours at 10-80 degrees C, and keeping for 5-24 hours, and after centrifugal degassing at a speed of 500-2000 revolutions/minute, obtaining the casting liquid. The spin coating equipment is used to prepare polyimide film on the support film. The polyimide membrane is placed in deionized water for a coagulation bath at 5-90 degrees C, and the time is controlled at 1-12 hours. The polyimide film in the coagulation bath is put into the organic solvent containing the crosslinking agent, the temperature is controlled at 10-50 degrees , and the time is controlled at 1-12 hours. The cross-linked polyimide film is sent to a vacuum drying oven for drying, and the vacuum oven is held at 10-80 degrees C for 5-24 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing a polyimide film for desorbing carbon dioxide from an organic solvent in diethyl carbonate (DEC), diethyl succinate (DES), and dimethyl 2,5-dioxadipate (DMC-C2) (Claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared membrane has the characteristics of good solvent resistance, high membrane uniformity, high CO2 separation factor and large flux.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a polyimide film involves dissolving polyimide raw material powder of the 2-methyl-5-(2-(o-tolyl)-1,3-dioxo-isoindoline-5-carbonyl)isoindoline-1,3-dione derivative of formula (I) in an organic solvent, while adding inorganic nanomaterials, stirring for 1-12 hours at 10-80 degrees C, and keeping for 5-24 hours, and after centrifugal degassing at a speed of 500-2000 revolutions/minute, obtaining the casting liquid. The spin coating equipment is used to prepare polyimide film on the support film. The polyimide membrane is placed in deionized water for a coagulation bath at 5-90 degrees C, and the time is controlled at 1-12 hours. The polyimide film in the coagulation bath is put into the organic solvent containing the crosslinking agent, the temperature is controlled at 10-50 degrees , and the time is controlled at 1-12 hours. The cross-linked polyimide film is sent to a vacuum drying oven for drying, and the vacuum oven is held at 10-80 degrees C for 5-24 hours.
   n=50-200.
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The organic solvent of the casting liquid is acetonitrile (ACN), N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF), N-methylformylmorpholine (NMP) or dimethylacetamide (DMAC). The inorganic nano material is one or a mixture of silicon dioxide (SIO2), titanium dioxide (TiO2) and metatitanic acid (TiO(OH)2). The material of the supporting membrane is polydimethyl sulfoxide, polyacetate fiber, polycellulose, polyacrylonitrile, polytetrafluoroethylene, polyvinylidene fluoride or polyethylene terephthalate. The crosslinking agent is one or a mixture of ethylene diamine (EDA), propylene diamine (PDA), and butane diamine (BDA). The organic solvent of the crosslinking agent is one or a mixture of methanol (MeOH), acetone (ACT), and isopropanol (IPA). Preferred Conditions: The base film has a pore size of 20-80 micrometer, and a thickness of 10-100 micrometer.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI New polyamine chain extender i.e.            N,N'-(4,4'-methylenebis(4,1-phenylene)bis(methylene))dipropan-2-amine            useful in surface modification treatment agent,            modified calcium hydroxide, and microporous            polyurethane elastic material.
AU TENG X
   LI X
   LIU Z
   ZHAO G
   YANG X
   ZHANG C
   YU T
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 202140457D
AB    NOVELTY - Polyamine chain extender i.e.                N,N'-(4,4'-methylenebis(4,1-phenylene)bis(methylene))dipropan-2-amine                (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in surface modification                treatment agent, modified calcium hydroxide, and                microporous polyurethane elastic material (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) utilizes surface-anchored polyamine chain                extender and reactive tertiary amine catalyst                modified calcium hydroxide particles which can be                uniformly dispersed in the polyether polyol, can                also absorb carbon dioxide to generate chemical                foaming agent water, which acts as foaming core,                enabling foaming reaction to proceed efficiently                and uniformly. The microporous polyurethane elastic                material has high construction stability, uniform                product cells, and excellent impact absorption                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polyamine chain extender i.e.                N,N'-(4,4'-methylenebis(4,1-phenylene)bis(methylene))dipropan-2-amine                of formula (I) is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also                included for:
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) surface modification treatment agent,                comprising polyamine chain extenders and reactive                tertiary amine catalysts, where mass ratio of                polyamine chain extender to reactive tertiary amine                catalyst is 2-9:1, polyamine chain extender is the                polyamine chain extender, reactive tertiary amine                catalyst is                N,N,N',N''-tetramethyl-N''-3-aminopropyldiethylenetriamine,                bis(tetramethyldiethylenetriaminopropyl)amine,                N,N,N'-trimethyl-N'-hydroxyethyl bisaminoethyl                ether, N,N,N'-trimethyl-N'-aminopropyl diaminoethyl                ether, and/or N,N,N'-trimethyl-N'-hydroxyethyl                ethylenediamine;
   (3) modified calcium hydroxide obtained by                reacting calcium oxide under the action of the                surface modification treatment agent and deionized                water, where mass ratio of calcium oxide to surface                modification treatment agent is 10:0.5-3, mass                ratio of calcium oxide to deionized water is                1:10-30, reaction temperature is 50-95 degrees C,                and the reaction time is 1-4 hours; and
   (4) microporous polyurethane elastic material,                comprising isocyanate prepolymer component,                combination material component and catalyst                component, where isocyanate prepolymer component                includes 30-70 pts. wt. preferably 40-60 pts. wt.                polyether polyol, 1-30 pts. wt., preferably 5-15                pts. wt. plasticizer, 10-40 pts. wt., preferably                15-35 pts. wt. polyisocyanate, components of the                composition include 5-25 pts. wt., preferably 8-20                pts. wt. polyether polyol, 10-40 pts. wt.,                preferably 20-30 pts. wt. plasticizer, 1-5 pts.                wt., preferably 1.5-3.5 pts. wt. chain extender,                30-70 pts. wt., preferably 35-65 pts. wt.                conventional solid filler, 0.3-2 pts. wt.,                preferably 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. modified calcium                hydroxide and 0.1-2 pts. wt., preferably 0.2-1.5                pts. wt. carbon dioxide capture agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises reacting                  4,4'-bis(aminomethyl)diphenylmethane with acetone                  in presence of hydrogen and catalyst, where molar                  ratio of 4,4'-bis(aminomethyl)diphenylmethane to                  acetone is 1:3-5; catalyst is palladium/hydrogen                  type zeolite catalyst, and the amount is 1-4% of                  the mass of 4,4'-bis(aminomethyl)diphenylmethane;                  hydrogen pressure is 2-5 MPa; the reaction                  temperature is 100-150 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: The catalyst component is                  organometallic catalyst, preferably zinc                  neodecanoate, bismuth neodecanoate, and                  dibutyltin di(dodecylsulfide), where amount is                  0.1-0.6% of the amount of the components of the                  combined material. The functionality of the                  polyether polyol is 2-3, and the molecular weight                  is between 1000 and 6500; and/or the plasticizer                  is long-chain chlorinated paraffin, tributyl                  citrate, acetyl tributyl citrate, dioctyl                  terephthalate, chlorinated palm oil methyl ester                  and epoxidized soybean methyl oleate. The                  polyisocyanate is diisocyanate diphenylmethane or                  its derivatives; chain extender is                  dimethylthiodiaminotoluene,                  3,5-diethyltoluenediamine,                  4,4'-bis-sec-butylaminodiphenylmethane, and/or                  1,4-bis-sec-butylaminobenzene. The conventional                  solid filler is calcium carbonate, talc, kaolin,                  white carbon black and magnesium oxide; and/or                  the carbon dioxide capture agent is                  monoethanolamine, diethanolamine,                  triethanolamine, diisopropanolamine, and                  methyldiethanolamine. The microporous                  polyurethane elastic material has isocyanate                  index of 1-1.5.

PT P
PN CN112403476-A
TI High-efficiency metal catalyst useful for methanol steam cracking, comprises copper, nickel, aluminum oxide, potassium oxide and cerium(IV) oxide.
AU ZENG Q
   GU J
   CAI Q
   LIU H
   BO W
AE CAI Y (CAIY-Individual)
GA 202126453G
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficiency metal catalyst, is claimed. The catalyst shape is a fine particle with a diameter of 50-60nm, and catalyst is composed of 50-60 wt.% copper, 20-40 wt.% nickel, 3-8 wt.% aluminum oxide, 3-8 wt.% potassium oxide and 2-6 wt.% cerium(IV) oxide.
   USE - The high-efficiency metal catalyst is useful for methanol steam cracking (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst does not use noble metal, which greatly reduces the cost of methanol cracking hydrogen production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing high-efficiency metal catalyst, comprising (a) preparing carrier, where the carrier is made of aluminum oxide, which is firstly ground into a 60-80 mesh and activated at 500 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain aluminum oxide powder, (b) adding aluminum oxide powder into a three-necked flask, adding small amount of water, stirring and dissolving in a constant temperature water bath at 70 degrees C for 30 minutes, adding nitric acid solution and continue stirring for 4 hours, adding mixed solution of copper(II) nitrate, nickel(II) nitrate, potassium nitrate and cerium(IV) nitrate and stirring for 1 hour, slowly evaporating to dryness in a water bath at 80 degrees C, dry in an oven at 110 degrees C overnight, and calcining at 350 degrees C for 3 hours in a muffle furnace air atmosphere to prepare catalyst; and
   (2) use of high-efficiency metal catalyst in process of steam hydrogen production, comprising first uniformly filling the catalyst in the methanol steam hydrogen production reaction tank, heating the catalyst by heating the reaction tank, then passing the original methanol and water vapor into the preheating zone of the inlet pipe of the reaction tank, preheating, entering the reaction tank, making methanol quickly undergo a cracking reaction in a steam atmosphere and a constant temperature environment, converting the carbon-hydrogen-oxygen-containing organic matter in methanol into a hydrogen-rich mixture of hydrogen and carbon dioxide, where the mixed gas after the reaction passes through a condenser, a carbon dioxide chemical absorber, the tail gas collection and purification system inside the dryer obtains pure hydrogen, and the cracking reaction temperature is 200-300 degrees C, and the corresponding reaction conditions are: the pressure of water vapor is 4-5 atmospheres, and the mass ratio of water vapor and methanol feed volume per hour is 1-2/1, and the mass ratio of total feed amount of methanol and steam per hour to the catalyst is 10-30/1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The high-efficiency metal catalyst comprises 52-58 wt.% copper, 25-35 wt.% nickel, 4-7 wt.% aluminum oxide, 4-7 wt.% potassium oxide and 3-5 wt.% cerium(IV) oxide. Preferred Components: The raw material methanol is a coal chemical product. Preferred Method: The cracking reaction temperature is 220-280 degrees C when the reaction tank is used.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN112375910-A
TI Recycling waste power battery powder by            dynamically reducing and roasting waste power battery            powder, dissolving, filtering, adding purified water            into filter residue, adding concentrated sulfuric acid,            leaching and nickel soap extracting.
AU LI J
   DU C
   ZHU W
   HUANG X
AE HUNAN DEJINGYUAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202120320M
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling processing method of waste power                battery powder involves dynamically reducing and                roasting waste power battery powder, dissolving,                filtering, introducing carbon dioxide into                filtrate, separating precipitate, and drying; (B)                adding purified water into filter residue, adding                concentrated sulfuric acid, leaching, and nickel                soap extracting using P204 to obtain                sodium-containing saponification water phase                liquid, manganese-containing stripping liquid,                zinc-containing aluminum back extraction liquid and                cobalt-containing magnesium nickel extraction                residual liquid; and (C) extracting                manganese-containing stripping liquid by Cyanex 272                (RTM: dialkyl phosphinic acid extractant)                extractant, precipitating zinc-containing aluminum                back extraction liquid, processing                cobalt-containing magnesium nickel extraction                residual liquid, removing cobalt and magnesium to                obtain cobalt-magnesium-loaded organic and pure                nickel liquid, and stripping                cobalt-magnesium-loaded organic.
   USE - The method is used for recycling waste power                battery powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The recovery rate of the lithium is improved                by about 10%. The reduced nickel and cobalt are                reduced. The manganese is changed from high valence                state to low valence state is more complete. The                subsequent leaching process does not consume                reducing agent. The recycling of lithium is                economical and efficient. The recovery process of                cobalt and manganese considers the influence of                nickel recovery, using nickel saponification, and                the recovery cost of nickel is low.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling processing method of waste power                battery powder involves: (A) dynamically reducing                and roasting waste power battery powder to obtain                reduced and roasted battery powder, dissolving,                filtering to obtain filtrate and filter residue,                introducing carbon dioxide into filtrate,                separating generated precipitate by solid-liquid,                and drying to obtain lithium carbonate; (B) adding                purified water into filter residue, adding                concentrated sulfuric acid under stirring                condition, leaching to obtain solution containing                nickel, cobalt and manganese, and nickel soap                extracting using P204 to obtain sodium-containing                saponification water phase liquid,                manganese-containing stripping liquid,                zinc-containing aluminum back extraction liquid and                cobalt-containing magnesium nickel extraction                residual liquid; and (C) extracting                manganese-containing stripping liquid by Cyanex 272                (RTM: dialkyl phosphinic acid extractant)                extractant to obtain manganese sulfate,                precipitating zinc-containing aluminum back                extraction liquid to obtain zinc aluminum slag,                processing cobalt-containing magnesium nickel                extraction residual liquid by mixed extracting                agent, removing cobalt and magnesium to obtain                cobalt-magnesium-loaded organic and pure nickel                liquid, stripping cobalt-magnesium-loaded organic                to obtain cobalt solution and magnesium solution,                producing cobalt sulfate from cobalt solution, and                entering magnesium solution into waste water                treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Reduction and roasting is                  done at 400-650 degrees C for 1-3 hours under                  hydrogen atmosphere at pressure of 1-1.5 kPa.                  Introducing carbon dioxide in filtrate is done at                  60-70 degrees C for 2-5 hours. The final pH value                  of added sulfuric acid is 1.5-2.5. Preferred                  Components: The mixed extractant is mixture of                  P507 and Cyanex 272 (RTM: dialkyl phosphinic acid                  extractant).
