TI Separate aeration upflow aerobic granular sludge bed comprises water inlet pipe, water distribution tank, aeration tank, water collecting tank, flow-up reaction tank, reflux corridor and water outlet well.
AU QI W
   SHI Q
   LI B
   LIU L
AE SHANDONG TAISHAN ZIER ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021168444
AB    NOVELTY - Separate aeration upflow aerobic granular sludge bed comprises water inlet pipe (1), water distribution tank (2), aeration tank (3), water collecting tank (4), flow-up reaction tank (5), reflux corridor and water outlet well. The water inlet pipe is set to water distribution tank and is used to pass sewage to be treated into water distribution tank. An aeration device is set at bottom of aeration tank. The bottom of flow-up reaction tank is set with multiple lower-flow water passage. A lower leakage passage communicating with inlet end of downflow water passage is set in water collecting tank. A multiple water outlet is evenly opened on side walls of downflow water passage. The upper part of one end of reflux corridor is set to outlet well, and other end is set to water distribution tank. The flow-up reaction tank is cultured with particle sludge. The flow-up reaction tank is set with dissolved oxygen probe for measuring dissolved oxygen value.
   USE - Used as separate aeration upflow aerobic granular sludge bed.
   ADVANTAGE - The bed: realizes the self-circulation of sewage under the action of aeration without a power source; reduces energy consumption; separately carry out sewage treatment; cooperates with other sewage treatment units in the sewage plant to oxidize organic matter and ammonia nitrogen to carbon dioxide and nitrate nitrogen through aerobic reaction; and converts anaerobic reaction inside the large particle sludge and the upper part of the flow-up reaction tank into nitrate nitrogen into nitrogen and removes it.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for processing sewage treatment based on the separate aeration upflow aerobic granular sludge bed by cooperating with traditional nitrification and denitrification wastewater treatment process for wastewater treatment or anaerobic ammonia oxidation sewage treatment process for sewage treatment, using aeration device to aerate and oxygenate the sewage entering the bottom of the aeration tank, realizes the oxygenation of sewage, removing organic matter and ammonia nitrogen, oxidizing organic matter in water to carbon dioxide under aerobic conditions by microorganisms, oxidizing ammonia nitrogen to nitrate nitrogen, carrying out the reaction denitrification reaction under the action of anaerobic microorganisms, consuming organic matter in the water and converts nitrate nitrogen into nitrogen for removing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the separate aeration upflow aerobic granular sludge bed.
   Water inlet pipe (1)
   Water distribution tank (2)
   Aeration tank (3)
   Water collecting tank (4)
   Flow-up reaction tank (5)

PN CN112293473-A
TI Performing cold-preservation of green peppers involves harvesting in the morning and evening when temperature is cool without dew and rainy weather, and using rust-free scissors when cutting the green pepper together with the handle.
AU LIU J
   LIU Y
   LIU Z
AE JIANGSU KANGRUN AGRIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY (JIAN-Non-standard)
   LIU J (LIUJ-Individual)
GA 202113681V
AB    NOVELTY - Performing cold-preservation of green peppers involves harvesting in the morning and evening when temperature is cool without dew and rainy weather, and using rust-free scissors when cutting the green pepper together with the handle. The green peppers are harvested, graded according to the dark green, enlarged, hard and bright green peppers, and then green pepper leaves, pest green peppers, injured green peppers and young peppers are removed. The selected green peppers are selected according to appropriate proportion and placed into the bag with the hole in the bag. The green peppers are placed into storage. The bagged green peppers are placed at temperature of 15-20 degrees C for 2.5-4 hours for pre-cooling, during which environmental humidity in the pre-cooling area of the green peppers is ensured. The temperature is adjusted in the cold storage to keep at 8-12 degrees C, and ensured that the relative humidity in the storage space is 85-95%.
   USE - Method for performing cold-preservation of green peppers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents gray mold, ensures storage effect, air not circulating bacteria, reduces respiratory strength of green pepper, condensation phenomenon in storage, prolongs green pepper storage period, maintains freshness of green pepper, ensures refrigerated storage effect, improves storage micro environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing cold-preservation of green peppers involves harvesting in the morning and evening when temperature is cool without dew and rainy weather, and using rust-free scissors when cutting the green pepper together with the handle. The green peppers are harvested, graded according to the dark green, enlarged, hard and bright green peppers, and then green pepper leaves, pest green peppers, injured green peppers and young peppers are removed. The selected green peppers are selected according to appropriate proportion and placed into the bag with the hole in the bag. The green peppers are placed into storage. The bagged green peppers are placed at temperature of 15-20 degrees C for 2.5-4 hours for pre-cooling, during which environmental humidity in the pre-cooling area of the green peppers is ensured. The temperature is adjusted in the cold storage to keep at 8-12 degrees C, and ensured that the relative humidity in the storage space is 85-95%. The oxygen concentration is 3-5%, carbon dioxide concentration is 1-5%, and slaked lime is placed next to the storage stack to adjust the carbon dioxide concentration, then organized units to conduct regular inspections, turned over the library, and cleaned up rotten and diseased fruits in time.

PN WO2020252523-A1; AU2020296474-A1
TI Separation and recycling of textile fabrics used as e.g. stuffing, involves mixing shredded textile material feed with sulfuric acid, heating and pressurizing mixed shredded material, dewatering, filtering, and discharging polyester fiber.
AU SPEIGHT R
   OHARA I
   ZHANYING ZHANG J
   MOLLER D
   JONES A
   ROSS G
   ZHANYING Z J
AE BLOCKTEXX LABS PTY LTD (BLOC-Non-standard)
   BLOCKTEXX LABS PTY LTD (BLOC-Non-standard)
GA 2020C9602U
AB    NOVELTY - Separation and recycling of textile fabrics involves shredding a material feed, mixing the shredded feed material with an aqueous solution of sulfuric acid and water, heating and pressuring the mixed feed material and aqueous solution in a sealed container to a temperature and pressure sufficient for acid catalyzation resulting in a liquid comprising cellulose particles and polyester fiber, dewatering the output from the (S3) to obtain a prewashed dewatered fiber mix, filtering the free liquid to recover cellulose particles present and using at least some of the filtered liquid to supplement the aqueous solution, washing the dewatered fiber mix obtained with a liquid wash and agitating the mixture for a period of time, dewatering the output to obtain the washed dewatered mix, and outputting both the recovered cellulose particles and the polyester fibers present in the washed dewatered mix.
   USE - Separation and recycling of textile fabrics for reuse e.g. as stuffing in punching bags or converted to non woven materials such as insulation and floor matting.
   ADVANTAGE - The separation and recycling of textile fabrics provides a highly efficient, zero waste recycling ecosystem recovering more than 95% of the raw materials from the polyester and cellulose product, and results in carbon dioxide equivalent reduction with every 1 kg of textiles recycled leading to 30 kg carbon dioxide equivalent reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation and recycling of textile fabrics involves shredding a material feed comprising the textile fabrics, mixing the shredded feed material with an aqueous solution of sulfuric acid and water, heating and pressuring the mixed feed material and aqueous solution in a sealed container to a temperature and pressure sufficient for acid catalyzation resulting in a liquid comprising cellulose particles and polyester fiber, dewatering the output to obtain a prewashed dewatered fiber mix, filtering the free liquid to recover cellulose particles present and using at least some of the filtered liquid to supplement the aqueous solution, washing the dewatered fiber mix obtained with a liquid wash and agitating the mixture for a period of time, dewatering the output to obtain the washed dewatered mix and filtering the resultant free liquid to recover cellulose particles, and outputting both the recovered cellulose particles and the polyester fibers present in the washed dewatered mix. The textile fabrics are formed of blended polyester and cellulosic material. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for the separation and recycling of textile fabrics.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The aqueous solution comprises approximately 2-4% sulfuric acid by solution, preferably 2.5% sulfuric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Component: The washed dewatered fiber mix output comprises recycled polyethylene terephthalate of high grade and quality.

PN CN111661849-A
TI Preparing food grade white carbon black by inorganic acid segmented precipitation by adding high-purity sodium silicate and additive, adjusting temperature, aging suspension, preparing filter cake into suspension, drying and grinding.
AU ZHANG J
   LU P
   YANG G
   XU Z
   CHEN L
AE KUNMING COLLEGE METALLURGY (KUNM-Non-standard)
GA 202092187T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing food grade white carbon black by an inorganic acid segmented precipitation involves (i) preparing food grade white carbon black precipitate by adding 20-90 g/l high-purity sodium silicate as a silicon source, adding an additive, adjusting temperature to 60-90 degrees C, adding dilute sulfuric acid to the reactor, stopping adding dilute sulfuric acid to the solution, when the pH of the solution reduced to 9.5 and simultaneously introducing carbon dioxide and nitrogen mixed gas into the reactor until the pH of the solution is reduced to 8 and stopping aeration to obtain a suspension and (ii) performing the food grade white carbon black precipitation post-treatment by aging the suspension for 30-60 minutes, adjusting pH to 7 with dilute hydrochloric acid, aging for 30-60 minutes, filtering, repeatedly washing the obtained filter cake with deionized water for 3-4 times, removing impurity ions and preparing the filter cake into suspension, drying and grinding.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing food grade white carbon black by an inorganic acid segmented precipitation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts segmented control of silicon dioxide colloid nucleation, growth and polymerization process, realizes regulation and control of the surface structure and the hydroxyl group distribution of the white carbon black particles and is a new method for control synthesis of the food-grade white carbon black.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing food grade white carbon black by an inorganic acid segmented precipitation involves (i) preparing food grade white carbon black precipitate by adding 20-90 g/l high-purity sodium silicate as a silicon source, adding an additive, adjusting temperature to 60-90 degrees C, adding dilute sulfuric acid with the mass fraction of 10% to the reactor, stopping adding dilute sulfuric acid to the solution, when the pH of the solution reduced to 9.5 and simultaneously introducing carbon dioxide and nitrogen mixed gas into the reactor until the pH of the solution is reduced to 8 and stopping aeration to obtain a suspension and (ii) performing the food grade white carbon black precipitation post-treatment by aging the suspension for 30-60 minutes, adjusting pH to 7 with dilute hydrochloric acid, aging for 30-60 minutes, filtering, repeatedly washing the obtained filter cake with deionized water for 3-4 times, removing impurity ions and preparing the filter cake into suspension with the mass concentration of 10%, drying and grinding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves adopting sulfuric acid precipitation for rapid nucleation in the early stage and carbonization precipitation for slow growth in the later stage, where the pH of the solution at the end of the sulfate precipitation is 9.5. The step (ii) involves drying by spray drying in the air flow of 240-270 degrees C with a spray dryer. Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide and nitrogen mixed gas comprises 25-40 vol.% carbon dioxide, where the gas velocity of the mixed gas is 0.4-1.2 l/minute. Preferred Components: The impurity ions comprises sodium ion, chloride ion, carbonate ion and sulfate ion.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The additive in step (i) is polyethylene glycol (PEG)6000 with the mass concentration of 1-6 g/l.

TI Use of human amniotic membrane epithelial cell            exosomes for e.g. preparing of medicines for repairing            ovarian function after chemotherapy damage, and            reducing damage to ovary, and preventing and treating            premature ovarian failure.
AU LAI D
   ZHANG Q
   SUN J
   HUANG Y
AE INT PEACE MATERNITY & CHILD HEALTH HOSPI (ITPE-Non-standard)
GA 202087539P
AB    NOVELTY - Use of human amniotic membrane epithelial cell                exosomes, is claimed.
   USE - The microRNA-1246 is useful for preparing                medicine for repairing ovarian function. The human                amniotic membrane epithelial cell exosomes are                useful for preparing of medicines for repairing                ovarian function after chemotherapy damage,                reducing damage to the ovary, preventing and                treating premature ovarian failure or preventing                and treating infertility caused by chemotherapy                medicines (all claimed), can significantly                increasing the number of ovarian follicles damaged,                inhibiting acute ovarian vascular injury and                granulosa cell apoptosis and partially inhibiting                the activating of primordial follicles induced by                chemotherapy medicines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the human amniotic membrane epithelial                cell exosomes comprising (i) isolating the human                amniotic membrane epithelial cells from fresh                amniotic membrane tissue of healthy complete-term                mothers by 0.25% trypsin digestion for 10-15                minutes, preferably 12 minutes, resuspending in                Dulbecco's modified essential medium/Ham's F-12                (DMEM/F12) medium containing fetal bovine serum,                inoculating in a cell culture dish, placing at 37                degrees C cell incubator containing 5% carbon                dioxide for culture, using 0.25% trypsin to                digesting the adherent cells for 2-3 minutes,                preferably 2 minutes, when the cell confluence                reaching 70-80%, resuspending in DMEM/F12 medium                containing fetal bovine serum, readjusting the cell                density and inoculating in the cell culture dish,                placing at 37 degrees C cell incubator containing                5% carbon dioxide, aspirating the complete medium                containing serum when the cell density reaching                70-80%, slowly washing with phosphate-buffered                saline for 2-3 times, preferably 3 times, replacing                the serum-free DMEM/F12 medium for culturing for 20                hours, preferably 24 hours, slowly aspirating the                medium, collecting the supernatant by centrifuging                at 1500-2500 g, preferably 2000 g for 20-40                minutes, preferably 30 minutes using a 0.2 mu m                filter to filtering the obtained medium to removing                residual cell debris to obtained conditioned                culture medium, and (ii) transferring the                conditioned culture medium obtained above into a                concentrating tube for volume concentration,                transferring the concentrated conditioned culture                medium into a centrifuging tube, adding exosome                extraction reagents, uniformly shaking and mixing,                incubating at 2-8 degrees C, centrifuging at 4                degrees C under 8000-12000 g on the second day,                centrifuging for 0.5-1.5 hours, and attaching tiny                precipitates exosomes to the bottom of the tube                wall.

TI Triangular prism processing technology comprises            e.g. dividing the bulk raw material into large-scale            semi-finished products, grinding, polishing, bonding,            immersing in water, chamfering, laser cutting,            separating, grinding and polishing.
AU HAN W
   GE W
   DENG Y
AE HANGZHOU MDK OPTO ELECTRONICS CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 202062963A
AB    NOVELTY - Triangular prism processing technology                comprises e.g. dividing the bulk raw material into                large-scale semi-finished products, grinding and                polishing, bonding the large piece-shaped                semi-finished products processed together by a                water-soluble glue to obtain large block-shaped                semi-finished product, dividing again the large                block semi-finished product to obtain small block                semi-finished product, grinding and polishing the                upper and lower sides formed by cutting the small                block semi-finished product to obtain straight                four-sided prisms with all four sides bonded to                each other, immersing the straight quadrangular                prism in water to degumming to obtain many single                straight quadrangular prisms, and cleaning the                obtained straight quadrangular prisms, chamfering                one side of the straight prism, laser cutting the                straight quadrangular prism into a strip-shaped                triangular prism along the chamfered side,                separating, grinding and polishing.
   USE - The method is useful triangular prism                processing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes laser cutting method,                which improves the material utilization rate and                improves the production efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Triangular prism processing technology                comprises (i) dividing the bulk raw material into                large-scale semi-finished products, (ii) grinding                and polishing the upper and lower sides formed by                dividing the large semi-finished product, (iii)                bonding the large piece-shaped semi-finished                products processed in step (ii) together by a                water-soluble glue to obtain large block-shaped                semi-finished product, (iv) dividing again the                large block semi-finished product of step (iii) to                obtain small block semi-finished product, (v)                grinding and polishing the upper and lower sides                formed by cutting the small block semi-finished                product in step (iv) to obtain straight four-sided                prisms with all four sides bonded to each other,                (vi) immersing the straight quadrangular prism in                step (v) in water to degumming to obtain many                single straight quadrangular prisms, and cleaning                the obtained straight quadrangular prisms, (vii)                chamfering one side of the straight prism cleaned                in step (vi), (viii) laser cutting the straight                quadrangular prism in step (vii) into a                strip-shaped triangular prism along the chamfered                side, (ix) separating the long triangular prism in                step (viii) by a carbon dioxide cracker, (x)                grinding and polishing the cut surface of the long                triangular prism in step (ix), (xi) laser cutting                the long triangular prism after grinding into a                rough finished triangular prism, (xii) separating                the crude product of the triangular prism in step                (xi) by a carbon dioxide cracker, and (xii)                grinding and polishing the rough surface of the                triangular prism to form finished triangular                prism.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (0) before step                  (i) comprises trimming the raw materials with                  irregular corners into block-shaped raw materials                  with flat surfaces and four sides at right                  angles. The method between steps (i) and (0)                  comprises printing cutting mark for cutting                  alignment on the surface of the long triangular                  prism. The method further comprises cleaning the                  finished triangular prism to make the surface                  dirty.

PN CN111073208-A
TI Acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene material used for three-dimensional printing, comprises acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene resin, antioxidant, compatibilizer, impact modifier, inorganic filler, deodorizing agent and plasticizer.
AU GUO B
   LV T
   WANG J
AE UNIV TSINGHUA INNOVATION CENT RES INST (UYQI-Non-standard)
GA 202038018D
AB    NOVELTY - Acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene material comprises 42-99.4 wt.% acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene resin, 0.1-3 wt.% antioxidant, 0.1-5 wt.% compatibilizer, 0.1-10 wt.% impact modifier, 0.1-20 wt.% inorganic filler, 0.1-10 wt.% deodorizing agent and 0.1-10 wt.% plasticizer.
   USE - The acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene material is used for three-dimensional printing (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has high printing accuracy and smooth printing, and is not easy to cause problems such as drawing, cracking and warping, which affect the quality of printed products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene material, which involves (i) preparing the above-mentioned materials other than plasticizer, placing the raw materials in a premixer for uniformly mixing, heating at 60-80 degrees C and heat-preserving for 30-120 minutes to obtain a premix, (ii) adding the premix obtained in step (i) to a twin-screw extruder, injecting plasticizer through a gear pump, injecting water, carbon dioxide and/or nitrogen in the middle of the extruder, pumping out water, carbon dioxide and nitrogen in the vacuum pumping section, maintaining the vacuum pressure in the vacuum pumping section at -0.05 to -0.1 MPa, pelletizing to obtain acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene granules, where the screw speed of the twin-screw extruder is 50-400 rpm and the barrel temperature is 170-280 degrees C, and (iii) drying the acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene granules, using the polymer spinning process to add the granule product to the extruder which uses the traction machine to draw, adjusting the material thread by the adjustment of the extrusion speed and the traction speed, shaping the material by water/air cooling, and winding by a servo motor/torque motor, where the material wire is controlled at 1.1-3.5 mm, the extruder speed is controlled at 10-200 rpm and the barrel temperature is controlled at 170-260 degrees C, traction winding speed is controlled at 10-120 m/min, and the cooling water temperature is controlled at 40-95 degrees C; and
   (2) the use of the acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene material for three-dimensional printing, where the printing speed is controlled to 10-150 mm/second, the print temperature is controlled to 190-260 degrees C, the print layer height is controlled to 0.05-0.2 mm, the print bottom plate temperature is controlled to 40-110 degrees C, and the printing room temperature is controlled to 40-100 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic filler comprises barium sulfate, glass microspheres, titanium dioxide, attapulgite, boron nitride, calcium carbonate, talc powder and/or clay. The deodorizing agent porous aluminum silicon, organic chelating agent, nano-deodorizing agent and/or inorganic silicate material with single pore structure.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antioxidant comprises tetra ( beta -(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl) propionic acid) pentaerythritol ester, tris (2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl) phosphite, triethylene glycol bis( beta -(3-tert-butyl-4-hydroxy-5-methylphenyl) propionate), beta -(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl) octadecyl propionate and/or dioctadecyl alcohol pentaerythritol diphosphite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene resin is an acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene terpolymer produced by bulk, emulsion or suspension polymerization. The compatibilizer comprises a blend of maleic anhydride grafted acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene and styrene and/or a cyclic anhydride grafted acrylonitrile-butadiene-styrene. The impact modifier comprises natural rubber, styrene, acrylonitrile-butadiene rubber, styrene-based thermoplastic elastomer and/or thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer. The plasticizer comprises odorless and environmentally-friendly medium and high molecular weight polyesters with molecular weight of 1500-5000 and viscosity of 1500-8000 cps.

PN CN110918998-A; CN110918998-B
TI High damping specific grade aluminum/titanium composite material, prepared by e.g. mixing pure titanium powder and ammonium bicarbonate particles, press-forming, heating, discharging carbon dioxide and ammonia, sintering and post processing.
AU JIANG H
   ZHANG P
   WANG Y
   LIU C
   QIN F
AE UNIV GUILIN TECHNOLOGY (UYGI-C)
   UNIV GUILIN TECHNOLOGY (UYGI-C)
GA 2020259814
AB    NOVELTY - High damping 5083 aluminum/titanium composite material, is claimed. The reinforced phase of the titanium-based composite material is 5083 aluminum alloy. The substrate is porous pure titanium block obtained by sintering pure titanium powder. The pore-forming agent is ammonium bicarbonate particles having a particle size of 300-500 mu m, and content of reinforcing phase in the substrate is 16-48 wt.%. The 5083 aluminum/titanium composite material has excellent damping performance, lower density than pure titanium, can control the size of reinforced phase, and there is no problem of interface reaction. The damping performance of the composite material is improved at 30-200 degrees C when compared with its substrate material, the internal friction value of composite material is increased by 220-750% at 30 degrees C compared with its internal consumption value, and the internal friction value is 190-380% higher than that in the substrate at 200 degrees C.
   USE - Used as high damping 5083 aluminum/titanium composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has excellent damping performance within 30-200 degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing high damping 5083 aluminum/titanium composite material, comprising (i) placing pure titanium powder and ammonium bicarbonate particles in a V-shaped powder mixing machine, mixing by rotating for 5 hours, placing a certain amount of mixed powder in a square mold, press-forming by a universal material testing machine at room temperature under a pressing force of 100-300 MPa and placing pressed blank in a quartz tube-type sintering furnace, where the mold specification is length 30 mmxwidth 10 mmxheight 5 mm, (ii) continuously passing argon gas as protective gas having a purity of 99.9% into the quartz tube-type sintering furnace, heating sintering furnace from room temperature for 30 minutes, heating to 150-300 degrees C for 1-2 hours, continuously passing high-purity argon gas to discharge carbon dioxide and ammonia produced by the decomposition of ammonium bicarbonate, then heating to 1050 degrees C, maintaining for 1-2 hours for sintering, and cooling to room temperature with the furnace to obtain porous pure titanium, and (iii) placing 508 aluminum alloy in a small smelting furnace, heating smelting furnace to 800 degrees C, completely melting aluminum block, placing sintered porous pure titanium in the melting furnace, reacting at 800 degrees C for 2 hours to allow the molten aluminum to penetrate into porous pure titanium, and taking out the sample, air-cooling to obtain final product.

PN CN110915889-A
TI Smart control technology based on the storage and preservation of agricultural products comprises e.g. pre-processing the agricultural products, choosing the storage environment, and setting up storage environment control equipment.
AU LI J
   LU H
   LI X
   WANG Y
AE ZHEJIANG QIUSHI ARTIFICIAL ENVIRONMENT (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202026053U
AB    NOVELTY - Smart control technology based on the storage and preservation of agricultural products comprises e.g. (1) pre-processing the agricultural products before storage, and choosing the storage environment after pre-processing, (2) setting up storage environment control equipment, installing heater, temperature controller, humidifier, fan, intelligent temperature control ventilation system and pressure regulator in the storage environment, using high temperature of heater, sterilizing the sealed storage environment, controlling storage environment temperature by temperature controller, controlling the storage environment humidity by humidifier, providing fan, controlling the nitrogen concentration, carbon dioxide concentration and air pressure in the storage environment through smart temperature control ventilation system and pressure adjustment device, (3) uploading the parameters corresponding to agricultural products pretreatment and storage environment parameters to server.
   USE - The method is useful for smart control technology based on the storage and preservation of agricultural products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Smart control technology based on the storage and preservation of agricultural products comprises (1) pre-processing the agricultural products before storage, and choosing the storage environment after pre-processing, (2) setting up storage environment control equipment, and installing a heater, temperature controller, humidifier, fan, intelligent temperature control ventilation system and pressure regulator in the storage environment, using the high temperature of the heater, sterilizing the sealed storage environment, controlling the storage environment temperature by a temperature controller, controlling the storage environment humidity by a humidifier, providing a fan, controlling the nitrogen concentration, carbon dioxide concentration and air pressure in the storage environment through the smart temperature control ventilation system and pressure adjustment device, (3) uploading the parameters corresponding to the agricultural products pretreatment and storage environment parameters to the server through wireless communication technology, retrieving the storage parameters of the server by experts, and obtaining the corresponding simulated process parameters and the corresponding preservation data of agricultural products through server simulation control, and storing the optimal storage environment, environmental control parameters and corresponding agricultural product preservation data information on the server, (4) retrieving the optimal storage environment control parameters in the server, and setting the relevant parameters of the heater, temperature controller, humidifier, fan, intelligent temperature control ventilation system and pressure adjustment device, and collecting preservation data corresponding to agricultural products in stages, and (5) according to the comparison between the actual preservation data of the agricultural products and the simulation data, if it exceeds the reasonable range, notifying the expert team to simulate the control again and adjusting the optimal parameters, and notifying the user to call.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The pre-processing includes cleaning treatment, air drying treatment and sterilization treatment. Preferred Components: The server follows up the user's request, and sends the simulated process parameters to the intelligent network communication controller, and the intelligent network communication controller according to the process recipe, and remotes intelligent control storage environment control equipment operation. The server compares the actual periodic preservation data corresponding to the agricultural products with the preservation data corresponding to the agricultural products under the simulated situation, and forms an alarm prompt to feed back to the client. The user knows the corresponding preservation data of agricultural products through remote control, when the real-time temperature and humidity of fruits and vegetables do not meet the set value, and then the user remotely sends control signals to the server through intelligent network communication control requesting expert help.

PN CN110791476-A
TI Isolating and culturing human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells useful for treating chronic insomnia, comprises e.g. collecting umbilical cord under sterile conditions, culturing, removing tissue block and detecting by flow cytometry.
AU ZOU B
   ZHANG M
AE TONGREN FANTEER BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 202016789K
AB    NOVELTY - Isolating and culturing human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells comprises (i) collecting the umbilical cord under sterile conditions; (ii) cutting the umbilical cord into small sections, and removing the arteries and veins comprising two arteries and one vein; (iii) cutting into glass bottles and aliquot directly into petri dishes; (iv) adding culture medium, mixing uniformly, set in culturing at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide in saturation humidity incubator overnight and adding 1 ml culture medium; (v) half volume changing for 48 hours and volume changing 3-4 days for every 3-4 days; (vi) removing the tissue block; (vii) washing twice with phosphate-buffered saline when the number of cells reaches to 108, digesting by typsin, adding medium into stop digestion; and (viii) detecting Epstein-Barr virus, cytomegalovirus, HIV virus, hepatitis B virus, mycoplasma, anaerobic culture, and fungal detection, which must be negative and judged as qualified, freezing and storing.
   USE - The cells are useful for treating chronic insomnia.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simpler and more efficient and easy to implement and reduces the cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Isolating and culturing human umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells comprises (i) collecting the umbilical cord under sterile conditions and sending to the laboratory; (ii) cutting the umbilical cord into small sections about 1 cm each, and removing the arteries and veins comprising two arteries and one vein; (iii) cutting into glass bottles and aliquot directly into 60 mm petri dishes; (iv) adding 2 ml culture medium, mixing uniformly, set in culturing at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide in saturation humidity incubator overnight and adding 1 ml culture medium; (v) half volume changing for 48 hours and volume changing 3-4 days for every 3-4 days; (vi) removing the tissue block after 15-20 days after the primary cells reaches to 60-80% of the culture dish, which gives 1:2 passage; (vii) washing twice with phosphate-buffered saline when the number of cells reaches to 108, digesting by adding 0.05% typsin-ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid solution, adding medium into stop digestion, making 1-3x 106 cell suspension and detecting by flow cytometry, which shows CD34 is negative, CD109 is negative, and the CD29 and CD44 positive rates are more than 95%; and (viii) detecting Epstein-Barr virus, cytomegalovirus, HIV virus, hepatitis B virus, mycoplasma, anaerobic culture, and fungal detection, which must be negative and judged as qualified, freezing and storing in -196 degrees C liquid nitrogen tank.
TF ACTIVITY - Hypnotic.

PN CN110698094-A; CN110698094-B
TI Explosion-proof preparation method for cement production based on online monitoring, comprises adding limestone, fly ash and gypsum, dividing viscous raw meal into raw meal blocks, calcining and grinding in clinker.
AU WEI Y
   CHENG F
   WEI M
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN TECHNOLOGY (UYGL-C)
GA 202008421P
AB    NOVELTY - Explosion-proof preparation method comprises (i) adding limestone, fly ash and gypsum are pre-dried at room temperature and drying under carbon dioxide protection to less than 2% moisture content, (ii) introducing raw materials into ball mill and grounding and adding multi-gap and double-control ball, (iii) placing raw materials in a high-temperature furnace for heating treatment, heating at 450 degrees C, removes chemically bound water from raw meal ingredients, heating to 900-950 degrees C, make amorphous material in raw material into crystal shape, (iv) heating, mixing prepared raw material with water and other materials, mixing slowly stir until uniformly mixed to obtain sticky raw meal, (v) dividing viscous raw meal into raw meal blocks of same size, placing raw meal blocks in a furnace for calcination. chilling in air, making clinker, and (vi) introducing cement clinker into a fine grinding mill for grinding and grinding clinker to a suitable particle size.
   USE - The explosion-proof preparation method is useful for cement production based on online monitoring(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves dispersibility of material and prevents explosion-proof preparation of two-pronged cement production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Explosion-proof preparation method comprises 50-60 pts. wt. limestone, 40-60 pts. wt. fly ash, 5-10 pts. wt. gypsum powder, 30-45 pts. wt. fine sand, 2-5 pts. wt. polypropylene fiber, 0.5-2 pts. wt. water reducing agent, 0.2-0.5 pts. wt. additives, 10-15 pts. wt. dispersant, 15-30 pts. wt. multi-gap double ball and 100-150 pts. wt. water. The method comprises (i) adding limestone, fly ash and gypsum are pre-dried at room temperature, drying under carbon dioxide protection to less than 2% moisture content, (ii) introducing raw materials into ball mill and grounding, adding multi-gap and double-control ball, (iii) placing raw materials in a high-temperature furnace for heating treatment, heating at 450 degrees C, removes chemically bound water from raw meal ingredients, heating to 900-950 degrees C, make amorphous material in raw material into crystal shape, (iv) heating, mixing prepared raw material with water and other materials, mixing slowly stir until uniformly mixed to obtain sticky raw meal, (v) dividing viscous raw meal into raw meal blocks of same size, placing raw meal blocks in a furnace for calcination. Where, the calcination temperature is 1400 degrees C, chilling in air, making clinker, and (vi) introducing cement clinker into a fine grinding mill for grinding and grinding clinker to a suitable particle size.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the particle size of the raw meal is controlled to 200-300 mesh, and is monitored online by a sensor and sensor comprises coal dust sensor, an oxygen content sensor, and a temperature sensor. The multi-gap dual-control ball comprises grinding core and a self-peeling multilayer adsorption shell (2). The self-peeling multilayer adsorption shell comprises on outer surface of the grinding core (1). An elastic airbag (3) is connected to center of inner wall of the grinding core (1). The elastic airbag (3) is filled with a multiple of free heat-conducting impact copper balls (4). A grinding aid is filled between the grinding inner core and elastic bladder. The grinding inner core is excavated with a multiple of evenly distributed ultra-fine holes. The elastic airbag is further filled with a three-state filler (5), and three-state filler (5) comprises nitrogen, conductive silicone oil, and thermally conductive sand and ratio of the three is 1:1:0.1-0.2. The self-peeling multilayer adsorption shell comprises modified bentonite, and the grinding aid is triisopropanolamine, ethylene glycol, propylene glycol, diethylene glycol, and sugar ether. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the temperature is increased to 450 degrees C at a temperature increase rate of 10 degrees C/minutes , and then raised to 900-950 degrees C at a temperature increase rate of 50 degrees C/minutes for 30 minutes, and water is rapidly cooled after 1-2 hours. In the step (iv) the polypropylene fibers are evenly dispersed, After there is no solidarity, adding additive to cement. The additive is a mixture of white carbon and an activator and mixing ratio is 1:1 and activator is a liquid activator. The self-peeling multilayer adsorption shell comprises multiple of peeling single layers, and thickness of the peeling single layers sequentially becomes diluent from inside to outside, and fineness of the raw materials becomes larger in order. The preparation of modified bentonite (a) washing , drying, crushing, sieving pretreatment measures to purify natural bentonite into fine powder materials of 300-500 mesh, using different screens to classify bentonite by fineness, mixing classified bentonite with deionized water, adding small amount of dispersant, stirring, allowing to stand , filtering and drying, (b) drying bentonite from room temperature to 300 degrees C, contacting water vapor with bentonite by inert gas nitrogen as carrier gas, heating to 350-400 degrees C for 2-4 hours, passing in 30 minutes of hydrochloric acid steam and cooling naturally, removing bentonite to room temperature and washing with absolute ethanol and deionized water.

PN CN110628713-A
TI Extracting and preparing adipose-derived stem cell            by collecting adipose tissue from hip, cutting, washing            using phosphate-buffered saline, adding with type I            collagenase and EDTA, centrifuging, incubating, and            culturing cells.
AU ZHANG P
AE ANHUI KEMEN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202002554J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting and preparing                adipose-derived stem cell involves (a) collecting                adipose tissue from above hip using a sterile                needle, (b) cutting the collected adipose tissue,                (c) washing the adipose tissue fragments with                phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), wiping adipose                tissue, and soaking adipose tissue in physiological                saline, (d) performing enzymolysis by adding mixed                solution of type I collagenase and EDTA to the                adipose tissue fragments, (e) adding an appropriate                amount of PBS to the adipose tissue fragments,                mixing and filtering, and centrifuging at low                speed, (f) incubating in incubator containing                carbon dioxide, changing the medium for the first                time after 48 hours, discarding the suspended                cells, changing medium, passaging, and growing the                cells from the tissue block into primary cells, (g)                passing the third-generation cells twice by flow                cytometer for detecting, and (h) transferring the                cells to the medium, and culturing to obtain                adipose-derived stem cells.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting and                preparing adipose-derived stem cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids bacterial contamination of                adipose tissue during the cultivation process. The                produced stem cell is easily adapted to the human                environment as its culture temperature imitates the                temperature environment of the human body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting and preparing                adipose-derived stem cell involves (a) collecting                adipose tissue from above hip using a sterile                needle, (b) cutting the collected adipose tissue,                (c) washing the adipose tissue fragments with                phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), wiping adipose                tissue using ethanol cotton, and soaking adipose                tissue in physiological saline for sterilizing                adipose tissue, (d) performing enzymolysis by                adding mixed solution of type I collagenase and                EDTA to the adipose tissue fragments, and shaking                thoroughly in a 37 degrees C water bath, (e) adding                an appropriate amount of PBS to the adipose tissue                fragments, mixing and filtering, and centrifuging                at low speed, (f) incubating in a 37 degrees C                incubator containing carbon dioxide, changing the                medium for the first time after 48 hours,                discarding the suspended cells, changing medium                every 3 days, passaging, and growing the cells from                the tissue block into primary cells, (g) passing                the third-generation cells twice by flow cytometer                for detecting, and observing whether the cells are                concentrated to 128 cells/l, and (h) transferring                the cells to the medium when the cells were                concentrated to 128 cells/l, and culturing for 2                weeks to obtain adipose-derived stem cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves (a)                  collecting 10 ml adipose tissue from above hip,                  (b) cutting the collected adipose tissue into a                  size of 2 mm, (c) washing the adipose tissue                  fragments 5 times with phosphate-buffered saline                  (PBS), wiping adipose tissue using ethanol                  cotton, and soaking adipose tissue in                  physiological saline for 2 minutes, (d) (i)                  adding 10 ml mixed solution of type I collagenase                  and EDTA in a volume ratio of 1:3 to the adipose                  tissue fragments, or (ii) adding 20 ml mixed                  solution of type I collagenase and EDTA in a                  volume ratio of 1:2 to the adipose tissue                  fragments, and shaking thoroughly in a 37 degrees                  C water bath for 30 minutes, (e) centrifuging at                  low speed of 800 revolutions per minute (rpm) for                  10 minutes, and (f) incubating in a 37 degrees C                  incubator containing 5 vol.% carbon dioxide,                  changing the medium for the first time after 48                  hours, discarding the suspended cells, changing                  medium every 3 days, passaging after 7 or 8 days,                  and growing the cells from the tissue block into                  primary cells. The mixed solution of EDTA and                  type I collagenase is stirred at a speed of 500                  rpm for 5-10 minutes, and stored in an                  environment of 2-8 degrees C.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN110613649-A
TI Preparing Tieguanyin extract whitening sunscreen base liquid involves crushing Tieguanyin tea leaves, mulberry leaves, red-leaf snake grape leaves, honeysuckle, tea tree flower raw materials, and then drying to obtain powdery raw materials.
AU ZHANG Z
   LIN H
   CHEN C
   LIN Z
AE UNIV HUNAN AGRIC (UYAG-C)
   FUJIAN ANXI TIEKUANYIN GROUP CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202003550D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Tieguanyin extract whitening sunscreen base liquid involves crushing Tieguanyin tea leaves, mulberry leaves, red-leaf snake grape leaves, honeysuckle, tea tree flower raw materials to 100-300 mesh, and then drying to a moisture content of 8-10% to obtain powdery raw materials, soaking ethanol solution and ethyl acetate at a mass concentration of 30-70% into an extraction solution in a volume ratio of 3: 1L/L. The powdery raw material is added to the extraction solution at a mass ratio (5-8): 1kg/kg and soaked for 15-30 minutes after the soaking. The raw material soaking solution is prepared. The 30-40% oil phase is added, shaked and stirred for 30 minutes to obtain the emulsified material. The emulsified material is placed in an extraction kettle in a supercritical extraction device.
   USE - Method for preparing Tieguanyin extract whitening sunscreen base liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare Tieguanyin extract whitening sunscreen base liquid, which obtains a stable whitening sunscreen base liquid, and provides a high-value-added utilization mode for the comprehensive utilization of Tieguanyin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Tieguanyin extract whitening sunscreen base liquid involves crushing Tieguanyin tea leaves, mulberry leaves, red-leaf snake grape leaves, honeysuckle, tea tree flower raw materials to 100-300 mesh, and then drying to a moisture content of 8-10% to obtain powdery raw materials, soaking ethanol solution and ethyl acetate at a mass concentration of 30-70% into an extraction solution in a volume ratio of 3: 1L/L. The powdery raw material is added to the extraction solution at a mass ratio (5-8): 1kg/kg and soaked for 15-30 minutes after the soaking. The raw material soaking solution is prepared. The 30-40% oil phase is added, shaked and stirred for 30 minutes to obtain the emulsified material. The emulsified material is placed in an extraction kettle in a supercritical extraction device. The pressure of the extraction kettle is set to 20-25 mega pascal, and the carbon dioxide flow rate of 10-15L/h to extract for 30-45 minutes. The pressure of the extraction kettle is 40-50 mega pascal, and the flow rate of carbon dioxide to 20-25 L/h after extraction for 30 to 45 minutes. The product is shaped, and the valve at the lower end of the extraction kettle is opened to collect the liquid to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: The raw material comprises 50 to 60 pts. wt. Tieguanyin tea, 20 to 30 pts. wt. mulberry leaf, 10 to 20 pts. wt. red snake grape leaf, 5 to 10 pts. wt. honeysuckle and 5 to 10 pts. wt. tea tree flower. preferred Components: The oil phase includes stearic acid, glyceryl monostearate, isopropyl palmitate, olive oil, sterol esters, paraben methyl esters, paraben propyl esters.

PN CN110551204-A
TI Preparing pluripotent mesenchymal stem cell secretin comprises e.g. inoculating the mesenchymal stem cell in Eagle medium, mixing uniformly, culturing, filtering the mesenchymal stem cell base solution and rinsing the mesenchymal stem cell.
AU WANG B
   LIN S
   LI G
AE SHENZHEN GANGHUAJIAN MEDICAL CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5793P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pluripotent mesenchymal stem cell secretin comprises e.g. (1) inoculating the mesenchymal stem cells in Eagle medium and culturing to obtain a mesenchymal stem cell suspension, (2) inoculating the mesenchymal stem cell suspension into the mesenchymal stem cell culture medium, mixing uniformly, transferring to the bioreactor, allowing to stand for 2 hours, stirring at 60 rpm/minutes and culturing under the conditions of 5% carbon dioxide and 5-20% oxygen for 7-14 days to obtain a mesenchymal stem cell base fluid, (3) filtering the mesenchymal stem cell base solution to obtain mesenchymal stem cells, rinsing the mesenchymal stem cells 3 times with phosphate buffered saline, adding 100 ml serum-free basal medium and continue culturing for 24 hours, and then collecting the medium to obtain a mesenchymal stem cell secretin stock solution, and (4) filtering the mesenchymal stem cell secretin stock solution through a 70 mu m pore size cell filter to obtain a filtrate.
   USE - The mesenchymal stem cell is useful for improving skin cell and bone cell proliferation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, fast, convenient, and easy for large-scale continuous production, does not requires any biological factors, realizes the rapid proliferation of mesenchymal stem cells, and can efficiently and continuously produce mesenchymal stem cell secretions by changing the cell culture medium, which reduces the production cost. The mesenchymal stem cell: has a higher secretory active ingredient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing pluripotent mesenchymal stem cell secretin comprises (1) inoculating the mesenchymal stem cells in Eagle medium and culturing to obtain a mesenchymal stem cell suspension, (2) inoculating the mesenchymal stem cell suspension into the mesenchymal stem cell culture medium, mixing uniformly, transferring to the bioreactor, allowing to stand for 2 hours, stirring at 60 rpm/minutes and culturing under the conditions of 5% carbon dioxide and 5-20% oxygen for 7-14 days to obtain a mesenchymal stem cell base fluid, (3) filtering the mesenchymal stem cell base solution to obtain mesenchymal stem cells, rinsing the mesenchymal stem cells 3 times with phosphate buffered saline, adding 100 ml serum-free basal medium and continue culturing for 24 hours, and then collecting the medium to obtain a mesenchymal stem cell secretin stock solution, and (4) filtering the mesenchymal stem cell secretin stock solution through a 70 mu m pore size cell filter to obtain a filtrate, then centrifuging the filtrate at 1500 g for 10 minutes, collecting the supernatant, freeze-drying the supernatant to obtain a lyophilized powder to obtain mesenchymal stem cell secretin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mesenchymal stem cells are derived bone marrow, adipose, skin, umbilical cord blood, umbilical cord, and/or placenta. In the step (1), the Eagle medium is a modified Eagle medium supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum and 1% antibiotics, i.e. Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) medium. In the step (1), the density of intermediate mesenchymal stem cells in the mesenchymal stem cell suspension is 100000/100 ml to 10 million/100 ml, preferably 1 million/100 ml. In the step (2), the mesenchymal stem cell culture medium is a microcarrier base solution made of a sterile biological microcarrier and a cell culture medium at a ratio of 3 g: 100 ml. In the step (3), the serum-free basal medium is a serum-free alpha -minimum essential medium, i.e. alpha -minimum essential medium medium. Preferred Method: In the step (1), pre-culturing the mesenchymal stem cells using monolayer culture, where the inoculation amount of the mesenchymal stem cells is 0.5 thousand/cm2, and culturing at 37 degrees C for 2-3 generations.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The biological microcarrier is polycaprolactone, polylactic-co-glycolic acid copolymer, hydrogel, sodium alginate, chitin, or polystyrene.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological; Osteopathic.

PN CN110483004-A; CN110483004-B
TI Preparation of aerogel for reuse of glue solution, involves mixing ethyl orthosilicate and deionized water, leaving still, mixing glue solutions, stirring, adding catalyst C01 and catalyst C02, aging, adding modifier and drying.
AU JING F
   WU J
   CUI H
   QIN D
   FAN K
   YIN L
   HUA W
   LUO X
AE HT-HYSA ZHENJIANG SPECIALTY MATERIALS CO (HTHY-Non-standard)
GA 2019A04188
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of aerogel involves mixing ethyl orthosilicate, absolute ethanol and deionized water to obtain silica glue solution (A), leaving still for 20 minutes, mixing glue solution (A) and circulating glue solution (B), adding catalyst C01 and catalyst C02, stirring for 3-10 minutes, injecting mixed glue into gel mold with fiber felt to be gelled to obtain fiber felt composite wet gel (C), aging at room temperature for 12-24 hours, adding modifier for hydrophobic modification and subjecting to carbon dioxide supercritical drying treatment. The preparation of circulating glue solution (B) involves mixing fiber felt coil, orthosilicate, absolute ethanol and deionized water, adding 1-3 wt.% catalyst (C01) and adding 1-3 wt.% catalyst (C02), stirring for 5 minutes, turning on glue circulation device, closing glue circulation device after cycle is completed for 40 minutes and waiting for glue to gel, where residual glue in pipeline and excess glue in gel tank are circulating glue (B).
   USE - Preparation of aerogel for reuse of glue solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of aerogel involves mixing ethyl orthosilicate, absolute ethanol and deionized water in mass ratio of 1:(2-2.5):(0.3-0.35), stirring at room temperature for 10-20 minutes to obtain silica glue solution (A), leaving still for 20 minutes, mixing glue solution (A) and circulating glue solution (B) in mass ratio of 1:(0.1-1), stirring for 2-6 minutes, adding catalyst (C01), stirring for 5-10 minutes, adding catalyst (C02), stirring for 3-10 minutes, injecting mixed glue into gel mold with fiber felt to be gelled to obtain fiber felt composite wet gel (C), aging fiber felt composite wet gel (C) at room temperature for 12-24 hours, adding 10-40 %mass modifier for hydrophobic modification and subjecting hydrophobically modified fiber felt composite wet gel (C) to carbon dioxide supercritical drying treatment. The preparation of circulating glue solution (B) involves mixing fiber felt coil, orthosilicate, absolute ethanol and deionized water in gel tank in mass ratio of 1:(2-2.5):(0.3-0.35), stirring at room temperature for 30 minutes, adding 1-3 wt.% catalyst C01, stirring for 5 minutes, adding 1-3 wt.% catalyst C02, stirring for 5 minutes, piping glue solution to gel tank containing fiber felt coil material, turning on glue circulation device, so that glue liquid fully enters inside of fiber mat, closing glue circulation device after cycle is completed for 40 minutes and waiting for glue to gel, where residual glue in pipeline and excess glue in gel tank are circulating glue (B).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst C01 1-3 mol/L aqueous ammonia. The catalyst C02 is hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, ammonium fluoride or ammonium chloride having concentration of 0.1-0.4 mol/L. The mass ratio of the ethyl orthosilicate in the glue solution (A) to the added catalyst C01 is 1:(0-0.025). The mass ratio of the catalyst C01 to the catalyst C02 is 1:(0-0.6).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The modifier is dimethyldiethoxysilane, hexamethyldisilazane or trimethylchlorosilane.

PT P
PN CN110343176-A
TI Preparing monoclonal antibody against Mycobacterium tuberculosis by observing female mice, emulsifying Mycobacterium tuberculosis liquid, immunizing mice, fusing cells, screening antibody-positive cells, and recovering to get antibody.
AU ZHANG J
AE UNIV PUTIAN (UVPT-C)
GA 201989143B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a monoclonal antibody against Mycobacterium tuberculosis, involves (A) observing 4 BALB/C female mice before immunization, (B) taking M.tuberculosis liquid as an antigen, and emulsifying with Freund's complete adjuvant, (C) subcutaneously injecting the emulsified liquid into the mouse, (D) sacrificing the mice in a carbon dioxide gas sealed chamber, removing the mouse spleen cells and myeloma cells, squeezing and grinding, centrifuging, and discarding supernatant to prepare a cell suspension, (E) adding a fusogenic agent polyethylene glycol to form hybridoma cells, (F) detecting the hybridoma cells, and screening the antibody-positive hybridoma cells, (G) cloning the positive hybridoma cells, and freezing and recoverying the cells, and (H) taking BALB/C female mice, carrying out intraperitoneal injection of liquid paraffin, carrying out intraperitoneal inoculation of hybridoma cells, and withdrawing ascites to obtain monoclonal antibody.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a monoclonal antibody against M.tuberculosis.
   ADVANTAGE - The monoclonal antibody is only for the human type strain, does not react with the bovine M.tuberculosis, ensures that the single antibody can specifically target the human serotype disease, has high specificity and high sensitivity, helps doctors to diagnose heart disease, enhances the specificity of the test, enhances the accuracy of the test results, and improves the detection speed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing a monoclonal antibody against Mycobacterium tuberculosis, involves (A) observing 4 BALB/C female mice, that are 8-10 weeks old, one week before immunization, (B) taking M.tuberculosis liquid as an antigen, and emulsifying with Freund's complete adjuvant, (C) subcutaneously injecting the emulsified liquid into the mouse for immunization, (D) sacrificing the mice in a carbon dioxide gas sealed chamber, removing the mouse spleen cells and myeloma cells by aseptic operation, squeezing and grinding in a dish, centrifuging in a centrifuge, and discarding the supernatant to prepare a cell suspension, (E) adding a fusogenic agent polyethylene glycol to the cell suspension to fuse lymphocytes with myeloma cells and form hybridoma cells, (F) detecting the hybridoma cells, and screening the antibody-positive hybridoma cells, (G) cloning the positive hybridoma cells, and freezing and recoverying the cells, and (H) taking BALB/C female mice, carrying out intraperitoneal injection of 0.5-0.9 ml liquid paraffin, carrying out intraperitoneal inoculation of hybridoma cells after 1-3 weeks, where the hybridoma cells proliferate, produce and secrete monoclonal antibody in the mouse abdominal cavity, and the mouse abdomen is swollen after 2 weeks, and withdrawing ascites by a syringe to obtain a large amount of monoclonal antibody.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (C), performing second exemption after 2-4 weeks, which involves intraperitoneally injecting the same dose of antigen 1 time every 2 weeks to the mice, boosting mice 4 times, and boosting the immunogen diluted with phosphate buffered saline for the last time. In step (D), the sacrificed mice are soaked in 75% ethanol for 5-8 minutes, and transferred to a clean bench. In step (F), the antibody-positive hybridoma cells are screened by indirect ELISA method. Preferred Conditions: In step (D), the ratio of spleen cells to myeloma cells is (2-4):1, and the centrifugation is carried out at 2000 rpm for 5-9 minutes. In step (G), the liquid is changed 8-24 hours before freezing, so that the hybridoma cells are in the logarithmic growth phase, and transferred to liquid nitrogen ray for preservation, and the hybridoma cells are recovered after being frozen for 3 months. Preferred Cells: In step (E), BALB/C mouse peritoneal macrophages are used as feeder cells, and BALB/C mouse peritoneal macrophages are inoculated into 96-well culture plates one day before the fusion.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC A05-H03; A11-C03A; A12-W11L; B04-F10B2; B04-G07; B04-G21; B12-K04G2B; D05-H11A1
IP C07K-016/12

PT P
PN CN110180543-A
TI Preparing solid load type copper oxide-copper activated carbon sphere photocatalyst useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprises e.g. obtaining mixed solution by reacting phenol, aldehyde and ethylene glycol with water.
AU ZHANG X
   REN G
   ZHANG C
   LI R
   LIU J
   WANG Y
   FAN C
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201976096L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing solid load type copper oxide/copper activated carbon sphere photocatalyst comprises adding phenol, aldehyde component and ethylene glycol into the three-mouth flask with water, stirring, adding triethylamine, controlling the bath temperature, stirring to obtain mixed solution, adding copper salt and polyvinyl alcohol to the mixed solution, maintaining the temperature, continuously reacting, adding hexamethylenetetramine, reacting, filtering by suction, washing with dilute alkali solution, drying to obtain transition metal copper-phenolic resin ball, placing in tubular furnace, carbonizing under nitrogen protection, switching and activating the water vapor, finishing reaction, cooling to room temperature, washing the sample with dilute acid, washing with distilled water until neutral, and drying at room temperature to obtain solid load type copper oxide/copper activated carbon sphere photocatalyst.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process and mild conditions, utilizes easily available raw materials, provides stable products, and is suitable for industrial production. The photocatalyst: has high adsorption capacity, high activity, and high stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing solid load type copper oxide/copper activated carbon sphere photocatalyst comprises (i) adding 30-60 pts. wt. phenol, 40-80 pts. wt. aldehyde component and 15-35 pts. wt. ethylene glycol into the three-mouth flask with 200-300 pts. wt. water, stirring for 0.5-1.5 hours, adding 2-5 pts. wt. triethylamine, controlling the bath temperature at 70-120 degrees C, and stirring for 0.5-2 hours to obtain mixed solution, (ii) adding 5-10 pts. wt. copper salt and 1-9 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol to the mixed solution, maintaining the temperature at 60-120 degrees C, continuously reacting for 0.5-2 hours, adding 1-3 pts. wt. hexamethylenetetramine, reacting for 4-8 hours, filtering by suction, washing with dilute alkali solution, drying for 10-24 hours at 60-100 degrees C for 10-24 hours, and obtaining transition metal copper-phenolic resin ball, and (iii) placing the transition metal copper-phenolic resin ball in tubular furnace, carbonizing under nitrogen protection at 500-1000 degrees C, switching and activating the water vapor at 700-1000 degrees C at a temperature increasing speed of 1.5-5 degrees C/minute for 1-3 hours, after finishing the reaction, cooling to room temperature, washing the sample 3 times with dilute acid, washing with distilled water until neutral, and drying at room temperature to obtain solid load type copper oxide/copper activated carbon sphere photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the phenol is metacresol, phenylphenol, and/or 6-tert-butyl metacresol; the aldehyde component is formaldehyde or furfural. In the step (ii), the dilute alkali solution is potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide and/or aqueous ammonia; the copper salt is copper nitrate, copper acetate and/or copper chloride. In the step (iii), the dilute acid is dilute nitric acid, sulfuric acid and/or hydrochloric acid.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E11-P; E11-Q02B; E31-N03C; E31-N05C; E35-A; J04-E04A; J04-E04C; A05-C01B; A10-E05B; A10-E09B; A12-W11K

PT P
PN CN110104619-A
TI Preparing high temperature resistant lightweight            boron nitride aerogel material comprises e.g. uniformly            mixing phenol, aldehyde, alcohol, silicon source and            urea, and and uniformly stirring.
AU WU X
   SHEN X
   CUI S
   DING J
   AN C
   CAI Y
   MA J
   AN W
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 201970696T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high temperature resistant                lightweight boron nitride(BN) aerogel material                comprises (1) uniformly mixing phenol, aldehyde,                alcohol, silicon source and urea, and uniformly                stirring to obtain hydrolyzed N-doped                organic-silica binary sol system; (2) pouring the                binary sol system obtained in step (1) into mold,                reacting until gel, and placing for 10-25 hours;                (3) adding an aging liquid to gel obtained in the                step (2), placing in oven, and carrying                replacement; (4) subjecting the wet gel obtained in                the step (3) to carbon dioxide supercritical drying                treatment to obtain N-doped CF-silicon dioxide                aerogel; (5) adding the aerogel obtained in the                step (4) to boron source, heat-treating under an                inert atmosphere to obtain C-BN composite aerogel                containing silicon oxide; (6) placing the composite                aerogel obtained in the step (5) in an air                atmosphere, calcining and removing carbon; and (7)                soaking the material obtained in the step (6) in                hydrofluoric acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high                temperature resistant lightweight boron nitride                aerogel material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has wide raw materials source; is                economical, simple, and suitable for mass                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high temperature resistant                lightweight boron nitride(BN) aerogel material                comprises (1) uniformly mixing phenol, aldehyde,                alcohol, silicon source and urea, and uniformly                stirring to obtain hydrolyzed N-doped                organic-silica binary sol system; (2) pouring the                binary sol system obtained in step (1) into mold,                reacting until gel, and placing for 10-25 hours;                (3) adding an aging liquid to gel obtained in the                step (2), placing in oven, and carrying                replacement; (4) subjecting the wet gel obtained in                the step (3) to carbon dioxide supercritical drying                treatment to obtain N-doped CF-silicon dioxide                aerogel; (5) adding the aerogel obtained in the                step (4) to boron source, heat-treating under an                inert atmosphere to obtain C-BN composite aerogel                containing silicon oxide; (6) placing the composite                aerogel obtained in the step (5) in an air                atmosphere, calcining and removing carbon; and (7)                soaking the material obtained in the step (6) in                hydrofluoric acid for 0.5-1.5 hours to finally                obtain boron nitride aerogel; where in step (1),                the phenol, aldehyde, alcohol, silicon source and                urea are uniformly mixed in molar ratio of                1:(1-5):(10-30):(0.1-1.2):(0.2-1.5); in the step                (5), the boron source is mixed with urea in molar                ratio (0.2-5):1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  phenol is catechol, resorcinol, cresol,                  hydroquinone, aminophenol, nitrophenol and/or                  pyrogallol; the aldehyde is formaldehyde,                  acetaldehyde, cinnamaldehyde, butyraldehyde,                  and/or anisaldehyde; the alcohol is ethanol,                  methanol, ethylene glycol, cyclohexanol and/or                  1-butanol. In the step (1), the silicon source is                  triaminopropyltriethoxysilane, vinyl triperoxide                  tert-butyl silane, butadiene-triethoxysilane                  and/or gamma -glycidyloxypropyltrimethoxysilane.                  In the step (3), the aging liquid is ethanol,                  methanol, acetone, diethyl ether, normal-pentanol                  and/or isopropanol. In the step (5), the boron                  source is boric acid, diborane, borax, and/or                  borate. Preferred Method: In the step (1),                  stirring temperature is 20-50 degrees C; the                  stirring time is 0.5-4 hours. In the step (3),                  the replacement temperature is 25-75 degrees C;                  the number of replacement is 3-8 times, and the                  replacement time is 8-24 hours. In the step (4),                  the drying treatment is carbon dioxide                  supercritical drying process comprises taking                  carbon dioxide as the drying medium, the reaction                  temperature is 50-70 degrees C, the pressure in                  the reaction kettle is 8-12 MPa, the gas release                  rate is 5-20 l/minutes, and the drying time is                  8-20 hours. In the step (5), the heat treatment                  temperature is 1000-1500 degrees C; the heating                  rate is 3-5 degrees C/minute, and the heat                  treatment time is 3-10 hours. In the step (6),                  the air thermal calcination temperature is                  400-800 degrees C; the heating rate is 2-10                  degrees C/minute, and the heat treatment time is                  2-5 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E12-C02; E31-Q03; J04-X

PT P
PN CN110013497-A
TI Traditional Chinese medicine soft capsule used for improving immunity by heating Ganoderma lucidum spore essential oil, and homogenizing with Ganoderma lucidum spore powder, ginseng, Acanthopanax, radix astragali and Rhodiola root powder.
AU ZHANG X
   WEI Y
AE ZHANG X (ZHAN-Individual)
   WEI Y (WEIY-Individual)
GA 2019638199
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of traditional Chinese medicine soft capsule involves carbon dioxide supercritical fluid extracting of Ganoderma lucidum spore powder; ultrasonically soaking Ganoderma lucidum spore residue in diluted hydrochloric acid solution, centrifuging, combining, filtering, concentrating, vacuum-drying and crushing; cutting ginseng, Acanthopanax and radix astragali, adding ethanol, heating, extracting, filtering, combining extracts, concentrating, vacuum-drying and crushing; cutting Rhodiola root, adding ethanol, heating, extracting, filtering, combining extracts, filtering and concentrating; heating Ganoderma lucidum spore essential oil, adding Ganoderma lucidum spore powder, ginseng, Acanthopanax and radix astragali mixed powder and Rhodiola root powder, stirring, adding officinal vegetable oil, grinding, sieving, homogenizing and performing vacuum treatment; and cooling, granulating, washing and drying.
   USE - The traditional Chinese medicine soft capsule is used for improving immunity (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The capsule has synergistic effect between components, short production period, small equipment investment and low energy consumption, and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of traditional Chinese medicine soft capsule comprises: (A) taking 800-1200 pts. wt. Ganoderma lucidum spore powder and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid extracting to obtain Ganoderma lucidum spore essential oil; (B) ultrasonically soaking Ganoderma lucidum spore residue in diluted hydrochloric acid solution for 1.5 hours, centrifuging, adding diluted hydrochloric acid solution, ultrasonically soaking for 1.5 hours, combining soaking solutions, filtering, concentrating, vacuum-drying and crushing to obtain Ganoderma lucidum spore powder; (C) cutting 400-600 pts. wt. ginseng, 400-600 pts. wt. Acanthopanax and 800-1000 pts. wt. radix astragali, adding ethanol, heating, extracting under reflux for 1.5 hours, filtering, adding ethanol, heating, extracting under reflux for 1.5 hours, filtering, combining extracts, concentrating, vacuum-drying and crushing to obtain ginseng, Acanthopanax and radix astragali mixed powder; (D) cutting 800-1000 pts. wt. Rhodiola root, adding ethanol, heating, extracting under reflux for 1.5 hours, filtering, adding ethanol, heating, extracting under reflux for 1.5 hours, combining extracts, filtering, concentrating, vacuum-drying and crushing to obtain Rhodiola root powder; (E) heating Ganoderma lucidum spore essential oil to 40-50 degrees C, orderly adding Ganoderma lucidum spore powder, ginseng, Acanthopanax and radix astragali mixed powder and Rhodiola root powder, uniformly stirring, adding officinal vegetable oil, uniformly stirring at 40-50 degrees C, grinding by colloid mill for 2 times, sieving by 100-mesh sieve, high-pressure homogenizing and performing vacuum treatment to obtain capsule material; and (F) preparing soft capsule with content of 0.55 g, cooling, granulating, washing and drying.
TF ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)
