PT P
PN CN109737433-A
TI Method for processing small organic waste gas, involves collecting organic waste gas by direct connection, first entering heat exchanger, and reducing processing energy consumption, followed by recovering high-temperature flue gas.
AU JI H
   QIN P
   XIA P
   CHEN D
   ZHOU Y
AE HEFEI HENGLI EQUIP CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 2019451097
AB    NOVELTY - A small organic waste gas processing method involves collecting organic waste gas by direct connection, first entering a heat exchanger, and preheating at 400 degrees C by a heat exchanger, reducing processing energy consumption, after the heat exchange of the exhaust gas is raised, entering the gas to the combustion chamber through the pipeline, considering that the exhaust gas may come from an oxygen-free environment, arranging the air supply port on the pipeline at the same time, insulating the pipeline, heating the exhaust gas by a number of electric heating wires in the heating zone of the combustion chamber, raising the temperature at 630-820 degrees C, wearing a heating wire inside the ceramic tube, heating evenly, oxidizing the common volatile organic compounds to carbon dioxide and water, reasonably setting the electric heating tube power according to the exhaust gas volume, after the incineration treatment, and reducing the temperature 400 degrees C.
   USE - Method for processing small organic waste gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A small organic waste gas processing method involves collecting organic waste gas by direct connection, first entering a heat exchanger, and preheating at 400 degrees C by a heat exchanger, reducing processing energy consumption, after the heat exchange of the exhaust gas is raised, entering the gas to the combustion chamber through the pipeline, considering that the exhaust gas may come from an oxygen-free environment, arranging the air supply port on the pipeline at the same time, insulating the pipeline, heating the exhaust gas by a number of electric heating wires in the heating zone of the combustion chamber, raising the temperature at 630-820 degrees C, wearing a heating wire inside the ceramic tube, heating evenly, oxidizing the common volatile organic compounds to carbon dioxide and water at 630-820 degrees C for 0.3-0.5 seconds, ensuring the residence time by properly setting the size of the combustion chamber, ensuring the gas turbulence by providing a spoiler baffle in the combustion chamber, reasonably setting the electric heating tube power according to the exhaust gas volume, after the incineration treatment, reaching the degradation rate of organic matter in the exhaust gas is 99.9% or more, recovering the high-temperature flue gas is 50% or more of the thermal energy through the heat exchanger, and reducing the temperature 400 degrees C. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for processing an organic waste gas.
DC Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23)); Q77 (Furnaces, kilns, ovens, retorts (F27))
MC Q73-A05; Q73-U20; Q77-D; Q77-U20
IP F23G-005/44; F23G-007/06; F27D-017/00

PT P
PN WO2019071279-A1; ZA201707838-A
TI Water treatment system for treating water for use in hydroponic plant cultivating system comprises filter for filtering unwanted particles, anti-scaling mechanism, coagulator, oxidation-reduction controller, and pH controller.
AU VAN DER MERWE P G
AE CLEAN AIR NURSERIES INTELLECTUAL PROPERT (CLEA-Non-standard)
   CLEAN AIR NURSERIES INTELLECTUAL PROPERT (CLEA-Non-standard)
GA 201941932S
AB    NOVELTY - A water treatment system for treating water for use in hydroponic plant cultivating system, comprises filter for filtering unwanted particles; anti-scaling mechanism to inhibit scaling and precipitation of insoluble solids on the filter; coagulator for coagulating minerals and heavy metals contained in the water; oxidation-reduction controller for measuring and controlling oxidation-reduction potential of the water; pH controller for measuring and controlling the pH value of the water; carbon dioxide enriching apparatus for enriching the water with carbonic acid; de-clustering mechanism for de-clustering water molecules of the water; and aerator for increasing the level of dissolved oxygen in the water.
   USE - A water treatment system for treating water for use in hydroponic plant cultivating system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydroponic plant cultivating system includes carbon neutrality, can operate 'off-the-grid', and provides its own water as water is harvested from the atmosphere. The hydroponic plant cultivating system does not use any petrochemical derivative products such as herbicides, pesticides and fungicides. The hydroponic plant cultivating system mitigates most of the challenges associated with agriculture and operates independent of most climatic, topographic, temperature, soil or water condition and can be installed almost anywhere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) treating water which involves adding a chemical substance to inhibit scaling and precipitation of insoluble solids on filter membranes; subjecting the water to 1st filtering process; coagulation of minerals and metals dispersed in the water; measuring and controlling oxidation-reduction potential of the water; measuring and controlling the pH value of the water; subjecting the water to 2nd filtering process; de-clustering of water molecules; subjecting the water to a high density high frequency pulsator; introducing carbon dioxide and anionic surfactant into the water; and introducing oxygen in the form of nano-bubbles into the water; and
   (2) hydroponic plant cultivating system comprising water treatment system for treating water; greenhouse type structure; plant support materials of elongate wave shaped form arranged substantially vertically in the greenhouse type structure; each plant support material including plant receiving zones defined longitudinally and on opposing sides of the plant support material for supporting plants in an off-set configuration relative one another; and a flow channel defined inside the plant support material for channeling the flow of water inside the plant support material where flow channel is connected with plant receiving zones for introducing water to the plant receiving zones.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the system for treatment of water for use in a hydroponic plant cultivating system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The filter includes a multi-layered Moringa oleifera seed cake filter. The anti-scaling mechanism includes the use of hydrochloric acid and sulfuric acid. The coagulator includes the use of aluminum sulfate. Oxidation-reduction controller includes ion-exchange resin and stripping material. The pH controller controls the pH through the release of anions and cations by ion-exchange resins. Carbon dioxide enriching apparatus includes a carbon dioxide scrubber arrangement for scrubbing carbon dioxide from a high carbon dioxide concentration stream. Carbon dioxide scrubber arrangement includes an organic anionic surfactant for facilitating the scrubbing of carbon dioxide to form carbonic acid.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))

PT P
PN CN109574048-A; CN109574048-B
TI Treating waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material, comprises e.g. pulverizing waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material and sieving, and placing into the reduction furnace and passing reducing gas reduction.
AU BAO J
AE BAO J (BAOJ-Individual)
GA 2019335140
AB    NOVELTY - Treating waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material, comprises e.g. (i) pulverizing waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material and sieving, and placing into the reduction furnace and passing reducing gas reduction, generating exhaust gas by reduction, taking out, spraying and absorbing to obtain the spray absorbing liquid, cooling material, sealing discharged material and storing to obtain raw material, (ii) placing raw material in pure water, adding hydrazine hydrate solution, heating and stirring and filtering and washing to obtain filtrate and filter residue, then vacuum drying, subjecting mixture to high-pressure nitrogen gas flow pulverization and subjecting obtained pulverized material to electromagnetic separation to obtain reduced iron powder, (iii) passing filtrate obtained in the step (ii) through carbon dioxide, adjusting the pH of solution and filtering the reaction, filtering to obtain battery grade lithium carbonate and (iv) filtering.
   USE - The method is useful for treating waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, low cost, and short process flow.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material, comprises (i) pulverizing waste lithium iron phosphate cathode material and sieving, taking out sieved material and placing into the reduction furnace and passing the reducing gas reduction, generating exhaust gas by reduction, taking out waste gas generated by induced draft fan, spraying and absorbing to obtain the spray absorbing liquid, cooling material to a temperature of less than 45 degrees C, sealing discharged material and storing under nitrogen protection to obtain raw material, where the reduction temperature is 350-500 degrees C and the reduction time is 5-7 hours, (ii) placing raw material in pure water, then adding hydrazine hydrate solution, heating at 60-75 degrees C and stirring for 1-2 hours and then filtering and washing to obtain a filtrate and a filter residue, then vacuum drying the filter residue, subjecting mixture to high-pressure nitrogen gas flow pulverization and subjecting obtained pulverized material to electromagnetic separation to obtain reduced iron powder, (iii) passing filtrate obtained in the step (ii) through carbon dioxide at a temperature of 30-45 degrees C, adjusting the pH of solution to 7.5-8.5, and filtering the reaction for 1-2 hours, filtering to obtain battery grade lithium carbonate, and obtained filtrate is returned to the step (ii) to washing the raw material, and (iv) subjecting spray absorbing liquid obtained in the step (i) to precision filtration, performing tributyl phosphate (TBP) (6-9) extraction, performing back extraction of aqueous ammonia to obtain diammonium phosphate solution, concentrating and crystallizing to obtain diammonium phosphate crystals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i); the sieving using a 50-80 mesh sieve, the reducing gas is hydrogen, methane, acetylene, carbon monoxide, natural gas and/or water gas; the reduction process ensures that the pressure in the reduction furnace is 50-500 Pa higher than the atmospheric pressure, and the spray is absorbed by pure water. In step (ii); the mass ratio between raw material and pure water is 1:3-5; the hydrazine hydrate solution is added so that the concentration of hydrazine hydrate in the slurry is 0.01-0.02 mol/l; the washing includes washing with 50-60 degrees C hot water until the pH of the wash water to 7.5-8, then stop washing, mixing wash water and the mother liquor and the drying temperature under vacuum drying is 60-75 degrees C. In step (iii); the mother liquor after the precipitation of lithium carbonate is returned to the step (ii) to slurry the raw material. In step (ii); the sorting material obtained by electromagnetic sorting is returned to the step (i) for reduction. In the step (iv), the precision filtration spray absorbing liquid is filtered by a ceramic membrane, the pore diameter of the ceramic membrane is 20-30 nm, and the pH of the stripping solution obtained after stripping aqueous ammonia is 7.4-8.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); Q77 (Furnaces, kilns, ovens, retorts (F27)); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PT P
PN CN109517795-A
TI Obtaining porcine neural crest stem cells, by placing type I collagen in dish, crushing tissue, soaking adherent cells in phosphate buffered saline (PBS) washing solution, and performing sub-bottle subculture and expansion culture.
AU SONG X
   ZHANG K
   YANG S
   CUI X
   DU L
   ZHANG B
   YANG Y
   YANG C
   GUAN W
AE SHANXI ACAD AGRIC SCI FEED VETERINARY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019294624
AB    NOVELTY - Method for obtaining porcine neural crest stem cells, involves placing type I collagen in dish, placing the culture dish, crushing tissue in culture solution, placing the pork neck adipose tissue in culture solution, covering the sterilized coverslip, supplementing the culture solution, releasing the cell colonies from adipose tissue as primary neural crest stem cells, culturing the medium, aspirating the cell culture medium, diluting the adherent cells by adding phosphate buffer solution, discarding the washing solution, adding accutase digestive juice to the mixture, digesting, detaching the adherent cells, adding the culture solution to terminate the digestion, transferring the cells to other culture dishes, soaking the adherent cells in phosphate buffered saline (PBS) washing solution, discarding the washing solution, adding accutase, digesting the cells, adding the culture solution to terminate the digestion, and performing sub-bottle subculture and expansion culture.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining porcine neural crest stem cells (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for obtaining porcine neural crest stem cells, involves placing type I collagen in dish, placing the culture dish in 37-37.5 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, crushing the obtained pig neck adipose tissue in culture solution, moisturizing the surface of the culture dish with the culture solution, placing the pork neck adipose tissue in culture solution, covering the sterilized coverslip, supplementing the culture solution in 37-37.5 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, releasing the cell colonies from the adipose tissue as primary neural crest stem cells, culturing the medium, aspirating the cell culture medium on the fourth culture day, diluting the adherent cells by adding phosphate buffer solution, discarding the washing solution, adding accutase digestive juice to the mixture, digesting the mixture to 80% or more at 37-37.5 degrees C, detaching the adherent cells, adding the culture solution to terminate the digestion, transferring the cells to other culture dishes, discarding the culture solution when the density of the primary cultured cells reaches 90% or more, soaking the adherent cells in phosphate buffered saline (PBS) washing solution, discarding the washing solution, adding accutase, digesting the cells to 37% or more at 37-37.5 degrees C, adding the culture solution to terminate the digestion, and performing sub-bottle subculture and expansion culture, where the culture solution is 5-15% fetal bovine serum Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM) culture solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The culture solution comprises 1 mmol/l sodium pyruvate, 0.05 mg/ml uridine, 50 U/ml DMEM medium of penicillin, 50 U/ml streptomycin and 10% fetal bovine serum. 10-20 mu l sputum collagen is obtained in 35 mm culture dish, the sample is spread with disposable squeegee and placed at 37 degrees C after 1 hour and 5% carbon dioxide incubator. The culture solution is statically cultured in 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for one week, and the fresh culture solution is replaced once a week. The cells are digested at 37 degrees C for 5-10 minutes to more than 80% of the adherent cells.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN109467694-A
TI Preparing high molecular weight nylon 9T resin comprises e.g. adding raw materials into the polymerization tank, adjusting pH, reacting, discharging material from the polymerization tank and extruding in a twin-screw extruder.
AU ZHANG Y
   QIU S
AE GUANGZHOU WANJIACHENG PLASTIC CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201926527B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high molecular weight nylon 9T resin comprises (i) adding raw materials into the polymerization tank, adjusting pH to 7.1-9.2, reacting at 30-250 degrees C for 3-15 hours at a vacuum pressure of -0.02 MPa to -0.1 MPa to obtain a small molecular by-product and water to obtain a material, and (ii) discharging material from the polymerization tank, transferring by a mobile device into a twin-screw extruder for solid phase polycondensation reaction, passing the inert gas, carbon dioxide, reacting at a vacuum pressure of -0.03 MPa to -0.1 MPa, precipitating small molecular by-products and moisture, extruding material from a twin-screw extruder, cooling, pelletizing, drying and then packaging to obtain final product, where raw materials includes 100 pts. wt. low molecular weight nylon 9T powder, 5-60 pts. wt. water, 0.01-8 pts. wt. catalyst and 0.01-3 pts. wt. heat stabilizer; and low molecular weight nylon 9T powder is a by-product obtained by the Japanese Kuraray Company in the process.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high molecular weight nylon 9T resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The resin: has excellent chemical property, mechanical behavior and thermal performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high molecular weight nylon 9T resin comprises (i) adding raw materials into the polymerization tank, adjusting pH to 7.1-9.2, reacting at 30-250 degrees C for 3-15 hours at a vacuum pressure of -0.02 MPa to -0.1 MPa to obtain a small molecular by-product and water to obtain a material, and (ii) discharging material from the polymerization tank, transferring by a mobile device into a twin-screw extruder for solid phase polycondensation reaction, passing the inert gas, carbon dioxide, reacting at 160-370 degrees C at a vacuum pressure of -0.03 MPa to -0.1 MPa, precipitating small molecular by-products and moisture, extruding material from a twin-screw extruder, cooling, pelletizing, drying and then packaging to obtain final product, where raw materials includes 100 pts. wt. low molecular weight nylon 9T powder, 5-60 pts. wt. water, 0.01-8 pts. wt. catalyst and 0.01-3 pts. wt. heat stabilizer; low molecular weight nylon 9T powder is a by-product obtained by the Japanese Kuraray Company in the process of synthesizing nylon 9T resin from monomeric terephthalic acid and monomeric quinone diamine; and catalyst is sodium metasilicate or sodium hydroxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (i), pH is 7.1-8.2.

PT P
PN CN109280228-A
TI Wear-resistant tread rubber useful in development of vehicle polymer material technology industry, comprises e.g. mixed rubber, antioxidant, carbon black, plasticizer, zinc oxide, stearic acid, vulcanizing agent and wear-resistant filler.
AU LEI C
   LIU X
AE LEI C (LEIC-Individual)
GA 201915878S
AB    NOVELTY - Wear-resistant tread rubber comprises 100-120 pts. wt. mixed rubber, 3-6 pts. wt antioxidant, 20-30 pts. wt carbon black, 7-9 pts. wt. plasticizer, 3-4 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 2-4 pts. wt. stearic acid, 4-8 pts. wt. vulcanizing agent and 30-40 pts. wt. wear-resistant filler, where wear resistant filler is prepared by e.g. mixing graphene oxide with water, adding 1-2 times coupling agent the mass of graphene oxide, ultrasonically dispersing, stirring reaction mixture, filtering to obtain modified graphene oxide mixture, mixing modified graphene oxide mixture and carbon nanotubes, adding 50-100 times the mass of modified graphene oxide, stirring, mixing, adjusting pH to 8-9 to obtain modified graphene oxide mixture, mixing obtained modified graphene oxide mixture and low melting point alloy, ultrasonically dispersing, adding aqueous glucose beta lactone solution, allowing to stand under carbon dioxide atmosphere, filtering to obtain gel, washing, lyophilizing, pulverizing and sieving.
   USE - The wear-resistant tread rubber is useful in development of vehicle polymer material technology industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Wear-resistant tread rubber comprises 100-120 pts. wt. mixed rubber, 3-6 pts. wt. antioxidant, 20-30 pts. wt. carbon black, 7-9 pts. wt. plasticizer, 3-4 pts. wt. zinc oxide, 2-4 pts. wt. stearic acid, 4-8 pts. wt. vulcanizing agent and 30-40 pts. wt. wear-resistant filler, where wear resistant filler is prepared by mixing graphene oxide with water at a mass ratio of 1:100-1:200, adding 1-2 times coupling agent the mass of graphene oxide, ultrasonically dispersing, adjusting pH to 3.5-4.5, stirring reaction mixture, filtering to obtain modified graphene oxide mixture, mixing modified graphene oxide mixture and carbon nanotubes at a mass ratio of 2:1-3:1, adding 50-100 times the mass of modified graphene oxide, stirring, mixing, adjusting pH to 8-9 to obtain modified graphene oxide mixture, mixing obtained modified graphene oxide mixture and low melting point alloy at a mass ratio of 100:2-100:7, adding 0.02-0.04 times modified graphene oxide mixture, addition amount of polyethene polyamine and modified graphene oxide is 0.1-0.2 times the amount of basalt fiber, ultrasonically dispersing, adding aqueous glucose beta lactone solution, allowing to stand under carbon dioxide atmosphere, filtering to obtain gel, washing, lyophilizing, pulverizing and sieving to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio between mixture of natural rubber and butadiene rubber is 3:1 to 2:1. The antioxidant is 2,2,4-thimethyl-1,2-dihydropuinoline polymer, N-(1.3-dimethylbutyl)-N'-phenyl-p-phenylendiamine or N-Isopropyl-N'-phenyl-P-phenylenediamine. The plasticizer is di(2-ethylhexyl) phthalate. The vulcanizing agent is sulfur, benzoyl peroxide or accelerator tetramethyl thiuram disulfide. The coupling agent is 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane and 3-glycidoxypropyltrimethoxysilane in a mass ratio of 1:1. The low melting point alloy is lead bismuth alloy, where mass fraction of lead is 44.5% and mass fraction of bismuth is 55.5%. The polyethene polyamine is diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine or ethylenediamine.

PN CN109045365-A
TI Method for preparing fusion-resistant wearable medical catheter, involves adding polyurethane, polylactic acid, polyethylene terephthalate and polycarbonate to reaction furnace, followed by charging reaction furnace and heat preservation.
AU FEI Y
AE FEI Y (FEIY-Individual)
GA 201901112R
AB    NOVELTY - A fusion-resistant wearable medical catheter preparing method involves adding polyurethane, polylactic acid, polyethylene terephthalate and polycarbonate to a reaction furnace, heating and adding antifoaming agent to the furnace, pressurizing and defoaming, mixing hydroxy silicone oil, sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, disodium hydrogen phosphate, sorbitol and calcium stearate into reaction furnace, heating the reaction and cooling to room temperature, charging the reaction furnace with carbon dioxide gas, venting, and heating the reactor, injecting the reaction liquid into a medical guide co-extruder, washing the extruded conduit with sodium citrate solution and ultrapure water and drying, subjecting the dried catheter to surface activation, infiltrating the surface activated conduit in a solidifying liquid, rinsing the catheter with ultrapure water, ultrasonic cleaning the catheter, ultrasonic sterilization of the catheter, drying and packaging to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing fusion-resistant wearable medical catheter.
   ADVANTAGE - The fusion-resistant wearable medical catheter with better wear resistance, and can meet various demands in the industry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A fusion-resistant wearable medical catheter preparing method involves adding 11-16 pts. wt. polyurethane, 5-12 pts. wt. polylactic acid, 2-6 pts. wt. polyethylene terephthalate and 3-6 pts. wt. polycarbonate to a reaction furnace, heating the reaction to 180 degrees C, adding 1-4 parts antifoaming agent, pressurizing and defoaming, continuously reacting for 30-50 minutes, recovering to normal pressure for 1 hour and keeping warm, mixing 2-7 parts hydroxy silicone oil, 2-3 parts sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, 4-7 parts disodium hydrogen phosphate, 3-7 parts sorbitol and 1-2 parts calcium stearate uniformly into reaction furnace, heating the reaction to 150 degrees C, after the reaction for 3 minutes, cooling to room temperature, charging the reaction furnace with carbon dioxide gas, leaving at room temperature for 10-25 minutes, venting, and heating the reactor to 160 degrees C and preserving heat for 30 minutes, injecting the reaction liquid into a medical guide co-extruder, adopting the co-extruder needle valve type hot runner system to extrude the material, washing the extruded conduit casting with sodium citrate solution and ultrapure water and drying at 80 degrees C, subjecting the dried catheter casting to surface activation, sonicating for 30-35 minutes, infiltrating the surface activated conduit in the solidifying liquid to perform curing, rinsing the catheter with ultrapure water and naturally drying, ultrasonic cleaning the catheter, ultrasonic sterilization of the catheter, drying and packaging to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solidifying liquid comprises soybean oil, glycerin, sodium citrate, glycine, 2-methyl imidazole, arabic gum and water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antifoaming agent comprises phenylethyl oleate, lauryl phenylacetate, dimethyl silicone oil and polyoxypropylene glyceryl ether.

PT P
PN CN109034537-A
TI Method for dynamic evaluation of flood resource wetland utilization value, involves calculating optimal flood resource utilization to achieve goal of minimum risk and maximum benefit for flood resource utilization.
AU LV S
   WANG F
   ZHAO H
   KANG P
   CHEN X
AE UNIV NORTH CHINA WATER RESOURCES & ELECT (UNCW-C)
GA 2018A4949G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sharing the amount of flooded water from the wetland and the increased wetland use area according to the practice of flood diversion. The dynamic annual flood resource wetland utilization value classification and estimation method are used to relatively clearly describe the wetland value and facilitate quantification. The wetland value is divided into direct value and indirect value. The valuation value is slightly rough because the level of protected species is different, when the alternative market price method is used to calculate the fixed amount of benefits for the protection of any rare species. The recharge wetland water is used by leisure tourism value flood resources to avoid wetland shrinkage and ecological degradation which in turn drives leisure tourism. The optimal flood resource utilization is calculated through correlation analysis to achieve the goal of minimum risk and maximum benefit for flood resource utilization.
   USE - Method for dynamic evaluation of flood resource wetland utilization value.
   ADVANTAGE - The method introduces the utilization of flood resources into the dynamic evaluation of the service value of the wetland ecosystem, and focuses on the prior art to focus on the overall estimation of the wetland service function, without highlighting the value contribution of the water resources to the wetland. The wetland value assessment is mostly for the typical annual analysis. The method focuses on the impact of flood resources on the quality and quantity of wetlands in the absence of continuous annual dynamic assessment, and the value of wetland utilization of flood resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for dynamic evaluation of flood resource wetland utilization value involves reacting 6 moles (264 g) carbon dioxide and 6 moles (108 g) water, to obtain 180 g D-glucose and 6 moles (192 g) oxygen, and further reacting to obtain 162 g polysaccharide. The plant fixes carbon dioxide, releases oxygen and discharges greenhouse gas, where greenhouse gas mainly includes methane and nitrous oxide, and the value of greenhouse gas emissions depends on the gas flux. The economic value of the two gases separately is evaluated, and the value of greenhouse gas emissions based on the benefit area of the flood resource utilization in the wetland is calculated, based on the emission fluxes of methane and nitrous oxide, and emissions values of methane and nitrous oxide proposed in Pearce's economic analysis of climate change.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of a method for dynamic evaluation of flood resource wetland utilization value. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
DC T01 (Digital Computers)
MC T01-J04A; T01-J04B2; T01-J05A2C; T01-J05B2

PN CN108862867-A; CN108862867-B
TI Device used for forming and separating elemental sulfur in situ comprises reaction device and collecting device, where reaction device is provided with separation membrane, and separation membrane is biofilm-microfiltration membrane.
AU HUANG C
   WANG A
   LI Z
   CHEN F
   ZHAO Y
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 201895517B
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a reaction device (1) and a collecting device (2), where the reaction device is provided with a separation membrane, and the separation membrane is a biofilm-microfiltration membrane double-layer structure. The separation membrane divides the reaction device into a reaction zone and an accumulation zone (13), the biofilm is located on one side of the reaction zone, and the microfiltration membrane is located on one side of the aggregation zone. The collecting device is divided into a gas collecting device and a liquid collecting device, the gas collecting device collects gas discharged from the reaction device, and the liquid collecting device collects liquid and solid discharged from the reaction device, and the gas collecting device is connected in the reaction zone, the liquid collection device is connected to the accumulation zone.
   USE - Device used for forming and separating elemental sulfur in situ.
   ADVANTAGE - The device introduces the concept of forming and separating solid particles in situ into a membrane bioreactor, uses the pressure of water to immediately pass the elemental sulfur formed on the surface of the microorganism through the microfiltration membrane, and collects the elemental sulfur passing through the microfiltration membrane in time, that is, vulcanizes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for forming and separating elemental sulfur in situ, which involves:
   (A) introducing an inert gas such as argon gas into the reaction device to maintain an anaerobic state in the reaction device;
   (B) disposing a separation membrane in the reaction device, where the separation membrane is a biofilm-microfiltration membrane double-layer structure, and a liquid circulation direction in the reaction device flows through the biofilm first, passes through the micro filter membrane on which a sulfur oxidizing bacteria is grown, and contacting the waste water to be treated with the biofilm to generate nitrogen and carbon dioxide gas and elemental sulfur solids on the biofilm;
   (C) discharging and collecting nitrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas in the reaction device, and separating the elemental sulfur formed on the biofilm through the microfiltration membrane to separate the elemental sulfur from the biofilm, where the microfiltration membrane blocks the passage of the sulfur oxidizing bacteria; and
   (D) performing solid-liquid separation of the elemental sulfur and the treated wastewater solution, and collecting the elemental sulfur.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device.
   Reaction device (1)
   Collecting device (2)
   Feeding device (4)
   Water storage device (6)
   Accumulation zone (13)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The microfiltration membrane has a pore size greater than 20 nanometer and less than 1 micrometer. The collecting gas is performed simultaneously with separating the solid.

PN CN108854470-A; CN108854470-B
TI Preparation of polyionic liquid crosslinked gel gas separation membrane involves mixing formaldehyde, glyoxal, glacial acetic acid, deionized water and 1,4-butanediamine in reaction vessel, and reacting to obtain homogeneous liquid.
AU MENG J
   YU Y
   HAO T
   YIN J
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 2018957099
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of polyionic liquid crosslinked gel gas separation membrane involves taking 1-5 pts. wt. formaldehyde, 3-8 pts. wt. glyoxal, 3-8 pts. wt. glacial acetic acid, 5-10 pts. wt. deionized water and 1-5 pts. wt. 1,4-butanediamine in reaction vessel, stirring, reacting at 20-100 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours to obtain homogeneous liquid, extracting with an extractant, distilling extract layer to obtain viscous dark brown polyionic liquid, dissolving polyionic liquid in deionized water, adding lithium bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide, stirring to obtain turbid solution, precipitating, stirring, filtering, washing, filtering, drying to obtain solid as polyionic liquid, taking 1-10 ml 10-30 wt.% polyionic liquid-methanol solution, adding 0.01-2 g crosslinker with epoxy functional group, adding 0.1-7 ml 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazoline bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide salt, stirring, pouring on glass plate, heating at 40-60 degrees C for 1-3 hours, and heating at 60-120 degrees C for 12-24 hours.
   USE - Preparation of polyionic liquid crosslinked gel gas separation membrane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of polyionic liquid crosslinked gel gas separation membrane with carbon dioxide solubility and high gas diffusibility, achieves fast and efficient separation of carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of polyionic liquid crosslinked gel gas separation membrane involves taking 1-5 pts. wt. formaldehyde, 3-8 pts. wt. glyoxal, 3-8 pts. wt. glacial acetic acid, 5-10 pts. wt. deionized water and 1-5 pts. wt. 1,4-butanediamine in dry reaction vessel, stirring in an ice bath, stirring in ice bath, reacting at 20-100 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours to obtain homogeneous liquid, extracting with an extractant, distilling the extract layer under reduced pressure to obtain viscous dark brown polyionic liquid, dissolving polyionic liquid in deionized water, adding lithium bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide, stirring to obtain turbid solution, precipitating, stirring for 8-12 hours, filtering, washing with pure water, filtering, vacuum drying to obtain solid as polyionic liquid, taking 1-10 ml 10-30 wt.% polyionic liquid-methanol solution, adding 0.01-2 g crosslinker with epoxy functional group, adding 0.1-7 ml 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazoline bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide salt, stirring, pouring on glass plate, heating under vacuum condition at 40-60 degrees C for 1-3 hours, and heating at 60-120 degrees C for 12-24 hours. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for polyionic liquid crosslinked gel gas separation membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The extractant is tetrahydrofuran, toluene or acetone. The epoxy functional group-containing crosslinker is trimethylolpropane triglycidyl ether, 5-norbornene-2,3-dicarboxylic acid diglycidyl ether, polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, 5,6-epoxy norbornane-2,3-dicarboxylic acid diglycidyl ether or (3,4-epoxycyclohexyl)methyl 3,4-epoxycyclohexylcarboxylate.

PN CN108617757-A
TI Preserving grapes by harvesting grapes, pruning root grape spike shaft, cooling, fumigating with sulfur dioxide, soaking in preservative, drying, adding processed grapes into fresh-keeping bag, tying and storing processed grapes.
AU LIU X
AE LIPU WANJIAXING FRUIT & VEGETABLE SPECIA (LIPU-Non-standard)
GA 201880051E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preserving grapes involves (a) harvesting fully ripe grapes, sorting, removing moldy fruit and broken fruit, pruning root grape spike shaft at the first branch and sealing with wax, (b) placing grapes into refrigerator and fumigating with sulfur dioxide, (c) soaking the treated grapes in preservative at 8-10 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, draining and drying in cool and ventilated place, where the preservative comprises Aloe extract, gamma -aminobutyric acid, cottonseed oil, sodium alginate, chitosan, aminooxyethyl vinyl glycine, gibberellin and distilled water and (d) adding the processed grapes into fresh-keeping bag and tying, where the fresh-keeping bag is provided with air holes with the pore diameter of 0.5-1.5 mm and the number of stomata is 3-7/100 cm2, placing the grapes in storage room which is filled with fresh gas and maintaining the temperature of storage room, where the fresh-keeping gas comprises sulfur dioxide, oxygen, carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving grapes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prolongs the shelf life of grapes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preserving grapes involves (a) harvesting fully ripe grapes, sorting, removing moldy and broken fruit, pruning root grape spike shaft at the first branch and sealing with wax, (b) placing grapes into refrigerator, reducing the temperature to 10-12 degrees C at a rate of 4 degrees C/hour for 5-7 hours and fumigating with 200 ppm sulfur dioxide for 12 hours to kill the pathogens, (c) soaking the treated grapes in preservative at 8-10 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, draining and drying in cool and ventilated place, where the preservative comprises 20-22 pts. wt. Aloe extract, 15-20 pts. wt. gamma -aminobutyric acid, 13-18 pts. wt. cottonseed oil, 5-7 pts. wt. sodium alginate, 11-15 pts. wt. chitosan, 8-12 pts. wt. aminooxyethyl vinyl glycine, 3-5 pts. wt. gibberellin and 40-60 pts. wt. distilled water and (d) adding the processed grapes into fresh-keeping bag and tying, where the fresh-keeping bag is provided with air holes with the pore diameter of 0.5-1.5 mm and the number of stomata is 3-7/100 cm2, placing the grapes in storage room which is filled with fresh gas and maintaining the temperature of storage room at 0-1 degrees C and humidity of 8-92%, where the fresh-keeping gas comprises 0.01-0.03 %mass sulfur dioxide, 5-7 %mass oxygen, 2-4 %mass carbon dioxide and 88.97-92.99 %mass nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The Aloe extract is prepared by taking 5 pts. wt. Aloe vera, crushing, adding 7 pts. wt. distilled water, boiling for 20 minutes, cooling to room temperature, stirring well and filtering with gauze. The fresh-keeping gas is changed once a week. In step (d), the grapes are inspected once a month, and the deteriorated grape grains are cleaned in time. Preferred Components: The fresh-keeping bag is a transparent polypropylene or polyethylene storage bag has a thickness of 50-60 mu m. Preferred Composition: The preservative comprises 21 pts. wt. Aloe extract, 17 pts. wt. gamma -aminobutyric acid, 15 pts. wt. cottonseed oil, 6 pts. wt. sodium alginate, 13 pts. wt. chitosan, 10 pts. wt. aminooxyethyl vinyl glycine, 4 pts. wt. gibberellin and 50 pts. wt. distilled water.

PN US2018264395-A1; US10639581-B2
TI Cartridge for mounting an air treatment material comprises a housing, and air treatment material received within the housing to remove carbon dioxide received within housing, and spaced from inner walls of housing by resilient sheets.
AU OCOIN J R
   SENOFONTE L P
   JUNG E I
   BONFIGLIO G
AE HAMILTON SUNDSTRAND CORP (SUNH-C)
GA 2018740215
AB    NOVELTY - Cartridge (20) for mounting an air treatment material comprises a housing (26, 28); and air treatment material received within the housing to remove carbon dioxide received within the housing, and spaced from inner walls of the housing by resilient sheets. The air treatment material is hydroxide sheets. There is an inlet opening into the housing for air flowing across the air treatment material and an outlet opening on an opposed end of the housing, and with there being top and bottom surfaces (22, 24) and side surfaces forming a perimeter about the air treatment material. The resilient sheets extend substantially continuously across the side surfaces and the top and bottom surfaces at the inlet end to increase airflow across the air treatment material. The resilient sheets have cutout portions (44, 46, 50, 56, 58) to result in lower spring force at positions spaced from the inlet end.
   USE - The cartridge is useful for mounting an air treatment material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - By properly selecting the respective sizes of the openings, the densities, along with potentially selecting different foams, the spring force from the two sheets can be controlled. The cutouts provide lesser support at more central areas where the housing is less stiff than it is at the perimeter. This facilitates the expansion of the sheets without distorting the housing top, the housing sides and the housing bottom.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an enclosed inhabited space comprising an enclosure to be inhabited by a living entity, and containing air, and a system for delivering the air across a cartridge containing air treatment material; a housing; air treatment material received within the housing, and spaced from inner walls of the housing by resilient sheets the air treatment material is metal hydroxide sheets; there being an inlet opening into the housing for air flowing across the air treatment material and an outlet opening on an opposed end of the housing, and with there being top and bottom surfaces and side surfaces forming a perimeter about the air treatment material, and the resilient sheets extending substantially continuously across the side surfaces and the top and bottom surfaces at the inlet end to increase airflow across the air treatment material.

   Cartridge (20)
   Surfaces (22, 24)
   Housing (26, 28)
   Air treatment material (34)
   Cutout portions (44, 46, 50, 56, 58)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The air treatment material is metal hydroxide sheets. It is lithium hydroxide sheets. The resilient sheets are foam. The foam sheets (52, 54) also form a substantially continuous perimeter at an outlet side of the housing. The foam sheets at the top and bottom surfaces include foam sheets, where the foam sheets at the top and bottom surfaces have different spring forces. A 1st of the foam sheets at the top and bottom surfaces, which is positioned adjacent the air treatment material, has a lower spring force than a 2nd of foam sheets at the top and bottom surfaces which is positioned on an opposed side of the 1st relative to the air treatment material. PT P
PN CN108439440-A
TI Preparing magnesium oxide using olive rock ore comprises e.g. crushing and grinding olive rock ore, adding into sulfuric acid solution, adding water, stirring and heating, fully dissolving, and filtering to obtain filtrate.
AU ZHANG Q
AE ZHANG Q (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201868224W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing magnesium oxide using olive rock ore comprises (1) crushing and grinding the olive rock ore, adding into sulfuric acid solution, adding water, stirring and heating, fully dissolving, and filtering to obtain filtrate; (2) continuously adding sulfuric acid solution to the filtrate, adjusting the system pH, introducing high-purity oxygen from the bottom of the solution, reacting and stirring, reacting, and filtering to remove precipitated iron hydroxide; (3) passing high-purity ammonia gas from bottom of filtrate, adjusting pH of the system, controlling the stirring speed, reacting, distilling off water under reduced pressure, and slowly dissolving magnesium hydroxide as the volume of the solution decreases; (4) filtering, drying the obtained magnesium hydroxide, calcining for one time, obtaining crude magnesium oxide, dissolving the crude magnesium oxide in hydrochloric acid, introducing mixed gas of high-purity carbon dioxide and high-purity ammonia gas into reaction liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing magnesium oxide using olive rock ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The magnesium oxide: has little impurity and high quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing magnesium oxide using olive rock ore comprises (1) crushing and grinding the olive rock ore into 60-80 mu m particle size, adding into sulfuric acid solution, adding water, stirring and heating to 80-90 degrees C, fully dissolving, and filtering to obtain filtrate; (2) continuously adding sulfuric acid solution to the filtrate, adjusting the system pH to 2-4, introducing high-purity oxygen from the bottom of the solution, reacting and stirring for 4-6 hours, reacting at 20-30 degrees C, and filtering to remove precipitated iron hydroxide; (3) passing high-purity ammonia gas from the bottom of the filtrate, adjusting the pH of the system to 8-10, controlling the stirring speed at 10 revolutions/minute, reacting at 25-35 degrees C for 40-60 minutes, distilling off water under reduced pressure, and slowly dissolving magnesium hydroxide as the volume of the solution decreases; (4) filtering, drying the obtained magnesium hydroxide, calcining for one time, where the calcination temperature is 350-400 degrees C, obtaining crude magnesium oxide, dissolving the crude magnesium oxide in hydrochloric acid, introducing mixed gas of high-purity carbon dioxide and high-purity ammonia gas into reaction liquid, and reacting is stirring for 40-60 minutes; filtering to obtain basic magnesium carbonate, drying basic magnesium carbonate, subjecting to secondary calcination at 600-700 degrees C for 2-4 hours at heating rate of 40-50 degrees C/minutes, slowly cooling to room temperature at rate of 15-30 degrees C/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the steps (1) and (2), the concentration of the sulfuric acid solution is 30-50%. In the step (3), the temperature of vacuum-distilled water is 70-80 degrees C. Preferred Components: In the step (1), the mass ratio of water to olive rock ore is 1:8. In the step (4), the volume ratio of high purity carbon dioxide to high purity ammonia gas is 1:1-1.5. In the step (4), drying the magnesium hydroxide at 80-100 degrees C. In the step (4), drying the basic magnesium carbonate at 80-100 degrees C.

PN CN108395202-A; CN108395202-B
TI Preparation of fiber-modified aerogel material            involves mixing e.g. solution containing ethanol, and            mixed fiber obtained using e.g. dopamine hydrochloride            solution and alumina fiber, pouring into mold, sealing,            and processing.
AU HE J
   PANG W
   CHEN Y
   HU Z
AE FOSHAN XIHUA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
   SHENZHEN GUORIHONG ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201865282G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of fiber-modified aerogel material                involves configuring 50-80 pts. wt. dopamine                hydrochloride solution to adjust pH to 9-10, adding                5-10 pts. wt. jute fiber felt, 1-3 pts. wt. alumina                fiber and 2-5 pts. wt. sepiolite fiber, passing                nitrogen gas to make the solution saturated with                nitrogen, sealing, irradiating and agitating,                taking out, washing with deionized water, drying to                obtain the treated mixed fiber, mixing zirconium                oxychloride octahydrate, titanium tetrachloride and                deionized water, in a volume ratio of 1:1:3 to                obtain solution (A), mixing tetraethyl                orthosilicate and ethanol mixed in a volume ratio                of 3:1 to obtain solution (B), mixing solution (A)                and solution (B), placing in an ice water bath,                adding 3-5 pts. wt. propylene oxide, stirring,                adding mixed fiber and polydopamine nanofibers,                stirring, pouring into a mold, sealing, taking out,                soaking in acetone, replacing acetone once every 8                hours, taking out from acetone, and drying by                supercritical carbon dioxide.
   USE - Preparation of fiber-modified aerogel                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces material with excellent                heat insulation effect, bending strength,                flexibility and compressive strength, thus avoiding                sudden breakage of the material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of fiber-modified aerogel material                involves configuring 50-80 pts. wt. dopamine                hydrochloride solution to adjust pH to 9-10, adding                5-10 pts. wt. jute fiber felt, 1-3 pts. wt. alumina                fiber and 2-5 pts. wt. sepiolite fiber, passing                nitrogen gas to make the solution saturated with                nitrogen, sealing, irradiating and agitating using                2.5 MeV and 40 mA electron accelerator, with an                irradiation dosage rate of 100-200 kGy/hour,                irradiation dosage of 500-1000 kGy, and at stirring                speed of 100-200 rpm, taking out, washing with                deionized water, drying at 30-40 degrees C to                obtain the treated mixed fiber, mixing zirconium                oxychloride octahydrate, titanium tetrachloride and                deionized water, in a volume ratio of 1:1:3 to                obtain solution (A), mixing tetraethyl                orthosilicate and ethanol mixed in a volume ratio                of 3:1 to obtain solution (B), mixing 30-50 pts.                wt. solution (A) and 20-30 pts. wt. solution (B),                stirring for 2-3 hours, placing in an ice water                bath, adding 3-5 pts. wt. propylene oxide, stirring                for 30-40 minutes, adding mixed fiber and                polydopamine nanofibers, stirring, pouring into a                mold, sealing at 50-55 degrees C for 24-27 hours,                taking out, soaking in acetone for 24 hours,                replacing acetone once every 8 hours, taking out                from acetone, and drying by supercritical carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The alumina fiber and                  sepiolite fiber, has a length of 30-50 mm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The jute fiber felt                  has length of 50 mm, width of 30 mm, and                  thickness of 50 mu m. The dopamine hydrochloride                  solution has a concentration of 5-10 mg/mL.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Property: The polydopamine                  nanofiber has a length of 70-100 nm.

PN CN107596863-A
TI Concentrating solar energy distributed thermal reforming hydrogen production device comprises reactor body discharged through gas liquid separator unit, high temperature heat pipe, outlet of gas liquid separator which is set with compressor.
AU TANG D
   JIANG B
   YUAN D
   SUN Y
   ZHENG J
   LI M
   ZHANG C
   WANG D
   FENG Y
   WANG S
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201809219C
AB    NOVELTY - Concentrating solar energy distributed thermal reforming hydrogen production device comprises a raw material preheating, reactor body unit discharged through the gas-liquid separator, gas, hydrogen storage unit, raw material preheating and high temperature heat pipe. The outlet of the gas-liquid separator (6) is set with the first compressor (7-1) of the product gas through the pressure swing adsorption device, where mixed gas is carbon dioxide and unreacted methane. The gas purification unit comprises second heat exchanger (1-2) and pressure swing adsorption device.
   USE - Concentrating solar energy distributed thermal reforming hydrogen production device.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen production device reduces the complexity of the solar thermal chemical hydrogen plant and avoids the direct reactor quartz window cleaning and maintaining air tightness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Concentrating solar energy distributed thermal reforming hydrogen production device comprises a raw material preheating unit, a reactor body unit discharged through the gas-liquid separator (6), a gas, the hydrogen storage unit, the raw material preheating unit, and high temperature heat pipe (14). The discharged condensed water is reused after being collected as the raw material. The outlet of the gas-liquid separator is set with the first compressor (7-1) of the product gas through the pressure swing adsorption device. The gas purification unit comprises a second heat exchanger (1-2) and a pressure swing adsorption device. The cooling water flows in the pipe and the product gas flows in the shell side, the second heat exchanger outlet set with the first compressor. Compressed product gas is directly into the pressure swing adsorption device through the pressure swing adsorption device. The trace amount of carbon dioxide and methane are separated to obtain high purity hydrogen and carbon monooxide, and then compressed by the compressor through the second and third storage portion into the hydrogen to carbon monoxide. Hydrogen storage unit is composed of compressor and a gas storage tank. Gas purification unit is obtained purified hydrogen, containing 99.9% pure hydrogen compressed by the compressor to 70Megapascal in the tank. The compressor and gas storage tank are connected by one way valve, and gas tank is equipped with an overpressure releasing device, a hydrogen leakage alarm device, and pressure gages which is attached to security facilities.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of concentrating solar energy distributed thermal reforming hydrogen production device.
   First heat exchanger (1-1)
   Second heat exchanger (1-2)
   Gas-liquid separator (6)
   First compressor (7-1)
   Heat pipe (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The concentrating solar distributed thermal reforming hydrogen production device where high-temperature heat pipe selects liquid sodium as the heat pipe working fluid and the shell material use nickel-chromium alloy. High temperature heat pipe uses anti-gravity design, the internal circulation power capillary force, and suction wick material is selected three metal mesh which is 400 in number. The adsorbent used in the pressure swing adsorption unit is selected from alumina, Activated carbon or molecular sieve. Preferred Condition: The total number of adsorption pots is 4, number of equalization times is 1 before purging, and the number of equalization times after purging is 1.

AE JIANG X (JIAN-Individual)
GA 202259781T
AB    NOVELTY - The aid has an explanation table (1) that is arranged in the greenhouse. A display (2) is provided on the explanation table. A monitoring mechanism is arranged vertically on the side of the explanation table. The monitoring mechanism is composed of a lifting module (3) and a monitoring module (4). The lifting module is used to adjust the vertical height of the monitoring module. The monitoring module is used to monitor the carbon dioxide content in the greenhouse in real time, so that the carbon dioxide content at different heights from the ground is compared and explained. An infrared carbon dioxide sensor is arranged on an one-way air intake assembly to detect the carbon dioxide content in the suctioned air. A turbine cleaning assembly is located at the air inlet of the one-way air intake assembly, and is used to perform variable speed cleaning on the suction air to eliminate the influence of impurities on the monitoring data.
   USE - Intelligent greenhouse carbon dioxide content monitoring teaching aid for soil-less cultivation soil-free.
   ADVANTAGE - The monitoring teaching aid is simple and convenient to operate by comparing and monitoring, which is good for improving explanation efficiency, and learning understanding. The monitoring module can monitor the carbon dioxide content of different ground height in the greenhouse, so as to explain the corresponding carbon dioxide fertilizer amount control to different layers of soil-free flower shelf. The air resistance when the one-way air inlet assembly is used for driving the turbine cleaning assembly to work, it not only saves energy, and the turbine cleaned assembly through the auxiliary shaft when working, speed reducing gear, gear shifting, a transmission structure composed of a cleaning shaft and a speed reducing disc, and the brush is capable of cleaning the filter screen on the cylinder.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the intelligent greenhouse carbon dioxide content monitoring teaching aid for soil-less cultivation soil-free.

TI Producing a polyalkylene carbonate resin comprises polymerizing carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide in the presence of a catalyst in an organic solvent to form crude product solution, and separating each containing resin solution.
AU KIM W
   PARK S
   KIM S
   CHEUL S S
   MIN K
   WOO W H
AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 202117386Y
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a polyalkylene carbonate resin comprises polymerizing carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide in the presence of a catalyst in an organic solvent to form crude product solution including a polyalkylene carbonate resin, unreacted carbon dioxide, unreacted alkylene oxide, a residual catalyst and an organic solvent, while supplying a stripping gas including an inert gas and the organic solvent vapor, a residual monomer stream including unreacted alkylene oxide and unreacted carbon dioxide, a residual catalyst, and a polyalkylene carbonate resin are obtained from the crude product solution, separating each containing resin solution, adding an organic solution containing a catalyst coagulant to the resin solution to separate the residual catalyst from the resin solution, obtaining purified solution of a polyalkylene carbonate resin, condensing the residual monomer stream to a solution state, separating unreacted carbon dioxide and degassed gas from the condensed solution, and recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a polyalkylene carbonate resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the recovery rate of unreacted monomers, and separation performance; achieves high purity; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing a polyalkylene carbonate resin comprises polymerizing carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide in the presence of a catalyst in an organic solvent to form crude product solution including a polyalkylene carbonate resin, unreacted carbon dioxide, unreacted alkylene oxide, a residual catalyst and an organic solvent, while supplying a stripping gas including an inert gas and the organic solvent vapor, a residual monomer stream including unreacted alkylene oxide and unreacted carbon dioxide, a residual catalyst, and a polyalkylene carbonate resin are obtained from the crude product solution, separating each containing resin solution, adding an organic solution containing a catalyst coagulant to the resin solution to separate the residual catalyst from the resin solution, obtaining purified solution of a polyalkylene carbonate resin, condensing the residual monomer stream to a solution state, separating unreacted carbon dioxide and degassed gas from the condensed solution, and recycling the gas-liquid separated solution to the polymerization step.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the method for producing a polyalkylene carbonate resin (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The degassing gas comprises nitrogen gas and organic solvent vapor as an inert gas. The organic solution containing the catalyst coagulant further comprises a second diluted organic solvent, and the viscosity of the resin solution to which the organic solution containing the catalyst coagulant is added is diluted to 100-300 cPs. The first or second diluted organic solvent is the same as the organic solvent contained in the crude product solution. The catalyst includes a zinc-based catalyst, and a polyalkylene using water as the catalyst coagulant. Preferred Method: Between the step of forming the crude product solution and the step of supplying a degassing gas, adding a first diluted organic solvent to the crude product solution to dilute the viscosity of the crude product solution to 100-300 cPs. The separation of the residual catalyst includes adding an organic solution containing a catalyst coagulant to the resin solution, and precipitating the catalyst from the resultant product and filtering the supernatant from which the residual catalyst is separated. After the filtration, the catalyst is recovered by removing the solvent from the lower layer liquid containing the residual catalyst. The condensation step is performed at a temperature of less than or equal to 90 degrees C in the presence of cooling water, and multi-stage compression and condensation of the residual monomer stream. The gas-liquid separated solution contains an organic solvent and unreacted alkylene oxide, and is pressurized to a pressure of greater than or equal to 20 bar in a pump to be recycled to the polymerization step.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN US2022213566-A1; CA3144748-A1; NO202200021-A
TI Direct reduction of iron involves generating            metallic iron from its ore by removing oxygen from ore            using reducing gaseous mixture feed that produces            carbon dioxide by-product, and geologically            sequestrating the by-product.
AU MOTAMEDHASHEMI M
   LEVEEN L
   TRUE B
AE NUCOR CORP (NUCR-C)
   NUCOR CORP (NUCR-C)
GA 202287848D
AB    NOVELTY - A direct reduction of iron (DRI) process                involves generating metallic iron from its ore by                removing oxygen from the ore using a reducing                gaseous mixture feed that produces a carbon dioxide                by-product; and geologically sequestrating the                by-product.
   USE - A direct reduction of iron (DRI)                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The DRI process provides for the capability of                producing blue hydrogen and subsequently the                production of blue steel. The method and system                increases the amount of carbon monoxide in the                syngas, which otherwise results in more, rather                than less, carbon dioxide produced during DRI,                where the present method prevents or eliminates                emission of that excess carbon dioxide by-product                to the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) DRI system comprising direct reduction                unit configured to receive a source of iron ore and                a reducing gaseous mixture feed comprising at least                hydrogen and/or carbon monoxide, scrubber unit                configured to receive a top gas feed from the                direct reduction unit, top gas separator unit to                receive the top gas feed from the scrubber unit and                to provide a carbon dioxide by-product feed and an                unreacted hydrogen and carbon monoxide feed,                process gas separator unit configured to receive                the unreacted hydrogen and/or unreacted carbon                monoxide from the top gas separator unit and to                provide a carbon monoxide feed and an essentially                pure hydrogen feed, and carbon dioxide geological                sequestering unit configured to receive the carbon                dioxide by-product feed from the scrubber                unit;
   (2) producing ammonia during direct reduction                of iron ore, which involves separating air into an                oxygen stream and a nitrogen stream, introducing                the oxygen stream to a partial oxidation unit for                providing syngas comprising hydrogen and carbon                monoxide, generating metallic iron from its ore and                a top gas in a direct reduction unit using the                syngas, separating the top gas into an essentially                pure stream of hydrogen, combining the essentially                pure stream of hydrogen with the nitrogen stream,                and producing ammonia;
   (3) producing a hydrocarbon-based fuel during                direct reduction of iron ore, which involves                generating metallic iron from its ore and a top gas                in a direct reduction unit using a syngas                comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, separating                the top gas into an essentially pure stream of                hydrogen and an essentially pure stream of carbon                dioxide, combining the essentially pure stream of                hydrogen with the essentially pure stream of carbon                dioxide, and producing hydrocarbon-based fuel;                and
   (4) retrofit system for DRI unit comprising                scrubber unit configured to receive a top gas feed                from a direct reduction unit, top gas separator                unit to receive the top gas feed from the scrubber                unit and to provide a carbon dioxide by-product                feed and an unreacted hydrogen and carbon monoxide                feed, process gas separator unit configured to                receive the unreacted hydrogen and/or carbon                monoxide from the top gas separator unit and to                provide a carbon monoxide feed and an essentially                pure hydrogen feed, and carbon dioxide geological                sequestering unit configured to receive the carbon                dioxide by-product feed from the scrubber                unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: Reducing gaseous                  mixture feed comprises hydrogen and carbon                  monoxide.Preferred Processes: Additional carbon                  monoxide is added to the reducing gaseous mixture                  feed. Carbon dioxide by-product is separated from                  water vapor and unreacted reducing gaseous                  mixture feed prior to geological sequestering.                  Carbon dioxide by-product is dried prior to                  geological sequestering. Carbon dioxide                  by-product is compressed to a supercritical state                  prior to geological sequestering. Unreacted                  hydrogen from the reducing gaseous mixture feed                  is separated from water vapor and unreacted                  reducing gaseous mixture feed prior to geological                  sequestering. Unreacted carbon monoxide is                  recycled to the reducing gaseous mixture feed                  such that a concentration ratio of hydrogen to                  carbon monoxide introduced to a direct reduction                  unit comprising iron ore is between 3 and 0.6.                  The method further comprises introducing the                  metallic iron to an electric arc furnace, which                  is continuous or semi-continuous. Ammonia is                  produced with concurrent carbon dioxide                  sequestering to provide blue ammonia.Preferred Devices: Carbon dioxide geological                  sequestering unit is coupled to one or more                  subterranean oil reservoirs, natural gas                  deposits, un-mineable coal deposits, saline                  formations, shale, and basalt formations. The                  system further comprises a hydrogen storage unit                  configured to receive the essentially pure                  hydrogen feed from a pressure swing absorption                  unit or a cryogenic unit. The system further                  comprises electric arc furnace configured to                  receive the metallic iron. Electric arc furnace                  is configured to receive the metallic iron                  continuously or semi-continuously. The system is                  closed loop.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Hydrocarbon fuel is                  produced with concurrent carbon dioxide                  sequestering to provide blue hydrocarbon                  fuel.Preferred Component: Hydrocarbon-based fuel                  is jet fuel.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Ganoderma lucidum polysaccharide                with high antitumor activity comprises e.g. (1)                crushing the Ganoderma lucidum raw medicinal                material into 10-30 meshes of Ganoderma lucidum                granules, (2) placing crushed G. lucidum granules                into the extraction tank of the high-pressure                supercritical extraction device, sealing, and                opening the high-pressure carbon dioxide pump,                static extraction for 30-60 minutes, then opening                the fine adjustment valve for dynamic extraction,                controlling the carbon dioxide flow rate in the                separation tank pressure 80-120 bar, temperature                30-50 degrees C under the condition of 10-16                kg/hour, extracting the extract for 2-4 hours, and                then removing fat-soluble and low-polar components;                and (3) after carbon dioxide extraction, adding 3-6                times of clean water to the extraction tank, the                weight is equal to 3-6 times the weight of the                added G. lucidum granules, heating and extracting,                then vacuum concentrating and freeze-drying.
   USE - The method is useful for Ganoderma lucidum                polysaccharide with high antitumor activity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple extraction and                separation, and is suitable for industrial                production, and can obtains extract of Ganoderma                lucidum polysaccharide with strong antitumor                activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Ganoderma lucidum polysaccharide                with high antitumor activity comprises (1) crushing                the Ganoderma lucidum raw medicinal material into                10-30 meshes of Ganoderma lucidum granules, (2)                placing crushed G. lucidum granules into the                extraction tank of the high-pressure supercritical                extraction device, sealing, and opening the                high-pressure carbon dioxide pump, increasing the                pressure to 600-2000 bar, temperature is 40-60                degrees C, static extraction for 30-60 minutes,                then opening the fine adjustment valve for dynamic                extraction, controlling the carbon dioxide flow                rate in the separation tank pressure 80-120 bar,                temperature 30-50 degrees C under the condition of                10-16 kg/hour, extracting the extract for 2-4                hours, and then removing fat-soluble and low-polar                components including oil, triterpene and pigment in                G. lucidum cell structure; and (3) after carbon                dioxide extraction, adding 3-6 times of clean water                to the extraction tank, the weight is equal to 3-6                times the weight of the added G. lucidum granules,                heating to the temperature of 70-80 degrees C, the                carbon dioxide is pressurized to 80-100bar, and                keeping the extraction pressure for 1.5-3 hours,                then vacuum concentrating and freeze-drying to                obtain light brown G. lucidum polysaccharide. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for Ganoderma                lucidum polysaccharide extracting device comprising                a carbon dioxide steel bottle (1), a carbon dioxide                storage tank (2), an extracting tank (3),                separating tank (4), pipeline and adjusting valve,                a heat exchanger I, a high pressure pump; the heat                exchanger II, valve and a collecting valve. Where                further comprising a clean water tank, water pump,                extracting liquid outflow valve, and the clean                water tank is connected with the extracting tank                through a pipeline and a water pump; the lower part                of the extracting tank is equipped with extracting                liquid outflow valve.

AU ZHOU S
   BI J
   CHEN B
AE UNIV XIAN SHIYOU (UXSY-C)
GA 2022B99807
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves analyzing the velocity                curve of the reduced pressure wave and the ductile                propagation velocity curve of the crack by a                Battelle hyperbolic method when the ductile                propagation velocity curve of the crack is                intersected with the curve of the reduced pressure                wave. The fracture velocity and the speed of the                reduced pressure wave are the same at a certain                moment. The pressure of the crack tip is not                reduced under the condition where the crack can be                continuously expanded when the ductile expansion                speed curve of the crack is tangent to the                decompression wave curve. The corresponding                toughness value in the case is the minimum                toughness value required for preventing the crack                from expanding where the crack can just                automatically stop. The corresponding toughness                value in this case is greater than the toughness                value required to prevent the crack from                propagating where the crack can be                self-arrested.
   USE - Method for predicting crack ductility of                impurity-containing supercritical carbon dioxide                conveying pipeline.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing pipeline crack                ductility expansion prediction through software                simulation, thus reducing the number of the needed                sensor and monitoring device, greatly reducing the                cost of the pipeline monitoring system, reducing                the buried pipeline, under the special topographic                conditions such as submarine pipeline, and reducing                the difficulty of monitoring the crack ductile                expansion, and hence increasing the stability of                the monitoring system.

AU FENG M
AE FENG M (FENG-Individual)
GA 202194979W
AB    NOVELTY - Degradable plastic based on poly(propylene carbonate) (PPC) comprises 30-40 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 60-70 pts. wt. propylene oxide, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. crosslinking auxiliary agent, 1-10 pts. wt. filler and 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. plasticizer.

   WU X
   ZHUO L
   MO W
AE GUANGDONG ACAD SCI MICROBIOLOGY INST (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202184312E
AB    NOVELTY - Ganoderma leucocontextum strain Z160097 is preserved at Guangdong Microbial Culture Collection Center under preservation number (GDMCC NO: 60965), and is new.
   USE - Ganoderma leucocontextum strain Z160097 used as medicinal fungus and for preparing Ganoderma leucocontextum polysaccharide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Ganoderma leucocontextum strain Z160097 has small distribution range, and ensures rapid and accurate identification that lays foundation for industrial development and protection of property right.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing Ganoderma leucocontextum polysaccharide, which involves using Ganoderma leucocontextum strain Z160097 as raw material to extract Ganoderma leucocontextum polysaccharide contained in it; and
   (2) a method for culturing Ganoderma leucocontextum Z160097, which involves using 31 wt.% cotton seed hull, 58 wt.% wood chip, 10 wt.% bran, 1 wt.% calcium carbonate and 62 wt.% water, and has natural pH; inoculating Ganoderma leucocontextum Z160097 on the cultivation material, cultivating it in culture room at 25 degrees C for 30-40 days so that hyphae become full of fungus bag, entering outlet room, controlling temperature between 15-18 degrees C and air humidity between 85-90% and light of 100-200lux, controlling carbon dioxide content above 2000ppm, where temperature and relative humidity of air remain unchanged after the original base is produced, adjusting light to 200-300lux, maintaining carbon dioxide content at above 2000ppm, and when the stalk elongation period ends and enters the opening period then temperature is maintained between 15-18 degrees C and relative humidity of the air is adjusted between 90-95%, and light is 300-500lux, and carbon dioxide content is less than 1200ppm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The edge of white pulp of Ganoderma leucocontextum cap turns from light yellow to reddish brown then it is harvested one week after the powder is sprayed.
