PT P
PN KR2021077391-A
TI Manufacturing ductile nano-porous carbon fabric in            e.g. next-generation ductile electrodes, comprises e.g.            drying polymerized oxide fiber in mixed solution of            zinc chloride and ammonium chloride and heating to            carbonize.
AU NOUR F S A
   OH H
   PARK J W
   JUNG M J
AE UNIV GYEONGNAM NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY IND (KOAD-C)
GA 2021757132
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing ductile nano-porous carbon                fabric comprises preparing an oxidizing agent for                ductile nano-porous carbon fibers; impregnating the                artificial fabric with the oxidizing agent and                magnetically stirring to obtain oxidized fibers;                impregnating the oxidized fiber in a solution of                organic nanoparticles (polypyrrole) and                magnetically stirring to obtain a polymerized                oxidized fiber; and impregnating and drying the                polymerized oxide fiber in a mixed solution of zinc                chloride and ammonium chloride and heating to                carbonize to obtain a ductile nano-porous carbon                fabric.
   USE - The ductile nano-porous carbon fabric is                useful in next-generation ductile electrodes for                electrochemical energy storage applications e.g.                supercapacitors and batteries.
   ADVANTAGE - The ductile nano-porous carbon fabric has high                storage capacity of hydrogen, methane and carbon                dioxide adsorption compared commercial products and                synthetic activated carbon fiber fabrics, high                specific surface area (SSA) and specific pore                volume (SPV), high porosity with a unique                heteroatom content required in various industrial                fields, improved chemical stability, activated                porous carbon fiber which is versatile, expandable                to other gases, ductile, efficient, and safe to                handle, thus having excellent energy-related gas                storage (carbon dioxide, hydrogen) and greenhouse                gas (carbon dioxide) adsorption performance; and is                environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) ductile nano-porous carbon fabric                comprising polypyrrole which is polymerized in the                fabric through the process of impregnation in a                solution containing polypyrrole; and
   (2) adsorbent comprising ductile nano-porous                fabric.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Method: The ductile nano-porous                  carbon fabric is physically activated by heating                  it in a carbon dioxide atmosphere. The soft                  nano-porous carbon fabric is chemically activated                  using potassium hydroxide solution. The oxidizing                  agent comprises 1 g ammonium persulfate in 50 ml                  distilled water. The carbonization process is                  carried out by immersing the polymerized oxide                  fiber in a solution containing 5 wt.% zinc                  chloride and 5 wt.% ammonium chloride for 1 hour,                  drying it in air at 100 degrees C, and then                  maintaining it at 850 degrees C in a nitrogen                  atmosphere for 1 hour. In the physical activation                  process step, the soft nano-porous carbon fabric                  is treated by blowing 100 ml carbon                  dioxide/minute at 900 degrees C for 1 hour, and                  then washed with 1.5 M hydrogen chloride and                  distilled water and drying in a vacuum at 100                  degrees C. The chemical activation process is                  performed by immersing the soft nano-porous                  carbon fabric in potassium hydroxide solution for                  1 hour, drying it in air, and then maintaining it                  at 900 degrees C in a nitrogen atmosphere for 1                  hour and washing with 10 wt.% hydrogen chloride                  and distilled water. The pore size is reduced and                  the specific surface area is increased by                  physically activating the ductile nano-porous                  carbon fabric by heating it in a carbon dioxide                  atmosphere or chemically activating it using a                  potassium hydroxide solution. The ductile                  nano-porous carbon fabric is capable of                  reversibly storing up to 10 wt.% hydrogen at 77K                  or 1 wt.% hydrogen at 298 K or 20 mmol/g of                  methane at 298 K or 30 mmol/g carbon dioxide at                  298 K; and selectively adsorbs carbon dioxide                  rather than methane so that the carbon                  dioxide/methane selectivity is set to a maximum                  of 30; and has a specific surface area of                  1000-1900 m2/g and micropores of 0.77-2.6 nm                  after activation. The ductile nano-porous carbon                  fabric comprises nitrogen and oxygen atoms.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves carrying out a third                operating mode in the third period between the                second period and the first period of time. The                compressed air in the adsorber is partially freed                from water and carbon dioxide in the third                operating mode. The air product is removed from the                distillation column system (10) and a unit is                heated in the heat exchanger (1). An adjustable                proportion of the compressed air cooled in the heat                exchanger or an adjustable amount of further                compressed air is used to partially free water and                carbon dioxide in the adsorber. The third operating                mode is carried out until the first end (11) of the                heat exchanger is again at the first temperature                level.

Reformer has greater than or equal to 3 plates arranged in parallel, and source gas circulation chamber where source gas circulates, and thermal medium circulation chamber where thermal medium circulates which are formed between adjacent plates to alternate in the direction in which the plate is arranged in parallel; modification catalyst is accommodated in the source gas circulation chamber interior; and electric power supply component is comprised so that electric power may be supplied to each plate. The plant further includes boiler, where addition steam contains 2nd steam produced by the boiler. The plant further includes vaporizer, where addition steam contains 3rd steam produced by vaporizer. Device is a solid oxide fuel cell, where a portion of carbon dioxide is accepted from the outside of the plant. Preferred Process: A portion of waste gas of the gas engine is supplied to reformer as a raw material of reforming reaction of source gas. Preferred Condition: Reaction temperature of reforming reaction of source gas is 373-700 K.

TI Self-extinguishing indoor power distribution cabinet for use during fire disaster, has cabinet unit whose upper end is provided with driving mechanism for driving fan to rotate when fire is in cabinet unit.
AU ZHOU Q
AE ZHOU Q (ZHOU-Individual)
   SHANDONG GUANGRUI ELECTRIC POWER TECHNOLOGY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202108197W
AB    NOVELTY - The cabinet has a sliding plate (9) whose side wall is provided with multiple air holes (10) that are matched with a radiating hole (2). A lower end of the sliding plate is elastically connected with an inner bottom portion of a cabinet unit (1) through a spring. A lower end of the sliding plate is embedded with a permanent magnet. An upper end of the cabinet unit is embedded with a storage battery (13). An upper end of the cabinet unit is provided with a driving mechanism for driving a fan (4) to rotate when fire is in the cabinet unit. An impeller in the fan is made of magnetic material.
   USE - Self-extinguishing indoor power distribution cabinet for use during fire disaster.
   ADVANTAGE - The cabinet guides the large amount of carbon monoxide in the cabinet unit into the reaction chamber through the fan rotation, so that the reaction plate reacts with carbon monoxide to generate iron and carbon dioxide at high temperature. The carbon dioxide flows out through the air outlet so as to increase the concentration of carbon dioxide in the cabinet unit and inhibit the fire in the cabinet unit. The cabinet avoids the high concentration of carbon monoxide in the cabinet unit so as to avoid harm to the health of the worker.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a self-extinguishing indoor power distribution cabinet.
   Cabinet unit (1)
   Radiating hole (2)
   Fan (4)

AB    NOVELTY - Controlling air conditioner indoor machine comprises starting the fresh air device, obtaining the user's height G, and adjusting the rotation speed of the upper air blower and the lower air blower according to the height G. where the air-conditioning indoor unit comprises an upper air blower and a lower air blower located below the upper air blower, and a fresh air device that guides outdoor air upstream of the upper air blower and the lower air blower.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for air conditioner indoor machine for executing indoor machine control method.

PT P
PN WO2020049082-A1; EP3846937-A1; US2021354114-A1
TI Preparing catalyst used for synthesizing methanol, involves taking indium oxide in form of indium oxide and one additional metal selected from noble metal, and preparing catalyst by co-precipitation of saline solution.
AU STEWART J
   CURULLA-FERRE D
   PEREZ-RAMIREZ J
   MONDELLI C
   FREI M
AE TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   ETH ZUERICH (ETHE-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   ETH ZUERICH (ETHE-C)
   ETH ZUERICH (ETHE-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
GA 2020206953
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst involves taking indium oxide in the form of indium oxide, and one additional metal selected from a noble metal. The average particle size of noble metal phase is 0.05-5 nm as determined by STEM-EDX. The catalyst is prepared by co-precipitation of a saline solution at a pH above 8.5 comprises an indium salt and a salt of one additional metal selected from a noble metal, and optionally further comprises a salt of one alkaline earth metal.
   USE - Method for preparing catalyst used for synthesizing methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare catalyst is able to increases productivity, and also provides long term measurements proved the superior stability of materials in comparison to copper-based systems over 100 hours on stream with high selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for synthesizing methanol, which involves:
   (A) providing a feed stream which comprises hydrogen and carbon oxides selected from carbon dioxide or a mixture of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, where carbon dioxide represents from 1-50 mol.% total molar content of the feed stream, carbon monoxide is contained from 0-85 mol.% of the total molar content, and H2 is comprised from 5-99 mol.% total molar content of the feed stream;
   (B) providing a catalyst by putting feed stream with catalyst at a reaction temperature of 373 Kelwin (99.85 degrees C) under a pressure of 0.5 mega Pascal; and
   (C) recovering the methanol from the effluents by a separation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst further comprises one alkaline earth metal, preferably incorporated simultaneously with indium salt and salt of one additional metal selected from a noble metal at the co-precipitation stage. The one additional metal is a noble metal selected from ruthenium (Ru), rhodium (Rh), palladium (Pd), silver (Ag), osmium (Os), platinum (Pt), copper (Cu), gold (Au), iridium (Ir), and any combination. Preferred Conditions: The co-precipitation is performed at a pH above 9, and a temperature of 293 Kelwin (19.85 degrees C). The calcination of the catalyst is performed at a temperature of 473 Kelwin (199.85 degrees C), with preference of 573 Kelwin (299.85 degrees C). The ln203 is present in the form of particles having an average crystal size of less than 20 nm as determined by XRD, preferably less than 10 nm. The average particle size of obtained noble metal phase on catalyst is less than 4 nm, with preference less than 2 nm as determined by STEM-EDX. Preferred Compositions: The obtained calcined catalyst comprises 0.01-10.0 wt.% additional noble metal based on the total weight of the calcined catalyst. The catalyst after calcination content indium oxide in the form of indium oxide based on the calcined catalyst is ranged from 50-99.99 wt.%, preferably 93-99.5 wt.%. The molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the feed stream is 1:1, preferably 3:1. Preferred Process: The catalyst is activated in situ by raising the temperature to 553 Kelwin (279.85 degrees C) in a flow of a gas feed stream for activation, is selected from inert gases, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide or mixture, preferably gas feed stream for activation or comprises an inert gas, more preferably nitrogen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN110835459-A
TI Composite material containing polylactic acid, carbon dioxide, propylene oxide copolymer, polypropylene carbonate, antioxidant, vegetable oil polyol, polylactic acid copolymer, antibacterial agent and ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer.
AU LIU Q
AE SHENZHEN XINGU NEW MATERIAL IND CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202019115T
AB    NOVELTY - A composite material comprises 6-75 parts mass polylactic acid, 10-15 parts mass carbon dioxide, 10-15 parts mass propylene oxide copolymer, 5-10 parts mass starch, 40-50 parts mass polypropylene carbonate, 25-28 parts mass antioxidant, 25-28 parts mass bamboo fiber, vegetable oil 1-5 parts mass polyhydric alcohol, 1-7 parts mass polylactic acid copolymer, 25-28 parts mass antibacterial agent, and 5-10 parts mass ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer.
   USE - Composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material increases stability of chemical properties and compatibility of interface with other material, and has excellent anti-aging and oxidation resistance. The antibacterial agent enhances antibacterial properties and increases safety. The polypropylene carbonate enhances toughness of the material. The prepared composite material is biodegradable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the composite material, which involves filtering polylactic acid, carbon dioxide and propylene oxide copolymer, starch, polypropylene carbonate, vegetable oil polyol, polylactic acid copolymer, and ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer to obtain mixture (A), feeding mixture (A) into pulverizer to obtain pulverized material, sieving the pulverized material, screening to obtain screening material, grinding antioxidant, bamboo fiber and antibacterial agent to form mixture (B), adding mixture (B) and agitator into stirring kettle to obtain mixture(C), extruding the mixture (C) and pelletizing to obtain composite material (D), feeding the composite material into pre-prepared pulverizer, soaking with absolute ethanol, filtering, adding filtrate and acetone solution, dispersing to obtain composite material (D), cleaning the obtained composite material (D) to obtain cleaned product (E), feeding the cleaned product (E)into vacuum kettle and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The starch is one or more of corn starch, wheat starch, cassava starch, mung bean starch, sweet potato starch, sweet potato starch, potato starch, wheat starch, and water chestnut starch.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The antioxidant is one of vitamin C and gallic acid. The bamboo fiber is one or more of talc, calcium carbonate, and mica powder. The vegetable oil polyol is one or more of castor oil polyol, soybean oil polyol, peanut oil polyol, coconut oil polyol, palm oil polyol, linseed oil polyol, cottonseed oil polyol, corn oil polyol, sunflower oil, pine nut oil, and tung oil polyol. Preferred Process: The method involves pulverizing the mixture (A) at 140-175 degrees C for 1-1.5 hours at speed of 800 rpm, stirring mixture (C) at 170-185 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours at speed of 600-850 rpm, immersing the pulverized material with anhydrous ethanol having concentration of 98%, filtering, adding filtrate and acetone solution, dispersing for 11-13 hours at 35-65 degrees C and drying at 80-90 degrees C.

AE DALIAN ZHONGDING CHEM CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 2019841931
AB    NOVELTY - Multifunctional adsorbent comprises an active                component, a carrier and a binder. The active                component comprises nickel, copper, manganese or                its compound. The carrier comprises aluminum oxide,                silicon oxide and/or titanium oxide. The binder                comprises diatomaceous earth, kaolin, high alumina                cement, pseudoboehmite and/or titanate.
   USE - The adsorbent is used for removing hydrogen,                oxygen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and water                in gas, and purifying hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen,                argon, helium gas and organic gases (all claimed),                and semiconductor, optical fiber, steel and                chemical applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent adopts multi-metal compound as                active component, utilizes abundant domestic                mineral resource and reduces the production                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing the adsorbent,                involving (i) drying the above-mentioned raw                materials and pulverizing to 350 mesh or less, (ii)                mixing the pulverized raw materials, and stirring                well to uniformly mix the components, (iii)                ball-forming the obtained mixture into form a                spherical shape, a tableting type, or a strip                forming strip, (iv) curing the obtained product,                washing and drying for 6-24 hours and (v) calcining                the obtained product at 350-500 degrees C for 4-8                hours and cooling; and
   (2) the use of adsorbent for removing                hydrogen, oxygen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide                and water in the gas at a time, where removal depth                is less than 1 parts per billion and purifying                hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen, argon, helium gas and                organic gases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred composition: The adsorbent                  comprises 25-75 %mass active component, 15-55                  %mass carrier and 5-25 %mass binder, where the                  mass ratio of nickel and manganese is 0.5-12, the                  mass ratio of copper and manganese is 0.01-1, and                  the mass ratio of nickel and copper is 1-41.                  Preferred Components: The nickel-containing                  compound is chosen from nickel carbonate, basic                  nickel carbonate, nickel hydroxide and/or nickel                  oxide. The manganese-containing compound is                  chosen from manganese carbonate, manganese                  hydroxide and/or manganese dioxide. The                  copper-containing compound is chosen from copper                  carbonate, basic copper carbonate, copper                  hydroxide and/or copper oxide.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: Alternately, synthesis                  gas production system comprises an electrolysis                  device including an anode chamber comprising an                  anode, and a cathode chamber comprising a                  cathode, which are divided by a diaphragm, a                  cathode-side circulation line for circulating a                  cathode solution containing carbon dioxide,                  connected to cathode chamber, a catalyst supply                  device for supplying carbon monoxide production                  catalyst to cathode solution, provided in                  circulation line, a gas composition detection                  device for measuring ratio of hydrogen to carbon                  monoxide in the produced gas which is produced in                  cathode chamber, a bypass line provided in                  parallel with the cathode-side circulation line,                  in which the upstream end and the downstream end                  are connected to the cathode-side circulation                  line, a flow control valve (24) for controlling                  the flow rate of the cathode solution flowing in                  the bypass line, and a solid-liquid separation                  device (37) provided in the bypass line. The                  system further comprises a carbon dioxide                  absorption tank (30) for contacting gaseous                  carbon dioxide with the cathode solution, and                  causing the cathode solution to absorb carbon                  dioxide, provided in the cathode-side circulation                  line, a gas-liquid separation device (25) for                  separating the product gas and the cathode                  solution, provided in the cathode-side                  circulation line, and an anode-side circulation                  line for circulating an anode solution, connected                  to the anode chamber.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carbon monoxide                  production catalyst in the form of a powder, an                  aqueous solution or a suspension is supplied to                  the cathode solution. The target range is set,                  such that the molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon                  monoxide is 0.25-2.5. The carbon monoxide                  production catalyst comprises zinc, zinc oxide,                  zinc peroxide, zinc sulfate, zinc nitrate, zinc                  chloride, zinc bromide, zinc iodide, zinc                  acetate, silver, silver(I) oxide, silver(II)                  oxide, silver(III) oxide, silver nitrate, silver                  chloride, silver bromide, silver acetate, gold,                  gold(I) chloride, gold(III) chloride,                  tetrachloroaurate, gold(III) bromide,                  tetrabromoaurate, gold acetate and/or gold(III)                  acetate. The carbonization tower is provided with a mixing slurry pipe, an air outlet pipe, a temperature exchanger and stirring slurry from top to bottom. The slurry outlet pipe is connected to the circulation pipe. The circulation pipe is connected to a mixer (2) through a circulation pump. A mixer is connected to the mixing slurry pipe. The carbon dioxide generator is connected to the mixer and the outlet pipe through a pipe. The bottom of the air outlet pipe is provided with a horn type air outlet. A horn-shaped slurry discharging pipe is arranged on a side wall of the mixed slurry pipe. An agitating paddle is connected to the agitating motor through a stirring shaft.
   USE - Intermittent bubbling carbonization apparatus for light calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus realizes the effective uniform distribution of carbon dioxide, so that the contact area of carbon dioxide and calcium hydroxide is increased, the speed of the carbonization reaction is accelerated, the carbonization is more uniform, and the obtained calcium carbonate has a uniform particle size.

PN CN108624394-A
TI Processing and utilization of Cannabis shelled seed involves vacuum drying, cooling, pulverizing and sieving shelled hemp seed material, and then charging treated hemp seed powder into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment.
AU LU X
AE ZHENAN JINHENGXIANG CEREAL & OIL IND (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201880644J
AB    NOVELTY - Processing and utilization of Cannabis shelled seed involves vacuum drying, cooling, pulverizing and sieving shelled hemp seed material. The hemp seed powder treated is charged into the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment, and then subjected to pressure-extraction extraction to obtain hemp oil; the extraction conditions are: carbon dioxide flow rate 15-25l /h, extraction temperature 25-45 degrees C, extraction pressure is 15-25mpa, then the extraction pressure is increased to 25-35mpa to continue extraction for 1.0-2.0 hours. During the total extraction time, the separation pressure of the first 0.5-1.0 h is 10-15 mpa, and then the separation pressure is reduced to 8-10 mpa. The extract is discharged from the separation tank every 10-20 minutes during the extraction, and the collection is carried out according to the sensory quality of the extracts at different stages.
   USE - Method for processing and utilization of Cannabis shelled seed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to process and utilize Cannabis shelled seed in a cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - After the extraction operation is completed, the separation valve of the separation kettle is opened, the outlet valve of the extraction kettle is closed, the extraction kettle is maintained in a high pressure state for a period of time, and then the outlet valve of the extraction kettle is quickly opened completely, so that the residue of the degreased hemp seed is quickly discharged. After puffing, further puffing treatment is obtained to obtain fluffy brown to grayish white skim hemp seed powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The drying condition of the shelled hemp seed is vacuum drying at 60-80 degrees C. The moisture content is 5-8%, and the mesh size of the crushed sieve is 80-120 mesh. Preferred Process: The first 10-20 minutes and the last 10-20 minutes are the two stages. The extracts are collected separately, and the two extracts are subjected to full refining treatment to produce high-quality sesame oil; while the extracts collected in the remaining middle section during the extraction are collected and collected, and subjected to simple refining treatment or no refining treatment to obtain high quality Fructus Cannabis oil.

PN CN108043463-A; CN108043463-B
TI High performance catalyst preparation involves dissolving rigid monomers with amino groups, rigid monomers with aldehyde groups and emulsion stabilizers in polar organic solvents with stirring for forming high internal phase emulsion.
AU MENG J
   YIN J
   ZHANG T
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 2018410504
AB    NOVELTY - High performance catalyst preparation involves dissolving rigid monomers with amino groups, rigid monomers with aldehyde groups and emulsion stabilizers according to mass ratio of 1:0.4-1.6:1.5-4.5:5-13 in polar organic solvents with stirring for forming high internal phase emulsion. A non-polar organic solvent is added dropwise, where the polar organic solvent and the non-polar organic solvent are provided in a volume ratio of 1:3-5.
   USE - Method for preparing high performance catalyst for carbon dioxide resource (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the high performance catalyst in a simple and cost-effective manner by using cheap and easily available raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High performance catalyst preparation involves dissolving rigid monomers with amino groups, rigid monomers with aldehyde groups and emulsion stabilizers according to mass ratio of 1:0.4-1.6:1.5-4.5:5-13 in polar organic solvents with stirring for forming high internal phase emulsion. A non-polar organic solvent is added dropwise, where the polar organic solvent and the non-polar organic solvent are provided in a volume ratio of 1:3-5. The reaction mixture is heated at 140-170 degrees C for 72-96 hours, where acetone, tetrahydrofuran, dichloroethane and water are successively washed and filtered to obtain a solid. The obtained solid is soaked in an aqueous solution of metal ions for 18-24 hours, and then filtered and dried to obtain the high-performance catalyst for carbon dioxide resource.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic topography photograph of a high-performance catalyst for recycling carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal ion is zinc, cobalt, copper or iron.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amino group-containing rigid monomer is melamine, p-phenylenediamine, trimesotriamine or biphenyldiamine. The rigid monomer having an aldehyde group is paraformaldehyde, terephthalaldehyde, trimesane or biphenyldicarboxaldehyde. The emulsion stabilizer is F127 type, P123 type or F68 type stabilizer. The polar organic solvent is dimethylformamide, dimethylacetamide or dimethyl sulfoxide. The non-polar organic solvent is petroleum ether, Isopar (RTM: a biochemical for proteomics research) G or Isopar (RTM: petroleum-based raw materials) M.

PT P
PN CN107841760-A; CN107841760-B
TI Producing gas diffusion electrode for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to hydrocarbons, involves treating porous carbon material in air to obtain gas diffusion substrate, and deposition of copper catalyst layer.
AU QIU Y
   ZHANG H
   ZHONG H
   LI X
   ZHANG T
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201825227L
AB    NOVELTY - Producing gas diffusion electrode involves treating porous carbon material in air to obtain a gas diffusion substrate. The deposition of copper catalyst layer under the protection of an inert atmosphere, an acidic copper salt solution containing an additive is used as a plating solution to control temperature of the plating solution. The copper particles are attached to the particles in a pulsed electro-deposition manner at gas diffusion substrate surface to get gas diffusion electrode. The gas diffusion electrode prepared is soaked in a concentrated hydrochloric acid solution.
   USE - Method for producing gas diffusion electrode for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce gas diffusion electrode, which has high selectivity, and fine and uniform catalytic layer particle, greatly expand electrochemical reaction area, and suitable for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing gas diffusion electrode involves treating porous carbon material in air to obtain a gas diffusion substrate. The deposition of copper catalyst layer under the protection of an inert atmosphere, an acidic copper salt solution containing an additive is used as a plating solution to control temperature of the plating solution. The copper particles are attached to the particles in a pulsed electro-deposition manner at gas diffusion substrate surface to get gas diffusion electrode. The gas diffusion electrode prepared is soaked in a concentrated hydrochloric acid solution having a concentration of 36-38%, cleaned and dried with inert gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous carbon material is selected from one or more carbonized, non-woven carbon paper, carbon cloth and carbon felt. The acidic copper salt solution contains cupric sulfate, copper dichloride, and cupric nitrate, and the concentration of the copper ion is 0.05M-1.5M. The acidic copper salt solution contains an additive, and additive is selected from one or more inorganic salts sodium chloride, potassium(I) bromide, and potassium iodide containing a halogen element, or cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, polyvinyl alcohol, one or more thiourea or glycine. Preferred Conditions: The heat treatment temperature is carried out at 300-800 degrees C for 2-6 hours. The porous carbon material has a porosity of 70-90%. The static water contact angle of the gas diffusion substrate ranges from 60-130 degrees. The pH of the copper salt solution is adjusted to 0.1-1.0 by using sulfuric acid. The concentration of the additive is 0.1-3.0 mM. The gas used in the inert atmosphere is selected from one or two of nitrogen or argon, the gas flow rate is 20-100 ml min-1, and the optimal gas flow rate is 40-70 ml min-1. The bath control temperature is room temperature at 80 degrees C, the optimal temperature at 30-60 degrees C, and stirring speed of 100-1000 revolution per minute, and the optimum stirring speed of 300-600 revolution per minute. The pulse electrodeposition, the deposition is pulsed current deposition or pulse potential deposition. The pulsed current deposition density of 10-200mAcm-2, the best deposition current is 60-120mAcm-2, and oxidation etching current density of 1-50mAcm-2, and best etching current is 5-30mAcm-2. The pulse period is 1000-10000, the best pulse period is 3000-6000, and duty cycle is 20-90%, and the optimal duty cycle is 40-75%. The pulse potential deposition is -0.5-0V (relative to the standard hydrogen electrode (SHE)), the best deposition potential is -0.3V to -0.1V. The oxidation potential is 0.35-0.6V (relative to the standard hydrogen electrode (SHE)), the best oxidation potential is 0.4-0.5V. The pulse period is 1000-10000, the best pulse period is 3000-6000, and duty cycle is 20-90%, and the optimal duty cycle is 40-75%. The pulse electrodeposition and the auxiliary electrode in the deposition process use one of a copper slice or a copper slice, and its purity is not lower than 99.9%. The gas diffusion electrode sample is soaked in a concentrated hydrochloric acid solution, the soaking temperature is normal temperature for 10-30 minutes, and the optimal soaking for 15-25 minutes.

PN WO2022073055-A1; AT524247-A1
TI Producing methanol from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, comprises reacting carbon dioxide hydrogen in presence of manganese-promoted molybdenum(IV) sulfide catalyst.
AU PACHOLIK G
   FOETTINGER K
AE UNIV WIEN TECH (UYWI-Non-standard)
GA 202251690H
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methanol from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, comprises reacting carbon dioxide hydrogen in presence of manganese-promoted molybdenum(IV) sulfide catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for producing methanol from carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is carried out in a selective and economical manner; has a wide temperature range and achieves increased production of methanol even at lower temperatures; and assures reduced formation of undesirable products e.g. carbon monoxide and methane. The catalyst: is sulfur tolerant and has high specificity; and catalyzes carbon dioxide hydrogenation in a highly selective manner compared to known sulfur-resistant catalysts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) catalyst comprising manganese-promoted molybdenum(IV) sulfide having a composition manganese-molybdenum di-sulfur (0.1-0.5); and
   (2) preparing the manganese-promoted molybdenum(IV) sulfide catalyst, comprising: (a) mixing water, ammonium molybdate, thiourea and a water soluble manganese(II) salt in a desired molar ratio, (b) autoclaving the obtained mixture in an autoclave at 150-250 degrees C, and increasing the pressure to a level that part of the water remains as a liquid, and maintaining temperature and pressure until thiourea decomposes, (c) washing the obtained mixture, (d) drying the washed mixture, and (e) calcining the washed and dried mixture by inert gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reaction takes place at a pressure of greater than or equal to 10 bar. The partial pressure ratio of carbon dioxide to hydrogen is 1-3.5, preferably 1-3. The manganese-promoted molybdenum(IV) sulfide catalyst has a composition containing manganese-molybdenum di-sulfur (0.1-0.5), preferably (0.2-0.4). The source of carbon dioxide is flue gas. The catalytically active part of the catalyst contains manganese-molybdenum di-sulfur(0.1-0.5), preferably manganese-molybdenum di-sulfur (0.2-0.4). The catalyst has a support containing aluminum oxyhydroxide and/or aluminum oxide. Preferred Method: An inert gas is additionally produced while carbon dioxide is reacted with hydrogen in presence of the manganese-promoted molybdenum(IV) sulfide. The reaction takes place at 160-240 degrees C. The mixture is admixed with a carrier, prior to the calcination step (e), The carrier is precipitated from a precursor compound during step (b).
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI New tertiary amine groups-based carbon dioxide responsive tackifying polymer useful in plugging high-permeability layers in reservoir production, and as plugging control agent.
AU FENG Y
   HUANG X
   SU X
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 202207713Y
AB    NOVELTY - Tertiary amine groups-based carbon dioxide responsive tackifying polymer (A) are new.
   USE - (A) is useful in plugging high-permeability layers in reservoir production, and as plugging control agent (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (A): has better response tackifying effect at lower use concentration. The method: utilizes acrylamide, which is hydrophilic monomer to ensure that the polymer has excellent water solubility, long-chain alkyl polyoxyethylene ether methacrylate, which is hydrophobic monomer, and dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate, which is tertiary amine monomer that provides carbon dioxide responsiveness; and protonates tertiary amine group into quaternary ammonium salt cation after carbon dioxide is introduced, the electrostatic repulsion makes molecular chain stretch, the cross-linked network generated by the association of hydrophobic groups is stretched, and the viscosity of the system is greatly increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Tertiary amine groups-based carbon dioxide responsive tackifying polymer (A) of formulae (I-IV) are new.
   R = 8-24C alkyl;
   x:y = 1:1.5-9; and
   z = 0.3-0.9% of (x+y).
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   preparation of (A);
   tertiary amine groups-based carbon dioxide responsive tackifying and blocking agent, comprising (A); and
   use method, comprising preparing (A) into aqueous solution with mass concentration of 0.4-2% as plugging control agent.
   (1)
   (2)
   (3)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (A), comprises mixing three monomers of acrylamide, tertiary amine monomer and long-chain alkyl polyoxyethylene ether methacrylate with deionized water under the condition of co-solvent, stirring until dissolve completely, then adding crushed ice cubes into the solution, stirring until dissolve, then pouring into thermos when there is little float left in the ice cubes, then adding alcohol chain transfer agent and azo initiator solution, passing nitrogen to remove oxygen, then adding initiator solution and redox initiator solution, passing nitrogen again to remove oxygen, and sealing the reaction for 10-24 hours to obtain polymer system with higher viscosity, where the molar ratio between acrylamide and tertiary amine monomer is 1:1.5-9; and the amount of long-chain alkyl polyoxyethylene ether methacrylate is 0.3-0.9% the total amount of acrylamide and tertiary amine monomers.Preferred Components: In the hydrophobic monomer long-chain alkyl polyoxyethylene ether methacrylate, the range of long-chain alkyl group R is alkyl group having 8-24C, and the polyoxyethylene ether polymerization degree is 15-30. The co-solvent is urea. The alcohol chain transfer agent is isopropanol. The azo initiator is VA-044 solution, and the solution concentration is 0.5 wt.%. The initiator is tetramethylethylenediamine; and the redox initiator includes sodium bisulfite solution and potassium persulfate solution.

TI Producing antibody protein by inoculating and culturing cell, controlling pH, adding hydrochloric acid, maintaining pH until harvesting cell, adding carbon dioxide gas, harvesting cells and collecting antibody protein product carbon dioxide.
AU SUN R
   CHENG L
   CHEN X
AE SHANGHAI YAOMING BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E08505
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing antibody protein involves inoculating and culturing a cell at 36-37degreesC, preferably 36.5degreesC, controlling the initial pH at 7plus minus0.20 as first pH, reducing the culture temperature to 30-32degreesC, preferably 31degreesC, after the cell growth to the exponential period, adding hydrochloric acid at cooling day to reduce the pH to 6.7-6.90 as second pH, where the first pH is more than the second pH, maintaining the pH until harvesting the cell, adding carbon dioxide gas to reach the first carbon dioxide at 50-80 mmHg after the pH is stable, maintaining the pH and increasing the carbon dioxide gas to the second partial pressure at 80-110 mmHg after the cell growth reaches the plateau period, where the second partial pressure is more than the first partial pressure, harvesting the cells and collecting the antibody protein product carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing antibody protein and controlling glycosylation level in antibody protein (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for controlling glycosylation level in production of antibody protein, which involves performing the above-mentioned steps.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The days from the cooling day to the cell growth plateau phase are 3-7 days, preferably 5 days, after the cooling day. The carbon dioxide gas partial pressure is increased after the stage period, and the carbon dioxide is continued for 2-4 period of day and the cell carbon dioxide is harvested. The culturing is performed in Actipro (RTM: Powdered cell culture medium). The method further involves using Actipro (RTM: Powdered cell culture medium) to culture before inoculating. The method further involves supplementing CB7a/b culture medium during the culturing process. The change in the level of glycosylation is chosen from reduction of high polymannosyl forms, reduction of fucose levels, defucosylation and reduction of galactosyl levels. The high mannose type is pentamannose. The cells are harvested and the antibody protein is extracted at 14 days.Preferred Composition: The concentration of added hydrochloric acid is 1 N.Preferred Component: The antibody protein is chosen from immunoglobulin (Ig)G1, IgG2, IgG3, IgG4 and fusion protein with IgG fragment crystallizable (Fc) end structure, preferably IgG1 or fusion protein with IgG1 Fc end structure.

TI Forming amine-functionalized solid carbon dioxide            sorbent by providing support material, and applying            amine layer onto portion of surface of support material            by performing cycle of molecular layer deposition with            precursors.
AU WEIMER A W
   LAI A
   PFEFFER R
   LIN J
AE UNIV COLORADO (COLS-C)
   ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
   UNIV COLORADO (COLS-C)
   ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
GA 2021539342
AB    NOVELTY - Method of forming an amine-functionalized                solid carbon dioxide sorbent involves providing a                support material, and applying an amine layer onto                at least a portion of a surface of the support                material by performing at least one cycle of                molecular layer deposition (MLD) with a first                precursor and a second precursor, where the first                precursor comprises an amine precursor.
   USE - The method is useful for forming                amine-functionalized solid carbon dioxide sorbent                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides for improved process for                creating reliable and efficient carbon capture                materials, without increasing the cost or                complexity of manufacture and use. The carbon                dioxide sorbent has the ability to regenerate amine                active sites through cycles of adsorption and                desorption without any noticeable depletion in                amine groups. The carbon dioxide sorbent has an                increased durability and useful life relative to                conventional solid sorbents, allowing the advantage                of low energy regeneration in industrial use                without the increased costs associated with                replacing depleted conventional sorbents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                amine-functionalized solid carbon dioxide sorbent                comprising a support material, an amine layer,                where the amine layer forms chemical bonds to the                support material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The surface of the                  support material is purged with nitrogen gas                  between the first precursor and the second                  precursor. The step of at least 5 cycles,                  preferably at least 10 cycles of MLD are                  performed. Preferred Components: The second                  precursor comprises water. The support material                  comprises silica-based support material, and the                  silica-based support material comprises fumed                  silica, silica fume, porous silica, mesoporous                  silica, silica gel, silica aerogel, silica                  nanotubes, and/or silica foam. The support                  material comprises alumina, titanium, zirconia,                  metal-organic frameworks, polymers, carbon                  nanotubes/fibers, zeolites, carbon black, carbon                  particles and/or activated carbon. The support                  material comprises a nano-powder. The support                  material is a porous material, and a total                  surface area of the support material is coated                  with the amine layer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first precursor                  comprises aminopropylsilane, preferably                  aminopropyltriethoxysilane (mono-amine or APTS),                  N1-(3-trimethoxysilylpropyl) diethylenetriamine                  (tri-amine), and/or                  N-(3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl)ethylenediamine                  (di-amine). Preferred Properties: The amine layer                  has a thickness corresponding to at least one                  cycle of MLD with a first precursor and a second                  precursor. The thickness of the amine layer                  corresponds to at least five cycles of MLD.

PN DE102019119543-A1; WO2021009312-A1; IN202217002584-A; CN114401939-A; EP3999485-A1
TI Method for producing a target compound comprises providing starting gas mixture having olefin, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, subjecting olefin to hydroformylation, separating carbon dioxide and subjecting to dry reforming with methane.
AU MEISWINKEL A
   ZANDER H
   HAIDEGGER E
   KIENDL I
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 202108315K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a target compound comprises providing a starting gas mixture having at least one of olefin, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, subjecting the olefin to hydroformylation (2) in at least a part of the starting gas mixture to obtain an aldehyde, where the olefin has a carbon chain with first carbon number and the aldehyde has a carbon chain with a second carbon number, which is one greater than the first carbon number, partially separating the carbon dioxide contained in the starting mixture upstream and/or downstream of the hydroformylation, partially subjecting the separated carbon dioxide to dry reforming (3) with methane to obtain carbon monoxide, where at least a part of the carbon monoxide obtained in the dry reforming is used in the hydroformylation.
   USE - The method is useful for producing the target compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces or prevents carbon dioxide emission and recycles large quantities of methane which are available as potential feedstock.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system for production of the target compound.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for producing the target compound.
   Hydroformylation (2)
   Dry reforming (3)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The starting gas mixture undergoes oxidative coupling of a paraffin, preferably methane. The aldehyde from the hydroformulation is the target compound, or the aldehyde is further converted to the target compound. The aldehyde is hydrogenated to an alcohol which has a carbon chain with the second carbon number. The alcohol is dehydrated to an olefin having a carbon chain with the second carbon number. The first carbon number is two and the second carbon number is three. The methane that is subjected to the oxidative coupling is at least partially separated from natural gas and in which at least one paraffin is transferred from the natural gas to a post-catalytic vapor cracking step following the oxidative coupling. The carbon monoxide obtained in the dry reforming is obtained in a product mixture which further includes at least hydrogen. The product mixture from the dry reforming is subjected to a water gas shift. The product mixture from the dry reforming and/or the product mixture from the water gas shift are at least partially fed unseparated to the hydroformylation. The carbon monoxide and the olefin from the starting gas mixture are at least partially subjected to the hydroformylation without prior separation from one another. The starting mixture is compressed to a pressure level at which the carbon dioxide is separated off and the hydroformylation is carried out, and in which the dry reforming is carried out at a lower pressure level. The method is carried out completely non-cryogenically downstream of the provision of the starting gas mixture and the dry reforming. Preferred Components: The starting gas mixture includes a paraffin, at least part of the paraffin passing through the hydroformylation unconverted, being separated off downstream of the hydroformylation, and being used again in providing the starting gas mixture.

PT P
PN CN111455012-A; CN111455012-B
TI Preparing polyhydroxy fatty acid ester, by inoculating microbial strains on culture medium for aerobic fermentation, adding activated strain, and introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide to perform fermentation.
AU HU C
   SHAO M
   XU X
   ZHU Q
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI NANJING GREEN MFG IND (CHSC-Non-standard)
   INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
   ZHONGKE NANJING GREEN MFG IND INNOVATION (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 202075200V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing polyhydroxy fatty acid ester from carbon dioxide, involves inoculating microbial strains on the culture medium for aerobic fermentation to obtain activated strains, adding activated strain to the fermentation broth, and introducing mixed gas containing carbon dioxide to perform pressurized fermentation to obtain polyhydroxy fatty acid ester.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyhydroxy fatty acid ester from carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for industrial mass production, is cost-effective and environmentally-friendly, and provides no reaction residue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microbial species comprises Alcaligenes eutrophus, Pseudomonas aeruginosa or Chromobacterium violaceum. The microbial strains are inoculated on the culture medium according to the coverage rate of 1-10%. The composition of the culture medium includes organic carbon sources, inorganic salts and yeast extract. The conditions for aerobic fermentation on the medium are: temperature of 30-45 degrees C, rotation speed of 100-400 revolutions per minute (rpm), time is 24-48 hours, and pH of 7-9. The gas introduced during the aerobic fermentation is a mixed gas of oxygen and nitrogen. The volume fraction of mixed gas of oxygen is 2-5%. The fermentation broth does not contain an organic carbon source. The composition of the fermentation broth includes inorganic salts. The activated strain is added, the volume concentration of the activated strain in the fermentation broth is 1-5%. The mixed gas comprises carbon dioxide, hydrogen and oxygen. The volume composition of the mixed gas includes 10-80% carbon dioxide, 5-20% hydrogen, and 1-5% oxygen, and nitrogen (remainder). The pressure during fermentation is 0.1-5 MPa, preferably 2-5 MPa. During the fermentation process, the pH of the fermentation broth is maintained at 6-9, preferably 7-8. The fermentation temperature is 20-35 degrees C. The fermentation time is 1-24 hours. The method involves extracting and separating the strains to obtain polyhydroxy fatty acid esters, crushing, and performing crushing and dissolution, centrifugal separation, product and drying process. The method involves inoculating microbial strains on the culture medium at a coverage rate of 1-10% for aerobic fermentation, introducing composition of the medium comprising organic carbon source, inorganic salt and yeast extract, and introducing mixture of oxygen and nitrogen during aerobic fermentation.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN CN111019043-A; CN111019043-B
TI New carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch-type temperature-sensitive polymer containing diacetone acrylamide and N-acetylformamide.
AU WANG Y
   REN J
   ZHAO J
   XIA H
AE UNIV GUIZHOU EDUCATION (UYGU-Non-standard)
GA 202033944L
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch-type temperature-sensitive polymer containing diacetone acrylamide and N-acetylformamide (I) is new. (I) prepared by a two step synthesis reaction.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch-type temperature-sensitive polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes low cost, easily available raw materials, has simple process, and is environmentally friendly, avoids the generation of by-products and system pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide/nitrogen switch-type temperature-sensitive polymer containing diacetone acrylamide and N-acetylformamide of formula (I) is new. (I) prepared by a two step synthesis reaction.
   m = 100-1000; and
   n = 100-1000.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) adding diacetone acrylamide, N-acetylformamide, and distilled water in a three-necked round-bottom flask equipped with a thermometer, electric stirrer, and nitrogen tube, stirring dissolving passing nitrogen for 30 minutes, adding initiator, heating the water bath at 20-50 degrees C, reacting for 6-12 hours under the protection of nitrogen, dropping the reaction droplet into acetone to precipitate white precipitate, suction filtering, vacuum drying the filter cake to obtain copolymer of diacetone acrylamide and N-acetylformamide, (ii) dissolving the obtained copolymer and distilled water in a 250 ml three-necked flask, stirring, heating at 75 degrees C under the protection of nitrogen, slowly adding sodium hydroxide solution dropwise within 1 hour, reacting for 36 hours, stop heating, adjusting the pH to 7 with dilute hydrochloric acid, concentrating by rotary evaporation, dropping the concentrated drop into acetone to precipitate white precipitate, suction filtering, vacuum drying the filter cake to obtain final product, and determining the molecular weight by gel permeation chromatography. Preferred Components: In step (i), the initiator is water-soluble redox initiator, preferably potassium persulfate, ammonium persulfate and/or hydrogen peroxide, and the reducing agent is sulfite, bisulfite, ascorbic acid and/or pyrosulfite. The molar ratio of the oxidant component and the reducing agent component is greater than 1. The molar ratio between diacetone acrylamide and N-acetylformamide in step (i) is 5/1-1/1, preferably 3/1-1/1. The molar ratio between diacetone acrylamide and N-acetylformamide monomers, and initiator in step (i) is 500/1-10/1, preferably 100/1-10/1. The mass ratio between diacetone acrylamide and N-acetylformamide, and water in step (i) is 1/10-1/2, preferably 3/20-3/10. The mass ratio between copolymer and distilled water in step (ii) is 8/25-1/25.

PT P
PN CN108314750-A; CN108314750-B
TI Synthesizing diacetone acrylamide and acrylamide copolymer comprises e.g. taking carbon dioxide as medium, persulfate-bisulfite redox system as initiator and polymerizing with diacetone acrylamide and acrylamide comonomer precipitate.
AU LAN P
   LV J
AE UNIV JIAXING (JIXA-C)
   UNIV JIAXING (JIXA-C)
GA 201859637H
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing diacetone acrylamide and acrylamide copolymer comprises taking carbon dioxide as medium, persulfate and bisulfite redox system as initiator, maintaining critical temperature and pressure of carbon dioxide, and then polymerizing with diacetone acrylamide and acrylamide comonomer precipitate to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing diacetone acrylamide and acrylamide copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The copolymer: has no toxicity; uses non polluting medium supercritical carbon dioxide instead of organic solvent; avoids environmental pollution caused by use of toxic, harmful, volatile and flammable organic solvents; is environmentally friendly; and saves energy conservation and resource conservation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The persulfate in redox system is potassium persulfate, sodium persulfate or ammonium persulfate, and the bisulfite in redox system is potassium bisulfite, sodium bisulfite or ammonium bisulfite. The mol of the diacetone acrylamide in the two monomers is 0-70 wt.%, and the mol of acrylamide in two monomers is 30-100 wt.%. The total monomer concentration of diacetone acrylamide and acrylamide is 2-6 mol/l. The initial ratio of persulfate to bisulfite in the initiator is 1:1, and the initial amount of the initiator is 0.08-0.3 mol/l. Preferred Method: The method comprises adding diacetone acrylamide, acrylamide monomer and initiator into a reaction kettle, sealing, where the initial concentration of the initiator is 0.05 0.5 mol/l, replacing carbon dioxide by air in the reaction kettle, where the amount of acrylamide in total monomer concentration of the diacetone acrylamide is 1-8mol/l, then closing the inlet valve of the reaction kettle, heating and continuously increasing pressure in the reaction kettle, when the reaction temperature reaches to 32-40 degrees C and pressure in the reaction kettle is (i.e. the pressure of the carbon dioxide in the kettle) to 8 MPa, again increasing pressure to 45 MPa, then stirring and reacting for 3-20 hours until the reaction is finished. The controlling the pressure in the reaction kettle is 20-30 MPa. The reaction temperature is 33-38 degrees C. The reaction time is 4-10 hours. The method comprises after the reaction is finished, cooling the reaction kettle by water to room temperature, opening the gas outlet valve of the reaction kettle, reducing the pressure to normal pressure, taking out the product i.e. water-free diacetone acrylamide and acrylamide copolymer.

A                flow adjusting valve (17) is adjusted on the gas                fertilizer branch pipe to make the plant greenhouse                to reach proper carbon dioxide concentration.
   USE - Method for providing gas fertilizer to plant                greenhouse by using carbon dioxide discharged by                coal-fired unit.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the control of the                nitrogen dioxide emission of the coal-fired unit,                and realizes the carbon dioxide as the gas                fertilizer recycling, improves the plant growth,                reaches the double-harvest of economic benefit and                carbon emission-reducing social benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for providing gas fertilizer to plant greenhouse by                using carbon dioxide discharged by coal-fired                unit.

PN KR2021053573-A; WO2021091182-A1; KR2294427-B1
TI Manufacturing formic acid using synthesis gas            involves supplying syngas to water gas conversion unit            to convert carbon monoxide contained in syngas into            hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and supplying a mixed gas            to formate generating unit.
AU JUNG K
   KIM H G
   PARK J M
   HOON K S
   YUNSUNGHO
   HO P K
   JUNG K D
   KIM S H
   YOON S H
   PARK K H
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 2021504910
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing formic acid using synthesis gas                involves supplying the syngas to a water gas                conversion unit to convert carbon monoxide                contained in the syngas into hydrogen and carbon                dioxide, supplying a mixed gas containing hydrogen                and carbon dioxide converted by the water gas                conversion unit to a formate generating unit                equipped with a catalyst to generate formate, and                supplying the formic acid salt to a formic acid                separation unit to separate formic acid. The                synthesis gas is is derived from the group                consisting of hydrocarbons having 1 to 5 carbon                atoms, coal, biomass, biogas, and steelworks                by-product gas.
   USE - Method for manufacturing formic acid using                synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost effective as it is not                necessary to use purified carbon dioxide and                hydrogen as a reaction raw material for the                hydrogenation of carbon dioxide since synthesis gas                is used as a reaction raw material. The product                does not contain a catalyst component so a separate                catalyst recovery system is not required, and                catalyst particles are prevented from getting                crushed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for forming formic acid using synthesis                gas, which comprises a water gas conversion unit                for converting carbon monoxide contained in the                synthesis gas into hydrogen and carbon dioxide by a                water gas reaction of the synthesis gas, a formate                generating unit for generating formate by reacting                the mixed gas containing hydrogen and carbon                dioxide with an amine-based compound in the                presence of a catalyst, and a formic acid                separation unit for separating formic acid from the                formic acid salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active metal is                  selected from iridium (Ir), ruthenium (Ru),                  palladium (Pd), gold (Au), iron (Fe) and cobalt                  (Co). The porous carrier is a porous organic                  framework, a covalent organic framework, covalent                  triazine frameworks, porous aromatic frameworks                  and porous organic polymers. The porous carrier                  is selected from titania, alumina, gallia,                  zirconia, and silica in which nitrogen or                  phosphorus is contained in the skeletal                  structure.                                                                                                                              
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for                  manufacturing formic acid using synthesis gas                  involves supplying the combustion exhaust gas and                  the amine compound to the carbon dioxide capture                  unit to produce a carbonic acid-amine adduct. The                  carbonic acid-amine addition compound is supplied                  to the formic acid generating unit for producing                  formic acid. The carbonic acid-amine adduct is                  supplied to the formate generating unit when the                  molar ratio (b/a) of carbon dioxide (a) and                  hydrogen (b) contained in the mixed gas is lower                  than 2.0. The separate carbon dioxide is                  additionally supplied to the formate generating                  unit together with the mixed gas. The active                  metal is supported on a porous carrier containing                  at least one of nitrogen and phosphorus.                  Preferred Components: The amine-based compound is                  a compound of formula (I).R to R =same or different from each other,                  each selected from hydrogen, 1-6C alkyl group and                  3-10C cycloalkyl group or bonded to each other to                  form an aliphatic ring or an aromatic ring, where                  alkyl group and the cycloalkyl group of R to R                  are substituted or unsubstituted with one or more                  substituents selected from 1-10C alkyl group and                  6-10C aryl group.The amine-based compound is selected from                  trimethylamine, triethylamine, tripentylamine,                  tripropylamine, tributylamine, trihexylamine,                  N,N-dimethylbutylamine, dimethylcyclohexylamine,                  dimethylphenethylamine or isoamylamine. The amine                  compound is selected from dialkyl piperazine                  (N,N-dialkyl piperazine), morpholine, alkyl                  morpholine, alkyl piperidine and                  alkylpyrrolidine. The porous carrier includes a                  triazine structure or a heptazine structure. The                  porous carrier is selected from the group                  consisting of pyridine bonded nitrogen                  (pyridinic-N), pyrrole bonded nitrogen                  (pyrrolic-N), pyrazole bonded nitrogen                  (pyrazole-N), and graphite bonded nitrogen                  (graphitic-N).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU CAI N
   LUO Y

AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)

GA 2018166289
AB    NOVELTY - Temperature self-supporting carbon dioxide and water vapor co-electrolysis device comprises reaction kettle, reaction kettle internal fuel electrode chamber and tubular solid oxide electrolytic cell. The fuel electrode chamber and tubular solid oxide electrolytic cell are sealed and connected. The outer side of tubular solid oxide electrolytic cell is provided with an air chamber. The reaction kettle fuel electrode chamber and tubular solid oxide electrolytic cell are surrounded with annular insulating material.
   USE - Temperature self-supporting carbon dioxide and water vapor co-electrolysis device used for carbon dioxide and water vapor co-electrolysis useful for synthesizing methane, ethylene, low carbon olefin, gasoline or diesel liquid fuel (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application of temperature self-supporting carbon dioxide and water vapor co-electrolysis device for carbon dioxide and water vapor co-electrolysis comprising gradually heating gas from room temperature to electrolysis zone operation temperature, filling fuel electrode chamber and air chamber in the reaction kettle, continuously circulating gas until tubular solid oxide electrolytic cell is preheated, when air chamber pressure is stable, switching the fuel electrode chamber inlet gas with carbon dioxide (CO2)/water (H2O)/hydrogen (H2) gas, when fuel electrode chamber pressure is stable, connecting an external circuit power, controlling the temperature of tubular solid oxide electrolytic cell chemical reaction zone to optimize the fuel yield and prevent chemical reaction zone runaway, and ensuring that the air chamber and fuel electrode chamber pressure and temperature are optimized, where air chamber/fuel electrode chamber inlet flow ratio is more than 10.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: When synthesizing methane, the method comprises using nickel-based catalyst, maintaining the chemical reaction zone temperature at 350-450 degrees C, and maintaining the air chamber inlet gas temperature at 150-250 degrees C. When synthesizing ethylene, low carbon olefin, gasoline or diesel liquid fuel, the method comprises using iron-based catalyst, maintaining the chemical reaction zone at 300-350 degrees C, and maintaining the air chamber inlet gas temperature at 100-150 degrees C.

PN CN114804119-A
TI Controlling silicon dioxide particle size involves            drying silicon dioxide particle by silicon dioxide            slurry through pressure type spray drying tower, where            pressure-type spray drying tower includes nozzle and            spray piece arranged at discharge port of nozzle, and            spray piece with spray piece.
AU XU H
   LI X
   GAO H
   HU J
   LI P
AE SHANDONG LIANKE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022A1188S
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling silicon dioxide particle size                involves drying silicon dioxide particle by silicon                dioxide slurry through pressure type spray drying                tower. The pressure-type spray drying tower                includes a nozzle and a spray piece arranged at the                discharge port of the nozzle, and the spray piece                is provided with a spray piece through hole. The                particle size control of the silica particles is                mainly through the pore size of the through holes                of the spray sheet, the spray pressure of the                pressure spray drying tower, the solid content of                the silica slurry and the carbon dioxide. The                silicon paste viscosity is adjusted to                achieve.
   USE - Method for controlling silicon dioxide                particle size.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the fluidized bed                secondary drying process, realizes the final                product particle size distribution condition is                controllable, and satisfies client requirements of                different industries.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pressure of the                  high-pressure feed pump causes the silica sizing                  material to pass through the nozzle to form fine                  droplets, which are then rapidly dried by                  sufficient heat exchange with hot air to obtain                  silica particles. The silica particles dried by                  the pressure spray drying tower have a moisture                  content of 11.4-12.6%, the silica particles after                  secondary drying in the fluidized bed, the water                  content is reduced to 5.2-5.9% and then                  discharged from the outlet of the fluidized bed.                  The silica particles after the pressure spray                  drying enter the fluidized bed through the feed                  port of the fluidized bed, the filtered clean air                  is heated by the heater and sent to the bottom of                  the fluidized bed by the blower, and the sieve                  plate is contacted with the silica particles to                  form a fluidized state to achieve gas-solid heat                  and mass exchange to achieve the purpose of                  drying. The diameter of the through holes of the                  spray sheet is 2.6-3.0 mm, when the particle size                  of the silica is 20-60 meshes, the spray pressure                  of the pressure spray drying tower is 1.2-1.5                  megaPascal, the solid content of the silica                  slurry is 25-30%, the viscosity of the silica                  slurry is less than 500CPs, and the temperature                  in the fluidized bed is 32-36&#8451;.

PT P
PN CN114644966-A
TI Brewing aroma-type dry white wine comprises            selecting high-quality crystal grapes for fermentation,            stopping fermentation to obtain dry white wine when            wine reaches normal alcohol content and residual sugar            content.
AU YANG L
   WANG C
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 202285485B
AB    NOVELTY - Brewing aroma-type dry white wine comprises                (i) selecting high-quality crystal grapes for                fermentation, when the carbon dioxide weight loss                in the fermentation bottle is less than 0.2g and                stopping the fermentation; and (ii) stopping                fermentation to obtain dry white wine when the wine                reaches normal alcohol content and the residual                sugar content is below 4 g/liter.
   USE - The method is useful for brewing aroma-type                dry white wine, preferably dry white wine is low                degree wine brewed by white grape or red skin white                flesh grape juice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method brews aroma-type dry white wine                with mellow taste, full wine body, outstanding                fruit aroma, cream flavor, comfortable and                refreshing, fresh and pleasant, aftertaste thus can                meet the sensory requirement of more consumers in                the country with high quality and low price.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method of brewing                  aroma-type dry white wine preferably comprises                  (i) removing stem of crystal grape, sorting,                  putting into sterile bag and placing at                  -20degrees Celsius; (ii) selecting artificial                  cold pressing method, freezing the grape,                  squeezing and extruding juice, crushing and                  adding sulfurous acid and controlling the free                  sulfur dioxide content to less than 40 mg/L;                  (iii) adding 0.07-0.09 g/liter pectinase; (iv)                  adding 0.5-0.6 g/liter bentonite; (v) controlling                  the fermentation temperature at 22degrees Celsius                  and fermenting for 5-7 days; (vi) selecting                  bentonite as lower glue material; (vii)                  transferring the raw wine to brown reagent bottle                  and refrigerating at 4degrees Celsius; and (viii)                  observing periodically, pouring into bottle and                  refrigerating continuously. The step (iii)                  comprises adding 0.08 g/liter pectinase which is                  added the prepared pectinase to the grape mash                  while stirring, sealing it at a low temperature                  and allowing it to stand for 3 hours. The method                  of adding 0.6 g/liter bentonite comprising adding                  the prepared bentonite slurry into the grape                  juice while stirring, sealing it at a low                  temperature and allowing to stand for 3 hours.                  The temperature-controlled fermentation in the                  step (v) is closed fermentation, and three days                  before the fermentation, releasing the carbon                  monoxide in the fermentation bottle is released                  twice every day to reduce the carbon monoxide                  pressure in the bottle, recording the weight loss                  and carrying out once a day until the end of                  fermentation. The step (iv) comprises selecting                  bentonite as the sizing material, where the                  amount of the sizing material is determined to be                  0.44-0.6 g/L through the sizing test, adding the                  sizing material, allowing to stand at 4degrees                  Celsius for 7 days and centrifuging at 4000                  revolutions/minute for 5 minutes to obtain clear                  wine. The step (vii) comprises refrigerating the                  wine at 4degreesC for a total of 5 months during                  which the bottle is poured twice.

The filtrate is prepared by adding water that is 5-8 times weight of remaining raw materials to remaining raw materials except patchouli, soaking for 30-55 minutes, decocting at 95-115degrees Celsius for 15-30 minutes, and decocting for 15-30 minutes, decocting at 75-85degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours and filtering to obtain the filtrate. The method involves after crushing patchouli, adding water, using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology for extracting patchouli extract, adding water to remaining raw materials except patchouli, soaking, decoction, and finally filtering to obtain filtrate, concentrating obtained filtrate under reduced pressure to extract with relative density of 1.25-1.45, after drying obtained extract, adding patchouli extract, uniformly stirring, coating on carrier, and after curing for 20-30 hours, cutting according to specifications to obtain traditional Chinese medicinal composition in form of patch. The patchouli extract is prepared by crushing patchouli to 50-80 mesh, adding water 10-15 times weight of patchouli, soaking for 1-3 hours, under condition that carbon dioxide flow rate is 1.2-1.4 l/hour and extraction temperature at 19-23degrees Celsius, extracting obtained extract for 2-3 hours to obtain patchouli extract. The method involves drying obtained extract to moisture content of 5-10% at 55-60degrees Celsius, and preparing obtained filtrate by adding water that is 5-8 weight times remaining raw materials to remaining raw materials except patchouli, soaking for 30-55 minutes, decocting at 95-115degrees Celsius for 15-30 minutes, and decocted at 75-85degrees Celsius for 1-2 hours, and filtering to obtain filtrate.
