PT P
PN CN112940497-A; CN112940497-B
TI Preparing electromagnetic shielding polyamide 6/polystyrene composite foam material comprises dispersing functionalized carbon nanotubes in molten caprolactam monomer, adding graphene and polystyrene, removing water and polymerizing.
AU WU B
   ZHU K
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202171007B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing electromagnetic shielding polyamide 6/polystyrene composite foam material comprises (a) dispersing functionalized carbon nanotubes in molten caprolactam monomer to obtain caprolactam/functionalized carbon nanotube suspension, (b) adding graphene and polystyrene to caprolactam/functionalized carbon nanotube suspension obtained in the step (a) at 120-140 degrees C, heating, stirring for 2-4 hours, removing water under vacuum at 130-160 degrees C, adding initiator sodium hydroxide and activator toluene diisocyanate to obtain polymerization reaction system, polymerizing at 150-200 degrees C for 20-60 minutes to obtain composite material and (c) subjecting composite material obtained in the step (b) to supercritical carbon dioxide foaming treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing electromagnetic shielding polyamide 6/polystyrene composite foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares composite foam material, which can meet the requirements of low reflection, high absorption and high electromagnetic shielding effectiveness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the functionalized carbon nanotubes includes hydroxylated carbon nanotubes or carboxylated carbon nanotubes. In the caprolactam/functionalized carbon nanotube suspension obtained in the step (a), the concentration of functionalized carbon nanotubes is 0.001-5 wt.%. The polymerization reaction system of step (b) comprises 10-30 wt.% polystyrene, 0.001-5 wt.% graphene, 0.1-1 wt.% sodium hydroxide, 0.1-1 wt.% toluene diisocyanate and remaining amount of caprolactam/functionalized carbon nanotube suspension. Preferred Method: The parameters of supercritical carbon dioxide foaming treatment in step (c) includes performing foaming at 100-150 degrees C under 9-15Mpa and maintaining pressure for 30-300 minutes.

PT P
PN CN112588082-A
TI Method for processing trimethylamine malodorous gas, involves collecting malodorous gas containing trimethylamine and ethyl chloride, exchanging heat between malodorous gas and cooling medium and entering cooled gas into acid washing tower.
AU YU P
   LI H
   WANG D
   KONG F
AE SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202135996Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting malodorous gas containing trimethylamine, methanol, ethyl chloride, and benzyl chloride generated during the production of tetramethylammonium hydroxide under the suction action of a fan. Heat is exchanged between the malodorous gas and a cooling medium. The cooled gas is entered into an acid washing tower to absorb and catch acid liquid sprayed from top to bottom in the acid washing tower. An aqueous solution containing plant extract is used to absorb part of the soluble gas components and entrained acid. The acid cleaned from the top of the acid washing tower is entered into an absorption oxidation tower. Residual water-insoluble components and trimethylamine are absorbed and oxidized to carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - The method is useful for processing trimethylamine malodorous gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has wide gas source adaptability, strong concentration adaptability, good malodorous component removing effect and is suitable for multi-component malodorous gas treatment containing trimethylamine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acid washing tower is an aqueous inorganic acid solution, and the inorganic acid is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid, and/or phosphoric acid, with a concentration of 0.1-0.5 mol/l. The plant extract is natural degradable amino acids, essential oil, vitamin, and mineral extract. The absorption and oxidation tower uses an aqueous solution containing hydrogen peroxide and ferrous ion in a molar ratio of 4-8. The adsorbent used in the absorption oxidation tower is porous activated carbon, and the type of activated carbon is coconut shell activated carbon, apricot shell activated carbon, and/or monolithic honeycomb activated carbon.

TI Physical simulation of slag foaming in rotary furnace, involves adding acid solution and oil into converter physical model made of organic glass to simulate molten steel and converter slag, and adding sodium carbonate particles.
AU ZHANG B
   WANG R
   LIU C
   HU C
   JIANG M
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 202130605U
AB    NOVELTY - The physical simulation method involves adding acid solution and oil into converter physical model made of organic glass, respectively simulating molten steel and converter slag, adding sodium carbonate particles above furnace opening of converter physical model, opening top blowing oxygen gun to supply gas, using oxygen lance jet action, settling sodium carbonate particles through oil layer to acid solution-oil interface, reacting sodium carbonate particles with acid solution to generate a large amount of carbon dioxide gas, so as to simulate converter slag foaming, and recording change of foam liquid level height.
   USE - The method is useful for physical simulation of molten slag foaming in rotary furnace.

AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic material stably loaded on                biomass porous substrate comprises renewable                biomass porous substrate, photocatalyst and                cellulose gel layer. The photocatalytic material has three-dimensional                hollow structure.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The photocatalytic                  material is prepared by drying lithium chloride                  and N, N-Dimethylacetamide, adding lithium                  chloride to dimethylacetamide, mixing to obtain                  cellulose solvent system, adding photocatalyst to                  cellulose solvent system, mixing, adding                  activated cellulose, mixing at room temperature                  to obtain cellulose impregnation solution with                  0.1-3% cellulose, adding porous material of                  biomass to cellulose impregnation solution,                  wetting impregnation liquid to substrate,                  removing substrate, soaking in crosslinking                  solution for gelation of 1-6 hours, freeze drying                  product and performing vacuum treated for 1-6                  hours at 100-160 degrees C. Preferred Components:                  The biomass porous substrate is Cordyceps, kapok,                  catkins and/or straw. The mass of photocatalyst                  is 0.1-100% of the mass of cellulose. The                  photocatalyst is titanium dioxide, carbon                  nitride, zinc oxide, zirconia, cadmium sulfide,                  tin oxide and/or powder. The crosslinking agent                  is citric acid, glutaraldehyde and/or                  epichlorohydrin. The solvent is water, ethanol                  and/or octane. TI Producing conductive carbon thin film involves placing polymer source on stage, positioning target substrate on top of polymer source and providing light toward the polymer source to form conductive carbon thin film.
AU HEE W
   SEOK J Y
   KWONSIN
   SEUNG-HYOUN L
   KIM H
AE KOREA INST MACHINERY & MATERIALS (KIMA-C)
GA 202113079Y
AB    NOVELTY - Producing conductive carbon thin film involves placing polymer source on the stage, positioning a target substrate on top of the polymer source and providing light toward the polymer source to form a conductive carbon thin film on the surface of the target substrate, and as the light is provided to the polymer source. AB    NOVELTY - Processing of medium/high-temperature mechanical vapor recompression wastewater involves carrying out mechanical vapor recompression wastewater evaporation and organic material degradation reaction in reaction evaporator, preheating, adding high-salt and high-chemical oxygen demand wastewater to reaction evaporator, carrying out degradation of organic material in liquid phase, and oxidation of organic material to water and carbon dioxide under the action of catalyst and oxidizing agent, concentrating liquid phase, crystallizing, obtaining mixed salt, compressing gas phase, adding to heating device of the reaction evaporator, and providing heat for degradation reaction of organic material.
   USE - Processing of medium/high-temperature mechanical vapor recompression wastewater.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst is iron powder, manganese dioxide, vanadium pentoxide, sulfuric acid and/or nitric acid solution. When the catalyst is liquid, the usage amount of the catalyst is 0.1-10% of mass of high-salt and high-chemical oxygen wastewater. When the catalyst is solid, the filling volume of the catalyst is 20-22% of inner volume of reaction evaporator. The oxidizing agent is air, oxygen, ozone, hydrogen peroxide solution, hypochlorite solution, dichromate solution and/or permanganate solution. The salt in high-salt and high-chemical oxygen wastewater is sodium chloride, potassium chloride or sodium sulfate. High-salt and high-chemical oxygen wastewater has salt content of 8-9 wt.% and content of chemical oxygen demand of 30000-60000 ppm.

PN CN111995517-A
TI Preparing ethyl eicosapentaenoate comprises e.g. mixing and reacting glyceride-type fish oil with alkali catalyst, adding pH regulator into reaction liquid, extracting, separating and purifying primary product of ethyl eicosapentaenoate.
AU PAN H
   YIN J
AE SHENZHEN INST ADVANCED TECHNOLOGY (CAAT-C)
GA 2020C14771
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ethyl eicosapentaenoate comprises mixing and reacting glyceride-type fish oil having purity of not less than 50% with alkali catalyst to obtain first reaction liquid, adding pH adjusting agent into first reaction liquid to adjust first reaction liquid to neutral, and stratifying first reaction liquid, taking upper layer liquid of first reaction liquid, and washing the upper layer liquid to obtain second reaction liquid containing ethyl eicosapentaenoate and ethyl docosahexaenoate, extracting and purifying second reaction liquid by a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method to obtain ethyl eicosapentaenoate extract, performing distillation of ethyl eicosapentaenoate extract by molecular distillation to obtain primary product of ethyl eicosapentaenoate, separating and purifying primary product of ethyl eicosapentaenoate by high performance liquid chromatography to obtain ethyl eicosapentaenoate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ethyl eicosapentaenoate.
   ADVANTAGE - The ethyl eicosapentaenoate has purity of more than 96%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for washing upper layer liquid is carried out by washing the upper layer liquid many times with deionized water at a temperature of 45-90 degrees C. The extracting and purifying step of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method comprises setting extraction and purification device as parameters: the temperature of the extraction kettle is 32-52 degrees C; the temperature of the separating kettle I is between 35 and 55 degrees C; the pressure is between 4 and 10 MPa; the temperature of rectifying I in rectifying column is 35-50 degrees C; the temperature of the rectifying II is 40-60 degrees C; the temperature of the rectifying III is 42-65 degrees C; the temperature of the rectifying IV is 60-85 degrees C; controlling the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 1-20 l/h; adding second reaction liquid into reaction kettle, adjusting the pressure of extracting kettle to 10-25 MPa, adjusting the pressure of rectifying column to 9-13 MPa, the reaction time is 0.5-3 hours, collecting the separating kettle I to obtain grease, adjusting pressure of extraction kettle to be 10-25 MPa, adjusting pressure of rectification column to 12-15 MPa, reaction time is 0.5-3 hours, collecting ethyl eicosapentaenoate extraction liquid in separation kettle I. The distillation parameters of molecular distillation method are: preheating temperature is 60-90 degrees C, the vacuum degree is 0.1-1 Pa, distillation temperature is 65-140 degrees C, titration rate is 0.4-5 ml/minutes, the scraping speed is 300-400 revolutions/minute, distilling ethyl eicosapentaenoate extraction liquid obtained by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction for many times, and at least one distillation temperature is not less than 70 degrees C and at least one distillation temperature is 140 degrees C in the distillation. The sampling amount of high performance liquid chromatography separation and purification is 10-10000 ml per time, sampling flow speed is 10-1000 ml/minute; and flow rate of the mobile phase is 50-5000 ml/minute. The high performance liquid phase chromatographic separation method purifying processing step comprises dissolving ethyl eicosapentaenoate primary product in 5-30 times of organic solvent, filtering by 0.22 mu m-pore filter film to obtain filter liquid, taking C18 as fixed phase, organic solution having mass percentage content of 85-100 % as mobile phase, carrying out high performance liquid phase chromatography separation and purification to filter liquid, separating and purifying the product obtained by separating and purifying high efficiency liquid phase chromatogram by vacuum rotary evaporation to obtain ethyl eicosapentaenoate. Preferred Components: The alkali catalyst is sodium ethoxide, potassium ethoxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, rubidium hydroxide, and/or cesium hydroxide. The organic solvent is methanol, ethanol and/or acetonitrile. The organic solution is methanol aqueous solution, ethanol aqueous solution and/or acetonitrile aqueous solution. The pH regulator is citric acid, malic acid, tartaric acid, ascorbic acid and/or acetic acid.

PN CN111808808-A
TI Culturing mesenchymal stem cells on acellular allogeneic dermal scaffolds useful in e.g. preparation of artificial skin, involves preparing mesenchymal stem cell suspension and culturing with acellular allogeneic dermal scaffold.
AU CAO Y
   TENG R
   ZHANG H
AE BEIJING ZHENXIGU MEDICAL RES CENT LP (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020A5065J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for culturing mesenchymal stem cells on acellular allogeneic dermal scaffolds involves (a) preparing mesenchymal stem cell suspension by when the mesenchymal stem cells are subcultured to 70-90% confluence, collecting P3 generation cells to prepare a mesenchymal stem cell suspension, and (b) attaching by placing the acellular allogeneic dermal scaffold and mesenchymal stem cell suspension in an ultra-low adsorption reaction dish, using a complete medium for culture, incubating with shaking at 37 degrees C and incubating for another 2-3 days, where the culture conditions are temperature of 37 plus minus 0.5 degrees C, carbon dioxide concentration is 5 plus minus 0.2% and humidity is saturated humidity.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing mesenchymal stem cells on acellular allogeneic dermal scaffolds which is used in preparation of artificial skin used in biomimetic material medical field (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The artificial skin reduces depth of wound surface and provides relatively satisfactory healing effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Cell: The mesenchymal stem cells include adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells, umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells and endometrial mesenchymal stem cells, preferably adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cells. Preferred Method: The cultivation method involves (a) preparing mesenchymal stem cell suspension by subculturing the mesenchymal stem cells and culturing in complete medium until the cells are 70-90%, preferably 90% confluent and then collecting P3 generation cells to prepare mesenchymal stem cell suspension, where the complete medium is prepared by taking Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F12) as a basic medium, adding 20-30 mu g/ml, preferably 20 mu g/ml human fibronectin, 5-15 ng/ml, preferably 5 ng/ml basic fibroblast growth factor, 10-20 ng/ml human epidermal growth factor, 0.5-1.5%, preferably 0.5% ITS, 4-6%, preferably 4% human albumin, 0.5-1.5%, preferably 0.5% N-nitrosodiethylamine (NEAA), 0.05-0.15 mu mol/l, pew 0.05 mu mol/l hydrogenated cortisone and 0.05-0.15% beta -mercaptoethanol, and (b) placing the acellular allogeneic dermal scaffold and mesenchymal stem cell suspension in an ultra-low adsorption reaction dish, using a complete medium for culture, incubating with shaking at 37 degrees C and 40-60 revolutions/minute (rpm), preferably 40 rpm for 20-40 minutes, preferably 20 minutes, then stopping shaking, standing and continuously incubating for 3-6 days, where the culture conditions are temperature of 37 plus minus 0.5 degrees C, carbon dioxide concentration of 5 plus minus 0.2% and humidity of saturated humidity. The adipose-derived mesenchymal stem cell suspension is prepared by taking adipocytes, placing the adipocytes in a test tube, adding the same volume of digestion solution as the adipose tissue in the test tube, where the digestion solution includes trypsin-EDTA with a volume ratio of 1:1 and 0.1% type I collagenase, sealing the test tube, placing in a constant temperature shaker at 37 degrees C, shaking and digesting at 180-200 rpm, dividing into 3 layers, aspirating the lower layer of liquid in the centrifuge tube, transferring to a new centrifuge tube containing complete medium to terminate the digestion, closing the centrifuge tube, centrifuging, dividing into 2 layers, using a pipette to suck up the supernatant in the new centrifuge tube, removing, adding complete medium, gently pipetting to make a cell suspension, inoculating the cell suspension into a culture flask at a density of 0.5x 106/ml, placing in a 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, changing the medium for the first time after 12-24 hours, changing the medium every 3 days, passing the culture after the cells grow to confluence, aspirating the old medium in the culture flask, rinsing with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) for 2-3 times, then adding digestion solution for 3-5 minutes and gently pipetting the cells to remove the cells from the bottom of the bottle to obtain a single cell suspension. The adipose tissue is an adipose extract obtained directly by liposuction. The method for obtaining fat cells involves taking the fat tissue, squeezing into the centrifuge tube, adding the PBS buffer to the centrifuge tube containing the tissue, using 12-16 ml PBS buffer per g adipose tissue, pipetting 10-20 times with a pipette, then letting TO stand for 0.5-1.5 minutes, using a straw to suck out the liquid from the lower layer of the tissue and repeating until the sucked out liquid is transparent and does not contain blood cells. The method involves before the acellular allogeneic dermal scaffold is placed in the ultra-low adsorption reaction dish, cutting into a square portions with a side length of 2 cm and washing for three times with normal saline.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI Apple carbonated beverage manufacturing involves removing seeds of washed apple, making apple juice by juicing apples from which seeds have been removed and filtering them with micro filter, and then pasteurizing apple juice.
AU KO T
AE GGLIM CORP (GGLI-Non-standard)
GA 202088466R
AB    NOVELTY - Apple carbonated beverage manufacturing involves removing seeds of washed apple, making apple juice by juicing the apples from which the seeds have been removed and filtering them with a micro filter, and then pasteurizing apple juice. 0.3-0.7 volumes carbon dioxide gas is dissolved in apple juice at 1-5 degrees C, where the apple juice is packed. The apples are washed with ozone water, hypochlorous acid water having a pH of 5-6.5, and an effective chlorine concentration of 10-30 parts per million. The apple juice is placed in a stirring tank at 85-90 degrees C for 15-17 minutes to pasteurize apple juice, and then degassed.
   USE - Method for manufacturing apple carbonated beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method manufactures the apple carbonated beverage in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with improved productivity, high consumers preference and different flavor from conventional apple juice.

TI Preparation of self-supporting oxygen reduction catalyst used in battery cathode material, involves soaking porous carbon cloth in mixture solution containing metal ion and nitrogen-containing ligand, and pyrolyzing loaded cloth.
AU FANG J
   JIANG S
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 202084544X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of self-supporting oxygen reduction catalyst involves pyrolyzing pure cotton fabric in carbon dioxide atmosphere to obtain porous carbon cloth, soaking the carbon cloth in a mixed solution containing a metal ion and a nitrogen-containing ligand so that the carbon cloth absorbs the metal ion and the nitrogen-containing ligand, pyrolyzing the metal ion-absorbed carbon cloth in a nitrogen atmosphere.
   USE - Preparation of self-supporting oxygen reduction catalyst used in battery cathode material (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces oxygen reduction catalyst using inexpensive and easily available pure cotton fabric as carbon source. The catalyst has high specific surface area, high catalytic activity in alkaline environment equivalent to commercial platinum/carbon, excellent formaldehyde resistance and stability. The process is economical, simple. The cathode material using the oxygen reduction catalyst exhibits excellent battery performance and flexibility.

The areas of the top and bottom cells are not equal in the stacked cell at both ends. The ratio of area of the top cell to the area of the bottom cell is between 1.1-2.0, by increasing the area of the top perovskite cell to compensate the current of the top cell, and realized current matching of the top and bottom cells.

The electron layer transport material of perovskite cell is inorganic tin dioxide or titanium dioxide, or organic material (6,6)-phenyl-61C-butyric acid methyl ester (PCBM) Or fullerene 60C. The transparent electrode materials of the perovskite battery and the crystalline silicon battery are indium tin oxide, indium zinc oxide, hypoiodous acid or zirconium doped indium oxide. The crystalline silicon bottom cell is a planar silicon cell, a single-sided texturing or double-sided texturing silicon solar cell.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The conductive adhesive is a mixture of binder resin, metal conductive particles or metal oxide conductive particles and an organic solvent, preferably low-temperature silver paste or an indium tin oxide conductive adhesive, and pure transparent conductive oxide film indium tin oxide semiconductor, zinc tin oxide semiconductor or indium gallium zinc oxide semiconductor, and gold, platinum, silver or palladium metal nano array. TI Hydrogen enrichment of light hydrocarbon in production of hydromethane gas, involves mixing light hydrocarbon flow with aqueous flow, thermally-conditioning until supercritical condition is reached, and initiating reforming reaction.
AU TUMMINELLI G
   TUZZOLINO G
   GATTUSO C
   RIZZO R
   SANTORO F
AE ARCHIMEDE SRL (ARCH-Non-standard)
   ARCHIMEDE SRL (ARCH-Non-standard)
GA 202061954Q
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen enrichment of light hydrocarbon e.g. natural gas involves feeding a light hydrocarbon flow (Gin), feeding an aqueous flow (Win), mixing the light hydrocarbon flow with aqueous flow, thermally-conditioning the mixed flow comprising the light hydrocarbon flow and aqueous flow, until supercritical condition is reached, initiating reforming reaction of the mixed flow in supercritical condition, and separating an enriched light hydrocarbon from an aqueous fraction and from a carbon dioxide fraction of the reforming reaction product.
   USE - Hydrogen enrichment of light hydrocarbon e.g. natural gas in production of hydromethane gas used as fuel for industrial and domestic burner and boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and efficient hydrogen enrichment of light hydrocarbon with reduced energy consumption and harmful emissions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining the hydrogen enrichment of light hydrocarbon.
   Enrichment unit (2)
   Energy-conversion unit (4)
   Solar reactor (10)
   Light hydrocarbon flow (Gin)
   Aqueous flow (Win)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The reforming reaction is performed using a solar reactor (10), preferably solar concentrator device. The thermally-conditioning process is performed by radiating the solar reactor by solar radiation. The process involves feeding the light hydrocarbon flow by pressurizing the light hydrocarbon flow, feeding the light aqueous flow by pressurizing the light aqueous flow, mixing the light hydrocarbon flow and the aqueous flow, thermally-conditioning the mixed flow, pre-heating the mixed flow before passing into a receiver duct of the solar reactor by thermal energy accumulation device, performing reforming reaction, passing the reforming reaction product to the thermal energy accumulation device as a charging fluid, passing the reforming reaction product to a separator (s1) for discharging an aqueous fraction (f1), passing the reforming reaction product purged from the fraction (f1) to an expander connected to a power generator, passing the reforming reaction product purged from the fraction (f1) to a separator (s2) for discharging an aqueous fraction (f2), passing the reforming reaction product purged from the fraction (f1) and the fraction (f2) to an adsorption device configured for discharging carbon dioxide present in the reforming reaction product, and pressurizing the carbon dioxide and capturing in a carbon dioxide tank.

GA 202070501E
AB    NOVELTY - Coupled process of extraction and precipitation / drying of plant extracts using pressurized fluids involves obtaining dry extracts, rich in bioactive compounds, obtained from the vegetable parts, but specifically from the leaves from Hancornia speciosa. The process coupled with extraction and extraction/ precipitation via supercritical carbon dioxide through a simultaneous process is used.
   USE - Method for coupled process of extraction and precipitation/drying of plant extracts using pressurized fluids used for product for pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, foodstuffs and agricultural pesticides (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to coupled process of extraction and precipitation / drying of plant extracts using pressurized fluids reduces the time and expenses of energy in the process, reduces the probability of degradation of compounds, facilitates the storage and increases the efficacy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a product, which comprises pharmacologically active components, which can be applied as active components, preferably flavonoids such as rutin and catechin in different formulations.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Process: The preparation of Hancornia speciosa extrect includes collecting the leaves, drying, using for example, oven drying with air circulation at a temperature of 35-55 degrees C, more preferably 40 degrees C. The material should be crushed and separated by granulometry, preferably particles with a size between 0.5 a1 mm after drying. The material must be accommodated in a closed extraction cell and connected by pressurization pumps in suitable equipment for extraction by pressurized fluids, of any make, model and/or capacity. The extraction must occur under a pressure of 1-300 bar, more specifically from 100-150 bar, at a temperature of 0-400 degrees C, more specifically from 25-55 degrees C and flow rate from 1-100 milliliters per minute, more specifically from 1 milliliter per minute, using as organic solvents of different polarities, more specifically ethanole acetone. The prrocess includes the drying and precipitation process, the solution containing the obtained extract is sprayed in a stainless steel precipitation chamber connected to high pressure syringe pumps, the drying occurs by contacting the extract solution with supercritical carbon dioxide, under depression conditions of 1-300 bar, preferably at 100 and 150 bar, with emission of carbon dioxide between 1-100 milliliters per minute, preferably 25 milliliters per minute. The temperature inside the precipitation chamber must be between 0-200 degrees C, preferably at 40 degrees C. Preferred Condition: The coupled process lasting 10-240 minutes, preferably 30-120 minutes. The process includes obtaining extracts, precipitated, purified and concentrated in bioactive compounds in a single step.

TI Collecting and providing environmental information by server, comprises e.g. collecting environmental information and location information from user terminals, and calculating representative value of environment information.
AU KIM S K

GA 2020543687
AB    NOVELTY - Collecting and providing environmental information by server, comprises collecting environmental information and location information from a multiple of user terminals; calculating a representative value of the environment information for a multiple of regions geographically divided based on the environment information and the location information; and transmitting a representative value of the pre-calculated environment information for the specific area to the user terminal entering the specific area.
   USE - The method is useful for collecting and providing environmental information by server.
   ADVANTAGE - The method easily monitors the pollution of the environment based on the acquisition of various information on indoor and outdoor environmental conditions, e.g. dust concentration, temperature and humidity, carbon dioxide concentration; enables the user to receive environmental information of a current location with high reliability; and promotes efficient information transmission and reception, thus it can optimize communication traffic accordingly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for server for collecting and providing environmental information.

PN CN111196710-A
TI Rare earth coating useful for electrolytic anode to prevent high temperature oxidation and corrosion, comprises e.g. binder, high temperature self-healing filler, functional rare earth filler, and inorganic suspension agent.
AU LIU J
   LI R
   LIU Q
AE BAOTOU XIANGJI INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BAOT-Non-standard)
GA 202048656M
AB    NOVELTY - Rare earth coating comprises 20-60 pts. wt. binder, 25-50 pts. wt. modified high-temperature filler, 5-15 pts. wt. high temperature self-healing filler, 0.5-10 pts. wt. functional rare earth filler, and 0.5-5 pts. wt. inorganic suspension agent.
   USE - The rare earth coating is useful for electrolytic anode to prevent high temperature oxidation and corrosion (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The rare earth coating: can reduces the emission of carbon dioxide and fluorocarbon compounds, introduces rare-earth elements into the coating, improves the performance of the coating, and improves the quality of the electrolytic metal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the rare earth coating comprising mixing all the raw materials, homogenizing under high-speed stirring for 1-3 hours, and grinding to D50 less than or equal to 2 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The rare earth coating comprises 40 pts. wt. binder, 37.5 pts. wt. modified high-temperature filler, 10 pts. wt. high temperature self-healing filler, 5 pts. wt. functional rare earth filler, and 2.5 pts. wt. inorganic suspension agent. Preferred Method: The preparation of binder comprises mixing and stirring sodium silicate, alkaline silica sol and demineralized water until it is viscous. The preparation of the modified high-temperature filler comprises (i) mixing and reacting with aluminate coupling agent and super-fine alumina powder to obtain modified super-fine alumina powder; (ii) mixing the silane coupling agent, silicon dioxide and magnesium dioxide to perform surface modification to obtain modified mixed powder; and (iii) mixing and stirring the modified super-fine alumina powder obtained in step (i), modified mixed powder obtained in step (ii), calcined kaolin and titanium dioxide evenly to obtain modified high-temperature filler. The preparation of functional rare earth filler comprises mixing yttrium oxide and lanthanum cerium oxide powder in weight ratio of 1:0.5 to 1. Preferred Components: The weight ratio of the sodium silicate, alkaline silica sol and demineralized water is 0.2-1:0.05-0.1:0.5-1; the sodium silicate has numerical model of 2.8-3.4 and concentration of 28-33%, and it is potassium sodium silicate and/or sodium sodium silicate; the concentration of the alkaline silica sol is 28-35%, and the pH is 8-11; the mixing and stirring method comprises mixing and stirring under 50-100 degrees C and 0.05-1 MPa at speed of 1000-3000 revolutions/minute for 1-3 hours. In the step (i), the weight ratio of the super-fine alumina powder to the aluminate coupling agent is 100:0.5-2; the particle size of the ultrafine alumina powder is greater than or equal to 1000 mesh; the condition for the reaction modification is to react at 20-25 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours. In the step (ii), the weight ratio of the silica, magnesium dioxide and silane coupling agent is 50:50:0.5-2; the silane coupling agent is KH550 or KH560. The high-temperature self-healing fillers comprises three glass powders with melting points of 500-649 degrees C, 650-799 degrees C and 800-900 degrees C respectively, and are mixed in a weight ratio of 0.5-1:0.5-1:0.5-1.

TI Formation of lead-zirconium titanate type ferroelectric film for integrated circuit, involves baking liquid composition coated on substrate in mixed gas atmosphere and oxygen containing atmosphere.
AU SUZUKI S
   DOI T
   SOYAMA N
AE MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
GA 202026517E
AB    NOVELTY - Formation of a lead-zirconium-titanate type ferroelectric film involves coating a liquid composition for the film formation on a substrate, drying the film, carrying out temporary baking of the film at 150-200 degrees C in mixed gas atmosphere comprising inert gas and 1-10 vol.% formic acid gas, carrying out temporary baking at 400-500 degrees C in oxygen-containing atmosphere.
   USE - Formation of lead-zirconium-titanate type ferroelectric film used for integrated circuit of electronic components. Uses include but are not limited to vibration electricity generating elements, piezoelectric sensors, actuator, inkjet head and auto-focus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reliably produces the ferroelectric film without supplying ozone gas using an expensive UV irradiation device. The pyrochlore phase of the film can be transformed into perovskite phase by low temperature baking. Since the formic acid gas decomposes to water and carbon dioxide, the damage on human body and environment is reduced.

TI Cellulose composition used for producing cellulose            molded body, comprises water-soluble cellulose ether,            water-insoluble cellulose particles and water.
AU KAZUHISA H
   TAKANE S
   HAYAKAWA K
   SUDO T
AE SHINETSU CHEM CO LTD (SHIE-C)
   SHINETSU CHEM CO LTD (SHIE-C)
   SHINETSU CHEM CO LTD (SHIE-C)
   SHINETSU CHEM CO LTD (SHIE-C)
GA 202020669N
AB    NOVELTY - Cellulose composition comprises (A) a                water-soluble cellulose ether, (B) water-insoluble                cellulose particles and (C) water.
   USE - The composition is useful for producing a                cellulose molded body.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition serves as a novel biomass                material which uses biodegradable cellulose that                places little burden on the environment, exhibits                excellent shape retention properties, provides the                molded body with high flexibility containing no                resin component generates carbon dioxide at the                time of incineration, and composed of                carbon-neutral cellulose, can be easily molded by                various molding devices, can be twist and stretch                by hand by hand to form it and can be formed into                an arbitrary shape such as a rectangular                parallelepiped, a sphere, a ring and a U-shaped                stand, depending on the application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a cellulose molded body obtained by                molding and drying the composition; and
   (2) a method for producing the composition                involving mixing the components (A)-(c) to obtain                the composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises 30-150 parts mass component (B) and                  300-1000 parts mass component (C) based on 100                  parts mass component (A). Preferred Properties:                  The component (B) has a volume-based average                  particle size of 0.017-200 mu m as determined by                  wet laser diffraction scattering. The component                  (B) has an effective particle size of 0.017-1 mu                  m having a volume fraction of greater than or                  equal to 0.3% as determined by the wet laser                  diffraction scattering. Preferred Component: The                  component (B) is a low-substituted                  hydroxypropylcellulose having a hydroxypropoxy                  group substitution molar number (MS) of 0.1-0.4.                  The component (A) is at least one chosen from                  hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, hydroxyethyl                  methylcellulose and methylcellulose. Preferred                  Method: The method involves mixing which is a                  kneading extrusion process in which a kneading                  process and an extrusion process are continuously                  performed. The method involves mixing the                  component (B) comprising water-insoluble                  cellulose particle raw material powder and the                  component (C) to prepare a water-insoluble                  cellulose particle dispersion, grinding the                  water-insoluble cellulose particles in the                  water-insoluble cellulose particle dispersion and                  adding the component (A) comprising water-soluble                  cellulose ether raw material powder to the                  dispersion after the grinding treatment.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI New azo derivatives used as disperse dyes for dyeing supercritical fluids, prepared by using amine derivative and diazotizing by adding sodium nitrite or nitrosulfuric acid, dissolving an indole derivative.

   JUN K
   SHIN J
   AN K L
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 202009695F

   USE - Azo derivative used as disperse dyes for dyeing supercritical fluids.
   ADVANTAGE - The azo derivative has excellent solubility characteristics for super-critical carbon dioxide, and reduces the production price.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Azo derivatives of formulae (I) and (II), are new.
   A=a group of formula (i) or (ii);
   R1-R4=H, amide, nitro, acetyl, (C1-C20) alkyl, (1-20C) alkoxy, Halogen, cyano or SO2F; and
   R5-R11=H, (1-20C) alkyl, halo (1-20C) alkyl, (6-20C) aryl (1-20C) alkyl, hydroxy (1-20C) alkyl, (1-20C) alkoxycarbonyl (1-20C) alkyl group.

   (1) a method for dyeing supercritical fluid, which involves using azo-based dispersion dye for supercritical fluid dyeing; and
   (2) a method for preparing azo derivative used as azo-based disperse dyes for dyeing the supercritical fluid.

PT P
PN CN110577232-A; CN110577232-B
TI Preparing nano-calcium carbonate involves adding            ammonium chloride solution to the steel slag for            reaction to obtain the leaching solution of the calcium            chloride-ammonium chloride-ammonia-water system and            leaving it overnight.
AU TONG Z
   SUN J
   WANG J
   TAN Z
   WANG W
   CHEN J
AE UNIV YANGTZE NORMAL (UYYN-C)
   UNIV YANGTZE NORMAL (UYYN-C)
GA 202000106K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-calcium carbonate involves                adding ammonium chloride solution to the steel slag                for reaction to obtain the leaching solution of the                calcium chloride-ammonium chloride-ammonia-water                system, leaving it overnight, and setting aside,                adding sucrose to the leaching solution, and then                passing carbon dioxide into the leaching solution,                and simultaneously turning on the ultrasonic wave                with a power of 1500-1800 watts, when the pH of the                leaching solution is 7.1, the reaction is stopped,                and immediately sieved with a 250 mesh screen. The                sieved product is then rinsed with absolute ethanol                and dried in a vacuum drying oven to obtain                nano-scale calcium carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing nano-calcium                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare nano-calcium                carbonate that has simple process, resource saving,                eco-friendly and low cost, easy industrial                production, and good application prospect and                economic benefits.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The concentration of                  calcium chloride in the leaching solution is 0.4                  mole/L, the concentration of ammonium chloride is                  1 mole/L, and the concentration of NH4HO is 0.8                  mole/L. The reaction temperature is 40-60 degrees                  C. The temperature of the leaching solution                  during the reaction is 15-25 degrees C. The added                  amount of sucrose is 2% of the mass of calcium                  chloride in the leaching solution. The flow rate                  of the carbon dioxide is 45 ml/minute. The drying                  temperature is 80 to 125 degrees C, and the                  drying time is 1 to 18 hours.

PT P
PN CN110526294-A; CN110526294-B
TI Synthesizing anti-sintering molybdenum disulfide            used in e.g. formaldehyde oxidation, by mixing organic            amide and water, adding molybdenum and sulfur sources,            reacting, washing and drying to organic amide            intercalated molybdenum disulfide.
AU GAO Q
   SHU Y
AE UNIV JINAN GUANGDONG (UJIN-C)
   UNIV JINAN (UJIN-C)
GA 2019A3807V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for synthesizing anti-sintering                molybdenum disulfide, involves uniformly mixing                organic amide and water to obtain a mixed solution,                adding a molybdenum source and a sulfur source to                the mixed solution, transferring the solution into                a reaction kettle for reaction, washing and drying                the reaction product to obtain the organic amide                intercalated molybdenum disulfide, i.e.                anti-sintering molybdenum disulfide.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing                anti-sintering molybdenum disulfide, which is used                in liquid-phase biomass catalytic hydrogenation,                carbon dioxide reduction, formaldehyde oxidation,                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis, hydrogenation                desulfurization/denitrification, and                electrocatalytic hydrogenation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The organic amide intercalated molybdenum                disulfide nanosheet maintains the original nano                size and nanosheet morphology after calcination at                high temperature at 300-1000 degrees C, effectively                protects the edge active sites and framework                structure of molybdenum disulfide, improves the                efficiency of the material in the above-mentioned                applications, and has outstanding application                prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The amounts of the                  molybdenum source, sulfur source and organic                  amide satisfy: the molar ratio of the sulfur atom                  of the sulfur source to the molybdenum atom in                  the molybdenum source is (2-36):1, preferably                  10:1, the molar ratio of the organic amide to the                  molybdenum atom in the molybdenum source is                  (20-120):1, preferably 32.4:1, and the volume                  ratio of the organic amide and water is                  1:(0.1-10), preferably 1:1. The reaction is                  carried out at 150-280 degrees C, preferably 240                  degrees C for 1-30 hours, preferably 3 hours.                  Preferred Components: The molybdenum source                  comprises at least one of ammonium molybdate,                  sodium molybdate, molybdenum oxide and                  phosphomolybdic acid. The sulfur source comprises                  at least one of thiourea, thioacetamide, cysteine                  and sodium sulfide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic amide is                  at least one of N,N-dimethylformamide,                  N,N-dimethylacetamide and                  N,N-dimethylpropanamide.

PN CN110184399-A; CN110184399-B
TI Processing steel slag comprises performing oxidative desulfurization of steel slag by introducing mixed gas of argon and oxygen into molten steel slag, removing sulfur dioxide, and performing gas-based reduction and dephosphorization.
AU ZHANG J
   ZHOU H
   XU H
AE CISRI SUNWARD TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CISR-Non-standard)
GA 201976009J
AB    NOVELTY - Processing steel slag comprises (a) performing oxidative desulfurization of steel slag by introducing a mixed gas of argon and oxygen into molten steel slag, and removing sulfur dioxide (SO2) obtained by the oxidation from the slag phase, and (b) performing gas-based reduction and dephosphorization of the desulfurized steel slag obtained in step (a) by using reducing gas as mixed gas comprising carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, and obtaining P2 (phosphorus) by the reduction overflows from the slag.
   USE - The method is useful for processing steel slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has sulfur removal rate of 82-97%, phosphorus removal rate reaches greater than 80%; solves the sulfur in the steel slag; improves the recycling rate of steel slag; ensures complete utilization of steel slag, sulfur, phosphorus; is economical; and increases the use of sulfur and phosphorus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The amount of oxygen in the step (a) is 15-30 wt.%. The amount of carbon monoxide in the step (b) is 35-50 wt.%. Preferred Method: The oxidative desulfurization of steel slag in the step (a) is carried out for 10-15 minutes. The step (b) comprises obtaining sulfur dioxide-containing gas after the oxidative desulfurization, subjecting to cooling and desulfurizing by a desulfurization device, supplementing the mixed gas of argon and oxygen after desulfurization and returning to the oxidative desulfurization process for recycling. The step (b) includes cooling the mixed gas containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and P2, re-oxidizing P2 by carbon dioxide to phosphorus pentoxide during cooling, restoring the reducing gas composition before the reduction, separating phosphorus pentoxide and returning the carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide mixed gas to the steel slag reduction stage for recycling, returning the dephosphorization slag after the gas-based reduction and dephosphorization to the steelmaking equipment for recycling. The gas-based reduction and dephosphorization in the step (b) is carried out for 30-50 minutes. The step (a) comprises dedusting the desulfurized argon and oxygen mixed gas by a bag dust removal method. The step (b) comprises removing phosphorus pentoxide from the gas-solid mixture by a bag dust removal method.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)

The flocculant is composed of polyaluminum chloride, aluminum silicate, fly ash, sodium sulfite, and diatomaceous earth in a mass ratio of (1-2):0.8:0.5:0.3:0.6. TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar weight of the pyromellitic dianhydride is 0.15 mol, and the molar weight of 2,4,6-tris(4-aminophenyl)-1,3,5-triazine is 0.1 mol. GA 2019489552

The apples in the fresh-keeping bag are taken out, the air inside is taken, and the carbon dioxide, oxygen and nitrogen are charged in proportion. The apple in the fresh-keeping bag is sterilized. The apples in the fresh-keeping bag are placed in a low-temperature refrigerator for cold storage.
   USE - Method for preserving apples.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to preserve apples in simple and convenient to operate manner, effectively inhibits the respiration and metabolism of apples, delays the ripening and aging of apples, and maintains the quality of apples.

The apple in the fresh-keeping bag is placed in a refrigerating room for refrigerating, the refrigerating temperature is 15 degrees C, and the refrigerating time is 24 hours. The apple in the fresh-keeping bag is subjected to ultraviolet sterilization treatment.

AU YUN X
   XIA W
   YANG L
   WENPENG X
AE CHENGXI SHIPYARD CO LTD (CHSB-C)
GA 201901645U
AB    NOVELTY - Welding hull flat butt joint closed joint involves closing hull flat butt joint and controlling the spacing of the closed seam. The welding pad and the horse board are assembled, then the back of the seam is welded with a welding pad, and the fixed horse board is straddled on the closing seam and placed on the structural surface of the hull. The three-in-one welding trolley is used to carry out the welding of the closed seam by padding, filling welding and using submerged arc welding cover. The filling welding involves using carbon dioxide protective welding filling and submerged arc filling.
   USE - Method for welding hull flat butt joint closed joint.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to weld hull flat butt joint closed joint, which improves the quality and efficiency of welding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The three-in-one welding trolley comprises a guide rail, walking trolley, and welding gun disposed on the walking trolley and the welding torch is connected to the welder. The gasket bottom welding is carbon dioxide protective welding. The welding process parameters of the liner bottom welding are bottoming current 220A, voltage 25V, welding speed is 150 mm/minutes. The welding process parameters of the carbon dioxide protective welding filling are current 230 A, voltage 27 V, welding speed of 200 mm per minute. The submerged arc fill welding charge is carried out under current of 550-650A, voltage of 35-38V, welding speed of 300-550 mm/minute. The welding process parameters of the submerged arc welding cover surface are current 580A, voltage 32-33V, welding speed is 320-330 mm/minute. The three-in-one welding trolley is further provided with a trolley position sensor for recording the position of the walking trolley on the guide rail, a weld height detecting sensor for detecting a weld height, where the trolley position sensor and the weld height detecting sensor are respectively connected to the programmable logic controller (PLC), and the walking speed of the walking trolley in the three-in-one welding trolley is controlled by the PLC. A weld height detecting is further disposed between the submerged arc filling welding and the submerged arc welding cover step, and the welding height detecting step passes the welding position through the trolley position sensor and the weld height detecting sensor. The height information of each place is sent to the PLC controller and at the same time, in the submerged arc welding cover the PLC controller slows down the traveling speed at a relatively low weld height according to the height difference of the weld, or the weld height is relatively high. The speed of walking the trolley is accelerated, so that the weld height after the submerged arc welding cover is uniform. The multiple detection points distributed at equal intervals along the weld direction, using a trolley position sensor and a weld height detecting sensor Measure the height of the weld at the test point, calculate the moving average value, and adjust the speed of the walking car according to the moving average of the weld height in the submerged arc welding cover step, so that the weld height after the submerged arc welding cover is the same.

   (1) a method for preparing the capsule brewing herbal tea comprising (i) pulverizing the flos sophorae immaturus, flos chrysanthemi, reed rhizome, and honeysuckle flower and extracting the liquid by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, (ii) using dextrin to adsorb the extract and mixing the fried red bean and supercritical extract and (iii) packaging in the brewable capsule; and

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Properties: The herbal tea is in the form of granule or powder. Preferred Composition: The herbal tea further comprises soluble dextrin. The components are pulverized to 20-60 mesh. The extraction is carried out at 32-40 degrees C and at a pressure of 8-10 MPa for 1-2 hours. The carbon dioxide flow rate is 80-200 l/hour. The method of adsorption using dextrin in step (ii) comprises mixing the dextrin with the extract, stirring, filtering and allowing the extract to stand. Preferred Conditions: The capsule brewing machine has water temperature of 85-100 degrees C, water pressure of 5-9 MPa and brewing time of 2-5 minutes. The specific gravity of the herbal tea and water is 1:10-1:60.

PN CN108754390-A; CN108754390-B
TI Preparing small-diameter graphite crucible            protective coating useful for smelting radioactive            metals, comprises e.g. roughening inner wall of            crucible, cleaning and drying inner wall of crucible            and densifying surface of coating.
AU YU Y
   HOU W
   LIU J
   SHEN J
   HUANG L
   PENG H
   JI X
   ZHANG D
AE BGRIMM TECHNOLOGY GROUP (BEJM-C)
   BEIKUANG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEJM-C)
   BGRIMM NEW MATERIALS SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO (BGRI-Non-standard)
GA 201888520N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing small-diameter graphite crucible                protective coating comprises (i) roughening inner                wall of the crucible by precision inner hole                blowing technology for uncoated area; (ii) cleaning                and drying inner wall of the crucible after                roughening treatment by high-pressure carbon                dioxide cleaning technology; (iii) spraying a pure                molybdenum bar with a flame spray gun to prepare a                bottom layer; (iv) spraying a ceramic bar with a                flame spray gun to prepare a surface layer; (v)                densifying the surface of the coating by laser                densification; and (vi) removing protective                measures.
   USE - The crucible protective coating is useful for                smelting radioactive metals (claimed) and impurity                element contamination in other alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces coating with thickness of                200-1000 mu m and porosity between 10-30%, has                uniform and dense structure; and improves the                barrier property and shelf life of impurity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The purity of pure                  molybdenum bar in the step (iii) is greater than                  99%. The ceramic rod in the step (iv) is a                  microcrystalline high-purity YSZ ceramic rod with                  a purity of greater than 99.5% and an internal                  particle size of 5-100 nm. Preferred Method: The                  step (i) comprises subjecting inner surface of                  the crucible to close-range, low-pressure, and                  small beam blowing by a press-in inner hole                  blowing gun, where surface roughness after sand                  blowing is 8-15 mu m. The sand blowing operation                  comprises crushing white corundum sand or brown                  corundum sand by electrofusion to particle size                  of 60-150 meshes, blowing pressure is 0.5-2 atm,                  blown sand angle is 60-80 degrees , and the sand                  blowing distance is 50-150 mm. The step (ii)                  comprises using a high-pressure compressed air                  stream containing carbon dioxide to clean rapidly                  and drying inner surface of the crucible, where                  carbon dioxide mass is 50-80 %, inlet pressure is                  10-15 atm at 20-40 degrees C at 50 to 150                  liter/minute. The spraying parameter in step                  (iii) comprises maintaining oxygen pressure at                  1-1.5 MPa, acetylene pressure at 0.8-1.1 MPa,                  compressed air pressure is 0.6-0.8 MPa, wire                  feeding voltage is 10-15 V, spraying distance is                  150-350 mm and coating thickness is 0.05-0.15 mm.                  The spraying parameter in step (iv) comprises                  maintaining oxygen pressure at 1-1.5 MPa,                  acetylene pressure at 0.8-1.1 MPa, compressed air                  pressure is 0.6-0.8 MPa, wire feeding voltage is                  8-13 V, spraying distance is 150-270 mm and                  coating thickness is 0.2-1 mm. The cooling in                  step (iii) and (iv) comprises cooling surface of                  the work piece forcedly by compressed air during                  the spraying process to maintain the substrate                  temperature at 200 plus minus 20 degrees C. The                  laser densification in step (v) comprises                  densifying surface of the coating rapidly by a                  laser heat treatment gun or a laser-crushing gun                  with a bore, where spot size is 1-3 mm, the input                  power is 200-2000 W, and the depth of the                  densified layer is 0.05-0.2 mm.

   (2) manufacture of stretched sheet, which involves stretching the sheet in machine direction and/or transverse direction at rate of 50-300%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The crystalline polyolefin is polyolefin having crystallinity based on ethylene chain, and has crystal melting point of 90-135 degrees C and/or density of 0.9-0.97 g/cm3. The cross-copolymer is a copolymer containing 50-95 %mass ethylene-aromatic vinyl compound-aromatic polyene copolymer chain and an aromatic vinyl compound polymer chain, which are bonded through an aromatic polyene monomer unit, or a graft copolymer of 50-95 mol% ethylene-aromatic vinyl compound-aromatic polyene copolymer chain and an aromatic vinyl compound polymer chain. The aromatic vinyl compound-aromatic polyene copolymer chain contains 5-30 mol% aromatic vinyl compound monomer unit, 0.01-0.2 mol% aromatic polyene monomer unit and remaining ethylene monomer unit. The mass ratio of components (A) and (B) is 5:95-95:5. Preferred Properties: The ethylene-aromatic vinyl compound-aromatic polyene copolymer chain has weight average molecular weight of 50000-300000 and molecular weight distribution of 1.8-6. The resin composition has storage elastic modulus at 140 degrees C of 1x 106 Pa or more. The peaks attributable to the ethylene-aromatic vinyl compound copolymer and the aromatic vinyl compound polymer of cross-copolymer are observed by 1H-NMR measurement, and the content of the aromatic vinyl compound in the ethylene-aromatic vinyl compound is 5-B,30 mol%, and the content of the ethylene-aromatic vinyl compound copolymer is 50-95 %mass. The ethylene-aromatic vinyl compound copolymer and the aromatic vinyl compound polymer have a bond. The cross copolymer has melt flow rate of 0.05-50 g/10 minutes at 200 degrees C and 49 N load, and gel content of 0.2 %mass or less.

PT P
PN CN108547591-A
TI Screen pipe completion heavy oil horizontal well water blocking comprises e.g. injecting a high temperature blocking agent into formation through which the gel plugging agent has been injected through the horizontal well.
AU LIU J
   TENG L
   YANG Y
   JING D
   WANG S
   HUANG X
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2018758306
AB    NOVELTY - Screen pipe completion heavy oil horizontal well water blocking, comprises (i) injecting predetermined amount of nitrogen into formation through horizontal well, (ii) injecting a gel plugging agent into the formation into which the nitrogen has been injected through the horizontal well, the gel plugging agent forming a pre-blocking band in the formation, (iii) injecting a high temperature blocking agent into formation through which the gel plugging agent has been injected through the horizontal well, the high temperature blocking agent solidifying at a water discharge position in the formation and forming a high temperature resistant plugging section, and (iv) injecting predetermined amount of carbon dioxide into the formation into which the high temperature blocking agent has been injected through a horizontal well.
   USE - The method is useful for screen pipe completion heavy oil horizontal well water blocking.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves horizontal well water blocking effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the nitrogen injection amount is 10-15 million nm3, the nitrogen gas displacement is 1200 m3/h, and the construction pressure is 5-8 MPa. The step (iii) further includes: after injecting the high temperature blocking agent, closing the horizontal well for condensation; after the end of the condensation, the horizontal well is subjected to circulation washing; after the circulating washing, steam is injected into the formation through the horizontal well and the well is drained. The waiting time is 24 hours, the steam injection time is 24 hours, and the well time is 24 hours. The step (iii) further includes: injecting the displacement post-liquid into the horizontal well before closing the horizontal well for coagulation, where the injection amount of displacement post-liquid is 50 m3. Preferred Component: In step (ii), the gel blocking agent is composed of 0.15-0.3 wt.% polyacrylamide, 0.15-0.3 wt.% furfural resin, 0.3-0.4 wt.% organic chromium cross-linking agent, 0.1-0.15 wt.% hexamethylenetetramine-thiourea stabilizer and water (balance amount); the injection amount of gel blocking agent is 400-500 m3 , and the displacement of the gel blocking agent is 0.5 m3/minute at the time of injection, and the construction pressure is 10-13 MPa. In the step (iii), the high temperature blocking agent is composed of 40-50 wt.% aluminum silicate, 10-30 wt.% urea, 0.1-0.15 wt.% sodium sulfamate and water (balance amount); and the injection amount of the high temperature blocking agent is 200-300 m3 , and the displacement of the high temperature blocking agent is 0.2-0.3 m3/minute and the construction pressure is 13-16 MPa. In step (iv), the injection amount of carbon dioxide is 200-300 t, and the injection speed of the carbon dioxide is 3-5 t/hour.

AU ZHOU X

   ZHANG W
   BAO T
   DENG L
AE UNIV NAT DEFENSE TECHNOLOGY (UNDT-C)
   UNIV NAT DEFENSE TECHNOLOGY (UNDT-C)
GA 201855420H
AB    NOVELTY - A block-shaped nanoporous thermosetting nitrocellulose has density of 0.172-0.282 g.cm-3, volume shrinkage of 59.0-73.5%, specific surface area of greater than 100 m2.g-1, and an average pore diameter in the range of several nanometers to several tens of nanometers.
   USE - Block-shaped nanoporous thermosetting nitrocellulose for preparing nanocomposite energetic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrocellulose has low density, stable volume shrinkage ratio, large specific surface area, and uniform nanopore size. The cellulose is suitable for filling oxidants in nanopores to prepare energetic material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the block-shaped nanoporous thermosetting nitrocellulose, which involves (1) adding ordinary nitrocellulose, curing agent and reaction catalyst sequentially to acetone, mixing to form nitrocellulose sol, (2) gelling the sol, aging the nitrocellulose gel,(3) subjecting the gel to solvent replacement, and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The curing agent is 2,4-toluene diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, diphenylmethane diisocyanate, polyphenylpolymethylene polyisocyanate, or hexamethylene diisocyanate, preferably 2,4-toluene diisocyanate which is added in an amount of 30-40 %mass of the ordinary nitrocellulose. The reaction catalyst comprises dibutyltin dodecylsulfate, dibutyltin dilaurate, stannous octoate, and/or dibutyltin diacetate, preferably dibutyltin dilaurate which is added in an amount of 1-5 %mass of the ordinary nitrocellulose.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The thermal decomposition of the nitrocellulose has a starting temperature of less than 190 degrees C, peak temperature of 190 degrees C or more, and termination temperature of less than 195 degrees C. Preferred Composition: In step (1), the ordinary nitrocellulose has a nitrogen content of 11-13%. The concentration of ordinary nitrocellulose added in acetone is 40-100 g.L-1. Preferred Conditions: In step (2), the reaction temperature of gelling the sol is 30-50 degrees C. In step (3), the supercritical carbon dioxide drying is carried out at 45-55 degrees C and at 12-15 MPa, for 3-12 hours.

PN CN108165396-A
TI Preparation of seaweed non-alcoholic beer based on marine microbial enzyme involves taking fresh or dry seaweed, shearing, adding water, homogenizing, sterilizing, and inoculating Bacillus subtilis suspension and Lactobacillus plantarum.
AU HUANG K
   YIN H
   YU J
   HUANG S
   TIAN Y
   QIAN Z
   CHANG Z
   HU S
   ZHANG C
   YANG M
AE TSINGTAO BREWERY CO LTD (TSIN-Non-standard)
GA 201849141K
AB    NOVELTY - A seaweed non-alcoholic beer based on marine microbial enzyme is prepared by taking fresh or dry seaweed, shearing, adding water, homogenizing, sterilizing, inoculating Bacillus subtilis suspension and Lactobacillus plantarum suspension, fermenting, carrying out solid-liquid separation, obtaining seaweed enzyme, taking 60-70 wt.% malt and rice, crushing, adding water according to volume ratio of 1:(3-4), saccharifying at 63-65 degrees C for 60-80 minutes, filtering wort, adding hops, boiling for 45-75 minutes, concentrating at 10-13 degrees , taking wort, dietary fiber, syrup, sour material, natural spice and pigment, adding balance water, stirring, adding 0.35-0.65 wt.% carbon dioxide, carbonating, adding seaweed enzyme, stirring, diatomite filtering, and clarifying.
   USE - Method for preparing seaweed non-alcoholic beer based on marine microbial enzyme.
   ADVANTAGE - The drink has no fishy smell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The seaweed comprises 0.5-5 wt.%, preferably 0.5-2 wt.% red algae, 0.25-3 wt.%, preferably 0.5-1 wt.% green algae and 1-10 wt.%, preferably 2-5 wt.% brown algae, and algae enzyme is red algae enzyme, green algae enzyme or brown algae enzyme. The seaweed non-alcoholic beer based on marine microbial enzyme comprises 15-25 wt.% wort, 0.5-1.5 wt.% dietary fiber, 2-3 wt.% % syrup, 0.01-0.05 wt.% sour material, 0.01-0.02 wt.% natural spice and 0.04-0.06 wt.% pigment. The malt is Canadian malt, Australian malt, French malt or domestic malt. The red algae comprises laver, agar weed, asparagus, carrageenan or Eucheuma; green algae comprises Ulva or Enteromorpha; and brown algae comprises kelp, Undaria pinnatifida or Sargassum fusiforme. Preferred Method: The inoculating comprises inoculating 1.5 wt.% Bacillus subtilis suspension, stirring, fermenting at 40 degrees C for 3 days, cooling to 30 degrees C, inoculating 1 wt.% Lactobacillus plantarum suspension, stirred at 30 degrees C for 5 minutes, and standing for 2 days.

The catalyst for the reaction of ethane to form the cyclic styrene carbonate includes tetraethylammonium bromide and water.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing cyclic styrene carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of tetraethylammonium bromide to the water is 1:1. The tetraethyl ammonium bromide is 0.01t imes of the molar weight of epoxy ethane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction conditions for carbon dioxide and styrene oxide are reacted to form the cyclic styrene carbonate include a reaction environment of 1 MPa carbon dioxide pressure. The reaction conditions for carbon dioxide and styrene oxide are reacted to form the cyclic styrene carbonate include reaction temperature of 60-100 degrees C, preferably 100 degrees C. The reaction time of reacting carbon dioxide with styrene oxide to form the cyclic styrene carbonate is from 1-4 hours. The method of cyclic styrene carbonate preparation further comprises (b) separating a reaction product to obtain the cyclic styrene carbonate, where the separation step comprises (bi) extracting the organic phase; (bii) removing the extraction solvent; and (biii) chromatographically separating to obtain the cyclic styrene carbonate. The extraction solvent for extracting the organic phase in step (bi) is chloroform, where in step (biii) is obtained by column chromatography to obtain cyclic styrene carbonate. The method further comprises the step (c) recovering tetraethylammonium bromide to be recycled.

AU KONOV M A
AE KONOV M A (KONO-Individual)
GA 2021F0650N
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to agriculture, namely to the production of a complex fertilizer containing carbon dioxide. The method provides for obtaining a complex fertilizer containing carbonic acid and a component based on bulk material, including nutritional ingredients. As the specified component, a natural mineral is used - serpentinite, wet milled in a carbonic acid medium. Before the specified grinding, the serpentinite is first subjected to crushing to achieve a size acceptable for equipment used for wet grinding, then it is poured with carbonic acid and kept for at least 7 hours. In this case, wet grinding is carried out at the initial mass ratio of solid and liquid phases in the processed material S:L from 1.2:1 to 1.4:1 and is carried out until the resulting product reaches a pasty consistency with a particle size of not more than 0.02 mm.
   USE - Agriculture.
   ADVANTAGE - Ensuring the possibility of obtaining fertilizer as a single material applicable by directly introducing it into the soil during plowing or pre-sowing preparation while maintaining the properties due to the presence of carbon dioxide, as well as ensuring the gradual release of the latter.

PN CN113817517-A
TI Preparation and purification of biogas involves adding raw materials and fermentation broth to anaerobic fermentation tank, fermenting to form biogas, desulfurizing, removing carbon dioxide from desulfurized marsh gas, purifying to form biological natural gas, and passing to adsorption tower.
AU ZHANG P
AE ZHANG P (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 202205515M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation and purification of biogas involves adding raw materials to the anaerobic fermentation tank for preparing biogas, adding fermentation broth to the anaerobic fermentation tank, leaving still for fermentation of the raw materials and generation of biogas, passing the biogas to the filter equipment to remove the particulate impurities in the biogas, passing the biogas from which particulate impurities are removed to the pressurizing pump, then passing to desulfurization tower through pressurizing pump to obtain desulfurized marsh gas, passing the desulfurized marsh gas to clarified water vapor to remove carbon dioxide from marsh gas, purifying the marsh gas to form biological natural gas, and passing the biological natural gas to adsorption tower for drying the biological natural gas.
   USE - Preparation and purification of biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively increases production efficiency of the biogas and improves the purification efficiency of biogas to produce biological natural gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation involves grinding the raw materials and adding to anaerobic fermentation tank, adjusting the concentration of fermentation broth to 20-30%, adding to anaerobic fermentation tank containing the raw materials, adjusting the pH of solution in fermentation tank to 3.5-5.5, heating the anaerobic fermentation tank through heating device, so that internal environment of the anaerobic fermentation tank is maintained between 25-40degrees Celsius, leaving still for fermentation of the raw materials, and generating biogas to obtain biogas, stirring the raw material stack with heap turning device, so that the biogas can be fully volatilized, and filtering biogas using water-separating air filter.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The filler layer of the adsorption tower is hydrated aluminosilicate. The filler layer of the desulfurization tower is an iron oxide desulfurizer.

PN CN113663703-A
TI Preparation of cobalt phosphide-doped black phosphorus/graphitic carbon nitride nanocomposite material used in clean energy production, involves mixing cobalt phosphide-doped black phosphorus prepared using two-dimensional black phosphorus nanosheets with graphitic carbon nitride nanosheets.
AU LI C
   YANG T
   HU J
AE UNIV SUZHOU SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSO-C)
GA 2021D72462
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cobalt phosphide doped black phosphorus/graphitic carbon nitride nanocomposite material involves: (1) peeling block black phosphorus by using a solvent to obtain two-dimensional black phosphorus nanosheets; (2) uniformly dispersing precursor of cobalt phosphide and two-dimensional black phosphorus nanosheets in an organic solvent, and heating under a protective atmosphere to obtain a cobalt phosphide-doped black phosphorus; (3) taking carbon nitrogen compound as a precursor, calcining, and peeling to obtain graphitic carbon nitride nanosheets; and (4) uniformly mixing the cobalt phosphide-doped black phosphorus and the graphitic carbon nitride nanosheets.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cobalt phosphide-doped black phosphorus/graphitic carbon nitride nanocomposite material used in solar energy driving carbon dioxide conversion in clean energy production (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides cobalt phosphide-doped black phosphorus/graphitic carbon nitride nanocomposite material, which has excellent carbon dioxide reduction performance under illumination conditions, is simple in synthesis step, and ensures high selectivity of producing carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The peeling power in step (1) is 10-200 W, and the time is 1-5 days. The heating step in step (2) is carried out at 150-200degrees Celsius for 3-5 hours. The calcining step in step (3) is carried out by heating at 400-600degrees Celsius for 2-6 hours, and the peeling step is carried out at 400-600degrees Celsius for 1-4 hours. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of black phosphorus and solvent is 1: (1000-5000). The mass ratio of the precursor of cobalt phosphide and two-dimensional black phosphorus nanosheets is 1:(20-50). The mass ratio of cobalt phosphide-doped black phosphorus and graphitic carbon nitride nanosheets is 1:(1-100).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon nitrogen compound is dicyandiamide, urea, thiourea, cyanamide and/or melamine.
