AB    NOVELTY - Improving the reduction performance of                organometallic frame materials using a two-step                method comprises (i) subjecting the organometallic                framework material and the light reducing agent                alcohol complex to photoreaction to obtain a                reduced organometallic framework material, and (ii)                adding the organic amine and the substrate to the                reduced organometallic framework material obtained                in the step (i) for performing a dark reaction to                obtain a reduced product, where the substrate                comprises carbon dioxide, a photocatalytic hydrogen                production raw material or a completely halogenated                6-20C aromatic hydrocarbon.
   USE - The method is useful for improving the                reduction performance of organometallic frame                materials using a two-step method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has mild reaction conditions and                high reaction efficiency; and utilizes cheap and                low-toxic alcohol complexes as photoreductants to                reduce completely halogenated 6-20C aromatic                hydrocarbons, photocatalytic hydrogen production                raw materials and carbon dioxide to obtain their                reduction products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  organometallic frame material comprises                  titanium-based organometallic frame materials                  including materials from institute Lavoisier                  (MIL)-125(titanium), amino-MIL-125(titanium), and                  Centrum voor Oppervlaktechemie en.                  Katalyse(COK)-69(titanium). The alcohol complex                  comprises methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, and/or                  benzyl alcohol. The dosage of the organometallic                  framework material and the alcohol complex is 10                  mg organometallic framework material and 1-20 ml                  the alcohol complex. In the step (ii), the                  organic amine comprises ethanolamine, ethylamine,                  propyl amine, diethanolamine, diethylamine,                  dipropylamine, triethanolamine, triethylamine,                  tripropylamine, and/or tripentylamine. The                  concentration of the organic amine is above 0.1                  mol/l. The substrate a completely halogenated                  6-20C aromatic hydrocarbon means an aromatic or                  partially aromatic monocyclic, bicyclic or                  tricyclic hydrocarbon ring with 6-20 carbon                  atoms, and if the aromatic ring contains                  substituents, unless all hydrogen atoms other                  than the halogen substituent are replaced by                  halogen, preferably completely halogenated 6-14C                  aromatic ring. The completely halogenated 6-14C                  aromatic ring comprises an aromatic or partially                  aromatic monocyclic, bicyclic or tricyclic                  hydrocarbon with 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13 or 14                  carbon atoms ring, and all the hydrogen atoms on                  the aromatic ring except for the halogen                  substituent are replaced by halogen. The                  substrate comprises carbon dioxide,                  decabromodiphenyl ether, hexabromobenzene,                  decabromodiphenyl, or decabromodiphenyl ketone.                  The molar ratio of the substrate to the                  organometallic framework material is                  (1-3):1.Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  photoreaction is carried out by using ultraviolet                  light-visible light with a wavelength of 500 nm                  or less When MIL-125 (titanium) is used,                  ultraviolet light-visible light below 340 nm is                  used for illumination. When amino-MIL                  125(titanium) is used, the ultraviolet                  light-visible light below 500 nm is used for                  illumination. When COK-69(titanium) is used, the                  ultraviolet-visible light below 350 nm is used                  for illumination. In the step (i) and (ii), the                  reaction is carried out at 0-100degrees Celsius                  under an oxygen-free environment.

AE SHOUGANG GROUP CO LTD (SHGG-C)
GA 2021B3982A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves sending molten steel into a red-green-red (RH) refining station. A vacuum treatment is performed to make the molten steel enter into a vacuum chamber to circulate. Steel liquid circulating in the vacuum chamber is subjected to bottom blowing gas. The steel liquid is decarburized. The bottom blowing air source is provided with a section of bottom blowing inert gas and another section of the bottom blowing weak oxidizing air. The deoxidizing and alloying process is performed on the steel liquid. The RH refining process is completed.
   USE - Operation method for high-efficiency refining of low carbon steel using carbon dioxide, and can also be used in steel smelting.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of effectively and economically refining the steel liquid with high-efficiency, and prevents high temperature melting loss and erosion, and improves the decarburization efficiency, and protects the blowing element and prevents blocking of the blowing argon gas.

PN EP3872439-A1; DE102020105266-A1; US2021270585-A1; DE102020105266-B4
TI Projectile for ammunition of compressed air weapon, carbon dioxide-weapon or spring-loaded weapon, is made from mixture comprising metal and plastic, based on total weight of projectile.
AU WAGNER T
AE RUAG AMMOTEC GMBH (RUAG-Non-standard)
   RUAG AMMOTEC GMBH (RUAG-Non-standard)
   RUAG AMMOTEC GMBH (RUAG-Non-standard)
GA 2021A16060
AB    NOVELTY - A projectile is made of a mixture comprising metal and 2-15 wt.% plastic based on the total weight of the projectile.
   USE - Projectile e.g. diabolo used for ammunition of compressed air weapon, carbon dioxide-weapon or spring-loaded weapon such as gun.
   ADVANTAGE - The projectile is lead-free, i.e. produced without the addition of lead. The plastic used is able to absorb the metal content, in particular evenly. It can be selected in such a way that good processing is possible and the plastic is not too brittle. By means of the specific weight fraction of 2 to 15 wt.% by weight of plastic, an optimum of density in particular high density, and ductility is achieved, as a result of which improved precision can be achieved. The presence of a high proportion of metal increases the density of the projectile, which has a positive effect on precision.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of the projectile.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The projectile comprises 85-98 wt.% metal based on the total weight of the projectile. The metal is chosen from copper, iron, zinc, tin, magnesium, tungsten, hard metal and sintered metal. The metal in the projectile is in the form of particles. Preferred Property: The particles have average diameter of 5-15 mu m. The mixture has density of 4-12 g/cm3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The plastic is a thermoplastic elastomer. The thermoplastic elastomer is natural rubber or ethylene-alkyl acrylate copolymer, preferably ethylene-butyl acrylate copolymer.

PT P
PN CN113171770-A
TI Preparing catalyst comprises e.g. mixing chromium source, aqueous ammonia, structure guiding agent and deionized water, mixing obtained solutions, preparing sol-gel, introducing carbon dioxide gas and calcining obtained crystallized product.
AU DING J
   SHEN J
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 202188580B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst comprises (i) mixing chromium source, aqueous ammonia, structure guiding agent and deionized water to obtain first solution, mixing tin source and deionized water to obtain second solution, (ii) adding second solution into first solution, continuously stirring to form sol-gel at 60-90 degrees C, (iii) transferring sol-gel into supercritical reaction kettle and introducing carbon dioxide gas to carrying out crystallization reaction, and (iv) filtering obtained crystallized product, washing, drying, and calcining for 1-3 hours at 450 degrees C to obtain final product.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing 1,2-dimethylimidazole (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has small crystal grain size, large specific surface area, large exposed acidic active sites, rich amount of acid, high acid strength, high conversion rate of raw materials and yield of target product 1,2-dimethylimidazole; and is conducive to dehydration reaction of 2-methylimidazole and methanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for catalyst obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the chromium source is chromium nitrate or chromium chloride. The tin source is sodium stannate or stannous chloride. The structure guiding agent is tetrapropylammonium hydroxide, tetraethylammonium hydroxide or tetrabutylammonium hydroxide. Preferred Method: In step (i), the concentration of chromium source in first solution is calculated as chromium oxide, where molar ratio of each raw material is chromium oxide, ammonia, structure directing agent and water is 1:25-30:15-20:200-250, then stirring for 1-3 hours at rate of 80-120 revolutions/minutes at room temperature. In step (i): calculating the concentration of tin source in the second solution by tin(IV) oxide, and the tin source and deionized water are mixed according to the molar ratio of tin(IV) oxide, and water is 1:60-100, then stirring for 1-3 hours at rate of 80-120 revolutions/minutes at room temperature. In step (i), calculating concentration of the second solution and the first solution based on the molar ratio of tin(IV) oxide to chromium oxide of tin(IV) oxide and chromium is 1:1-4. The step (ii) comprises adding second solution dropwise to the first solution at a dropping acceleration rate of 1.5-2 ml/minutes, then stirring for 1-3 hours at a temperature of 60-90 degrees C at a stirring rate of 80-120 revolutions/minutes to obtain a sol-gel, where the mass ratio of the first solution and the second solution is 5-19.3:1. The step (iii) comprises carrying out crystallization reaction in the supercritical reactor, and introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reactor, and crystallizing for 0.5-3 hours in a supercritical state of 32-135 degrees C and a pressure of 7.5-25 MPa. The step (iv) comprises taking deionized water for washing until the pH of filtrate is 7, and the drying at 120 degrees C for 6-12.

PN CN112546529-A; CN112546529-B
TI Usage of industrial waste residue and waste gas to            achieve high alkaline red mud dealkalization, involves            mixing high-alkaline red mud, bittern, and desulfurized            gypsum, and pouring carbon dioxide-containing            industrial waste gas.
AU GAO Y
   NIU A
   LING D
AE ZHOUJI ENVIRONMENTAL SCI RES INST GUANGZHOU CO              LTD (ZHOU-Non-standard)
   ZHOUJI ENVIRONMENTAL SCI RES INST GUANGZ (ZHOU-Non-standard)
GA 202136867R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves (a) mixing high-alkaline                red mud, bittern, water, and desulfurized gypsum to                form a mixed slurry, and the ratio of red mud,                bittern, water, desulfurized gypsum is 1:                (0.01-0.1) according to the mass ratio: (2.5-6):                (0.05-0.2), (b) pouring carbon dioxide-containing                industrial waste gas into the mixed slurry,                controlling the slurry temperature to be 40-80                degrees C, the gas partial pressure in the reaction                tank is 0.1-4 MPa, stirring the slurry uniformly,                and the reaction time is 30-60 minutes, and                pressing filter again to dry the residue after                press filter.
   USE - Method for using industrial waste residue and                waste gas to achieve high alkaline red mud                dealkalization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the pH value of the red                mud, reduces the sodium ion content in the red mud,                and improves the utilization efficiency of the red                mud in the field of building materials and the                soilization of the red mud.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The high alkaline red                  mud is Bayer red mud or sintering red mud.                  Preferred Composition: The ratio of red mud,                  bittern, water is 1:(0.05-0.1):(2.5-6), and the                  ratio of desulfurized gypsum and red mud is                  0.5-2:10 when is Bayer process red mud. The ratio                  of red mud, bittern, water is 1:(0.05-0.1):5, and                  the ratio of desulfurized gypsum, red mud is                  0.5-1:10 when is sintering red mud.

TI Dust measurement feedback system, has transmission unit provided with refractive and reflecting planes, and intelligent terminal connected with communication module of controller through communication unit to realize transmission of data.

GA 202131102K
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a dust measuring transmission unit provided with a refractive plane and a reflecting plane. A detecting unit is provided with a controller, a photoelectric detector, a formaldehyde sensor, a carbon dioxide sensor, a sulfur dioxide sensor and a dust sensor. The photoelectric detector, the formaldehyde sensor, the carbon dioxide sensor, the sulfur dioxide sensor and the dust sensor are connected with the controller. An intelligent terminal is connected with a communication module of the controller through a communication unit to realize transmission of data. The intelligent terminal is an intelligent mobile phone and a computer. The refraction plane is a triangular prism.
   USE - Dust measurement feedback system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is convenient to observe detection data in real time, and has better safety performance and high precision so as to realize remote measuring dust concentration, and improve air quality.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a dust measurement feedback system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN112510565-A
TI Mixed gas insulating medium containing            environmentally friendly gas            heptafluoroisobutyronitrile in current gas insulating            transformer, gas insulating combined electric            appliance, comprises heptafluoroisobutyronitrile            gas.
AU YANG C
   YE Z
   ZHU H
   YANG H
   SUN L
   HUANG W
   LI X
   LIU M
   LUO G
   OUYANG J
AE YUNNAN POWER GRID CO LTD BAOSHAN POWER (CSPG-C)
GA 202128578F
AB    NOVELTY - Mixed gas insulating medium containing                environmentally friendly gas                heptafluoroisobutyronitrile comprises                heptafluoroisobutyronitrile gas,                octafluorocyclobutane gas, and carbon dioxide                gas.
   USE - Mixed gas insulating medium containing                environmentally friendly gas                heptafluoroisobutyronitrile used in current gas                insulating transformer, gas insulating combined                electric appliance, gas insulating power                transmission pipeline and other high voltage power                devices.
   ADVANTAGE - The medium has excellent insulating                performance and good environment-friendly                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing a mixed gas insulating medium                containing environmentally friendly gas                heptafluoroisobutyronitrile, which involves:
   (A) taking closed container and installing at                the bottom there is a high vacuum valve and a                bottom gas filling valve, where the top gas filling                valve is installed on the top, the closed container                is evacuated through the high vacuum valve;                and
   (B) removing heptafluoroisobutyronitrile gas                and octafluorocyclobutane gas from the top, filling                in gas filling valve and filling carbon dioxide gas                in from the bottom filling valve and filling gas                components according to the set content and mixing                to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The components in the                  mixed gas insulating medium comprises 20-25 wt.%                  heptafluoroisobutyronitrile gas, 5-10 wt.%                  octafluorocyclobutane gas, where the total volume                  percentage of heptafluoroisobutyronitrile gas,                  octafluorocyclobutane gas, and carbon dioxide gas                  is 100%. Preferred Conditions: The purity of the                  heptafluoroisobutyronitrile gas is 99.99%, the                  purity of the octafluorocyclobutane gas is 99.99%                  and the purity of the carbon dioxide gas is                  99.99%. The vacuum degree in the closed container                  meets that the internal pressure is 10 Pascal.                  The heptafluoroisobutyronitrile gas is filled                  from the top gas filling valve. The                  octafluorocyclobutane gas is filled with carbon                  dioxide gas from the bottom filling valve.

PT P
PN EP3789417-A1; WO2021043700-A1; CN114423797-A; EP4025621-A1; US2022306825-A1; JP2022547476-W
TI Manufacture of insulation component e.g. vacuum            insulation panels, involves injecting polyurethane            reactive mixture containing supercritical carbon            dioxide into pressurized cavity, curing mixture,            creating vacuum in cavity, and sealing.
AU VOR D E L
   REITHMEIER M
   HAHN C
   ALBERS R
   REITMEIR M
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
GA 202123743B
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of insulation component based on a                fine-cell and open-cell                polyurethane/polyisocyanurate foam in cavity of                molding tool involves (i) inserting a possibly                preformed insert into a cavity of the mold, (ii)                inserting a barrier film in the cavity or in the                insert, (iii) applying a pressure of 8-30 bar in                the cavity, (iv) injecting a polyurethane reactive                mixture containing supercritical carbon dioxide                into the pressurized cavity, (v) relaxing to                ambient pressure after time of 1-40 seconds                measured after the injection, (vi) curing the                polyurethane reactive mixture, (vii) creating a                vacuum of 0.001-0.5 mbar in the cavity, (viii)                sealing existing holes in the barrier film, and                (ix) finally demolding.
   USE - Manufacture of insulation component e.g.                vacuum insulation panels.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple manufacture of                insulation component based on fine-cell and                open-cell polyurethane/polyisocyanurate foam in                short period of time. The vacuum insulation panel                has high level of gas tightness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Between steps (ii) and                  (iii) and/or between step (vi) and (vii), a gas                  trap material (getter material) is                  introduced.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyurethane                  reactive mixture contains a isocyanate-reactive                  component (A) containing at least one polyol                  component (A1) with functionality of 2.5 or more                  (excluding 2.5), chosen from polyether polyol,                  polyester polyol, polycarbonate polyol,                  polyether-polycarbonate polyol and polyether                  ester polyol, on which the proportion of all                  primary hydroxyl (OH) functional groups present                  in component (A1) in relation to the total number                  of terminal hydroxyl (OH) functions in component                  (A1) is at least 30%, at least one catalyst                  component (A2) having Zerewitinoff-active                  hydrogen, at least one cell-opening connection                  (A3), supercritical carbon dioxide (A4),                  optionally propellant (A5) excluding                  supercritical carbon dioxide, and optionally                  auxiliaries and/or additives (A6), and at least                  one polyisocyanate component (B). The molar ratio                  of the number of moles of isocyanate groups to                  the number of moles of hydroxyl groups is                  80-400.

   USE - The preparation of copper-iron-carbon catalyst                is used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low cost, simple synthesis                process and good repeatability and is easy to                operate.

The inorganic copper salt is                  copper chloride, copper nitrate, copper sulfate,                  copper acetate and/or copper oxalate. The molar                  concentration of aqueous solution of inorganic                  metal salt is 0.5-2 mol/l. The carbon containing                  raw material is glucose, sucrose, fructose,                  starch and/or graphene oxide. The mass volume                  concentration of carbon containing solution is                  10-100 mg/ml. The mass ratio of inorganic copper                  salt, inorganic iron salt and carbon containing                  raw material is 20-80:20-80:100. TI Preparing nitrogen-sulfur co-doped waste biomass            activated carbon comprises e.g. pre-treating crop            straw, mixing with activator iron(II)            chloride/iron(III) chloride, activating and calcining,            and reacting with thiourea or thiosemicarbazide.

   SHI G
   XIAO H
   CHEN G
   SHI Y
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 202069482B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-sulfur co-doped waste                biomass activated carbon, comprises (i)                pre-treating crop straw, (ii) mixing pre-treated                crop straw obtained in step (i) with activator                iron(II) chloride or iron(III) chloride, then                activating and calcining in inert gas atmosphere,                cooling to room temperature and post-processing to                obtain straw-based activated carbon, (iii) mixing                straw-based activated carbon obtained in step (ii)                with thiourea or thiosemicarbazide, then activating                and calcining in inert gas atmosphere, cooling to                room temperature and post-processing to obtain                final product. The crop straw is eggplant straw,                pepper straw or tomato straw.
   USE - The nitrogen-sulfur co-doped waste biomass                activated carbon is useful for adsorbing carbon                dioxide and sulfur dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low cost and simple process;                utilizes cheap and easily available raw material                sources; and produces activated carbon having large                specific surface area, and excellent adsorption                effect on carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (ii), the mass                  ratio between activator and straw obtained in                  step (i) is 2-5:1. In step (iii), the mass ratio                  between thiourea or thiosemicarbazide and                  straw-based activated carbon obtained in step                  (ii) is 1:2-5. The inert gas is nitrogen.                  Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises washing                  the straw, drying, crushing and pre-treating at                  300-500 degrees C for 35-60 minutes. In step                  (ii), the activation and calcination are carried                  out at 600-800 degrees C for 60-100 minutes, and                  the post-processing is carried out by soaking in                  0.8-1.2 mol/l dilute hydrochloric acid, stirring                  at 45-65 degrees C for 1-2 hours, then repeatedly                  washing with deionized water to neutral, drying,                  and sieving through 160-200 mesh sieve. In step                  (iii), the activation and calcination are carried                  out at 550-650 degrees C for 35-60 minutes, and                  the post-processing is carried out by soaking in                  0.8-1.2 mol/l dilute hydrochloric acid, stirring                  at 45-65 degrees C for 1-2 hours, then repeatedly                  washing with deionized water to neutral, drying,                  and sieving through 160-200 mesh sieve.

GA 2020338484

First mixed gas of the hydrogen-containing VOCs waste gas and auxiliary fuel gas are burned to obtain hot waste gas. AU GUAN Y
AE DONGGUAN GUOYU SHOE MATERIAL CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 202028561D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing of thermoplastic                polyurethane particles. The thermoplastic                polyurethane particles are loaded into a reactor to                clean the reactor and discharge remaining air in                the reactor by low-pressure carbon dioxide. A                reaction kettle is heated by a hot water                circulation system, where temperature of the                reaction kettle is controlled between 30-90 degrees                centigrade. Carbon dioxide is added to the reactor                until reaching pressure in the reactor between                13-17Mpa. Temperature and pressure in the reactor                are respectively maintained at 30-40 degrees                centigrade and 5-20Mpa. The reaction kettle is                opened to release the pressure. The thermoplastic                polyurethane foam particles are pressure-preserved                and shaped to prevent collapse and cracking of the                thermoplastic polyurethane foam particles. The                thermoplastic polyurethane foam particles are                placed into a water vapor molding equipment.                Cooling and shaping operations of soles are                performed after forming the soles.
   USE - Manufacturing sole ultra-critical foaming                method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables ensuring better resilience                effect and high wear resistance.

PN CN110508102-A
TI Preparing polyaryl ether ketone Am-PAFEK and PIM-1 mixed film involves adding bisphenol, 4,4'-difluorobenzophenone, bisphenol hydrazone, bisphenol A, and 4-aminophenylhydroquinone into container, and performing polymerization reaction.
AU WANG Z
   HOU L
   CHEN W
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN TECHNOLOGY (UYGL-C)
GA 2019A13613
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyaryl ether ketone Am-PAFEK and PIM-1 mixed film involves adding bisphenol, 4,4'-difluorobenzophenone, bisphenol hydrazone, bisphenol A, and 4-aminophenylhydroquinone into reaction container, and performing polymerization reaction to obtain polyaryl ether ketone. The polyaryl ether ketone is dissolved to obtain a polymer solution. The 5,5',6,6'-tetrahydroxy-1,3,3,3', 3'-tetramethyl-1,1'-spiral-linked indole and tetrafluoroterephthalonitrile diene to reaction container, and then polymerized to obtain PIM-1 polymer. The PIM-1 polymer is dissolved to obtain a polymer solution. The obtained polymer solutions are mixed, stirred and paved into a film, which is soaked in methanol to obtain final product.
   USE - Method for preparing polyaryl ether ketone Am-PAFEK and PIM-1 mixed film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare polyaryl ether ketone Am-PAFEK and PIM-1 mixed film, which causes non-covalent bond interaction with carbon dioxide to enhance solubility of carbon dioxide in separation membrane, has high gas selectivity, good air permeability and broad application prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The method involves adding and mixing a mol of 4-aminophenylhydroquinone, b mol of bisphenol A, c mol of 4,4-difluorobenzophenone and d mol of bisphenol hydrazone, adding catalyst, water-carrying agent and solvent, refluxing it with water at 125-145 degrees C for 3-5 hours, removing water-carrying agent, and reacting it at a temperature to 175-185 degrees C for 6-20 hours to obtain polyarylene ether ketone, where a+b+d is c. The method involves adding n mol of 5,5',6,6'-tetrahydroxy-1,3,3,3',3'-tetramethyl-1,1'-spiral-linked indole to reaction container under protection of nitrogen, adding m mol of tetrafluoroterephthalonitrile, catalyst and solvent, mixing it together, heating and stirring it at 150-165 degrees C for 40-60 minutes to obtain a crude product, which is dissolved, filtered, washed and dried to obtain PIM-1, where n:m is 2-2.5:1.5-2. The mass ratio of polyaryl ether ketone and PIM-1 is 1-10:10. Preferred Component: The water carrying agent is toluene. The catalyst is potassium carbonate. The solvent is chloroform (CDCl3), N,N-dimethylacetamide (DMAc), sulfolane (TMS), N-methylpyrrolidone (NMP) or dimethylsulfoxide (DMSO).

PT P
PN TW201943673-A
TI Hydraulic cement-free fly ash binder material capable of reducing carbon dioxide emissions and being suitable for precast products.
AU FAN C
   CHAO S
AE FAN C (FANC-Individual)
GA 2020095480
AB    NOVELTY - Disclosed is a hydraulic cement-free fly ash binder material, wherein the binder material is formed by stirring and mixing fly ash and quicklime, wherein the chemical composition of the fly ash mainly consists of CaO and SiO2, and meets the standard of CNS3036; the main chemical composition of quicklime is CaO, and the content must be 90% or higher. Once the hydraulic cement-free fly ash binder material is mixed with water and a hydration reaction is generated, an excellent cementation and hardening effect is thus produced. The binder material of the present invention is used to completely replace traditional cement, and when it is used as the binder material for concrete or mortar, not only the amount of cement to be used is reduced, and carbon dioxide emissions are also decreased, thereby achieving the purpose of optimizing resource recovery of industrial waste. Furthermore, the disclosed has a property of short curing time, therefore, it is very suitable for precast products as it increases the use frequency of molds for precast products.

PT P
PN CN110272053-A; CN110272053-B
TI Preparation of high purity water glass involves contacting aluminum residue of fly ash with alkali solution, performing desiliconization treatment, carbonizing solid-liquid mixed slurry, and performing solid-liquid separation.
AU ZHAO L
   CHEN A
   WANG X
   LIU H
   XIAO Y
AE CHN ENERGY INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (SHGR-C)
   NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
GA 201982561M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high purity water glass involves (1) contacting aluminum residue of fly ash and/or fly ash with an alkali solution for alkali dissolution and desiliconization, (2) and contacting obtained solid-liquid mixed slurry with carbon dioxide to carry out carbonization treatment, and performing solid-liquid separation.
   USE - Preparation of high purity water glass.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is carried out continuously by carrying out alkali-soluble desiliconization and carbonization treatment without subjecting slurry after alkali-soluble desiliconization to solid-liquid separation and directly performing carbon separation. The method produces high purity water glass in one-step solid-liquid separation, and fully utilizes fly ash and coal ash extracted aluminum residue characteristics to act as precipitated growth nucleus and adsorbing sediment to improve efficiency of decontamination.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of low-iron silica, which involves contacting high purity water glass with carbon dioxide at 50-90 degrees C for secondary carbonization treatment, and drying obtained solid precipitate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The aluminum residue of fly ash and/or fly ash is calculated using silica. The alkali solution is used based on alkali metal element. The alkali solution is mixed with fly ash and/or powder. The carbon dioxide is used in an amount such that pH of solid-liquid mixed slurry is 9.3-13, preferably 9.5-12.5. The desiliconized slag obtained after solid-liquid separation is subjected to aluminum extraction. The secondary carbonization treatment is carried out at 65-85 degrees C using carbon dioxide in an amount such that pH of mixture is less than 9, preferably 8-9 (excluding 9). Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of alkali solution and fly ash and/or aluminum ash residue of fly ash is 0.6-2.4:1, preferably 0.8-2.2:1. The alkali solution comprises 5-50%, preferably 10-35% alkali. Preferred Conditions: The alkali-soluble desiliconization treatment temperature is 50-200 degrees C, preferably 70-160 degrees C. The alkali-soluble desiliconization treatment time is 5-180 minutes, preferably 15-90 minutes. The carbonization temperature is 50-99 degrees C, preferably 80-95 degrees C. The ultrapure water glass is contacted with carbon dioxide preferably at 90-99 degrees C. The secondary carbonization treatment temperature is 93-98 degrees C.
DC L01 (Glass - includes chemical compositions, batch treatment, furnaces, flat glass forming, hollow-ware forming, postforming and glass/ceramics, but not lens designs, bottling, bottle-washing, closures for containers, glazing designs, glass cutting, chamfering edges, printing on glass, disposing of used glass or the production of pure sodium silicate. Chemical aspects of optical fibres (C03).)

PN CN110257202-A
TI Preparing mixed wine involves mixing raw materials            of tobacco 73 and rose vine, and then crushing,            subjecting obtained grape pulp to carbon dioxide low            temperature impregnation, breaking stalk to obtain            grape vine, adding pectinase to vine.
AU DING Y
   REN F
   WANG Y
   WU X
   WANG Z
   HAN X
AE SHANDONG GRAPE INST (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019914956
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mixed wine involves mixing the raw                materials of tobacco 73 and rose vine, and then                crushing. The obtained grape pulp is subjected to                carbon dioxide low temperature impregnation, and                the stalk is broken to obtain grape vine. The                pectinase is added to the vine, Saccharomyces                cerevisiae is added after a period of time. Then                pre-fermented, after separation of the skin                residue, after entering the fermentation. The                fermentation is finished, and the grape is                fermented, when the residual sugar is less than                5g/L. The wine is fermented under low temperature                conditions, and the wine is aged. The wine is added                to the rubber and filtered.
   USE - Method for preparing mixed wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare mixed wine,                which has a bright deep ruby red color, the floral                and fruity flavor is more pure and pure, the taste                is fresh and mellow, the process is scientific and                reasonable, and the price is good.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The blending ratio of                  the tobacco 73 and rose fragrant grape raw                  materials is 4:6-6:4 (preferably 1:1). The                  crushing ratio is 10-20%, and the obtained grape                  pulp is filled into a carbon dioxide fermenter,                  and 100 mg/L of potassium metasulfite is added,                  and the mixture is immersed for 6-10 days at                  25-28 degrees C. The pectinase is added in an                  amount of 28 to 32 mg/L (preferably 30 mg/L). The                  Saccharomyces cerevisiae is added after an                  interval of 10-15 hours (preferably 12 hours).                  The Saccharomyces cerevisiae is preferably                  Saccharomyces cerevisiae E491, and the amount of                  the Saccharomyces cerevisiae E491 is controlled                  to be 150-250 mg/L (preferably 200 mg/L). The                  pre-fermentation treatment temperature is 24-26                  degrees C, the fermentation ends, after the                  fermentation, when the residual sugar is less                  than 5 g/L. Then 120mg/L potassium sulfite is                  added to obtain the original wine of the grape.                  The aging is carried out at a low temperature of                  16-18 degrees C for 4-6 months. The mass to                  volume ratio of the sizing agent to the aging                  wine is 1.0-1.5 g:1L (preferably 1.4g:1L). The                  sizing agent is specifically a mixture of                  bentonite, protein powder and sodium carbonate.                  The mass ratio of the bentonite, the protein                  powder and the sodium carbonate is                  0.8-2:0.1-0.5:0.1-0.5 (preferably 1:0.2:0.2).                  Preferred Process: The preparation method further                  comprises filling the sterilized product into a                  desired finished product.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

   GAO J

   LI F
AE JIANGXI GANFENG LITHIUM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201932502M
AB    NOVELTY - Industrially producing anhydrous lithium hydroxide balls comprises (i) drying industrial grade lithium hydroxide monohydrate to obtain anhydrous lithium hydroxide, (ii) crushing anhydrous lithium hydroxide to a fine powder having a diameter D50 of 10-50 mu m, (iii) powdering anhydrous lithium hydroxide to form a powder into a ball, (iv) sieving the lithium hydroxide ball, and continuously performing granulation into a ball by the small particle ball which does not meet the particle size requirement, and (v) sieving qualified spherical lithium hydroxide balls, and drying.
   USE - The balls are useful for battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The balls: does not drop powder; are suitable to carbon dioxide absorbent; have absorption efficiency of the absorbent absorbed by the absorbent tested according to the coal standard MT454-2008; and has absorption rate more than 85%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for recovering anhydrous lithium hydroxide balls comprising introducing carbon dioxide into the lithium hydroxide to be recovered, carbonizing, completely converting into lithium carbonate, drying, and packaging to obtain lithium carbonate for battery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (vi) packaging i.e., vacuum packaging. The step (v) drying and the step (vi) packaging further comprises a ball polishing step, where the ball polishing is surface-polished with anhydrous lithium hydroxide ball, and dried to obtain a water-repellent lithium hydroxide ball having a regular surface. In the step (i), drying is carried out at 160-200 degrees C for 1-4 hours, and the drying process is vacuum drying. In the step (iii), the powdering and forming ball is carried out in a nitrogen atmosphere in a disc pelletizer, where the powder adding water in the powder forming process is controlled to 8-20 wt.% lithium hydroxide powder. In the step (v), the drying is carried out at 160-200 degrees C for 1-4 hours. The step (iv) is performed by a square vibrating screen, and the step (v) is performed by using a vacuum dryer. In the step (vi), the ball is polished by using a ball polishing machine. In the step (ii), the micropowder is crushed by using a nitrogen source jet mill. In the step (vi), the packaging is carried out by using an automatic vacuum packaging machine, and is performed in a separate drying room. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the amount of water in the anhydrous lithium hydroxide is less than or equal to 4%. In the step (vi), the amount of the substance of the water in the anhydrous lithium hydroxide is less than or equal to 2%.

PN CN108998690-A
TI Preparing silicon carbide particles/AZ91D composite material involves placing AZ91D-type magnesium alloy in heating furnace to melt, then adding sulfur hexafluoride-carbon dioxide mixed protective gas and then cooling to solid.
AU INVENTOR U
AE QIDONG CHUANGLV LANDSCAPE ENG CO LTD (QIDO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A35478
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing silicon carbide particles/AZ91D composite material involves placing 20-30 pts. wt. AZ91D-type magnesium alloy in a heating furnace to melt, then adding sulfur hexafluoride-carbon dioxide mixed protective gas in volume ratio is 35:1 and then cooling to solid, stirring the molten liquid to form stable vortex, pre-heating 5-9 pts. wt. silicon carbide particles at 580-590 degrees C in a smelting furnace, stirring for 25-35 minutes, heating at 690-710 degrees C for 15-25 minutes, casting the molten liquid into mold, pre-heating at 380-390 degrees C, pressing at pressure of 985-995 MPa, cooling and solidifying to obtain silicon carbide particles/AZ91D, carrying out hot extrusion at speed of 3.5-4.5 mm/second, cutting and processing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing silicon carbide particles/AZ91D composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of silicon carbide particles/AZ91D composite material with improved toughness and hardness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pre-heating step is carried out at temperature of 585 degrees C. The stirring step is carried out for 30 minutes. The heating step is carried out at temperature of 700 degrees C for 20 minutes. The extruding step is carried out at speed of 4 mm/second. The pressing step is carried out at pressure of 990 MPa.

PT P
PN CN108607510-A; CN108607510-B
TI Nitrogen-doped porous carbon material used as carbon dioxide adsorbent, has preset specific surface area, pore volume, pore diameter, and nitrogen content, and is prepared by mixed molten salt template method.
AU WANG P
   ZHANG G
   CHEN W
   JIAO H
   LIU L
   CHEN Q
   DENG X
AE UNIV NORTHWEST MINZU (UNNA-C)
GA 201880474Q
AB    NOVELTY - A nitrogen-doped porous carbon material has specific surface area of 1200-2600 m2/g, preferably 2600 m2/g, pore volume of 0.60-1.20 cm3/g or 1.30 cm3/g, maximum pore diameter of 0.8-3.0 nm, preferably 2.8 nm, and nitrogen content of 4.5-6.5%, and is prepared by mixed molten salt template method.
   USE - Nitrogen-doped porous carbon material used as carbon dioxide adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen-doped porous carbon material is prepared by simple method, and has excellent adsorption capacity, selectivity and reusability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the nitrogen-doped porous carbon material, which involves dissolving a mixed molten salt in water to obtain aqueous solution (1), dissolving chitosan in an aqueous solution containing acetic acid to obtain aqueous solution (2), mixing the aqueous solutions (1) and (2) at room temperature, stirring, freeze-drying the mixture, calcining under a protection gas atmosphere, washing and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed molten salt is lithium chloride/zinc chloride, sodium chloride/zinc chloride, or potassium chloride/zinc chloride. The protection gas is argon or nitrogen. Preferred Composition: When the mixed molten salt is lithium chloride/zinc chloride, the mass ratio of lithium chloride to the mixed molten salt is 18-28 mol%. When the mixed molten salt is sodium chloride/zinc chloride, the mass ratio of sodium chloride to the mixed molten salt is 37-47 mol%. When the mixed molten salt is potassium chloride/zinc chloride, the mass ratio of potassium chloride to the mixed molten salt is 46-56 mol%. Preferred Condition: The aqueous solutions (1) and (2) are mixed and stirred for 0.5-2.0 hours. The calcination is carried out at 700-1500 degrees C for 1-2 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of chitosan to acetic acid is 0.1-10. The mass ratio of the mixed molten salt to chitosan is 0.2-5.0.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E11-Q02B; E12-A10; E31-N05C; E31-N05D; J01-D; J01-E02B3; A08-S02; A10-E05B; A10-E09; A12-W11; A12-W11D
IP B01D-053/02; B01J-020/20; B01J-020/28; B01J-020/30

PN CN207911652-U
TI Remote-controlled smart household bean sprout growing machine, has temperature sensor and carbon dioxide concentration detector which are arranged inside sealing cover and processing unit is mounted inside bean sprouts machine.
AU QI S
   GU H
   LI Z
AE QI S (QISS-Individual)
GA 201879167M
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model has one remote-control intelligent household bean sprout growing machine, belonging to the manufacturing technical field of household appliances, comprising a machine main body, a heating base, a water inlet pipe, a water inlet switch, a catalyst injector, a sealing cover and a temperature sensor; the heating pedestal and matched temperature sensor can ensure the temperature in the machine liner meets the bean sprout growth requirement, the central processor can control water inlet switch is needed to ensure needed moisture supply, catalyst injector under the control of the central processor can ensure timely in the growth of bean sprout supplemental catalyst. with a gravity sensor, water can be automatically selected for different beans, avoid the manual operation is not scientific, a carbon dioxide concentration detection function, it can alarm and prevent the beans in the germination process, because the breathing effect of bean sprout making bean sprout rotting, monitoring remote controller releases the labour without people operating the bean sprouting machine.
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); T01 (Digital Computers); W05 (Alarms, Signalling, Telemetry and Telecontrol)

PT P
PN CN108514704-A; CN208694091-U
TI Portable fire extinguisher for instantaneously generating gas, has valve core separated from sealing valve to make extinguishing agent tank is connected with nozzle, and nozzle for separating water-based fire suppressant.
AU XIA J
AE JIANGSU JISHIYU FIRE EQUIP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201873551B
AB    NOVELTY - The extinguisher has a switch valve provided with a valve rod. A middle part of the valve rod is fixed with a valve core. A lower end of the valve rod is connected with a valve tip. The valve tip is suspended on an upper part of a carbon dioxide bottle opening sealing film. A liquid carbon dioxide in the bottle is released to air to generate high pressure gas. The valve core is separated from a sealing valve to make the extinguishing agent tank is connected with a nozzle. The nozzle separates a water-based fire suppressant under the compression of high pressure gas.
   USE - Portable fire extinguisher for instantaneously generating gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The extinguisher can instantly generates high pressure gas, so that failure of fire extinguisher generated by pressure leakage can be avoided, and has high safety and low maintenance cost.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a portable fire extinguisher.
DC K01 (Fire fighting, fire-extinguishing compositions - excluding fire engines, sprinkler systems, hose reels and protective clothing (A62D with K2).); P35 (Life-saving, fire-fighting (A62).)
MC K01-A; P35-C01C1; P35-C03
IP A62C-013/68; A62C-013/76
